    相如及长，聪明秀丽，弱冠入泮。未几，范亦病卒。相如以父没，无所严惮，荒于遨游，不事举业，大母母氏叱辱交加，不顾也。且以为在家终有管束，乃窃白镪若干两，携带衣物亡去，以为囊中有物，遨游不患无资。狐夫人以术取回之。复以为典当衣物，可支年许。狐夫人以术焚之。不出旬日，相如空乏甚，欲归家而心惭，欲投友而面赧，进退维谷，陡生拙念，而终不忍为。独步野外，腹馁难忍，缢遂决。解带系木，伸颈而缢。缢后，觉有人解释之，苏而目之，盖颁白老人也。

    Explanation

    46

    Xiangru, as he grew older, was intelligent and attractive, entering the school system at the age of 20. Soon after, Fan also passed away from illness. With his father's death, Xiangru had no fear or respect for authority, wandering aimlessly and neglecting his studies. His mother-in-law and mother scolded and humiliated him, but he paid no heed. He believed that being at home would still subject him to constraints, so he secretly took several taels of silver, along with his clothes, and left home, thinking that with money in his bag, he would not lack for resources during his travels. The fox lady used magic to bring him back. He then pawned his clothes, thinking it would last him a year or so. The fox lady used magic to burn them. Within ten days, Xiangru was in dire poverty, feeling ashamed to return home and embarrassed to seek help from friends, finding himself in a difficult situation with no clear way out. He suddenly had a foolish idea but ultimately could not bring himself to act on it. Walking alone in the fields, he was extremely hungry and decided to hang himself. He tied a belt around a tree, extended his neck, and hanged himself. Afterward, he felt someone untie the rope, and he woke up to see an elderly white-haired person.

    47

    移时起谢，问曰：“老先生尊姓？”曰：“仆姓史，字得仁，今八十馀岁。君正妙龄，何缘而出此？仆家违此不远，可至寒舍详叙。”既至，相如自道姓名，历言遭遇之艰。史曰：“咎不在继母，使君勤攻诵读，何有叱辱之加？”遂馔以酒食。已，史曰：“送君还旧府何如？不然，仆闲园中有草舍三楹，可以下帷读，君能甘其寂寞乎？”相如曰：“能，但虑膏火无出。”史曰：“此小事耳。”遂引相如自内庭曲曲达园入室。史曰：“此园原有便门，因无人看守，将门扃锁，出入必由内庭。”言已辞去。相如见园中虽无多花卉，而夭桃文杏，翠柏苍松，皆可玩赏。

    Explanation

    47

    After some time, the elderly man stood up to thank him and asked, "What is your respected surname?" The man replied, "My surname is Shi, and my courtesy name is Deren. I am currently over eighty years old." The man said, "You are in your prime, so why did you do this?" The elderly man said, "My home is not far from here. You can come to my humble abode to discuss further." After they arrived, Xiangru introduced his own name and narrated in detail the hardships he has faced. The elderly man said, "The blame does not lie with the stepmother. If you had diligently studied and read, there would have been no scolding or humiliation." Then, he served him wine and food. After that, the elderly man said, "How about sending you back to your old residence? Otherwise, in my idle garden, there is a thatched cottage with three rooms where you can study in seclusion. Can you endure the solitude?" Xiangru said, "I can, but I worry about the cost of oil for lighting." The elderly man said, "This is a trivial matter." He then led Xiangru through the inner courtyard to the garden and into the room. The elderly man said, "This garden originally had a side gate. Because no one was guarding it, the gate was locked. Entry and exit must be through the inner courtyard." After bidding farewell and leaving, Xiangru saw that although there were not many flowers in the garden, the young peach trees, the elegant apricot trees, the dark green cypress, and the towering cedars were all enjoyable to view and appreciate.

    48

    室内明窗净几，满架书籍。视之，凡学堂应用之书大概悉备。及午，酒食由内送出。及夕，酒食如故，并衣服衾帐色色送到。相如心不自安，兀坐草堂，毫无所事。欲出游而园门扃锁，又不便从内庭出，怅闷已极。不得已，复理举业，高声朗诵以破闷怀。及晚，酒菜倍他日，来人曰：“家主人即出。”既而史至，二人对饮，冯为主而史为宾也。史曰：“闻君诵读，可喜可钦。然每日读书，必按课作文，近今之可师事者其谁乎？”曰：“某进士其可。”言至此，史即辞去。至第三日早，史出诗文题各一以授相如。

    Explanation

    48

    In the study room, the windows were bright and the desk clean, with bookshelves filled with books. Upon inspection, it was evident that all the books required for the school were largely available. At noon, food and drink were brought out from inside. By evening, the food and drink remained as before, along with various items of clothing, bedding, and curtains. Xiangru felt uneasy and sat alone in the thatched cottage, with nothing to do. He wished to go out for a walk, but the garden gate was locked, and it was also inconvenient to leave through the inner courtyard, causing him great frustration. Unable to do anything else, he resumed his studies, reading aloud to relieve his boredom. By nightfall, the food and drink were more abundant than usual, and a servant said, "The master of the house is about to come out." Soon after, Shi arrived, and the two drank together, with Feng being the host and Shi the guest. Shi said, "Hearing you read is pleasing and admirable. However, every day when reading, one must follow the curriculum to write essays. Who among the recent scholars can be considered a teacher?" Xiangru replied, "A certain Jinshi (imperial scholar) might be suitable." Upon hearing this, Shi took his leave. On the third day early in the morning, Shi gave him one poetry and one prose topic to write.

    49

    相如曰：“此题出自谁手？”曰：“某进士也。”相如曰：“何以得此？”曰：“仆已代君投刺纳贽矣。可速作，日夕仆自走领转送某进士。看毕，仆仍送还。”言已，即告辞曰：“勿误功课。”及夕，史果待于园中。相如急为录清，卷交而史去，后遂习以为常。一日，诗、文题均难，深心构思，不觉睡去。及醒，日已过午。及夕，稿尚未脱，史待于园已多时。相如出辞，兼告以故。史曰：“诺。君速去作文，勿顾仆。”屡辞屡诺，而史仍弗去。相如遂急为草创，夜半录清，而史始去。相如于此心实有不忍焉。后早创速录，史至即交，无烦立俟。

    Explanation

    49

    Xiangru asked, "Who is the author of this question?" The other person replied, "I am a Jinshi." Xiangru asked, "Why did you choose this question?" The other person said, "I have already acted on your behalf by presenting a card and offering a gift." Xiangru said, "Please hurry up and write, and I will come and pick it up and deliver it to the Jinshi every day and evening." After reading, I will return it. Do not neglect your studies. By evening, the historian was indeed waiting in the garden. Xiangru hurriedly copied it out, handed over the scroll, and the historian left. Later, this became a regular practice. One day, both the poetry and prose questions were difficult, and he deeply pondered them, not realizing he fell asleep. When he woke up, it was already past noon. The draft was still incomplete, and the historian had been waiting in the garden for a long time. Xiangru excused himself and explained the reason. The historian replied, "Okay. Please hurry and write, don't worry about me." Despite repeated refusals and agreements, the historian still did not leave. Xiangru then hurriedly started writing, finished it by midnight, and the historian finally left. Xiangru felt truly reluctant about this. Later, he started early and wrote quickly, handing it over as soon as the historian arrived, without making him wait.

    50

    相如目不窥园，屈指三载。一日，史曰：“大比临迩，可为报名投卷计矣。”曰：“诺。”史曰：“去时勿启园门，可由内庭出入，亦无令人看守书室。”相如悉应之。然每出入辄见二八女郎侍立庭内，审视之，容华如仙，秀曼都雅，不觉神驰。入闱之前三日，史敬理杯茗为相如送场，曰：“进场后珍重墨卷，堤防火烛，构思勿偏僻，全场自有望。”谆谆切嘱，俨同道学，相如悉敬聆之。曰：“适有一事，万望明示。

    Explanation

    50

    Sima Xiangru never looked out of the garden for three years. One day, the historian said, "The great comparison is near; it is time to prepare for registration and submission of scrolls." Xiangru replied, "Yes." The historian then said, "When you go, do not open the garden gate; you may enter and exit through the inner courtyard, and do not let anyone guard the study." Xiangru agreed to all of this. However, every time he went in or out, he would see a girl of sixteen standing in the courtyard. Upon closer inspection, she was as beautiful as a fairy, graceful and elegant, and he could not help but be captivated. Three days before entering the examination hall, the historian respectfully prepared tea and wine to send Xiangru off, saying, "After entering the hall, take good care of your written work, be cautious with fire and candles, do not think in isolated or obscure ways, and there will be hope for the entire competition." He repeatedly and earnestly advised, as if he were a scholar of the Way, and Xiangru respectfully listened to him. He then said, "There is something I wish to ask; please explain clearly."

    51

    昨见内庭有及笄之女，果系恩公何人？”史曰：“渠皇甫氏之子，拙荆之外孙女也。因渠父母双亡，故就养于此。”相如曰：“未报高厚，复有烦劳，自觉不情。但相如自幼未婚，不知恩公肯为伐柯否？”史曰：“场后归商令堂，不嫌寒微，自无不妥，盖主张全在老夫。不日进场，精神不宜外驰，请辞。”三场既毕，龙虎高张，相如得中经魁。衣冠谒史，叩谢鸿恩。史曰：“此皆令堂之慈惠，仆何力之有？”相如闻之愕然。史曰：“仆非他，令堂之父也。初，仆自东郊之从君而西也，令堂在舍下已等候多时，盼望眼红矣！嗣后甥按课作文，令堂每课必来，卷交始去。

    Explanation

    51

    I saw in the inner court a young woman of marriageable age. Who exactly is this benefactor?” the historian asked. “He is the son of the Huángfǔ family, my wife’s grandson. Since his parents have both passed away, he was taken in here.” Xiangru said, “I have not yet repaid your great kindness, and now I am causing you further trouble, which I feel is inappropriate. But Xiangru has remained unmarried since childhood, and I wonder if you would agree to arrange a match for me?” The historian replied, “After the examination, you will return to your mother-in-law’s home. If you do not嫌弃 humble origins, there is nothing wrong with it. The decision is entirely up to me. Soon you will enter the examination hall, so your spirit should not be distracted outwardly. Please excuse me.” After the three examinations were completed, the scene was grand and impressive, and Xiangru passed as the top candidate. Dressed in proper attire, he visited the historian to thank him for his great favor. The historian said, “This is all due to your mother’s kindness and benevolence; what merit do I have?” Xiangru was startled by this. The historian continued, “I am not someone else; I am your father-in-law. At the beginning, I followed you from the eastern suburbs to the west. Your mother had been waiting at my residence for a long time, her eyes turned red from anticipation! Afterward, your nephew wrote according to the curriculum, and your mother came for every lesson, leaving only after the exam was submitted.

    52

    夙昔甥完卷之甚迟也，甥徒知仆久候于园，不知令堂哭坏于舍下，以为甥半途而废，不可为也已。及夜半见甥文卷，仆告以故，始反悲为喜。不但此也，每逢一课，令堂必索某进士之阅卷观之，看得好则喜形于色，不好则泪含于目。其喜与泣之心怀，不知如何交迫。为甥故，令堂已形消骨立。昨于未张榜之先，令堂来此听信，闻甥中，即驰归报喜于令祖母矣。”相如闻之，抱头大哭，恨闻知之晚。即刻命驾急归，至家，伏地请罪。母命起，相如起立于侧，见母涕泣不已，劝之曰：“儿今已贵，母宜喜，勿过伤也。

    Explanation

    52

    For a long time, my nephew took a very long time to finish his examination. He only knew that I had been waiting for him in the garden, but he did not know that your mother had cried herself sick at home, thinking that he had given up halfway and was no longer trustworthy. When I showed him his completed exam in the middle of the night and explained the reason, she finally changed from sadness to joy. Not only that, but every time there was an exam, your mother would always seek out past exam papers of successful candidates to review. If she found them good, she would show her joy on her face; if not, tears would well up in her eyes. Her heart was torn between joy and sorrow, and it was hard to say how she felt. Because of my nephew, your mother has become extremely thin and weak. Yesterday, before the results were posted, your mother came here to hear the news. Upon learning that my nephew had passed, she immediately rushed home to report the good news to your grandmother. When Xiangru heard this, he cried with his head in his hands, regretting that he had learned about it too late. He immediately ordered his carriage and hurried home. Upon arriving, he fell to the ground and asked for forgiveness. His mother told him to stand up, and Xiangru stood by her side. Seeing his mother weeping continuously, he comforted her, saying, "Now that I have achieved success, you should be happy, and do not grieve too much."

    53

    ”夫人曰：“儿今虽贵，母心操碎矣！当汝窃镪而逃也，吾以术取回之，致汝手无分文，艰苦备尝。深夜自泣，泪浮枕簟，无人知耳。一知汝生拙念，仓皇无措，恐少迟戕汝性命，急烦汝外祖父速速拯救。见汝同外祖父偕归，吾心犹忐忑也。今汝已中，吾无挂心事矣，———然犹未也，尚未与汝完婚。”相如闻母言及此，曰：“儿正有一事禀白，外祖母之孙女皇甫氏，及笄未字，儿尝见之，不知可结婚姻否？”夫人曰：“吾有此心久矣，但有异类之嫌，不肯媒定。儿既欲之，可。”以故相如父子皆得狐夫人云。

    Explanation

    53

    The lady said, "My son is now honored, but my heart has been worn out! When you stole money and fled, I used my method to retrieve it, leaving you with no money and experiencing all kinds of hardships. I cried alone at night, tears floating on the pillow and mat, and no one knew. When I learned you had a foolish thought, I was in a state of panic and confusion, fearing that a slight delay would harm your life. I urgently asked your grandfather to save you quickly. Seeing you return with your grandfather, my heart was still uneasy. Now that you have succeeded, I have no worries, but still not yet, we haven't completed your marriage." Xiangru, upon hearing his mother's words, said, "I have something to report. The granddaughter of your grandmother, Huangfu, has come of age but is not yet married. I have seen her, and I wonder if we can marry?" The lady replied, "I have had this thought for a long time, but there is a fear of being considered different, and I was reluctant to arrange the match. If you desire it, then it is acceptable." Therefore, both Xiangru and his father obtained the fox lady.

    54

    虚白道人曰：余于价人，不羡其得艳妻，而羡其获内助。余于相如，不羡其得举于乡，而羡其母氏圣善。当相如之窃镪而逃，术取之以逼其向诵读之途，何其智也；当相如之陡生拙意，急救之以保其无性命之忧，何其仁也；及相如悔心转意，烦生父以诱掖之，阅文卷以期望之，使浪荡之子成功名中人，何其慈而义也！使天下之母氏尽如狐夫人之云为，何有嫡继生养之分？

    Explanation

    54

    The Daoist Master Xu Bai said: "Regarding the esteemed person, I do not envy him for having a beautiful wife, but I envy him for gaining a supportive partner. Regarding Sima Xiangru, I do not envy him for passing the local examination, but I envy his mother's virtue and wisdom. When Sima Xiangru stole money and fled, she devised a plan to force him onto the path of reading and studying—what wisdom! When he suddenly had a foolish idea, she rescued him to ensure his safety—what kindness! When he repented and changed his mind, she involved his father to guide and encourage him, read through his writings with hope, and made a wayward son into a successful and virtuous person—what compassion and righteousness! If all mothers in the world were like Fox Lady's actions, what distinction would there be between legitimate and illegitimate children?"

    55

    读此文而不落泪者，其人必不慈。 马竹吾

    　　有如此贤继母，可为黑心符加棒喝。可以人而不如狐乎？上元李瑜谨注

    　　益智录卷之二

    　　梅仙

    　　泰郡汤武，字乃文，谈者忘其邑里。读书别墅，斋临旷野。墙外有义地，坟墓无数，夜多青磷，时闻鬼哭。武豪放，悉置度外。一日，夜起乘凉，闻墙外有哭声，哀楚似女子，遂隔墙语之曰：“有何愁苦，如此其悲也？如可语人，可至敝斋明言。仆若能分忧，必竭力以妥幽魂。”言已，哭声亦止。武归斋，既而一丽人搴帘入，年已及笄，娟丽无双。知为夜哭之鬼，与之坐而问焉。女曰：“妾乃刘通判之女，父休官之后，妾适卒，因暂厝于墙东，今已七载。前后左右皆恶少，朽骨日久木坏，必致淆杂，是以悲耳。

    Explanation

    55

    Translation:

    Whoever reads this text and does not shed tears must lack compassion. Ma Zhuyu

    There is such a virtuous stepmother; she can be rebuked with a rod and shout. Can humans be less noble than foxes? Li Yu of Shangyuan respectfully annotates

    Yizhi Lu Volume Two

    Meixian

    Tang Wu from Taixian, courtesy name Naiwen, lived in a villa and his study faced an open field. Outside the wall was a cemetery with countless graves, often seen with phosphorescent lights at night, and sometimes heard with ghostly cries. Wu was bold and placed these matters outside his concern. One night, he got up to cool off and heard a woman crying outside the wall, her voice sorrowful like that of a girl. He spoke through the wall, saying, "What troubles do you have that make you so sad? If you can tell me, come to my study and speak openly. If I can help, I will do my best to comfort your spirit." After he spoke, the crying stopped. Wu returned to his study, and soon a beautiful woman entered, pulling back the curtain. She was a young woman, graceful and beautiful beyond compare. Knowing she was the ghost who had been crying, he sat down with her and asked about her. She said, "I am the daughter of Judge Liu. After my father retired from office, I died and was temporarily buried on the east side of the wall. Now it has been seven years. The people around here are all young ruffians. After a long time, the wooden coffins will rot, leading to the mixing of bones, which is why I am so sad."

    56

    ”武曰：“移厝异地，亦易事也。”女曰：“妾父母久归故里，此处又别无戚属，安有惠及泉壤者？”言已，潸然泣下。武曰：“仆欲移之，但不识其处。”女曰：“绿杨西有小石碣，上书‘刘通判爱女之墓’，棺木尚存，固易识。”武遂自任曰：“明晨决移，勿涕泣。”女闻之，反悲为喜。武欲与欢好，女曰：“妾不忍祸君子，夜台朽骨，不同人生，恐促寿命。”武乃止。将寝，女始去。明日，武果将女榇移厝高原。及晚，女来伸谢，敛衽端肃，不胜感激。于是武读而女伺之，渴为烹茶，饥为具馔。武甚德之，亦不究其物之所自来。

    Explanation

    56

    Wu said, "Relocating to a different place is also an easy matter." The woman replied, "My parents have long returned to their hometown, and there are no other relatives here. How could there be anyone who would benefit the dead?" She finished speaking and tears welled up in her eyes. Wu said, "I want to move it, but I don't know where." The woman said, "West of the green willows there is a small stone tablet, inscribed with 'Tomb of the beloved daughter of Liu Tongpan.' The coffin still exists, so it is easy to identify." Wu then volunteered to say, "I will decide to move it tomorrow morning, and do not cry." Upon hearing this, the woman turned from sadness to joy. Wu wanted to enjoy her company, but the woman said, "I cannot bear to bring misfortune to the gentleman. The bones in the night hall are different from living people, and I fear of shortening one's lifespan." Wu then stopped. As they were about to sleep, the woman left. The next day, Wu indeed moved the woman's coffin to a high place. By evening, the woman came to express her gratitude, with her sleeves gathered and demeanor respectful, she was overwhelmed with gratitude. While Wu was reading, the woman attended to him, brewing tea when he was thirsty and preparing food when he was hungry. Wu highly valued her, and did not investigate where the things came from.

    57

    一夕武欲归，女曰：“不可。”武曰：“仆之家室，何不可？”女不言，固问之。曰：“闻妾言而君怒，妾不言；言之而君不听，妾仍不言。”武曰：“悉惟命是听。”女曰其事如此如此，可如此如此以处之。武闻言大怒，操刀欲往。女夺其刀而掷之，曰：“妾言何如耶？闻之若是怒，见之则怒更甚，君诚不可与共事矣！”武谢过。女曰：“俟气平，妾与偕往。”移时，女曰：“可以行矣，妾在暗中相助。”及大门，门自辟；至寝户，户自开。灯明于室，妻郑氏正与人欢寝。二人见武，急欲起遁，如有人按抚，不得起。武睹其情形，知为女暗助，遂将二人赤身缚之。岳家固不远，遂托妻暴病，将郑翁诳至。郑见女与奸夫赤身缚于床，遂谓武曰：“生杀惟君，何需吾见？”武曰：“杀之污吾刀。”释二人缚，即遣氏从郑大归。后郑醮氏他姓，奸夫仍与往来，悉为后夫所杀。

    Explanation

    57

    One night, Wu wanted to return home, and the woman said, "It is not possible." Wu replied, "What is wrong with my family?" The woman remained silent, and Wu persisted in asking. She said, "If I speak and you become angry, I will not speak; if I speak and you do not listen, I will still not speak." Wu said, "I will fully follow your command." The woman explained the situation in detail, saying, "Handle it like this, like this." Wu heard this and became very angry, holding a knife and wanting to go. The woman snatched the knife and threw it, saying, "How is my advice? If you are so angry upon hearing it, you will be even more angry upon seeing it. You truly cannot work with me!" Wu apologized. The woman said, "Wait until your anger subsides, and I will go with you." After some time, the woman said, "We can go now, and I will assist you in secret." Upon reaching the main gate, the door opened by itself; upon reaching the bedroom door, it opened by itself. The lamp lit the room, and Wu's wife Zheng was having an affair with someone. The two saw Wu and tried to escape, but as if someone was holding them down, they could not get up. Wu saw the situation and realized that the woman had secretly helped him, so he tied the two naked. Since the father-in-law's house was not far, Wu pretended that his wife was seriously ill and deceived Zheng to come. Zheng saw the woman and her lover tied naked on the bed and told Wu, "Life and death are at your discretion; why do I need to see?" Wu said, "Killing them would soil my knife." He released their bindings and sent the woman back to her family. Later, the woman married another man, and the lover continued to see her, but they were all killed by the new husband.

    58

    武出妻后，门户失守，乃移读于家，女伺之如故。武以新鳏，复欲犯之。女曰：“妾诚不敢以祸君子者报君子，今将为君谋一佳人，聊用自代。”武曰：“谁何？”女曰：“某山悬崖间有梅一株，君曾见否？”曰：“见之。其梅生于立崖之半，去地约三丈，冰姿玉骨，无人攀折，故暗香浮动，辄闻数里。前同友人临赏，尝赠之以诗曰：‘芳梅何故惹诗人，瘦骨清魂占早春。和靖已遥今有我，相逢莫谓两无因。’”女曰：“谐矣，诗中已有因缘矣！是梅业已成仙，然可图也。梅仙恶爆竹如畏鈇鑕。每当岁除，各庄爆竹连续，梅仙闻声，仓皇无措，或匿石缝，或伏土坑，越日乃敢出。君以新洁酒器一具置于梅下，周围拥之以土而留其口，三更后，用千头火炮去梅百步放之。妾观其动静，三夜后再为之计。”武悉如女言。至第四日晚，女忽至，曰：“可矣。渠伏于器已两夜，每至晓方出。

    Explanation

    58

    After Wu divorced his wife, the family's honor was compromised, and he moved to study at home. The woman continued to watch over him as before. Being newly widowed, Wu again wanted to offend her. The woman said, "I truly dare not repay a gentleman with harm; now I will find a good woman for you, to take my place." Wu asked, "Who?" The woman replied, "There is a plum tree on a cliff in a certain mountain. Have you seen it?" Wu said, "Yes, I have seen it. The plum tree grows halfway up the cliff, about three zhang from the ground. It has an ice-like posture and jade-like bones, and no one has picked or broken it. Therefore, its faint fragrance floats in the air and can be smelled for several miles. Previously, while visiting with friends, I gave it a poem: 'Why does the fragrant plum blossom attract poets? Its slender bones and clear spirit claim the early spring. Now, there is me, as distant as the poet He Jing. When we meet, do not say there is no reason.'" The woman said, "It's fitting, there is already a connection in the poem! This plum tree has already become a spirit, but it can be captured. The plum blossom spirit hates firecrackers as much as it fears axes. On New Year's Eve, when firecrackers are set off in each village, the plum blossom spirit hears the sound, panics and at a loss, and hides in rock crevices or buries itself in earth pits, only daring to come out the next day. You should place a clean wine vessel under the plum tree, surround it with soil while leaving an opening, and set off a thousand firecrackers a hundred steps away from the plum tree at midnight. I will observe its movements, and after three nights, we will plan again." Wu followed the woman's instructions. On the fourth evening, the woman suddenly arrived and said, "It can be done. It has been hiding in the vessel for two nights, and only comes out at dawn."

    59

    今夜施为，仍如前宵，火炮及半，再燃以续之，竿挑急赴梅下。数步外，将火炮掷地，用猪脬胞蒙固酒具之口，抱归置几上，焚香拜祝，渠自出。然可求不可强也。”武复如女言，抱器归，置几礼拜。多时，觉身后有人抚其肩曰：“吓死妾矣！”武回首视之，仙姿之娟，迥异凡丽，或月里嫦娥可与为伍。拥之于怀，亦不甚拒。梅曰：“勿尔。请问君置妾于室，为酒棋乎？床第乎？”武曰：“酒则量狭，棋非素好也。

    Explanation

    59

    Tonight's actions will be the same as last night. When the firecrackers are half gone, light more to continue. Quickly pick up the pole and rush to the plum tree. A few steps away, throw the firecrackers on the ground. Use a pig bladder to cover the mouth of the wine vessel. Carry it back, place it on the table, burn incense, and make a wish. She will come out on her own. However, it can be sought but not forced." Wu followed the girl's instructions, carried the vessel back, placed it on the table, and performed the ritual. After some time, he felt someone patting his shoulder and said, "You scared me to death!" Wu turned around to see a woman with a beautiful, ethereal appearance, unlike any ordinary beauty, perhaps comparable to the moon goddess Chang'e. She embraced him, and he did not resist much. The woman said, "Don't do that. Please tell me, why did you place me in your room, for wine and chess or for intimacy?" Wu replied, "As for wine, my capacity is limited; as for chess, I am not particularly fond of it."

    60

    ”及寝，遍体芳馥，偎爱之际，不啻博山炉火，一气凌紫霞矣。乃以腕代枕而问曰：“识妾之由，构妾之术，果谁之教乎？”武曰：“仆自识卿，独出心裁，何待人言？”梅不信，固问之，遂以女对。梅曰：“此鬼颇义，勿相负。”武兴未足，复求欢好。梅曰：“欢尽此夜耶？贪欢无厌，大损人寿，忠告不可，妾自去，不复来矣！”武乃罢。晓起，操作家务若素谙。邻里妇女来观如蚁，旬日不断。梅颇厌之，谓武曰：“妾请暂别，五日自至。”遂去不归，武无计可施。

    Explanation

    60

    Upon lying down, the entire body was fragrant. During the embrace, it was no different from the fire of the Bosshan incense burner, rising in one breath to the purple clouds. Using her wrist as a pillow, she asked, "Who taught me the way to recognize and cultivate you?" B said, "I recognized you on my own, with unique ingenuity, why should I wait for others to tell me?" Mei did not believe him and pressed him further, so he revealed the truth. Mei said, "This ghost is quite righteous, do not betray her." B, still desiring more, sought further intimacy. Mei said, "Is this all the pleasure for this night? Greed for pleasure is endless, it greatly damages one's life. If you do not accept my sincere advice, I will leave and never return." B then ceased. In the morning, Mei rose and handled household chores as if she had always been there. Neighbors and women came to see her like ants, and this continued for ten days. Mei found this tiresome and said to B, "I will temporarily leave; I will return in five days." She then departed and did not return, leaving B with no recourse.

    61

    一夕女至，武告以梅不归，且求计于女。女曰：“欲令归亦易，使人用火炮远远放之，渠惧必至。”武如女言，梅果至。曰：“此又是小鬼头助纣为虐，妾必有以报之。”言已，女至，梅深怼女。女曰：“妾系异物，不可近人，故烦仙人相代耳。”梅曰：“小鬼头非乃文之妻，何谓相代？且他事皆可代，天下有代人作妇者乎？然亦不能常代也。”遂谓武曰：“君生平曾见丽人否？”武曰：“见之。某庄富室万翁之女，娟丽无双。”梅谓女曰：“有一事相商。

    Explanation

    61

    One night, the woman arrived, and Wu informed her that Mei had not returned, and also asked for advice from her. The woman said, "If you want her to return, it is easy. Have someone use a cannon and fire it far away; he will be afraid and surely come." Wu followed the woman's advice, and Mei indeed returned. She said, "This is another little demon helping the tyrant; I will certainly repay him." After saying this, the woman arrived, and Mei deeply resented her. The woman said, "I am a strange being, unable to approach people, so I trouble the immortal to substitute for me." Mei said, "The little demon is not your wife; what do you mean by substitution? Moreover, everything else can be substituted, but is there anyone in the world who can substitute as a wife? However, it cannot be done constantly." She then said to Wu, "Have you ever seen a beautiful woman in your life?" Wu replied, "Yes. The daughter of a wealthy man named Wan from a certain village, she is uniquely beautiful." Mei said to the woman, "There is one matter to discuss."

    62

    ”俱出不归。次夕，梅至。问：“女何不来？”梅曰：“不日自至。”盖富室万某有女若兰，丽而贤，尚未字人。一夕家人团坐共话，若兰忽仆地卒，多时始苏，谓万家人曰：“尔等何人？胡为薄观不去？”家人曰：“汝病痴乎？”曰：“不痴。余女鬼刘氏，与汤乃文有婚姻之约，自恨异物，常怀惭愧。”闻者辄掩口而笑。自顾衣履，始知借躯而生，遂谓万曰：“汤乃文弦断未续，可讽以意，使通媒妁。儿非乃文不嫁也。”万素知武家，以门户不对，置之。若兰由是不言，亦不食。

    Explanation

    62

    Both went out and did not return. The next evening, Mei arrived. She asked, "Why didn't you come?" Mei replied, "She will come soon." It turned out that Mr. Wan from a wealthy family had a daughter named Ruolan, who was beautiful and virtuous but had not yet been betrothed. One night, the family sat together talking, and Ruolan suddenly fell to the ground and died. After a long time, she regained consciousness and asked the Wan family, "Who are you? Why did you not leave?" The family said, "Are you sick or crazy?" Ruolan replied, "I am not crazy. I am the female ghost Liu, who has a marriage agreement with Tang Naiwen. I regret being an otherworldly being and often feel ashamed." Those who heard laughed and covered their mouths. Looking at her clothes and shoes, she realized she had borrowed a body to live. She then said to Mr. Wan, "Tang Naiwen's string has been broken and not yet mended. You can advise indirectly and arrange a matchmaker. I will not marry anyone but Naiwen." Mr. Wan had always known the Wu family but considered the social status mismatch and set it aside. Ruolan then stopped speaking and eating.

    63

    万大惧，因烦交好者示意于武。武与梅商之，梅曰：“可，若兰非他，即君爱鬼刘氏。”武闻之愕然。梅曰：“前夕妾与刘氏之偕出也，妾将若兰之魂引置他处，使刘氏借躯而生。不然，君与万贫富不敌，何克结秦晋也？”武遂媒定之。合卺之夕，视若兰较昔尤艳绝也，然言皆刘氏之言，谈及梅仙之事备极详细。梅数夕不至。若兰归宁，梅夕至，武让之。梅曰：“燕尔新婚，妾在此，焉置之也？”于是绸缪数夕，若兰将归，梅亦辞去。

    Explanation

    63

    万 greatly feared, and through his intermediaries, he hinted to Wu. Wu discussed the matter with Mei, who said, "Yes, Lan is not someone else; she is the ghost of the lady you love, Liu." Wu was startled upon hearing this. Mei explained, "Last night, I went out with Liu, and I led Lan's soul to another place, allowing Liu to borrow her body and live. Otherwise, you and Wan cannot compete in wealth and poverty, how could you possibly marry into a wealthy family?" Wu then agreed to the marriage. On the wedding night, Wu saw that Lan was even more beautiful than ever before, but all the words she spoke were those of Liu, and when they talked about the affairs of the fairy Mei, they were extremely detailed. Mei did not come for several nights. When Lan returned to her parents' home, Mei came at night, and Wu rebuked her. Mei said, "In your happy new marriage, I am here, where would I put myself?" Thus, they spent several intimate nights together. When Lan was about to return home, Mei also left.

    64

    一夕，若兰忽曰：“君何人？斯此谁氏之室？吾胡为乎在此？”武笑曰：“卿颠乎？吾卿之夫也。某日过门，迄今已二旬矣。”若兰默然不语，武亦疑之。后梅至，武告其情而问之。梅曰：“妾为之易其魂耳。不易之以万，无以笃夫妇之情；不易之以刘，无以答爱鬼之义。然君与万，夫妇也。妾与君情同湛露，见阳自晞。行将度刘氏为鬼仙，妾亦从此不来矣。”武哀曰：“此后话耳，今兹未能。”武于是闻妻言似刘氏，则知为爱鬼符体；闻妻言似万氏，则以为艳妻对处。是武得一妻而二美俱矣。十年后，梅来渐稀，后竟不至。武但与若兰同居白头云。

    　　虚白道人曰：得花仙为妻，容或有之，究属罕闻；一佳人而有二魂，妻之如对二艳妻，更属创闻。武竟以移厝女榇一事而兼得之。以是知东坡之赠李荐，尧夫之赠曼卿，亦西伯泽枯骸、昌黎施旅榇之盛德也乎！

    Explanation

    64

    One night, 若兰 suddenly asked, "Who are you? Whose house is this? Why am I here?" 武 laughed and said, "Are you crazy? I am your husband. I passed by the door on a certain day, and it has been two weeks since then." 若兰 remained silent and speechless, and 武 also felt puzzled. Later, 梅 arrived, and 武 recounted the situation to her and asked her about it. 梅 said, "I have transformed her soul. Without transforming it to become 万, there would be no way to deepen the bond between husband and wife; without transforming it to become 刘, there would be no way to repay the love of a ghost. However, you and 万 are husband and wife. My feelings for you are as pure as morning dew, but they will naturally dry up when the sun rises. I will soon transform 刘 into a ghost fairy, and I will no longer come here." 武 sighed, "This is a matter for the future; I cannot do it now." 武 then realized that when his wife spoke like 刘, she was referring to the love ghost's form; when she spoke like 万, she was treating him as if he had two beautiful wives. Thus, 武 had one wife but two beauties. Ten years later, 梅's visits became gradually less frequent, and eventually, she stopped coming altogether. 武 only lived with 若兰 until old age.

    The Pure White Taoist said: To have a flower fairy as a wife, it might happen, but it is still rare; to have one beautiful woman with two souls, treating her as if she were two beautiful wives, is even more unheard of. 武 ultimately achieved this through the matter of moving the coffin of a woman. Thus, we can understand that the gifts given by Su Dongpo to Li Jian and by Ouyang Xiu to Man Qing were also acts of great virtue, like King Wen of Zhou reviving the dead and Han Yu providing a coffin for a traveler.

    65

    通幅秀丽。 汪雪马风印仲洵

    　　较柳州《龙城录》载“翠羽”条尤新艳。 马竹吾

    　　和靖以梅为妻，喻言耳，不谓汤生真有其事。文亦清新俊逸，足为寄春君生色。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　巨蝎

    　　栖霞东鄙卫道彰之妻崔氏，村妇之正气人也。家綦贫，而夫外出，仰十指为生，饔飧恒不给，每同及笄夫妹赴坡捋菜。看坡人某美其妹，故于地内设谷穗一堆，伺其拾取，逼而淫之。崔与妹行至谷所，意谓窃者畏人而弃，欲拾之，恐人疑已为偷。其妹曰：“置谷筐底，上覆以菜，人莫能见。”崔从之。甫欲行，而某已至，见之佯怒，谓崔曰：“真赃在此，合将汝嫂妹痛打，仍交地主，听其处置。倘肯使汝妹与吾欢好，则听汝携谷去。”崔不应，某乃以青梁秸极力向殴。崔畏其强横，复四顾无人，不得已允之。某大喜，抱女于怀，急欲为欢，而厌土地湿污，曰：“彼松林中有蓑衣一具，可铺而卧。”遂欲抱女往。

    Explanation

    65

    The entire text is beautifully crafted. Wang Xuemaofeng Yin Zhongxun

    It surpasses the entry on "Green Feathers" in Liu Zongyuan's Dragon City Record in its novelty and vividness. Ma Zhugu

    He Jing took a plum blossom as his wife, which is a metaphorical expression, but it is claimed that the story is true. The writing is fresh, elegant, and spirited, adding luster to the subject. Shangyuan Li Yu respectfully annotated

    Giant Scorpion

    Cui, the wife of Wei Daozhang from the eastern outskirts of Qixia, is a virtuous and upright woman. Her family is extremely poor, and her husband is away. They rely on manual labor for survival, and their basic needs are often not met. She often goes to the fields with her sister-in-law to gather vegetables. A man admires her sister-in-law and sets up a trap to force her into a sexual encounter. Cui and her sister-in-law suspect that someone might have hidden grain in the field and fear being accused of theft. Her sister-in-law suggests hiding the grain in a basket and covering it with vegetables to avoid detection. Cui agrees. Just as they are about to leave, the man arrives, feigns anger, and tells Cui, "The real evidence is here. You and your sister-in-law should be beaten severely and handed over to the landowner for his disposal. If you allow your sister-in-law to have a good time with me, then you can take the grain." Cui refuses, and the man violently beats her. Fearing his strength and seeing no witnesses, Cui reluctantly agrees. The man is delighted, takes the woman into his arms, and eagerly wants to proceed. However, he dislikes the wet and dirty ground and says, "There is a raincoat in the pine forest. We can lay it down and rest there." He then wants to take the woman there.

    66

    女曰：“勿尔，汝先去，吾随后即到。”某乃释女先去。崔促其妹往而遥望之。甫及林而遽返。问之，女曰：“某卧地呼痛，似不能起，可速逃。”遂弃菜谷归。旋闻某已死。其父趋视之，见其子遍体青紫，询于看坡曹侣，知其子甫与贾某饮酒归，遂以毒害喊禀之。官验后，将贾某传至问之，贾曰：“共饮属实，实无毒害之事。”官见贾冠年文弱，似非能毒人者。问其何为，答以读书。问其家有何人，曰：“惟老母在堂。”官将其母传至问之，曰：“身与某有瓜葛亲，身子懦弱，屡被某吓诈，凡某至身家，敬之不遑，何敢加害？”官谓贾曰：“其实毒死，汝与某共饮又属实，必汝不堪其扰而毒之。”贾口难分诉，遂诬服。

    Explanation

    66
    The woman said, "Do not do that. You go first, and I will follow shortly." The man then let the woman go ahead. Cui hurriedly sent his sister to follow and watch from a distance. Just as she reached the woods, she suddenly returned. When asked, the woman said, "A certain one was lying on the ground crying out in pain, seemingly unable to get up. You should quickly escape." She then abandoned the vegetables and returned home. Soon after, it was heard that the man had died. His father rushed to see him and found his son's body covered in bruises. He questioned the people present at the scene and learned that his son had just returned from drinking with a merchant named Jia. The officials conducted an investigation and summoned Jia to question him. Jia said, "It is true that we drank together, but there was no act of poisoning." The judge, seeing Jia's youthful and frail appearance, thought he seemed incapable of poisoning someone. When asked what he did, Jia replied that he was studying. When asked about his family, he said, "Only my mother is at home." The judge then summoned Jia's mother to question her. She said, "I have a close relationship with the deceased. My son is weak and timid, and he was often bullied by the deceased. Whenever the deceased came to our home, my son was too frightened to stand up, how dare he harm him?" The judge told Jia, "In reality, he was poisoned to death. You and the deceased drank together, which is true. It must be that you were unable to bear his harassment and poisoned him." Jia, unable to defend himself, was forced to falsely confess.
    67

    一日，崔氏与其妹在家口角。妹出辞不逊，崔怒曰：“曩者松林之事，幸看坡人即死，不然汝节已失，无颜见人，早自尽多日。”邻媪闻其言。媪与贾属至戚，遂走告贾母，贾母诉于官。官将崔氏传至问之，崔将诬赃逼淫及某死之情，历历言之。官怒曰：“某诚恶棍，死已后矣！”官复曰：“某逼淫之际有酒意乎？”崔曰：“有。”“有病意乎？”崔曰：“无。”官曰：“某先自赴林，汝妹隔几时去乎？”崔曰：“畏其强暴，刻即往，无多时。

    Explanation

    67

    One day, Cui and her sister had a quarrel at home. Her sister spoke disrespectfully, and Cui became angry and said, "In the past, during the incident in the pine forest, fortunately, the person who was watching the slope died immediately, otherwise your chastity would have been lost, and you would have been ashamed to face people, having committed suicide long ago." A neighborly old woman heard her words. The old woman was closely related to Mrs. Jia and ran to inform her. Mrs. Jia reported the matter to the official. The official summoned Cui to interrogate her. Cui recounted the fabricated charges of theft and forced indecency and the circumstances of his death in detail. The official was angry and said, "He was indeed a scoundrel, and he is already dead!" The official then asked, "Did he have the intention to drink during the act of forced indecency?" Cui replied, "Yes." "Did he have the intention to be ill?" Cui replied, "No." The official asked, "Did he go to the forest first, and how long after did your sister go?" Cui replied, "Fearing his strength and violence, she went immediately, not much time later."

    68

    ”官曰：“某非贾某毒死矣。岂有身已中毒，毫不暴躁而即死者乎？”官乃复诣松林，细验情形。见林中有巨穴，深不见底，穴中有物出入之迹。官令人以水灌之，内出巨蝎如琵琶。官谓某父曰：“汝子死于是物。汝子作恶，理合横死，而犹诬人求抵耶？”遂叱去之。归署，立破械出贾某，曰：“汝之得生，全赖崔氏。而氏之夫妹未字于人，汝可娶焉。”贾不语。官曰：“女虽累词讼，而未出头至公堂；纵遭颠险，犹然无瑕之白璧也。本县为媒，娶之不辱。”贾乃允从。官厚赠崔氏，为嫁妹资。

    Explanation

    68

    The official said, "The person was not poisoned by Jia. How could someone die immediately without showing signs of distress after being poisoned?" The official then returned to the pine forest to carefully examine the situation. He saw a large hole in the forest, deep and bottomless, with signs of something entering and exiting. The official ordered water to be poured into the hole, and a large scorpion, shaped like a lute, emerged. The official told the father of the deceased, "Your son died from this creature. Your son had done evil, and it was fitting that he died a violent death, yet you still falsely accused others to seek retribution?" He then angrily dismissed the man. Returning to his office, the official immediately released Jia from restraints and said, "Your survival is entirely due to Cui. And Cui's sister is still unmarried; you may marry her." Jia remained silent. The official said, "Although the girl has been involved in many lawsuits, she has not appeared in court; despite her misfortunes, she is still pure like a flawless jade. This county magistrate will act as a matchmaker; marrying her would not bring shame." Jia finally agreed. The official gave Cui a generous gift for the purpose of marrying her sister.

    69

    虚白道人曰：此祸淫之一事也。然林中果有若是巨蝎，不知伤几何人？而独伤于某，则知蝎也者，必神为之也。

    　　福善祸淫，理之常也。而人多不悟，何哉？ 汪雪马风

    　　蝎，毒物也。而能除淫凶，保名节，谓神为之，信也。 马竹吾

    　　明人郎瑛《续巳篇》中“蝎魔”一则，奇幻极矣，此则尤以理胜。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　上 官 勇

    　　上官德，陕西华阴人。娶任氏，生二子：长曰知，次曰仁。任卒，继娶马氏，生子勇。知性强悍，好报不平事，德屡戒终不听。知偶出游，见素相识之二人共殴一人，殴已复殴。知曰：“殴死人须偿命也！”二人怒曰：“君与此人相善乎？如相善，不妨相助！”知怒曰：“我以好言相劝，汝以恶言相伤。我即助之，其如我何？”二人亦怒，共赴知。被殴人亦起，各敌一人。知手重，殴及致命，其人仆地卒。知惧，即刻逃亡。多时，死者复苏，而逃亡之知不知也。德遣人四方踪迹之，迄无耗。

    Explanation

    69

    The Recluse of Pure White said: This is an instance of divine retribution for immoral acts. Yet, there is indeed such a giant scorpion in the forest, who does not know how many people it has injured? But it only injured a certain person, thus knowing that the scorpion is divinely ordained.

    Blessing the good and punishing the evil is a constant principle. Yet, people often fail to understand this, why is that? —— Wang Xue Ma Feng

    The scorpion is a venomous creature. Yet, it can eliminate immoral violence and preserve one's reputation, which is said to be divinely ordained, indeed. —— Ma Zhu Wu

    In the "Scorpion Demon" section of Ming scholar Lang Ying's Xu Ji Pian, it is extremely fantastical, but this story is especially strong in reason. —— Shangyuan Li Yu, careful annotation

    Shangguan Yong

    Shangguan De was from Huayin, Shaanxi. He married Ren Shi and had two sons: the elder was called Zhi, and the younger was called Ren. After Ren Shi died, he remarried Ma Shi and had a son named Yong. Zhi was strong-willed and brave, often taking up grievances for others. Despite repeated warnings from his father, he would not listen. One day, Zhi went out and saw two acquaintances beating a man, and after they stopped, they started again. Zhi said, "If you kill someone, you must pay with your life!" The two men angrily asked, "Do you know this person well? If so, why don't you help us?" Zhi replied, "I tried to reason with you, but you responded with harsh words. If I help him, what can you do to me?" The two men were also angry and attacked Zhi. The man being beaten also stood up and fought one of them. Zhi's blows were heavy, and he struck the fatal blow, causing the man to fall dead. Frightened, Zhi immediately fled. After some time, the dead man revived, but Zhi, who had fled, did not know this. Shangguan De sent people to search for him in all directions, but there was no news of him.

    70

    未几，德以病故。马氏陡生忌心，使仁经理家务，不令同幼子读，渐至役若佣仆，而食尤次之。勇年方十四，见兄饭疏食，于心不安，每食，求与兄偕。马不可，勇遂不食；马不得已听之。兄弟异馔，仁悉不在意。每食，勇必与兄易之，仁不可，勇乃先食仁所食，仁不得已亦食勇之食。马见之，忌心益甚。闲园有眢井，马托遗物于井，使仁入井寻之。仁乃以绳自系而下。既下，马断其绳而去。勇自塾归，不见兄，问之。

    Explanation

    70

    Not long after, De died of illness. Ma, suddenly filled with jealousy, ordered Ren to manage household affairs, forbidding him from studying with the young child, and gradually treated him like a servant, with his food being especially inferior. At the age of fourteen, Yong saw his elder brother eating coarse food and felt uneasy in his heart. Every time he ate, he asked to eat with his brother. Ma refused, so Yong refused to eat; Ma had no choice but to allow it. The brothers had different dishes, but Ren paid no heed. Every time they ate, Yong would always exchange his food with his brother, but Ren would not allow it. Yong then ate what Ren had eaten, and Ren had no choice but to eat Yong's food. Ma saw this and her jealousy grew even more. There was a dry well in the garden, and Ma placed some supposed lost items in the well, ordering Ren to go into the well to find them. Ren tied himself with a rope and descended. After descending, Ma cut the rope and left. Yong returned from school and did not see his brother, so he asked about it.

    71

    马曰：“他出未回。”勇不信，前后寻觅，至闲园，闻井有人声；听之，乃兄呼己名而求救也。勇曰：“兄且少耐。”乃觅长绳，一首系井旁之树，一首入井，令兄执之而上。既上，问之，仁以实告。勇曰：“母有害兄意，宜善避之，勿以从命为孝。”仁诺之。母知，鞭勇。勇毫无悔心。一日，仁赴贺戚家，大醉而归。马见之喜甚。勇以往昔母见兄必怒，今反喜，知必有故。乃伪为赴塾，未几旋归，而门已合。恐母害兄，知家有狗窦甚阔，由之入。

    Explanation

    71

    马说：“他出去了还没回来。”勇不相信，四处寻找，到了闲园，听到井里有人声；仔细听，原来是哥哥叫自己的名字求救。勇说：“哥哥先忍耐一下。”于是找来一根长绳，一头系在井旁的树上，一头放入井中，让哥哥抓住绳子爬上来。哥哥上来后，勇问他，仁如实相告。勇说：“母亲有伤害哥哥的意图，你应该好好躲避，不要把服从命令当作孝顺。”仁答应了他。母亲得知后，鞭打了勇。勇毫无悔意。一天，仁去亲戚家庆祝，喝得大醉而归。马见到他非常高兴。勇想起以前母亲见到哥哥必定生气，现在反而高兴，知道一定有原因。于是假装去学堂，不久就回来了，却发现门已经关上了。担心母亲会伤害哥哥，知道家里有一个很大的狗洞，于是从那里进去。

    72

    见母以绳缢兄项，将绳从窗中递出，势将自外牵之以经兄，急谏曰：“不可！任氏母舅固刁生，倘缢死其甥，舅固有以处母氏；若男也，亦必不得其死然。”马氏惧，乃罢。勇向仁项解结，仁醒，曰：“将害兄乎？”勇曰：“非也，母为之，而弟救之耳！”勇见母购信石而密藏之，窥知其处，乃以物之似信石者易之，仍置旧处。勇自塾归食，马谓勇曰：“今日干糇无异，可令汝兄先食。”勇笑从之。仁食已而去。及晚，勇谓母曰：“今日以信石毒兄耶？”母曰：“无之。”勇曰：“某处之信石何无有也？”母不答。

    Explanation

    72

    Seeing his mother attempting to strangle his brother with a rope, he hurriedly advised, "No! The brother-in-law of the mother is a cunning man. If he strangles his nephew to death, he will have a way to deal with the mother; if it were a boy, he would also not die easily." The mother, frightened, stopped. 勇 untied the knot from 仁's neck, and 仁 woke up, asking, "Are you going to harm my brother?" 勇 replied, "No, it was the mother who did it, but I saved him!" 勇 saw the mother buy and secretly hide "信石" (a type of poison), and upon finding out where it was hidden, he replaced it with something similar and put it back in the same place. Returning from school, 勇 ate, and the mother told him, "Today's food is different; let your brother eat first." 勇 smiled and agreed. 仁 ate and then left. By evening, 勇 asked the mother, "Did you use '信石' to poison my brother today?" The mother denied it. 勇 asked, "Where is the '信石' now?" The mother did not answer.

    73

    勇曰：“昨幸以□者易之，不然兄此时早见阎罗王矣！儿昨已言之，任氏舅挑三唆四，架李告张，颠倒曲直，全凭词讼之工；变乱是非，善逞笔端之利。若害其亲甥，吾家势将灭门矣！”勇且暗请任至其家，令兄陪饮。任曰：“勇甥若是肥，仁甥若是瘦，无乃所食不同乎？”仁曰：“每日同食，无异馔。”任曰：“仁瘦如是，必有心事。果尔，不妨向舅言之，勿抑郁以致疾。”马闻之，遍体汗出，由是害仁之心顿息，而视如刺眼之钉，虽秦仪复生，万言不能改也。或谓勇曰：“汝与仁生非一母，何疏母而亲兄？”勇曰：“百母一父，亲兄弟也。知有母而不知有父者，是禽兽也。”闻者辄叹美。

    Explanation

    73

    Yong said, "Fortunately, I replaced [someone/something] with [someone/something else], otherwise you would have already met the king of hell! I told you yesterday that Uncle Ren is meddling in affairs, stirring up trouble, confusing right and wrong, relying entirely on the skill of litigation; distorting right and wrong, skillfully using the power of the pen. If he harms his own nephew, our family will be exterminated!" Yong then secretly invited Ren to his home, and had his brother accompany him for a drink. Ren said, "Yong is so fat, and Ren is so thin, is it not because they eat differently?" Ren replied, "We eat together every day, there is no difference in our food." Ren said, "Ren is so thin, there must be something troubling his mind. If so, you might as well tell your uncle about it, do not let it fester and lead to illness." Ma heard this and broke out in a cold sweat, and thus his heart to harm Ren ceased, and he saw Ren as a thorn in his eye, even if Qin Yi were to be reborn, a thousand words could not change it. Someone said to Yong, "You and Ren were not born of the same mother, why are you distant from your mother but close to your brother?" Yong replied, "One hundred mothers make one father, brothers are still close. Those who know their mother but not their father are no better than beasts." Those who heard this all sighed in admiration.

    74

    时邻近起会，勇欲往观，马不许。勇哀之，马曰：“谁与同往？”曰：“兄也可。”马曰：“恐害汝。”勇曰：“二兄圣贤也，果欲相害，儿无今日矣！”马乃许。兄若弟嬉戏同往。及会，至人众处，男女拥挤，仁、勇忽失散。兄寻弟，弟亦觅兄。勇见同窗某，问兄耗。某诳之曰：“由此路寻汝去矣！”勇信之，跋涉十数里，未见兄而日已暮。勇审其处，乃任舅氏之居也，遂往投而告以故。任翁媪见勇，甚喜，视若亲甥。勇恐母之倚望也，早旦欲去。任曰：“仆已烦人代禀汝母，知汝在此，或无忧。”而所烦之人忘之。仁在会场觅弟及晚，音信皆无，意弟先归，而家人未见。

    74

    When the neighbors were holding a gathering, Yong wanted to go and watch, but Ma refused. Yong pitied him, and Ma said, "Who will go with you?" Yong replied, "My elder brother can also go." Ma said, "I am afraid it will harm you." Yong said, "My two elder brothers are sages. If they really wanted to harm me, I wouldn't be here today!" Ma then agreed. Brother and younger brother went together to play. When they arrived at the gathering, they found a crowded place with both men and women. Ren and Yong suddenly got separated. Brother searched for younger brother, and younger brother also looked for brother. Yong saw a classmate and asked about his brother. The classmate lied to him, saying, "Follow this path to find him!" Yong believed him and walked for many miles, but he didn't see his brother and it was already evening. Yong realized he was at the residence of his uncle Ren, so he went there and told them the reason. Uncle Ren and Aunt Ren were very happy and treated Yong like their own nephew. Yong was afraid his mother would be worried, so he wanted to leave early in the morning. Uncle Ren said, "I have already asked someone to inform your mother that you are here, so she may not worry." But the person I asked forgot about it. Ren searched for his brother at the gathering until evening, but there was no news. He thought his brother had gone home first, but the family hadn't seen him.

    75

    马氏曰：“汝将吾儿伤害，又造伪言以相欺，汝尚欲安然独处乎？”仁曰：“吾弟非犹夫人之弟，况与吾偕出而不与吾同归，吾何忍家居？”马诟詈万端，竟夜不息。仁早起寻觅，终日无信，不敢见母，借宿邻居。或有自宁羌来者，言路逢一人貌似勇。仁乃早起遄行，沿路问访，并无消息。所带资斧，二日已罄，夜宿庙宇，日丐村庄。会有四川成都行客，仆死于店，不能自行。店主见仁，欲令佣于行客。仁思有家难归，遂从之。客姓张，自有苏杭货肆，见仁忠诚，至家，令赴肆生理，仁遂止焉。勇留任家五日，任送之归。马见之喜极，如获再生。勇曰：“吾兄安在？”家人曰：“三日前觅汝未回。”勇乃窃母财物，闻兄赴宁羌，亦问途而去。路逢同里无赖，偕行二日，渠见勇囊资丰裕，因于路饵之以药，尽窃其所有而去。会有贵州乔姓大商载货而归，见勇倒卧路侧，摇之不醒，遂载以后车。二日后，勇病乃瘥。乔问之，一一实告。乔无子，遂以勇为义。

    Explanation

    75

    Ma said, "You hurt my son and fabricated false words to deceive me. How can you still wish to remain at ease alone?" Ren replied, "My brother is not like your brother, and since he went out with me but did not return with me, how could I bear to stay at home?" Ma cursed him in countless ways, all night without stopping. Ren got up early to search, all day without any news, and did not dare to see his mother, so he stayed overnight at a neighbor's house. Someone who came from Ningqiang said they met a person who looked like Yong. Ren then got up early and hurried on, asking around the way, but there was no news. The money he brought was exhausted in two days, so he slept in a temple at night and begged in villages during the day. There was a traveler from Sichuan Chengdu whose servant died in the inn and could not travel on his own. The innkeeper saw Ren and wanted to hire him for the traveler. Ren thought it was hard to return home, so he agreed. The traveler's name was Zhang, and he had a shop in Suzhou and Hangzhou. Seeing Ren's loyalty, upon returning home, he sent Ren to the shop to make a living, and Ren stayed there. Yong stayed at Ren's house for five days, and Ren sent him back. Ma was extremely happy to see him, as if reborn. Yong said, "Where is my brother?" The family said, "Three days ago, they searched for you but you did not return." Yong then stole his mother's belongings, heard that his brother went to Ningqiang, and also asked the way and left. He met a fellow villager who was a rascal and traveled together for two days. The rascal saw that Yong had plenty of money, so he lured him with medicine on the road, stole everything he had, and left. There was a wealthy merchant from Guizhou named Qiao who was returning home with goods. He saw Yong lying unconscious on the side of the road, shook him but he did not wake up, so he loaded him onto the rear carriage. Two days later, Yong's illness then subsided. Qiao asked him, and he told him everything truthfully. Qiao had no children, so he adopted Yong as his son.

    76

    仁之在成都货肆也，十数年间，已成总柜，而资本已有其半。时有欠货债者，反以诈赖控仁。时邑尊乔公颇有政声，见仁名，立刻传讯。仁词直，判令欠货债者立限清还，徼迟重责。仁归，方与店友颂乔宰仁明，忽有人报邑尊至。仓猝间，邑尊已入。仁审视之，勇也。盖勇已从乔姓，中会，部选成都县令矣。兄弟相见，悲喜交集，各诉艰辛，不胜酸恻。勇曰：“明晨迎兄至署，再为细叙。”自是兄若弟听夕恒相聚晤。一日，仁见勇有忧色，问之。勇曰：“兹有参将，与弟有言语之失。

    Explanation

    76

    Ren was in a shop in Chengdu, and over ten years, he had risen to become the head of the business, having already accumulated half of the capital. At that time, there was someone who owed goods but tried to defraud and sue Ren. The magistrate Qiao, who had a good reputation in governance, heard of Ren's name and immediately summoned him. Ren's words were straightforward and just, so the magistrate ruled that the debtor must clear the debt within a set period, and if delayed, he would be severely punished. After Ren returned, he praised Magistrate Qiao's fairness with his shop friends. Suddenly, someone reported that the magistrate had arrived. In a hurry, the magistrate had already entered. Ren looked closely and saw it was Yong. It turned out that Yong had taken the surname Qiao, passed the imperial examinations, and was appointed as the county magistrate of Chengdu. The brothers met, and their emotions were a mix of joy and sorrow. They each recounted their hardships, unable to hold back their emotions. Yong said, "Tomorrow morning, I will welcome my brother to the office and we will talk in detail." From then on, the brothers would meet and talk every evening. One day, Ren saw Yong with a worried expression and asked him. Yong said, "There is a military commander who has insulted me with his words."

    77

    渠上游见喜，屡遭谤毁，恐被参劾，是以忧耳。”仁问其姓氏，勇曰：“渠与吾家同姓，与大兄同名。”仁曰：“焉知非吾兄乎？”勇曰：“貌或似之，但意大兄何由至此？是以不敢相认。”仁曰：“吾试访之。”一日，参将乘马出，仁大声曰：“非吾大兄乎？”参将闻之，下马相见。审视果然，于是偕至官衙展叙。仁曰：“兄与乔县尹有隙乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“乔尹非他，即吾兄弟之弟也。”知曰：“渠姓乔，何得云尔？”仁历叙之。并马到县，兄弟团□，其喜可知。后勇乞假回陕，奉母于蜀而养焉。三子属属，马氏底豫。

    Explanation

    77

    He was favored by the higher-ups, but frequently faced slander and defamation, and feared being impeached, which is why he was worried. Ren asked about his surname and given name, and Yong replied, "He shares the same surname as my family and has the same name as my elder brother." Ren said, "How can we know he is not my elder brother?" Yong said, "The appearance is somewhat similar, but I wonder how my elder brother could have come here. That is why I dare not recognize him." Ren said, "I will try to visit him." One day, the general riding out on horseback, Ren called out loudly, "Is that not my elder brother?" The general heard and dismounted to meet. After careful examination, it turned out to be true, and so they went together to the government office to catch up. Ren asked, "Do you have a dispute with Magistrate Qiao?" The general replied, "Yes." Ren said, "Magistrate Qiao is not someone else; he is my younger brother." The general said, "He is surnamed Qiao, how could you say that?" Ren explained it in detail. Riding together to the county, the brothers reunited, and the joy is self-evident. Later, Yong requested leave to return to Shaanxi, serving his mother in Sichuan and taking care of her. The three sons were all present, and the Ma family was at peace.

    78

    虚白道人曰：余闻此事，不禁为之叹美数次。以十馀岁之幼童，而能与兄易疏食，则食果取小之义不足言矣。不禁叹美！委曲救兄，不惧母劳，则兄弟如手足，伤之不能再生，此义勇知之深矣。不禁叹美！闻兄觅己而亡，不畏艰辛，窃藏而追寻之，此情纯出于天性，不禁叹美！至若仁遭继母之难，实有浚井完廪之势，而不闻有怨言，则不禁为仁叹美！兄弟团圆，一致富而二致贵，悉出不意，则不禁为知等合家叹美！不知后世亦有叹美如余者否？

    Explanation

    78

    The Taoist recluse Xu Bai said: Upon hearing this story, I could not help but admire it several times. At the age of more than ten, the young boy was able to share his sparse food with his elder brother, demonstrating the virtue of taking the smaller portion of fruit, which is beyond words. I could not help but admire him! He went to great lengths to save his brother, fearless of his mother's labor, showing that brothers are as close as hands and feet, and the injury is so severe that it cannot be healed, indicating his deep understanding of righteousness and courage. I could not help but admire him! Upon hearing that his brother had searched for him and perished, he did not fear hardship, secretly hiding and searching for him, showing that his emotions were purely natural and instinctive. I could not help but admire him! As for Ren, who faced the difficulties from his stepmother, who was in a position to harm him like digging a well or completing a granary, yet he remained silent and without complaint, I could not help but admire him! The brothers reunited, both achieving wealth and status, all unexpected, and I could not help but admire the entire family. I wonder if future generations will also admire this story as much as I do?

    79

    读之令人孝弟之心油然以生。 汪雪马风

    　　上官知之遭遇，较《聊斋》之张诚更苦；上官仁之敬恭，视《志异》之张诚倍笃。至于上官知之逢仁、勇，张千户之遇讷、诚，俱出意外，悉见友恭之感格。此篇之文尤真恳朴至，情切理深，其文其事洵可与张诚之传并传矣！ 王植三

    　　是有功伦纪文字。 马竹吾

    　　事与《聊斋?张诚》相类。叙次参错有法，自可与“张诚传”并传。 叶芸士

    　　观仁、勇友爱，易食、藏毒、谏母数事，叹王氏之祥、览不得专美于前。 杨子厚

    Explanation

    79

    Reading it evokes the natural emergence of filial and fraternal love in one's heart. — Wang Xuemafeng

    The misfortunes of Shangguan Zhi are even more severe than those of Zhang Cheng in "Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio"; the respect and reverence for Shangguan Ren surpass those shown to Zhang Cheng in "Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio." As for Shangguan Zhi encountering Ren and Yong, and Zhang Qianguan meeting Ne and Cheng, all were unexpected, fully demonstrating the effect of brotherly love and respect. This text is especially sincere, simple, and profound, with intense emotions and deep reasoning; its writing and events can indeed be transmitted alongside Zhang Cheng's biography! — Wang Zhisan

    This text is beneficial to the moral and social order. — Ma Zhuwu

    The events in this text are similar to those in "Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio" involving Zhang Cheng. The narrative is structured with a methodical alternation, making it suitable for transmission alongside "Zhang Cheng's Biography." — Ye Yunshi

    Observing the love and care between Ren and Yong, the events of easily eating, hiding poison, and advising the mother, one laments the good fortune of the Wang family and notes that others cannot outdo it. — Yang Zihou

    80

    如读《枤杜》、《棠棣》诸章。先生必笃于友爱者，故言之亲切有味如此。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　蜈蚣

    　　章邑焦荫泉先生为诸生时，尝设帐于余之邻庄。余时馆黄台山，时相往来。谈及章邑一事，其人之姓名、里居备悉，余咸忘之。撮记其事：有甲某者，奉母孝，而家綦贫。身躯雄伟，惟日樵柴一肩，市以养母。一日肩柴归，见一女郎出于其前，以为道路之常，不遑顾而过之。女郎呼而问途，甲息肩于路，视之，乃静女其姝也。眉目送情，不觉为之神驰。女曰：“由此达某，是正路乎？”曰：“然。但汝所问之处，日暮途远，决不能到。”女曰：“吾将借宿前村耳。”甲将担柴走，女复曰：“君家有闲房否？”曰：“诚有之，老母在堂，不敢自专。”女曰：“烦君先容，妾后至，可乎？”曰：“可。”甲归，向母言之。母意容留女流亦与人方便事，许之，而女已至。见女姿容异俗，与甲言毫无羞惭，疑之。

    Explanation

    80

    Reading the chapters of Tang Du and Tang Di from the Book of Songs. The scholar must be deeply devoted to friendship and love, hence the language used is so intimate and flavorful. Annotation by Li Yu from Shangyuan.

    Centipede

    When Mr. Jiao Yinquan from Zhang County was a student, he once taught at a nearby village. At the time, I was teaching at Huangtai Mountain, and we visited each other frequently. We discussed an incident from Zhang County, and although the details of the person's name and place of residence were fully known, I have forgotten them. Here is a brief record of the event: There was a person named Jia who was filial to his mother but extremely poor. He had a strong and impressive physique, and he carried firewood daily on his shoulder to sell and support his mother. One day, while returning home with firewood, he saw a young lady in front of him. Thinking it was just a common sight, he passed by without looking. The young lady called out and asked for directions. Jia stopped to look, and saw that she was a beautiful and quiet girl. Her eyes conveyed affection, and he was captivated by her. She asked, "Is this the right way to reach a certain place?" He replied, "Yes, but the place you are asking about is far and it will be dark, so you will not be able to get there." She said, "I will stay overnight at the village ahead." Jia was about to leave with his firewood, but the girl spoke again, "Do you have an empty room?" He said, "Yes, but my mother is at home, and I cannot make decisions alone." She said, "Please ask your mother first, and I will come later." He agreed. When he returned home, he told his mother. His mother thought it was a convenient thing to let a woman stay, so she agreed. By the time he returned, the girl had already arrived. Seeing that the girl's appearance was unusual and that she spoke to him without any shame, he was suspicious.

    81

    母乃将女安置闲房，呼甲至卧室训之曰：“彼系女流，不宜与之长言。”甲唯唯而出。女见老母不在，谓甲曰：“君宿何处？”甲不应。女眼一瞪，若望而生畏，乃曰：“与母同室，各住一间。”女曰：“夜勿扃户，妾将至。”甲诺之。及晚，甲遵母训，严关其扉而寝。至三更时，女以指弹窗，呼令开门。甲若有不敢不开之势，启户视之，非女，乃一怪物，若布袋状，上下相等，不分首足。幸打柴之巨斧在侧，执而挥之，物嗥而去。火之，见削物下颔一片如蒲扇。及曙，寻其血踪觅之。至某山，见素所塞之石孔外有蜈蚣一条，长约丈许，粗如巨碗，尚曲曲未死，再斧之，立毙。盖甲尝打柴至是，见石孔有巨物出入之迹，恐出为害，乃以巨石塞之。隔二日视之，石复出，甲又塞之。妖物之来，或为此也。甲之不死，幸哉！

    Explanation

    81

    The mother then placed the girl in a quiet room and called Jia to her bedroom to give him instructions, saying, "She is a woman, and it is not appropriate to have a long conversation with her." Jia nodded and left. The girl, seeing that her mother was not there, asked Jia, "Where will you be staying?" Jia did not respond. The girl looked at him with a look of fear, and then said, "Live with the mother in the same room, each in their own space." She added, "Do not lock the door at night, I will come to you." Jia agreed. By evening, Jia followed his mother's instructions and locked the door before going to sleep. At midnight, the girl tapped on the window with her finger and called for Jia to open the door. Jia seemed to be afraid to not open the door, so he opened it and saw that it was not the girl, but a monster, shaped like a bag, with equal height from top to bottom, and no distinction between head and feet. Fortunately, a large axe used for chopping wood was nearby, and Jia grabbed it and swung it at the creature, which howled and went away. When Jia burned it, he saw that the creature's lower jaw was shaped like a fan. By dawn, Jia followed the blood trail and found that it led to a certain mountain, where he saw a centipede that was about a zhang long, as thick as a large bowl, still wriggling and not yet dead. He swung the axe at it again, and it died immediately. It turned out that Jia had once been chopping wood in that area and saw a large creature coming and going from a stone hole, fearing that it would cause harm, so he blocked it with a large stone. Two days later, when he checked, the stone had moved again, so Jia blocked it again. The arrival of the monster may have been due to this. Jia's survival was fortunate.

    82

    虚白道人曰：孝之必获神佑也，审矣！盖妖物既化女身以惑甲，必令甲死于女身。乃扣关时似女子，而启户视之非女，或妖物仍托为女，惟甲自视非女。不然，甲将死于女，何能执斧伤妖物，而自得不死也？

    Explanation

    82

    虚白道人曰：孝之必获神佑也，审矣！盖妖物既化女身以惑甲，必令甲死于女身。乃扣关时似女子，而启户视之非女，或妖物仍托为女，惟甲自视非女。不然，甲将死于女，何能执斧伤妖物，而自得不死也？

    Translation:
    The Daoist recluse Xubai said: "Filial piety will surely receive divine protection, indeed! The demonic entity, having transformed into a female form to bewitch Jia, must cause Jia to die as a woman. When Jia knocked on the door, it appeared like a woman, but when he opened the door and looked, it was not a woman. Perhaps the demonic entity still pretended to be a woman, but only Jia saw himself as not a woman. Otherwise, Jia would have died as a woman, so how could he have wielded an axe to injure the demonic entity and survive?"

    83

    疏宕有逸致。 汪雪马风

    　　巨石塞石孔，恐出为害，此亦埋蛇之心也。仁人神所佑，妖物安能害之。 盖防如

    　　笔无纤尘，是参之太史以著其洁者。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　申 术 士

    　　康熙中，登州周围二百里苦旱，夏仲犹赤地无青草。太守某竭诚拜祷，旬日不应。乃集六房老吏，问有求雨术否。一人曰：“某处申术士，善能祈雨。”太守曰：“可速请来！”其人曰：“不惟请之不至，一闻此信，当必逃避。惟亲临拜恳，或不推诿。”太守闻之，立即访之。不带从人，直诣其庄；入其门，登其堂，见申方观书于窗下。申不胜惊讶，曰：“不知公祖辱临，有失远迓。”太守曰：“斋宿拜谒，敬有所求。”曰：“何事？”曰：“旱魃为虐，黎民憔悴，既能普济，何得坐视？”申曰：“士实不能，以告者过耳。

    Explanation

    83

    A Style of Broad and Free Flowing Elegance. The Flowing and Majestic Wind.

    A large stone blocks a stone hole, fearing that it might cause harm, this is also a heart as firm as burying a snake. Benevolent people are blessed by the divine, how could evil spirits harm them? This is a precaution.

    The brush has no trace of dust, it is referred to the Taishi to establish its purity. Annotated with care by Li Yu of Shangyuan.

    Shen, the Ritual Master

    During the reign of Kangxi, the area around Dengzhou suffered from a severe drought, with the summer season still barren and without green grass. The magistrate sincerely prayed, but for ten days there was no response. He then gathered senior officials from the six departments and asked if there were any techniques for summoning rain. One official said, "There is a ritual master named Shen in a certain place who is skilled in summoning rain." The magistrate said, "Quickly invite him to come!" The official replied, "Not only would he not come when invited, but upon hearing this news, he would certainly flee. Only by personally visiting and pleading might he not shirk responsibility." Upon hearing this, the magistrate immediately went to visit him. Without bringing attendants, he directly went to Shen's residence; he entered the door, ascended the hall, and saw Shen reading a book by the window. Shen was greatly surprised and said, "I did not know that Your Excellency had come, I failed to receive you properly." The magistrate said, "I fasted and visited, with respect, I have a request." Shen asked, "What is it?" The magistrate said, "The drought demon is causing harm, the common people are suffering, if you can universally save, how could you stand by and do nothing?" Shen replied, "The scholar truly cannot, the person who reported this was mistaken."

    84

    ”太守曰：“民胞物与，贤士岂无其责？不能，求其能；既能而诿曰不能，则曩者之求其能也，其意何居？”申感太守之诚，许之，曰：“文祈乎？武祈乎？”曰：“有以异乎？”申曰：“文祈，设坛拜祷，须迟时日；武祈，即日可雨。”太守曰：“大旱望雨，度日如年，武祈甚善。武祈之法何如？”申曰：“某处去海十数里有古庙，可于庙中建坛。再于海边用铁锁一条，长约二丈，铁匠十二名，各设炉火将锁炼红，共举而掷于海。且预选快马二匹，公祖务与士并马急赴建坛所，不可远离。此其法也，明晨便可行之。”太守一一应之，遂辞归，悉如申言预备。

    Explanation

    84

    The Governor said, "All people are my fellow beings, all things are my companions. How could a virtuous scholar not have a responsibility? If one cannot, then seek someone who can; if one is capable but claims to be incapable, then what was the purpose of seeking one's ability in the past?" Shen was moved by the Governor's sincerity and agreed, saying, "Is it a literary request or a martial one?" "Is there any difference?" Shen replied, "A literary request involves setting up a platform for prayers, which requires some time; a martial request can bring rain immediately." The Governor said, "In a great drought, waiting for rain feels like a year. A martial request is very good. How does the martial request work?" Shen said, "There is an ancient temple several miles from the sea. A platform can be built in the temple. Additionally, on the beach, a long iron chain, about twenty feet long, can be prepared. Twelve blacksmiths can each set up a forge and heat the chain red-hot, then together lift and throw it into the sea. Also, pre-select two swift horses, and the official must ride alongside the scholar to the platform site without delay. This is the method, and it can be carried out tomorrow morning." The Governor agreed to all of this and returned home, preparing everything as Shen had said.

    85

    明早太守至，见申用方桌若干张，露地建坛三层，上设避雨器具，遍施符水，自言：“吾得到此，可无惧。”设施毕，遂同太守赴海岸，各焚香三拜，已，令太守先乘马俟之。申见锁已炼红，乃仗剑拈诀念咒，呵曰：“速掷！”匠人各用火剪将锁剪起，齐力掷之于海。海水势如汤沸，声如雷鸣。申乃执剑乘马与太守并马而驰，身后雷电交作。既而，雷电如在头上盘旋。申大惧，一手兼执太守马辔，面如土色，曰：“速走！速走！”及庙外下马，申乃笑曰：“吾无忧矣！”入门，令太守避雨庙中，且嘱曰：“雨足时，可即示下。”申自登坛趺坐。既而大雨倾盆，多时，太守曰：“雨足矣！”申呵曰：“止！”立刻雨止云收。申下坛曰：“此乃降龙之正术，未免与龙结仇，惟自处于正而后可行此术。倘有不正，龙必报之，吾太师与师皆死于龙，此故不可轻易行之也。吾亦将从此隐矣。”太守酬以财物，坚辞不受而去。

    Explanation

    85

    Early the next morning, the prefect arrived to see Shen using several square tables, having built a three-tiered altar in the open ground. On top of the altar, rain shelters were set up, and talisman water was sprinkled everywhere. Shen said to himself, "I have arrived here, so I have nothing to fear." After the setup was completed, he then went to the coast with the prefect. Each burned incense and bowed three times, and afterward, Shen ordered the prefect to ride ahead and wait for him. When Shen saw that the lock had been heated to a red glow, he drew his sword, chanted a spell, and commanded, "Hurry! Throw it!" The craftsmen each used fire tongs to lift the lock and together threw it into the sea. The sea water surged like boiling water, with a sound like thunder. Shen held his sword and rode a horse, galloping alongside the prefect. Behind him, lightning and thunder raged. Shortly thereafter, the lightning and thunder seemed to swirl around his head. Shen was greatly afraid, holding the prefect's horse reins with one hand, his face pale as chalk, and he cried, "Hurry! Hurry!" When they reached the temple and dismounted, Shen smiled and said, "I have no worries now!" He entered the temple and ordered the prefect to seek shelter from the rain inside, instructing him to signal when the rain was sufficient. Shen ascended the altar and sat cross-legged. Soon, it began to pour heavily, and after some time, the prefect said, "The rain is sufficient!" Shen commanded, "Stop!" Immediately, the rain ceased and the clouds parted. Shen descended from the altar and said, "This is the proper method to subdue dragons, but it inevitably makes one an enemy of the dragon. One can only practice this after maintaining righteousness. If one acts unjustly, the dragon will surely retaliate. My master and my teacher both died at the hands of the dragon, which is why this cannot be done lightly. I will also retreat from this place." The prefect offered gifts of money and goods, but Shen firmly refused and left without accepting.

    86

    虚白道人曰：以正正人，千古定理。祷雨之术小术也，犹惕惕然有不正之惧，况大于是者乎？古今之身名俱败、隙末凶终者，咸谓运数应尔，然未必非不正之所致也。

    　　语有关系，非徒以笔力见长。 汪雪马风

    　　伯温先生未卒之先，以所习之学术封授其子，戒勿习。又曰：“上或思我，问遗言：愿为政以德。”“政”之为言“正”也，先生死不忘正君，是以正行术；戒子勿习，正子不正，慎言术之不可正也。 盖防如

    　　郭璞以术杀身，自处于不正也。若申君者，以正为术，可以正天下之邪术矣。 马竹吾

    Explanation

    86

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: "To correct people with correctness is an eternal truth. The technique of praying for rain is a small technique, yet it still carries a sense of caution about not being correct. How much more so for larger matters?"

    Those throughout history who have ended up both dishonored and with a bad ending are all said to be the result of fate, but it may not be entirely due to their lack of correctness.

    Words have their significance, not just because of writing skills. - Wang Xue Ma Feng

    Before the respected Mr. Bao Wen passed away, he bestowed his learned scholarship upon his son and warned him not to practice it. He also said, "If the emperor thinks of me and asks about my last words: I wish to govern with virtue." "The word 'govern' means 'correct.' The master did not forget to correct the ruler even in death, therefore practicing with correctness. He warned his son not to practice, lest his son become incorrect, and to be cautious about the possibility of the technique being incorrect." - Gai Fang Ru

    Guo Pu was killed by his own technique, having placed himself in an incorrect position. If it were Shen Jun, he would use correctness as a technique, and it could correct the evil techniques of the world. - Ma Zhu Wu

    87

    萨真人之感王天君，律身以正故也。读此而先生之律身以正可知矣！ 上元李瑜谨注

    　　袁 岫 云

    　　余砚友孙香雨，邑庠生，工诗词。尝设帐于趵突泉之白雪楼，功课之暇，时至泉上遨游。一日，值诸徒课期，命题后，殊觉闷倦，乃信步出游。不觉已到泉上，见二八女郎及老妪在焉。睨之，华妆艳绝，洵生平未睹之妹丽也。疑是贵家宅眷，心存顾忌，不便狂视。而女郎眉目传情，反若有意。未几，妪先女后相将俱去。孙目送之，女回顾含笑，嫣然百媚俱生。孙转念一想，此必仙人，世岂有娟丽之女情态如是者乎？尾之，已不知去向。越六日，复往泉上游赏，而前日之女郎及妪又先在焉。女郎之情意态度较前更觉可亲，直有形违神合，欲言复止之情。妪见之，急促女行，女回顾，妪辄以身障之。孙魂魄都迷，颠倒不能自主。急尾之，止违数武，忽失所在。孙决其非人，归斋冥想，仰慕殊切。

    Explanation

    87

    The True Person Sa was moved by the Heavenly Lord Wang because he regulated himself with rectitude. By reading this, one can understand that the master regulated himself with rectitude! Carefully annotated by Li Yu from Shangyuan.

    Yuan Xiuyun

    My fellow scholar, Sun Xiangyu, was a student in the local school and excelled in poetry and verse. He once set up a lectern at the White Snow Tower of Baotu Spring and, in his spare time from studies, would sometimes go to the spring for leisurely strolls. One day, during a lesson period with his students, after setting a topic, he felt unusually bored and restless, so he took a leisurely walk. Before he knew it, he had arrived at the spring, where he saw a young woman and an elderly woman. Glancing at them, he noticed the young woman was exquisitely dressed and stunningly beautiful, truly the most beautiful maiden he had ever seen in his life. He suspected she was a lady from a wealthy family, so he held back and did not stare openly. But the young woman conveyed emotions through her eyes and eyebrows, as if she had an intention. Soon, the elderly woman and the young woman left together. Sun watched them leave, and the young woman glanced back and smiled, her beauty radiating in a hundred ways. Sun thought to himself, this must be an immortal, for in the world, how could there be such a beautiful and graceful woman with such an enchanting demeanor? He followed them, but soon lost track of where they went. Six days later, he returned to the spring for another leisurely stroll, and the young woman and the elderly woman were there again. The young woman's feelings and demeanor were even more endearing than before, as if they were physically apart but spiritually connected, wanting to speak but hesitating. The elderly woman saw this and hurriedly urged the young woman to leave, and when the young woman glanced back, the elderly woman blocked her with her body. Sun was so entranced that he was disoriented and could not control himself. He hurriedly followed them, but they were only a few steps away when they suddenly disappeared. Sun decided they were not human, and upon returning to his study, he deeply contemplated and admired them.

    88

    又值生徒不在，寂寞难堪，遂作七绝五首以寓渴想。其一曰：“仙颜一睹梦魂驰，肠断巫山止自知。今夜月明谁共赏，珊珊环佩莫来迟。”其四则余忘之矣。录毕，时已二鼓，以灯火焚之。未几，一丽人自外入曰：“狂郎之情何极也？”视之，即白日所见之女郎。大喜，遂狎抱之。颜添羞红，灯光之下，较昼见时尤艳绝矣。女撑拒曰：“勿遽尔！一言未宣，而辄如此以相接，何情极之不能待也？”孙乃释之，问曰：“卿鬼耶？狐耶？”女不答。孙曰：“卿即鬼狐，亦慰素愿，言之亦自无妨。”女曰：“妾非鬼狐，君既以鬼狐疑妾，即以鬼狐视妾可也，何穷诘焉？”孙曰：“妙龄几何？”曰：“年十六矣。”“芳名为甚？”女不答。孙曰：“岂有终夜谈笑不知姓名者乎？”女曰：“妾袁氏，小字岫云。

    Explanation

    88

    Again, when the students were not present, the loneliness was unbearable, so he composed five quatrains to express his longing. The first one says: "A glimpse of her divine beauty makes my soul fly, my heart breaks, only known to the mountains of Wu. Who will share this bright moon tonight? Do not be late, with your delicate jade rings." The fourth one, I have forgotten. After recording them, it was already the second drumbeat, and he burned them with a lamp. Soon after, a beautiful woman entered from outside and said, "How extreme is the passion of this madman?" Seeing her, it was the young lady seen during the day. Greatly delighted, he embraced her. Her face turned a deeper shade of red, and under the light, she was even more beautiful than during the day. The woman pushed him away and said, "Do not do it so hastily! Before I have even spoken a word, you act like this, how can your passion not wait?" Sun released her, and asked, "Are you a ghost or a fox?" The woman did not answer. Sun said, "Even if you are a ghost or a fox, it would still satisfy my long-held wish, and it would be no harm to tell me." The woman said, "I am neither a ghost nor a fox. Since you suspect me of being a ghost or a fox, then consider me as such. Why interrogate me further?" Sun asked, "How old are you?" She replied, "I am sixteen years old." "What is your name?" The woman did not answer. Sun said, "How can there be someone who talks and laughs all night without knowing their name?" The woman said, "I am Lady Yuan, with the nickname Xiuyun."

    89

    ”既而曰：“妾失言矣！奈何令君知妾小字？愿君切记勿呼！”孙曰：“适作七绝五首，以道切慕冀幸之怀，云卿知之否？”云曰：“适戒君勿呼妾名，始闻之而即呼之，然亦不能禁君之不呼也。妾与君初相会，佳作何由而知？”孙为缅述之，随读随讲。云曰：“读之可耳，勿讲也。无谓佳作意旨高深而为人所不易解也，以妾论之，俚句耳。”孙兴扫，不复读。既而孙曰：“夜深矣，宜其寝乎？”云曰：“合卺需酒。”孙曰：“今夜之酒，明宵补之可耳。”遂寝。及醒，而云已去。次夜，孙静坐俟云，忽闻人高声笑言曰：“孙诗人尚未寝耶？”孙方欲起迓，而云已至面前。孙曰：“勿高声，学生或未寐耳。”云曰：“不妨，妾一至，即大声搅闹，保渠不与闻也。前宵欢会，无酒沽我，实一憾事，今沽之否？”孙曰：“与徒同楼，恐有不便，是以未沽。

    Explanation

    89

    After a while, she said, "I spoke in error! How could I let you know my given name? Please remember not to call me by it!" Sun replied, "I just now composed five seven-character quatrains to express my deep longing and hopes. Do you know about them?" The cloud said, "I just warned you not to call me by my name, and I just heard it and immediately called you. But I cannot prevent you from calling me. I and you first met, how could I know your excellent works?" Sun recounted them in detail, reading and explaining as he went. The cloud said, "Reading them is fine, but do not explain. Do not say that the excellent work has profound meaning and is difficult for others to understand. According to me, they are just plain phrases." Sun lost interest and did not read again. After a while, Sun said, "It is late at night, should we go to sleep?" The cloud said, "We need wine for the wedding ceremony." Sun said, "The wine for tonight can be made up tomorrow night." They went to sleep. When they woke up, the cloud had already left. The next night, Sun sat quietly waiting for the cloud. Suddenly, he heard someone laughing loudly, saying, "Has the poet Sun not yet gone to sleep?" Sun was just about to get up to greet, and the cloud had already arrived in front of him. Sun said, "Do not speak loudly, the student may not be asleep yet." The cloud said, "It does not matter. As soon as I arrive, I will make a lot of noise, ensuring that he does not hear. The previous night's joyful gathering, there was no wine to drink, it was really a regrettable matter. Shall we buy some now?" Sun said, "Being in the same building as the disciples, fearing it might be inconvenient, that is why we did not buy any."

    90

    ”云曰：“吝耳！何恐之有？妾已带酒来矣。”孙曰：“安在？”曰：“此其非耶？”见酒具自外飞入，若有人捧托，不见其人。杯箸肴果，一一如是。孙奇之，曰：“反宾作主矣！”云微笑。孙此际饮同佳丽，倚偎谈笑，小登科之乐不及此。曰：“昨睹卿面，盼望殷切，不料卿应念而至，小生何修而得此？”云曰：“妾与君有宿分，即君弗盼望妾亦自至，以了其缘，盖恐迟则无及耳。”言之凄楚。孙曰：“春秋方富，稍迟何伤？且今夕何夕，何烦深虑？吾与卿行令以饮。”云笑从之。饮至更深，酒酣始寝。孙求与欢好，云曰：“乐事之浓尽在此乎？”曰：“非此无以取真乐耳。”事已，同枕共话。云曰：“此事君务慎密，不可以告人，倘风声播扬，妾亦不便来矣。切嘱，切嘱！且君体固弱，妾亦不宜屡至，当来则来，勿悬望也。”自此六七日辄一至，至则对饮竟夜，亦有不寝而去之时，孙亦听之。

    Explanation

    90

    "Cloud said, 'Frugal, indeed! What is there to fear? I have brought wine with me.' Sun asked, 'Where is it?' Cloud replied, 'Is this not it?' She saw the wine utensils flying in from outside, as if carried by someone unseen. The cups, chopsticks, dishes, and fruits all entered in the same manner. Sun was amazed and said, 'The roles have been reversed!' Cloud smiled. At this moment, Sun was drinking with the beautiful lady, leaning close and laughing, finding the joy of a minor success far less than this. He said, 'Yesterday, I saw your face and longed for you with great earnestness. I did not expect you to come as I had hoped. What merit do I have to deserve this?' Cloud said, 'I have a predestined fate with you. Even if you did not long for me, I would still come, to fulfill our destiny. I fear that if we delay, it will be too late.' Her words were filled with sorrow. Sun said, 'You are still in your prime; what harm is there in a slight delay? And what is there to worry about tonight? Let us play a drinking game.' Cloud smiled and agreed. They drank until late into the night, becoming drunk before finally going to sleep. Sun asked for intimacy, and Cloud said, 'Is the greatest pleasure in this?' He replied, 'Without this, there is no true pleasure.' Afterward, they lay together in the same bed and talked. Cloud said, 'You must keep this secret and not tell anyone. If rumors spread, I will no longer be able to come. I urge you, I urge you! And since your body is naturally weak, I should not come too often. When I come, I will come; do not wait anxiously.' From then on, she would come every six or seven days. When she came, they would drink all night, and sometimes she would leave without sleeping. Sun also accepted this."

    91

    一日，有契友某忽至，相约明午赴佛山聚饮，孙诺之。既而同某赴泉游玩，忽天降细雨，某曰：“惜无酒胾，若有之，相与遣此阴雨，其趣岂不更进一层乎？”孙笑应之。某起赏识扁联，孙亦从之。一回首，见肴酒已列桌上。孙心知岫云之供给，遂谓某曰：“请吃酒！”某愕然曰：“乌得此？”孙曰：“斋僮送至耳。”曰：“吾何以未见来人？”孙曰：“君游瞩之际，渠置之即去，故未见耳。”宾主对饮，雨止而某始去。明日，孙欲赴友人之约，而畏赤日行天，忽忆有乡人所遗草笠在此，遂戴之而往。未出关门，风吹帽落，而帽带已断。戴之则须以手按，执之则物为无用，行将寄放于素相识之铺中，旋视之，则带已续矣。以为非岫云为，其谁为？遂戴之。

    Explanation

    91

    One day, a close friend named Sun suddenly arrived, and they agreed to meet the following noon in Foshan for a gathering. Subsequently, Sun and his friend went to a place called "Spring Play," where it suddenly started to drizzle. The friend said, "It's a pity we don't have any prepared meat dishes. If we did, we could enjoy the rainy weather together, and wouldn't the fun be even deeper?" Sun laughed and agreed. The friend then stood up to admire a couplet, and Sun followed. As they turned around, they saw food and drink already laid out on the table. Sun knew that "Xiu Yun" had provided the food and drink, so he said to the friend, "Let's drink!" The friend was surprised and asked, "How did this happen?" Sun replied, "The servant brought it." The friend asked, "Why didn't I see the servant?" Sun explained, "While you were admiring the couplet, the servant placed the food and drink and left, so you didn't see him." The guest and host drank together, and the rain stopped just as the friend left. The next day, Sun wanted to meet a friend but was concerned about the hot sun. Suddenly, he remembered a straw hat left behind by a villager, so he put it on and went. As he was about to leave the gate, the wind blew the hat off, and the strap was broken. If he wore it, he would have to hold it, and if he held it, it would be useless, so he decided to leave it at a shop he knew. Soon after, he saw that the strap had been mended. He thought, "It must have been 'Xiu Yun' who mended it, who else could it be?" So he put the hat back on.

    92

    至，则七人同酌，皆素所知之能饮者，递行酒令，畅情快意。后以大杯豁拳，孙自觉酒足，意甚畏之。六人皆然，势难自异，因亦效尤为之。既负，举杯未饮而酒已干，屡试皆然，甚德岫云。故六人皆醉，孙独清醒而归。孙以云数夕不至，心颇念之，而岫云忽至，曰：“数日未晤，致君悬念，心殊不安。”孙见云，先谢泉上、佛山之事。云曰：“妾虽不明来，时同老妪暗窥，恐君他有差失，送酒、续带犹小节也。”孙不胜感激。云曰：“饮乎？”曰：“饮。但未知辱临之期，肴酒未备，奈何？”云曰：“勿庸，妾自致之。前日谓君吝者，亦戏言耳。”未几，肴酒满案。孙欲豁拳，负饮胜唱。曰：“饮可耳，唱未素谙。”孙垂首不语。云意孙有嗔意，乃曰：“倩人代唱可乎？”曰：“可。”云曰：“今有名妓乎？”曰：“有，兰君色艺双绝，素有一面之交。

    Explanation

    92

    Upon arriving, the seven people drank together, all of whom were known for their ability to drink. They took turns following the drinking rules, expressing their feelings and enjoying themselves freely. Later, they used large cups and played a drinking game. Sun felt he had had enough to drink and was very wary of continuing. The other six people were in the same situation, and it was difficult for him to stand out. So, he also followed suit. After losing, he lifted the cup but the wine was already gone, and this happened every time. He greatly appreciated Xiu Yun. Therefore, the six people were all drunk, but Sun returned alone and sober. Sun missed her very much because she had not come for several days. However, Xiu Yun suddenly arrived and said, "I haven't seen you for several days, causing you to worry, and I felt very uneasy." Sun thanked Xiu Yun first for the matters in Quanshang and Foshan upon seeing her.
