Xiu Yun said, "Although I didn't come openly, I often secretly observed with the old woman, fearing that you might have made some mistakes. Sending wine and replacing the belt are minor matters." Sun was deeply grateful. Xiu Yun asked, "Shall we drink?" Sun replied, "Yes, but I don't know when you would come, so the food and wine are not prepared. What should I do?" Xiu Yun said, "Don't worry, I will bring them myself. What I said about your stinginess the other day was just a joke." Soon, the food and wine were laid out on the table. Sun wanted to play the drinking game again, but after losing, he would drink and win by singing. He said, "Drinking is fine, but I'm not familiar with singing." Sun lowered his head and remained silent. Xiu Yun thought Sun was angry and asked, "Can someone else sing for you?" Sun replied, "Yes." Xiu Yun asked, "Is there a famous courtesan now?" Sun replied, "Yes, there is Lan Jun, who is both beautiful and talented, and I have met her once before."

    93

    ”云乃起，面南，口中念念有辞。既而一丽人抱琵琶入，视之，兰君也。孙乃与之坐，饮以酒，使令唱。兰定弦润喉，唱曲一成。孙曰：“音出佳人之口，分外盈耳。”云曰：“《想多情》曲甚好，可唱与吾二人听。”兰闻之不悦，答言不会。孙曰：“云卿，渠既不会，可令随便唱他曲也可。”云曰：“既如此，不敢相强。”遂袖出红巾一条，挂于襟扣。兰见之，心惊胆怯，遂改口曰：“实会之，唱不好耳。”云曰：“明系故意轻慢，罚令立唱！”兰果立身唱之。已，曰：“孙相公，妾立已久，何不一为缓颊？”孙代为讲情，云首可之。兰曰：“多谢云仙宽恕。”云复怒曰：“吾名亦许贱人呼乎？”兰大惧，齿震震有声。孙委曲代恳，云怒少息。欲遣之，孙欲留与同宿。云曰：“妾非醋葫芦中人，得渠自代，非不欲。

    Explanation

    93

    Cloud rose and faced south, muttering words under her breath. Soon after, a beautiful woman entered carrying a pipa. Upon seeing her, it was Lady Lan. Sun then sat with her and offered her wine, commanding her to sing. Lan tuned her strings and warmed up her voice, then sang a song. Sun said, "The sound coming from the beautiful woman's mouth was especially pleasing to the ear." Cloud said, "The Xiang Duoqing song is very good, sing it for us to hear." Lan heard this and was displeased, answering that she didn't know the song. Sun said, "Cloud, since she doesn't know it, let her sing another song instead." Cloud said, "If that's the case, I won't force her." She then pulled out a red scarf and tied it to her collar button. Lan saw this and was startled and fearful, so she changed her mind and said, "I do know it, but I sing it poorly." Cloud said, "It's clearly intentional disrespect; I'll make her stand and sing!" Lan indeed stood and sang. Afterwards, she said, "Master Sun, I've been standing for a long time, why don't you intercede on my behalf?" Sun interceded on her behalf, and Cloud agreed. Lan said, "Thank you, Cloud, for your forgiveness." Cloud became angry again and said, "Am I to be called by lowly people?" Lan was greatly frightened, her teeth chattering loudly. Sun pleaded on her behalf, and Cloud's anger subsided slightly. Cloud wanted to send her away, but Sun wanted to keep her for the night. Cloud said, "I'm not someone who gets jealous; if she can replace me, I'm not unwilling."

    94

    ”遂解红巾一展，旋纳袖中，而兰已失其所在。问之，云曰：“不必多问。”乃出白金五两曰：“持此赴院中，可播三宵之欢。”后以所佩红巾授孙，曰：“兰君之魂在内，见面时解巾示之，渠自醒。君佳期在即，妾亦不宜宿此，廿日妾始至。”遂告辞，留之不可而去。午后，孙持巾赴院中，指名索见，鸨儿辞之以病。孙曰：“吾正为其病而来。”其人曰：“相公姓孙，设教于白雪楼乎？”孙疑而问之。曰：“兰君夜来忽得迷症，摇之不醒，自言魂在白雪楼，明日孙相公必携带而至。”遂导孙见兰。孙解巾示兰曰：“卿见此否？”兰忽醒，急起申谢。乃敬设酒桌，相与共饮；嬉戏弹唱，夜深始寝。将去，授以酒资等费，兰坚辞不受。孙连去二次，自觉无趣，不复往，专俟云来。至期果至，曰：“其新孔嘉，妾如秋扇之捐矣！”孙谢过，遂绸缪如初。

    Explanation

    94

    He then unfolded the red scarf and quickly put it in his sleeve, and Master Lan had already disappeared. When he asked about it, she said, "There's no need to ask further." She then took out five taels of white silver and said, "Carry this to the courtyard, and it can bring three nights of joy." Later, she gave her red scarf to Sun and said, "The soul of Master Lan is inside. When you meet him, unfold the scarf to show it to him, and he will wake up. Your good fortune is approaching, and I should not stay here. I will come on the twentieth day." She then took her leave, and Sun could not persuade her to stay. In the afternoon, Sun went to the courtyard with the scarf, asked for Master Lan by name, and the madam declined, citing illness. Sun said, "I came specifically because of his illness." The woman replied, "Are you Master Sun, who teaches at the White Snow Tower?" Sun was puzzled and asked her. She said, "Master Lan suddenly fell ill last night, and he would not wake up even when shaken. He said his soul was at the White Snow Tower, and tomorrow, Master Sun will surely bring him there." She then led Sun to see Master Lan. Sun unfolded the scarf and showed it to Master Lan, asking, "Have you seen this?" Master Lan suddenly woke up and quickly stood up to express his gratitude. They then respectfully set up a table with wine and drank together, playing and singing late into the night. As Sun was about to leave, Master Lan offered him money for the wine and other expenses, but he firmly refused. Sun went twice more but found it uninteresting and stopped going, waiting only for the woman to return. On the appointed day, she indeed arrived and said, "How fresh and lovely you are, but I am like a fan discarded in autumn!" Sun apologized, and they resumed their intimacy as before.

    95

    后云来更稀，至解馆时孙已有病，尚可支持。云至曰：“今夜之会，终身之别。”孙惊讶问故。曰：“一言难尽，要之，妾与君缘分尽矣！”孙固求后会。曰：“无已，早春来塾时再为一会。”未曙而去。春正，孙力疾赴斋。云至曰：“病体何如？”孙曰：“诸药罔效，冥路近矣！”云曰：“死生有命，听之而已。终岁之好，而不一视贵恙，终为缺事。且今将永别，情不忍昧，妾非鬼狐，实某山神之女也。事已至此，不妨语人。君有砚友某人乎？”曰：“有之。”曰：“某作《益智录》，可语之以为一则。”遂别。孙病归，路由余斋，言之甚详。后月馀，孙以病故。孙向余言之时，余尚有志未逮。有仙如此，笔墨有光矣！

    Explanation

    95

    The meetings became even rarer, and by the time of the school's closure, Sun was already ill, but he could still manage. When the cloud arrived, she said, "This night's meeting is a lifelong farewell." Sun was surprised and asked why. She replied, "It is difficult to express in one word, but in short, my fate with you is finished!" Sun insisted on a future meeting. She said, "If not, then again at the school in early spring." She left before dawn. In early spring, Sun struggled against his illness to go to the school. The cloud arrived and asked, "How is your illness?" Sun said, "All medicines are ineffective, the path to the netherworld is near!" The cloud said, "Life and death are predestined, just accept it. Our friendship over the years, and yet I never once visited you in your illness, which is ultimately a regret. And now that we are about to part forever, I cannot bear to hide the truth. I am not a ghost or fox spirit, but actually the daughter of a mountain deity. Since things have come to this, there is no harm in telling others. Do you have a scholar friend named [name]?" Sun replied, "Yes, I do." She said, "[Name] wrote the Yizi Lu, you can tell him to add this as an entry." They then parted. Sun returned home ill, passing by my study, and told me in great detail. A month later, Sun died of illness. When Sun told me, I still had aspirations that I had not yet achieved. With such a deity, my writing is illuminated.

    96

    虚白道人曰：美哉仙乎！云为高伦类矣！夫云之与孙相交，往来必以数日，非节制嗜欲、敦笃恩义者不能也；送肴酒暗为应客，逢落帽明为续带，非无违夫意、善执妇功者不能。为代唱，度兰君之幽魂；了宿缘，知孙郎之寿数；窥其微，即无起死回生之术。若责以坐视夫病而不救，不无小补，惜友人思虑之未及此也。

    Explanation

    96

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: "How beautiful the fairy is! Cloud is superior to all kinds of beings!" Indeed, Cloud's interactions with Sun are such that they last for several days, and only those who can restrain their desires and cultivate deep affection can achieve this. She secretly serves food and wine to entertain guests and openly fixes a fallen hat, showing her skillful and considerate behavior. Only those who do not defy their husband's wishes and are good at household duties can do this. She sings on behalf of Lanjun's soul and understands the karmic connection, knowing Sun Lang's lifespan. However, she sees the subtle signs but lacks the power to bring the dead back to life. If one were to blame her for not helping her husband when he was ill, it would be a minor benefit, but it is a pity that my friend did not consider this.

    97

    孙先生何修而得此！ 汪雪马风

    　　予与孙香雨甚相契，竟不知其有如此好遇合。但既系山神之女，且具如许神通，竟不能以丹药延其寿数，予甚疑之。侯百里

    　　昔沈交有口辩，时人谓其舌妙。吾谓神女之舌妙，由于先生之笔妙也。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　某伟

    　　娼优等八款，某某等县人贱之尤甚。凡考试有犯款者之子，非本童互结，即廪膳不保，且诸生以教是徒为辱。康熙年间，有某姓名伟者，身犯八款之二而家巨富，生有三子，乃用多金请明童诲之。凡邻近读书家有可庆吊事，厚其赠贿而不列名。且闻诸生会饮，必敬备肴酒使人送去，致使文人踏青，皆戏谓不必多带肴酒，某伟必有所饷。既而果然，如是者已数十次矣。某处枫树极多，秋后叶红，颇有可观，学士约定日期同往赏玩。至期，赴约者十数人，而某伟之饷盛他日。

    Explanation

    97

    Translation:

    What virtue or merit did Mr. Sun possess to deserve this? — Wang Xue and Ma Feng

    I was very close to Sun Xiangyu, yet I did not know he had such a favorable encounter. However, since she is the daughter of a mountain deity and possesses such great supernatural powers, she still cannot extend her lifespan with elixirs, which greatly puzzled me. — Hou Baili

    In the past, Shen Jiao was known for his eloquent speech, and people of his time praised his skillful tongue. I say that the goddess's eloquence is due to the author's skillful writing. — Li Yu of Shangyu respectfully annotated

    Mr. Wei

    Prostitutes, actors, and others in eight categories are held in even greater contempt in certain counties. The children of those who fall into these categories are not guaranteed to receive government stipends if they do not form mutual agreements with local children, and scholars consider teaching these people a disgrace. During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, there was a wealthy man named Wei who fell into two of these categories but had a large fortune and three sons. He used a large sum of money to hire a bright child to teach them. Whenever nearby families of scholars had celebratory or mourning events, he generously gave gifts but did not include himself in the guest list. Moreover, when he heard that scholars were having a drinking party, he always respectfully prepared food and wine and sent someone to deliver it, causing scholars to jokingly say that they didn't need to bring much food and wine, as Mr. Wei would definitely provide something. Indeed, this was the case, and such occurrences happened dozens of times. In a certain place, there are many maple trees, and after autumn, the leaves turn red, making it quite beautiful. Scholars agreed on a date to go out together to enjoy the scenery. On the appointed day, about ten or more people attended the gathering, and Mr. Wei's provision was more abundant than usual.

    98

    遂相谓曰：“某伟具馔已非一次，设渠有事相烦，吾等代谋之，亦不为素餐也。”遂令送馔之人将某呼至。佥曰：“汝来矣，可坐而饮。”某曰：“诸位在此，焉有小人坐处？”曰：“汝不坐，可立饮几杯。”某如命饮毕。佥曰：“屡饶盛馔，于心不安，倘汝有不能为之事，可明言之，吾等竭力玉成。”某曰：“无他事。犬子三人长及弱冠，欲烦诸位代请一师教之，不知可否？”众闻之，皆有难色。一人曰：“于先生其可乎？虽身居进士而家计维艰，婉言之，当必允从。”众曰：“可。

    Explanation

    98

    They then said to each other, "Master Wei has prepared feasts more than once. If he has something to trouble us, we will plan for him on our behalf, and it will not be in vain." They then ordered the person who brought the feast to call Master Wei. All said, "You have come, you can sit and drink." Master Wei said, "With all of you here, how could there be a place for a lowly person to sit?" They replied, "If you don't sit, you can stand and drink a few cups." Master Wei drank as commanded and finished. All said, "We have often provided you with abundant food, and it makes us uneasy. If you have something you cannot do, please speak clearly, and we will do our best to help you." Master Wei said, "I have nothing else. My three sons have reached adulthood, and I want to trouble all of you to ask on my behalf for a teacher to teach them, is this possible?" Everyone heard this and all had a difficult expression. One person said, "Is it possible to ask Master Yu?" Although he is a jinshi, his family's finances are difficult, but if we speak gently, he will surely agree. Everyone said, "It can be done."

    99

    ”遂谓某曰：“汝在此等候，于先生庄违此不远，吾等同往，可立得佳音。”既至，谈延闲语，无敢倡言其事者。多时，于问曰：“君等无事，难得同来，何不言之，公同商酌？”一人曰：“先生居恒无事，设帐亦可破闷。”于曰：“无论无处设教，或有之，谁肯作曹丘生者？”曰：“有。”曰：“谁何？”其人欲言复止，佥曰：“言之先生勿嗔。”于曰：“众为吾谋，何嗔之有？”佥以某对。于不悦曰：“仆为某训子耶？”佥曰：“待贾而沽，不必苛择人家。

    99

    Then he told me, "Stay here and wait. Mr. Yu's residence is not far from here. We will go together, and you can immediately receive good news." Upon arriving, we engaged in casual conversation, and no one dared to speak about the matter. After a long time, Mr. Yu asked, "You all have nothing to do, and it is rare to come together. Why don't you speak about it and discuss it together?" One person said, "Mr. Yu usually has nothing to do. Setting up a lecture hall could also relieve boredom." Mr. Yu replied, "Regardless of whether there is no place to teach, or if there is, who would be willing to be like Cao Qiu Sheng?" Someone said, "There is." Mr. Yu asked, "Who?" The person hesitated to speak, and everyone said, "Speak to Mr. Yu without angering him." Mr. Yu said, "The group is planning for me. How could I be angry?" Everyone suggested that I speak. Mr. Yu was displeased and said, "Am I to teach the son of someone?" Everyone said, "Wait for the right price before selling, and it is not necessary to be overly selective about the family."

    100

    ”于再思曰：“是或一道，束脩五百金，其肯出也，仆即降心从之。”佥曰：“请归问之。”见某曰：“谐矣，书金未免过多。”某曰：“几何？”曰：“白金五百两。”曰：“不多，不多。”佥曰：“夫如是，汝归取贽敬，即日代为奉之。”某将行，一人曰：“贽敬从厚，若简则吾等代书可也。”某诺而去。众议曰：“今某之事，十两头不为薄也。”未几，某回，曰：“五十两不为薄乎？”佥曰：“不薄。”某曰：“尚别有奉恳，明年入学之日，敢烦诸位光陪。”佥曰：“固所愿也。饮酒小事，吾等可代奉贽敬去矣。”遂去。

    Explanation

    100

    Yu Zaisi said, "If that is the case, a gift of 500 pieces of gold, if he is willing to give it, I will comply." They all said, "Please go and ask about it." When they saw someone, they said, "It is agreed, but the gift of gold is not too much." The person asked, "How much?" They replied, "500 pieces of white gold." The person said, "Not too much, not too much." They all said, "In that case, you go and bring the gift of respect, and we will present it on your behalf today." The person was about to leave, and one person said, "If the gift of respect is generous, if it is simple, we can write it for you." The person agreed and left. The group discussed, "Now, for this person's matter, 10 pieces of gold are not too little." Soon after, the person returned, saying, "Is 50 pieces of gold not too little?" They all said, "Not too little." The person said, "There is still another request, on the day of enrollment next year, may I trouble all of you to accompany me with honor." They all said, "It is our pleasure. Drinking is a small matter, we can present the gift of respect for you." They then left.

    101

    春正，诸生果陪于公入塾，畅饮竟日，于亦心豫。凡先生馔，某必亲身伺候，食必以箸夹食物以进。忽忘而以手，于怒曰：“贱爪子，粘污食物，其谁食？”某唯唯，急以箸夹之以进。一日，于公谓学生曰：“晚夕园门内似有人行礼，其何以故？”曰：“老父谢老师之教。”于曰：“每日如此乎？”曰：“自上学至今，无间日。”于恻然曰：“去语汝父，今而后不必如是；每日饮馔，亦不必亲身奉进。”及清明，于谓某曰：“仆家居诸维艰，书金急需一半。”某唯唯；“三日后即用轿车往接，不可迟延。”某唯唯。

    Explanation

    101

    In the spring, the students indeed accompanied Mr. Yu into the school, drinking merrily all day, and Mr. Yu was also pleased. Whenever the teacher's food was served, I must personally wait on him, and food must be served with chopsticks. Suddenly forgetting and using my hand, Mr. Yu became angry and said, "You lowly hand, you've contaminated the food, who would eat it?" I responded humbly, "Yes, yes," and quickly picked up the food with chopsticks to serve it. One day, Mr. Yu told the students, "In the evening, it seems someone is performing a ritual inside the garden gate. What could be the reason?" They replied, "My father thanks the teacher for his instruction." Mr. Yu asked, "Is this done every day?" They answered, "From the time I started school until now, without a break." Moved with compassion, Mr. Yu said, "Go tell your father, from now on, there's no need to do so; every day, there's no need for you to personally serve the food." By the time of Qingming Festival, Mr. Yu told me, "I am having difficulties at home and urgently need half of the money." I responded humbly, "Yes, yes." "In three days, I will send a carriage to pick you up, do not delay." I responded humbly again, "Yes, yes."

    102

    于至家，见房宅焕然一新，极似出卖而为他人改作也者。问之家人，始知上学以后东家代为修理，今告竣尚未久也。于前后审视，约费千馀金亦未必如是坚固，且闻某按日供给，享用一无所缺。喟然叹曰：“待先生如此其忠且敬也，殆于不可之议，实蕴于方寸矣！”乃考期临迩，定期令某肆筵设席，于乃折简召邻近诸生，并请素相识之廪膳。既至，于曰：“仆之徒学成望售，烦诸位左袒之。”盖诸生非于年家子侄，即世交晚辈，谁肯有违言。于是三子同年入泮；十年之内会殿其二，而一领乡荐，皆于公循循善诱之功焉。

    Explanation

    102

    At Yu Zhi's home, I saw the house completely renewed, looking very much like it had been sold and rebuilt by someone else. I asked the family members, and they told me that after the children went to school, a neighbor had taken care of the repairs, and the work was completed not long ago. Upon inspecting the house thoroughly, I found that even if it had cost over a thousand gold coins, it might not have been as sturdy. Moreover, I heard that someone was providing daily supplies, and there was nothing lacking in the enjoyment. I sighed and said, "The master is treated with such loyalty and respect, it is almost beyond reproach, truly deeply felt!" As the exam period was approaching, I arranged for a feast to be set up, and Yu Zhi sent a brief message inviting nearby scholars and long-time acquaintances who received government stipends. When they arrived, Yu Zhi said, "My students hope to succeed in their studies, so I ask you all to support them." Since these scholars were either his relatives or close friends, none of them would have any objections. Thus, three of his sons passed the examination in the same year; within ten years, two of them passed the imperial examinations, and one was recommended for a higher examination, all due to Yu Zhi's gentle guidance.

    103

    虚白道人曰：谚云：“天下无难事，最怕心不专。”诚哉是言也！以犯款之家，转而为绅士宦门，未有不以为难者矣。而某竟以挥金如土得之，可知贱者亦不可自贱也。

    　　如规如讽。 汪雪马风

    　　某伟延师训子，行时时之方便；于公烦友左袒，作种种之阴功。师弟显贵，岂非从阴骘中得来哉！ 盖防如

    　　厚德食报亦宜。 黄琴轩

    　　待先生如此其忠且敬，其子之会殿也，宜哉！予尝见世之厚待先生者，其子弟每多发达；待先生刻薄者，其子弟往往不肖。是编可为请先生者作一箴规。 侯百里

    　　敬师如此，宜得美报。且古人有不循资格之说，此事可以恕论。 马竹吾

    Explanation

    103

    虚白道人曰：谚云：“天下无难事，最怕心不专。”诚哉是言也！以犯款之家，转而为绅士宦门，未有不以为难者矣。而某竟以挥金如土得之，可知贱者亦不可自贱也。

    The Taoist priest Xubai said: A proverb goes, "There is no difficult task in the world, but the greatest fear is lack of focus." Indeed, this saying is true! For a family that has been punished or disgraced to transform into a family of officials and gentry, it is something that no one would consider easy. Yet, someone achieved this by spending money extravagantly, showing that those of low status should not undervalue themselves.

    　　如规如讽。 汪雪马风

    　　某伟延师训子，行时时之方便；于公烦友左袒，作种种之阴功。师弟显贵，岂非从阴骘中得来哉！ 盖防如

    　　厚德食报亦宜。 黄琴轩

    　　待先生如此其忠且敬，其子之会殿也，宜哉！予尝见世之厚待先生者，其子弟每多发达；待先生刻薄者，其子弟往往不肖。是编可为请先生者作一箴规。 侯百里

    　　敬师如此，宜得美报。且古人有不循资格之说，此事可以恕论。 马竹吾

    　　Someone great invited a teacher to educate his son, always acting with convenience; in public affairs, he supported his friends, performing various good deeds. The teacher and his students became prominent and wealthy, which could not have come from unseen benefits, could it? Gu Fangru

    　　Those with great virtue should also be rewarded. Huang Qinxuan

    　　Treating the teacher with such loyalty and respect, it is fitting that his son passed the imperial examinations and achieved high office! I have often seen that those who treat their teachers well often have descendants who achieve great success; those who treat their teachers harshly often have descendants who are unworthy. This compilation can serve as a guideline for those who invite teachers. Hou Baili

    　　Treating teachers with such respect, one should receive a good reward. Moreover, there is an ancient saying that one should not be bound by formal qualifications, and this matter can be excused. Ma Zhugu

    104

    于先生诸般骄傲，某伟敬如神明，在正人亦难，况小人乎？ 余云川

    　　为方袍幅巾添多少声价。 冉星航

    　　小人未尝不欲自附于君子，使吴次尾、陈定生诸公稍宽，圆海南渡可无兴钩党之狱矣！是知元礼龙门之峻不如太邱道广之为愈也。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　于媪

    　　邑东鄙某村有于媪者，自女家归，筐携粮米数升，内有京钱八百。天炎物重，首汗如珠。后路有幼妇追及之，于视之，乃邻村王氏妇，因烦代提携。王从之。王行速而于迟，王曰：“吾于前村待之可乎？”曰：“可。”王乃先行，及于媪继至，粮内之钱已无有矣。于问之，王答言未见。盖此钱乃于女纺绩零星积聚，背夫周母，数虽无多，于视之真以一当百，以百当千，忽而失去，何苦如之！遂以骂代哭，势将用武。适邑侯叶芸士先生来自东，闻媪妇口角有故，遂呼而问之。于哭诉情实，真堪怜悯。视王氏，容貌幽雅，乡村美妇人也；暑衣袗綌，腰缠青蚨，隐隐外露。

    104

    Mr. Yu's various prideful traits, I, a certain Wei, revere him as if he were a deity. Even upright gentlemen find it difficult, let alone petty people? Yu Yunchuan

    How much prestige they have added to their plain robes and headbands. Ran Xinghang

    Petty people have never failed to wish to associate with the virtuous. If officials like Wu Cixi and Chen Dingsheng would be somewhat more lenient, the southern crossing of Yuanhai would not have led to the persecution of the partisans! This shows that the high gate of Yuanli is less beneficial than the broad path of Taixiu. Shangyuan Li Yu respectfully annotated

    Madam Yu

    In a certain village in the eastern outskirts of the county, there was an old woman named Yu who returned from her daughter's home, carrying several sheng of grain in a basket, inside which were eight hundred wen of capital money. The weather was hot, and the items were heavy; sweat dripped from her forehead like pearls. Behind her, a young woman caught up with her. Yu looked and saw that it was the wife of Wang from the neighboring village, so she asked her to help carry it. Wang agreed. Wang walked quickly, while Madam Yu walked slowly. Wang said, "Can I wait for you in the village ahead?" Madam Yu said, "Yes." Wang then went ahead. When Madam Yu arrived later, the money inside the grain had disappeared. Madam Yu asked her, and Wang replied that she hadn't seen it. These coins were gathered bit by bit by Madam Yu's daughter, secretly from her husband, to support her mother. Although the amount was not large, Madam Yu regarded these coins as worth one hundred wen for one wen and a thousand wen for one hundred wen. Suddenly losing them, what a great sorrow! She then cursed instead of crying, almost ready to use force. Just then, County Magistrate Ye Yuns, coming from the east, heard the argument between the old woman and the young woman and called them over to inquire. Madam Yu cried and recounted the truth, truly deserving pity. Looking at Wang, she was an elegant and beautiful woman of the countryside; her summer clothes were made of coarse linen, and she had a few coins tied around her waist, barely visible.

    105

    欲令男役搜寻，恐致羞愧，旁有古庙一所，遂于庙中鞫此事。令役呼地方至，使沽酒四两，以权权之，两数不足。遂将卖酒某传至，曰：“地方沽酒，与汝钱否？”曰：“如数交给。”曰：“钱既如数，奈何分两不足？欲加重责，怜汝乡愚无知，罚汝出京钱八百，不许少数短底，可速取来！”既取至，即将此钱面给于媪曰：“汝钱或忘女家，勿向王索也。”并遣去之。问某曰：“汝生意几年矣？”曰：“五年。”曰：“有外欠否？”曰：“外欠二百馀千。”邑侯曰：“讨要之不无小补。”遂按帐代索。嘱役曰：“乡农之家，恒无存项，有钱者如数清还，无钱者以粟折之，如有故违，传至重责。”未几，欠帐悉清。谓某曰：“罚出钱文，知汝负屈，今尚有怨心乎？”某呼青天而去。

    Explanation

    105

    The officials wanted to have male servants search, but feared causing shame. Nearby was an ancient temple, so they proceeded to interrogate this matter in the temple. They ordered the servant to call the local people, and instructed him to buy four liang of wine, using it as a standard to weigh it. The weight was insufficient. Then they summoned the wine seller, and said, "Did the locals pay you the money for the wine?" The seller replied, "I was paid in full." "The money was given in full, why is the weight insufficient? We wanted to impose a heavier punishment, but pitied you for your rural ignorance. We will impose a fine of eight hundred coins to be paid in the capital, and do not allow shortchanging. Please fetch it quickly!" When it was fetched, they handed the money directly to the old woman, saying, "Your money might have been forgotten by your daughter; do not ask the king for it." They then sent them away. They asked the wine seller, "How long have you been in business?" He replied, "Five years." "Do you have any external debts?" "I have external debts of over two hundred thousand." The county magistrate said, "Recovering it would not be without some benefit." They then reviewed the accounts and collected the debt on behalf of the old woman. They instructed the servant, "In rural households, there is often no ready cash. Those who have money should pay in full, and those who do not should pay in grain. If there is any deliberate violation, they will be severely punished." Soon, all the debts were settled. They said to the wine seller, "By paying the fine, we know you are wronged. Do you still harbor resentment?" The man called out to heaven and left.

    106

    文笔简净。 汪雪马风

    　　叶公以不忍人之心，行不忍人之政，其文亦与之俱传。盖防如

    　　此可谓法外意。 马竹吾

    　　昔钱穆决一滞狱，苏长公曰：“所谓霹雳手也。”钱曰：“安能霹雳手，仅免葫芦蹄。”芸士先生决此狱真所谓“霹雳手”也，不得以庸吏之“葫芦蹄”目之。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　李义

    　　新城李曰公，农人也。家虽不裕，而衣食不缺。年及立而无子，遂养异姓之子为己子，因名曰义。时年十四，令入外塾读。甫二年，义曰：“吾天资愚鲁，不能读书，愿作生意。”李以义年幼，不以为可。义曰：“先用十数千作本，无利则止。”李许之，遂给以本资二十千。义入市墟，视物价之低昂，贱则积之，贵则鬻之，二十年间，家业较昔大数倍矣。

    Explanation

    106

    Simple and Clean Writing
    Wang Xue Ma Feng

    Leaf Gong, with a compassionate heart, practiced benevolent governance, and his writings were also passed down along with his deeds. This can be called going beyond the law. Judge Qian Mu once resolved a difficult case, and Su Shi said, "He is truly a 'Thunderbolt Hand.'" Judge Qian replied, "How could I be a 'Thunderbolt Hand,' I am just free from the label of a mediocre official." Mr. Yunshi resolved this case and is truly a 'Thunderbolt Hand,' not to be labeled as a mediocre official.

    Li Yi

    Li Yigong from the new city was a farmer. Although his family was not wealthy, they did not lack food and clothing. When he reached adulthood at the age of 20, he had no children, so he adopted a child from a different family as his own son and named him Yi. At the age of 14, Yi was sent to a private school to study. After two years, Yi said, "I am naturally dull and slow-witted, unable to read, and wish to engage in business." Li Yigong, thinking Yi was too young, did not agree. Yi said, "First, use ten thousand coins as capital, and if there is no profit, stop." Li Yigong agreed and gave him twenty thousand coins as capital. Yi went to the market, observing the fluctuating prices, buying in bulk when prices were low and selling when prices were high. Over the course of twenty years, his family's wealth grew several times over.

    107

    初，李得义为子之后五年，亲生一子，以利名之。利渐长，不齿义，恒摈义不同食；义贸易买来食物，利不食。李嘱义勿买，义不听，利亦暗食之。利完婚后，利妻役嫂若婢，义妻毫无愠色。李尝安慰之，义夫妇同曰：“吾弟夫妇年轻，理合儿等多操作，即靡室劳、靡有朝，父不与闻可也。”李闻之甚喜。忽利欲与义各爨，李试之曰：“家业悉汝兄挣来，宜与之均分。”利怒曰：“渠非吾兄，何得与吾平分？略分家财，吾不禁，已待之极厚矣！”李不言。自此李不市业产，义劝之亦不听，义亦不知其父有何深意。利常言与义各居，李支吾至六七年。利渐仇视义，势难同居。李不得已，遂谓义曰：“汝弟欲与汝分居。”义曰：“吾弟欲之，亦可。”李欲言复止者三。义窥知父意，言难出口，曰：“分则分耳，产业等等吾分毫不要，自幼惯作生意，当不至饿殍。

    Explanation

    107

    In the beginning, five years after Li De Yi became a father, he gave birth to a son whom he named Li. As Li grew older, he began to look down upon Yi and constantly excluded him from eating together. Yi would trade and buy food, but Li refused to eat it. Li advised Yi not to buy the food, but Yi did not listen, and Li secretly ate it. After Li's marriage, Li's wife treated Yi's wife like a servant, but Yi's wife showed no sign of anger. Li often comforted her, and Yi and his wife said together, "Our younger brother and his wife are young, so it is reasonable for them to do more work. Even if they do not have a home to manage, do not have morning duties, the father should not interfere." Li heard this and was very pleased. Suddenly, Li wanted to live separately from Yi. Li tested Yi by saying, "All the family wealth was earned by your elder brother, it should be divided equally." Li became angry and said, "He is not my elder brother, how can he share equally with me? Divide the family wealth a bit, I won't object, I have already treated him very well!" Li did not speak. From then on, Li stopped buying property, and Yi advised him, but he did not listen. Yi also did not know what his father's deeper intentions were. Li often spoke of living separately from Yi, and Li hesitated for six or seven years. Li gradually came to view Yi with hostility, and it was difficult for them to live together. Li had no choice but to tell Yi, "Your younger brother wants to live separately from you." Yi said, "If my younger brother wants to, then it is fine." Li wanted to say something but stopped three times. Yi sensed his father's intention and found it hard to speak, saying, "If we are to separate, I will not take any part of the property. I have been accustomed to doing business since childhood, I will not starve to death."

    108

    但乞吾弟给吾住处，使妻子不至露地宿，已不胜铭感矣！”言之不禁酸楚。李言：“不必伤悲，吾自有以处之。”谓利曰：“给汝兄住宅一所，财物若干，犹不足十分之一也，汝愿之乎？”利尚有吝意而勉应之。李复曰：“家财既不平分，吾生养死葬之事，悉与汝兄无与乎？”曰：“渠非吾兄，何用渠？”李即使之各居。及数日，李见义闲居，遂特造义所。义竭力供奉，欢若平素。

    Explanation

    108

    But I beg my younger brother to give me a place to stay, so that my wife and children do not have to sleep in the open. I would already be overwhelmed with gratitude!" Speaking these words, he could not help but feel a pang of sorrow. Li said, "There is no need to be sad; I have my own way of dealing with it." Turning to Li, he said, "Give your elder brother a house and some property; it is still not even one-tenth of what I could give. Do you wish for it?" Li, though hesitant, agreed reluctantly. Li then said, "Since the family property is not divided equally, all matters concerning my upbringing and burial, are they entirely unrelated to your elder brother?" Li replied, "He is not my elder brother, why should I use him?" Li then had them live separately. After several days, Li saw Yi living in peace, so he specially visited Yi's place. Yi did his best to serve, and they were as happy as usual.

    109

    李曰：“利不弟，皆吾溺爱所致，得勿有怨心乎？”义曰：“娶妻生子，恩同昊天，怨何敢有？”曰：“汝连日家居，无本作生意乎？”曰：“儿朋友尚多，可通假而理生意。”李曰：“虽然，亦需资本。吾连年不值产业，积白镪若干，可敌汝弟家产三分之一，寄埋在此，俟夜静无人可取而用之。”遂指示其处。李酒后泣曰：“利不肖，渐肆饮赌，吾死后必不能守成。可念吾养育之恩，无令转乎沟壑，死亦瞑目矣！”义慨然曰：“父即无是嘱，断不能视弟如路人。”于是李约五六日辄诣义家，后直五六日一归利所。

    Explanation

    109

    Li said, "Li, my younger brother, your misfortunes are all due to my excessive indulgence. Do you have any resentment?" Yi replied, "Marrying and having children, the gratitude is as vast as the sky. How could I dare to harbor resentment?" Li said, "You have been at home for days without capital to conduct business?" Yi said, "I still have many friends who can lend me money to conduct business." Li said, "Even so, you still need capital. I have not been fortunate with my property for many years, and I have accumulated a certain amount of silver, which can rival one-third of your younger brother's estate. It is buried here for safekeeping. Wait until night when it is quiet and no one is around to retrieve and use it." He then pointed out the location. After drinking, Li wept and said, "Li is unworthy, gradually indulging in drinking and gambling. After my death, he will certainly not be able to maintain the family's fortunes. Think of my nurturing kindness, do not let him be reduced to the state of a beggar. Even in death, I can rest in peace!" Yi said resolutely, "Even if the father had not given this instruction, I would not be able to treat my younger brother like a stranger." Thus, Li agreed to visit Yi's house every five or six days, and later, he would go to Li's place once every five or six days.

    110

    未几，李以病故。利见义生意兴隆，攀令平摊殡资，义从之。殡后，利资无着，兼有酒博之债，乃伪货地于义，得价而不与成契，曰：“俟后加利奉还。”义亦不与理较，曰：“吾知此而故为者，不敢预以无信待吾弟也。”利游惰不事事，兼且大肆饮赌，复欲出地于义。义曰：“非某作中不可。”盖某者利之所畏，而实义之相好者也。未十年，除住宅外，利之产业荡然无所存，而归于义者十之八九。一日，义妻谓义曰：“利弟家一日一餐难，可少恤之。”义可之，遂以为常。

    Explanation

    110

    Not long after, Li died of illness. Li saw Yi's business thriving and asked Ping to share the funeral expenses. Yi agreed. After the funeral, Li had no means of support and had debts from gambling. He then pretended to sell land to Yi, received payment but did not give a written contract, promising to return the money with interest later. Yi did not argue about it, saying, "I know this and did it intentionally; I dared not treat my younger brother without trust." Li was idle and did nothing, and also engaged in excessive drinking and gambling. He again wanted to sell land to Yi. Yi said, "It cannot be done without someone." That person was someone Li feared, but was actually someone Yi was close to. Not ten years later, except for the house, Li's property was completely gone, and most of it belonged to Yi. One day, Yi's wife said to Yi, "Li's family struggles to have one meal a day; can you show some compassion?" Yi agreed, and it became a regular practice.

    111

    利以此时至义家代理家务，井井有条，义亦甚喜。义欲佣一饭妪，利曰：“弟妇其可。”于是利夫妇代义操作若仆妪焉。及数年，时值阴雨，兄弟借酒谈衷曲。义曰：“设令产业如昔，弟仍不能老守田园？”利曰：“彼一时，此一时也。今弟有十分之一，自能存活矣。”义曰：“若然，弟之产业货于吾者悉在。今收成在即，汝夫妇可即归，预备收获。农器牲畜可暂取用于此，从容渐置可也。且吾有此心久矣，有其心而无其事者，盖恐弟性未定耳。今既定矣，勿庸疑议。”利复旧业，循分度日，依然成安乐之家。今闻义、利悉卒，其子侄怡怡如胞焉。

    Explanation

    111

    At the time, Li arrived at Yi's household to manage the household affairs, which he did in an orderly manner, and Yi was very pleased. Yi wanted to hire an old woman for cooking, and Li said, "Your wife would be suitable for the job." Thus, Li and his wife took over Yi's household chores as if they were servants. After several years, during a rainy season, the brothers drank wine and talked about their inner feelings. Yi asked, "If our property were as it was before, would Brother still not be able to stay in the countryside?" Li replied, "That was then, and this is now. Now that Brother has one-tenth of the property, he can sustain himself." Yi said, "If that is the case, all the property of Brother that was sold to me is still here. Now that the harvest is imminent, you and your wife can return immediately to prepare for it. Agricultural tools and livestock can be temporarily taken from here, and gradually acquired over time. Moreover, I have had this thought for a long time. I have the intention but not the action because I feared that Brother's character was not yet stable. Now that he is stable, there is no need for doubt or discussion." Li resumed his old business, lived within his means, and once again became a happy family. Now that Yi and Li have both passed away, their nephews and nieces are as close as brothers and sisters.

    112

    虚白道人曰：奇哉！义也。于养育之父，而厚恤其子，为奇；以异父之弟，前曾刻薄相待，乃举所货弟产业如数让给之，更奇。然岂过分哉，惟仁人君子能之耳。

    　　读之令人感叹不置。 汪雪马风

    　　李公可谓义利分明。 黄琴轩

    　　《五代史》有《义儿传》，义而不义，负义之名者多矣。为李义者，完得义字分量；记李义者，写出义字胸怀。有此事不可无此文。 马竹吾

    Explanation

    112

    The White Void Hermit said: How extraordinary! This is righteousness. To show great care for a stepchild, raised by a father, is remarkable; to transfer the entire property acquired from a stepbrother, whom one had previously treated harshly, is even more remarkable. Yet, is this excessive? Only benevolent and virtuous men can do such things.

    Reading this makes one unable to stop sighing. Wang Xue Ma Feng

    Mr. Li can be said to have a clear distinction between righteousness and profit. Huang Qin Xuan

    The "History of the Five Dynasties" has a "Biography of the Righteous Son," where righteousness is not always truly righteous, and many bear the name of righteousness. To fully embody the weight of the character "义" in the case of Li, and to write out the heart and mind of righteousness—there cannot be such an event without this text. Ma Zhu Wu

    113

    义自义，利自利，亦已各行其是矣。卒之谋利之利，竟成为向义之利，是则义之以义为利，而不以利为利所致也。义利之分，如是如是。 秦次山

    　　维系纲常，主持名教，有功世道之文，可作宋儒语录读。竹吾马先生评尤允。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　养子胜儿，义兄恤弟，讽世何深焉。 渔樵散人志

    　　应 富 有

    　　应有，字富有，福建泉州人，少年拔贡，遐迩知名。居诸清苦，以设帐为生。夫设帐谋馆，谋之臧则喜形于色，谋之否则热生于中；得局如田禾之逢雨，失馆似秋草之经霜，天下事未有苦于此者。而有性鲠直，不屑烦人代谋，是以至残腊尚未有局，家徒壁立，甑冷囊空，困苦异常。一日，夜寝不寐，偶思晨炊无米，忽闻鸡唱，反恨鸣之甚早。妻宗氏曰：“鸡既鸣矣，明星有烂，君可以兴。”有曰：“案头诗韵不能换朝餐，早起何为？”宗曰：“东邻某尚欠女工钱二百文，可取来以济然眉之急。”有遂取之籴米。

    Explanation

    113

    Righteousness is righteousness, profit is profit, each following its own path. Ultimately, the profit from pursuing profit becomes a form of righteousness, which is due to valuing righteousness as profit rather than profit as profit. The distinction between righteousness and profit is clear, as it is. Qin Zishan

    Maintaining the norms of society and leading the moral teachings, writings that benefit the world and its ways can be read like the sayings of Song dynasty Confucian scholars. Master Ma of Zhugu evaluates this especially aptly. Li Yu from Shangyuan respectfully annotates

    Raising a son named Sheng, the righteous elder brother cares for his younger brother, how profound is the satire of the world. Fisherman and woodcutter record

    Ying You, courtesy name Fu You, is from Quanzhou, Fujian province, a young man selected as a juren (a scholar-official), known far and wide. Living in poverty, he makes a living by teaching. Teaching and seeking a position, if the plan is good, joy shows on the face; if the plan fails, anxiety arises in the heart. Getting a position is like crops encountering rain, losing a position is like autumn grass encountering frost, there is nothing in the world more difficult than this. Yet he has an upright and straightforward nature, not willing to bother others to help find work, therefore, by the end of the year, he still has no position, the house is empty except for the walls standing, the pot is cold and the bag is empty, extremely difficult and hard. One day, sleepless at night, occasionally thinking about having no rice for breakfast, suddenly hearing the rooster crow, hating that it crows too early. His wife, Zong, said, "The rooster has crowed, and the stars are bright, you can get up." He said, "The poetic rhythm on the desk cannot be exchanged for breakfast, why get up early?" Zong said, "The neighbor owes two hundred coins for sewing work, you can take it to relieve the urgent need." He then took it to buy rice.

    114

    宗炊饭将熟，适值屋塌，满釜灰尘，而釜亦为砖石击破。有呵呵大笑曰：“吾命何如此之穷也！”忽闻扣门声急，趋视之，乃表兄赵德盛，手牵大马，匆匆谓有曰：“吾事忙，不暇坐语。弟书馆定否？”曰：“尚未。”曰：“有一美馆，书金五百千，明春自来迎接，带来贽敬五两。”并帖交有，乘马而去。有执银、帖而入，满面春色。宗曰：“有何喜事？”有曰：“天无绝人之路。”遂向妻历言之。妻曰：“赵表兄物故数载，有何美局之能荐？”有方惊悟，曰：“舅氏之子，安有虚言？今虽已卒，其言可信。且有贽敬在此，不患卒岁无资。”及春正，友人闻之，皆言鬼言不可信，而有独笃信之。

    Explanation

    114

    As the rice was almost cooked, just then the house collapsed, filling the pot with dust, and the pot was also broken by bricks and stones. One laughed heartily and said, "How poor is my fate!" Suddenly, there was a loud knocking at the door. He ran to see it, and it was his cousin Zhao Desheng, leading a large horse. Zhao hurriedly told him, "I am busy, no time to sit and talk. Have you decided on the school?" He replied, "Not yet." Zhao said, "There is a good school, the tuition is five thousand coins. I will come to pick you up next spring, bringing a gift of five taels." He handed over the letter and rode away on the horse. He took the silver and the letter and entered, with a face full of joy. His brother asked, "What good news is there?" He replied, "Heaven does not close all doors." He then told his wife about it. His wife said, "My cousin Zhao has been dead for several years. How could he recommend such a good opportunity?" He was startled and realized, "My uncle's son, how could he lie? Although he is dead, his words are trustworthy. Besides, there is a gift here. There is no worry about lacking funds for the year." By the beginning of spring, friends heard about it and all said that the words of the dead are not to be trusted, but he alone believed it.

    115

    既望，无耗，有亦心疑焉。忽过午车马来接，薄暮始达，见一颁白者，盛服候于门。下车，揖让而入。甫坐，有曰：“先生尊字？”曰：“昨写去简帖，陈清虚即仆字，后以字作名，友人另送一字曰伴石。”曰：“先生高寿？”曰：“九十七矣。今岁令徒系仆二孙。”遂令出见行礼，一年十四，一年九岁。未几，盛馔肆设，酌酒下菜皆美婢，悉目所未经见者。筵终已二鼓，衾帐维新，就寝后，自忖东家施为，不解其为何许门阀也。

    Explanation

    115

    On the full moon night, nothing had happened, and even if something had, I would have doubted it. Suddenly, past noon, a carriage came to fetch me, and I only arrived at dusk. I saw an old man with white hair, dressed in fine clothes, waiting at the door. I bowed and entered. Just as I sat down, someone asked, "What is your courtesy name, sir?" I replied, "I wrote a letter yesterday, where my courtesy name is Chen Qingxu. Later, I used my courtesy name as my given name, and a friend gave me another courtesy name, Ban Shi." They then asked, "How old are you, sir?" I replied, "I am ninety-seven years old. This year, I made my disciples adopt my two grandsons." So, I had them come out to perform the ritual of respect. One is fourteen, the other is nine years old. Soon after, a lavish feast was set up, and the serving of wine and dishes was done by beautiful maids, all of whom I had never seen before. The banquet ended by the second drumbeat, and the bedding was new. After going to bed, I pondered what the host had done. I could not understand what kind of family they were.

    116

    嗣后常见前婢同二八女郎由斋门往来，从窗窥之，较群婢尤美艳。将及清明，赵忽至，应以疏远让之。赵曰：“吾在五阎罗王殿下为主簿，公事实繁，不敢计及私情。”应曰：“既为冥司主簿，人之寿数，宜了若指掌。”曰：“载载不爽。”应曰：“弟之眷属如何？”曰：“他皆无虞，惟现在弟妇病将不起，当急回家看视。吾先代向贵东言之。”言已竟入。既而仆夫整驾展軨而发，至家，宗氏固别来无恙也。未几，暴病，五日寻卒。殡事甫毕，东家遣人来接。应遂将门户器具烦邻佑看守，乘车而去。

    Explanation

    116

    Afterward, Ying often saw the maid moving back and forth with a young lady of sixteen through the gate of the study. Peeping through the window, he found her more beautiful and charming than the other maids. As the Qingming Festival approached, Zhao suddenly arrived, and Ying distanced himself. Zhao said, "I serve as the registrar under the five King Yamas, and public affairs are numerous; I dare not consider personal feelings." Ying replied, "Since you are the registrar of the underworld, you should know people's lifespans as clearly as if they were in your hand." Zhao said, "I never make mistakes." Ying asked, "How are my family members?" Zhao replied, "They are all safe, except my current wife is ill and will not recover; I should quickly return home to take care of her. I will first inform your master." After saying this, he entered. Soon after, the servant prepared the carriage and set off. Upon arriving home, Zongshi was indeed well. Not long after, she fell gravely ill and died within five days. The funeral arrangements were just completed when the master sent someone to fetch Ying. Ying then entrusted the household items to his neighbors to guard and left by carriage.

    117

    至斋，每念断弦事小，无后为大，不觉潸然泣下。念此等苦衷，穷而无告，惟赵兄系属至戚，复幽明殊途，不得已，于夜静无人时焚香默祷，冀赵辱临。比及三夜，赵忽至，曰：“吾弟连日盼望，愚兄以公事繁冗，不得应念而至，抚衷亦难自安。弟之心事，时挂胸怀，续弦之事，弟亦有素愿否？”应曰：“清贫如洗，纵有所愿，亦难遂。”赵曰：“试言之，无论贫富。”应终觉难以启齿，嘿嘿不语。赵曰：“贵东之笄女，弟见之否？”曰：“见之屡屡矣。

    Explanation

    117

    In the temple, whenever he thought about the minor matter of his wife's death and the major issue of having no heir, he couldn't help but shed tears. Reflecting on such hardships, being poor and having no one to turn to, he only had his brother Zhao, a close relative, who was deceased. Given the separation between the living and the dead, he had no choice but to burn incense and silently pray at night when no one was around, hoping that Zhao might appear. After three nights, Zhao suddenly appeared and said, "My younger brother has been looking forward to this for days. My older brother was busy with public affairs and couldn't come to meet your thoughts, and even when reflecting on my own heart, I couldn't feel at ease. Your concerns are always on my mind. Do you have any long-standing wish regarding remarriage?" He replied, "I am as poor as washed out, and even if I have a wish, it is difficult to fulfill." Zhao said, "Try to speak, regardless of wealth or poverty." He still found it hard to speak and remained silent. Zhao then asked, "Have you seen the young woman from the east side of the town?" He replied, "I have seen her repeatedly."

    118

    ”“愚兄为弟媒之可乎？”曰：“得此为妇，恨无金屋以贮。媒之不谐，恐招羞辱，愿吾兄自重。”赵有愠色曰：“似此异物，与结婚姻，荣莫大焉，岂有不谐之理！且愚兄为媒，谅亦不敢不从。”言已，负气入。未几，出曰：“谐矣！吾弟家中无人，可就此过门，俟解馆日携眷同归可也。”且即请择期，应低首不语。赵曰：“尚有不如意之事乎？”曰：“事固如意，但嫁娶之事，礼文浩费，恐一时力不及耳。”赵曰：“勿虞此，一切礼仪，兄悉任之，一文钱可不用也。”应曰：“若然，请兄代择佳期。

    Explanation

    118

    "Brother, may I act as a matchmaker for you?" he asked. "To have such a woman as my wife, I wish I had a golden house to keep her in," the younger brother replied. "If the match fails, it might bring shame; I hope you will take this seriously," he added. Slightly annoyed, the elder brother said, "To marry such an extraordinary woman would be the greatest honor. How could the match fail? Besides, since I am acting as a matchmaker, I am sure you will not dare to refuse." Saying this, he left in a huff. A short while later, he returned and said, "It's agreed! Your home is empty, so you can come to his home now. When the teaching period ends, you can bring your family back together." He then asked the younger brother to choose a good date. The younger brother lowered his head and remained silent. "Is there anything else that makes you unhappy?" the elder brother asked. "The matter itself is agreeable, but the rituals and expenses for a wedding are considerable, and I fear we may not have the resources at the moment," the younger brother replied. "Don't worry about that. I will take care of all the rituals and expenses. Not a single coin is necessary," the elder brother said. "In that case, please let me choose a good date."

    119

    ”赵曰：“月初即为夏季天月，德俱在甲，初五日甲午，午为月之明，星且为六合，兼合不将，是日嫁娶，吉莫如之。届期，愚薄暮即至，不误弟事。”至期，赵果至，袖出白金二百两为贺。时已燃灯之时，赵手指曰：“此处可以上灯。”而灯即上；“此处可以结彩。”而彩即结。凡应用之物，无不随手而具。未移时，内外焕然一新。应衣冠行礼，合卺后，出谢赵。赵曰：“弟今夜花烛，愚亦事忙。”遂辞而去。

    Explanation

    119

    Zhao said, "The beginning of the month marks the summer, with virtue concentrated on the day of Jia. On the fifth day of the month, which falls on Jia Wu, Wu represents the brightness of the moon, and the stars are aligned in a favorable configuration known as Lihe, making it both auspicious and harmonious. This day is the most auspicious for marriage. By the appointed time, I will arrive shortly before dusk, without disrupting your affairs." On the day, Zhao indeed arrived, taking out 200 taels of silver from his sleeve as a gift. It was already time for the lamps to be lit, and Zhao pointed and said, "Here you can light the lamp." The lamp was lit; "Here you can decorate with colorful ribbons." The decorations were put up. All necessary items were readily available. Within a short time, the interior and exterior spaces were transformed and looked brand new. The formal attire and rituals were carried out, and after sharing a wine cup, the bridegroom went out to thank Zhao. Zhao said, "Your wedding night is tonight, and I am also busy with my own affairs." Then he took his leave and departed.

    120

    应复入洞房，见新人红妆坐帐，群婢侍立左右，不觉失言曰：“吾何修而得此。”新人曰：“大丈夫之遭遇，朝为田舍郎，暮登天子堂。即食前方丈，侍妾数百人，亦分内事耳，况下此者乎？”应闻之，肃然起敬，曰：“吾过矣！吾过矣！”既而群婢皆散，应谓女曰：“叨列婚姻，未知世系，此属憾事。”女曰：“妾言之，恐君惊讶。”应曰：“即举家鬼狐，不妨明言也。”女曰：“即如君言，举家皆狐，而妾独非。”应问独非之故。女曰：“君同邑曾侍郎，实妾生父也。”应曰：“愿闻其详。”女曰：“所可详也，言之长也，容日细述可耳。”

    Explanation

    120

    Ying returned to the bridal chamber and saw the bride, dressed in red and seated in the bridal canopy, with a group of maids standing on either side. Unconsciously, he spoke out, "What virtue have I practiced to attain this?" The bride replied, "The fate of a true man can change dramatically; from a farmer in the morning to a minister of the emperor in the evening. Even eating with a full banquet before you and having hundreds of concubines to serve you is within the realm of possibility, let alone lesser achievements." Upon hearing this, Ying was deeply impressed and said, "I was wrong! I was wrong!" Afterward, the maids all dispersed, and Ying told the bride, "To be honored by marriage, yet unaware of your family lineage, this is a regrettable matter." The bride said, "I mention it, fearing that you might be startled." Ying replied, "Even if the entire family were ghosts or fox spirits, there is no need to hide the truth." The bride said, "As you say, the entire family are fox spirits, but I am not." Ying asked why she was different. The bride explained, "You and I are from the same town, and the former Censor, who was my biological father, is your fellow townsman." Ying said, "I wish to hear more details." The bride responded, "There are many details to share, but the story is long. Perhaps I can tell it in greater detail another day."

    121

    应在陈家设帐五年，妻已一胎生双子矣。一日，陈薄具酒酌，与应夫妇同酌共话，曰：“仆祖居湖北，家中尚有二子一孙，为女故，居此五载，今将旋归。且贤婿恶运已过，美运继至，车已雇妥，明晨可以早发。今具白金一千五百两为赆，五百赠女。五年书金支使有限，另具银若干在此，携带而归，可无恨鸡鸣之早矣。”应夫妇闻之，不胜酸楚。陈曰：“勿为此儿女之态也！时已薄暮，汝夫妇可急整行装，勿临时惶促。”应遂收拾细软并可携带之物，甫毕，车已到门。临行，陈以五色布袱授女，长短如被，曰：“履之，数千里之遥可顷刻而至。”遂授以咒语，曰：“勿轻用，勿传人。”已，立视升车，依依而别。

    Explanation

    121

    Ying taught at Chen's house for five years, during which his wife gave birth to twins in one pregnancy. One day, Chen prepared wine for a toast and toasted together with Ying and his wife. Chen said, "My ancestors lived in Hubei, and there are still two sons and a grandson at home. Because of the daughter, I have stayed here for five years, and now I will return home. Moreover, your son-in-law's bad luck has passed, and good fortune is to follow. The carriage has been hired, and we can leave early tomorrow morning. I have prepared 1,500 taels of white gold as a gift, and 500 taels are given to the daughter. The 5 years' salary and expenses were limited, and additionally, some silver is here. Take it back, and you won't regret waking up early." Ying and his wife were very sad to hear it. Chen said, "Don't act like children! It is already late afternoon, you two should quickly pack your belongings, and don't be in a rush at the last minute." Ying then packed his valuables and other items that could be carried, and just as he finished, the carriage had arrived at the door. On the way out, Chen gave the daughter a five-colored cloth bundle, as long and wide as a quilt, and said, "Wear it, and you can travel thousands of miles in an instant." He then taught her a spell, saying, "Do not use it lightly, do not tell others." After that, they watched as she went up the carriage and parted with reluctance.

    122

    是年，应举于乡。曾侍郎以父丧故丁忧家居，应妻陈氏欲往认亲，应阻之。陈氏曰：“天下有无父母者乎？君何阻之之不情也？”应曰：“何以知曾公为卿父？”陈曰：“生父中会后，私于邻村某观之道姑而生妾，弃于路旁，养父抱养于湖也。”应曰：“有凭证乎？”曰：“无凭敢冒认耶？”应许之。陈乃直造曾府，请见夫人。夫人问其来意，陈曰：“有诗一首，不解其意，特请大人指教。”乃以诗呈夫人。夫人视之，白绢帕一幅，上题句云：“早识生为累，何如汝勿生。抱来难割爱，捐去倍钟情。梦枉蛇祥叶，心期鸟覆成。他年如聚晤，持此证分明。”下书公姓名，笔墨是其手迹。

    Explanation

    122

    In that year, 应 took the provincial examination. Mr. Zeng, a former official, was at home in mourning due to the death of his father. 应's wife, Chen, wished to go and recognize her father, but 应 stopped her. Chen said, "Is there anyone in the world without parents? Why are you so unfeeling in stopping me?" 应 replied, "How can you know that Mr. Zeng is your father?" Chen said, "My biological father, after passing the imperial examination, had a relationship with a nun from a nearby village and had me. He abandoned me by the roadside, and my adoptive father raised me in the lake." 应 asked, "Do you have any proof?" Chen replied, "If I had no proof, how could I dare to claim kinship?" 应 agreed. Chen then went directly to Mr. Zeng's residence and asked to see his wife. The wife asked her purpose, and Chen said, "I have a poem that I don't understand, and I especially ask for your guidance." She then presented the poem to the wife. The wife read it, and it was a white silk handkerchief with the following lines: "Early on, I knew that birth was a burden, so how could I have been born? It was hard to part with me when I was brought back, but letting me go was even more filled with emotion. My dream was in vain, like a snake's omen, but my heart hoped for a bird's covering to come true. In the future, if we meet again, this will serve as clear proof." The poem was signed with Mr. Zeng's name, and the handwriting was unmistakable. The wife recognized the handwriting and the content, and she confirmed that Mr. Zeng was indeed Chen's father. The story ended with the family reunited, and the truth of Chen's lineage was finally established.

    123

    反覆寻绎，似为生女而弃之也者，究未知其原因，遂使侍婢以诗呈公。既而，公持诗来言曰：“是诗从何处得来？”陈曰：“小女生时，怀中有此。”公曰：“尚记汝之生辰乎？”陈曰：“小女得年二十三岁，养父言抱养时，适在是年闺七月初七日之晨。”公曰：“真吾女也！”遂谓夫人曰：“此弃诸路侧之女也。”夫人曰：“吾女肘后有红记如钱。”视之果然。盖道姑返俗归曾，即陈氏之生母也。曾夫妇大喜，如爱女之再生，改陈氏为曾氏，遂问抱养之详。曾氏止讳言陈公为狐，其馀一一细述。曾喜之不胜，遂谓女曰：“明日汝夫妇同来，如三晨谢亲之礼，万勿草草！”氏辞归。次日应夫妇盛服至，行翁婿礼，留之信宿，送之归。陪送之物载以后车数乘，应因而巨富。

    Explanation

    123

    After repeatedly pondering and analyzing, it seemed to be the case of a girl being abandoned, but the exact reason remained unknown. He then had a servant girl present the poem to him. Afterwards, the official came with the poem and said, "Where did you get this poem?" Chen replied, "When my little daughter was born, she had it in her arms." The official asked, "Do you still remember your birthdate?" Chen said, "My little daughter is now twenty-three years old. My adoptive father said we took her in on the morning of the seventh day of the seventh month of that year." The official exclaimed, "She is truly my daughter!" He then told his wife, "This is the girl who was abandoned on the roadside." His wife said, "Our daughter has a red mark on her elbow like a coin." Upon seeing it, it was indeed true. It turned out that the nun had returned to secular life and married into the Zeng family, making her Chen's birth mother. The Zeng couple was overjoyed, as if their daughter had been reborn. They changed Chen's surname to Zeng and then asked about the details of the adoption. The Zeng family hesitated to mention that Chen Gong was a fox, but they described the rest in detail. The Zeng couple was overjoyed and told the girl, "Come tomorrow with your husband, as if it were the third morning of a thanksgiving ritual, and do not be careless!" Chen bid them farewell. The next day, the couple came dressed in formal attire, and they performed the ritual of a father-in-law and son-in-law. They stayed for two nights and then sent them back. The gifts were loaded onto several subsequent carriages, and Chen Gong became extremely wealthy as a result.

    124

    一日，曾女归宁，见父忧形于色，问之母。母曰：“汝父在京时尝有错误，今忽得僚友信息，仇人某御史等将交章奏参，是以忧耳。”曾女曰：“是果无一法以处之耶？”夫人曰：“某尚书与汝父系师生，若通一信息，事可中寝。但在一二日之间，迟则无及。六千里之遥，一二日书信安能得到？”曾女曰：“此易事，女曾受仙人秘法，能驾五色祥云，送信京师，往来保不日暮。”夫人喜极，与女同见曾公言之。公虽半信半疑，事属紧急，姑为一试，遂令女治装。修书甫毕，女亦结束而出。

    Explanation

    124

    One day, Zeng's daughter returned home to visit her parents. She noticed her father's worry was evident on his face and asked her mother about it. The mother said, "Your father made a mistake when he was in the capital, and recently he received news from his colleagues that a certain official, who is an enemy, will submit multiple reports to accuse him, which is why he is worried." Zeng's daughter asked, "Is there truly no way to handle this?" The mother replied, "A certain high-ranking official is your father's former teacher and student. If we can convey a message to him, the matter might be stopped. However, it must be done within one or two days; otherwise, it will be too late. Six thousand miles away, how can a letter arrive within one or two days?" Zeng's daughter said, "This is easy. I have learned a secret technique from an immortal and can ride a five-colored auspicious cloud to deliver messages to the capital, ensuring I return before sunset." The mother was extremely delighted and shared Zeng's daughter's proposal with her husband. The husband, though half-believing and half-doubting, decided to try it out for now due to the urgency of the situation and ordered the daughter to prepare her luggage. As soon as the letter was finished, Zeng's daughter also completed her preparations and left immediately.

    125

    曾乃以书授女，见女以五色布袱铺地，跃身履之，忽化为五色祥云，飘飘而起，倏忽不见。曾女直造某尚书内宅，由空而降，婢媪共疑为仙。曰：“吾非仙人，请见老夫人，有急事禀白。”众引见之。时值尚书与夫人并坐，曾女自言身系曾侍郎之女，为父送信到此。尚书见信巳刻封寄，午初已到，不胜惊讶，曰：“令尊之书有一事未尝叙明，不好办理，且吾有他故请教，敬答华函，立候回音可乎？”女曰：“可。”于是持某书而南，得父书而北，复携某书而南，斜阳尚在西山也。曾公得书启视，内言事皆处妥，反忧为喜。由是曾益爱其女。后曾官至尚书，应之会殿、升任兵备道，盖曾力居多焉。

    Explanation

    125

    Then, Zeng gave the letter to his daughter. Seeing her lay a five-colored cloth on the ground and leap upon it, she suddenly transformed into five-colored auspicious clouds, rose lightly into the air, and vanished instantly. Zeng's daughter directly went to the inner residence of a certain Grand Secretary, descending from the air, with the maids and female servants all suspecting her to be a fairy. She said, "I am not a fairy. Please allow me to see the old lady; I have an urgent matter to report." They led her to see the Grand Secretary and his wife, who were sitting together. Zeng's daughter introduced herself as the daughter of Vice-Chancellor Zeng, who had sent her to deliver a letter. The Grand Secretary, seeing that the letter was sealed at the hour of the snake and had arrived by the hour of the horse, was greatly surprised and said, "Your father's letter has not mentioned one matter clearly, making it difficult to handle. Moreover, I have another matter to consult you on. I respectfully respond with a formal letter and await your reply, can I do so?" The girl replied, "Yes." Thus, she took the letter to the south, received her father's letter to the north, and then carried another letter to the south, as the slanting sun was still in the west. Upon receiving the letter, Zeng opened it and read that all matters had been properly handled, transforming his worry into joy. Therefore, Zeng loved his daughter even more. Later, Zeng rose to the position of Grand Secretary, responded to the palace assembly, and was promoted to the position of Military Commissioner, primarily due to Zeng's efforts.

    126

    虚白道人曰：观应公之性鲠直，而家窭贫，甚至家徒壁立，甑冷囊空，几疑一生无发迹时矣。然果终身穷困，人将以应公为口实，以为鲠直如是，宜为人所遗弃，而上达无期也。乃应公以校书为生，不屑烦人以代谋；以婚姻非耦，而劝媒者自重。如矢之操不易，生平之愿自遂，直道岂有妨于命数哉！

    　　应公是何等遭际。 黄琴轩

    126

    虚白道人曰：Observing Mr. Ying's character as straightforward and upright, yet his family is impoverished, to the point where the house has nothing but walls standing, the steamer is cold and the bag is empty, one almost doubts that there would ever be a time of success or advancement in his life. But if he were to remain poor and destitute for life, people would use Mr. Ying as a pretext, thinking that his straightforwardness is like this, it is appropriate for him to be abandoned by people, and there would be no hope of advancement. However, Mr. Ying made a living by copying books, he did not trouble others to make plans for him; because marriage was not a suitable match, and he advised matchmakers to take themselves seriously. Like the steadfastness of an arrow, his lifelong aspirations were fulfilled on his own, uprightness does not hinder one's fate!

    What kind of circumstances did Mr. Ying encounter? — Huang Qinxuan

    127

    书中自有颜如玉，吾闻其语矣，今见其人也。 盖防如

    　　变幻离奇处见造化，惨澹经营处见文心。 马竹吾

    　　晋傅长虞云：“酒色之杀人，甚于作直。”为酒色死，人不为悔，逆畏以直致祸；此由心不正直，故以苟且为明哲耳。读是篇而知正直之人固为神之所福者也。然正直如先生，而未为神之所福也，何居？ 上元李瑜谨注

    　　宋 蕙 娘

    　　乾隆壬辰，某抚宪奉旨登岱祭碧霞元君，至泰安，择日致祭。县尹某立即出示，凡遐迩进香之人，不许是日上山，且使人扫除殿宇，务令清洁。至期，县尹先行，复于大殿大肆陈设毕，始请抚宪拈香。甫进殿门，恰当礼拜之处有纸锞一堆。抚宪曰：“似此竟不除去！”县尹大骇，旋见神案上有单帖一纸，上书商河某里居幼女宋蕙娘遥祭。抚宪执向县尹曰：“既曰遥祭，则焚纸锞者并未到此，其中必有神佑。”转瞬帖、灰俱杳，抚宪不胜惊异。祭事毕，回省，札谕商河县查访其事。

    Explanation

    127

    Within the book, there is a beauty like jade; I have heard this saying, and now I see the person. This is a metaphor for the inherent value and beauty found in literature and learning.

    In the strange and marvelous transformations, one sees the work of nature; in the painstaking efforts, one sees the writer's heart. — Ma Zhugu

    Fu Changyu of the Jin Dynasty once said: "The killing power of wine and women is greater than that of being straightforward." To die for wine and women, people do not regret it, but they fear that being straightforward will bring disaster; this is because the heart is not upright, so they consider compromise as wisdom. Reading this passage, one knows that upright people are indeed blessed by the gods. Yet, although upright like the master, he was not blessed by the gods. Why is that? — Shangyuan Li Yu respectfully annotates

    Song Hui娘

    In the year 1782 during the reign of the Qianlong Emperor, a certain governor-general, upon imperial command, ascended Mount Tai to worship the Lady of the Azure Clouds. Upon arriving in Taian, he chose a date for the sacrifice. The county magistrate immediately issued an announcement, stating that people from far and near who come to offer incense are not allowed to ascend the mountain on this day. Moreover, he had the temple buildings cleaned, ensuring they were spotless. On the appointed day, the county magistrate went first, then set up elaborate decorations in the main hall, and finally invited the governor-general to light incense. As soon as he entered the main hall, there was a pile of paper offerings right where one would bow. The governor-general said, "How could this not be removed!" The county magistrate was greatly startled, and then saw a single sheet of paper on the altar, which read that a young girl named Song Hui娘 from a certain village in Shanghe had made a remote offering. The governor-general took the paper and said to the county magistrate, "Since it is a remote offering, the person who burned the paper offerings did not come here, and there must be divine protection." In a moment, the paper and ashes disappeared, and the governor-general was greatly astonished. After the worship ceremony, he returned to the province and issued an order to investigate the matter in Shanghe County.

    128

    盖有宋梦麟者，世居商河，居诸不裕，以训蒙为业。其妻忽得怪症，巫医穷于治术，惟坐视其死已耳。其女蕙娘，青春十四，每夜长跪院中祷祝，兼言若得母愈，亲身登岱进香，以报神庥。祷至半月，母病渐愈，一月而瘥。嗣女欲践前言，家中清贫，资斧无出，且无长兄可以作伴。女有堂叔某，每年赴泰安烧香，遂问之曰：“自脚下至岱顶，有几百里路？”其叔曰：“自此至省二百四十里，自省至泰安一百八十里，自泰安至山顶四十里，共计四百六十里。”女复问曰：“几百步为一里？四百六十里约有多少步？”其叔曰：“三百六十步一里，共计十六万五千六百步。

    Explanation

    128

    There was a man named Song Menglin, who lived generation after generation in Shanghe. His family was not wealthy, and he made teaching children his profession. His wife suddenly fell ill with a strange disease, and the doctors and shamans were unable to cure it, only watching as she died. His daughter, Huinang, was fourteen years old and prayed every night by kneeling in the courtyard, hoping that if her mother recovered, she would personally climb Mount Tai and offer incense to thank the gods. She prayed for half a month, and her mother's illness gradually improved, fully recovering in a month. Later, the daughter wanted to fulfill her promise, but the family was poor, with no money to spend, and there was no older brother to accompany her. She had an uncle from the father's side who went to Taian every year to burn incense. She asked him, "How many miles is it from here to the summit of Mount Tai?" Her uncle replied, "From here to the provincial capital is 240 miles, from the capital to Taian is 180 miles, and from Taian to the summit is 40 miles, totaling 460 miles." The daughter then asked, "How many steps are there in a mile? How many steps are there in 460 miles?" Her uncle answered, "There are 360 steps in a mile, totaling 165,600 steps."

    129

    ”女切记之，乘间告父曰：“登岱之愿，势难自还。叔言至岱若干里，共计若干步，女欲于院中周围来往自步之，步满其数，即为女已登岱焚香礼拜，以了其愿，不知可否？其父嘉其用心之诚，设想之奇，许之。女于是每日除朝饔午飧外，自于院落内步之。但莲步延迟，终日仅走一万馀步。日晚报步数于父，父代记之。六七日之后，足力不及，步数渐少。至十六日，其父谓之曰：“再走五千馀步，即足其数矣。”女闻之喜甚，次日早起急步，朝食为之不暇，至午后未初之时，已足其数。其父用红帖代书邑里、姓名，并神资同焚之。焚后，清风一度，其灰毫无所存。此孝女遥祭之事，其日时即抚宪登岱拈香之日时也。

    Explanation

    129

    The girl remembered this advice and took the opportunity to tell her father, saying, "The wish to climb Mount Tai is difficult to fulfill on my own. My uncle said it is X miles to Mount Tai, totaling Y steps. I wish to walk back and forth in the courtyard to reach the number of steps. Once I have walked the full number of steps, it will be as if I have climbed Mount Tai, burned incense, and worshipped, thus fulfilling my wish. Is this possible?" Her father praised her sincerity and creativity and agreed to her plan. The girl then began walking in the courtyard every day, except for her morning and noon meals. However, her steps were slow, and she could only walk about ten thousand steps in a day. She reported her daily step count to her father, who kept a record for her. After six or seven days, her strength was not enough, and the number of steps she could take decreased. On the sixteenth day, her father told her, "If you walk another five thousand steps, you will have reached the total." Hearing this, the girl was very happy and got up early the next morning to walk quickly. She was so eager that she did not even have time for breakfast. By late afternoon, she had completed the required number of steps. Her father then used a red slip of paper to write her hometown, name, and offerings, which he burned together. After the burning, a gentle breeze blew, and the ashes disappeared completely. This was the story of the filial daughter performing a distant sacrifice, which coincided with the day and time when the governor climbed Mount Tai and offered incense.

    130

    商河令查明备由呈详。抚宪见日时相符，知为孝心所感，不胜欣慕，遂以白金二百两赠蕙娘为奁资，且谕商河令使有以厚恤之。令亦以百金为赠。

    　　虚白道人曰：蕙娘所为之事不奇，然属在幼女则奇；奉父母之命为之不奇，然出自心裁则奇。其设想既出乎寻常，神默佑必见于格外。其事似无，其理实有之也。

    　　《书》云：“至诚感神。”观于此而益信。 张子澄

    　　语云：“时之所不生，念专者能取之；地之所不育，志笃者能出之；身之所不到，思诚者能致之。”蕙娘可谓征致有灵矣。 盖防如

    　　《书》曰：“至诚感神。”可见诚则未有不灵者。况以幼女而有此孝思，其心实发于至诚，其为神所默佑，固理之常，无足怪者。 侯百里

    　　天露其倪，巧于牖世。 马竹吾

    Explanation

    130

    The magistrate of Shanghe, after investigating and presenting a detailed report, found that the date and time matched, knowing that it was moved by filial piety. He could not help but be delighted and admired, and thus gave 200 taels of silver as a dowry to Hui Niang, and also ordered the magistrate of Shanghe to provide substantial relief. The magistrate also gave an additional 100 taels as a gift.

    虚白道人 said: Hui Niang's actions were not extraordinary, but they were extraordinary when performed by a young daughter. Following the command of her parents was not extraordinary, but acting on her own thoughts was extraordinary. Her ideas were beyond the ordinary, and divine silent protection must be seen as exceptional. The matter seemed non-existent, but the principle was actually real.

    The Book of Documents says: "Greatest sincerity moves the gods." By observing this, one believes even more. 张子澄

    It is said: "What time does not produce, those who concentrate their thoughts can obtain it; what the earth does not nurture, those with strong will can produce it; what the body cannot reach, those who think sincerely can achieve it." Hui Niang can be said to attract the divine. 盖防如

    The Book of Documents says: "Greatest sincerity moves the gods." It is evident that sincerity cannot fail to be effective. Especially for a young daughter to have such filial thoughts, her heart truly stems from greatest sincerity, and her being divinely protected is a natural course of events, nothing to be surprised at. 侯百里

    Heaven reveals its traces, skillfully opening a window to the world. 马竹吾

    131

    可与唐高愍女、宋童八娜并传。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　顾 道 全

    　　顾道全，山西灵丘人。业儒，应童子试，天资过人，且好读书。年十七，父母欲为完婚，顾不欲，曰：“入泮后未晚。”父母喜其有志，亦遂听之。县府试皆列前名，院试辄不录，科岁皆如是。年逾弱冠矣，父母强为毕姻，顾虽不欲，不敢再言。及奠雁届期，而顾出亡灵东界直省易州。易有富室黄成，顾与有倾盖交，因往投之。黄知其学业，遂留以训子。是年提学试易，黄子秀录顾改作课文，取为案元。顾欲辞馆他往，黄不许。顾曰：“师童而徒生，俗人视之，甚属不雅。”黄曰：“学问是学问，功名是功名，岂可以功名论学问哉？”固留之，顾乃止。黄曰：“仆京师有生意一处，房舍甚多，仆欲送先生与令徒到彼处用功，不识可否？”顾曰：“仆正欲到玉京一游。”黄送顾师生到京，遂为顾纳监，曰：“先生之文，既利小试，必利大场。

    Explanation

    131

    A Woman of Sorrow and a Boy of Eight, Together in Legend. An Annotation by Li Yu of Shangyuan

    Con Gu Daoquan

    Gu Daoquan was from Lingqiu, Shanxi. He studied Confucianism and took the examination for young scholars, possessing extraordinary talent and a love for reading. At seventeen, his parents wished to arrange his marriage, but he did not desire it, saying, "It is not late to enter the school after the age of 15." His parents were pleased with his ambition and allowed him to continue his studies. He ranked among the top in the county and prefectural examinations but was not admitted in the academy examination, and this pattern continued in the regular and supplementary exams. By the time he had surpassed the age of 20, his parents forced him to complete his marriage, though he was reluctant and dared not speak against it. When the time for the wedding ceremony arrived, Gu left his home in Lingqiu and went to Yizhou in Zhili Province. In Yizhou, there was a wealthy family named Huang Cheng, with whom Gu formed a friendship at first sight and thus went to stay with them. Huang recognized his academic abilities and hired him to tutor his children. That year, during the provincial examination in Yizhou, Huang's son, who was selected, recorded Gu's revised essay, which was chosen as the top entry. Gu wanted to leave and go elsewhere, but Huang refused. Gu said, "A teacher who is younger than his student, ordinary people would find this very unseemly." Huang replied, "Scholarship is scholarship, and fame and fortune are fame and fortune; how can one judge scholarship by fame and fortune?" He insisted on keeping Gu, who then stayed. Huang said, "I have a business in Beijing with many rooms. I wish to send you and your students there to study. Would you consider this?" Gu replied, "I am just about to visit the capital." Huang sent Gu and his students to Beijing and helped him enroll in the Imperial Academy, saying, "Your writing is advantageous for small examinations; it will certainly be advantageous for the major examinations."

    132

    敬为纳监，今岁与令徒同举于乡，以师生作同年，岂非衣冠盛事乎！”顾曰：“必如君言，始不负盛情矣。”黄闻场中应用之物，举为致办。场期临迩，黄令人将场具一一取来。顾笑曰：“如此场具，合以大车载之。”黄曰：“多乎哉？”顾曰：“十分之一已敷用矣。”及入场，顾与黄秀同号。顾曰：“何遇之巧也？”既而下题，六艺二诗悉出于顾生一人之手。及二三场皆同号，顾知其中有故，不在遇合矣。二三场之文，皆顾代作。三场既毕，顾谓黄曰：“学生所录之文，其中必矣。然细玩之，总不如仆卷绵密，其中当在仆名之后。”黄曰：“得中幸甚，前后一也。”顾曰：“仆论文之成色耳。”及放榜，黄秀高捷经魁，顾落孙山之外。黄心喜面悲，极为劝慰。顾曰：“借福泽为文章吐气，于愿已足。仆之不中，命也，于文何咎焉？”遂辞馆，黄留之甚力。顾曰：“自今而后，矢不读书作文，留之无益。”黄于是货车送之。

    Explanation

    132

    Respectfully, I have been admitted as a student, and this year I passed the provincial examination together with your disciple. To consider teacher and student as fellow examinees, isn't this a great event for the gentry?" Gu replied, "If it is as you say, then I will live up to your generous feelings." Huang heard that the items needed for the examination would be prepared and provided. As the examination period approached, Huang instructed someone to bring all the examination supplies. Gu smiled and said, "Such an amount of supplies would require a large cart to carry them." Huang asked, "Is it too much?" Gu replied, "One-tenth would be sufficient." When they entered the examination hall, Gu and Huang had the same examination number. Gu said, "What a coincidence!" After the questions were announced, all six arts and two poems were written by Gu alone. By the second and third sessions, they still had the same examination number, and Gu knew there must be a reason, not just a matter of chance. The papers for the second and third sessions were all written by Gu. After the third session was over, Gu told Huang, "The paper I recorded must have passed. However, if you look closely, it is not as detailed and thorough as mine, and it should be listed after my name." Huang said, "If I pass, I will be very fortunate, and it's the same whether it's before or after." Gu said, "It's just about the quality of the paper." When the results were announced, Huang passed the imperial examination with high distinction, while Gu failed. Huang was happy inside but sad on the outside, and tried very hard to console Gu. Gu said, "To use your good fortune to give my writing a boost, it's enough to fulfill my wish. My failure is my fate, and it has nothing to do with the paper." He then left the teaching position, and Huang tried very hard to keep him. Gu said, "From now on, I will never read or write again, and it would be pointless to keep me." Huang then sent him off by truck.

    133

    顾出京半日，顿觉饥渴，欲就野店买用饮食。至店甫坐，后来一轿车，坐一少年，丰姿秀丽，至店亦下车拂尘，既而搴帘扶一二八女郎下。顾视之，其生姿之美，国色也。顾素老成，魂魄亦为之飞越。既而，少年向顾曰：“先生何往？”顾答以回籍，曰：“似曾相识，竟记不清也。”少年曰：“昨乡试头场与君同号，何忘之耶？”顾曰：“是也，尊姓贺，万福其大名也。适从何来？”贺指幼女曰：“此小妹，昨因外祖家有娶妻之事，前往接轿，今接回耳。”顾曰：“字何清门？”贺曰：“尚未。”既而，沽酒谈心。

    Explanation

    133

    After leaving the capital for half a day, Cong suddenly felt hungry and thirsty and wanted to stop at a roadside inn to buy food and drink. Upon arriving at the inn and just sitting down, a carriage arrived later. A young man sat in it, with a handsome and elegant appearance. He also got out of the carriage and brushed off the dust. Then he pulled the curtain and helped a young lady, aged sixteen or seventeen, get out. Looking at her, her natural beauty was like that of a national treasure. Cong, usually mature and composed, was also captivated by her beauty. Then the young man spoke to Cong: "Where are you going, sir?" Cong replied that he was returning home. The young man said, "It seems familiar, but I can't remember." Cong replied, "Yes, your name is He Wanfu." The young man asked, "Where did you come from just now?" He pointed to the young girl and said, "This is my younger sister. She went to fetch the bridegroom's carriage for my maternal grandfather's wedding last night and is returning now." Cong asked, "What is her family background?" The young man replied, "Not yet." Then they bought wine and talked.

    134

    贺曰：“场中之事，六艺皆君自作，乃令徒高捷，君落第，岂非命乎？”顾曰：“学生中亦佳，可知非文之不足领荐也。”贺曰：“先生尚欲设帐乎？弟可为先生成一美局。”顾本不欲复蹈故辙，为女故，可借此为近芳容之阶级，遂曰：“既有美意，敢劳清神。且愿赁室一楹，存身以俟之。”贺曰：“寒舍即可下榻。”顾甚喜。贺曰：“日已向夕，道之云远，至舍下再谈可也。”遂算还酒食之资，各自升车，日暮始至。顾欲买饭自度，贺不可，食必与俱。一日设酒清谈，约邻生王某为陪，言及文章，顾侃侃而谈，贺与王心悦诚服。贺欲师事顾，顾不欲，遂结为友。凡顾改作文章，贺视如珍宝，曰：“真天下之奇才也！”一日，贺曰：“喜信报君知，来岁恩科已准。”顾曰：“矢不读书，况下场乎？”贺惊问其故，顾不言；亟问之，仍不言，贺乃止。

    Explanation

    134

    贺说：“考场上的事情，六艺都是你自己做的，却让别人高升，自己落榜，这难道不是命运吗？”顾说：“学生中也有优秀的人，可见这并不是因为文章不够优秀而无法被推荐。”贺说：“先生还想设馆教书吗？我可以帮助先生成就一番事业。”顾原本不想再重蹈覆辙，但为了女儿的缘故，可以借此机会接近她的芳容，于是说：“既然有美意，那就辛苦你了。我愿意租一间屋子，暂住等待。”贺说：“寒舍可以供您下榻。”顾非常高兴。贺说：“天色已晚，路途遥远，到了我的住所再谈吧。”于是算还了酒食的费用，各自登车，直到日暮才到达。顾想自己买饭吃，贺不同意，一定要一起用餐。一天设酒清谈，邀请邻居学生王某作陪，谈到文章，顾侃侃而谈，贺与王心悦诚服。贺想拜顾为师，顾不愿意，于是结为朋友。凡是顾修改的文章，贺都视为珍宝，说：“真是天下奇才！”一天，贺说：“好消息告诉您，明年恩科已经批准。”顾说：“我发誓不再读书，何况还要参加考试吗？”贺惊讶地问他原因，顾不说；再问，仍然不说，贺便不再追问。

    135

    初，顾之从贺而西也，原为贺万福之妹美。衷情无由达，思惟王生可作冰人，而交浅不可言深，因屡市肴酒与王畅饮。既熟，遂以情告。王曰：“可，姑为君作伐柯之斧。”王乃乘间告贺。贺使妻请妹至，曰：“客舍顾生，烦王生为妹作媒。论顾生之才学，中会如拾芥，因恨功名迟暮，矢不应试，不听规劝。”妹不语。贺复曰：“如顾生听人解劝也，未始不可与结丝萝。”贺妹曰：“未有不受劝之人，在劝之善不善耳。”贺闻妹言，知已意肯，遂与顾结婚姻，即贺室成婚。合卺之夕，如鱼得水。顾曰：“洞房花烛之乐，远胜金榜题名。”更置功名于度外矣。及过三、九、六日，贺氏曰：“闻君不事举业，胡为乎？”顾曰：“命薄。

    Explanation

    135

    At the beginning, when Gu followed He to the west, it was originally about the beauty of He Wanfu's younger sister. His true feelings had no way to be expressed, so he thought that Wang could act as a matchmaker. However, with their shallow acquaintance, he could not speak of deep matters. Therefore, he often bought delicacies and wine to drink with Wang. Once they became acquainted, he then revealed his feelings. Wang said, "Yes, I will temporarily act as an axe for you to cut the wood." Wang then took the opportunity to inform He. He sent his wife to invite his sister-in-law, saying, "There is a guest named Gu staying at our house. Could you, Wang, act as a matchmaker for my sister-in-law? Regarding Gu's talent and learning, achieving success would be like picking up mustard seeds. However, because he regretted that his fame and achievements came too late, he vowed not to take the exams and would not listen to advice." The sister-in-law remained silent. He then said, "If Gu listens to advice, he has never been unsuitable for marriage." He's sister-in-law replied, "There is no one who does not accept advice; it depends on whether the advice is good or not." Upon hearing this, He realized that she was willing and agreed to marry Gu. They married in He's house. On the wedding night, it was like fish finding water. Gu said, "The joy of the bridal chamber and the red candles far surpasses passing the imperial examinations." He placed his aspirations for fame and achievement outside his considerations. After three, nine, and six days, He's wife said, "Hearing that you do not pursue officialdom, why is that?" Gu replied, "My fate is thin."

    136

    ”氏曰：“妻随夫贵贱，君命薄，妾亦与之俱薄。然君文果人屡录之而售，君屡录之而不售，妾即与君乞丐终身，夫何憾！乃一试而辄诿以命数，顿弃前功，君何视君文太高，而视君命太卑也？”顾不语。氏复曰：“黄秀之录君文而中也，未必君文系必中之文，或渠福命应尔。何也？未试之他人也；君自作自录而不中也，不可信君文宜中而不中，或文中尚有瑕疵。何也？止君自谓必中也。一试不第，宜再加功苦，以图后售，乃竟顿灰心志，几于自暴矣。语云：‘男儿当自强。’君何不自强若是也？”顾面红过耳，无言可答。女复曰：“君是举也，不能扬名声以显父母，是无父子也；既是读书人，不能衣紫服朱，是无君臣也；谠言正论而不听，是无朋友也；不能得一官半职以封赠妻室，是无夫妇也。

    Explanation

    136

    The wife said, "A wife follows the status of her husband. Your fate is thin, and so is mine. Yet, if your writings are indeed recorded by Huang Xiu and succeed, while your repeated recordings and failures do not, I will beg with you for a lifetime, why should there be any regret? After just one attempt, you attribute it to fate and suddenly abandon previous efforts. Why do you look down on your own writing but undervalue your own fate?"

    He remained silent. The wife continued, "When Huang Xiu records your writing and it succeeds, it is not necessarily because your writing is destined to succeed. Perhaps it is due to his good fortune. Why? Because it has not been tested by others. When you yourself write and record it and it fails, it is not credible that your writing should succeed but does not. Perhaps there may still be flaws in the writing. Why? Because you believe it should succeed. After one attempt and failure, you should work harder to achieve success in the future. Instead, you have given up hope and almost abandoned yourself. The saying goes, 'A man should strive to be strong.' Why are you not striving so hard?"

    He turned red in the face and had no words to answer. The wife said again, "In this matter, you cannot promote your reputation to honor your parents, which means there is no father and son relationship. As a scholar, you cannot wear purple and red robes, which means there is no ruler and subject relationship. If you do not listen to sincere and correct advice, there is no friendship. If you cannot obtain a minor official position to honor your wife, there is no marital relationship."

    137

    人伦有五，君弃其四，斯时君应自愧死，尚高自位置也？当结亲之时，妾兄与妾商之，言君无志功名，不听解劝。彼时妾相君为翰院之才，遂曰：‘人未有不受劝者。’妾兄闻妾之言，遂以妾归君。今果执迷不悟，甘为庸庸碌碌之辈，妾诚有眼无珠，不足相天下士！且君曰命薄，自薄之也；妾之命薄，以君自薄君命而薄也。妾不惟君是怨，其谁怨乎？”言已，涕泣不已。及夜，顾寐忽醒，见灯明于室，妻已悬梁自缢。急起释放之，幸缢时未久，既而复苏。氏曰：“君救妾何为？妾请死，不为无气无火者之妇也！”顾复寝。氏复于暗中结绳，为顾所见，曰：“卿必欲自尽也？”氏曰：“妾以命与君作戏耶？”顾曰：“卿勿死，仆心志悉为卿移。”氏曰：“信乎否也？”顾曰：“决不诳汝！”氏乃反悲为喜。

    Explanation

    137

    The Five Bonds of human relationships are five, and the ruler has abandoned four of them. At this time, the ruler should feel ashamed and die, yet he still places himself in a high position! When we were getting married, my brother and I discussed with you, saying that you had no ambition for fame and glory, and you did not listen to our advice. At that time, I thought you had the talent to be a scholar at the Hanlin Academy, so I said, "No one is beyond persuasion." My brother heard what I said and thus gave me to you. Now you persist in your error without realizing it, content to be a mediocre and unambitious person. I truly have no eyes, unworthy to be the wife of a man of the world! Moreover, you say your fate is thin, belittling yourself; my fate is thin because you belittle your own fate. I do not only blame you; who else should I blame?" She finished speaking and wept uncontrollably. By night, I woke up from sleep and saw the lamp lit in the room. My wife had already hanged herself. I quickly got up and released her. Fortunately, she had not been hanged for long, and she soon revived. She said, "Why did you save me? I want to die, not to be the wife of an unfeeling and unresponsive man!" I went back to sleep. She again tied a rope in the dark, and I saw it, saying, "Are you determined to take your own life?" She replied, "Do I take my life as a game with you?" I said, "Do not die, my heart and aspirations have all been moved by you." She asked, "Is this true?" I said, "I will never deceive you!" She then changed from sadness to joy.

    138

    顾曰：“仆一用功，诸事悉置度外，夫妇之情疏，勿深怨也！”氏曰：“不惟不怨，君读而妾伺之；伺之不周，妾也任其咎。”顾于是读于寝室，顾寝氏始寝，顾兴氏亦兴。食无时，食则现成；饮虽频，饮无少待。顾曰：“卿真仆之贤内助也！”读及数月，顾忽拍案自言曰：“去年乡试之文，洵非必中之文。不中在文，非关命也！”氏曰：“妾言何如也？”场期不远，顾与贺同赴京师。既入场，顾与贺系前后号，易于传递。顾代作首艺，同中前魁。次年复为会试同年，而顾则馆选授翰林院庶吉士。顾回家祭扫省亲，至家，见一少妇立母侧，问之。母曰：“此汝嫡妻周氏也。汝出亡之后，汝父谓花烛之期断不可改，遂按日时过门，俟汝回家时再成大礼，谁意竟迟至四五年也。今晚行合卺之礼可耳。”顾视之，容颜与贺不分孟仲。顾以父母年高，遂告终养焉。

    Explanation

    138

    Gu said, "When I am engrossed in my studies, I set aside all other matters, and our marital affection may seem distant. Do not harbor deep resentment!" The woman replied, "Not only will I not resent you, but while you read, I will attend to you; if I fail to attend to you properly, I will take the blame." Gu then read in the bedroom, and both he and his wife began to sleep. When they woke up, they also rose. Meals were irregular, but always ready-made; drinking was frequent, and there was no need to wait for drinks. Gu said, "You are truly my virtuous helper!"
