After several months of reading, Gu suddenly pounded the table and said to himself, "Last year's provincial examination essay was certainly not one that must be selected. The failure lies in the essay, not in fate!" The woman asked, "How is my advice?" The exam period was not far off, so Gu and He went to the capital together. After entering the examination hall, Gu and He were in adjacent examination rooms, making it easy to pass notes. Gu wrote the first essay on behalf of He, and they both passed the exam and ranked first. The following year, they both passed the metropolitan examination, and Gu was selected to serve as a scholar in the Hanlin Academy. Gu returned home to pay respects and visit his family. Upon arriving home, he saw a young woman standing by his mother's side and asked about it. His mother said, "This is your legitimate wife, Zhou. After you went into hiding, your father said that the wedding date could not be changed, so he arranged for the wedding to proceed as planned, waiting for you to return home before completing the ceremony. Who would have thought it would be delayed for four or five years! We can proceed with the wedding tonight." Gu looked at her and found her appearance comparable to He's. Considering his parents' advanced age, Gu decided to stay home and care for them.

    139

    虚白道人曰：甚矣，人之不可自是也！如顾某之功苦，贾用不售，其不免于悲叹也宜矣。然文章无止境，当益求其奥妙；功名有定时，不可必之目前。乃以人录其文而捷，遂以己之不中归于命数，直谓命中无是功名，致欲尽弃前功，甘老林泉，岂不可惜！幸有贤妻以死劝之，遂致联捷，不然亦止为一时狂士耳。甚矣，人之不可自是也！

    Explanation

    139

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: How great is the danger of a person being overly self-assured! Just as Mr. Gu's efforts were arduous, yet his talent went unappreciated, it is indeed appropriate that he could not escape sorrow and lamentation. However, literature has no limits; one should strive to explore its profound mysteries. Success in career and reputation has its time; it cannot be guaranteed in the present moment. He attributed his success to others recording his writings and thus attributed his own failure to fate, simply believing that he was not destined for such success, leading him to abandon all previous efforts and content himself with a life in the mountains and forests—how regrettable! Fortunately, his virtuous wife, by sacrificing herself, persuaded him, and he subsequently achieved success. Otherwise, he would have remained just a momentary madman. How great is the danger of a person being overly self-assured!

    140

    令几试不售，遂尔焚弃笔砚，谓中式由命不由文者，读之自必憬然悟矣。 张子澄

    　　可以释躁，可以平矜，举业之金箴也。 马竹吾

    　　读“学问是学问”二语，可知进士不必优于布衣；观贺氏劝顾生之言，可见文人不必胜于女子。高傲、愤激均无所用。杨子厚

    　　薛居正举进士不第，为“遣愁文”以自解，寓意倜傥，识者谓其有公辅之量。读此文，足令康了秀才矜平躁释。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　张清

    　　德州东偏张清者，农人也。家嗜牛脔，世养宰牛。盖养宰牛者，市瘦牛而养之，肥则卖给回人，宰之而货其肉。其养之也，取牛之踏粪粪其地，其利尽在土田；其卖之也，取牛之肥贵倍原价，其利胜权子母。所养之牛，少则六七十头，多则百馀头，洵取民利之巧且忍者也。

    Explanation

    140

    Translation:

    Those who fail several examinations and thus burn and discard their writing tools, believing that success in exams is determined by fate rather than one's writing ability, will surely gain a clear understanding upon reading this. — Zhang Zicheng

    It can relieve restlessness and calm arrogance, serving as a golden rule for scholars. — Ma Zhuwu

    By reading the phrase "learning is learning," one can understand that those who pass the imperial exams are not necessarily superior to commoners; by observing the words of He Shizhi advising Gu Sheng, one can see that scholars are not necessarily superior to women. Arrogance and indignation are all useless. — Yang Zihou

    Xue Juzheng failed the imperial exams and wrote an essay called "Dispelling Sorrow" to console himself. The essay is full of free-spirited and unconventional ideas, and wise people believed he had the potential to become a high-ranking official. Reading this essay would certainly make the scholar Kang Le calm and peaceful. — Li Yu of Shangyuan, careful annotation

    Zhang Qing

    Zhang Qing, a farmer from the eastern outskirts of Dezhou, had a family that loved beef and had been involved in raising and slaughtering cattle for generations. Those who raise and slaughter cattle buy thin cattle and fatten them, selling them to Hui people when they are fat and then slaughtering and selling their meat. In raising them, they use the cow dung to fertilize the land, with all the profit lying in the land. In selling them, the profit from the fattened cattle is double the original price, even greater than that from lending money. The number of cattle they raise ranges from sixty to seventy to over a hundred, indeed making them very cunning and ruthless in taking advantage of the people.

    141

    一日，清在集场市一肥牛，其价甚廉。有一农人，知是牛力大调良，愿加原价二千以转市之。清不欲，复加二千，清仍不欲，农人乃止。清货于本集回民，较农人加价多得数百文。回以是集牛脔甚少而价昂，遂立杀是牛而货之。清持价归，其意得甚。甫至家，即有二役执票来拘，清曰：“谁人控我？”役曰：“汝自作之事，尚不知耶？”乃缧绁其项，牵之去。二役在路苛索差礼。清曰：“来时不容少迟，吾身边分文未有，奈何？”二役怒目曰：“当衙役者吸风度日耶？”清曰：“其理固然，吾岂不知？无已，俟结案回家时加倍奉酬。”遂格外多许之，役乃喜。路经一山，见山下有牛若干，口吐人言，向清索命。二役曰：“即为是案传来，于汝等明冤，未经过堂，汝等不得无礼。”众牛乃散。清始知已死。

    Explanation

    141

    One day, Qing bought a fat cow at the market. Its price was very cheap. There was a farmer who knew this cow was strong and well-trained, and he wanted to add two thousand to the original price to resell it. Qing did not want to, and the farmer again added two thousand, but Qing still did not want to. The farmer then stopped. Qing sold the cow to a Hui person in this gathering place, earning hundreds of coins more than the farmer. The Hui person thought the pieces of beef in this gathering place were few and the price high, so he immediately killed the cow and sold it. Qing carried the money home, feeling very pleased. Just as he arrived home, two officials came with a warrant to arrest him. Qing asked, "Who accused me?" The official replied sarcastically, "You don't even know what you did?" They then bound his neck and dragged him away. The two officials demanded extra fees on the way. Qing pleaded, "I don't have a single coin, what can I do?" The officials angrily retorted, "Do officials live by breathing air?" Qing promised, "If not, I will double the payment when I return home after the case is settled." So he promised extra, and the officials were pleased. On the way, they passed a mountain and saw several cows at the foot of the mountain, speaking human words and demanding Qing's life. Since you were summoned for this case, you must be innocent. Before the trial, you cannot be disrespectful. The cows then dispersed. Qing then realized he was dead.

    142

    忽睹都城，入城后，见一官衙势如臬署。及入，见王者怒坐堂上，一牛伏阶下。王令与牛对质。牛所控是实，王怒曰：“是为巧取人财，忍心害物，合受刀山！”万鬼群和，声如雷鸣。即有马面之鬼捽去。清见一山，极峻峭，上有利刃，纵横如密笋，山上之人皆剖肠刺腹。鬼促清上，清觳觫哀啼，退缩不前。鬼以巨锤击首，痛楚不堪。忽王命将清提回。清闻之，如获再生。清见王怒色转和，心少放。王曰：“汝所为之事，固无再生之理，因汝生时曾救母子二命，王嘉乃行，使汝还阳。务痛改前非，勿蹈故辙，不然冥责之惨终不能免也。”清唯唯。王使原差二鬼送之，至己门，二鬼曰：“前言不可食也！”清曰：“诺。”及入而苏，死已二日。遂起，立命家人市金银纸箔二块，速作冥资，亲于大门外焚之。

    Explanation

    142

    Suddenly, he saw the capital city. Upon entering, he saw an official building that looked like a judicial office. Upon entering further, he saw the king seated angrily on the dais, with a cow crouching at the steps. The king ordered a trial between him and the cow. The cow's accusation was true, and the king angrily said, "This person has cleverly taken people's wealth, showing no mercy and harming living beings; he deserves to suffer in the knife mountain!" The myriad spirits joined in agreement, their voices like thunder. Immediately, a horse-faced demon dragged him away. Clear saw a very steep mountain with sharp blades arranged like dense bamboo shoots, and people on the mountain had their intestines cut and bellies pierced. The demon urged Clear to go up, but Clear trembled, cried out in sorrow, and retreated. The demon struck his head with a huge hammer, causing unbearable pain. Suddenly, the king ordered him to be brought back. Clear felt as if reborn. Clear saw the king's anger turn to gentleness, and his heart relaxed a bit. The king said, "Your actions, although they did not allow for a second chance at life, were commendable because you saved the lives of a mother and child when you were alive. I commend your actions and send you back to life. You must deeply repent your past wrongdoings, do not repeat old mistakes, otherwise, you cannot escape the severe punishment in the afterlife." Clear nodded. The king sent two original demons to escort him, and upon reaching his own door, the demons said, "The previous words cannot be eaten!" Clear replied, "Yes." Upon entering, he revived, having been dead for two days. He then got up and immediately ordered his family to buy two pieces of gold and silver paper money, quickly preparing offerings for the afterlife, and personally burned them outside the main gate.

    143

    初，清见村妇围一少妇共相劝解，问之。一妇曰：“适见此妇来此坐地，闻其所抱之子哭声甚急，倏忽不哭。众妇疑之，急视之，见此妇以带围子项，时将勒死。妇言渠夫贸易于德，二年不归，抱子寻夫，迄今不见。资斧断绝，羞于行丐，将勒死其子而自尽耳。”此时尚多妇女劝解少妇，一妇曰：“不必多相劝，在此百劝百应，设移时彼至他处仍勒其子，谁常从之作解劝人也？”清闻之，曰：“是也。”遂问其来历。妇曰：“妾夫李智，济阳人。”清曰：“汝暂在吾家存身，俟旺月时吾送汝归。”少妇闻之，含泪顿首。妇在清家住及两月。是时，李智归，窥其室，不见其妻，遂踪迹至德。夫妇相见，清且稍为之赆，遣之归。清之救母子二命，盖此事也。

    Explanation

    143

    Initially, Qing saw a group of village women surrounding a young woman, trying to persuade her. He asked them about it. One woman said, "We just saw this woman come here and sit down. We heard the crying of the child she was holding, which was very urgent, but suddenly it stopped. The other women were suspicious, so they quickly looked and saw that this woman was about to strangle her child with a rope around its neck. She said that her husband had been trading in Dezhou for two years and hadn't returned. She had come to look for him with her child, but she hadn't seen him. Her funds were exhausted, and she was ashamed to beg. She was going to strangle her child and then take her own life." At that time, many women were still trying to persuade the young woman. Another woman said, "There's no need to keep persuading her. Even if we persuade her here, if she goes somewhere else, she will still strangle her child. Who will always be there to persuade her?" Qing heard this and said, "That's true." He then asked about her background. The woman said, "My husband is Li Zhi, from Jiyang." Qing said, "You can stay temporarily in my home. I will send you back when the time is right." The young woman, upon hearing this, bowed with tears. The woman stayed in Qing's home for two months. At that time, Li Zhi returned and looked into his room but did not see his wife. He then traced her to Dezhou. When the couple met, Qing gave her some money to start her journey and sent her back. Qing's act of saving the lives of the mother and child was indeed this event.

    144

    清苏后，力戒家人不食牛脔，将所养宰牛尽货于庄农使用之家；有回人冒市者，追回令卖。见州尊禁宰杀耕牛，以重农功而清盗贼事告示甚善，遂录之以戒子孙。其告示云：“照得农耕莫先于畜牧，屠杀实伏乎盗机。故连比赃窝，牛只自一以累十；详明条律，罪名由杖以至流。纵己物而宰于私，亦官刑而使之戒。典至肃也，令綦严也。近日以来，浇风寖盛，但图利市，恒昧本源。夫卖剑而买之者，为犁雨耕云之助；而鼓刀而割之者，启逾墙穿穴之萌。则有大胆回民，横行土棍，借汤锅以为召号，收鬻贩而聚朋徒。犉九十以何多，糁生饿眼；法三千而罔畏，狠积刚肠。弗顾邑灾，惟恃庖丁善解；竟同蹊夺，何论犁子为騂。方待时而易田畴，乃乘间而来草窃。求售贱价，任他来路不明；韬匿残皮，直欲化赃灭迹。

    Explanation

    144

    After the Qing dynasty, one strictly prohibited family members from eating beef and sold all the cattle raised to farmers for use. If a Muslim sold meat in the market, they were pursued and ordered to sell it. Seeing that the local official banned the slaughter of oxen to emphasize agricultural importance and clear up theft, the notice was very good, so it was recorded to warn descendants. The notice read: "It is known that agriculture cannot precede animal husbandry, and slaughter indeed harbors the potential for theft. Therefore, those involved in accumulating stolen goods, from one ox to ten, are subject to severe penalties; the laws clearly state that the punishment ranges from corporal punishment to exile. Even if the ox belongs to oneself and is slaughtered privately, it is still subject to official punishment and serves as a warning. The regulations are extremely strict, and the orders are very stringent. Recently, the风气 has become increasingly corrupt, focusing only on profit and market gains while neglecting the fundamental principles. Those who sell swords and buy them again are aiding in the plowing of rain and clouds; those who wield knives and cut are opening the way for theft and burglary. There are bold Hui people and rowdy local ruffians who use the excuse of boiling meat to gather followers and accumulate accomplices. How many cattle are there, and how many hungry eyes are there; how many laws are there, and how many hardened hearts are there. They do not care about the disaster in the county, relying only on the butcher's skill; they act like thieves, how can they distinguish between oxen and horses. They wait for the right time to change the fields, then take advantage of the opportunity to steal. They sell at low prices, regardless of whether the source is clear; they hide the remaining skin, intending to erase the evidence of theft.

    145

    遂令以力济人之物，血洒肉飞；因有忍心害理之人，架供案给。观其觳觫，匪惟喘月堪惊；攘及牺牲，岂止逸风足虑。犯科最巨，设禁宜严。为此示仰汉回人民等知悉，大武有一元之目，太牢非馈食之常。即美珍特重炙心，然无故奚容胾脔。戒生灵之恣杀，寿验歧胡；祛隐器之梯媒，卧安春暖。马帷狗盖，推施博爱之仁；鼠社狐城，屑弭祷张之幻。自示以后，务各改移故辙，洗涤前愆。毁尔灶煁，静尔砧斧。黄犊不赍于盗，乌犍得老息于农。庶毕来既升，可佐十千之耦；虽赏不窃，何虞三五之群。红杏村头，深播一犁甘雨；绿杨堤畔，斜冲两角晴烟。将与我民演乡教之祝辞，绘太平之景象。薄言观者，岂不懿哉！倘敢桀骜顽梗，不我聪听，一经查访拘拿，定行重处。与噬脐而靡及，盍善刀而深藏。凛遵毋违，恺切特示。”自清至今，盖已三世，世世温饱，闻今已有功名焉。

    Explanation

    145

    There are now people with cruel hearts who harm reason, and they set up offerings on tables. Observing their trembling, not only is their breathing frightening, but seizing sacrificial animals is not just a breeze worth worrying about. The transgression is the most severe, and the prohibition should be strict. Therefore, this edict is intended to inform the Han and Hui people and others. The Great Wu has one eye, and the Great Sacrifice is not a regular offering. Even if precious delicacies are specially roasted, without a reason, how can it be cut into large pieces? To prevent the wanton killing of living beings, test the longevity of different groups; remove hidden traps and lures, and rest in peace and warmth. Promote broad love and kindness, and dispel the illusions of prayer and incantation. From now on, everyone must change their old ways, cleanse past sins, destroy their hearths and stoves, and quiet their chopping blocks and axes. Yellow calves will not be stolen, and black oxen can rest in peace on farms. May all come and ascend, to assist ten thousand couples; even if rewards are given, they will not be stolen, how can there be worry about groups of three or five. At the head of the red apricot village, deeply sow a plow of sweet rain; by the green willow embankment, slant and rush with two horns of clear smoke. Present to my people the blessings of village education, and depict the scene of peace. Those who see it, how excellent it is! If anyone dares to be rebellious and stubborn, not listening to me, once investigated and apprehended, they will definitely be severely punished. Like gnawing at one's navel and being unable to reach, why not sharpen the knife and hide it deeply. Strictly follow and do not violate, sincerely and particularly shown. From the Qing dynasty to the present, it has been three generations, and every generation has enjoyed prosperity. It is said that some have already achieved fame and success.

    146

    虚白道人曰：赏不僭而刑不滥，神道称至公焉。见清之恶作，即加以刀山之刑；闻清有善行，立示以还阳之路：不以见劝赏畏刑之至意乎？及清顿改前恶，神福其后人，神之嘉人之改过自新也尤至。

    Explanation

    146

    The Virtual White Daoist said: "Rewards are not excessive and punishments are not excessive; the divine path is called the utmost fairness. When the clear sees evil actions, they immediately impose the punishment of the knife mountain; when the clear hears of good deeds, they immediately show the way back to life: is this not the utmost intention to encourage rewards and deter punishments? When the clear suddenly reforms their previous evil, the gods bless their descendants; the gods especially commend people who repent and reform themselves."

    147

    读之足令屠牛食牛者不寒而栗，至告示一篇，尤为剀切。 张子澄

    　　“牢”从“牛”，“狱”从“犬”，不食牛犬，牢狱可免。 盖防如

    　　牛乃上天玄武之精，下土犬牢之气，非郊祀不敢用，非天神不敢歆，则牛固非民间所宜食也。况犁万顷之田，有功于世；龁三春之草，无害于人。安忍既食其力，复食其肉哉！乃耕云喘月，陇头之血汗未干；刳骨剥皮，庖中之肢体已解。可哀也！夫若地方官严禁缉拿，使屠户知警，固可造福无穷。即士民相戒勿食，亦可永免牢狱，岂不善哉！是文剀切痛快，洵有功世道，当急付剞劂，以劝世人。 侯百里

    　　《周书?王会》解后附伊尹四方献令，《左氏传》、《太史公书》此例尤多；正文后载禁宰杀耕牛告示，取谳最古。补叙救二命事，亦得离合之法。 马竹吾

    Explanation

    147

    Reading this would be enough to make those who slaughter and eat oxen shiver with fear, and the accompanying notice is especially clear and forceful. Zhang Zicheng

    "牢" comes from "牛," and "狱" comes from "犬." By not eating oxen and dogs, one can avoid prison. This is because oxen are the divine essence of the Black Warrior (玄武) from the heavens, and the earthly, demonic energy of dogs and prisons. Oxen should not be used except in the context of sacrificial rituals, and only heavenly deities should enjoy the sacrifice. Therefore, oxen are not suitable for consumption by the common people. Moreover, oxen plow vast fields, contributing to the world; they eat the grass of spring without harming anyone. How can one bear to both benefit from their labor and then eat their flesh? They toil under the sky, their blood and sweat on the fields not yet dry; their bones are cut open, and their limbs are dismembered in the kitchen. How pitiful! If local officials strictly prohibit and apprehend, making butchers aware of the warning, it can certainly bring infinite blessings. If scholars and common people warn each other not to eat, they can permanently avoid prison. Is this not a good thing? This writing is clear, forceful, and enjoyable, truly beneficial to the world. It should be urgently printed to encourage the world. Hou Baili

    After the explanation of "Wanghui" in the Zhou Book, there is an appendix of edicts issued by Yi Yin for the four directions, and such examples are especially numerous in the Zuo Zhuan and the Records of the Grand Historian. The main text follows with a notice prohibiting the slaughter of oxen, taking the oldest judgment. The narrative of saving two lives is supplemented, also following the method of separation and combination. Ma Zhugu

    148

    昔有一满州侍御，请为回民开杀牛禁，上痛斥之。此文牖世觉民，足辅王法所不及，是有功于世道者。上元李瑜谨注

    　　金瑞

    　　明贵阳金公，字凤翔。举人大挑，分发四川候补县，以事故未赴。有二子，曰瑞曰璋，悉从塾师读。有岳某者，亦世家子，自赴瑞塾，值师不在，相与赌棋，争着致怒。瑞、璋同殴岳，岳立卒。瑞、璋急归语父。金公大惊，曰：“殴人立毙，在法必辟，汝兄弟谁毙岳？”瑞曰：“儿与争棋怒殴，实儿毙之。”公曰：“若然，吾率汝投案请罪。”将行，璋曰：“非兄毙岳，儿见渠殴兄，情极竭力向殴而毙之，于兄无与。”公谓璋曰：“汝毙岳，汝偿岳命，勿后悔！”璋曰：“杀人偿命，理也，何悔之有？况以自作之孽累及亲兄，问心不安。”瑞曰：“实儿毙之。”璋曰：“非兄毙之。”公曰：“兄不攀弟，弟不攀兄，可谓贤矣！”言至此，公亦无主意，俯首不语。

    Explanation

    148

    There was once a Manchu censor who requested the lifting of the ban on slaughtering cattle for Muslims. The emperor severely criticized him. This text enlightens the world and awakens the people, sufficiently supplementing the law where it falls short, thus being beneficial to the world. Shangyuan Li Yu carefully annotated it.

    Jin Rui

    During the Ming dynasty, there was a man from Guiyang named Jin Fengxiang, who was a juren (a degree obtained through the imperial examination) and was assigned to serve as a county magistrate in Sichuan, on reserve, but did not take up the post due to an incident. He had two sons, named Rui and Zhang, who all studied under a private tutor. There was a man named Yue, also from a prominent family, who went to Rui's school when the teacher was not present and played chess with him. They quarreled and became angry, and Rui and Zhang beat Yue together, causing him to die immediately. Rui and Zhang hurriedly returned home and told their father. Jin Fengxiang was greatly surprised and said, "To kill someone and have them die instantly, by law, it is punishable by death. Which of you killed Yue?" Rui said, "I quarreled and beat him in anger, so I killed him." The father said, "If that is the case, I will take you to the authorities to confess and seek punishment." As they were about to go, Zhang said, "It wasn't my brother who killed Yue. I saw him beating my brother, in a moment of extreme emotion, I beat him and killed him, it has nothing to do with my brother." The father said to Zhang, "If you killed Yue, you will pay for his life, do not regret it!" Zhang said, "Killing someone and paying with your life is just, what is there to regret? Besides, if I involve my dear brother in my own fault, I cannot bear it in my heart." Rui said, "It was really me who killed him." Zhang said, "It wasn't my brother who killed him." The father said, "The elder does not blame the younger, and the younger does not blame the elder, they can be called virtuous!" After saying this, the father also had no idea, he bowed his head and remained silent.

    149

    盖瑞、璋非一母，璋系继母魏氏所生，魏氏在侧，闻瑞言则暗喜，闻璋言则隐忧。及闻瑞、璋争认杀人事，情不自禁，遂谓公曰：“瑞儿既认杀岳，即宜使瑞偿之。”公怒曰：“璋儿亦认毙岳，奈何使瑞偿命？”因而瑞认杀岳则公向瑞面掌之，璋欲偿命则魏指璋额詈之。瑞曰：“儿兄弟终须一人偿岳命，使弟偿之，遗母终身之忧，心实不忍。”璋母曰：“若汝言，无母之子即无人痛？定使汝弟偿之！”公曰：“若然，必先令瑞远行以避之。”于是先遣瑞外出，后同璋赴邑见尹。尹与岳属至亲，伪谓公曰：“杀人自投，罪减一等。”公归，尹令璋供瑞同殴，欲并收之以泄忿，璋不供。尹用极刑刑璋，璋死而复生凡二次，而前言不改。尹乃止，罪璋以详府。金公闻之，忿恨交迫，致疾卒。

    Explanation

    149

    Rui and Zhang were not born of the same mother; Zhang was the son of Hui, the stepmother. When Hui was present, she secretly rejoiced at Rui's words but felt hidden worry at Zhang's. Upon hearing that Rui and Zhang were arguing over who killed their father, she could not help herself and said to the father, "Since Rui admits to killing the father, Rui should be made to pay for it." The father angrily replied, "Zhang also admits to killing the father, so why should Rui pay for it?" Consequently, when Rui admitted to killing the father, the father struck Rui in the face; when Zhang wanted to take responsibility, Hui cursed Zhang. Rui said, "One of us brothers must take responsibility for the father's death. If it is my brother who takes responsibility, it will cause my mother lifelong worry, and I truly cannot bear it." Hui said, "If you say so, then a child without a mother will have no one to mourn for? I will definitely make your brother take responsibility!" The father said, "If that is the case, I must first send Rui away to avoid conflict." Thus, he first sent Rui away and then took Zhang with him to see the magistrate. The magistrate, being a close relative of the father, pretended to say to the father, "If someone kills themselves, their crime is reduced by one degree." After the father returned home, the magistrate ordered Zhang to accuse Rui of joint assault, intending to arrest both to vent his anger, but Zhang refused to accuse Rui. The magistrate used extreme punishment on Zhang, causing him to die and revive twice, but Zhang did not change his statement. The magistrate then stopped and punished Zhang, sending the case to the provincial office. Jin, upon hearing about the incident, became furious and died of illness.

    150

    尹差役押璋赴郡，刚出城，役索钱于璋，璋弗与。役以杖击之，璋仆而昏，觉有人以药丸纳其口，旋以手掩其口鼻与目曰：“佯为死，即不死。”璋吞丸后觉气不出而无闷，遂仰卧于途，不少动。闻役曰：“凶犯既死，可急禀官。”为间，闻官来验尸，命委尸沟壑。官去后，役亦自去。多时，觉有人摇之，璋恐押役回视，不敢动。其人曰：“吾非他，即嘱君佯为死之人也。”璋开目视之，满天星斗；急起，见一女子立面前，不辨妍媸。女曰：“可速走！迟则不得。”遂携手同行，其疾如飞。至一洞，洞有灯火，璋视女，及笄佳人也。揖谢之，女直受不辞，曰：“谢之诚宜也，微妾，君必死于押役之手。

    Explanation

    150

    The magistrate's constable escorted Zang to the county seat. Just as they left the city, the constable demanded money from Zang, but Zang refused to give it. The constable struck him with a stick, causing Zang to fall unconscious. Zang then felt someone put a pill in his mouth and immediately covered his mouth, nose, and eyes, saying, "Pretend to be dead, but do not actually die." After swallowing the pill, Zang felt unable to breathe but without discomfort. He then lay on his back on the road, not moving much. Hearing the constable say, "The criminal is already dead; you should quickly report to the official," and later hearing that the official had come to examine the body and ordered the body to be left in a ditch, the constable left after the official departed. After a long time, Zang felt someone shaking him. Fearing that the constable might return to look, he did not dare to move. The person said, "I am not someone else; I am the one who instructed you to pretend to be dead." Zang opened his eyes and looked at the sky, which was full of stars. He quickly stood up and saw a woman standing in front of him, unable to distinguish her beauty. The woman said, "You can quickly run! If you delay, you won't be able to." They then walked together by the hand, moving as fast as flying. They arrived at a cave with lights. Zang looked at the woman, who was a beautiful woman of marriageable age. He bowed and thanked her, and the woman accepted it directly without hesitation, saying, "It is indeed appropriate to thank me; without me, you would have surely died at the hands of the constable."

    151

    ”女设酒胾与璋同酌。璋曰：“卿系何仙？祈明示。”女曰：“君既知妾为仙，不必深究。”问女名，答以降仙。璋视洞中止一榻，曰：“仆寝何所？”女指榻曰：“在此。”曰：“卿坐寝乎？”女笑而不言。及寝，璋牵女与同榻，女曰：“刑伤未愈，宜静养之。”璋曰：“既不疼痛，伤痕可不顾也。”遂同寝。月馀，伤痕平复。女曰：“久居此无益，妾为君相一令居。”遂出游，而宿处主人悉竭力供奉，似与女有戚谊。问女，女亦不实言。一日，宿一人家，值阴雨不能行，主人陪话中庭。忽一小狐骤入，主人呵曰：“有客在此，惹客笑话矣！”狐忽化为十数岁之幼女，降仙牵女于怀曰：“小妹露吾行藏矣。”璋以知降仙为狐。

    Explanation

    151

    The woman set out meat dishes and wine for璋 to drink together.璋 said, "You, what kind of immortal are you? I beg you to enlighten me." The woman replied, "Since you know I am an immortal, there is no need to delve deeper." When asked about her name, she answered that she was a descending immortal.璋 looked around the cave and saw only one bed, and said, "Where shall I sleep?" The woman pointed to the bed and said, "Here." He asked, "Are you sleeping on the bed?" The woman smiled but did not speak. When they lay down,璋 took the woman and they slept together. The woman said, "The wound has not yet healed, so it is best to rest quietly."璋 said, "Since there is no pain, the scar can be ignored." So they slept together. After a month, the scar had healed. The woman said, "Staying here for too long is not beneficial. I will find a suitable place for you to live." They then set out, and the host at the place of lodging exerted every effort to serve, as if there were some kinship with the woman. When asked about the woman, she did not give a truthful answer. One day, they stayed at a household, but due to heavy rain, they could not continue their journey. The host entertained them in the courtyard. Suddenly, a small fox rushed in, and the host exclaimed, "There are guests here, and this will cause them to laugh!" The fox suddenly transformed into a young girl of ten or so years old. The descending immortal took the girl in her arms and said, "My little sister has revealed our secret." Zhang realized that the woman who descended was a fox spirit.

    152

    一日，璋与女少休茶肆，一宦门之子见女，立视良久而去。既而肆主指女问璋，璋以妹对。肆人曰：“欲字人否？”曰：“不欲。”肆人曰：“爱汝妹者，某宦之子。君孤身至此，恐事不由君，不若嫁妹于彼，多索聘金以裕资斧为愈也。”璋暗商于女，女曰：“可。君带金北行，日暮向门前有五柳者投宿，不过二更，妾自至。”璋见肆人言所欲，肆人曰：“可。”遂以五十金给璋，璋自去。某宦子遣婢媪以艳衣衣女，舁之去。夜与同寝，极尽绸缪。明晨视之，乃其胞妹。女归见璋曰：“某宦之子，即以极刑刑君之子也。”遂以侮之之实语璋，璋大喜曰：“卿代仆泄夙忿矣！”

    Explanation

    152

    One day, Zhang and his sister took a brief rest in a tea house. A son from a high-ranking official's family saw her, stood and looked at her for a long time before leaving. Soon, the owner of the tea house pointed to her and asked Zhang, who answered that she was his sister. The owner said, "Do you want to marry someone?" Zhang replied, "No." The owner said, "The one who loves your sister is the son of that official. You arrived alone here, fearing that matters will not be under your control; it might be better to marry your sister to him, asking for a large dowry to enrich your resources." Zhang secretly discussed with his sister, who said, "Yes. You take the money and go north, at dusk, go to the place where there are five willow trees to stay the night, no later than two hours past midnight, I will come myself." Zhang saw the owner and told him what he wanted, and the owner said, "Yes." So, he gave Zhang fifty gold pieces, and Zhang left. The son of the official sent a maid to dress the sister in fine clothes and took her away. At night, he slept with her, indulging in intimacy. The next morning, he saw that she was his own sister. The sister returned and told Zhang, "The son of that official was the one who inflicted severe punishment on your son." She then told Zhang about the details of the insult, and Zhang greatly delighted and said, "You have avenged my long-held resentment on my behalf!"

    153

    后游至同州，女曰：“此处有一乐土，未知君福命能消受否？”盖州有富室董某，有一女而无子，降仙与璋往投之，愿为佣工。董见之，喜，以璋文弱，使理轻举，居前庭；使女伴女治针黹，居后院。而璋与女实每夜同处。久之，女曰：“君见主人之女否？”曰：“未也。”女曰：“诚佳人也。”璋曰：“比卿如何？”曰：“妾实不及其娟丽。”璋曰：“卿能使仆一见颜色否？”女曰：“不惟使君见之，将使君妻之。”璋急问其期，女曰：“何急也！约不远耳。”及二鼓，女曰：“妾视其寝未。”遂去。

    Explanation

    153

    After traveling to Tongzhou, the fairy said, "There is a happy land here, but I wonder if your fortune and destiny can bear it?" It happened that there was a wealthy family named Dong in the state, who had a daughter but no son. The fairy and Zhang descended to offer their services, willing to work as hired laborers. Dong welcomed them warmly, and because Zhang was cultured and weak, he was assigned to handle light work in the front courtyard, while the fairy managed needlework with the other women in the back courtyard. In reality, Zhang and the fairy spent every night together. After some time, the fairy asked, "Have you seen the master's daughter yet?" Zhang replied, "Not yet." The fairy said, "She is indeed a beautiful person." Zhang asked, "How does she compare to you?" The fairy replied, "I am not as graceful and beautiful as she." Zhang said, "Can you let me see her face?" The fairy responded, "Not only will you see her, but I will also arrange for you to marry her." Zhang eagerly asked when this would happen, and the fairy said, "Why are you so eager? It won't be long." By the second drumbeat, the fairy said, "I will check if she is still awake," and then left.

    154

    既而返曰：“女睡熟矣！女若问君名，可实告之，其馀勿轻言。”女送璋至董女楼而返。灯火尚明，时方盛暑，见女白身卧帐中，潜就淫之。女觉而醒，俟璋事已，问曰：“汝金璋耶？”璋曰：“然。”“何得到此？”璋不答，起身而杳，女大疑。次夜复然。璋以董女之问语女，女曰：“渠若再问，答以仙助之，勿言妾也。”第三夜，女设酒胾以俟，二更后，不见璋，遂自言曰：“金郎，来则来耳，何俟妾寐？”璋应声入曰：“仆来矣！”女酌酒奉之，复问到此之由，璋以狐女之言答之，女信之。盖楼系活梯，女父母以女及笄，昼则设梯，夜则捐去，固非凡人所能到也。

    Explanation

    154

    Afterward, he returned and said, "The woman is asleep! If she asks your name, you may tell her the truth, but do not speak of anything else lightly." The woman escorted Jin to the tower of Dong's daughter and then returned. The lamps were still lit, and it was the height of summer. He saw the woman lying naked in her bed, and he secretly approached her. The woman woke up, and after Jin had finished, she asked, "Are you Jin?" Jin replied, "Yes." "How did you get here?" Jin did not answer, got up, and disappeared, leaving the woman greatly puzzled. The next night, the same thing happened again. Jin told the woman what Dong's daughter had asked, and the woman said, "If she asks again, tell her that a fairy helped you, and do not mention me." On the third night, the woman set out a feast in anticipation, but after the second watch, Jin was not seen. She then spoke to herself, "Jin, if you are coming, come then, why wait for me to sleep?" Jin responded and entered, saying, "I have come!" The woman poured him a drink and offered it to him, and again she asked why he had come. Jin answered with the words of the fox woman, and the woman believed him. The tower was connected by a movable ladder, and the woman's parents set up the ladder during the day and removed it at night, so it was certainly beyond the reach of ordinary people.

    155

    饮际，问璋履历，璋仍以狐女为姊，讳其为狐，其馀历言之。女闻之，伤悲之情如夫妇。及期月，女有娠，女母梁氏见而疑之，语于董。董曰：“夜无楼梯，谁能上之？”梁曰：“固然，然女之情形实可疑。”夜，董与妻窃听之，果有男女微言之声。暗设梯，梁氏上，穴窗窥之，见有男子与女对语。扣门而入，则惟女一人。问女曰：“适见有男子在此，其人焉往？”女曰：“诚有之，其人之去来俱有仙助。”曰：“其人为谁？”女答以金璋。梁曰：“惜也，其为佣工！”女曰：“今为佣工，其实是宦门之子。”遂历言璋之家世与遭遇。

    Explanation

    155

    During the drinking, they asked Zhan about his life history. Zhan still considered the fox girl as his sister, avoiding mentioning that he was a fox, and recounted the rest in detail. The girl, upon hearing this, felt sorrow like that of a married couple. After a month, the girl became pregnant. Her mother, Liang, saw this and became suspicious, telling her husband, Dong. Dong said, "At night, with no ladder, who could come up?" Liang replied, "Indeed, but the girl's situation is truly suspicious." At night, Dong and his wife secretly listened, and indeed, they heard soft voices of a man and a woman. Liang secretly set up a ladder, climbed up, and peeped through a hole in the window, seeing a man speaking with the girl. They knocked on the door and entered, finding only the girl inside. They asked her, "Just now, we saw a man here. Where did he go?" The girl replied, "There was indeed such a person, and his comings and goings were all aided by divine power." They asked, "Who was that person?" The girl answered, "Jin Zhan." Liang said, "Alas, he is just a hired worker!" The girl said, "Now he is a hired worker, but he is actually the son of an official family." She then recounted Zhan's family background and his experiences.

    156

    梁语于董，且曰：“吾二人无子，久欲得赘婿赖以奉养，金某有家不能归，赘之大有裨益。且渠与女有私已经岁，亦不得不婿之。”董从之，爰卜吉行合卺礼。璋与女方对饮，狐女忽至。董女起身曰：“姊盍早来？”狐女曰：“吾非汝姊，实良人之嫡妻。”谓璋曰：“君得令居，无需妾，请永别。鬻妾之资，妾带之去矣！”言已不见。董女惊讶，急问璋。璋曰：“渠为狐，妻卿之故，悉渠之力也，兼于仆有救命之恩。”遂并叙之。女曰：“渠既有恩，何故鬻之？”璋复叙鬻之之故，女笑曰：“得人之身价，复以人之胞妹自代，狐姊可谓巧于报复矣！”未二年，董翁卒，璋改金姓为董氏，产业悉璋承受矣。

    Explanation

    156

    "Liang told Dong, saying, 'The two of us have no children, and for a long time we have wanted to take in a son-in-law to rely on for support. Jin has a family but cannot return, and taking him in would be greatly beneficial. Moreover, he has had a private relationship with your daughter for years, so we cannot help but take him in as a son-in-law.' Dong agreed, and they chose a favorable day to perform the wedding ceremony. Zhang drank with the daughter, when suddenly a fox girl appeared. Dong's daughter stood up and said, 'Why did you come so late, sister?' The fox girl replied, 'I am not your sister; I am the legitimate wife of your husband.' She told Zhang, 'You have a good place, and you do not need me. Please part ways forever. I took the money from selling me and left with it!' She then disappeared. Dong's daughter was surprised and quickly asked Zhang. Zhang said, 'She is a fox. She took your daughter as her wife, and it was all her effort. She also saved my life.' He then explained the whole story. The daughter said, 'If she has done you a favor, why did you sell her?' Zhang again explained why he sold her, and the daughter laughed and said, 'She got the price of a person, and then replaced it with her own sister. The fox sister can truly be said to be clever in taking revenge!' Two years later, Dong's father died, and Zhang changed his surname from Jin to Dong, inheriting all the property."

    157

    金公卒后，继妻魏氏尽有殡葬。闻金璋死，痛子之切，遂得迷症，弃财毁物；仇人岳某复施以暗算。及病愈，家产一无所有。不得已佣媪于人，岳家不许主人容留之，乞食亦无与之者。魏乃远离居邑，日丐村镇，夜宿瓦窑，百苦并尝矣。

    　　金瑞之出亡也，不知焉往之善，顺路而适，数月之久，未获立足之地。游至徽州，资斧将尽，不得已佣身于人，伺候书室。主人亦姓金，塾师贾孝廉与金公同年，学生惟东人一子一侄，曰震曰霖，俱十六岁。一日，师有公事，命题而行。及午，震、霖俱不食，盖为文章无只字也。瑞曰：“勿虞，吾代作之。”立为草创，令震、霖录之，日夕，二艺俱成。师见之大骇，曰：“此文非汝二人所能为也。”震、霖以实告，师语金公。

    Explanation

    157

    After Jin Gong's death, his second wife, Hui Shi, took full responsibility for the funeral arrangements. Upon hearing that Jin Zhang had died, she was overwhelmed with grief and fell into a state of delirium, discarding her wealth and destroying her possessions. Her enemy, Yue, then plotted against her. After her recovery, she found herself penniless. Desperate, she took up work as a servant, but Yue's family would not allow the master to keep her, and no one would give her food. Hui Shi then moved far away from her hometown, begging in villages during the day and sleeping in earthen kilns at night, enduring all kinds of suffering.

    Meanwhile, Jin Rui, after escaping, did not know where to go and wandered aimlessly, traveling for several months without finding a place to settle. He eventually arrived in Huizhou, where his funds were nearly exhausted. Forced to take up work, he became a servant in a household. The master, also surnamed Jin, employed a tutor named Jia, who was a scholar of the imperial examination system and had been a contemporary of Jin Gong. The tutor had two students, a son and a nephew, both sixteen years old, named Zhen and Lin. One day, the tutor had to attend to official business and left them with a topic to write on. By midday, neither Zhen nor Lin had written a single word. Jin Rui said, "Do not worry, I will write it for you." He quickly drafted the piece, instructing Zhen and Lin to copy it. By evening, both essays were completed. The tutor was shocked and said, "This writing is beyond what you two could produce." Zhen and Lin told him the truth, and the tutor informed Jin Gong.

    158

    公问瑞曰：“有此才学，胡为出亡到此？”瑞实言之。金公喜曰：“令尊与仆与师同年中式，大同年也。以年侄作佣僮，大失友义矣！”使从贾师读，认为侄。应童试而售，联捷，钦点主政，签分兵部。遇乡人，问家景，知父、弟俱亡，母氏不知所往，遂大恸。乡人劝之曰：“令堂无倚，当急寻而奉养之，哭无益也。”瑞遂弃官寻母。至居里，借宿旧邻家，细询母音，知母尝佣于某村甲某。诣甲问之，甲言佣此数日即辞去。瑞急于周围村庄细访之，月馀无耗。囊物不多，日不敢饱，盖恐费用不继也。
    一日，访至一庄，庄人曰：“数月前有一老妇病故于此，不知其姓氏，庄人葬于庄首庙地中。”瑞不敢谓非其母，亦不敢谓是其母，因向其墓而哭。忽来一少妇，以大兄称瑞，曰：“死者非老母，欲见老母，务急于某山下寻之。”言已即不见。瑞大喜，以为仙人指示，急赴某山寻之，数日仍无耗。一日遇雨，避雨山下石庙中。

    Explanation

    158

    The official asked Rui, "With such talent and learning, why did you flee here?" Rui spoke frankly. The official Gold was pleased to say, "Your father and I and my teacher were of the same year in the imperial examination; we are great contemporaries. To make the younger nephew a servant greatly violates the friendship!" He instructed Rui to study under the merchant teacher and adopted him as a nephew. Rui passed the children's examination and achieved consecutive success, being appointed by imperial decree as the head of the office and assigned to the Ministry of War. When he met a fellow villager, he asked about the family situation and learned that his father and brother had both died, and the whereabouts of his mother were unknown. He then wept bitterly. The fellow villager advised him, "Your mother has no support; you should urgently find her and take care of her. Crying does no good." Rui then resigned his position to search for his mother. Upon reaching the village, he stayed with an old neighbor, carefully inquired about the mother's whereabouts, and learned that she had worked for someone in a certain village. He visited the person to ask, and the person said she had worked there for a few days and then left. Rui searched the surrounding villages carefully, but after more than a month, there was no news. His bag contained little, and he dared not eat to his fill, because he feared that expenses would not continue.

    One day, he visited a village, and the villagers said, "A few months ago, an elderly woman died here. We do not know her surname, and the villagers buried her in the temple grounds at the head of the village." Rui dared not say it was not his mother, nor could he say it was his mother, so he wept at her grave. Suddenly, a young woman appeared and addressed Rui as her elder brother, saying, "The deceased was not your mother. If you wish to see your mother, you must urgently search for her at the foot of a certain mountain." After speaking, she disappeared. Rui was greatly delighted, thinking it was a divine indication, and hurried to the mountain to search. For several days, there was still no news. One day, encountering rain, he sought shelter in a stone temple at the foot of the mountain.

    159

    须臾雨止，见一老妇以绳捆柴，拽之下山，雨过泥滑，失足而仆，泣曰：“吾金瑞儿见之，不知如何痛心也！”瑞闻而未真，急视之，衣服褴缕，面颜黑瘦，悉不类母。既而，其妇复仆，自言如前。瑞急趋之曰：“金瑞在此。”妇拭目视之曰：“金瑞儿，你可来了！”瑞闻语音，知为母，急曰：“吾母……”即昏倒泥途，不省人事。半晌始苏，见母坐泥地而泣，恐悲悼致母恸，遂强笑曰：“吾母子得会面，即万分之幸也。”急起扶母起。母命拽柴，瑞欲弃之，自思资斧将尽，不得已，一手扶母，一手拽柴而行。瑞曰：“母居何处？”曰：“不远。”盖山下数十步外有瓦窑。行至其处，曰：“吾居于此。”瑞见之，泪涔涔下，恐母见，回首自抆。

    Explanation

    159

    A moment later, the rain stopped, and he saw an elderly woman binding firewood with a rope and dragging it down the mountain. After the rain, the mud was slippery, and she lost her footing and fell, crying, "I saw my son Jīn Ruì, I don't know how heartbroken I am!" Jīn Ruì heard her but did not believe it, so he looked at her urgently. She was wearing ragged clothes, her face was dark and thin, and she looked nothing like his mother. Soon after, the woman fell again, speaking as before. Jīn Ruì hurriedly went to her and said, "Jīn Ruì is here." The woman wiped her eyes and looked at him, saying, "Jīn Ruì, you have finally come!" Jīn Ruì recognized her voice and knew it was his mother, so he exclaimed, "My mother..." and fainted on the muddy road, unconscious. After a long while, he regained consciousness and saw his mother sitting in the mud and crying. Fearing that his sorrow would cause his mother to grieve deeply, he forced a smile and said, "My mother and I are fortunate to meet, it is a great blessing." He quickly got up and helped his mother up. The mother ordered him to drag the firewood. Jīn Ruì wanted to abandon it, but thinking to himself that his funds were nearly exhausted, he could not help it. With one hand supporting his mother and the other dragging the firewood, he walked. Jīn Ruì asked, "Where does your mother live?" She replied, "Not far." There is a tile kiln several dozen steps down the mountain. Walking to that place, she said, "I live here." Jīn Ruì saw it, and tears streamed down, fearing that his mother would see, he turned back and wiped his tears.

    160

    扶母低首入窑，砂釜、乞筐在侧，瑞不胜酸楚。母问瑞离家后之景况，瑞以联捷等事语之，母喜极。瑞急赴近村籴米炊饭，见母甘食如蜜，一喜一悲。而次日即无用度，母曰：“汝已居官，不惯乞丐事。汝居此，吾代汝为之。”瑞泣曰：“为养母，即赴汤蹈火亦分内事，况行丐乎？”言已，母子俱哭。忽来一少妇对母伏拜，起，复向瑞肃。瑞视之，即令赴山下寻母之人。母曰：“子为谁妇？”答以次男妇。

    Explanation

    160

    The narrator carried his mother into the kiln, her head bowed in humility. A clay pot and a begging basket were placed nearby. The narrator could not bear the bitterness of the situation. The mother asked about the narrator's circumstances after leaving home, and he told her about his successive success in the imperial examinations, which made her extremely happy. The narrator hurriedly went to a nearby village to buy rice and cook, seeing his mother eat sweetly like honey, expressing mixed emotions of joy and sorrow. The next day, there were no resources, and the mother said, "You are already an official and are not used to begging. You stay here, and I will do it on your behalf." The narrator wept and said, "Taking care of my mother is a duty within my proper role, even if it means enduring great hardship, let alone begging?" After speaking, both mother and son wept. Suddenly, a young woman came, prostrated and bowed to the mother, and then showed respect to the narrator. The narrator looked at her and recognized her as the person who was sent to find the mother in the mountains. The mother asked, "Who are you to him?" She answered, "I am the wife of the second son."

    161

    母惊曰：“吾子未室而死，子何言之妄也？”妇曰：“不妄，母次子未死，现居同州某处，家富有，改名董璋，今科已领乡荐。媳积蓄碎银数十两，可作资斧往就之。”言已，置银于地而杳。瑞母子不胜惊喜，先换银数两作路费。换银时为草窃窥见之，乘夜窃银去，所剩钱文无几。乃扶母而行，十数里外，母不能步，瑞背负而行。里许力尽，少休再走，穷日之力止行四五十里。二日后，足泡腿酸，瑞亦不能前进。幸有同州货车回空，瑞少许以资，求其方便，车主怜而载之。既至车主之家，违璋所居仅六七十里，瑞暗喜。明早负母而行，日将午，少休于路。

    Explanation

    161

    The mother was startled and said, "My son had not married before he died; why are you speaking such nonsense?" The woman replied, "It is not nonsense. Your second son is still alive; he currently resides in a certain place in Tongzhou. He is wealthy, has changed his name to Dong Zhang, and has already passed the local recommendation examination this year. His wife has saved several taels of silver, which can be used as travel expenses to go and join him." After speaking, she placed the silver on the ground and disappeared. Rui and his mother were overwhelmed with joy and surprise, and they first exchanged some silver for travel expenses. When they were exchanging the silver, it was seen by a thief, who stole the silver at night, leaving only a few coins remaining. They then carried the mother on their back and walked. After several miles, the mother could no longer walk, so Rui carried her on his back. After about a mile, they were exhausted and rested briefly before continuing. With all their strength in a day, they could only manage to walk forty to fifty miles. Two days later, their feet were blistered and their legs were sore, and Rui could no longer proceed. Fortunately, there was a truck from Tongzhou returning empty, and Rui offered a small amount of money for a favor. The truck driver felt pity and agreed to take them. After arriving at the truck driver's home, they were only sixty to seventy miles away from where Dong Zhang lived, and Rui secretly rejoiced. The next morning, they carried the mother on their back again, and by midday, they rested briefly on the road.

    162

    忽对面一小车来，上坐一媪与一少妇，后有空车二乘。媪下问瑞曰：“君金姓耶？”瑞曰：“然。”媪复指瑞母问瑞曰：“此君之母也？”瑞复应之。媪回语少妇，少妇急下，当途而拜曰：“次男妇董氏请母安。”起，复向瑞问兄好。母惊曰：“汝又是次子妇耶？果尔，汝勿遁！”董氏曰：“媳迎接来迟，负罪非轻，何敢遁！媳实亦不能遁也。”母曰：“汝夫何不来？”董曰：“会试未归。”爰扶母升车而归。至家，母曰：“昨有一事，迄今惊讶。

    Explanation

    162

    A small carriage suddenly appeared, with an elderly woman and a young woman seated inside, followed by two empty carriages. The elderly woman descended and asked Rui, "Do you have the surname Jin?" Rui replied, "Yes." The elderly woman then pointed to Rui's mother and asked Rui, "Is this your mother?" Rui confirmed. The elderly woman turned to the young woman and said something to her. The young woman hurriedly got off the carriage and bowed on the road, saying, "The second son's wife, Dong, greets you respectfully, Mother." She stood up and then asked Rui about his brother's well-being. Rui's mother was surprised and said, "Are you the second son's wife again? If so, you must not run away!" Dong replied, "I was late in greeting you, and I have committed a serious offense. How could I run away? I truly cannot run away." Rui's mother asked, "Why isn't your husband here?" Dong answered, "He is away taking the imperial examination." They then helped Rui's mother get into the carriage and returned home. Upon arriving, Rui's mother said, "There was an event yesterday that still surprises me."

    163

    ”董问之，母以少妇口称子媳，面奉路资，旋即不见语之。董曰：“渠实子媳，而实狐也。媳兹之奉迓，亦狐姊言母将至。”瑞急治行李，赴京觅弟，遂上疏自陈弃官寻母并殴死岳某，孽由自作，与弟璋无干，情愿干罪等情。上嘉瑞孝璋弟，悉行赦免；令璋复金姓会试，下科亦会殿。值母生辰，肆筵庆祝，狐女忽至，母喜之不胜，忘其为狐。及晚，璋问狐女曰：“卿之来，殆亦夫妇之情不能恝乎？”曰：“非也。一为祝母寿，一为妾有大喜事，特来相告耳。”璋问之，狐女曰：“妾以赞助君昆仲之故，得免劫数也。”言已即不见。

    Explanation

    163

    Dong asked him, and the mother spoke through a young woman as if she were the daughter-in-law, presenting travel funds formally, and then disappeared without speaking to him. Dong said, "She is actually the daughter-in-law, but is actually a fox. The daughter-in-law came to welcome, and it was also the fox sister who said the mother was coming." Rui quickly prepared his luggage and went to the capital to find his brother, then submitted a memorial to himself, stating that he had abandoned his official position to find his mother and beat to death his father-in-law, that the sin was self-inflicted, and had nothing to do with his brother Zhang, and was willing to take the blame and other matters. The emperor praised Rui's filial piety and Zhang's brotherly love, and all were pardoned; Zhang was ordered to resume his original surname and take the imperial examination, and also took the imperial examination in the next session. On the occasion of his mother's birthday, a feast was held to celebrate, and the fox girl suddenly arrived, and the mother was extremely pleased, forgetting that she was a fox. By evening, Zhang asked the fox girl, "Your coming, is it also because of the affection between husband and wife that cannot be ignored?" She replied, "No. One is to wish the mother a long life, and one is because I have a great joy, just to inform you." Zhang asked her, and the fox girl said, "I was able to avoid the calamity because I helped you and your brothers." After speaking, she immediately disappeared.

    164

    虚白道人曰：使金璋轻身代兄而竟杀其身，金瑞弃官寻母而终丧其官，人将谓造物梦梦，而为之嗟咨感慨不置也。狐拯济之，指引之，使瑞、璋孝弟之行，名于当时，传于后世，狐之功可谓巨矣！然狐即以此举得免劫数，是狐之所为，不啻造物为之。

    　　读之使人生孝友之心，开豁达之念，非独以其文字佳也。 王植三

    　　伏应之妙，一篇如一句，斯真有数文字。 马竹吾

    Explanation

    164

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: If Jin Zhang sacrifices himself to save his brother and ultimately dies, and Jin Rui resigns his position to seek his mother and ultimately loses his position, people will think that the Creator is foolish or indifferent, and will lament and be moved without end. The fox rescues and guides them, making Rui and Zhang's filial and fraternal deeds famous in their time and passed down to future generations. The fox's merit can be considered great! Yet the fox, by this act, escapes the cycle of calamity, and what the fox does is no less than what the Creator does.

    Reading this will inspire filial and fraternal feelings, open up broad and open-minded thoughts, not just because of its fine writing. Wang Zisant.

    The subtlety of the response, as if one sentence throughout, this is truly a work of great literature. Ma Zhuwu.

    165

    砭世砺俗，有功于纲常不浅，不得以谀说目之。上元李瑜谨注

    　　益智录卷之三

    　　苏 玉 真

    　　吴兴萧培之，世家子。十五岁入泮，年已及冠，尚未卜凰，读书于宅旁别业。一日，读至二更许，忽闻窗外有人曰：“萧相公勤读哉！”闻其声，娇婉似女子；既面搴帘入，视之，果静女其姝也。萧知其非人，故问之。女曰：“深夜奔人，可留名作节孝坊耶？君得丽女，妾爱才郎，邂逅相遇，与子偕臧，亦幸甚矣，何穷诘焉！”遂相狎。已，复问之。女自言为狐。自是每夜必至。几一月，狐忽曰：“君何二十许无伉俪也？”曰：“老母苛索：门户当，求淑女；姿容美，责门第，故迟延至今耳。”狐曰：“何须远求，东邻苏孝廉女，小字玉真，才貌双绝，岂非佳耦？”萧曰：“门第相若，居诸甚殊，渠焉肯俯就？”狐曰：“曾媒之否？”曰：“未也。”狐曰：“姑媒之，若其不谐，妾请代谋。”

    Explanation

    165

    Criticizing and correcting societal ills has done significant service to the fundamental social norms and moral order, and should not be dismissed as flattery. Annotated by Li Yu of Shangyuan.

    Volume Three of the Yizhi Lu

    Su Yuzhen

    Xiao Peizhi of Wu County, a member of a prominent family, entered school at the age of fifteen and was already in his twenties. He had not yet married and was studying at a separate residence near his home. One day, while reading until around midnight, he suddenly heard someone outside the window say, "Mr. Xiao, you are diligent in your studies!" The voice was gentle and soft, like that of a woman. She then pulled aside the curtain and entered, and upon seeing her, he realized she was indeed a quiet and beautiful woman. Xiao knew she was not human and asked her why she had come. She replied, "Can you leave a name on a memorial arch for filial piety and chastity? You gain a beautiful woman, and I love a talented man. We met by chance, and together with you, we shall be virtuous, which is fortunate indeed. Why ask further?" They then became intimate. Later, Xiao asked her again, and she revealed that she was a fox. From then on, she visited him every night. After nearly a month, the fox suddenly asked, "Why have you not married by now?" Xiao replied, "My mother has strict demands: the family background should be appropriate, and she seeks a virtuous woman; she demands beauty and a good family background, so it has been delayed until now." The fox said, "Why look far for someone? The daughter of the scholar-official Su to the east, nicknamed Yuzhen, is unparalleled in both talent and beauty. Isn't she a perfect match?" Xiao said, "The family backgrounds are similar, but the living conditions are very different. How could she agree to marry down?" The fox asked, "Have you proposed through a matchmaker?" Xiao replied, "Not yet." The fox said, "Let's try matchmaking. If it doesn't work out, I will take care of it."

    166

    萧即告于母，烦至友作伐，果不谐。狐怒曰：“渠何高自位置也！君果欲妻之，妾能百方以谋之。”曰：“矢欲得之为妻。”狐曰：“此心不可以境地移也。”萧曰：“诺。”盖玉真幼从父学，工于诗，凡有题咏，必使小青衣呈于其父。偶成一绝句，其父阅之，批云：“押韵稳妥，设想新奇。”其诗云：“绣罢频呼姊妹看，暖风晴日满阑干。花间打散双蝴蝶，飞过东墙又作团。”一日，夜深不眠，玉真犹自反覆涵咏，忽一少年自外入，视之，西邻萧培之也。女惊讶曰：“深夜来此何为？”曰：“特来请教耳。”萧见女独坐长榻，遂亦与之并坐，曰：“昨烦冰人，何故相拒？”女曰：“此事非吾二人所能主也。”萧曰：“此事非吾二人所能主，实吾二人所可为也。”女闻之，颜红过耳，羞惭无以自容，欲行而生牵之。女曰：“请看吾所作之诗好否？”遂以所作之七绝授萧，实欲借此而逃。萧曰：“不暇阅此。”言已，忽若梦醒，仍兀坐书斋，其诗尚在手也。

    Explanation

    166

    萧 immediately informed his mother, troubling a close friend to act as a matchmaker, but it indeed did not succeed. The fox, angered, said, "Why does he place himself so high! If you truly wish to marry her, I can devise a hundred ways to accomplish it." He replied, "I vow to make her my wife." The fox said, "This heart cannot be swayed by circumstances." 萧 said, "Agreed." Yùzhēn, who was young, learned from her father and excelled in poetry. Whenever there was a poem or verse, she would have the young maid present it to her father. Accidentally, she composed a short poem. Her father read it and commented: "The rhyme is steady, and the conception is novel." The poem read: "After finishing the embroidery, she often called her sisters to admire it; warm breezes and sunny days filled the balustrades. In the flowers, the double butterflies were scattered; they flew over the eastern wall and formed a cluster again." One day, deep into the night, Yùzhēn still repeatedly pondered over the poem. Suddenly, a young man entered from outside. Looking at him, it was Xiāo Péizhi from the western neighbor. The girl was surprised and asked, "Why are you here so late at night?" He said, "I have come only to ask for your advice." Seeing the girl sitting alone on the long couch, 萧 also sat beside her and said, "Yesterday, I troubled the matchmaker, why did you refuse?" The girl said, "This matter is not something the two of us can decide." 萧 said, "This matter is not something the two of us can decide, but it is something the two of us can do." Hearing this, the girl's face turned red beyond her ears, and she felt ashamed and could not bear it. She wanted to leave, but he held her back. The girl said, "Please tell me if my poem is good?" She then handed the seven-character quatrain she had composed to 萧, intending to use it as an excuse to escape. 萧 said, "I have no time to read this." After he spoke, suddenly, as if waking from a dream, he still sat alone in the study, and the poem was still in his hand.

    167

    阅之，羡慕至极，遂援笔书于诗后曰：“今生若能得此为妇，当预筑金屋以俟之。”欲狐来向渠言之，而狐竟不至。次日，无心读书，遂作七绝云：“曾向天台访玉真，当头片月皓如银。深沉院落重关锁，谁念萧郎是路人？”后书“邻生萧培之拜赠”。及晚，回忆玉真华容，意欲再往，恨梦不复灵。既而一女子搴帘入；视之，正东邻女苏玉真也，大喜。亦曰：“深夜来此何为？”女若痴若迷，不知所为。萧遂拥之于怀，腮连目睨，情态难书。

    Explanation

    167

    After reading it, he admired it to the utmost and took up his pen to write after the poem: "If I could have this woman as my wife in this life, I would build a golden house in advance to wait for her." He wished the fox to come and tell him, but the fox did not come. The next day, without heart for reading, he composed a seven-character quatrain: "I once visited Yuzhen at Tian Tai, with a bright moon like silver overhead. The deep and secluded courtyard is locked by heavy gates, who would think of Xiao Lang as a stranger?" He then wrote "Neighbor Xiao Peizhi respectfully presents" at the end. By evening, recalling Yuzhen's beautiful appearance, he wished to go again, but regretted that his dreams were no longer vivid. Soon after, a woman pulled the curtain and entered; looking at her, it was indeed Su Yuzhen, the girl from the east neighbor, and he was greatly pleased. She also said, "What are you doing here at night?" The girl seemed dazed and confused, not knowing what to do. Xiao then embraced her, her cheeks and eyes gazing, her emotions and demeanor difficult to describe.

    168

    欲与欢好，女不可，曰：“不嫌媸丑，愿琴瑟永谐；若私合，则决不敢从。盖妾一失节，君必厌弃，彼时妾既不可以二夫，势又难以归君，终身无依，苦何如之。”萧矢以必娶，乃以家藏翡翠玉如意为凭，女亦以揥发小金如意为赠，斯时惟听萧生之所为矣。而女忽杳，萧深以为憾。次夕，狐至。萧以连宵之事告之，狐但微笑。萧曰：“此皆卿之所为耶？”言之面有愠色。狐曰：“君欲与玉真作夫妇，度君于彼，度女于此，正以笃君伉俪之情，乃以不得苟合怼妾，岂一日之欢可毕百年之好乎？”培之怃然谢过。

    Explanation

    168

    Desiring to be intimate with her, the woman refused, saying: "I do not disdain ugliness and beauty; I only wish for a harmonious and lasting relationship. If we were to secretly unite, I would absolutely not agree. If I were to lose my virtue, you would surely reject me. At that time, I could not have two husbands, and the circumstances would make it difficult to return to you. I would be left alone for life, how terrible it would be!" Xiao vowed to definitely marry her, and presented his family's jade如意 as a token of his commitment. The woman also gave him a small gold如意 used for combing hair as a gift. At that time, she would only follow Xiao's lead. However, the woman suddenly disappeared, and Xiao deeply regretted it. The next evening, a fox appeared. Xiao told the fox about the events of the previous night, and the fox merely smiled. Xiao said, "Is this all your doing?" His face showed a hint of anger. The fox replied, "You wished to be husband and wife with Yuzhen, guiding you to her and me to you, all to strengthen your marital bond. Yet you resent me for not allowing a fleeting union. Is a single night's pleasure enough to fulfill a lifetime of happiness?" Xiao looked thoughtful and apologized.

    169

    苏孝廉虽拒萧媒，比邻而居，时相往来。一日，偶诣萧斋，值萧不在，见案头有诗一首，视之，乃其女所作之七绝，评语甚亵，遂怀归。复于女闺门外拾萧生赠诗，大疑，告其妻。妻曰：“谓女与有私，吾家门之深严，则断断不能；若云其无，何以女诗在彼，生诗在此？其中必有他故。不若示意于萧，使渠通媒灼，既结丝萝，则群疑皆释。”苏以为然。言际，玉真之大婢春芳窃听之，遂告玉真。中心暗喜。苏甫欲示意于萧，而萧中乡科矣；复欲示意，而萧会且殿，官翰林院编修矣。向也萧通媒于苏而苏不欲，今也苏欲示意于萧而萧遽贵，苏恐有攀援之议，事遂中寝。

    169

    Scholar Su Xianlin, though he had initially refused Xiao's proposal of marriage, lived next door and often visited each other. One day, he happened to visit Xiao's study, only to find Xiao absent. On the desk, he saw a poem, which he read and found it to be a seven-character quatrain written by Xiao's daughter, with comments that were very disrespectful. He then took the poem back. Later, he also picked up Xiao's poem given to the girl outside her room, greatly suspicious, and told his wife. His wife said, "If you say that the daughter had an affair, our family's strict rules would certainly not allow it; if you say it is not true, why is the daughter's poem there and Xiao's poem here? There must be some other reason. It would be better to hint to Xiao, to make him go through the formal process of matchmaking. Once the marriage is arranged, all doubts will be resolved." Scholar Su agreed. While talking, the chief maid Chunfang of Yu Zhen secretly listened and then told Yu Zhen, who secretly delighted. Scholar Su was just about to hint to Xiao, but Xiao passed the provincial examination; again, he wanted to hint, but Xiao was about to become a palace scholar and became a compiler in the Hanlin Academy. Before, Xiao tried to arrange a match with Su but Su did not want to; now, Su wants to hint to Xiao but Xiao has suddenly become prestigious. Scholar Su feared there might be rumors of seeking favors, and the matter was thus abandoned.

    170

    初，玉真闻大婢春芳之言，以为心愿易遂。后闻萧贵，而姻事未有成说，衷怀蕴结，针黹懒作，茶饭亦渐减，遂恹恹似病。春芳窥其心，曰：“姑娘以婢作心腹，如有心事，房中可与言者惟婢一人。”女不答。移时春芳复曰：“得无为萧郎之事乎？”女闻之愕然，以心事被渠猜破，遂曰：“实为此。”因将梦魂之事，历历言之。且曰：“有信物在此，欲遣人执往以探其意，惜无其人。”春芳曰： “婢男妆，令王老阍人伴往，其可乎？”女曰：“可，但难言于父母。”春芳曰：“奴代禀之。”玉真有庶母弟某与女同庚，春芳遂冒其名而往。

    Explanation

    170

    Initially, Yu Zhen heard what the senior maid Chun Fang had to say and thought her wishes would be easily fulfilled. Later, upon hearing about Xiao Gui, and since there was no definite arrangement for the marriage, she became deeply troubled, losing interest in her needlework and gradually reducing her meals, eventually appearing listless and ill. Chun Fang observed her inner turmoil and said, "Miss, you consider me as your confidante. If you have any heart matters, the only one in the room who can speak with you is me." Yu Zhen did not respond. After a while, Chun Fang repeated, "Is it not about the matter of Xiao Lang?" Yu Zhen was startled to have her inner thoughts guessed and admitted, "It is indeed about this." She then recounted her dreams and experiences in detail. She added, "I have a token of sincerity here, and I want to send someone to deliver it to test his feelings, but I lack a suitable person." Chun Fang suggested, "I can dress as a man and accompany an old gatekeeper to go there. Would that be acceptable?" Yu Zhen replied, "It would be fine, but it would be difficult to explain to my parents." Chun Fang volunteered, "I will speak to them on your behalf." Yu Zhen had a step-brother of the same age, and Chun Fang decided to go under his name.

    171

    既相见，萧曰：“君非苏某兄。”春芳曰：“然，弟乃苏某兄之表弟王某。”萧见其丰姿清秀，举止儒雅，心甚爱之，曰：“足下见弟，有何指教？”春芳曰：“家表兄有一物，言系阁下所遗，遣弟送还之。”遂出一物交培之。视之，乃所赠玉真之翡翠玉如意也。大骇曰：“令表姊字人乎？”曰：“未也。”“将议字人乎？”曰：“亦未也。”春芳佯问曰：“阁下睹物，辄问家表姊字人与否，其何以故？”培之曰：“实告君，弟与令表姊梦会二次，信誓旦旦，尝以是物为赠。今烦足下送还者，试余心耳。请将原物带回，弟心犹初心，迄今未少易也。”遂将如意仍交春芳。

    Explanation

    171

    After they had met, Xiao said, "You are not Brother Su." Chunfang replied, "Indeed, I am Brother Su’s cousin, Wang." Xiao admired his refined appearance and scholarly demeanor, and he felt a deep affection for him. He asked, "What advice do you have for me?" Chunfang said, "My cousin has an object, which he says belongs to you, and he has sent me to return it to you." He then handed an object to Xiao. Upon seeing it, Xiao realized it was the jade green jade lucky charm that he had given Chunfang’s cousin. He was greatly startled and asked, "Has your cousin been betrothed?" Chunfang replied, "No, she has not." "Is she about to be betrothed?" "No, she is not either." Chunfang asked mockingly, "Why do you, upon seeing this object, immediately ask whether my cousin has been betrothed?" Xiao said frankly, "I will tell you frankly. I have dreamt of meeting your cousin twice, and we made solemn vows. I once gave this object as a gift. I am testing my heart. Please return the original object. My heart remains as it was at first, and it has not changed since then." He then returned the jade lucky charm to Chunfang.

    172

    既而庖人奉馔，培之执杯劝饮，春芳辞以素不会饮。时狐从培之在京，遂耳语曰：“客非苏之表弟，实玉真之大婢春芳也。”萧闻之大喜，遂执杯强劝之。春芳不得已，勉饮一杯，飞红上双颊，灯下观之，尤艳绝。培之曰：“敢问妙龄？”答言十七。曰：“令正青春？”答曰：“长弟二春，尚未过门。”培之笑曰：“摽梅愆期，在足下或可支持，令正当抱子之年，尚未经人道，可谓怨女矣！”春芳闻之，羞红满面，无以自容。未几，席终彻馔，从人皆散。培之曰：“今宵与足下同榻如何？”春芳辞以不惯。

    Explanation

    172

    Afterward, the cook served the meal, and Pēi Zhī took a cup to urge Chūnfāng to drink, but she refused, saying she never drinks. At that moment, Hú, who was with Pēi Zhī in the capital, whispered, "The guest is not Su's cousin, but actually Chūnfāng, a servant of Yùzhēn." Xiāo, upon hearing this, was greatly pleased and insisted on offering her a drink. Chūnfāng, unable to refuse, reluctantly drank a cup, and a flush rose to her cheeks. Under the lamp, she appeared especially beautiful. Pēi Zhī asked, "May I inquire about your age?" She replied, seventeen. He then asked, "Is your wife still young?" She answered, "My younger brother is two years old, and the marriage has not yet taken place." Pēi Zhī laughed and said, "The plum tree is delayed in blooming; perhaps you can endure it, but at your age, you should be bearing children and have not yet experienced intimacy. You could be called a woman who laments her unmarried status!" Chūnfāng, upon hearing this, blushed deeply and was ashamed to face others. Shortly after, the banquet ended, and the dishes were cleared. The attendants all left. Pēi Zhī then said, "What about sharing a bed tonight?" Chūnfāng refused, saying she was not accustomed to it.

    173

    培之曰：“足下花烛后，洞房亦将自寝耶？”春芳曰：“渠系女子，固自乐意。”培之曰：“卿系男子，仆亦不乐意与卿同榻矣。”春芳闻之，含羞不语。培之曰：“仆素识卿，为玉真闺中良友，今自投罗网，尚能逃乎？鱼网之设，鸿则离之，仆何得之巧也？”因而拥女于怀，欲与欢好。女曰：“妾已到此，势固难免。但妾奉命而来，苟事先主人，不惟妾陷不义，君亦不情。不嫌微贱，愿作小星，今则未敢从命。”培之再三恳求，女曰：“无已，请不脱中衣，听君之所为，他则请死不从。”未几，狐入。培之向狐言之。狐曰：“真义女也，可听之以成其义。”培之归，言其事于母。母令娶苏为伉俪，纳春芳为副室，狐亦遂绝。

    　　虚白道人曰：合婚姻于两家，各如其意，而不致勉强；度男女于一处，两笃其情，而不令苟合，皆用情之正也。而狐之撮合若是，狐亦近人情矣。吾人之情，用之于偏则偏，用之于正则正，用情者可不慎与？

    Explanation

    173

    Péi said, "After the flower candles, will you sleep alone in the bridal chamber?" Chunfang replied, "She is a woman, and naturally happy." Péi said, "You are a man, and I am not willing to share a bed with you." Chunfang heard this and blushed, remaining silent. Péi said, "I have known you for a long time, and you are a good friend of Yuzhen. Now that you have voluntarily entered a dangerous situation, can you still escape? Even a well-set trap cannot catch a wise person, how clever am I?" He then embraced the woman, desiring to engage in intimacy. The woman said, "I have come here, and it is inevitable. But I came here on orders, and if I precede the master, not only will I fall into impropriety, but you will also be unfeeling. If you do not嫌弃 my lowly status, I am willing to be a minor star, but I dare not obey now." Péi persisted in his requests, and the woman said, "If there is no other way, please do not remove my middle clothes, and allow me to submit to your will, but I will die rather than comply with anything else." Soon after, the fox entered. Péi told the fox about the situation. The fox said, "She is truly a virtuous woman, and you should allow her to maintain her integrity." Péi returned home and told his mother about the matter. His mother ordered him to marry Su as his wife and take Chunfang as a concubine, and the fox finally ceased its involvement.

    　　Xubai Daoist said: To unite marriages between two families, each according to their own wishes, without forcing anyone; to bring men and women together in one place, fostering deep affection without allowing hasty union, all are expressions of proper emotion. And the fox's arrangement was so, showing that the fox also understands human feelings. Our emotions, when used improperly, lead to imbalance, but when used properly, they lead to harmony. How can those who use emotions be careless?

    174

    苏孝廉以萧贵而辍婚议，亦自不凡。 马竹吾

    　　狐以两诗作合，可谓之诗媒。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　义狼

    　　省会东南多山，狼时成群，山村人习见之，亦无大恐。有木工贾才者，屡行山路，见一小狼哀鸣于狼穴之口。盖大狼为猎者获之，故小狼啼饥也。才乃抱之归，刀其尾，取名如意，当犬养之。及二年，大于犬，家人叱之，尚有畏意，里人不敢恶意以向。嗣狼于邻里之童子目视眈眈，若有吞噬之意。才惧其伤人，谓之曰：“当日汝母已死，非我抱养于家，早饿死山中。兹已长大，不宜常在庄中，今日送汝归山。”遂饱之以饭，送之深山。才回而狼随之，才曰：“送我乎？可不必送。”狼乃止。才行里许，回视之，狼犹蹲石遥望也。

    Explanation

    174

    Su Xiaolian, a scholar-official, stopped the marriage proposal due to Xie's nobility, which is indeed extraordinary. Ma Zhugu

    The fox arranged the marriage through two poems, which can be called a poetic matchmaker. Shangyuan Li Yu respectfully annotates.

    The Righteous Wolf

    The southeast of the provincial capital has many mountains, and wolves often gather in groups. People in mountain villages are accustomed to seeing them and are not greatly afraid. There was a carpenter named Cai, who frequently traveled through the mountain paths. One day, he saw a small wolf crying pitifully at the entrance of a wolf den. It seemed that the mother wolf had been captured by hunters, so the small wolf was hungry. Cai took it back, cut off its tail, and named it "As You Wish," raising it like a dog. After two years, the wolf grew larger than a dog. The family scolded it, but it still had a sense of fear. People in the village did not dare to treat it maliciously. The wolf then looked at the children in the neighborhood with a fierce gaze, as if it had the intention to devour them. Cai was afraid it would hurt someone and said to it, "If your mother had not died, I would not have taken you in, and you would have starved to death in the mountains. Now that you have grown up, you should not stay in the village. Today I am sending you back to the mountains." He then fed it a full meal and sent it to the deep mountains. Cai returned, and the wolf followed him. Cai said, "Are you sending me? You don't need to send me." The wolf stopped. After walking about a mile, he looked back, and the wolf was still sitting on a rock, looking far away.

    175

    才原木工，恒日暮后归家，一日少晚，至中途，遇三狼当道。才虽手持丈竿，亦难恃以无恐，幸身后三步外即峭壁，遂退而依之，以护后身。既而复来二狼，未几，有十馀头，环居面前。才窘极，大言曰：“今吾合死于此矣！”言已，一狼闻其语音，忽起，头向外，尾向才，退及才之面前，似欲使才视其尾也者。时月色微明，才视狼无尾，知是如意，曰：“如意救我！”如意力逐群狼使散。才甚喜，曰：“如意送我！”至家，才曰：“吾今不遇如意，葬于狼腹中矣！”以饭饱之使去。自此如意屡候于途，遇则送之。即庄人遇群狼，内有短尾者，以如意呼之，辄率群狼而去。

    Explanation

    175

    Tài, the original woodworker, consistently returned home after dusk. One day, he was slightly late and, on his way, encountered three wolves blocking his path. Although he held a long pole, it was difficult to rely on it to feel fearless. Fortunately, three steps behind him was a sheer cliff, so he retreated and leaned against it to protect his back. Soon after, two more wolves came, and not long after, there were over ten, surrounding him in front. He was extremely distressed and said loudly, "Now I am destined to die here!" After he spoke, one wolf heard his voice and suddenly stood up, head facing outward, tail pointing towards Tài, retreating to in front of him, as if wanting to make him see its tail. At that time, the moonlight was faint, and Tài saw that the wolf had no tail, knowing it was Rúyì. He called out, "Rúyì, save me!" Rúyì forcefully drove away the pack of wolves. He was very happy and said, "Rúyì, send me!" Upon arriving home, he said, "If I hadn't met Rúyì today, I would have been buried in the wolves' bellies!" He fed it with food to make it leave. From then on, Rúyì often waited on the road, and when encountered, it would send him. When villagers encountered a pack of wolves, among them was one with a short tail, and calling it Rúyì, it would lead the pack away.

    176

    一夜，才闻扣门声急，起问之，不应。启门视之，一狼突入，伏于庭前不动。才疑是如意，烛之，果然。见狼汗出如洗，旁有布褡，内有白金二百两。盖众狼伤行客，遗此于路，衔来以报才恩也。后辄夜间衔物来，才以小康。又久之，白日亦来，或伏才家，或卧街巷，好事者每以饭食之。才卒，葬于庄外，人屡见如意卧墓旁，年馀始不见。

    Explanation

    176

    One night, the talent heard the urgent knocking on the door. He got up to ask, but there was no response. When he opened the door and looked out, a wolf suddenly rushed in and lay motionless in front of the courtyard. Suspecting it to be "Like Intention," he lit a candle to see, and indeed it was. He saw the wolf sweating profusely, with a cloth bag beside it containing two hundred taels of white gold. It is said that the pack of wolves had injured a traveler, left this on the road, and brought it to repay the talent's kindness. Later, they would come at night bringing objects, and the talent lived in relative comfort. After some time, the wolf would also come during the day, sometimes lying in the talent's house and sometimes resting in the streets. Those who were fond of it often fed it with food. When the talent died, he was buried outside the village. People often saw "Like Intention" lying by the grave, and after more than a year, it was no longer seen.

    177

    虚白道人曰：古语云：“狼子野心。”狠毒之心，惟此物为最。然不忘养育之恩，辄有以报之，则人背恩负义者，愧斯狼矣。

    　　人面狼心者，吾闻其语矣；狼面人心者，未见其人也。施报分明，可以人而不如狼乎？ 盖防如

    　　豺字从才，狼字从良，兽之有才良者也。观此而宋王懿之白狼童子又不足言矣！ 上元李瑜谨注

    　　姚 五 官

    　　姚法武，农人也。家赀不裕，可足当年吃著。妻惠氏，生一女，乳名五官。十岁时母卒。姚复娶羊喻利之妹为继室，生一子，甫二岁而姚卒。羊言于其妹曰：“妹正青春，无人照应，若将此处产业尽货于人，吾庄邻近择市沃田，兄为兼理，岂不两便？”氏以为美意，从之。羊乃阴将姚产粮籍悉改拨己名，而其妹不知也。

    Explanation

    177

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: An ancient saying goes, "A wolf cub has a wolf's heart." Of all creatures, only this one possesses the most cruel and vicious nature. Yet, it never forgets the kindness of nurture and always finds a way to repay it. Therefore, those who betray kindness and act unrighteously should be ashamed before this wolf.

    Those with human faces but wolf hearts, I have heard of them; those with wolf faces but human hearts, I have not seen such a person. Distinctions between giving and receiving are clear. Can humans be inferior to wolves in this regard? It is said that

    The character for jackal is composed of "talent," and the character for wolf is composed of "goodness." These are animals that possess some qualities of talent and goodness. Considering this, even the legendary white wolf child associated with the Song Dynasty ruler Wang Yi is not worth mentioning! This is respectfully annotated by Shangyuan Li Yu.

    Gao Wuguan

    Yao Fawu was a farmer. His family's wealth was not abundant, just enough for the current year's needs. His wife, Lady Hui, gave birth to a daughter, whom they called Wuguan. At the age of ten, her mother passed away. Yao then married the sister of Yang Yuli as his second wife, and she gave birth to a son who was just two years old when Yao died. Yang said to her sister, "You are still in the prime of your youth, and no one is taking care of you. If you sell all the family's property to others, and I choose fertile fields near the market for you, and I will manage them for you, wouldn't that be mutually convenient?" The sister thought it was a good idea and agreed. Yang then secretly changed all of Yao's property records to her own name, while her sister was unaware of this.

    178

    有富室张某，爱五官慧丽，欲购作妾，知羊为渠舅氏，遂诣羊所，直言其事而许以白金二百两。羊大喜，遂将五官诳至其家，强付张从人舁去。其银则羊吞使过半，氏未得蓰之一焉。嗣逼氏醮，氏以夫死未久，且以有三岁幼子，不忍委弃，矢不改嫁。羊言之再四，氏终不从。羊邻庄有棍徒李某，闻羊妹美而寡，烦羊媒之，啖以重酬。羊亦不与妹谋，令李强夺之以去。氏至李家，矢不与李同寝，百词劝解，氏卒不听。李怒，赤其身而鞭之，上下几无完肤，滨于死矣。比伤愈，复欲犯之，氏仍不从。李怒曰：“吾为汝费用十馀金，人财两空，吾岂甘心！”氏曰：“若然，吾以原金自赎可乎？”李曰：“可。”氏所得五官身价犹在，遂举以授李，李乃听氏自去。氏欲依兄，恐复逼令改节，遂栖止孤贫院后，佣于富室作针黹，而去家少远矣。羊氏所生之子，姚在时命名曰增，羊视如犬豕。年方五岁，即使丐食于乡，见之者无不恨羊之无良也。

    Explanation

    178

    A Wealthy Man Zhang

    There was a wealthy man named Zhang who fell in love with a woman named Wuguan because of her beauty. He wished to buy her as a concubine and knew that Yang was her maternal uncle. So he went to Yang's place, directly stated his intention, and offered two hundred taels of silver. Yang was very happy and tricked Wuguan to his home, then forcibly handed her over to Zhang's people who carried her away. The silver was mostly taken by Yang, and Wuguan did not receive even a fraction of it. Later, Yang forced Wuguan to divorce her husband. Wuguan, whose husband had recently died and who had a three-year-old son, could not bear to abandon them, and vowed not to remarry. Yang spoke to her repeatedly, but Wuguan still refused.

    Yang's neighbor, a ruffian named Li, heard that Yang's sister was beautiful and widowed, and asked Yang to act as a matchmaker, offering a large reward. Yang did not consult his sister and ordered Li to forcibly take her away. Wuguan went to Li's house, vowed not to sleep with him, and despite many attempts to persuade her, she refused to comply. Li became angry, stripped her naked and beat her, leaving her almost dead. After she recovered, Li again wanted to violate her, but Wuguan still refused. Li angrily said, "I spent more than ten taels of silver for you, and now both man and money are wasted, how can I be satisfied!" Wuguan said, "If so, may I redeem myself with the original money?" Li said, "Yes." Wuguan still had the money she received from Zhang, so she gave it to Li, and Li allowed her to leave.

    Wuguan wanted to stay with her brother, but feared he would again force her to change her status, so she stayed in a poor house behind a temple, worked for a wealthy household doing needlework, and lived far from home. Yang's son, whom Yao named Zeng when he was alive, was treated by Yang like a dog or pig. At five years old, Yang forced him to beg in the village, and those who saw him all hated Yang for his wickedness.

    179

    五官媵于张室，嫡妻妒忌，竟日不堪其苦，幸得张某钟爱，稍自宽慰。闻弟增羸弱乞食，中心如结，不得已向张哀恳，求其悯怜。张知其妻不能容物，以增托至戚孟翁。孟固良善，见增稚弱，付佣媪顾赡之，时给钱纟昏以资衣履。及长，身价与佣工同，且将增每岁所得代权子母，年逾弱冠，积项若干矣。五官在张室无所出，张卒，嫡不能容，遂遣五官使返羊所。羊见其颜色未衰，欲纳为妾，五官不从，羊使其妻醉之以酒而淫之。五官见羊辄詈，且日寻自尽，羊惧，欲售之，苦无主。忽忆甥在孟氏，渐成家业，遂托异姓女欲醮，劝增娶之。增从之。及过门，姊与弟本不相识，五官见房舍系母家故物，遂谓增曰：“汝非姚增乎？”增曰：“然。”曰：“吾系汝之胞姐，以姊弟作夫妇，行同禽兽矣！”遂令增各寝。次日，五官令增将羊未字之女诳至，强令与增合卺。

    Explanation

    179

    Five Officials served as a concubine in Zhang's household. The principal wife was jealous, and she endured unbearable suffering all day long. Fortunately, she was favored by Master Zhang, which slightly comforted her. Hearing that her brother Zeng was weak and begging for food, she felt deeply distressed. Out of necessity, she pleaded with Zhang, asking for his compassion and mercy. Zhang knew that his wife could not tolerate others, so he entrusted Zeng to his close relative Meng Gong. Meng Gong was kind and good-hearted. Seeing that Zeng was young and weak, he entrusted a hired woman to take care of him, providing him with small amounts of money to buy clothes and shoes. When Zeng grew up, his status was the same as that of a hired worker, and Meng Gong managed his earnings each year, accumulating a certain amount by the time he reached the age of twenty. Five Officials had no children in Zhang's household. After Zhang's death, the principal wife could not tolerate her, so she sent Five Officials back to Yang's place. Yang saw that she was still in good health and wanted to take her as a concubine. Five Officials refused, and Yang made his wife get her drunk with wine and defiled her. Whenever Five Officials saw Yang, she would curse him, and she often sought to commit suicide. Yang feared her, wanting to sell her, but found no buyer. Suddenly remembering her nephew in the Meng family, who had gradually established a family business, she entrusted a woman from another family to marry, advising Zeng to marry her. Zeng followed her advice. When he passed through the door, his sister and brother did not originally know each other. Five Officials saw that the house belonged to her mother's family and said to Zeng, "Are you not Yao Zeng?" Zeng replied, "Yes." She said, "I am your biological sister. For a sister and brother to marry, it is the same as the behavior of beasts!" She then ordered Zeng to sleep separately. The next day, Five Officials ordered Zeng to bring Yang's unmarried daughter to deceive her into coming, forcing her to marry Zeng.

    180

    五官畏羊凶横，哀邻里护庇。数日，羊闻之，果来强夺女，势将用武。邻佑俱忿，羊惧，始自去。次日，羊讼于官，五官亦喊禀在案。官问五官曰：“诱羊女强为弟妇，果有诸？”曰：“诚有之，然有下情上诉。”遂将霸产等情，历历言之，且曰：“渠将氏嫁增，以同胞作伉俪，灭绝天伦，幸被氏识破，未至乱伦。氏实情急，故将渠女诳至，配于氏弟。”言已大哭。官闻之大怒，当堂重责羊某，且将羊女判为增妇。官谓五官曰：“汝气可以平乎？”五官曰：“姚氏之产，渠不合霸去；嫁母卖氏之资，渠不宜吞使；祈一一追还。”官问羊之产业，羊实言之。盖羊霸产以后，复置田亩若干。官曰：“一半足汝享用。”遂断给姚增一半为业，案乃结。增遂寻归母氏，五官有私蓄，母氏有剩馀，而增遂成殷实之家。未几，羊夫妇继亡。羊无子，别无戚属，产业悉归于增。

    Explanation

    180

    The Five Senses feared Yang's violent and oppressive behavior and pitied the neighbors who protected and sheltered them. After several days, upon hearing this, Yang indeed came to forcibly take the daughter, intending to use force. The neighbors and protectors all became angry, and Yang, fearing retribution, finally left on his own. The next day, Yang sued in court, and the Five Senses also reported the case to the authorities. The judge asked the Five Senses, "Did you entice Yang's daughter and force her to be the wife of your younger brother?" They replied, "Indeed, it is true, but there is a lower-level complaint to appeal." They then clearly explained the circumstances of seizing property and other matters, and said, "He intended to marry her to Zeng, with his own sibling as a married couple, destroying the natural order of family relationships. Fortunately, she recognized and exposed this, preventing incest. In her desperation, she deceived her to come and married her to her younger brother." After speaking, they cried loudly. The judge, upon hearing this, became very angry and severely punished Yang in court, and judged Yang's daughter to be the wife of Zeng. The judge asked the Five Senses, "Can your anger be calmed?" The Five Senses said, "Yao's property, he should not have seized it; the funds from the mother-in-law who sold her daughter, he should not have swallowed them; we pray to recover them one by one." The judge asked about Yang's property, and Yang truthfully stated it. Since Yang seized the property, he later acquired several fields. The judge said, "Half is sufficient for your enjoyment." He then decided to give half of the property to Yao and Zeng, and the case was concluded. Zeng then returned to his mother. The Five Senses had private savings, and his mother had surplus, and thus Zeng became a wealthy family. Shortly after, Yang and his wife died in succession. Yang had no children, and no other relatives, so all of his property belonged to Zeng.

    181

    虚白道人曰：羊某之恶，可谓极矣！然霸甥产而己产悉归于甥，市姚女而己女终氏于姚，欺人者恒自欺，盖天理循环之常也。

    Explanation

    181

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: "The evil of Yang can be said to be extreme! However, when the son of a powerful man is born, all the wealth of the father belongs to the son; when a woman is bought from Yao, the woman of the buyer eventually belongs to Yao. Those who deceive others always deceive themselves. It is the constant law of heaven's way."

    182

    淫人妻女，报在妻女。天网恢恢，疏而不漏。 盖防如

    　　报应痛快，宜浮大白赏之。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　瑞红

    　　山西平阳尹尚德，郡诸生。素封已十世，故家第为一乡之冠。舍旁花园，通于内室，园内修楼厅，以为留客读书所。花木成蹊，亭榭相联。有方塘半亩许，外接小渚，以铁篦密笼其口，内养横尾金银鱼数百头。至尚德时，长尺许、粗如碗口者不可胜数，一时浮游，池水尽赤，洵一方之巨观也。初，尚德之伯无子，以尚德继伯，则生父无子，其伯与父遂议各为娶一妻室，生孙则各承其祧。适有邵某孪生二女，与尚德年相若，伯媒其长，父聘其次，同日定祥，容华双绝。次年又各生一子，尚德喜之不胜。然双斧交伐，体渐瘦弱，复值生继之亲相继俱终，积劳成疾，医不效。因移居花园中，独宿书室以养疴，佣一僮服事焉。

    Explanation

    182
