If the fox were to repay you with resentment, even though it is a different kind of creature, it would not act so wickedly. It can be known that if one has no intention to harm others, others will not have the intention to harm you. The virtue of cherishing life is not a trivial matter!

    Writing casually can be entertaining, and it can make one's thoughts dissipate. — Ma Zhugui

    This fox was indeed full of emotion. The Grand Secretary was greedy for extending his own life, causing the fox to die, thus betraying the fox! — Ye Yunsheng

    The narrative is concise, but the commentary does not match the thoroughness of Ye Yunsheng's. — Fishing and Woodcutting Scattered Man

    The Grand Secretary, a Confucian scholar, has such elegant and graceful words, showing that writing the Plum Blossom Ode by Song Guangping does not hinder his iron will. — Shangyuan Li Yu, carefully annotated

    224

    琼 华 岛

    　　向青云，琼州人。聪明盖世，工于染翰，遐迩知名，而不得采一芹。其父在日，以泛海为业；家计萧条，仍理父业。同伙七人，俱以向文弱，使司会计，不令操重务。向念十馀年功苦一旦尽付流水，未免心热，遂将素肄诗文悉携船上，另置一舱，停舟时披读，以破闷怀。一日，放船正好，忽西北风大作，急下锚而锚本断，紧持舵而舵杆折，舟忽纵忽横，惟任其随风飘流而已。其风三日夜不止，及至风息船住，六人尽没于水，惟存向一人。视舱水已过半，幸船止于山麓，可跃而下，遂急运书籍粮米等物。运甫毕而船沉。上视十数步外有石洞，虽不深广，亦可暂避风雨。向已三日不食，急欲造饭，而船上所载之淡水已没于海，不得已以海水煮米，咸难下咽。甫二日，哮喘难支。忽见洞旁有湿痕，审视，细流涓涓，殆类器漏。尝之，甚甘。以盂接之，半日仅溢两碗许，然以为饮食，亦足一人需，方寸为之少宽。

    Explanation

    224

    琼华岛

    Xiang Qingyun, a man from Qiongzhou, was exceptionally intelligent and skilled in writing and calligraphy. He was famous far and wide, yet he failed to pass the imperial examination. His father made a living by sailing the sea, and the family was poor. Xiang continued his father's business. Among the seven companions he was with, all considered him weak and appointed him to handle accounting, not allowing him to take on heavy tasks. Thinking that years of hard work would be lost in an instant, Xiang became frustrated and decided to take all his poems and writings on the boat, placing them in a separate cabin. He read them when the boat stopped, to relieve his boredom. One day, as the boat was set to sail well, suddenly a strong northwest wind arose. He quickly dropped anchor, but the anchor broke. He held the rudder tightly, but the rudder broke, and the boat suddenly swayed back and forth, only allowing it to drift with the wind. The wind did not stop for three days and nights. When the wind finally ceased and the boat came to a halt, the six others were all drowned, leaving only Xiang alive. He looked and saw the water in the cabin had risen halfway. Fortunately, the boat came to a stop at the foot of a mountain, where he could jump ashore. He quickly moved the books, grains, and other supplies. Just as he finished, the boat sank. Looking up, he saw a stone cave about ten or more steps away, though not deep or wide, it could serve as a temporary shelter from wind and rain. Xiang had not eaten for three days and was eager to cook food. But the fresh water on the boat had been washed away by the sea, so he was forced to cook rice with seawater, the saltiness of which was hard to swallow. After just two days, he was suffering from asthma and could barely stand. Suddenly, he noticed a damp mark beside the cave. Examining it, he saw a thin stream trickling, almost like a leak. He tasted it and found it very sweet. Using a bowl to collect it, in half a day it only filled two bowls, but as drinking water, it was enough for one person, and his mind was slightly eased.

    225

    饭后，时于洞左右游瞩，半里之遥，见有蚰蜒小径，似尝有人往来。向遂衣冠，攀藤附葛而上，忽见鸡犬桑麻，无异中国。少顷，有数少年自庄中出，皆明时服饰，见向，俱惊讶却退。未几，一老人来曰：“此华夏人物。”邀至家，具酒食，问向姓名。向以实告。老人曰：“君衣冠是时王之制否？”向曰：“然。”老人曰：“此地名琼华岛，地面纵横各四百里。岛人惟张、赵二姓，世结婚姻，相传系蜀汉名将之后。盖安乐公东迁之时，张、赵二公之后人有隐于海滨者，后凌夷，以船为家，被风飘泊至此。

    Explanation

    225

    After the meal, Xiang roamed and gazed around the cave. Half a li away, he saw a winding, narrow path that seemed to have been traveled by people. He then dressed in formal attire and climbed up by clinging to vines and climbing plants. Suddenly, he saw chickens, dogs, and mulberry trees, no different from what one might find in China. A short while later, several youths came out from the village, all dressed in clothing of the Ming era. When they saw Xiang, they were all surprised and stepped back. Soon after, an old man came over and said, "This is a person from the Chinese mainland." He invited Xiang to his home, prepared a feast, and asked Xiang's name. Xiang told him the truth. The old man asked, "Is your attire in accordance with the emperor's regulations of the time?" Xiang replied, "Yes." The old man said, "This place is called Qionghua Island. The island spans four hundred miles in both length and width. The island's inhabitants are only the Zhang and Zhao surnames, who have intermarried for generations. It is said they are descendants of famous generals of Shu Han. When Anle Gong (the last ruler of Shu Han) was exiled eastward, some of Zhang and Zhao's descendants hid by the seaside. Later, they declined and lived on boats, being blown here by the wind."

    226

    彼时惟各遗一幼子，天降二仙女以为之配，生育日繁，迄今丁壮已有二十馀万，吾张公之后。”向曰：“有君师否？”曰：“有之。众于二姓中择秉性平和者数人，阄立其一，听其约束。若薨，另行择立，不世及。”向问赋税轻重。老人曰：“此地无所谓赋税，有沃田三十顷，摊夫耕种，以所获子粒奉君上，其馀地亩毫无所敛。”向方欲再问风土人情，老人曰：“有一奇事，吾有笄女未字于人，连宵梦仙人曰‘当配中华人’。因思亘古未见中华人，梦何妄也？今君到此，天作之合，今宵即令事君。”向唯唯。

    Explanation

    226

    At that time, each left behind only one young son. Heaven sent two fairy maidens to marry them, and their offspring multiplied daily. By now, there are over twenty thousand strong men and women, descendants of my Master Zhang. Xiang asked, "Do you have a ruler or teacher?" The old man replied, "Yes. From among several people chosen for their even temper, they draw lots to appoint one as their leader, and follow his commands. If he dies, they will choose another in the same way, and it will not pass down through generations." Xiang then asked about the lightness or heaviness of the taxes. The old man said, "There is no such thing as taxes here. There are thirty hectares of fertile land, which is distributed among the farmers to cultivate, and the grain they harvest is given to the ruler. No other land is taxed." Xiang was about to ask more about the customs and people, when the old man said, "There is a strange event: I have a daughter who has come of age but is not yet married, and she has had a dream for several nights of an immortal who said, 'She should marry a man from Central China.' I thought that since Central Chinese men have never been seen before, the dream must be false. Now that you have arrived, it is a match made by heaven, and tonight she will be given to you." Xiang nodded in agreement.

    227

    老人辞去。向思羁旅之人，每苦无所依，今得婚此，窃喜衣食有赖。然及燃灯时无耗，向心疑曰：“事中寝耶？抑另择吉日耶？”未几，有红妆丽人出，曰：“请官人就寝。”向莫知所以，坚坐不动。女复曰：“卧榻在内室，请官人从妾入。”向遂从女入，见室炳双烛，红帐鲜新，而帐内无人，疑甚。既而丽人合户，始知丽人即新人。向曰：“合卺之礼，仅如此也？”女曰：“此上等仪礼。下次室无灯烛，欢会终夜，不知妍媸。

    Explanation

    227

    The old man left. Xiang, a traveler who often suffered from having no place to rely on, now found himself married, secretly rejoicing that food and clothing were dependent on this union. However, when the lamp was lit, there was no sign of the expected ceremony. Xiang's heart doubted and said, "Did it sleep in the matter? Or did they choose another auspicious day?" Not long after, a beautiful woman in red makeup appeared and said, "Please, the official, go to sleep." Xiang did not know why, and stayed firmly seated without moving. The woman repeated, "The bed is in the inner chamber; please, the official, follow me inside." Xiang then followed the woman into the room, where he saw two bright candles and a fresh, red canopy. Yet, there was no one inside the canopy, and he was very puzzled. Soon after, the woman closed the door, and Xiang realized that the woman who had closed the door was the bride. He said, "Is the ceremony of the joined cups only like this?" The woman replied, "This is the highest level of ritual. Next time, the room will have no lamps or candles, and there will be rejoicing and meeting all night, but it is uncertain whether it will be beautiful or ugly."

    228

    ”向不觉为之喷饭，曰：“明晨有何礼数？”女曰：“无之，但随妾称呼已耳。”及寝，俨然处子，而毫无羞惭。次日，向曰：“海边尚存用物若干，祈遣人从仆往取。”女从之。女见书曰：“此何物？”向以书对。女曰：“有何用处？”曰：“读之可得功名。”曰：“君试读与妾听。”向遂披时文，按节循拍，抑扬读之。女曰：“高低相宜，顿挫有节，岛中之歌无出此右者。”向复将五、七言律诗对女读之。女曰：“语异韵同，更觉盈耳。”向读于室，女听之，恒终夜不厌。

    Explanation

    228

    Xiang was so amused that he spit out his food, saying, "What formalities will take place tomorrow morning?" The woman replied, "None, just follow my designation." When they went to bed, she appeared pure and innocent, without any shame. The next day, Xiang said, "There are still some items by the sea, please send someone to fetch them." The woman agreed. When the woman saw the book, she asked, "What is this?" Xiang showed her the book. She asked, "What is its use?" He replied, "Reading it can lead to success or fame." She said, "You should try reading it to me." Xiang then read the current literary works, following the rhythm and cadence, with a measured and varied tone. She said, "It is well-balanced and rhythmic, there is no song in the island that surpasses this." Xiang then read five-character and seven-character regulated verse to her. She said, "The words are different but the rhythm or rhyme is the same, it sounds even more pleasant to the ear." Xiang read in the room, and the woman listened to him all night without getting tired.

    229

    向偶立门外，见一少年紾年长者之背，旁观者笑曰：“如此强壮，尚不敌十馀岁之童子。”问之，渠盖胞兄弟也。又见一少妇负气而行，一人强留之。众人曰：“彼欲大归，即宜听之，何为强留？”问之，渠盖夫妇也。向曰：“何无伦常如此？”向厌室徒壁立，手书匾联以润之。张偶见之，曰：“此中华绝技，仆之家藏殆不及此。”回首见书，曰：“此中华书籍乎？”曰：“然。”张曰：“书中所言何事？”向曰：“大概五伦五常，孝弟忠信耳。”张问何为五伦，向大略言之。张曰：“此诚仆闻所未闻。有此名士，不可不禀于上人。”遂去。多时，同一中年人来，像貌超群，服饰埒于张。向略与为礼。张曰：“适才所言之五伦，祈详言之。

    Explanation

    229

    One day, standing outside, I saw a young man twisting the back of an elder, and the onlookers laughed and said, "So strong, yet still cannot defeat a child of ten or so." When I asked the young man, he revealed that they were cousins. I then saw a young woman walking with a sense of grievance, and someone tried to detain her. The onlookers said, "If she wants to return home, she should be allowed to do so; why detain her?" When I asked, it turned out they were husband and wife. I remarked, "Why are there no proper relationships like this?" I was disgusted by the empty room and the bare walls, so I wrote inscriptions on plaques and couplets to embellish them. Zhang happened to see them and said, "These are unique skills of China; my own collection falls far short of this." He turned and saw the inscriptions and asked, "Are these Chinese books?" I replied, "Yes." Zhang asked, "What do they discuss?" I explained, "They mostly talk about the five relationships and the five virtues, specifically filial piety, fraternal respect, loyalty, and trustworthiness." Zhang asked what the five relationships were, and I gave a brief explanation. Zhang said, "I have never heard of these before. Such a learned person should be reported to a higher authority." He then left. After some time, a middle-aged man arrived, with an extraordinary appearance and attire similar to Zhang's. I greeted him briefly. Zhang said, "Could you please explain in detail the five relationships you mentioned earlier?"

    230

    ”向遂细细讲究，兼将五伦中之十义，十义中之体用，体用中之功效，功效中之次序，凡其中之可以言传者，一一切言之。其人闻之大喜，曰：“此治国平天下之道，当急为讲堂，聚岛人而训诲之。吾岛虽在化外，亦无难渐臻上理矣。”向曰：“惟秉国成，始自为政，君亦徒有其愿已耳！”张曰：“此即岛中之王公。”向居然起敬，与为长揖。岛君曰：“此何礼也？”向曰：“中华平等相见如此。”因与讲朝觐会典之礼。岛君曰：“华夏之君若是之尊耶？”遂以向为上宾。讲堂竣，聚岛人于中，令向讲书以训之。向先为讲伦常，听者有足蹈手舞者矣，有俯首涕泣者矣，皆因乍聆伦理，鼓动天良也。向择其少慧者，教之书读，十年后为之开科取士，俗同风一，亦有诸夏气象矣。

    Explanation

    230

    Xiang then carefully and thoroughly expounded, explaining the ten virtues within the five relationships, the substance and function within those virtues, the efficacy within the substance and function, and the sequence within the efficacy—covering everything that could be conveyed through words, one by one. The listener heard this and was greatly pleased, saying, "This is the way to govern the state and pacify the world. We should immediately build a lecture hall, gather the islanders, and instruct them. Although our island is beyond the civilized world, it will not be difficult to gradually attain a higher level of governance." Xiang replied, "Only by holding the reins of the state can one begin to govern; you only have the wish, that's all!" Zhang said, "This is the king or prince of the island." Xiang suddenly showed respect and performed a formal bow. The island king/prince said, "What is this ritual?" Xiang said, "In Huaxia, people treat each other equally in this way." He then explained the rituals of court audience and the ceremonial code. The island king/prince said, "Is the ruler of Huaxia so exalted?" He then elevated Xiang to the status of a distinguished guest. When the lecture hall was completed, he gathered the islanders in it and ordered Xiang to lecture and instruct them. Xiang first explained the moral norms and social ethics, and the listeners had some who danced with their feet and hands, and others who bowed their heads and wept—both because they had just heard about ethics and their innate goodness was stirred. Xiang selected those who were young and wise, taught them to read, and after ten years, held examinations to select officials. The customs became unified and harmonious, and the island also took on the atmosphere of the Central Plains.

    231

    向于公务之暇，每于岛中遨游。至一山，虽不甚高大，而峪中濒涧处多人参，遂遣人采取，按法制之。妻问制此何用，向曰：“此天朝宝物也。”复于海边得鼍盖一枚，心知其异，悉宝藏之，以为有此二物，至中国可致巨富。

    　　岛西八百里有秀云岛，其人丁等于琼华，而秉性强悍。忽使人来，责令琼华每年出银米若干以为常贡，如不应允，即兴问罪之师。岛君大惧，遂委其事于向。向对使人曰：“银米若干，猝难立办，请限五月。”使人乃去。向乃令岛人制火炮、弓箭、器械，每日操演之。及期，西岛复使人督催。向怒，割来使两耳，释令归，岛人大恐。向曰：“不与抗衡，而听其诛求，则岛中所出，悉输于彼，吾人不将饿殍乎？与其死于饿殍，孰若死于征战！且一日之胜，数世赖之。

    Explanation

    231

    During his spare time from official duties, Xiang often roamed around the island. He came upon a mountain that, though not very high, had a valley near a stream where there were many ginseng plants. He then sent people to collect them and process them according to regulations. His wife asked what use these were, and Xiang replied, "These are treasures of the Heavenly Dynasty." He also found a turtle shell on the beach, knowing it was unusual, he kept it as a treasure, believing that with these two items, he could become extremely wealthy in China.

    Eight hundred miles west of the island lies the Island of Show Clouds, whose population is equal to that of Qionghua, and whose people are strong and fierce. Suddenly, someone came and demanded that Qionghua provide a certain amount of silver and rice annually as regular tribute. If they did not agree, they would launch a punitive expedition. The island's ruler was greatly frightened and entrusted the matter to Xiang. Xiang replied to the envoy, "A certain amount of silver and rice cannot be prepared immediately; please give us five months." The envoy then left. Xiang then ordered the islanders to make cannons, bows, arrows, and weapons, and to practice every day. When the time came, the Western Island again sent someone to urge them. Xiang was angry and cut off the envoy's ears, then let him go. The islanders were greatly frightened. Xiang said, "If we do not resist and allow them to demand everything, all that we produce will be taken by them, and we will starve. Rather than die of starvation, it is better to die in battle. Moreover, a single victory can benefit future generations."

    232

    ”岛人咸悟，遂尊向为军师，曰：“愿听调度，死而无憾！”向曰：“岛西有长蛇谷一条，为敌入岛必由之路，可于彼处用计擒之。”分岛人为三队，各与一令。第五日，西岛人果乘船至，勇如虎貔，势不可敌。登岸后，琼之第一队先到，略与接战，诈败走。敌以为怯，尽力追入长蛇谷。既入，不见岛人；方欲出谷，滚木擂石已断去路。既而箭如雨下，炮如雷鸣，敌大惧欲退，而谷口已为琼二队人杜塞，西岛人悉困谷内。越三日，其头目请琼主将，将有言。向至，问之。

    Explanation

    232

    The islanders all realized and then honored Xiang as their military advisor, saying, "We are willing to follow your orders, even if it means death, without regret!" Xiang said, "To the west of the island lies a long snake-like valley, the only route enemies must take to enter the island. We can use a plan to capture them there." He divided the islanders into three teams, each with a command. On the fifth day, the people from the western island indeed arrived by boat, as brave as tigers and wild boars, their momentum was unstoppable. After landing, the first team of Qiong arrived first, engaged in a brief battle, feigned defeat, and retreated. The enemy thought they were cowardly and pursued them into the long snake-like valley with all their might. Once inside, they did not see any islanders; just as they wanted to leave the valley, rolling logs and stones blocked their path. Soon after, arrows rained down like rain and cannons roared like thunder. The enemy was greatly frightened and wanted to retreat, but the entrance to the valley had already been blocked by the second team of Qiong. The people from the western island were completely trapped inside the valley. After three days, their leader requested to speak with the main general of Qiong, saying he had something to say. Xiang arrived and questioned them.

    233

    曰：“若肯解围，愿劝吾主臣服，不复侵犯。”向许之，约各留军装器械，鱼贯而出。敌如约，徒手乘船而去。向曰：“贼众虽去，贼首必亲至，宜再以计破之。”遂暗中传令，惟日与岛君饮宴以俟之。未几，西岛主果尽率其众而来。既至，见边陲无备，督众遂进。既入谷，岛人夹谷而出其后，铳炮弓箭以攻之。西岛人急奔出谷。既上岛，岛人整列于三里之外，西岛主令其众曰：“退既无路，当进攻！”众遂蜂拥而进，尘土陡起，半陷入坑坎中。

    Explanation

    233

    He said, "If you are willing to lift the siege, I would persuade my lord to submit and promise not to invade again." Xiang agreed to this, and they agreed to leave behind their military equipment, departing one after another in an orderly manner. The enemy adhered to the agreement, leaving unarmed and by boat. Xiang then said, "Though the enemy's forces have departed, their leader will surely come in person. We should devise another plan to defeat them." He secretly gave orders, arranging daily feasts with the island lord to wait for them. Soon, the Western island leader indeed led all his men to come. Upon arriving, they saw that the border was unprepared and ordered his men to advance. Once they entered the valley, the islanders surrounded them from behind in the valley, attacking with guns, cannons, bows, and arrows. The Western island men rushed out of the valley in haste. Once on the island, the islanders formed up three miles away, and the Western island leader ordered his men, "There is no way to retreat; we should attack!" His men then rushed forward in a swarm, and dust suddenly arose, with half of them falling into pits and ditches.

    234

    岛人复以箭炮从纵从之，坑坎为满，遂各用敌人腰带缚之。拥西岛主至军帐，向亲解其缚，待以宾礼，谓之曰：“与君邻岛，宜相亲睦，不虞君之涉吾地也，何故？”西岛主俯首不语。向复曰：“吾主欲送君还岛，世世结好，不知肯辱收吾主否？”西岛主曰：“若蒙大惠，愿时听调遣，岁输常贡。”向曰：“贡不必常，以物将敬可也。”遂尽释所获敌人，令先归船，以俟其主。设盛宴以款西岛主，筵未终，兴辞。向亲送至海边，立视其开船而去。周围岛峪闻此事者，悉输贡通好，尊其礼教。

    Explanation

    234

    The islanders then used arrows and cannons to attack from all sides, filling the pits and depressions with their efforts, and proceeded to bind the enemy with their own belts. They brought the chief of the western island to the military tent, where the general personally untied him and treated him with the courtesy of a guest, saying, "Since we are neighboring islands, we should be friendly and harmonious. Why did you trespass on our territory?" The western island chief bowed his head and remained silent. The general then said, "My lord wishes to send you back to your island, to establish everlasting friendship. Would you be willing to accept our lord's favor and obey his commands?" The western island chief replied, "If we receive your great favor, we will be willing to obey your commands and pay regular tribute." The general said, "Tribute does not have to be regular; it is sufficient to offer gifts as a sign of respect." They then released all the captured enemies, allowing them to return to their ships, waiting for their leader. A grand feast was held to entertain the western island chief, but before the feast was over, he stood up to take his leave. The general personally saw him off to the shore and watched as he set sail. All the surrounding islands that heard of this event paid tribute, established friendly relations, and respected his rites and teachings.

    235

    向居岛三十年，二子皆长矣。时念老母，岛无船只，不得归省。一日，谓妻张氏曰：“设有机会，卿能从仆归否？”张氏曰：“妾实不能。昔岛中夫妇，夫不家，妇必改嫁。兹聆雅训，颇知节义，之死矢靡他，亦足以报情深。但妾无子，终身无依；君无子，无后为大。君携次子去，长子留此，代君操理岛务。老母百年，万望君还归。”向许之，遂将军师之事陆续属长子。一日，岛人报曰：“现有估船二只，被风飘泊岛下。

    Explanation

    235

    He had stayed on the island for thirty years, and both his sons had grown up. He often thought of his elderly mother, but the island had no boats, so he could not return to visit her. One day, he told his wife, Zhang, "If there is an opportunity, could you accompany me back?" Zhang replied, "I truly cannot. In the past, if a man in the island did not marry, his wife would remarry. Now that I have heard your wise teachings, I understand the importance of loyalty and righteousness. Until death, I will not change my loyalty, which is enough to repay your deep affection. But I have no children, so I will be alone forever; you have no children, and not having descendants is a great concern. You should take the younger son with you, leave the elder son here to manage the island affairs on your behalf. When my mother passes away, I hope you will come back." He agreed, and he gradually entrusted the affairs of the general and strategist to the elder son. One day, someone from the island reported, "There are two merchant ships currently, blown by the wind and anchored at the island."

    236

    ”向大喜，遂带仆从三十馀人至岛下。舟子见之，大惧，仓皇无措。向乃止从人于百步外，孤身上船，问其来历，言载客货赴广东。向亦自道回中华之意。舟人曰：“闻前有秀云岛，其人犷恶，恐为所害。”向曰：“有仆在，可保无虞。”且以人参二斤为谢。舟人喜曰：“风少息，明晨即可开船，请速治装。”翌日，向与次子携所蓄财物上船，张与长子送至海边。向于船上建立大旗，上书“琼华岛军师”五字，与妻子挥泪而别。前至秀云岛，岛人见其大旗，各有畏心，遂让过。

    Explanation

    236

    Xiang was greatly pleased and then took thirty or more servants to the island. The boatman saw them and was greatly frightened, acting in a panicked and disorganized manner. Xiang then had his followers stop at a hundred steps away and went alone onto the boat. He asked about their origins, and they said they were carrying passengers and goods to Guangdong. Xiang also explained his intention to return to China. The boatman said, "I have heard that there is a Xiu Cloud Island ahead, and its people are fierce and evil, and we might be harmed." Xiang replied, "With my servants, there is no danger," and he gave two pounds of ginseng as a gift. The boatman happily said, "The wind will calm down a bit, and we can set sail tomorrow morning, please hurry and prepare." The next day, Xiang and his second son carried their accumulated wealth onto the boat. Zhang and his eldest son saw them off to the beach. Xiang raised a large flag on the boat, inscribed with the five characters "Qionghua Island Military Advisor," and he bid farewell to his wife and children with tears. They arrived at Xiu Cloud Island, and the islanders saw the large flag and each had a sense of fear, so they let them pass.

    237

    向至家，幸喜老母康健，以岛君所馈金帛为母寿，人参、鼍盖等物，渐鬻于市，遂成巨富。次子论婚绅族，悉以家务付之。越岁，向母卒，窀穸事毕，每欲货船回岛，舟人悉不知其处，无敢去者。忽一人持书至，启视之，乃长子所寄。言奉母命备船奉迓，兼言父去后秀云复事侵扰，危在旦夕，望父速归，以救岛人云云。遂谓次子曰：“吾去探汝母兄，三年即归。”遂去不复返。

    　　虚白道人曰：泛海遇风，独生于岛，固向之幸也。而其实不惟此，盖岛地若干大，岛人若干众，向一至，风俗顿移，悉知伦常，天实为之也。夫知其事原于天，则知其迹似向一人之幸，其实为琼华岛二十万众之幸矣。当向未入岛之时，淡水尽没，以海水造饭，以致哮喘难支，是泛海无河水，直等于无粮食也。因有感于海咸河淡之说，附论于此：

    Explanation

    237

    Xiang returned home and was overjoyed to find his old mother in good health. He used the gold and silk gifts from the island lord to celebrate his mother's birthday. Ginseng, turtle shell, and other valuable items were gradually sold in the market, and he became very wealthy. His second son married into the gentry, and all household affairs were entrusted to him. After a year, Xiang's mother died, and the burial arrangements were completed. Whenever he wanted to sell the ship and return to the island, the boatmen did not know where it was, and none dared to go. Suddenly, someone brought a letter, which upon opening revealed it was from the eldest son. The letter said that according to the mother's command, a boat was prepared to welcome him, and it also mentioned that after the father left, Xiu Yun was again being harassed, in danger of life and death, and hoped the father would return quickly to save the people of the island. He then told the second son, "I am going to visit your mother and elder brother; I will return in three years." He then left and never returned.

    The Taoist monk named Xubai said: Wandering at sea and encountering a storm, surviving alone on the island, this was indeed Xiang's good fortune. But in fact, it was not just that; the island is so large, and the islanders are so numerous, that when Xiang arrived, the customs changed dramatically, and everyone came to know the principles of propriety and morality, which were truly the work of heaven. If one knows that the matter originated from heaven, then one realizes that what seemed to be the good fortune of one person, in reality, was the good fortune of the twenty million people on Qionghua Island. When Xiang had not yet entered the island, fresh water was completely gone, and he had to cook with seawater, leading to severe asthma and difficulty breathing, making it as if there was no fresh water, just like there was no food. Inspired by the saying that the sea is salty and rivers are fresh, I add this commentary here.

    238

    夫海何以咸？河何以淡？今人未之言也，古人亦未之道也，闲尝殚私见浅识以论之。河之水出于泉，泉之水缘于雨。何言之？时雨之降，半沉于地；沉地之水，半出于泉。是以高上之原水少，下隰之地泉多，以知隰地之泉，咸高原雨水滋注所由致也。即若名泉大源，昼夜混混，似非细微之滋注所能致，然亦不过其来脉远，滋处众，究非地中固有之水出而为泉也。不然何以旱则泉涸，涝则泉旺乎？雨淡则河淡，此其故非显然易见，不待深维而知之者乎？若夫海，则大异是矣。为天池，为巨壑，百川赴之而无盈时，尾闾泄之而无虚日。禹时十年九涝而水不加益，汤时八年七旱而涯不见损。任江汉之朝宗，惊洪涛之无际。而欲创言以论，不啻以蠡测之矣。而味咸之说，觉有微理之可寻也。盖海者，万水之所归也。

    Explanation

    238

    Why is the sea salty, and why are rivers fresh? No one today has spoken of this, and even the ancients have not discussed it. I have often used my limited knowledge to discuss it. The water of rivers comes from springs, and the water of springs originates from rain. How can this be explained? When rain falls, half of it seeps into the ground; the water that seeps into the ground, half of it emerges as springs. Therefore, the water on high plains is scarce, while springs are abundant in low, marshy areas. This shows that the springs in marshy areas are nourished by the rainwater from highlands. Even for famous springs and great sources, which flow day and night, it seems unlikely that such vast amounts of water could come from mere tiny seepages. However, it is simply that their sources are far away, and they receive water from many places, but ultimately, they are not water that naturally exists underground and emerges as springs. Otherwise, how can it be that springs dry up during droughts and become abundant during floods? If rain is fresh, then rivers are fresh, and this reason is not clearly visible, needing no deep thought to understand it? As for the sea, it is quite different from this. It is like a heavenly pool, a vast ravine, with all rivers flowing into it without ever filling it, and the drain at its bottom never empties it. During the reign of Yu the Great, there were nine years of flooding in ten, yet the sea did not overflow; during the reign of Tang the Great, there were seven years of drought in eight, yet the sea's shores did not diminish. It accepts the tributaries of the Yangtze and Han rivers and the boundless surges of the sea. To attempt to create a theory to explain it is no different from trying to measure the vast ocean with a cup. Yet the theory of the sea's saltiness seems to have subtle principles that can be explored. The sea is the destination of all waters.

    239

    所归皆淡，而海水独咸，将毋以海至深大与？海即深大，而万水之赴，千古不息，何以水不加多，咸不少减也？试汇古说参形势以明之。尝闻地为水悬，海水者，或即悬地之水流露者也。又闻天地如鸡卵，地乃卵中之黄，海水者，或即卵中之清乎？且南极至北极八十万里，而南海至北海仅四十九万里。四十九万里以外非尽海乎？东极至西极九十一万里，而东海至西海仅三十五万里，三十五万里之外不尽水乎？盖以咸属黑，黑属子。

    Explanation

    239

    All waters tend to be fresh, yet the sea alone is salty. Is it not because the sea is so deep and vast? Even though the sea is deep and vast, countless rivers flow into it ceaselessly throughout history. How is it that the volume of water in the sea does not increase, and its salinity does not decrease? Let us examine ancient theories and the geographical situation to understand this. I have heard that the earth is suspended in water, and the sea might be the result of water seeping from the earth. I have also heard that the heavens and the earth are like a chicken egg, with the earth being the yolk and the sea being the egg white. Moreover, the distance from the South Pole to the North Pole is eighty million li, while the distance from the South Sea to the North Sea is only forty-nine million li. Is the area beyond forty-nine million li not entirely the sea? The distance from the East Pole to the West Pole is ninety-one million li, while the distance from the East Sea to the West Sea is only thirty-five million li. Is the area beyond thirty-five million li not entirely water? It seems that salt belongs to the color black, and black belongs to the concept of "子" (son or offspring).

    240

    子水者，天一所生之水也。既为天一所生之水，即为天地固有之水。则是地之所及，水实负之；地之所不及，必尽属水也。地中水负，地外水连，是以天不碍其左旋，日不妨其右运也。且闻之庄子云：“中国之在海内，似稊米之在大仓。”观是则中国之水可知矣，海之为水亦可知矣。海也者，犹以灌注而见其加多乎？犹以杂投而易其本性乎？此海水之咸所由见端者也。然海之不测，犹之天地，乃以有限之知识，创言立论，实属卤莽。而愚有所见，以特心思，即政君子耳！

    Explanation

    240

    The water of Zi, which is the water born by Tianyi. Being the water born by Tianyi, it is the water inherently present in the universe. Therefore, wherever the earth reaches, water carries it; where the earth does not reach, it must all belong to water. Water carries within the earth and connects outside the earth. Thus, the sky does not obstruct its left rotation, and the sun does not hinder its right movement. Moreover, it is said in the Zhuangzi that "China in the sea is like a grain of millet in a large granary." From this, we can understand the nature of China's water, and the nature of the sea can also be understood. Is the sea's saltiness still seen as increasing through pouring in? Is it still seen as changing its original nature through mixed throwing in? This is where the saltiness of seawater begins to be seen. The immeasurability of the sea, to create theories and establish arguments with limited knowledge is indeed rash. But I have my own views, and with special thoughts, I am just a scholar.

    241

    所言悉浅显之理，而不测之精微，合盘托出，洵千古未有之奇谈也。

    　　陶靖节所著《桃源记》，人皆以为寓言，观此而信地之类如桃源者，不一而足。朱子惓惓助藉彻彻之义，惜未见此乐土以慰之。其文与事，尤妙在不蹈袭《桃源记》之一字。

    　　《论语?子欲居九夷》章，吾读此篇砉然以解。政术兵钤，足征抱负；终军请缨，未见实事。拟以此补之。马竹吾

    　　于难测之中细探精义，以补前人之未发，洵理如牛毛茧丝，文如日光玉洁。

    　　海咸之说，凿凿言之，确有至理。文亦有抽茧剥蕉之妙。 叶芸士，道号沧粟

    　　读此篇见先生经世之学，怀才不售，惜哉！上元李瑜谨注

    　　穷岛之民，易于教化，向生之事，盖得于意外。于居夷浮海，圣人复慨之，况我辈乎？ 渔樵散人

    　　杜仲

    Explanation

    241

    The discourse encompasses simple and clear principles, as well as the profound and subtle aspects that are hard to measure, all revealed in full. Truly, this is an unprecedented marvel in history.

    Tao Qian's Peach Blossom Spring, which everyone considers an allegory, shows that there are many places like Peach Blossom Spring, not just one. Zhu Xi earnestly sought to clarify and thoroughly understand the principles, but he regretted not seeing this paradise to console him. The writing and events are especially remarkable in that they do not merely copy Peach Blossom Spring.

    The chapter from the Analects titled "Confucius Wanted to Live in Jiuyi" provided me with sudden insight. Political strategies and military tactics are sufficient to demonstrate one's aspirations; Zhong Jun volunteered to serve the state, but his deeds were not seen. I intend to supplement this. Ma Zhugu

    In the unpredictable, one carefully explores the profound meanings to supplement what previous scholars have not yet discovered. Truly, the principles are as fine as the fibers of a cocoon, and the writing is as pure and bright as sunlight on jade.

    The theory of the sea's salinity is clearly and convincingly stated, indeed containing profound truths. The writing also has the charm of peeling a banana and unwinding a cocoon. Leaf Cloud, Daoist name Cangsu

    Reading this piece reveals the author's practical learning for governing the world, but his talents were not recognized, how unfortunate! Shangyuan Li Yu respectfully annotated

    The people of remote islands are easy to educate and civilize, the process of turning toward life is likely to happen unexpectedly. Even sages would feel moved by this, how much more so for us? Fisher and Woodcutter

    Du Zhong

    242

    儒医杜仲，晋人。其父、祖咸以医为业，至仲益精其术。贫不索贽，富不苛求，实以济世为志。

    　　一日，有美少年执重贽奉请，自言家君患病，月馀不瘥，特请医治。问其姓氏，答言姓沈名实。问其里居，答言不远。门外有小车一乘，祈即同乘往，仲从之。路甚生疏，逾两时始至，见一人立候于门。下车，实曰：“此弟表兄江某。”仲揖之，并入客舍。茶毕，便请诊视。既入，见一及笄女郎立病榻前，微睨之，娟丽绝伦。见仲入，缓缓而去。诊视毕，出。江曰：“家母舅病势如何？”仲曰：“尚可调理，但脾土太弱，须迟时日，非两越月不能全愈。”仲立方后告辞。实固留之，仲乃止。实曰：“明晨奉迓，祈早辱临。”仲诺之。饮酒间，江忽抚衷叫苦。问之，曰：“吾有胃气疼之病根，恒数日一犯，犯则心如刀搅，痛不可堪，愿先生施救。”仲曰：“此易治。”以针刺之，应手而愈。复立一方，曰：“连服三剂，可终身无此患。”江不胜感激。

    Explanation

    242

    Scholar-physician Du Zhong, a native of the Jin state. His father and grandfather both practiced medicine, and by the time Du Zhong came along, he had further refined their art. He did not demand gifts from the poor nor impose excessive demands on the wealthy, truly aspiring to benefit the world through his work.

    One day, a handsome young man brought a heavy gift and respectfully invited Du Zhong to treat his father, who had been ill for over a month without recovery. When asked about his name, the young man replied that his name was Shen Shi. When asked about his residence, he said it was not far away. There was a small carriage outside, and he requested that Du Zhong accompany him there. Du Zhong agreed. The journey was unfamiliar, and they arrived after more than two hours. Upon arrival, they saw a man waiting at the door. After getting out of the carriage, Shen Shi said, "This is my cousin, Jiang." Du Zhong bowed to him, and they entered the guesthouse together. After tea, Shen Shi asked Du Zhong to examine his father. Upon entering, they saw a young woman of marriageable age standing by the sickbed. Du Zhong gave her a brief glance and found her beauty unparalleled. She saw Du Zhong enter and slowly left the room. After the examination, Du Zhong stepped out. Jiang asked, "How is my uncle's condition?" Du Zhong replied, "It can be treated, but his spleen is too weak. It will take some time, and it will not fully recover in less than two months." After prescribing a formula, Du Zhong prepared to leave. Shen Shi insisted that he stay, and Du Zhong agreed. Shen Shi said, "I will come to fetch you tomorrow morning, please arrive early." Du Zhong agreed. While drinking, Jiang suddenly clutched his chest and cried out in pain. When asked what was wrong, he said, "I have a root cause of stomach pain that occurs every few days. When it happens, it feels like a knife twisting in my heart, unbearable. Please save me, Master." Du Zhong said, "This is easy to treat." He performed acupuncture, and the pain immediately subsided. He then prescribed another formula, saying, "Take three doses in succession, and you will be free from this condition for life." Jiang was deeply grateful.

    243

    仲每视病，辄见女郎，后直出入不避。仲借久候脉息以偷视之。沈家每奉饮馔，江恒陪坐。一日，见仲俯首蹙额，遂问曰：“夫子若有不豫色然？”仲曰：“然。家务累心，不能忘怀。”江曰：“有何愁苦，如是耿耿？”仲曰：“同酌有日，不妨直言。弟幼婚邻庄邵氏，定期过门，暴病身亡，是以不能漠然。”江曰：“如君才貌，无难再觅良匹。”仲曰：“苦无佳者。”江曰：“君平生未见丽人耶？”曰：“见之。势分不同，见犹未见。”江曰：“君试言所见丽人里居。”仲曰：“远在千里，近则咫尺耳。”江会其意而笑曰：“君注意家母舅前之表妹乎？”仲不答。江曰：“舍表妹小字芳卿，年十七，德容兼备。

    Explanation

    243

    仲每视病，辄见女郎，后直出入不避。仲借久候脉息以偷视之。沈家每奉饮馔，江恒陪坐。一日，见仲俯首蹙额，遂问曰：“夫子若有不豫色然？”仲曰：“然。家务累心，不能忘怀。”江曰：“有何愁苦，如是耿耿？”仲曰：“同酌有日，不妨直言。弟幼婚邻庄邵氏，定期过门，暴病身亡，是以不能漠然。”江曰：“如君才貌，无难再觅良匹。”仲曰：“苦无佳者。”江曰：“君平生未见丽人耶？”曰：“见之。势分不同，见犹未见。”江曰：“君试言所见丽人里居。”仲曰：“远在千里，近则咫尺耳。”江会其意而笑曰：“君注意家母舅前之表妹乎？”仲不答。江曰：“舍表妹小字芳卿，年十七，德容兼备。”

    Translation:
    Whenever Zhong attended to patients, he would often see a young lady, who later entered and exited without hesitation. Zhong took the opportunity to observe her pulse and breath while pretending to check her health. The Shen family would always serve him food and drink, with Jiang sitting beside him. One day, Jiang noticed Zhong with a worried expression and asked, "Is there something troubling you, Master?" Zhong replied, "Indeed. Household matters weigh heavily on my mind, and I cannot forget them." Jiang inquired, "What is causing you such distress and unease?" Zhong responded, "There is a time for sharing wine; I may as well speak frankly. In my youth, I was betrothed to a family in a neighboring village, the Shao family, and we were to marry. However, she suddenly fell ill and passed away, which is why I cannot remain indifferent." Jiang said, "Given your talents and appearance, it should not be difficult to find another suitable match." Zhong replied, "Alas, there are no good prospects." Jiang asked, "Have you never seen a beautiful woman in your life?" Zhong answered, "I have. But our circumstances are different, so even though I have seen her, it is as if I have not." Jiang then said, "May I ask where the beautiful woman you have seen resides?" Zhong replied, "She is far away in thousands of miles, but near, she is just a step away." Jiang understood his meaning and smiled, "Are you thinking of your maternal uncle's previous cousin?" Zhong did not respond. Jiang continued, "My cousin, whose pet name is Fangqing, is seventeen years old and possesses both virtue and beauty."

    244

    君注意之，可谓赏鉴不误。但家母舅择配殊苛，媒必不谐，今可设计以谋之。”仲曰：“计将安出？”江曰：“此时正当用君之际，明晨往迓，君托故不来，弟借之进说，或可允从。”仲大喜。次日仲果不至，沈惧以语江。江曰：“杜先生昨见表妹，烦甥作媒，甥未暇与舅言。今之不来，殆为此乎？”沈不语。江复曰：“仲仪表不俗，门阀极清，未始不可与结秦晋。”良久，沈始应之。江曰：“若然，甥需自往，一为报喜，兼请渠前来诊视。”沈曰：“可。”复曰：“仆大病在身，不便成礼，病愈送女于归，无烦亲迎也。”江诺之。次日，江诣仲，以允亲告，遂与仲同乘而回。仲视病时不见芳卿，心甚怅怅，犹不如未结亲得睹彼美而忘朝饥也。

    Explanation

    244

    The gentleman, please pay attention to this, as it truly shows your discerning eye. However, my maternal uncle is very strict in choosing a match, and surely the matchmaker will not succeed. Now, we can devise a plan to deal with it. "Zhong asked, 'What is the plan?' Jiang replied, 'At this moment, it is the right time to use you. Tomorrow morning, I will go to meet him, and you can use an excuse to not come. I will use this opportunity to make a proposal, and perhaps he will agree.' Zhong was very pleased. The next day, as expected, Zhong did not come. Shen was afraid and told Jiang. Jiang said, 'Mr. Du saw my cousin yesterday and asked me to act as a matchmaker. I did not have time to tell my uncle. His absence today, perhaps it is because of this?' Shen remained silent. Jiang continued, 'Zhong has an unremarkable appearance and a very clear lineage, so it is not impossible for us to marry. After a long while, Shen finally responded. Jiang said, 'If so, I must go myself, to report the good news and also invite him to come for a consultation.' Shen said, 'Yes.' Jiang added, 'I am seriously ill, so it is inconvenient to perform the ceremony. After recovery, I will send my daughter back home, and there is no need for a formal wedding.' Jiang agreed. The next day, Jiang went to Zhong to inform him of the agreement, and they returned together. When Zhong was attending to the patient, he did not see Fangqing, and his heart was very troubled, still not as happy as when he had not yet been engaged and could see her beauty and forget his hunger.

    245

    仲理沈疾至四十馀日，沈已杖而能行。一日，仲来甚晚，诊视后，时已燃灯，宿于沈室。甫二鼓，仲将就寝，而芳卿忽至曰：“妾父忽生异心，将害君，可速归！”仲大惧，曰：“深夜无烛，不谙路途，何以得归？”芳急以纸扇一柄授仲，曰：“摇之而归，不致迷途；且执此而行，其速异常。”仲展视之，惟绘一圆月。受扇后犹把握女腕，不忍即去。芳曰：“利刃临项，尚恋恋如此，可谓色胆如天！少迟欲去不得，尤惨于生离。”仲曰：“从此无见面期乎？”芳曰：“妾非君不嫁，请待之！”言已，促仲归，仲遂释芳行。果所行之路如同白日，旁观则黑不见物，且觉行如鸟飞，移时至家。回忆芳情，五内铭感。及沈遣人害仲，而仲去已多时。沈问家人曰：“谁遣仲行？”芳卿曰：“女实遣之。”沈怒。芳曰：“请父勿怒。

    Explanation

    245

    仲理患重病已四十多天，沈已能拄着拐杖行走。一天，仲来得很晚，诊视之后，天色已晚，点燃了灯，住在沈的房间里。刚过二更，仲准备就寝时，芳卿忽然到来，说：“我父亲突然起了歹心，要加害于您，赶快回去吧！”仲非常害怕，说：“深夜没有蜡烛，不熟悉路途，怎么能回去？”芳急忙递给他一把纸扇，说：“摇动它就能回去，不会迷失方向；而且拿着它走，速度异常快。”仲展开一看，只见扇上画着一轮圆月。接过扇子后仍紧握着芳的手腕，不忍心立即离开。芳说：“利刃在颈，还如此依依不舍，可以说是胆大包天！稍有迟疑就无法离开，比生离死别还要悲惨。”仲说：“从此再无相见之期吗？”芳说：“我非你不嫁，请耐心等待！”说完，催促仲回去，仲于是松开芳的手离开了。果然，他所走的路如同白昼，旁人看来却是漆黑一片，而且感觉像鸟儿飞翔一样，不久便到了家。回忆起芳的情意，内心铭感不忘。等到沈派人加害仲时，仲已经离开很久了。沈问家人：“是谁让仲离开的？”芳卿说：“是我让他走的。”沈大怒。芳说：“请父亲不要生气。”

    246

    父既以女字仲，仲即女夫，岂有坐视夫死而不救者乎？”沈曰：“吾以汝字之，未尝以汝嫁之，何得以仲为夫？”芳曰：“父以女字仲，而女外视之，此为故违父命，是不孝也；既有夫妇之名，即有夫妇之义，明知其义而不为，是不义也。女虽女流，不为不孝不义之举。”沈曰：“汝意如何？”曰：“嫁之。”沈曰：“决不由汝！”芳曰：“女亦决从父之治命，不从父之昏命！”沈大怒，嘱家人勿令芳出门，盖恐其逃归杜仲也。仲母欲为仲议婚他族，仲不欲。俟至年馀，无耗，仲亦疑之。时值清明，仲祭扫归，见二犬啮一犬，心甚怜之，遂拾石将二犬逐去。

    Explanation

    246

    The father had already betrothed the daughter to Zhong, making Zhong her son-in-law. Is there anyone who would sit by and watch their husband die without helping? Se said, "I betrothed you to him, but I never married you. How can you consider Zhong as your husband?" Fang replied, "Father betrothed the daughter to Zhong, but the daughter looks at it from the outside. This is deliberately disobeying the father's command, which is unfilial. Since there is the name of husband and wife, there must be the duty of husband and wife. Knowing the duty and not fulfilling it, this is unjust. Although you are a woman, you will not do an unfilial or unjust act." Se said, "What do you intend to do?" Fang replied, "Marriage him." Se said, "It will not be decided by you. You will also definitely follow the father's command for governance, not the father's command for marriage." Se became very angry and instructed the family not to let Fang leave the house, afraid that she might escape back to Du Zhong.

    Zhong's mother wanted to arrange another marriage for Zhong, but Zhong did not want it. After more than a year, there was no news, and Zhong also began to doubt. It was during the Qingming Festival, and Zhong returned after the ancestral worship. He saw two dogs biting one dog and felt very pity for it. He then picked up a stone and chased the two dogs away.

    247

    视之，非犬，乃小狐也。见其遍体尘土，将弃之而行。狐大号，若有求救之意。二犬见仲去，将复啮之。仲不得已，用布袱包裹，怀抱而归，置寝室地下，转瞬化为丽人。视之，乃芳卿也。大喜，扶卧榻上，进以米粥。及晚，代解其衣，拥之而卧。半夜，女始能言，曰：“非君救济，命丧犬腹。”仲曰：“卿欲何往？遭此大厄。”女曰：“妾特来事君。妾父执迷，不从妾志。妾欲自尽以报君，妾母怜之，遣实兄送妾，致遭此难。”仲曰：“卿真节义女也！”言已，欲与欢好。女曰：“妾心忐忑，遍体如瘫，爱妾者忍为此耶？”仲乃罢。晨兴朝母，操作家务如村妇。

    Explanation

    247

    Looking at it, it was not a dog, but a small fox. Seeing its body covered in dust, he was about to abandon it and leave. The fox roared loudly, as if seeking help. The two dogs, seeing Zhong depart, were about to bite it again. Zhong, having no choice, wrapped it in a cloth bundle and carried it home, placing it on the floor of his bedroom. In an instant, it transformed into a beautiful woman. Looking at her, it was none other than Fang Qing. Greatly pleased, he helped her lie down on the bed and fed her rice porridge. By evening, he helped her take off her clothes and held her as she slept. In the middle of the night, the woman could finally speak, saying, "If not for your rescue, I would have died in the dogs' stomachs." Zhong asked, "Where do you wish to go? You have suffered such a great misfortune." The woman replied, "I have come specifically to serve you. My father is stubborn and does not follow my wishes. I wanted to commit suicide to repay you, but my mother pitied me and sent my eldest brother to escort me, leading to this difficulty." Zhong said, "You are truly a woman of integrity and righteousness!" After speaking, he wanted to engage in intimacy with her. The woman said, "My heart is anxious, and my whole body feels paralyzed. How could a person who loves me do such a thing?" Zhong then desisted. In the morning, she rose to greet her mother and performed household chores like a village woman.

    248

    两月馀，沈实忽至。仲问其来意，曰：“家君旧病复翻，敬恳医治。”仲曰：“绝婚谋害，视若雠仇，断不能去！”实曰：“若然，老父之命必休。”仲曰：“夫也不良，今死已后。”实曰：“祈念小妹，一为枉驾。”仲曰：“是也。芳卿情意，时挂心头；渠若亲临，仆即遄臻。”实泣曰：“两月前送妹于归，途遇猎犬驱逐，迄今无耗，想已死于九泉之下。”仲曰：“夫如是，虽秦仪复生，不能说余往也矣！”拂袖而入。实含泪而去。

    Explanation

    248

    Two months later, Shen Shi suddenly arrived. Zhong asked him about his intentions, and Shen replied, "My father's old illness has relapsed, and I respectfully request treatment." Zhong said, "To sever the marriage and plot harm, regarding you as an enemy, I absolutely cannot go!" Shen said, "If that is the case, my father's life will surely be in danger." Zhong said, "My husband is unworthy, even after his death." Shen said, "I pray for my younger sister, if you could come once." Zhong said, "That is true. Your affection, my sister, is always on my mind; if he personally comes, I will immediately arrive." Shen wept and said, "Two months ago, when I sent my sister back home, we encountered hunting dogs chasing her, and there has been no news since then. I suppose she has died in the netherworld." Zhong said, "If it is like this, even if Qin Yi were to be reborn, he could not convince me to go!" He turned away and entered. Shen left with tears in his eyes.

    249

    仲以告芳卿，芳卿曰：“君医之否？”仲曰：“有卿在，仆安忍坐视？卿果卒，仆欲立视其死，以解宿怨，尚能为作犬马耶？”女曰：“忍哉夫子！”仲曰：“仆欲往视，路径不熟，奈何？”女曰：“妾与君同往。”遂就地画符两道，与仲各由其一而行。觉行之甚缓，而耳旁时闻风声，倾刻已至。女先入。举家方涕泣，众见女，俱反悲为喜，少长集询。女曰：“此非长言时也，贵客来矣，可速接迎之。”实闻，趋出，果见仲立门外，约与共入。甫坐，仲曰：“适在寒舍之言，盖戏言也，望勿介意！”实曰：“既肯光降，何幸如之！家君之疾，仍望尽心诊治。”女见沈，沈曰：“吾固疑汝未死。”遂问脱死之详，女历言之。仲入行翁婿礼，即赴病榻细诊六脉，曰：“此缘病根未除，遂致复发。前此再服十馀剂，可无今日矣！”沈闻之，惭愧交集。仲居沈家，日为调理，一月后强健如初。嗣女生二子皆贵，与仲偕老，无他异。

    Explanation

    249

    Zhong informed Miss Fangqing. Miss Fangqing said, "Are you a doctor?" Zhong replied, "With you here, how could I sit by and watch? If you indeed die, I want to stand and watch your death, to resolve the old grudge, can I still serve as a dog or horse?" The woman said, "How pitiful, sir!" Zhong said, "I want to go and see, but the path is unfamiliar, what can I do?" The woman said, "I will go with you." She then drew two talismans on the ground and each of them followed one. They felt it was very slow, but they heard the wind in their ears, and in no time they arrived. The woman went in first. The whole family was just in tears and sobs, but when they saw her, they all turned sadness into joy, and young and old gathered to ask. The woman said, "This is not the time for long explanations, the esteemed guest has come, you should quickly welcome him." Shen heard and hurried out, and indeed saw Zhong standing outside the door, agreeing to enter together. As soon as they sat down, Zhong said, "What I said earlier at your humble abode was just a joke, hope you do not take offense!" Shen said, "Since you have graced us with your presence, how fortunate! My father's illness, I still hope you will diagnose and treat with all your heart." The woman saw Shen, and Shen said, "I had suspected that you were not dead." He then asked about the details of escaping death, and she told him in detail. Zhong performed the father-in-law and son-in-law ceremony and then went to the sickbed to carefully diagnose the six pulses, saying, "This is because the root of the disease has not been removed, so it has recurred. If you had taken more than ten doses before this, there would be no today!" Shen heard this and was ashamed and grateful mixed together. Zhong stayed at Shen's house, treating him daily, and after a month, he was robust as before. Shen's daughter gave birth to two sons who were all noble, and she lived together with Zhong until old age, with no other strange occurrences.

    250

    虚白道人曰：不能博施于天下，或可实济于一方，此医生常谈也。旷观斯世，谁是有实济之心者？不见二百钱，辄托故力辞，志在济人者，果如是乎？而仲独贫不索贽，富不苛求，其得狐妇、生贵子，知亦造物报应之所致也。

    Explanation

    250

    The Taoist hermit Xubai said: "Unable to benefit the entire world, one might still actually benefit one place or region, which is a common saying among doctors. Broadly observing this world, who truly has a heart to benefit others? When faced with a modest sum of money, they decline due to an excuse, yet those who claim to help others, are they really like this? But Zhong, poor and not seeking a gift, wealthy but not being overly demanding, gained a mysterious wife and gave birth to a noble child, knowing that it was also due to divine retribution."

    251

    杜仲能医狐疾，如得其方，可补“牛经”、“马经”、“驼经”之缺。 马竹吾

    　　医狐之方，余亦知之。或问何方？曰：狐最多疑，医之以果。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　笔致体格可继《聊斋》长亭传之后。 渔樵散人

    　　隗 士 杰

    　　华阴隗俊，字士杰。与友会饮，大醉，踉跄归。路经华下，路旁有孤松甚巨，忽酒涌，仆卧松下不能起，移时睡去。夜央始醒，见身卧渠渠夏屋翠帐中，一笄女端坐对面床，目垂如睡。视之，其美如玉，思欲起诱之，身一动而所见悉杳，身卧孤松下。大惊，急起而归。每忆女郎，寝食俱废，特诣之，抚松盘桓，毫无动静。因思前酒后遇女，黄昏后伪醉，故经其处，仰卧如前。星全时，身仍卧广厦。笄女谓隗曰：“请速起，勿谓伪醉可以欺人。”隗喜，急起，欲牵女。女拒之曰：“请少耐。

    Explanation

    251

    Du Zhong can treat fox diseases, and if its formula is obtained, it can fill in the gaps in "Cattle Classics," "Horse Classics," and "Camel Classics." Ma Zhugu

    The formula to treat fox diseases, I also know it. If someone asks what the formula is, I would say: Foxes are most suspicious, treat them with fruit. Upstream Li Yu respectfully annotated.

    The style and structure can follow after "The Long Pavilion" in "Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio." Fisher and Woodcutter

    Wei Shijie

    Wei Jun of Huayin, courtesy name Shijie. He drank with friends, got drunk, and staggered home. On the way through Huaxia, there was a very large solitary pine tree by the road. Suddenly, he vomited, fell down under the pine tree, and could not get up, falling asleep after some time. He woke up at midnight, seeing himself lying in a spacious summer house with green curtains, and a woman with a hairpin sitting opposite him on the bed, her eyes drooping as if she were sleeping. Looking at her, she was as beautiful as jade, and he thought about getting up to seduce her. When he moved, everything disappeared, and he found himself lying under the solitary pine tree. He was greatly surprised and hurriedly got up to return home. Every time he remembered the girl, he neglected his sleep and meals. He specially went to see her, touched the pine tree, and there was no response. He then thought about the girl he met after drinking, pretending to be drunk at dusk, so he passed by that place, lying on his back as before. When the stars were fully out, he was still lying in a spacious house. The woman with a hairpin said to Wei, "Please get up quickly, do not think that pretending to be drunk can deceive people." Wei was delighted, quickly got up, and wanted to take the girl. The girl refused and said, "Please be patient."

    252

    ”隗就坐，女酌酒奉之，曰：“君正人，而福命太薄，故自荐以赞君。请暂自酌，妾去即来。”少时，执一册入，开册示隗。隗视之，上列己名，注云：“二十三岁以疾卒。”隗大惊。女曰：“勿惊。君于‘二’字上添写一‘百’字，君可享寿百二十三岁矣！”隗添写讫，女执册如授人，册即不见。女谓隗曰：“君于五福，先得曰寿之一，可庆可贺！”隗问女履历，女曰：“妾范氏，其他不必问。”止灯同寝。范谓隗曰：“嗣后君务夙归夜来。”一日，隗日暮即赴。至，惟寒涛在空，他无所见，疑之。移时，身立巨院中。范出厅奉迓曰：“君来何早也？日方暮，阳气犹盛，妾不知奉事，嗣请勿尔！”隗应诺。

    Explanation

    252

    Wai took his seat, and the woman poured him a cup of wine, presenting it to him and saying, "You are a virtuous man, but your fortune and destiny are too thin. Therefore, I have recommended myself to assist you. Please drink this wine for yourself, and I will return shortly." After a short while, she entered holding a book, opened it, and showed it to Wai. Wai looked at it, and his name was listed on it, with a note saying, "Dies of illness at the age of twenty-three." Wai was greatly shocked. The woman said, "Do not be alarmed. If you add the character '百' (meaning 'hundred') above the character '二' (meaning 'two'), you can live to be one hundred and twenty-three years old!" Wai added the character as instructed. The woman held the book as if to hand it over to someone, and the book immediately disappeared. The woman said to Wai, "You have received the first of the Five Blessings, longevity, which is worthy of celebration and joy!" Wai asked about the woman's background, and she replied, "I am from the Fan family. There is no need to ask about anything else." They extinguished the light and went to bed together. Fan said to Wai, "From now on, please return early in the evening and stay overnight." One day, Wai returned at dusk. Upon arriving, he saw only cold waves in the air and nothing else, which made him suspicious. After a while, he found himself standing in a large courtyard. Fan came out of the hall to greet him and said, "Why did you come so early? It is still early in the evening, and the yang energy is still strong. I did not know how to serve you properly, so please do not do that again!" Wai agreed.
    user
    Please perform a final review of the translation you just produced. Identify and correct any remaining issues, such as:

    1. Inaccuracies in Meaning: Ensure the translation accurately conveys the original meaning without omitting or adding information.
    2. Inconsistencies in Terminology: Verify that key terms and proper names are used consistently throughout the translation.
    3. Issues with Natural Flow: Check that the translation reads naturally in English, avoiding awkward phrasing or unnatural sentence structures.
    4. Idiomatic Expressions: Ensure that idiomatic expressions are rendered appropriately in English, capturing both the literal and implied meanings.
    5. Cultural and Historical Allusions: Confirm that cultural and historical references are accurately represented and explained when necessary.
    6. Syntactic Ambiguities: Make sure that any syntactically ambiguous constructions in the original text are clearly and logically translated.
    7. Proper Nouns and Titles: Verify that proper nouns, titles, and technical terms are rendered correctly and consistently.

    Here are 5 examples showing the expected review and revision format for Classical Chinese texts:

    Example 1

    Translation:
    As for farmers, merchants, artisans, and traders, as well as servants, laborers, and slaves, those engaged in fishing and butchering, feeding oxen and herding sheep—all have predecessors who can serve as role models. By seeking knowledge broadly from them, nothing is unhelpful in one's endeavors.

    Review and Revision:

    1. Inaccuracies in Meaning: The translation accurately conveys the original meaning.
    2. Inconsistencies in Terminology: No inconsistencies found.
    3. Issues with Natural Flow: The translation reads naturally in English.
    4. Idiomatic Expressions: The phrase "博学求之" is rendered as "seeking knowledge broadly," which is appropriate.
    5. Cultural and Historical Allusions: The cultural allusions to different social classes and occupations are accurately represented.
    6. Syntactic Ambiguities: The syntactic structure is clear and logically translated.
    7. Proper Nouns and Titles: No proper nouns or titles are present in this passage.

    Revised Translation:
    As for farmers, merchants, artisans, and traders, as well as servants, laborers, and slaves, those engaged in fishing and butchering, feeding oxen and herding sheep—all have predecessors who can serve as role models. By seeking knowledge broadly from them, nothing is unhelpful in one's endeavors.

    Example 2

    Translation:
    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    Review and Revision:

    1. Inaccuracies in Meaning: The translation accurately conveys the original meaning.
    2. Inconsistencies in Terminology: No inconsistencies found.
    3. Issues with Natural Flow: The translation reads naturally in English.
    4. Idiomatic Expressions: The phrase "两头四臂" is rendered as "two heads
    253

    先是，隗邻村郝某之女极美，隗见而爱之，因烦媒灼题亲，已有成说，尚未文定。土豪万某闻之，欲聘为子妇，使人强郝允亲，择日过门。一日，隗造范所。刚至，范曰：“万某之子某，带酒此时归，路出某处，遇仇人怒殴。君俟其殴已去后，某若未死，杖杀之，妾能使郝某之女仍归君。”隗不欲。范授杖恿怂之，隗始去。时月微明，果见二人厮打。未几，一人仆。隗心知仆者当是万某，怜之，遂大言曰：“御人贼，勿得伤人！”持杖直赴，胜者自去。及隗至，仆者亦起，视之果万某。隗送万至万家而回。范曰：“君不惟不杀万，而反救之，何也？”隗曰：“见死不救非仁人。且杀其人而娶其妻，仆即为鳏终身，不为！”范喜曰：“君诚不愧为正人，云为若是，终得丽偶。”

    Explanation

    253

    Previously, Wei, a man from a neighboring village, saw Hao's daughter, who was extremely beautiful, and fell in love with her. He then asked a matchmaker to propose marriage, and the marriage was agreed upon but not yet formally confirmed. A local power figure, Wan, heard about this and wanted to marry Hao's daughter to his son. He forced Hao to agree to the marriage and set a date for the wedding. One day, Wei went to see Fan. As soon as he arrived, Fan said, "Wan's son is returning home drunk right now. On the way, he encountered an enemy and was angrily beaten. You should wait until the beating is over. If he is not dead, beat him to death, and I can make Hao's daughter return to you." Wei did not want to do this. Fan gave him a stick and encouraged him, and Wei finally left. It was dimly lit by the moon, and he indeed saw two people fighting. Soon, one person fell. Wei knew the fallen person was Wan and pitied him. He loudly said, "Do not harm others!" He held the stick and went straight to them, and the winner left. When Wei arrived, the fallen person also got up, and he saw it was indeed Wan. Wei sent Wan home and returned. Fan said, "Why did you not only not kill Wan but also save him?" Wei replied, "Not rescuing someone when they are dying is not the act of a benevolent person. Moreover, killing someone and then marrying their wife, I would become a widower for life, I won't do that!" Fan happily said, "You truly do not shame yourself as an upright person, doing things like this, you will eventually find a beautiful partner."

    254

    一日，隗忧形于色。范问之，隗曰： “日用不继，是以隐忧。”范亦蹙额曰：“如之何？”多时始曰：“有一事可以致富，但于妾有害，嗣妾当阨时，祈君怜而拯之。”隗曰：“害由仆至，理合救拯。即不然，亦不宜坐视卿溺而不援之以手。”范曰：“山阴有一穴，如大狗窦。内一女狐仙，善睡，恒数月方醒。君择甲子日，蛇行而入，二十步外即宽大，亦明亮。石壁挂一铜镜，大如碗口，君怀之而出，镜中有美人，即狐仙之真容。地内有金银宝物，以镜照之，虽在黄泉，无不见；掘取之，可致巨富。”隗入穴取镜出。镜之妙用，悉如范言。范助隗掘取，数月之间，家藏无算矣。范劝隗止，隗从之。一日，范恐惧曰：“狐婢将至，见妾，怼之必深。渠若用武，妾难抵敌。祈君视妾有败势，即蓄气向宝镜后面竭力吹之，渠必仆。

    Explanation

    254

    One day, Wei showed his worry on his face. Fan asked him, and Wei said, "My daily expenses cannot be sustained, so I have hidden worries." Fan also frowned and said, "What can be done about it?" After a long time, he said, "There is something that can make us wealthy, but it will harm my wife. When my wife is in dire straits, I ask you to pity and rescue her." Wei said, "The harm comes from me, and it is reasonable to save and rescue. Even if it is inappropriate to sit idly by and watch you drown without offering a hand."

    Fan then said, "There is a cave in Shanyin, as large as a dog's den. Inside is a female fox spirit who is good at sleeping and often sleeps for several months before waking. Choose a day of the Jiazi cycle, crawl in like a snake. Twenty steps in, it becomes spacious and bright. A copper mirror hangs on the stone wall, as large as a bowl mouth. Take it out with you. There is a beautiful woman in the mirror, which is the true form of the fox spirit. There are gold and silver treasures underground. Use the mirror to reflect, and even if it is in the underworld, nothing is unseen. Dig and take, and you can lead to great wealth."

    Wei entered the cave and took out the mirror. The mirror's magical properties were exactly as Fan had described. Fan helped Wei dig and take the treasures, and within a few months, their family's wealth was immense. Fan advised Wei to stop, and Wei agreed.

    One day, Fan said in fear, "The fox maid is coming. If she sees me, she will surely be very angry with me. If she uses force, I will find it hard to resist. I ask you to watch for any signs of my defeat, then hold your breath and blow hard at the back of the treasure mirror. She will fall."

    255

    盖以镜中有渠形像也。”隗急曰：“卿不可乘其仆而伤之。”范应诺。且曰：“君与狐有宿分。君日置宝镜于怀，夜拥于衾，渠见君，必乐同枕衾。”言讫不见。第三日晚，隗酌酒自饮，狐女忽至，怒问曰：“谁令君窃取宝镜？”隗拽女坐，酌酒劝之饮。女连饮数杯。隗曰：“请勿怒，今夕非致怒时也。”女笑曰：“怒必择日耶？”曰：“非也。卿与仆对饮，不啻合卺，岂有合卺之夕而致怒者乎？”女不语。饮际，隗眈眈视女。女曰：“陋姿在镜中，君把玩数月，尚未看足耶？”隗曰：“仙姿在镜，如镜中之花，欲弄而不得，今得亲身攀折矣！”爰拽女同寝，女亦不拒。次日，仍问取镜之由。隗历言之，曰：“仆不知其为何仙。”女曰：“渠鬼仙也。其原尸在松下，故常依松。君执杖去从树北皮连击二十五击，渠晚上必来，妾将手刃之！”隗甚为范危。

    Explanation

    255

    The mirror reflects the image of the fox." Wei hurriedly said, "You must not take advantage of its vulnerability and harm it." Fan nodded in agreement. And he said, "You have a predestined relationship with the fox. You should keep the precious mirror close to you during the day and at night, under your pillow. When the fox sees you, it will surely be pleased to share your bed." He finished speaking and disappeared. On the third evening, Wei poured himself a drink and drank alone when the fox girl suddenly appeared, angrily asking, "Who made you steal the precious mirror?" Wei pulled the girl to sit down and poured her a drink to encourage her to drink. The girl drank several cups in a row. Wei said, "Please don't be angry, this evening is not the time to be angry." The girl laughed and said, "Is anger always on a specific day?" Wei replied, "No. You and I are drinking together, it's not just a wedding ceremony, how can there be anger on the night of a wedding?" The girl remained silent. During the drinking, Wei stared at the girl intently. The girl said, "Your ordinary appearance is in the mirror, you have been admiring it for months, still not enough?" Wei said, "The beautiful appearance in the mirror is like a flower in the mirror, wanting to play with it but can't, now I can personally pick it!" He pulled the girl to sleep together, and the girl did not resist. The next day, the girl still asked about the reason for taking the mirror. Wei explained it in detail, saying, "I don't know who she is." The girl said, "She is a ghostly immortal. Her original body is under the pine tree, so she often relies on the pine tree. You should go with a stick to the north side of the tree and hit it twenty-five times in a row, she will definitely come in the evening, I will personally kill her!" Wei was very worried for Fan.

    256

    隗如数击松归，及晚，方与狐对语，范忽至。狐女见之大怒，曰：“良人所取之镜，华之宝藏，山神令吾职守，不容假人。盖所假非人，彼赖以暴富，不啻助人为恶。今吾难免，必泄忿于汝始甘心。”言已，执杖向殴，范惧而出。狐女逐之，斗于庭前。隗恐夜黑二人有伤，令家人多烧烛以照之。移时，范不能支。隗如其数，大气吹镜后面，狐女果仆。范立俟不前，狐女起而复斗。隗视范将败，复吹，狐女复仆。连仆数次，气力已尽，隗劝入室。范亦从之入，曰：“事已至此，挽回无由，愿劝郎君散财济贫以赎姊罪。”狐女曰：“止有此一术以处之。”隗复应承之。狐女反恚为喜。隗设筵同酌以释夙嫌，狐女曰：“妾不能久奉箕帚。”范亦言月后即辞去。隗忧曰：“仆有二妻，不日即为鳏夫，不惟仆情难堪，卿等亦用心太忍矣！”狐女曰：“如君福命，自有丽偶。

    Explanation

    256

    Wei frequently struck pine and returned, and by evening, only then conversed with the fox. Fan suddenly arrived. The fox girl saw him and was greatly angered, saying, "The mirror that the good man took, a treasure of the noble house, the mountain god ordered me to guard, and does not allow lending to others. Because lending to the wrong person, they relied on it to become suddenly wealthy, no different from helping someone to do evil. Now I cannot avoid it, I must vent my anger on him to be satisfied." After speaking, she grasped a stick to attack, and Fan was afraid and fled. The fox girl chased him, and they fought in front of the courtyard. Wei was afraid that in the dark, the two would be hurt, and ordered the family to light many candles to illuminate. After some time, Fan could not withstand it. Wei did as he did, took a big breath and blew on the back of the mirror, and the fox girl indeed fell. Fan stood and waited without moving forward, and the fox girl got up and fought again. Wei saw that Fan was about to lose, blew again, and the fox girl fell again. She fell several times, exhausted her strength, and Wei persuaded him to enter the room. Fan also entered with him, saying, "The matter has reached this point, cannot be reversed, I wish to advise the young master to distribute wealth to the poor to atone for his sister's sin." The fox girl said, "There is only this one method to deal with it." Wei agreed again. The fox girl changed her anger to joy. Wei prepared a banquet and drank together to resolve long-standing grievances. The fox girl said, "I cannot serve as a wife for a long time." Fan also said that he would leave after a month. Wei worried and said, "I have two wives, soon to be a widower, not only is it difficult for me emotionally, you also are too cruel in your intentions!" The fox girl said, "If your fate is good, you will naturally have a beautiful match."

    257

    ”隗曰：“丽偶何在？有此家业，不难续娶，然所娶未必德容兼全；倘有不佳，憾遗终身。”狐女曰：“择之。”隗曰：“笄女不出闺门，何由而择？”狐女曰：“有宝镜可恃。”盖有美人，以镜照之，则美人之形容留镜中，若另照一人，而前人始杳。遂起身谓范曰：“敢烦仙姊代劳，明日务照一美人来，吾三人同观之。”范应诺，狐女始以镜授之。隗心疑曰：“镜在吾怀，渠何由取去？”摸之，已无有矣。次日，范复命。狐女视镜中人不佳，曰：“吾见犹厌，况良人乎？”隗视之，果摇首不语。凡照数人，隗俱不以为可。后照一美人来，狐女见之，曰：“得之矣！良人见之，必魂飞魄散，不能自由矣！”隗见曰：“得此，可不祈怜于卿二人矣。此谁氏之女？”范曰：“绅士仲氏，同郡郡内居，违此一百六十馀里。”隗急烦媒说合。将过门，范与狐女俱辞去。

    Explanation

    257

    ”Wei asked, 'Where is the beautiful couple? With this household, it is not difficult to remarry, but the one I marry may not have both virtue and beauty; if it is not good, it will be a regret for life.' The fox girl said, 'Choose one.' Wei replied, 'A woman of marriageable age does not leave the inner chambers, how can one choose?' The fox girl said, 'There is a precious mirror that can be relied upon.' There was a beautiful woman, and when you look into the mirror, the image of the beautiful woman remains in the mirror; if you look at another person, the previous image disappears. She then stood up and told Fan, 'I dare to trouble the fairy sister to do it, please make sure to bring a beautiful woman tomorrow, and we three will view it together.' Fan agreed, and the fox girl then gave the mirror to him. Wei wondered, 'The mirror was in my possession, how could she take it away?' He felt it, and it was no longer there. The next day, Fan reported back. The fox girl looked into the mirror and found the person not satisfactory, saying, 'Even I find it displeasing, how much more so the gentleman?' Wei looked at it and indeed nodded his head and remained silent. After looking at several people, Wei did not think any of them were suitable. Later, when a beautiful woman appeared in the mirror, the fox girl saw her and said, 'I have found her! The gentleman will surely be overwhelmed and lose his senses!' Wei saw her and said, 'With this, I can no longer seek pity from the two of you. Whose daughter is this?' Fan replied, 'A gentleman named Zhong, who lives in the same prefecture, more than one hundred sixty miles away.' Wei quickly troubled the matchmaker to arrange the marriage. When the wedding was about to take place, Fan and the fox girl both left.

    258

    隗届六旬，得疾，医药罔效。自知降年方永，何致不起，必有别故，遂谓妻仲氏曰：“卿为仆召嫡妻范氏去！”仲曰：“何处召之？”隗以击某处之松与击之之数语之。仲急命驾而往，路思曰：“击松而范自至，范必畏击。”因倍其数而击之。归，实告隗，隗曰：“二十五数，数半大衍，渠已不胜震惊。今以大衍之数击之，渠必仇卿。”仲曰：“妾不惧！”未几，范至，怒问隗曰：“君遣谁氏击松来？”隗未及答，仲应之曰：“吾！”范大怒。仲拽之坐曰：“吾语汝：妇之于夫，生则为终身之仰望，死则遗半生之憾恨。为之服，等于翁姑父母；一为寡，甚于鳏夫孤独。汝既列鬼仙，必知郎君之疾。既知之而度外置之，无志无良，不节不义，即鸣鼓而攻，还为过？尚以不韪责人耶？”范闻之，起身端肃曰：“吾之罪也！”谓隗曰：“君疾不能幸免，惟轻财好施始可愈。”隗曰：“仆捐资济物已三十馀年。

    Explanation

    258

    At the age of sixty, Wei fell seriously ill, and medical treatment proved ineffective. Realizing that his allotted lifespan was long, he wondered why he would not recover and concluded that there must be another reason. He then told his wife Zhong, "You should call my legitimate wife Fan here!" Zhong asked, "Where should I call her from?" Wei instructed her to strike a pine tree at a certain place and tell it the number of times to strike it. Zhong hurriedly arranged for a carriage and went to fetch Fan, thinking, "Striking the pine tree will bring Fan here, and Fan must be afraid of the strike." She doubled the number of strikes. Upon returning, she told Wei, "Twenty-five strikes, half of the large derivation, she has already been shocked beyond endurance. Now, striking her with the number of the large derivation, she will surely resent you." Zhong replied, "I am not afraid!" Soon after, Fan arrived and angrily asked Wei, "Who did you send to strike the pine tree?" Before Wei could answer, Zhong responded, "I did!" Fan became furious. Zhong pulled her to sit down and said, "Let me tell you: a wife, when alive, is the lifelong hope of her husband; when dead, she leaves behind half a life's regret. Wearing mourning clothes for her is equal to mourning for one's in-laws and parents; being a widow is even more pitiable than being a widower or an orphan. Since you are among the ghosts and immortals, you must know about my husband's illness. Knowing it and yet ignoring it, you lack both will and virtue, and your actions are neither proper nor just. Even if you attack with drums and bells, is it not a fault? And still, you blame others?" Fan, upon hearing this, stood up and spoke respectfully, "It is my fault!" She said to Wei, "Your illness cannot be avoided, but only by being generous and charitable can it be cured." Wei replied, "I have been donating money to help others for over thirty years."

    259

    ”范曰：“既往不说，非竭力捐白金三、四万不可也。”隗曰：“即欲捐之，亦须病愈。”范曰：“君存此心，可立愈。”隗曰：“定如卿言。”病果旬日愈。华阴地丁银三万六千馀两，时岁饥，民苦征徭。隗见邑尹，言愿代完合邑征赋，外奉尹银若干。尹喜，从之。交纳毕，范喜曰：“是举也，不惟君子孙福泽不可量，狐姊亦赖之免前愆矣！”未几，狐女果至，谢范曰：“妹之得免天谴，固以良人挥金如土，然皆吾姊恿怂之力也。

    Explanation

    259

    Fan said, "Do not dwell on the past; it is necessary to expend great effort and donate tens of thousands of taels of silver." Wei replied, "If one wishes to donate, one must first recover from illness." Fan said, "If you hold this heart, you can be healed immediately." Wei said, "Certainly as you say." Indeed, the illness was cured within ten days. The silver tax (地丁银) of Huayin amounted to more than thirty-six thousand taels. At that time, there was a famine, and the people suffered from taxes and corvée labor. Wei met with the magistrate of the county and said he was willing to pay the entire county's taxes on behalf of the people, and would additionally give the magistrate some silver. The magistrate was pleased and agreed. After completing the payment, Fan was delighted and said, "This act not only ensures that the noble descendants' blessings and fortune cannot be measured, but also allows the fox sister to rely on it to be forgiven for her past sins!" Shortly after, the fox girl indeed arrived and thanked Fan, saying, "My sister's escape from heavenly punishment is certainly because my husband spends money like dirt, but it is all due to my sister's encouragement and urging."

    260

    ”隗曰：“仆有一事，刻刻在念，不知卿等亦能为力否？”狐女曰：“何事？”隗曰：“大不孝之事也。”狐女曰：“易为之。”历相群婢，有一婢极媸，狐女曰：“此婢宜男，纳之必得令嗣。”隗丑之。狐女曰：“纳之，亦可少折消受娇妻美妾之福也。”谓范曰：“可以行矣！”言讫俱杳，不复至。隗年将古稀，仍无子，不得已纳丑女，果得子。其子孙不改其道，世享厥福。现今后人尤重读书人，凡贫儒与失馆游学者，偶至其家，以客礼待之，供给衣食，礼貌不衰。

    Explanation

    260

    Wei said, "I have something that is constantly on my mind, I wonder if you can help me with it?" The fox woman asked, "What is it?" Wei replied, "It is an act of extreme filial impiety." The fox woman said, "It is easy to do." She examined the maidservants and found one who was extremely ugly. The fox woman said, "This maid is suitable for bearing a son, if you take her as a wife, you will surely have a good heir." Wei found her ugly. The fox woman said, "If you take her as a wife, it will also somewhat reduce the enjoyment of having beautiful wives and concubines." She told Fan, "We can act now!" After speaking, they all vanished, and did not return. Wei, nearing seventy years old, still had no son, and had no choice but to take the ugly woman as a wife. Indeed, he had a son. His descendants did not change their ways, and enjoyed their blessings throughout generations. In modern times, people especially value scholars. If poor scholars or those who have lost their teaching positions or are traveling for study happen to visit his home, they are treated with the courtesy due to guests, provided with food and clothing, and the politeness does not diminish.

    261

    虚白道人曰：世之得外财而不能久享者，未必其福命薄，或以视财如命之所致也。盖非分之财，宜公之于人，不宜私之于己。如私之，则犯造物之所忌，岂能久享哉？若隗某之所为，可为得处富之道矣！其后人最重读书人，尤为可嘉。

    　　虚白道人之言，允哉！世之登月无仕、积多金者，非不赫奕一时也。乃或及子孙而馨焉，或仅及其身而罄焉，无他，不正故也。隗生之福，以正得之。彼华阴令之纳赂，其不能久享也，必矣！ 上元李瑜谨注

    　　何其酣畅淋漓耶！其事优于莲香传，文亦如骖之靳。渔樵散人志

    《益智录》（中） [清] 解 鉴 著
    作者:l4z5 发表时间: 2004/12/20 12:48 点击:247次 修改 精华 删除 置顶 来源 转移 收藏
    　　益智录卷之四

    Explanation

    261

    The Virtual White Man said: In the world, those who acquire wealth from external sources but cannot enjoy it for long are not necessarily people with thin fortune or fate; it may be due to their extreme attachment to wealth. Wealth that is not rightfully earned should be shared with others, not kept for oneself. If one keeps it, one violates what the Creator abhors, and how could one enjoy it for long? If Wei did this, it would be a way to properly handle wealth! His descendants highly value scholars, which is particularly commendable.

    The words of the Virtual White Man are indeed true! People who pass the imperial examinations but do not hold an official position, or those who accumulate a lot of money, are not unremarkable for a time. But sometimes their wealth fades away by the time it reaches their descendants, or it runs out during their own lifetime; there is no other reason; it is because of improper conduct. Wei's fortune was gained through proper means. The magistrate of Huayin accepting bribes will certainly not be able to enjoy his wealth for long! —— Carefully annotated by Li Yu of Shangyuan

    How refreshing and thorough it is! The story is better than Lotus Fragrance Transmission, and the writing is as smooth and well-coordinated as a team of three horses pulling a chariot. —— Recorded by Fisherman and Woodcutter Wanderer

    Wisdom Record (Volume 2) by Xie Jian of the Qing Dynasty

    262

    散花天女（稿本作“散花仙女”）

    　　孝子吴清泰，安徽太平诸生也。其祖居官廉正，馀资无多，卒于任。辇灵归，已罄所有。其父生长宦门，不谙勤俭，渐凌夷。至生为诸生时，清贫几饔飧不给。无已，货家中物作小生意，蝇头微利，不足以供双亲。闻有佣人誊册籍者，以名应之，昼理贸易，夜作抄胥，以所得钱为炊。老少异粮，己食粗粝，而以美者奉亲。亲偶以食馀赐之，伪食之，留以备甘旨。常自叹曰：“双亲千计苦，一日两餐难。”日用不足，父母恒终夜嗟咨。生闻之，心如锥刺，自恨实忝所生矣。时岁饥，中丞奉旨赈济。有同学某谓生曰：“盍往领之？”曰：“为一箪食往来廿馀里，实属不堪。且每走领妨碍他务，余一人或以无饥，其如受馁之二老何哉？”某谓：“闻中丞甚体恤士类，凡在诸生，不令赴赈厂，令于本学报名，照册予赈。”生半疑信。某曰：“录有中丞告示，可至敝斋一视。”生从之。其示云：“为分别周急以彰优礼事。

    Explanation

    262

    The Scattering Flower Celestial Maiden (Draft Version: "Scattering Flower Celestial Lady")

    Filsial son Wu Qingtai, a scholar from Taiping in Anhui, was born into a family with a long tradition of integrity. His grandfather, while in office, was upright and left little wealth behind him, which was exhausted upon his death. His father, raised in an official family, was not accustomed to diligence and frugality, and the family's fortunes gradually declined. By the time Wu Qingtai became a scholar, the family was so poor that they barely had enough for two meals a day. With no other choice, he sold household items to start a small business, earning only a meager profit that was insufficient to support his parents. He heard that there was a job copying documents, so he applied for it. During the day, he managed his small trade, and at night, he worked as a copyist, using the money he earned to buy food. The family rationed their food, with Wu Qingtai eating coarse grains while his parents received the better food. Occasionally, his parents would give him the leftover food, which he pretended to eat but saved for his parents. He often lamented, "My parents have endured countless hardships, and it is difficult for them to have two meals a day." The family's daily expenses were insufficient, and his parents would often lament throughout the night. Wu Qingtai, upon hearing this, felt as if needles were piercing his heart, and he deeply regretted not living up to his parents' expectations. At that time, there was a famine, and the provincial governor issued an order to provide relief. A classmate suggested, "Why don't you go and get it?" Wu Qingtai replied, "To travel over twenty miles for a single bowl of rice is truly unbearable. Moreover, going to collect relief would interfere with other tasks. Even if I am not hungry, what about my parents who are starving?" The classmate said, "I have heard that the provincial governor is very considerate of scholars. He does not allow them to go to the relief center. Instead, he has them register at their school, and relief is distributed according to a list." Wu Qingtai was half-doubtful. The classmate said, "There is a notice posted by the provincial governor. You can come to my study to see it." Wu Qingtai went, and the notice read: "To distinguish and assist those in urgent need to demonstrate preferential treatment."

    263

    照得官于诸生，素本优其礼貌；士之自处，各宜惜其身名。盖儒为席珍，品重连城之价；即室如悬磬，心轻托钵之缘。鸡鹤未可齐观，驽骥岂容同栈！兹当颁赈，宜有区分。近闻贫窭诸生，八口啼饥，动室人之交谪；两餐莫继，欲称贷而无门。思邀升斗之恩，颜殊壮士；背负橐囊以往，情类乞儿。杂中泽之嗷鸿，得勺水以活鲋。虽云锡赉自天，殊觉斯文扫地。衣冠盎歠，本部院诚目击神伤；舆隶签呼，尔诸生应怀惭颜赧。合行出示晓谕，为此示仰士子知悉：生不逢辰，蒙袂之形容足悯；贫原非病，子衿之体貌犹存。时当炊断囊空，周之可受；事等杯残炙冷，辱亦奚堪？自示之后，诸生不必再赴赈厂，各于本学报名，以凭照册发赈。庶隆施有别，而品无沾儒；实惠仍沾，而人不贱士。例恩膏于廪膳，可资萤练读书；期厚礼于笙簧，伫待鹿鸣赴宴。本部院之所以分别周急以彰优礼者，盖为此也。特谕。”生读毕大喜，即禀亲赴省。

    Explanation

    263

    Official Notice to Scholars

    Respected scholars,

    From ancient times, officials have always treated scholars with courtesy and respect. It is essential that each of you cherishes your own reputation and conduct yourselves with dignity. Scholars are like precious treasures, valued as highly as treasures worth cities. Even if your situation is as poor as a house as empty as a gong, you should not lightly regard the begging bowl. Chickens and cranes should not be equated, nor should slow horses and swift horses be treated equally.

    Now, as we distribute relief, it is important to differentiate and provide appropriate assistance. Recently, we have heard of poor scholars with eight mouths crying for food, causing their households to suffer criticism. Unable to have two meals in a row, they want to borrow but have no way. Wanting to receive even a small amount of help, their faces are quite that of heroes. Carrying a bag on their back, they feel like beggars. Like hungry swans in a marsh, getting a spoonful of water to save a fish, although it is said to be a gift from heaven, it feels that the dignity of scholars is being tarnished. Officials and scholars, the department sincerely feels heartbroken. The act of summoning students, you scholars should feel ashamed.

    It is appropriate to issue a notice to inform you: not born in good times, the appearance of covering one's face is pitiable. Poverty is not a disease, the demeanor of scholars still exists. When cooking is interrupted and the bag is empty, help can be received. Matters like cold food and cold wine, how can one bear the shame? After this notice, students do not need to go to the relief factory again. Each should register at their school to be issued relief according to the register. So that the distribution of relief is differentiated, and the reputation of scholars is not tarnished. Real benefits are still received, and people do not look down on scholars. The example of grace in the granary can support studying by the light of fireflies. Expecting generous treatment in the music of flutes and reeds, awaiting the banquet of the deer calling. The reason why the department distinguishes and helps those in urgent need to show preferential treatment is for this reason. Especially ordered.

    The student read it and was very happy, then reported to his parents and went to the province.

    264

    及领钱缗出，而夕阳已斜，急市米归。欣然曰：“至家与父母饱食数日，亦年来不可多得之福。”自晨未食，乐而忘饥，行至中途，顿觉肠鸣，痛不能步。路旁柳阴下有石凳，暂就稍息。炎欠有少女至，亦憩树下，柳黛莲钩，旷世无其娟丽。生亦无心顾眄。女乃目属而笑，袖出金置石上曰：“可携此速归，五日仍候君于是。”转瞬即不见。生异之。五日，至树下，女果俟焉。随女同行，数武外觉足不履地，迅急如飞。未几至一第，宏厂如厅事，床几衾帐悉具。

    Explanation

    264

    When he collected the money, the sun was already slanting, so he urgently bought rice and returned home. Joyfully, he said, "To eat to my fill with my parents for several days is also a rare blessing in recent years." Since morning without eating, he was happy and forgot his hunger. Walking midway, he suddenly felt intestinal rumbling, painful and unable to walk. By the roadside under the willow shade, there was a stone bench, and he temporarily rested a little. A young girl arrived, also resting under the tree, with beautiful eyebrows and feet, unparalleled in grace and beauty throughout the ages. He also had no intention to look at her. The girl then looked at him with her eyes and smiled, taking out gold from her sleeve and placing it on the stone, saying, "Can carry this and return quickly. Still wait for you here after five days." She disappeared in an instant. He found it strange. Five days later, he went to the tree, and the girl indeed waited there. He followed the girl to walk together, and after a few steps, felt his feet not touching the ground, swift and urgent like flying. Soon, he arrived at a mansion, spacious like a hall, with bed, table, blanket, and curtain all prepared.

    265

    女乃取酒与生酌，执杯劝饮，情意欢畅，不觉大醉。女扶卧榻上，遂并枕而寝，而巫山云雨，已入黄粱梦中矣。及晓，生问女邦族。女曰：“妾散花天女也，慕君孝，故自荐以分君忧耳。”自此衣食皆取足于女。越三载，父母闻之，虞女不育。生仰求于女，女笑曰：“君诚强人以所难，此事亦可强求也？且其权不在妾，奈何以自为政之事旁求于人也？”无何，女有身，生一子，名涛。女曰：“此子后有杀生祸。”十五岁为之完姻。又二年，生父母相继亡。殡后，女以纸封授涛曰：“有大难，可启视。”涛珍藏之。晨兴朝亲，二人已不知所往，但惶遽悲悼而已。

    Explanation

    265
