    The woman then took wine and shared it with the man, holding the cup and urging him to drink. Their emotions were joyful and harmonious, and they did not realize they were drunk. She helped him lie down on the bed, and they lay down together on the same pillow. In the midst of their intimacy, it was as if they were in a dream of yellow millet. By the next morning, the man asked about the woman’s background. She replied, “I am the Heavenly Flower Princess. I admired your filial piety, so I offered myself to share your burdens.” From then on, all their food and clothing came from her. After three years, their parents heard about them and worried that the woman would not bear children. The man turned to the woman, pleading, “You are truly asking me to do something difficult. This matter cannot be forced. Moreover, the power does not lie with me. Why should I ask others to do something that I should do myself?” Soon after, the woman became pregnant and gave birth to a son, whom they named Tao. She said, “This son will later cause trouble involving life and death.” At the age of fifteen, they arranged his marriage. Two years later, the man’s parents passed away one after another. After the funeral, the woman gave Tao a paper packet and said, “If there is a great calamity, you may open it and look inside.” Tao cherished it. In the morning, when they paid their respects to their parents, the two of them were nowhere to be found, leaving only anxiety and grief.

    266

    后二十年，涛偶伤人命，意欲逃遁，忽忆亲嘱，急启遗封视之。言：“本县尹吴澍，实汝胞弟。”涛喜投案请罪，县令以误伤论，纳赎得释。既归，县令踵至，自言籍入大兴，十七岁中进士，部选于此。父言弟与兄系同胞，故来相认。涛急与澍入署省亲，而亲已杳，兄弟乃知其父母皆仙人。澍以祖茔在太，遂徙太，与涛同居焉。

    Explanation

    266

    Twenty years later, Tao accidentally caused someone's death and intended to flee. Suddenly remembering a relative's instruction, he quickly opened the sealed envelope to read it. It said, "The magistrate of this county, Wu Shu, is actually your younger brother." Tao gladly surrendered himself to the authorities to confess his crime. The magistrate considered it an accidental injury and allowed him to pay a fine to be released. After returning home, the magistrate came to visit and said that he was from Daxing and became a Jinshi (high-ranking scholar) at the age of 17, and was selected by the Ministry of Personnel to serve here. Your father said that your younger brother and you are full siblings, so he came to recognize you. Tao quickly went with Wu Shu to the magistrate's office to see your parents, but they had already passed away. The brothers then realized that their parents were immortals. Wu Shu, because his ancestral tomb was in Tai, moved there and lived with Tao.

    267

    中丞施赈优士一示，其事卓越，固自可传。 马竹吾

    　　狗冢坡（稿本作“义犬”）

    　　长清蛾眉山北有狗冢坡，坡上有义狗冢。盖赵某者，居长之边鄙，而贾于城市。一日，谋本资以益之，抵家，鬻田数亩，得二百金。平明，携银策蹇赴城，不意其家之畜犬随之也。路径山坡，忽欲泄下，系驴于树而便焉。起即乘驴行，比至，始知银失；急欲返觅，时已日暮。因念通衢失物，安有再得之理？明日遣人寻之，无所见，惟以破财免灾自慰而已。后月馀，赵归，见犬死于坡上，毛革已脱败。以足蹴之，囊出。启视，白金宛在，始知犬为逻守遗金饿死。赵遂埋犬于山坡，坡亦以狗得名。

    Explanation

    267

    The Censor-in-Chief demonstrated relief and favor to the scholars, an act so outstanding that it naturally deserves to be passed on. — Ma Zhugu

    Dog's Grave Slope (the manuscript version is "Loyal Dog")

    In the north of Mount Emei in Changqing, there is a Dog's Grave Slope, on which stands a grave of a loyal dog. There was a certain Zhao who lived in the outskirts of Changqing and traded in the city. One day, he planned to increase his capital, arrived home, sold several acres of land, and got two hundred gold coins. At dawn, he carried the silver and rode a donkey to the city, unaware that his family's dog was following him. When he reached the slope, he suddenly needed to relieve himself, tied the donkey to a tree, and did so. Afterward, he got back on the donkey and continued, only to realize upon arrival that the silver was lost. He hurried to return to look for it, but it was already dusk. He thought that losing something in a busy street, how could it possibly be found again? The next day, he sent someone to look for it, but found nothing, so he consoled himself with the idea of breaking the loss to avoid disaster. After more than a month, Zhao returned and saw the dog dead on the slope, its fur and skin already deteriorated. He kicked it with his foot, and the bag came out. Upon opening it, the white gold was still there, and he realized that the dog had been guarding the lost gold and died of hunger. Zhao then buried the dog on the slope, and the slope also became known by the dog's name.

    268

    节 烈 女

    　　寿如山，晋人也。富有田产，一子一孙。未几而妇卒，未几子与媳相继卒，止剩一孙名得仁，尚未婚娶。及服满，将择吉，而仁复卒。仁所聘之妻边氏闻卜，欲奔丧，母不欲，曰：“渠家无可依之人，一为之服，势难择配。”氏曰：“‘母也天只，不谅人只！’至死靡他，实女素志也。”涕泣不已。母曰：“俟殡期与汝偕往。”氏曰：“闻丧宜往，夫何俟！”氏至寿室，对灵大恸，哀哭异常。初则人以为过当，继则咸以为可怜。及夕，母劝氏归。氏曰：“是即女家，将焉归？”劝之再四，不听，母自归。殡期尚违月馀，氏昼理丧务，夜傍柩寝，暇则呼天而泣，泪尽继之以血。邻里闻之，悉为之酸楚。比至葬，扶柩窀穸，颜色之戚，哭泣之哀，见者莫不痛惜。

    Explanation

    268

    Chaste and Loyal Woman

    Shou Rushan was a person from the state of Jin. He was wealthy with land and property, having one son and one grandson. Not long after his wife died, his son and daughter-in-law also passed away in quick succession, leaving only his grandson named Deren, who had not yet married. After completing the mourning rites, they were planning to choose an auspicious date for the wedding, but Deren died again. Upon hearing of the divination, Bian Shi, who was engaged to Deren, wished to rush to the funeral, but her mother did not want her to go, saying, "There is no one left in your family to rely on. If you mourn for him, it will be difficult to find a suitable match." She replied, "‘Mother, heaven, do not understand me!’ Until death, I will remain faithful, as this has always been my intention." She wept uncontrollably. Her mother said, "Wait until the funeral date and I will accompany you." She said, "When I hear of the death, I should go. Why wait!" She went to Shou's house and, in front of the spirit tablet, she wept bitterly, her grief being extraordinary. At first, people thought her reaction was excessive, but soon they all felt her plight was pitiful. By evening, her mother tried to persuade her to return. She said, "This is my family, where shall I go?" Despite repeated attempts to persuade her, she refused to listen, and her mother returned home. The funeral was still more than a month away. During the day, she managed the funeral arrangements, and at night, she slept beside the coffin. In her spare time, she called out to heaven and wept, until her tears ran out and were replaced by blood. The neighbors heard of her grief and were all moved to tears. When it came time for the burial, as she supported the coffin to the grave, her face showed deep sorrow and her crying was heart-wrenching. All who saw her could not help but feel sorrowful.

    269

    寿氏累世单传，无可过继，氏甚以无后为虞。忽念祖翁虽老，身体尚健，遂市美婢，令侍祖翁寝。甫二年许，诞生一子，名之曰静。襁褓中而婢忽卒，氏乃佣媪以乳之，自亲保抱，不轻托人。其长育顾复，直等劬劳于母氏。及静稍长，延师教之读。静聪慧，过目不忘，十六岁科试冠军，因得婚于尚孝廉之女。至如山以老卒，静心无忌惮，惟曼游是好。氏劝之，静以叔翁自居，置若罔闻。年馀，家产殆尽矣已。氏昼夜涕泣，勺饮不入口者三日。静心动，翻然自悔，亲诣氏榻，切言改过。言之再三，氏仍恸哭不已，静乃长跪榻前。静妻尚氏曰：“叔跪矣，贤侄妇宜稍宽恕。”氏闻之，亦起跪榻上曰：“当侄妇之奔丧也，吾家无祭墓人矣。

    Explanation

    269

    The Shou family had been single-line for many generations, with no one to adopt. The mother was deeply anxious about not having descendants. Suddenly, she thought of her elderly grandfather, who was still healthy, and decided to buy a beautiful concubine to serve him. After about two years, a son was born, and he was named Jing. When Jing was still in swaddling clothes, the concubine suddenly died. The mother then hired a wet nurse to breastfeed him and personally took care of him, not lightly entrusting him to others. She raised and nurtured him with the same diligence as a mother would. When Jing grew older, she hired a teacher to educate him in reading. Jing was intelligent, able to remember what he saw, and at sixteen, he won the championship in the imperial examination, thus marrying the daughter of a scholar-official.

    After the grandfather passed away, Jing became heartless and unrestrained, loving only to wander aimlessly. The mother tried to advise him, but Jing considered himself a younger uncle and ignored her advice. Within a year, almost all the family wealth was exhausted. The mother wept day and night, not drinking even a drop of water for three days. Jing was moved and repented, coming to his mother's bedside to earnestly speak about correcting his mistakes. He repeated his words several times, but the mother continued to weep uncontrollably. Jing then knelt before her bed. Jing's wife, Shang, said, "Uncle is kneeling, the virtuous niece-in-law should be a bit more lenient." Upon hearing this, the mother also got up and knelt on the bed, saying, "When my niece-in-law rushed to the funeral, there was no one left to perform ancestral rites in our family."

    270

    出冀幸万一之谋，得叔生焉。不幸庶祖姑复故，彼时侄妇抚叔，委干就湿，日夜保护，岂好自苦？盖以光前裕后悉在阿叔一人之身。今忽顿易素行，不事举业，侄妇之所以期望于叔者已成画饼，不惟死不瞑目，实无颜见祖翁于地下。”言已，复哭。静闻氏言，亦不禁与之俱哭。尚氏劝之曰：“侄妇三日未食，汝与叔同跪多时，请少节之。”氏乃拭泪，令尚扶静起。静令尚进食，氏不食，静夫妇苦劝之，氏乃食。氏以哀恸过度，致疾不起，静奉汤药如事母，月馀始瘥。嗣静偶废驰，氏辄饮泣不食。静恐稍负氏意，锐心取进，遂连捷南宫，官至御史。上疏陈情，封氏为少淑人，赐节孝匾额。七十馀岁无病卒，静之縗麻哭泣，如持亲丧焉。

    Explanation

    270

    In hopes of a slim chance, they welcomed the birth of a younger brother. Unfortunately, their grandmother had passed away again, and at that time, the niece-in-law took care of her younger brother, tending to him from infancy, protecting him day and night. Was this out of self-sacrifice? No, it was because the responsibility to bring glory to the ancestors and benefit future generations rested entirely on the younger brother. Now, he suddenly changed his usual conduct, not pursuing scholarly or official careers. The niece-in-law’s expectations for her younger brother had become like a distant dream, and she felt as if she could not rest in peace after death, nor face her ancestors with shame in the afterlife.” After speaking, she wept again. When Shang heard this, he could not help but join in her tears. Shang advised, “The niece-in-law has not eaten for three days, and you have been kneeling with your younger brother for a long time. Please take a break.” The niece-in-law then wiped her tears and had Shang help Jing stand up. Jing instructed Shang to serve food, but the niece-in-law refused to eat. Jing and his wife urged her repeatedly, and she finally ate. Due to her excessive grief, she fell ill and could not recover, and Jing cared for her as if she were his mother, and she recovered after more than a month. Later, when Jing became negligent, the niece-in-law would cry and refuse to eat. Jing was afraid of disappointing her and became determined to advance, achieving repeated success in the imperial examinations and rising to the rank of Censor. He submitted a memorial to the emperor expressing his feelings, and the niece-in-law was posthumously honored with the title of “Shao Shu Ren” and received a tablet inscribed with the virtue of filial piety and chastity. She died peacefully at over seventy years old, and Jing mourned her deeply, as if burying a close relative.

    271

    大义森严，至情悱恻。反复读之，令人落泪。 马竹吾

    　　牛鬼

    　　山海关以东深山庄，农家所畜耕牛，田事毕悉驱牧于深山，村中同佣一人看守。群牛在山，最畏虎伤，每逢虎至，群牛中自出一牛敌之。此常有之事，不足为异也，但不能胜耳。伊任者，佣于庄众，麾牛赴山，日渐深入。一日晨兴，方欲求牧于刍，忽来一虎。伊大惊，不禁大呼曰：“虎来矣！”呼未已，有一雄牛腾身出，直赴虎。伊身旁有树，遂登树而观之，见虎呈爪牙，牛恃蹄角，竞斗多时，胜负不分，虎自去。牛食蒸孔急。伊知牛已饥，恐虎再来，急下持干糇以饲之。饲已，虎果至。

    Explanation

    271

    Strict adherence to great principles, profound and poignant emotions. Repeatedly reading it makes one cry. Ma Zhuwu

    The Cow Demon

    In the deep mountain village east of the Shanhai Pass, farmers keep oxen for plowing fields. After the farming season ends, they drive the cattle to the mountains, where a hired person watches over them. When the cattle are in the mountains, they are most afraid of being injured by tigers. Whenever a tiger comes, one of the cows steps forward to confront it. This is a common occurrence, nothing unusual, but the cow cannot defeat the tiger. The hired person, employed by the villagers, drives the cattle into the mountains, gradually going deeper. One morning, as he was about to lead the cattle to graze, a tiger suddenly appeared. The hired person was greatly startled and could not help but cry out, "The tiger has come!" Before he finished speaking, a bull leaped out and rushed directly at the tiger. There was a tree near him, so he climbed up to watch. He saw the tiger showing its claws and teeth, while the bull relied on its hooves and horns, fighting fiercely for a long time. Neither side won, and the tiger eventually left. The cattle ate greedily due to hunger. The hired person knew the cow was hungry and feared the tiger might return, so he quickly climbed down and fed the cow dry rations. After feeding the cow, the tiger indeed returned.

    272

    牛膂力尤刚，斗虎益力，虎败去。伊反惊为喜，夜傍牛眠，自此恃以无恐。嗣有虎至，牛皆触去之，群牛赖以无伤。次年春暖，伊始驱牛归，向主人言牛之能，劝出钱共市一牛代其耕种，庄人从之。伊在山夜梦牛呼伊曰：“东人醒！东人醒！吾得食灵芝，颇有知，今夜当死。吾死后，吾之两角好收藏之，后有大用。”又曰：“倘逢不测，连呼‘牛鬼’数十声，吾自能迎救。”伊醒，以为幻不可信。晨视牛，果死。凡牛死于山，必剥其皮以给牛主，牛主始信。伊以是牛之异，止解收其两角而瘗之，庄人疑而辞焉。

    Explanation

    272

    The bull possessed extraordinary strength, and when it fought a tiger, it became even more powerful, ultimately defeating the tiger and driving it away. Upon this, the man was filled with joy, sleeping beside the bull at night, feeling secure and fearless thereafter. Whenever a tiger approached again, the bull would charge and drive it off, protecting the herd without any harm. The following spring, as the weather warmed, he began to drive the bull back, telling the owner about the bull's abilities and suggesting that they buy another bull to replace it for farming, which the villagers agreed to. One night, while in the mountains, the man dreamed that the bull called out to him, saying, "Wake up, Eastern man! Wake up! I have eaten the lingzhi mushroom and gained some knowledge; I shall die tonight. After I die, please collect my two horns and keep them, for they will be useful later." The bull then added, "If you encounter danger, call out 'niú guǐ' dozens of times, and I will come to your rescue." Upon waking, the man thought the dream was unreal and did not believe it. The next morning, he saw that the bull had indeed died. It was customary to remove the skin of a bull that died in the mountains and give it to the owner, who would then believe the story. However, the man, recognizing the bull's unusual nature, only collected its two horns and buried them. The villagers, however, doubted the story and refused to believe it.

    273

    伊由是入采参之伙入山采参，不知其居即众虎出没处也。伊以乘凉，为巢于树而宿。一日，日出时伙友尚贪眠未起，忽来数虎，噬杀诸友，饱食其肉。伊见之，魂魄俱失。多时，虎始去。伊欲下，恐虎复至，忽忆牛言，连呼“牛鬼”而下，向东急行。方数百步，忽见虎蝇随风飞来，知虎复至，急登树以避。及虎复去，日已斜，呼“牛鬼”者不知几千声矣。忽自东来一人，身躯硕大，状如青兕，仰面谓伊曰：“东人速下！有某在，可保无患。”伊既下，其人曰：“从某来！”其行甚缓，然竭力追随始不为所后。其人引伊至一第，庐舍坚牢，乃扶伊上坐，修家人礼。

    Explanation

    273

    From then on, the person joined a group that went into the mountains to gather ginseng.
    They did not know that their dwelling was where tigers roamed.
    He made a nest in the tree to sleep.
    Suddenly several tigers came, devoured all his friends, and ate their flesh.
    He saw it, and both his soul and spirit were lost.
    After a long time, the tigers finally left.
    He wanted to descend, but feared the tigers would return.
    Suddenly remembering what the cow had said, he called out "cow ghost" repeatedly while descending.
    Hurriedly headed east.
    After walking several hundred steps, suddenly saw tiger flies flying with the wind, knowing the tigers had returned, hurriedly climbed back up the tree to avoid them.
    When the tigers finally left again, the sun was already slanting, and he had called out "cow ghost" countless times.
    Suddenly a person came from the east, with a large body, looking like a green ox, and said to him, "Easterner, hurry down! With me here, you can be assured of no harm."
    After he descended, the person said, "Follow me!" His pace was very slow, but with great effort, he managed to keep up.
    The person led him to a mansion, with sturdy houses, and helped him sit down, performing the rites of a host.

    274

    伊深疑讶，心计曰：“是即吾呼之牛鬼耶？”问其姓名，曰：“不必问。”未几，饷以酒食，服役悉男仆。问：“何无妻室？”答以“不能交牝，无需乎妻”。又曰：“君慎勿出游；即出游，亦慎勿出二百步外。”伊莫解其言。一日，闷甚出门，不敢远游，顾青山环抱，景致颇佳。忽山坡一卧虎，见伊遽起，伊大惧奔回，而虎竟不至。越数日，其人曰：“君惊气定矣，可复理生计。”伊曰：“令仆何为？”曰：“君非为采参到此耶？某处人参最多，君可从吾采取。”路崎岖，行甚不易。曰：“可乘吾！”遂倒地化为犁牛，伊知为牛鬼，乘之不疑。行无数刻，见众虎戏跃，牛负伊直赴之，虎见牛悉畏避。伊见其地多参，急下掘之。日夕，携参乘牛归，牛复化为人，如是者得参无虑数百斤。

    Explanation

    274

    He was deeply suspicious and surprised, thinking to himself, "Is this the cow ghost I called?" He asked about the person's name, and the response was, "There's no need to ask." Soon after, he was fed with wine and food, and all the servants were male. He asked, "Why do you have no wife?" The answer was, "I cannot mate with females, so there is no need for a wife." The person then said, "You must not go out; if you do, do not go more than two hundred steps away." He did not understand the words. One day, feeling very bored, he went out but did not venture far, looking at the green mountains surrounding him, the scenery was very beautiful. Suddenly, a tiger lying on the hillside saw him and suddenly rose, and he was greatly frightened and ran back, but the tiger did not come. After several days, the person said, "You are now calm, and you can resume your livelihood." He asked, "What should I do with my servant?" The person replied, "Didn't you come here to collect ginseng? There is the most ginseng somewhere, and you can follow me to collect it." The road was rugged, and it was very difficult to travel. The person said, "You can ride me!" Then fell to the ground and transformed into a plow ox, and he knew it was a cow ghost, riding it without doubt. Traveling for many moments, he saw many tigers playing and jumping, and the ox carried him straight towards them, and the tigers saw the ox and all fled. He saw that the place had a lot of ginseng and hurriedly dug it up. Day and night, he carried the ginseng back on the ox, and the ox transformed back into a person, and in this way, he obtained several hundred pounds of ginseng.

    275

    先是，伊父卒，母再嫁；阅年，母后夫又死，贫而无依，遂至冻馁。一日偶赴市，见母衣敝衣提筐丐食。伊顿念母德，急市熟食奉母，乞母归家。或谓伊曰：“母氏改嫁，情义已绝，君敬礼若是，未免已甚。”伊曰：“不然。使母后夫未死而敬礼之，为已甚；母后夫既死，衣食有赖而敬礼之，为已甚；母即衣食无着，素无见闻，悬揣迎养而敬礼之，犹为已甚。若呺寒市墟，乞怜无人，行丐冷食，日不一饱，即素不相识，见之心犹不忍，况生母乎？虽母之旧过为已极，而母之大德终难忘，此事不可以概论。

    Explanation

    275

    Previously, his father died, and his mother remarried. After a year, his mother's second husband also died, leaving them poor and without support, which led to their freezing and starving. One day, he chanced to go to the market and saw his mother wearing tattered clothes and carrying a basket to beg for food. He suddenly remembered his mother's virtue and quickly bought cooked food to offer to his mother, begging her to return home. Someone said to him, "Your mother remarried, and affection and righteousness have been severed. You are showing such respect, which cannot help but be excessive." He replied, "Not so. If my mother's second husband had not died and I showed such respect, it would be excessive; if my mother's second husband had died and she depended on clothes and food, and I showed such respect, it would be excessive; if my mother had no clothes or food, and I speculated about supporting her and showed such respect, it would still be excessive. But if she is howling cold in the market, with no one to show pity to, begging for cold food, not eating a full meal in a day, even if I did not know her, seeing her would still move my heart, how much more so for my birth mother? Although my mother's past misdeeds have reached their limit, her great virtue is ultimately unforgettable. This matter cannot be generalized."

    276

    ”或闻之曰：“吾诚小人也！”伊在牛鬼所，每夜独宿，忽念日久未归，老母无依，则惧；转念得此横财，无难娶妇，则喜。喜惧交迫，反覆自言，双睫为之不交。牛鬼谓之曰：“既有心事，可速归。”伊曰：“无之。”牛鬼曰：“人之孝亲，处常易，处变难。君遇变而能曲尽子心，诚令人敬服。君所得人参不必多带，能换世钱二百千足矣。其钱除奉母外，尽市女衣，吾将为君娶妇。去途有虎，吾送君。”及出山，曰：“君务期十日归来，吾候于此。”伊至家，历言牛鬼等事于母。及制衣妥，已及十日，遂别母而去。牛鬼果俟以同归。

    Explanation

    276

    Someone heard and said, "I am truly a lowly person!" The character was in a place associated with demons, staying alone every night. Suddenly, he thought that he had not returned home for a long time, and his elderly mother was without support, then he felt fear. Then he thought about getting this unexpected wealth, and it would not be difficult to marry, then he felt joy. Joy and fear alternately overwhelmed him, and he repeatedly spoke to himself, his eyelids could not close. The cow demon said to him, "Since you have something on your mind, you should return quickly." The character said, "Nothing." The cow demon said, "The filial piety of a person, in normal times it is easy, in times of change it is difficult. You can fully express your filial heart in times of change, truly admirable and worthy of respect." The cow demon continued, "The ginseng you obtained does not need to be carried in large quantities. It can be exchanged for 200,000 coins, which is enough. The money, except for honoring your mother, buy all the women's clothing. I will arrange for you to marry." The cow demon warned, "There are tigers on the way. I will accompany you." When the character left the mountain, the cow demon said, "You must return within ten days. I will wait here." The character returned home and told his mother about the matters involving the cow demon and others. When the clothes were ready, it had already been ten days, then he bid farewell to his mother and left. The cow demon indeed waited and accompanied him back.

    277

    一日夜向晨，隧风大作，转瞬而息。牛鬼请伊起，曰：“君所制服安在？”伊曰：“悉在此。”牛鬼曰：“今兹用着矣！君执衣赴宅后空谷中，有女赤身卧，女不许亲，勿与衣，事必谐。”伊去，果有笄女赤身缀泣。伊曰：“勿泣，吾送衣来矣。”女曰：“君果衣吾，君诚好德人。”伊笑曰：“吾非好德，实好色也。汝不为吾妻，吾不衣汝！”女不语。伊曰：“汝何以到此？吾何以知汝到此而送衣于汝？其间实有神力，殆天之作合，汝何故违也？”女闻伊言有理，遂许之。女衣毕，同伊至家，自言傅氏，并言父兄名字及里居，遂成夫妇。傅见牛鬼，曰：“妾之被风到此，必渠为之也。”伊亦为历言其实。次日，牛鬼曰：“此处有荒地，因多虎，无人开辟。其虎吾悉逐去，君可耕治之。倘日后再有虎至，可束草为牛，安吾两角置大门外，虎自远避。”言已而杳。

    Explanation

    277

    One night, just before dawn, a sudden and strong wind arose, but it quickly subsided. The ox ghost asked the man to rise and said, "Where are the clothes you have control over?" The man replied, "They are all here." The ox ghost said, "This is useful now! Take the clothes and go to the empty valley behind the house. There is a woman lying naked, and she does not allow intimacy. Do not give her clothes, and the matter will surely harmonize." The man went, and indeed, there was a woman with a hairpin, lying naked and weeping. The man said, "Do not weep; I have come to give you clothes." The woman said, "If you indeed clothe me, you are truly a good person." The man smiled and said, "I am not good, but I really like beauty. If you do not become my wife, I will not clothe you!" The woman remained silent. The man said, "Why did you come here? How did I know you were here and give you clothes? There is indeed divine power in between, it is likely the work of heaven. Why do you disobey?" The woman heard his words and found them reasonable, so she agreed. The woman was clothed, and she went with the man to his home. She said her surname was Fu and mentioned the names of her father and brothers and their place of residence. They became husband and wife. The woman saw the ox ghost and said, "I was brought here by the wind, it must be him who did it." The man also recounted the events in detail. The next day, the ox ghost said, "There is a wasteland here, because there are many tigers, and no one has cultivated it. I have driven away all the tigers. You can cultivate it. If tigers come again in the future, you can tie grass to make a cow, place my two horns on the outside of the main gate, and tigers will naturally avoid it." He spoke and then disappeared.

    278

    伊念母，意欲迎养，恐妻不欲，因将母改嫁等情语妻，兼告己意，哀其曲从。傅氏曰：“可。但妾可以姑事之，不可姑称之。盖直称以姑，不惟妾意或有勉强，母心亦有惭愧。外祖家奚姓？”伊曰：“姓刘。”傅曰：“君伪言于母，言妾惟知母为君养母，而以刘母称之。妾尽妾之心，母量母之分，斯可久居而无违言。不然，妾即竭力奉事，若不如母意，必将谓妾不以姑事母，而妾心终难自白。”伊曰：“卿之言然。”伊将行，傅氏曰：“斯时妾父母必谓妾已死，祈君通一信息。”伊应诺。归见母后，易服赴傅翁居。见其门阀，知为巨室。请见傅翁，言其事。傅喜，急延入。傅媪细询，知为不误，遂以婿款伊，偕伊母入山探望。伊亦佣人开地，遂成富有。后与居日众，因为牛鬼庄。今户口约五十馀家，而伊姓过半。

    Explanation

    278

    The Protagonist, missing his/her mother, intended to bring her home to care for her but feared the wife might not agree. He/she therefore told the wife about arranging the mother's remarriage and also expressed his/her own intention, pleading with her to reluctantly comply. The wife said, "That's acceptable. But I can treat her as a mother-in-law, but I cannot call her that directly. Because calling her mother-in-law directly, not only might my feelings be forced, but the mother's heart would also feel ashamed. What is the surname of your maternal grandfather's family?" The protagonist replied, "It's Liu." The wife said, "You should pretend to the mother that I only know she is being taken care of by you and call her Liu Mother. I will do my best to serve her, and she will consider her role. Then we can live together peacefully without any complaints. Otherwise, if I try my utmost to serve her and do not meet her wishes, she will surely say that I do not treat her as a mother-in-law, and my heart will always find it hard to justify myself." The protagonist agreed, "Your words are right." As the protagonist was about to leave, the wife said, "At that time, my parents will certainly think I am dead. Please send a message to me." The protagonist promised. After returning and seeing the mother, the protagonist changed clothes and went to the residence of Mr. Fu. Seeing the family status, he/she knew it was a wealthy and influential family. He/she asked to meet Mr. Fu and told him about the matter. Mr. Fu was pleased and hast

    279

    虚白道人曰：牛鬼之报伊，固以伊处之甚厚，然亦为伊能曲尽人子之心也。邑有二孝廉，皆父殁母醮。一中式后，夜赴母后夫门首，三拜稽首而归，盖以母后夫犹存，居诸有赖；一迎养其母于家，盖以母后夫已卒，衣食无出。其迹虽异，其义则同。

    Explanation

    279

    Xubai Taoist said: The ghost rewarded him, indeed treating him very well, yet it was also because he could fully express the heart of a filial son. In a certain town, there were two officials known for their filial piety. Both had fathers who died and mothers who remarried. One, after passing the imperial examination, went to his mother's new husband's house at night, bowed three times and kowtowed, and then returned, because his mother's new husband was still alive and could provide support. The other welcomed and supported his mother at home, because his mother's new husband had died and there was no one to provide for her. Although their actions were different, their principles were the same.

    280

    华 月 娘

    　　闽人童达，美丰标，业儒，应童子试，遐迩知名。自负才貌，欲得丽偶。有邻街富翁华瑞亭女月娘，娟好无双，亦以择配太苛，及笄未字。华偶与友人共杯酒，语及媒事，友曰：“惟有童郎宜附婚姻，岂可以贫富论乎？量渠亦非常贫者。”华可之，言至此而止。月娘闻之，甚喜。盖月外家与童有瓜葛亲，庆吊时曾见之，得婚于童，实随素愿也。他日月娘赴外家，过城隍庙前，又遇童，不觉兴往情来，笑容可掬。童意女与我何若是多情也？旋闻华有婿己之议，始知女笑有因，遂烦至契作冰。念渠既有意，媒之必谐，乃终无成说，自悔齐大非耦，殊为多事。适有道姑募食于街，神宇肃凝，有仙人风度。募及半日，无一施者。童怜之，斋以素食，即请主于其家。濒行，以金钏一支授童曰：“有一事欲告人，恐人无福消受。

    Explanation

    280

    Hua Yue Nian

    A man from Fujian named Tong Da, handsome and well-proportioned, studied Confucian classics and took the examination for young scholars, becoming well-known both far and near. Pleased with his talent and appearance, he wished to find a beautiful wife. There was a beautiful daughter of a wealthy man named Hua Ruiting who lived next door, named Yue Nian, who was exceptionally beautiful and had been too strict in choosing a spouse, remaining unmarried despite reaching the age of marriage. Hua once discussed matchmaking with a friend over a cup of wine, and the friend said, "Only Tong Da would be a suitable match, how can one consider wealth or poverty? He is not extremely poor." Hua agreed, and the conversation ended there. Yue Nian heard this and was very happy. It turned out that Yue Nian's maternal family had a distant relative relationship with Tong Da, and they had met during celebrations and funerals. Marrying Tong Da was actually in line with her original wish. One day, when Yue Nian visited her maternal family, she passed by the city god temple and met Tong Da again, feeling an attraction and smiling warmly. Tong Da wondered why the girl seemed so affectionate towards him. Soon after, he heard that Hua had decided to arrange a marriage for her, realizing that her smile was due to this news. He then asked a close friend to act as a matchmaker. Believing that the girl had shown interest, he thought the match would be successful. However, the match ultimately failed, and he regretted his initial enthusiasm, thinking it was unnecessary trouble. Just then, a female Taoist priest was begging for food on the street, with a solemn and dignified appearance, exuding an air of otherworldliness. She had been begging for half a day without receiving any donations. Tong Da felt compassion for her and offered her vegetarian food, inviting her to stay at his home. On the verge of leaving, she gave a piece of jewelry to Tong Da and said, "I have something to tell you, but I fear you may not be able to bear it."

    281

    执此向南山乳石上三击之，石壁即开，可得仙女为配。”言已辞去。童得钏击石，崖壁大开。入，约行半里，有仙女伏案而书。见童，投笔问：“汝何人？何得到此？”对曰：“吾童达，蒙仙人指点而来。”女正容曰：“吾不论汝童与不童，达与不达，蓄有一物，若汝亦有之，则惟命是从。不然请出，勿生妄想！”遂脱臂上金钏示之。童曰：“此吾家故物，何得无有？”以所得钏相较，对偶天然。女含羞欲起，童偎抱之。拒曰：“何孟浪如此！稍假辞色，遂得步进步耶？”问其家世，曰：“妾仙人之女，母但言妾父刘氏，未闻其详。又以妾与红尘有缘，置于此俟之。屈指其期，知君今日必至。”遂将所书案上字授童，盖其姓名、年庚及来此时、日也。童亦将道姑募缘，临行告语之事向女言之。女凄然曰：“君所见即妾母也。”既而，洞忽黑暗，知日已暮，遂燃烛对饮。饮罢，息烛而寝。由是三五日一至洞府。

    Explanation

    281

    Final Translation

    Hold this and strike the rock at the southern peak three times. The stone wall will immediately open, and you can obtain a fairy as a match." After speaking, he left. The boy obtained the bracelet and struck the rock. The cliff wall opened widely. He entered and walked about half a li, where he saw a fairy sitting at a desk writing. Seeing the boy, she threw down her pen and asked, "Who are you? How did you get here?" The boy replied, "I am Tong Da, guided by a fairy to come here." The fairy said sternly, "I do not care whether you are a boy or not, or whether you are named Da or not. I possess an object, and if you also have it, then I will obey your commands. Otherwise, please leave; do not have wild thoughts!" She then removed the golden bracelet from her arm and showed it to him. The boy said, "This is my family's old object; how could I not have it?" He compared the bracelet he had with hers, and they matched perfectly. The fairy blushed and was about to stand up, but the boy embraced her. She resisted, saying, "How rash are you! With just a little favor, you have already taken steps forward?" She asked about his family background. He said, "I am the daughter of a fairy. My mother only says my father is Liu, but I do not know the details. She also said that I have a karmic connection with the mortal world and placed me here to wait for you. Counting the days, I knew that you would surely arrive today." She then gave the boy the words written on the desk, which were his name, age, and the time and date of his arrival. The boy also told her about the nun's alms and the message she gave him before departure. The fairy sighed and said, "What you saw is my mother." Suddenly, the cave became dark, and they knew it was evening. They lit a candle and drank together. After drinking, they extinguished the candle and slept. From then on, the boy visited the cave every three to five days.

    282

    约半年，童适家居，闻月娘卒，不胜感叹。先是月娘闻家人婚童之议，以为老父欲之，事无不谐，故遇童而笑。嗣闻童求亲未成，自觉失笑于童，不啻失身。嗣闻父将议婚他族，遂自经。童诣洞，言及月娘死，对女悼息。女曰：“与渠有亲乎？”曰：“无之。”“渠艳绝无伦乎？”曰：“然。”女曰：“渠即美丽，与君何涉？天下好女子无穷，若皆闻其死而为之耿耿，将终身无欢畅时矣！”童因备述议婚求亲以及女死之故。女曰：“此贞女也，不宜相负。

    Explanation

    282

    About half a year later, the child, now back home, heard of Moon Lady's death and could not help but feel deeply moved. Previously, when Moon Lady had heard of the family's intention to marry the child, she thought that her elderly father desired it, and that nothing would prevent the match, so she smiled at the child. Later, upon learning that the child's proposal of marriage had not succeeded, she felt embarrassed by having laughed at him, as if she had lost her virtue. Subsequently, when she learned that her father was planning to arrange a marriage with another family, she took her own life. The child visited the cave and spoke of Moon Lady's death, expressing his grief. The girl said, "Do you have any relation to her?" He replied, "No." She then asked, "Was she exceptionally beautiful?" He answered, "Yes." The girl said, "Even if she was beautiful, what does it matter to you? There are countless good women in the world. If you were to grieve over every one who died, you would never have a moment of joy or peace!" The child then recounted the details of the marriage proposal and the girl's death. The girl said, "She was a chaste woman; one should not betray her virtue."

    283

    渠死几日矣？”答以三日。曰：“妾有丹药，服之可苏，但不育耳。”童喜受丹，直诣华室，自言能活月娘。华方痛女，急问活之之术。曰：“活后如何？”华曰：“若果活也，以女妻之。”童急令启其榇，即榇中纳丹入吻。移时，月娘起坐，开目见童。家人告以“汝死已三日，今童郎活之。”赧然曰：“庙遇之后，意终身不得会面，不料复有今日。”其嫂在旁曰：“老父已许亲矣！”月娘闻之，羞红涨面，俯首不语。华请童出，款宾客舍，订桃夭之期而返。

    Explanation

    283

    The boy asked, "How many days has he been dead?" and was told it had been three days. He said, "I have a elixir; if taken, it can revive him, but it will not allow for reproduction." The boy happily accepted the elixir and went directly to the noble's room, claiming he could revive Lady Yue. The noble, grieving for his daughter, urgently asked about the method to revive her. He asked, "What happens after she is revived?" The noble replied, "If indeed she is revived, I will marry my daughter to him." The boy immediately ordered to open her coffin and placed the elixir in her mouth. After a while, Lady Yue sat up, opened her eyes, and saw the boy. The family told her, "You have been dead for three days; now the boy has revived you." She blushed and said, "After meeting at the temple, I thought we would never meet again; I did not expect to meet again today." Her sister-in-law said, "My father has already promised her in marriage!" Lady Yue, upon hearing this, turned red with shame, bowed her head, and remained silent. The noble invited the boy to the guest house, arranged the date for the wedding, and returned.

    284

    童归，往见仙女，面有忧色。女问：“宜喜而忧，何也？”曰：“卿不知人世之所急需者钱耶？仆亦知宁俭勿奢，但俭亦无着，奈何？”女曰：“如君言，君家有银无钱。”童曰：“钱且无之，银于何有？”女曰：“妾未见钱样，若银则致之无难。”信手指曰：“彼其非耶？”手指处果有白镪堆积。童自此每归辄任意取携。及期，女欲来观，童不欲，曰：“华家不知有卿，若见卿，则知仆有前室，口舌从此起矣！”女曰：“妾虽往，不令渠见也。”童许之。

    Explanation

    284

    The young man returned home and went to see the fairy, who had a worried expression on her face. The fairy asked, "Should you be happy but you are worried, why is that?" He replied, "You don't know that money is what the world urgently needs, do you? I also know that it's better to be frugal than extravagant, but even frugality has no means of support, what can I do?" The fairy said, "As you say, your family has silver but no money." The young man said, "We don't even have money, how can we have silver?" The fairy said, "I haven't seen what money looks like, but if it's silver, it's not difficult to obtain." She pointed with her finger and said, "Is that not it?" Where she pointed, there were indeed piles of white silver. From then on, whenever the young man returned home, he could take whatever he wanted. When the time came, the fairy wanted to come and see, but the young man did not want her to. He said, "The Hua family doesn't know about you. If they see you, they will know I have a previous wife, and arguments will start from here!" The fairy said, "I will go, but I won't let them see me." The young man agreed.

    285

    婚礼既毕，女曰：“礼数实繁，纳妾时何草草若彼？”月娘闻声，举首见女，曰：“姊姊为谁？”女曰：“吾刘氏，与妹事一良人。”月娘曰：“若然，嫡礼尚未修。”曰：“勿谓此，吾不与妹争嫡庶也。”童欲为设榻，女曰：“勿庸，君今夕新婚宴尔，妾在此无趣。”遂行。童始将女与药救济之事语月娘。月娘曰：“君胡不早言，岂有活命之恩，觌面不一展谢者？”童曰：“此非小恩，不可谢，当期有以报之已耳。”数日，童重诣洞。女曰：“妾与华妹爱憎由君，期不可愆，请往来以一月为度。”童笑从之。

    Explanation

    285

    The wedding ceremony was completed, and the woman said, "The rituals are indeed numerous, yet the concubine-taking was so hasty!" Moon Lady heard the voice and raised her head to see the woman, asking, "Who are you, sister?" The woman replied, "I am Liu, and I serve a virtuous man with your sister." Moon Lady said, "If that is the case, the proper rites for a wife have not yet been performed." The woman said, "Do not say that. I am not competing with you for the position of wife or concubine." The boy wanted to arrange a place for her, but the woman said, "Do not bother. You are just married and enjoying the festivities tonight, and I find it uninteresting to stay here." She then left. The boy then told Moon Lady about the woman and the matter of being rescued by medicine. Moon Lady said, "Why did you not mention it earlier? How can there be a favor of saving one's life without showing gratitude face-to-face?" The boy said, "This is not a minor favor; it cannot be thanked directly. One should wait for an opportunity to repay it." After several days, the boy visited the cave again. The woman said, "Love and hatred are at your discretion, but the deadline cannot be missed. Please meet once a month." The boy smiled and agreed.

    286

    后月娘果不育，童甚以为忧。女曰：“齐人以一妻一妾而处室者，非仅为养也，兼为裕后计。君有二妻，螽斯之庆，不得于彼，可求于此。君揖妾，妾为君育之。”童立与之揖。女笑曰：“岂有此理！戏君耳，设不如愿，当以再拜还之。”童曰：“还礼云乎哉，果出言不践，即北面稽首而不受。”未及期月，女曰：“妾果震动有娠，然妾能为君生之，不能为君养之，祈早与华妹商量。”见月娘，语以刘氏将生子，月娘曰：“渠生子为渠之子，与妾何与？”曰：“渠欲寄卿膝下。”曰：“若果给妾，即妾之子，复与彼何与？”月娘欣然自任，遂预佣乳母以俟。一夕，童与月娘谈笑未寝，女忽至曰：“妹欲报德，此其时也。”夜阑，产一子，月娘抱去交与乳母，命名葆元。月馀，女始辞归。又数年，童亦仙去。

    Explanation

    286

    Subsequently, Moon Lady indeed did not conceive, and the boy was very worried. The woman said, "People from the state of Qi who have one wife and one concubine do so not only for sustenance but also to ensure the continuation of the family line. You have two wives; if the blessing of many descendants cannot be obtained from one, it can be sought from the other. Bow to me, and I will bear it for you." The boy immediately bowed to her. The woman laughed and said, "How could there be such a thing! I was just joking with you. If it does not go as wished, I will return it with two bows." The boy replied, "Is it about returning the courtesy? If you do not keep your word, I will bow facing north and refuse it." Before the end of the month, the woman said, "I indeed feel the movement of pregnancy, but I can only bear it for you; I cannot raise it for you. Please discuss with Huamei early." She saw Moon Lady and told her that Liu would give birth to a son. Moon Lady said, "His son is his son; what does it have to do with me?" The woman said, "He wants to entrust it to your care." Moon Lady said, "If it is given to me, it is my son; what does it have to do with him?" Moon Lady gladly took on the responsibility and hired a wet nurse in advance to wait. One night, the boy and Moon Lady were talking and laughing without sleeping when the woman suddenly arrived and said, "I want to repay your kindness; this is the time." Late at night, she gave birth to a son, whom Moon Lady took and gave to the wet nurse, naming him Bao Yuan. After a month, the woman finally left. After several years, the boy also ascended to immortality.

    287

    江氏

    　　江氏，章邑窦诚之继室，适诚三祀而诚卒。嫡无出，江亦未育。夫堂弟诗有二子，诗嗜饮赌，子复肖之，以故清贫如洗。氏欲继一为嗣，决渠不能守成，日夜思虑，苦无臧谋。乃市妍婢，佣老媪伴焉，盖欲令诗交婢得子，以承宗祧也。示意婢媪，托故不家。诗闻婢美，乘嫂他出而往观之；见婢心悦，不肯遽去。媪遵江氏教，出酒食留诗，促婢侍饮。婢既怀春，诗复诱之，遂私合焉。江将归而诗始去。嗣江他出，诗辄与婢同寝处。

    Explanation

    287

    Jiang was the second wife of Duan Cheng from Zhang County. After three years of marriage to Cheng, Cheng passed away. No offspring came from the legitimate wife, and Jiang herself had not yet given birth. Her brother-in-law Shi had two sons, who were fond of drinking and gambling, and the sons inherited these traits, leading to their extreme poverty. Jiang wanted to adopt one of them as her heir but decided that he could not maintain the family. She thought about it day and night but found no good solution. She then bought a beautiful maid and hired an old woman to accompany her, intending to make Shi have a child with the maid to continue the family line. She instructed the maid and the old woman to pretend they did not belong to a household. Shi heard that the maid was beautiful and went to see her when Jiang was out. Seeing the maid, he was pleased and did not want to leave immediately. The old woman followed Jiang's instructions, served wine and food to Shi, and urged the maid to serve him. The maid, being in her prime, was lured by Shi, and they secretly consummated their relationship. When Jiang was about to return, Shi finally left. Subsequently, whenever Jiang was away, Shi would sleep with the maid.

    288

    亦有时乘间暗度，谓嫂防察所不及，而不知借种之情由也。及二年，婢有娠。江问诗曰：“婢之娠，叔知之否？”诗言不知。婢与媪共证之，诗不言。江曰：“叔既与婢有私，即合以婢赠叔，嗣听叔与婢往来，食饮与共，若家政则勿劳清神。”诗唯命。未几，婢果生男，名曰幸生。诗亦从此戒博饮，因屡至嫂家，有时代理细事，丝毫不妄费。江知其已改前非，渐以家务相托，诗处之井井有条理。幸生稍长，始殡诚。一切悉听诗经营，而己不与闻焉。

    　　虚白道人曰：智哉江氏，深虑逾男子矣！世之愚夫愚妇，视财如命。蓄得小康产业，过继非人，酗酒滥赌，抛财荡产；刻薄成家者，甚至老受饥寒。岂尽系于命数哉？亦由处之不善耳！

    Explanation

    288

    There are times when one takes advantage of gaps to secretly pass through, thinking that the sister-in-law's watchfulness would not reach, yet unaware of the reason for borrowing seeds. After two years, the maid became pregnant. Jiang asked Shi, "Does the maid's pregnancy mean you know about it?" Shi said he did not know. The maid and the old woman both confirmed it, but Shi remained silent. Jiang said, "Since you have had private dealings with the maid, you should give the maid to you, allowing you and the maid to come and go freely, share meals and drinks together, but do not trouble your mental faculties with household matters." Shi complied. Not long after, the maid gave birth to a boy named Xingsheng. Shi also began to abstain from excessive drinking and often visited the sister-in-law's house, sometimes handling minor household tasks, never wasting a single coin. Jiang realized that Shi had changed his ways and gradually entrusted him with household affairs. Shi managed everything neatly and orderly. When Xingsheng grew up, Jiang's husband passed away. All matters were left to Shi to handle, while Jiang remained detached.

    The Vubai Daoist said: How wise was Jiang! Her foresight surpassed that of ordinary men! In the world, foolish men and women view wealth as their lives. Those who accumulate modest fortunes and adopt unsuitable heirs, drink excessively and gamble recklessly, squander their assets and lose their properties. Even those who accumulate wealth through harsh and stingy means may end up suffering from hunger and cold in old age. Is it all due to fate? It is also due to improper handling.

    289

    大类阳翟贾人作用。为宗祀计，与谋人以利己者，心迹迥殊，是谓德慧术知。 马竹吾

    　　方太史（稿本作“方安仁”）

    　　方太史，字安仁，西蜀人。幼失怙，奉母孝。素明数术，屡推母生平不惟享年不永，且不得其死。有同年友以引见来京，闻伊遇异人，能先知，于是竭诚趋谒，语以所求。友曰：“请先诣萱堂，以观休咎。”公见之。出，复推以术。曰：“信矣！果不善终。”公惕然曰：“仆素谨懔，纵有罪恶，何至累及亲庭？”曰：“孽不由君，君母必有杀生太过之处。”公仰思之，谓：“惟闻母进食先祖母时，食中偶有蚁，因被叱辱，嗣母氏见蚁辄杀之，遇蚁穴，必坐守尽伤乃已。”友曰：“即此故也。宜急劝痛改，并多方市物放生，或可挽回天心。”公由是旦夕几谏，母亦听从。又为请善行，以冀免衍。

    Explanation

    289

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    Final Translation

    In all writing, it is like riding a swift steed — though it may possess a wild and unrestrained spirit, one must control it with bit and bridle, and must not let it run wild, straying from the proper path or indulging in aimless wanderings.

    Final Translation

    From ancient times, wise kings and sage emperors still required diligent study; how much more so, then, for common people! This matter is found throughout the classics and histories. I cannot elaborate further, but I will briefly mention a few essential points of recent times in order to enlighten you.

    Final Translation

    As for writings, their origin lies in the Five Classics: edicts, memorials, and military proclamations arise from the Shujing (Book of Documents); expository and argumentative writings arise from the Yijing (Book of Changes); songs, odes, and praises arise from the Shijing (Book of Poetry); ritual sacrifices, mourning, and elegies arise from the Liji (Book of Rites); memorials, admonitions, and inscriptions arise from the Chunqiu (Spring and Autumn Annals). Court regulations, military oaths and decrees, the proclamation of benevolence and righteousness, the elucidation of virtue and merit, governing the people and establishing the state—all are indispensable. As for cultivating the mind and spirit, offering gentle admonition in a relaxed manner, and savoring the flavor of such writings, these too are pleasant pursuits. When one has spare energy, then one may study them.

    Final Translation

    The practices of merchants from Yangzhai are similar. Acting for the sake of ancestral worship, those who plot with others for personal gain have motives and actions that are vastly different. This is called the virtue of wisdom and knowledge. Fang, the historian, courtesy name Anren, from the western part of Sichuan, lost his father at a young age and was filial to his mother. He has always been skilled in divination and numerology, and often predicted his mother's life events, not only that she would not live long but also that she would not die a natural death. A friend from the same examination class introduced him to the capital, where he heard that the friend had met a sage who could foresee the future. Thus, Fang went to see the friend with utmost sincerity and told him what he sought. The friend said, "Please first visit your mother, to observe the signs of good or bad fortune." Fang did so. Upon returning, the friend again used divination. He said, "Indeed! She will not die a natural death." Fang was alarmed and said, "I have always been cautious and respectful. Even if there are sins or wrongdoings, how could it involve the family?" The friend replied, "The sin is not yours. Your mother must have gone too far in killing living things." Fang thought about it and said, "I only heard that when my mother served food to my grandmother, there were sometimes ants in the food, and she was scolded and humiliated. Afterward, my mother would kill ants whenever she saw them and would sit and watch until all were injured before leaving." The friend said, "This is the reason. You should urgently advise her to deeply repent and buy living things to release them in many ways. Perhaps it can win back the heart of Heaven." Fang thus admonished his mother day and night, and she complied. He also performed good deeds, hoping to avoid calamity.

    290

    三年，奉特旨除温州知府，公奉母赴任。将抵温界，宿于逆旅，闻比店亦住一官云赴温州任者，疑之。投刺拜谒，既相见，询之。曰：“与君同职守，但有幽明之殊耳。”公知为郡城隍，肃然曰：“仆有一事，敢质神明。星家咸言母氏不得令终，信乎否也？”渠乃披册指示之，某年月日，雷击于府署。公毛骨寒栗，出涕哀求救援，谓：“父母百年，人子虽痛心泣血，尚可苟活。若遭天谴，何忍目睹？乞怜苦衷！”神不应，曰：“无已，止有一术。

    Explanation

    290

    After three years, the official was appointed governor of Wenzhou by imperial decree, and he accompanied his mother to take up his post. As they neared the borders of Wenzhou, they stayed at an inn. He heard that another official, who claimed to be going to Wenzhou, was also staying at the inn, which made him doubt this. He submitted his visiting card and paid respects. After meeting, he asked the other official. The official replied, "We have the same duties, but there is a difference between the visible and the invisible." The official then realized that he was the city deity. With respect, he said, "I have one matter to present to the divine. Astrologers all say that my mother will not have a good end. Is this true?" The deity then opened a book and pointed to a specific date, indicating that his mother would be struck by lightning at the government office. The official's hair stood on end with fear, and he shed tears, pleading for rescue. He said, "If my parents reach a hundred years old, even though I am deeply grieved and shedding tears, I can still barely survive. But if they suffer divine punishment, how can I bear to see it? I beg for mercy in my sincere feelings!" The deity did not respond and said, "If nothing else, there is only one method."

    291

    君到任之后，一切饮馔力求盛设，劝母遍尝，若是者日六七次；至于衣服、衾帐，用二三日即为更易。衣食禄尽，必早死十日，可免生前之惨。若恐殁后罹劫，则于旷野中掘坎一丈，将梓葬于穴底，以土覆之，尺许即止。再用木榇一具，内束草为人，书化者年命于其上，拥土使与地平，勿封勿树。别无他术矣，此即万分之幸！”公至署，悉如神言，供奉数月后，母果无病卒。公悲喜交集，急为营葬。殡后十日，浓云四合，迅雷震地，大骇。雨止，奔赴母墓视之，伪榇碎焉，草人亦焚墓侧，而尸免矣。俄闻城隍遭雷劫，公惊往视，即鸠工新其庙，塑所见而像之。庙成，备香楮牲牢，叩答神庥。乃得舆榇归里，合葬于亡父之阡。

    Explanation

    291

    After taking office, the official strove to arrange lavish banquets, encouraging his mother to taste everything, doing so six or seven times a day; as for clothes and bedding, he changed them every two or three days. When his food, clothing, and salary were exhausted, he must die ten days earlier, to avoid the suffering before death. If he feared that after death he would suffer calamity, he should dig a pit one zhang deep in the wilderness, bury the coffin at the bottom of the pit, cover it with soil, stopping at one chi. He should then use another wooden coffin, stuff it with straw to make a human form, write the name and age of the deceased on it, pile earth over it to level with the ground, and not seal or plant trees. There is no other method, this is a great fortune!” The official arrived at the office and followed the god's words exactly, serving for several months after which his mother indeed died without illness. The official was filled with both grief and joy, and quickly prepared the burial. Ten days after the burial, thick clouds gathered, and thunder shook the ground, greatly frightening him. When the rain stopped, he rushed to his mother's grave to see, and found the fake coffin shattered, the straw man burned beside the grave, but the body was spared. Soon after, he heard that the city god was struck by lightning, and the official was shocked and went to see, immediately gathering workers to rebuild the temple, having statues made of what he had seen. When the temple was completed, he prepared incense, paper money, and sacrificial offerings, paying homage and thanking the god for his blessing. He then was able to bring the coffin home and bury it together with his father in the ancestral tomb.

    292

    虚白道人曰：改过自新，而天仍罪之者，为其所为之善不敌其不善也。罪之而终宥之者，为其悔祸可嘉，兼有孝子为之感格也。岂真穴深丈馀，遂为龙所不知耶？余未之信也。

    　　李智

    　　邑有穷民李智，与同村张义缔生死交，昼则向街头谋食，夜则同止一室，友恭之谊，同胞不啻也。有棍徒王某利之，欲以义、智为爪牙，而义、智不从。王忿侮之，因二人同心，不至已甚。未几，王卒，而义忽病，昏迷中语“王某鞭我”。智视义，果遍体青紫。智虽痛恨，幽明殊途，无可如何。三日而义亦卒，智哀哭营葬之。夜梦义曰：“吾弟害我矣！葬我处违王某坟不远，渠不惟役我若仆，少不如意，辄痛杖我。且曰：‘今止汝一人，诟辱杀残由吾也！’”智醒，大怒。早诣义墓，叩祝起，自经于树。乡人哀其志，葬智义墓侧。至夜，邻人遥闻三更后有喧呼訇击之声。天明，王某尸骸悉掘扬于外。王家人掩之，至夜复然。王乃移厝他处。

    Explanation

    292

    The hermit named Xubai said: "Even if one repents and reforms oneself, if heaven still punishes him, it is because the good he does does not outweigh the bad. If one is punished but ultimately forgiven, it is because his repentance is commendable, and there are filial sons who move heaven on his behalf. Could it be that the grave is so deep that the dragon does not know about it? I do not believe it."

    In a village, there was a poor man named Li Zhi, who formed a bond of life and death with Zhang Yi from the same village. During the day, he went to the streets to earn a living, and at night, they stayed together in one room. Their friendship was as close as brothers. There was a ruffian named Wang who took advantage of them, wanting to use Yi and Zhi as his henchmen, but Yi and Zhi refused. Wang became angry and insulted them, but because the two were united, the insults did not go too far. Not long after, Wang died, and Yi suddenly fell ill, muttering in his delirium, "Wang beat me." Zhi looked at Yi and saw that his body was all bruised and purple. Although Zhi was filled with anger, he knew that the living and the dead had different paths, and there was nothing he could do. Three days later, Yi also died, and Zhi mourned and arranged for Yi's burial. At night, Zhi dreamed that Yi said, "My brother has harmed me! The place where I am buried is not far from Wang's grave. He not only treated me like a servant, but whenever I displeased him, he beat me severely. And he would say, 'Now that you are the only one left, I can insult, humiliate, and kill you as I please!'" Zhi woke up, greatly angered, and early in the morning, he went to Yi's grave, made vows, and hanged himself from a tree. The villagers, moved by his loyalty, buried Zhi beside Yi's grave. At night, neighbors heard loud shouting and banging sounds after midnight. At dawn, Wang's body was dug up and thrown out. Wang's family tried to cover it up, but at night, it happened again. Wang's body was then moved to another place.

    293

    虚白道人曰：自经以济死友之难，闻者咸以轻生非笑之。夫非笑诚是也。然患难不相恤，五伦中何需朋友矣？若李智者，其事虽不足为训，而足以敦薄俗焉。

    　　此事可与羊、左之传并传。 王萱堂

    　　人狐换亲（稿本作“许怀芳”）

    Explanation

    293

    The person of the empty white said: To sacrifice oneself to save a dying friend, those who hear speak of it as frivolous and foolish. Indeed, such criticism is correct. But if friends do not help each other in times of hardship, how can one need friends in the five relationships? As for Li Zhizhe, although his actions are not worthy of emulation, they are sufficient to improve shallow customs. This matter can be compared with the legendary friendship of Yang Zhi and Zuo Qiuming. Wang Xuantang.

    Notes on Uncertainty

    • 虚白道人 - The exact identity of this figure is unclear, so it is left as "the person of the empty white" to preserve the ambiguity.
    • 李智者 - The exact identity of this figure is unclear, so it is left as "Li Zhizhe" to preserve the ambiguity.
    • 羊、左之传 - The exact details of this legendary friendship are not provided in the source text, so it is left as "the legendary friendship of Yang Zhi and Zuo Qiuming" to preserve the cultural reference.
    294

    狐子吕昌，聘汉口绅士许公女怀芳为妻，许固不知其为狐也。亲迎后，公子崇德往迓其姊，见一狐卧门外，呼之起，乃吕家阍人也。归言于父，问女，始知其全家皆狐。父大惭悔，矢不与往还。吕有女玉兰甚美，许公子探姊时爱而私焉。怀芳知之，归宁极道吕女之贤，劝与弟论婚，因泄其事与母。母许，而父决言不可，以告玉兰。玉兰笑曰：“终有欲时，彼时无谓妾心狠也。”

    　　无何，崇德与同学生尤某口角致忿，以砚误击死之。许惴恐无策，怀芳曰：“女小姑玉兰能解此难。”许公曰：“果能，即令汝弟妻之。”怀芳与弟急至吕家，见玉兰语之故，且曰：“特与贤妹道喜！”兰急书符令崇德于闺门外焚之，始曰：“妹有何喜？”芳曰：“吾弟深慕德容，愿结婚姻耳。”兰冷笑曰：“多难之家，君子不过其门。今许氏有不测之祸，渠不日偿人命，使妹守望门寡耶？”芳泣曰：“不惟冀令德来教，兼祈施仙术救弟性命。

    Explanation

    294

    Foxy Prince Lü Chang betrothed himself to Miss Huai Fang, the daughter of a scholar-official named Xu from Hankou. Xu, however, was unaware that she was a fox spirit. After the wedding, Xu's son, Gongzi Chongde, went to greet his sister-in-law. He saw a fox lying outside the door, called it up, and discovered it was a servant of the Lü family. Upon returning home, Chongde informed his father, who then asked his daughter about the matter. Only then did he learn that the entire Lü family were fox spirits. The father was greatly ashamed and regretful, vowing never to maintain relations with them.

    Lü had a beautiful daughter named Yulan. During a visit to his sister, Xu's son Chongde fell in love with her and began an affair. When Huai Fang found out, she praised Yulan's virtues upon returning home and suggested that her brother marry her, revealing this to her mother. The mother agreed, but the father firmly refused and told Yulan about it. Yulan laughed and said, "There will be a time when the desire comes, and at that time, don't call me cruel."

    Not long after, Chongde got into a quarrel with a fellow student named You, who was accidentally killed with a stone. Xu was filled with fear and had no idea what to do. Huai Fang said, "My younger sister-in-law, Yulan, can solve this problem." Xu said, "If she can, then let my brother marry her." Huai Fang and her brother hurried to the Lü family, where they met Yulan and told her the situation. They also said, "We especially want to congratulate you, dear sister!" Yulan quickly wrote a talisman and instructed Chongde to burn it outside the inner chamber. Only then did she ask, "What is the good news, sister?" Huai Fang replied, "My brother deeply admires your virtue and wishes to marry you." Yulan coldly laughed and said, "A family with many difficulties, a gentleman does not pass by their door. Now the Xu family has an unexpected disaster, and he will soon pay for a life. Will you make me remain unmarried and watch over the door?" Huai Fang wept and said, "I hope not only for your guidance but also for you to use your magical skills to save my brother's life."

    295

    ”兰曰：“妹实能之，但许家以妹不齿于人，恐将……恐将惧置予于怀；将安将乐，弃予如遗也。”曰：“勿念前衍。弟亡，妹亦有不利焉！”曰：“何不利之有？柏舟自矢，省却无数烦恼矣！”德不得已，跪哀之曰：“不肖郎君自作孽，跪死于此，犹胜死于法场。”多时，终不应。德言膝痛难支，芳笑曰：“弟跪妹亦非一次，何今始知痛也？”兰亦笑曰：“此何时，尚为此谐语耶？大嫂与郎君来时，妹急焚符者，盖恐尤魂离体，谅此时已苏。然两月后尤仍死，付以丹药，可迟年馀。”德曰：“奈何？”曰：“死生有命，止可令渠不取抵于君已耳。”德喜，于是娶玉兰，而婚好倍笃焉。

    Explanation

    295

    Yulan said, "My younger sister is indeed capable of it, but the Xu family disdains her. I fear they will harbor resentment towards me. They will either be content or happy, and discard me like a forgotten object." Said, "Do not dwell on past mistakes. If the brother dies, the sister will also be at a disadvantage!" Said, "What disadvantage is there? Swear on the Bo Zhōu, save from countless troubles." Yulan was unwilling, and knelt down to beg, saying, "The unworthy son has made his own mistakes, kneeling here to die is better than dying on the execution ground." For a long time, she still did not respond. Yulan said, "When the elder sister and the young master come, the younger sister hurriedly burned the talisman, because I feared that the spirit of the deceased would leave the body, I believe he has already revived. But two months later, he will still die, give him the elixir, and it can delay his death by a few years." Said, "What about it?" Said, "Life and death are predestined; I can only ensure that he does not face retribution from you." Yulan was pleased, and thus married Yulan, and their marriage became even more harmonious.

    296

    伍 丽 娘

    　　明季卜鸿，字小丰，楚人也。性好读而质甚鲁。一日午后，信步游东皋，见遥峰缺处，日隐半规，遂宛然独笑曰：“佳境哉！今何兴致如此？”忽对面来一油壁香车，意车中当是美人。及近视之，乃中旬媪也。媪曰：“君非小郎也？”曰：“然。”曰：“特诣府敬迓，遇诸途，往返少驰十馀里。兹有一事，请升车至舍下商之。”卜迟疑不决。媪曰：“勿疑。所商之事，实君求之而不可必得者也。”卜从之。升车后，车行如飞，少顷至。延入，见有丽女在室，亦不走避。就坐，请其姓氏。媪曰：“言之勿骇，吾实狐。若论戚属，吾郎君之表伯母也。吾事君表伯伍公十馀年，生小女丽娘。不期彼谬听术士之言，将老身拘入坛内，以细纸蒙固，书符其上。君时年方七岁，以物击纸破，老身得脱其难，携女遁去。小 女 与 郎 君 同 庚，此 事 应 亦 记 忆。

    Explanation

    296

    Wu Li Nian

    During the late Ming Dynasty, there was a man named Bu Hong, whose courtesy name was Xiaofeng, and he was from Chu. He had a fondness for reading but was quite simple-minded. One afternoon, he strolled leisurely to the eastern hill, where he saw a gap in the distant mountains and the sun partially obscured behind it. He then laughed alone, saying, "What a beautiful scene! Why am I so in high spirits today?" Suddenly, a fragrant carriage with painted walls approached from the opposite direction. He thought that there must be a beautiful woman inside. Upon closer inspection, however, it turned out to be a middle-aged woman. The woman said, "Are you not the young master?" Bu replied, "Yes." The woman continued, "I have specially come to your mansion to respectfully greet you, and we met on the road. The round trip is slightly more than ten miles. There is a matter I wish to discuss with you, so please get into the carriage and come to my residence." Bu hesitated. The woman said, "Do not doubt. The matter we will discuss is something you have been seeking but cannot necessarily obtain." Bu agreed. After getting into the carriage, it sped off like a flying vehicle, and soon they arrived. She invited him inside, where he saw a beautiful young lady in the room, who did not shy away. They sat down, and Bu asked her surname. The woman said, "Do not be startled, I am actually a fox. If we consider family ties, I am the paternal aunt of your cousin, Wu Gong. I served your cousin Wu Gong for over ten years and bore his daughter, Li Nian. Unbeknownst to us, he mistakenly listened to a fortune-teller and confined me in a ritual circle, covering me with fine paper and inscribing a talisman on it. When you were seven years old, you broke the paper with an object, allowing me to escape the danger and take my daughter away. Your daughter and your cousin are of the same age, and you should also remember this event."

    297

    ”卜 谛 视 女，曰：“是，是，吾犹记表妹笑，两腮有小窝。”及晚，媪使女与同寝处，遂相欢洽。旦日，卜仍读如故。女听其读《乡党篇》，曰：“此真所谓句读犹未谙者！‘伤人乎不’是一句，‘虽疏食菜羹瓜’是一句。圣人仁民爱物，岂有不问马之理？每食必祭，故祭必齐如也。君守高头讲章，想皇、邢诸疏悉未之见耳。且君年已二十有奇，犹镇日朗诵四书白文，将以半部《论语》治太平乎？”卜谢之曰：“卿焉学？何深通理解如此？”女曰：“通不能深，已应试备博士弟子员矣！”卜惊问故。曰：“妾男装师事某孝廉十有一年，不惟掇芹，行将攀桂，君可中场外之举人。”卜益滋疑。曰：“何疑？妾之名，实冒君之字，曾不闻蜚声黉序者有卜小丰耶？”卜始知媪以女妻己，盖有夙志。于是卜不复读，日与丽娘谈宴，渐磨既久，豁然开朗。比玩味佳人妙品，由浅入深，亦渐渐知所造端，觉手疲口沫不啻也，帖括之业因而移情枕上。

    Explanation

    297

    Zhu carefully examined the woman and said, "Yes, yes, I still remember my cousin laughing, with dimples on her cheeks." By evening, the old woman made the woman share the same sleeping quarters with him, and they became very harmonious. The next day, Zhu continued reading as usual. The woman listened to him reading the "Xiangdang" chapter of the Analects and said, "This is truly what is meant by not understanding the punctuation! 'Shangren hu bu' is one sentence, 'Su shi caigeng gua' is another. A sage loves the people and all things; how could he neglect even a horse? One must always sacrifice before eating, so the sacrifice must be solemn. You adhere to high-level lectures, but I suspect you have not seen the commentaries by Wang Yangming and Xing Yun. Moreover, you are already twenty plus a little, yet you spend the whole day reciting the plain text of the Four Books. Do you think you can govern the world with just half of the Analects?" Zhu replied, "Where did you learn? Why do you understand so deeply?" The woman said, "I don't understand deeply, but I have already passed the exams to become a doctoral candidate!" Zhu asked in surprise, "Why?" She said, "I dressed as a man and served as a student to a certain scholar-official for eleven years, not only passing the first level but also about to pass the second level. You can be a juren (a successful candidate in the imperial examinations)." Zhu became more suspicious. She said, "Why be suspicious? My name is actually using your courtesy name. Have you ever heard of anyone named Zhu Xiaofeng being famous in the school?" Zhu realized that the old woman had arranged for this woman to marry him, as it was her long-standing plan. Thus, Zhu stopped reading and spent days talking and feasting with Li Niang. After a long time, he suddenly understood. When he appreciated the beauty of the woman, from superficial to profound, he gradually understood the origins, and his hands were tired and his mouth dry. His focus on studying shifted to the bed.

    298

    卜在女家住及半月，女欲与同归。卜曰：“嫡妻盖氏悍妒，奈何？”女曰：“妾不惧。渠若不妒，犹占便宜。”卜归，直告盖。及数夕，盖不令卜宿女室。女曰：“醋态露矣！”卜欲走告伍公，女曰：“不可，家务将兴，未可以戚我老父。”女将卧榻徙于盖室，卜止之，不听。至晚，女牵卜与同榻，盖如迷茫，不与争执。卜疑而问女。曰：“妾以小术箝制之耳。”盖由是畏女，后渐敬女如宾客。女亦不自当夕，卜愈贤之。

    Explanation

    298

    卜 stayed at the girl's house for about half a month, and the girl wanted to return home together. He said, "My legal wife, Lady Gai, is fierce and jealous, what should we do?" The girl replied, "I am not afraid. If he is not jealous, it still benefits me." Upon returning, he directly told Lady Gai. After several nights, Lady Gai did not allow him to stay in the girl's room. The girl said, "Her jealousy is revealed!" He wanted to go tell Elder Wu, but the girl said, "No, family affairs will arise, it would sadden my old father." The girl moved her bed into Lady Gai's room, and he tried to stop her, but she did not listen. By evening, the girl led him to share her bed, and Lady Gai seemed confused and did not argue. He doubted and asked the girl. She said, "I used a small trick to control her." Lady Gai then feared the girl and gradually treated her with respect, as if she were a guest. The girl also did not consider herself the main wife, and he admired her even more.

    299

    游戏三昧，巧不伤雅，较《聊斋》“黾翁头上”之讥，更觉敦厚。 马竹吾

    　　谷 一 穗

    　　丽江李际云，家素封。生三子，各为娶妻。秋收时，李自南亩归，手执谷三穗分给三媳，曰：“各好收存，来春将以为种，与汝等积私房。”及布谷催耕，问遗穗，惟少媳张氏存之，长与次皆掷弃矣。谓少者曰：“一穗之微，汝何珍重若是？”对曰：“一丝一粟皆天物，岂容暴殄？况老父之赐，何敢轻视！”公甚喜，遂以一穗作种，次年约获谷一升；又以一升作种，约得谷二石；更以二石作种，则满车满篝，所得不计其数矣。六七年间，千顷之地不足种所得之谷。公谓张氏曰：“今岁地中所收，皆一穗谷之子，利可自取。”张敬听之。二嫂面有惭色，张请与两嫂三分焉。公喜曰：“吾家守成有人矣！”乃将家务悉交幼子管理。

    Explanation

    299

    In the realm of writing, one achieves a state of profound absorption, skillful without harming elegance, even more so than the criticism of Min Gong in Liaozhai Zhiyi, which seems more sincere and honest. Ma Zhugu

    A Single Ear of Grain

    Li Jiyun of Lijiang, a wealthy man, had three sons, each of whom he married off. During the autumn harvest, Li returned from his fields in the south, holding three ears of grain to give to his three daughters-in-law. He said, "Each of you should keep them well, and next spring we will use them as seeds to accumulate private savings." When the cuckoo called for plowing, he asked about the leftover ears of grain. Only the youngest daughter-in-law, Zhang, kept hers, while the eldest and middle ones had discarded theirs. He asked the youngest, "How could you value such a small ear of grain so highly?" She replied, "Every thread and grain is a gift from heaven; how could we squander it? Besides, it is a gift from my father, how dare I treat it lightly!" Li was very pleased and used one ear of grain as seed. The following year, he harvested about a sheng of grain; using that sheng as seed, he harvested about two dan; using two dan as seed, he harvested so much grain that it filled carts and baskets, beyond count. Within six or seven years, a thousand acres of land were insufficient to plant the grain they had harvested. Li told Zhang, "This year's harvest from the land is all the offspring of that single ear of grain; the profit can be taken by us." Zhang listened respectfully. The other two sisters-in-law looked ashamed, and Zhang suggested dividing the grain equally among the three of them. Li was delighted and said, "Our family has someone capable of maintaining and preserving it!" He then handed over all household affairs to the youngest son to manage.

    300

    益智录卷之五

    　　翠玉

    　　东省贡院，沿传大场后，群狐侨寓其中，且指某某常见怪异以实之。郡生某，深以为妄。一日晚经其处，至云路牌坊，忽忆人言，决意入探以释众惑。过龙门，号舍皆漆黑，风吹草动，毛发森竖。届至公堂，伫立良久，更无少异，窃笑传言之讹。忽闻堂后有诵读声，遂摩娑复进。将至监临住所，见灯火射窗，读声清朗。窃听之，所读乃戚价“人臣事君以忠”制艺文，字句真着，声音宏亮，令人闻而忘倦。听之久，始入，曰：“先生勤读哉？”其人见生，离居逊坐。

    Explanation

    300

    Translation

    Wisdom Records Volume Five

    Jadeite

    In the eastern province's examination hall, it was said that a group of foxes had temporarily resided there, and specific individuals often witnessed strange phenomena to verify this. A county student found this deeply unbelievable. One evening, as he passed by the place, reaching the Cloud Road Archway, he suddenly remembered what people had said and decided to investigate to dispel everyone's doubts. Passing through the Dragon Gate, the examination rooms were all pitch black, and the slightest movement of the wind caused his hair to stand on end. Upon reaching the main hall, he stood there for a long time, and nothing unusual occurred, making him secretly laugh at the absurdity of the rumors. Suddenly, he heard a recitation coming from behind the hall, so he smoothed his clothes and stepped forward again. As he approached the official's quarters, he saw the light shining through the windows, and the reading voice was clear and loud. Listening in, he realized that what was being read was a zhiyiwen (eight-legged essay) by Qi Jia on the theme of "a minister serving his lord with loyalty." The words were well-written, and the voice was powerful, making it impossible to get tired of listening. After listening for a while, he entered and said, "Sir, you are diligent in your studies!" The official, upon seeing him, moved aside and offered a seat with humility.

    301

    生询姓氏，对言姓许名寅，纳监而事举业者。竹钅卢汤沸，以茶当酒，略与倾谈，心胸顿豁，生甚爱之，曰：“深院无偶，得勿岑寂乎？”许曰：“寒舍狭隘，下帷无地，故假此耳。”复笑曰：“人言此中多狐，仆居此月馀，毫无动静，可知讹传之不足凭信。”生曰：“仆亦为此来。闻君言，而仆疑释，将为播扬以释众疑。”言已，各大笑。生曰：“仆欲与君伴读，未知君意岂敢相强。”许曰：“固所愿也。但读于此，饔飧自尸，实属不堪，吾亦将还读于家。”移时，生告辞。许送至至公堂而回。嗣生辄遇许于街市，遇则点首而已。

    Explanation

    301

    The guest asked for the person's name, and the host replied, "My name is Xu Yin, and I am preparing for the imperial exams." The bamboo stove boiled water, and tea was served as wine. They had a brief conversation, and the guest's heart and mind suddenly opened up. The guest greatly admired the host and said, "With a deep courtyard and no partner, are you not lonely?" Xu Yin replied, "My humble and cramped dwelling has no place to hang a curtain for study, so I am just borrowing this place." He then smiled and said, "People say there are many foxes here, but I have lived here for more than a month and have seen no activity. This shows that false rumors cannot be relied upon." The guest said, "I also came for this reason. Hearing your words, my doubts were dispelled, and I will spread this to dispel everyone's doubts." After speaking, they both laughed heartily. The guest said, "I would like to accompany you in your studies. Without knowing your intention, how dare I force you?" Xu Yin replied, "It is indeed what I wish. But to study here, one must prepare their own meals, which is quite inconvenient. I will also return to study at home." After some time, the guest took his leave. Xu Yin accompanied him to the public hall and then returned. Subsequently, the guest would often meet Xu Yin in the market, and they would simply nod in acknowledgment.

    302

    一日，生同友人乘舟赴历下亭会饮。饮次，友人谈及贡院多狐，生力白其诬，因历述入探遇许之事，众未深信。未几，见一人乘船来。生视之，乃许寅也，曰：“此即去岁寓读贡院之许先生。”友始信之。许曰：“敬诣尊府，闻君泛舟明湖，故访问至此。”生问其来意，曰：“敬求刀圭耳。”生曰：“诚有之，但吾药非百病皆理也。欲以药医何症？”许曰：“妹天癸失调，业已三月，体热如烙，夜不能寐。”生曰：“仆药专治之，但须亲见颜色，细诊脉理，始可调引理治。”许请与生同往，生曰：“兹已半酣，明晨趋赴可也。”因询乔迁，一友曰：“此仆客也。税居寒舍闲院已两月。”生曰：“尊寓既悉，勿烦更辱命矣。”许复丁宁数语而去。盖生不业医，祖传此方，施送不索贽。

    Explanation

    302

    One day, the scholar and his friends went by boat to the Lixia Pavilion for a gathering and drinking. During the drinking session, one of the friends mentioned that the imperial examination hall was haunted by foxes. The scholar strongly denied this claim and recounted in detail his experience of entering and meeting Xu Yin, but the others did not fully believe him. Shortly after, they saw one person coming by boat. The scholar looked and saw it was Xu Yin, who said, "This is Xu, the gentleman who stayed and studied at the imperial examination hall last year." The friends then believed him. Xu said, "I respectfully came to your residence and heard that you were boating on the Bright Lake, so I came to inquire." The scholar asked about his purpose, and Xu replied, "I respectfully seek a small amount of medicine." The scholar said, "If it truly exists, but my medicine does not cure all diseases. What disease do you wish to treat with the medicine?" Xu explained, "My sister has irregular menstruation, already for three months, her body is hot like a branding iron, and she cannot sleep at night." The scholar said, "My medicine specializes in treating it, but I must personally see her condition, carefully diagnose her pulse, before I can properly treat her." Xu asked to go with the scholar, and the scholar said, "We are already half-drunk, we can go tomorrow morning." They then inquired about a change of residence, and one friend said, "This is a guest of mine. He rented a quiet courtyard in my humble home for two months." The scholar said, "I already know your residence, do not trouble yourself further." Xu repeated his request a few times and then left. Because the scholar does not practice medicine, this prescription was passed down from his ancestors, and he gives it away without asking for payment.

    303

    次日，生易服往。许导入内室。少坐，一婢扶二八女郎自内间出。睨之，大惊，心计曰：“此非群玉山头、瑶台月下，何得有此奇逢？”故假诊视，迟延多时，始留药录引而出。至家，与妻言之，妻曰：“此药专医妇女，如见妇女之美，而念想綦切，是先人借此以积善，至君而为丧德举也。”生深然之，而于心终不忘。后月馀，思欲一见颜色，少慰渴想。以许税居友人第，遂托故访友。冀因友善许，徐图如愿。友人言：许迁徙他所已数日。生大失所望，悒悒而归，以为所愿终身难遂矣。

    Explanation

    303

    The next day, the man changed his clothes and went. Xu led him into the inner chamber. After sitting for a short while, a maid supported a sixteen-year-old girl who emerged from the inner room. He glanced at her, greatly surprised, and thought to himself, "This is not on the top of the Jade Mountain or under the moonlit Jade Terrace, how could there be such a rare encounter?" So he feigned a diagnosis, delaying for a long time, and finally left with a prescription, escorted out. Upon returning home, he told his wife about it. His wife said, "This medicine is specifically for women. If one sees the beauty of women and has very strong thoughts about them, it is how the ancestors used it to accumulate virtue, but now it is a shameful act for you." The man deeply agreed with her, but in his heart, he could not forget. After a month or so, he wanted to see her appearance once more, to slightly alleviate his longing. Because Xu rented a residence from a friend, he made an excuse to visit a friend, hoping to befriend Xu through this and gradually plan to fulfill his wish. The friend said that Xu had moved to another place for several days. The man was greatly disappointed, returned home in a depressed state, thinking that his wish would never be fulfilled in his lifetime.

    304

    有执友官闽，寄函招之，生治任往。适值友公出未回，旅居以俟之。为客身闲，乘马郊游。路经一庄，有恶官之幼子，同乳妇嬉戏道侧。马惊，践幼子死。妇牵马辔不令行。生以鞭击之，重伤头颅，妇亦仆地卒。生大惧，策马急驰。出庄里许，回顾有二人乘怒马追之，势将迫近。生惶惧，遗策于地，马行益缓，造次颠沛，神昏意乱，忽前一人磬控而来，视之，乃许寅也，遂急呼曰：“许兄救我！许兄救我！”许回马与生并辔纡道而驰。生虞仇人穷追，止违两箭远，不知渠何故未见，仍由大路驰去也。生与许同驱千馀里，将至一庄，令生放马于野，生甚惜之。许曰：“君命且难保，尚惜代步耶？”释马同行入村。至一大门，许系乘引入。生乍脱大难，方寸忐忑，莫知所为，移时始起身展谢。

    Explanation

    304

    A close friend of mine was serving as an official in Fujian and sent me a letter inviting me to come. I prepared my belongings and went. Upon arrival, I found that my friend's official was out and had not returned, so I stayed as a guest and waited for him. Being a guest, I had free time and decided to ride a horse for a ride in the countryside. As I passed by a village, I saw the young son of an evil official playing with a wet nurse by the roadside. The horse was startled and trampled the young son, causing him to die. The woman grabbed the reins and would not let the horse go. In a panic, I struck her with a whip, seriously injuring her head, and she also fell to the ground and died. I was greatly frightened and whipped the horse, racing away. After leaving the village about a mile, I looked back and saw two people riding angry horses chasing me, and the situation was about to get close. I was terrified and dropped the reins on the ground, causing the horse to move slower. In a hurry and in distress, my mind was confused and thoughts were disordered. Suddenly, a person in front reined in and came. I looked at it and saw it was Xu Yin. I then shouted urgently, "Brother Xu, save me! Brother Xu, save me!" Xu turned the horse and raced side by side with me, taking a detour. I feared that the enemies were pursuing relentlessly, but they were only two arrow lengths away. I did not know why they had not seen us, and they still raced away along the main road. I rode together with Xu for more than a thousand miles and were about to reach a village. Xu ordered me to let the horse loose in the wild, and I very regretted it. Xu said, "Your life is still in danger, so why be attached to your horse?" He released the horse and we walked into the village together. We arrived at a large gate, and Xu tied his horse and led me inside. I had just escaped a great danger and was in a state of anxiety, not knowing what to do. After a while, I finally stood up and expressed my gratitude.

    305

    许曰：“仆能济一时之厄，实无万全之策。”生闻言大怖，泣求施救。许曰：“有一人能多方保护，但渠秉性冷落，恐君壮年盛气，不能忍耐。”生曰：“若得绝处逢生，即使唾骂，甘受不辞。不知其人为谁？”许曰：“即君施药医愈舍妹翠玉也。”生闻之，不禁反悲为喜，急请拜见。许遂引生入后院。至危楼下，大声呼曰：“阿妹，客来矣，可速出迓。”即闻一女子娇声笑，言曰：“霞妹来乎？”出见生，含羞急退，旁坐不语。生揖之，亦傲不还礼。许曰：“某兄逃难到此，祈吾妹怜拯之。”言已欲去，翠玉牵之曰：“小妹非陪客之人。置客于此而去，兄何大事糊涂也？”许曰：“某兄之难，兄实不能为力。妹肯济之，则援而止；不肯，则遣之去。自为斟酌，兄不与闻也。”拂袖而去。

    Explanation

    305

    Xu said, "I can help in a moment of crisis, but I have no foolproof plan." The man heard this and was greatly frightened, weeping and pleading for assistance. Xu replied, "There is one person who can offer much protection, but she has a cold nature and I fear your youthful arrogance may not be able to endure her." The man said, "If I can find life in a hopeless situation, I will willingly accept even abuse. Who is this person?" Xu said, "It is your younger sister Cuiyu, whom you treated with medicine and healed." Upon hearing this, the man could not help but turn from sadness to joy and urgently requested to meet her. Xu then led the man into the back garden. Upon reaching the balcony, Xu called out loudly, "Sister, the guest has arrived; please come out quickly." Soon, a young woman's voice could be heard laughing softly, asking, "Is it Xiaomei coming?" She appeared, blushing and quickly stepping back, sitting beside without speaking. The man bowed, but she remained aloof and did not return the greeting. Xu said, "My brother has fled here in distress; I ask you, my younger sister, to pity and rescue him." As Xu was about to leave, Cuiyu held him back, saying, "Little sister is not one to entertain guests. Leaving the guest here and going away, what great confusion is this?" Xu said, "My brother's plight, I am truly unable to help. If you will help, then assist and stop; if not, then send him away. Decide for yourself; I will not be involved." With that, Xu waved his sleeves and left.

    306

    玉静坐不语。生视之，着粉太白，施朱太赤，艳绝之姿，较昔病颜大不侔矣。然颜如桃李，神逼雪霜，令人望而生畏。久之，生曰：“曩医贵恙，得睹仙容，嗣未一见，迄今苑结中心。”玉曰：“苑结何为？施药济人，固属盛德，若借为渔色之媒，祖功宗德丧尽矣。且见美人而思之，亦徒然耳。天不能为君一己之私，令月老系赤绳也。”言已，仍不语。生欲去不忍，欲止无趣，遂起身告辞。玉曰：“君欲寻死耶？”生意女必有援留之语，竟不复言。生不得已，复自居。既而女呼婢进茗，欻有一婢提茶一壶，置女面前而去。女自酌自饮，不顾生。生笑曰：“卿以糊涂责令兄，有客在坐，呼茶自饮，不奉客，其礼何居？”女亦微笑，捧杯献生。饮已，复酌，而辞色未尝少假也。未几，踆乌西坠，女烧高烛。烛下观之，尤增妩媚，爱慕之极，渐忘顾忌。女举烛搴帘入内室，生亦从之。女莞然笑曰：“君诚色胆如天矣。”生遂狎抱之。女曰：“且勿尔。

    Explanation

    306

    Jade sat quietly without speaking. The man looked at her, and saw that she was heavily made up, with white powder and red rouge, possessing a beauty beyond compare, vastly different from her sickly appearance in the past. Yet her face was like peach and plum blossoms, her spirit as cold as snow and frost, making one look at her and feel afraid. After a while, the man said, "In the past, when I treated your illness, I got to see your divine appearance. Since then, we have not met again, and I have been deeply troubled by it." Jade replied, "What is the purpose of your distress? Giving medicine to save people is certainly a great virtue. If it is used as a means to pursue beauty, the virtues of our ancestors will be completely lost. Even if you think of a beautiful woman, it is also in vain. Heaven cannot be swayed by your personal desires, and the matchmaker cannot tie the red string for you." She finished speaking and remained silent. The man wanted to leave but could not bring himself to do so, and staying seemed pointless, so he rose to take his leave. Jade said, "Are you going to commit suicide?" The man thought she would surely have words to detain him, but she remained silent. Unable to do otherwise, the man returned to his seat. Soon, the woman called for a servant to bring tea. Suddenly, a maid appeared with a teapot, placed it in front of the woman, and left. The woman poured herself a cup and drank it, ignoring the man. The man smiled and said, "You are being foolish and ordering me. With a guest present, you call for tea and drink it yourself without offering it to the guest, what kind of etiquette is that?" The woman also smiled and offered the cup to the man. After he drank, she poured herself another cup, but her demeanor showed no sign of leniency. Soon, the sun set in the west, and the woman lit a high candle. Under the candlelight, she looked even more charming. The man's admiration grew, and he gradually forgot his reservations. The woman lifted the candle and pulled the curtain into the inner room, and the man followed her. She smiled gently and said, "You truly have a heart of stone like the sky." The man then embraced her. She said, "Do not do that."

    307

    衷怀夙愿，欲达君听。妾笃志炼修，誓不适人。今遇君……”生遂接口曰：“似此闲谈，兹不暇听。”遂代解裙衫，牵入罗纬，极尽绸缪。女曰：“廿载坚贞，被君轻薄殆尽。”时值秋月上弦。每夕，女嘱生先寝，或夜半潜起，多时始回。生疑之。望夕，女设酒胾与生楼台玩月。女竭力劝生，而己不多饮。二更许，生伪醉欲寝；女令自寝，生不可。既寝，生伪为酣睡。女以生酒后睡熟，暗起启扉去。

    Explanation

    307
