    Deeply harbored long-standing wishes, I wish to convey them to you. I have firmly determined to cultivate myself spiritually and have sworn not to marry anyone. Now that I have met you..." The man then interjected, "Such idle talk, I have no time to listen to it." He then undressed her and took her into the bed, engaging in a deep and thorough intimacy. She said, "Twenty years of steadfast loyalty have been almost entirely disrespected by you." It was the first quarter of the autumn moon. Every night, she would instruct him to sleep first, and sometimes she would secretly get up at midnight, taking a long time to return. He began to suspect her. On the evening of the full moon, she set up wine and meat with him on the pavilion to enjoy the moon. She exerted all her efforts to persuade him, but she did not drink much herself. Around the second watch, he pretended to be drunk and wanted to sleep; she ordered him to sleep alone, but he refused. After he had fallen asleep, he pretended to be sleeping soundly. She thought he was drunk and asleep, and secretly opened the door to leave.

    308

    尾之，花墙隐身，自隙窥伺，见女至楼下，身化为狐，对月礼拜。拜已，仍化为女。生胆豪，不介意，急回挑灯以俟之。女上楼见灯，愕然曰：“君几时起？燃灯何为？”生托口渴，觅饮，女信之。既而生笑曰：“夜深露冷，每霄拜月，得无劳乎？”女变色曰：“君何由而知？”生曰：“仆目间卿已三夜矣。”女正色曰：“如果见爱，祈无以异类为嫌。”生曰：“得蒙福佑，已极铭感，何敢复生异心。”女喜甚，恩爱如故。生乃知许兄妹皆狐，前此贡院之言，盖欺语也。

    Explanation

    308

    He followed her, hiding behind a flower wall and peeping through a crack. He saw the woman descend to the lower floor, her body transforming into a fox. She worshipped the moon. After the ritual, she transformed back into a woman. The protagonist, bold and unconcerned, quickly returned, lit a lamp, and waited for her. The woman, upon seeing the light, was startled and asked, "When did you get up? Why are you lighting a lamp?" The protagonist replied, "I'm thirsty, looking for something to drink," and the woman believed him. Later, the protagonist laughed and said, "It's late and cold outside. Every night, you worship the moon. Isn't that tiring?" The woman's face changed color and asked, "How do you know this?" The protagonist said, "I've seen you for three nights in a row." The woman, serious now, said, "If you truly love me, please don't be prejudiced against my being a fox." The protagonist replied, "I have been greatly blessed and am deeply grateful. How could I harbor any other thoughts?" The woman was very pleased, and their love and affection remained as before. The protagonist then realized that Xu's siblings were also foxes, and the words spoken at the examination hall were lies.

    309

    一夕，生与女游戏灯下，生曰：“仆来时，卿误以仆作霞姑，彼何人斯？”女曰：“妾义妹。”生曰：“容颜奚似？”女曰：“霞之娟丽，不惟君生平未睹，即妾亦不多概见。”生闻之倾动，急欲一见芳容，长揖哀请。女屈指曰：“翌午必来。”生为之夜不成眠，朝不暇食，盼望綦切。午初，忽闻一女大声笑言曰：“妹不频来，姊姊胡不出迓？”既入，见生，讶曰：“姊姊何时得主？无物以贺，何惭如之。”翠玉曰：“此狂生逃难到此，却之不忍，故降心从之。”霞曰：“得若个好男子旦夕作伴，极乐境地。姊曰降心，何欺人已甚？”未几，饮馔肆设，三人同酌，主客笑言，履舄交错。生频目注霞娘，屡以游词挑之。霞曰：“姊夫大不端正。”玉曰：“是直宜逐出，使仇人执去下水牢也。”生置若罔闻。酒渐酣，语益狎。

    Explanation

    309

    One night, the protagonist and a woman played under the lamp. The protagonist said, "When I came, you mistook me for Xia Gu, who is she?" The woman replied, "She is my sworn sister." The protagonist asked, "How does her appearance compare?" The woman said, "Xia's grace and beauty are not only something you have never seen in your life, but even I have rarely seen anyone as beautiful." Upon hearing this, the protagonist was greatly moved and urgently requested to see her beauty, bowing deeply and pleading earnestly. The woman counted on her fingers and said, "She will definitely come tomorrow at noon." The protagonist was so excited that he could not sleep at night and was too busy in the morning to eat, looking forward to it with great eagerness. Early in the afternoon, a woman was heard laughing loudly and saying, "Why didn't you come out to greet me when I didn't come often?" Upon entering, she saw the protagonist and was surprised, asking, "When did my sister get a husband? I have nothing to offer as a gift, how ashamed I feel!" Cui Yu said, "This mad scholar came here to escape trouble, and I couldn't refuse him, so I compromised." Xia said, "Having such a good man to keep me company every day would be a state of utmost happiness. Why would you say such a thing, sister?" Soon, food and drink were set out, and the three of them drank together, laughing and talking, with their shoes and socks mixed together. The protagonist frequently looked at Xia and tried to flirt with her using playful words. Xia said, "Brother-in-law, you are not very handsome." Cui Yu said, "He should be expelled and taken away by enemies to a watery dungeon." The protagonist ignored her comments. As the wine grew stronger, the conversation became increasingly intimate.

    310

    霞起，旋即不见。生问之，女曰：“已回家去矣。”生闷坐不语，不饮亦不食。玉笑曰：“君得陇望蜀耶？”生曰：“然。”玉曰：“连宵不堪君扰，得渠少代亦佳。”遂书符，令生揖而焚之。既而，霞笑入，曰：“姊姊大不长进，竟为人作牵头。”玉曰：“狂郎情极，妹可少施慈悲。”霞曰：“妹施慈悲，姊勿生妒嫉。”玉笑咄之，急于别榻展锦衾，而止设一枕。霞笑曰：“姊以处己之事处人耶？”时方暮，生即牵霞同寝。玉曰：“何情极之不能待也！”及夜半，霞呼曰：“姊姊呼他去，妹困矣，他尚不欲睡。”玉曰：“吾得浮生半夜闲，不管他人事。”生兴足，问霞曰：“翠玉系狐，卿必狐也？”霞曰：“否。妾翟氏，陕西人，从父逃荒到此，十六岁暴病殂谢。狐姊爱妾华容，丹活之。俾居市尘，佣二媪伴焉。”生闻之，情益笃。

    Explanation

    310

    The mist rose, and then it was immediately gone. The man asked about it, and the woman replied, "I have already gone home." He sat silently, not drinking or eating. The jade girl smiled and said, "Have you gained one thing and now desire another?" He replied, "Yes." She said, "Throughout the night, I could not bear your disturbance; having her take your place would also be good." She then wrote a talisman, instructing the man to bow and burn it. Soon after, the mist girl laughed and entered, saying, "Sister, you haven't grown much; you've ended up being someone's go-between." The jade girl said, "The madman's passion is extreme, so sister can show a little mercy." The mist girl said, "If I show mercy, don't let jealousy arise." The jade girl laughed and scolded her, hurriedly arranging a separate bed with a brocade quilt but only setting up one pillow. The mist girl laughed, "Sister, are you treating others as you treat yourself?" It was already dusk, and the man immediately took the mist girl to bed. The jade girl said, "Why can't you wait for the passion to reach its peak?" By midnight, the mist girl called out, "Sister, call him away; I'm tired, but he still doesn't want to sleep." The jade girl said, "I have a brief moment of leisure in the middle of the night, and I don't care about others' affairs." The man was satisfied and asked the mist girl, "Cui Yu is a fox; you must be a fox too?" The mist girl said, "No. I am Zhai, from Shaanxi. I followed my father to escape famine here. At sixteen, I suddenly fell ill and died. My fox sister loved me, named me Huarong, and revived me. She made me live in the city, with two maids to accompany me." Upon hearing this, the man's affection grew even stronger.

    311

    霞善戏谑，每同生赴楼后花园游瞩。一日，生自适，闻门外二人语，窃听之，言仇人觅生甚急，昨获其马，凶身必未远遁，如有获之者，赏银若干云云。生大惊，急回楼中。玉见举止异常，问之。生以所闻告。玉曰：“君可留须，以防察识。”生年三十，本不欲从玉谋，计及远害，勉从之。甫半年，须已长成。一夕，女设酒凗为生祖饯，曰：“此宅即君仇人别业，渠欲徙居之。兹已为君市马治任，君可明早登程。”言已，各怀酸恻，而霞娘尤甚，泪滚滚如断贯珠。玉曰：“妹勿尔。终令汝二人团聚。”述往冀来，絮谈不休。未几，远钟报晓，玉曰：“君可行矣。”急以盥器贮水，戟指书之，令生濯面。生面白，濯后颜如渥赭。玉贺曰：“无人盘诘矣。出闽后当以净水涤之。”生应诺。送生至大门外，促生乘。生犹恋恋，玉芳袖一展，二女已杳。生无奈，急乘而驰。

    Explanation

    311

    Xia was skilled in jesting and often accompanied the protagonist to stroll in the garden behind the building. One day, the protagonist was at ease when he heard two people speaking outside and secretly listened. They said that the enemy was urgently searching for the protagonist, having captured his horse the previous day, and the dangerous person must not have fled far. Anyone who found him would be rewarded with a certain amount of silver. The protagonist was greatly alarmed and quickly returned to the building. Yu noticed his unusual behavior and asked him about it. The protagonist told her what he had heard. Yu said, "You can grow a beard to prevent being recognized." The protagonist was thirty years old and originally did not want to follow her plan, but considering the risk of harm, he reluctantly agreed. After only half a year, the beard had grown. One night, the women prepared wine and bid farewell to the protagonist, saying, "This house is your enemy's secondary residence, and he wants to move into it. I have already bought a horse and prepared your luggage. You can leave tomorrow morning." After saying this, each felt sorrowful, especially Miss Xia, whose tears flowed like broken beads. Yu said, "Sister, don't be like that. I will eventually reunite you two." They recalled the past and hoped for the future, talking incessantly. Soon, the distant bell announced dawn, and Yu said, "You can go now." She quickly filled a basin with water, wrote with her finger, and ordered the protagonist to wash his face. The protagonist had a pale face, but after washing, his face turned dark red. Yu congratulated, "No one will question you. After leaving Fujian, you should wash it with clean water." The protagonist agreed. She sent him to the main gate and urged him to ride. The protagonist was still reluctant, but as Yu waved her sleeve, the two women disappeared. The protagonist was helpless and quickly rode away.

    312

    至家，见房舍尽成灰烬，大惊。问之邻人，始知家被火灾，妻子投亲山庄，已数月。生寻至，知幼子亦被焚病死，不胜凄楚。生家素倚赁租度日，宅遭回禄，入不敌出，数年后，厨无炊烟。妻劝行丐，生耻之。一日早起，将从妻谋，忽于床头得白金数百，生以为天赐，由是市产谋生，居诸少裕。数年后，清贫如故，妻适卒，不能备葬具。正踌躇间，忽见案上有白金百两，大喜，疑金为狐妻之赠，藉以营葬。殡后茕居，目鳏鳏恒不瞑。一日，见华妆丽人率五尺之童自外入，大愕，以为吾家无此眷属。审谛之，霞娘也。问童子为谁，曰：“君之子，姊生之，而妾养之。”从人移运财物，茅屋三楹几满。

    Explanation

    312

    Upon arriving home, he saw that the house had been reduced to ashes, and he was greatly shocked. After inquiring with the neighbors, he learned that his home had been destroyed by fire, and his wife and children had fled to a relative's countryside home, having been there for several months. Upon searching for them, he discovered that his young son had also died from the fire, and he was overwhelmed with sorrow. His family had always relied on rental income to make ends meet, but with the house destroyed by fire, their expenses far exceeded their income. Several years later, their kitchen had no smoke, indicating extreme poverty. His wife suggested begging, but he was ashamed of the idea. One morning, as he was about to follow his wife's advice, he suddenly found several hundred taels of white gold by his bedside. He believed it was a gift from heaven and used it to purchase property to make a living, which temporarily improved their circumstances. However, several years later, they remained as poor as before. His wife then passed away, and they could not afford proper burial arrangements. While he was pondering this, he suddenly saw a hundred taels of white gold on the table and was greatly delighted, suspecting that it was a gift from his fox wife to help with the funeral arrangements. After burying his wife and living alone in solitude, he often found himself unable to sleep, his eyes wide open. One day, he saw a beautifully dressed woman enter with a five-foot-tall child. He was greatly startled, thinking that there was no such relative in his family. Upon closer examination, he realized it was Xianiang. He asked who the child was, and she replied, "He is your son, born by my sister and raised by me." She then moved and transported their belongings, and their three-room thatched house was nearly full.

    313

    生疑为梦，多时惊定而喜，始问子名。曰：“男子之生，父名之。子未见父，谁敢命名？”生曰：“子生于闽，可名福生。玉盍同来？”曰：“姊来二次，君不知耶？”生闻之茫然。曰：“床头之金，殡妻之资，悉姊亲身送到。”生深为感佩。自是财雄一村。生有富戚，久绝往还，闻生陡发，备礼进谒。见福清秀，面订为婿。生叹曰：“今乃知福厚之不可忽也。”福完婚之日，悲泣思母。生语霞，霞遂书符，令福三拜稽首而后焚之。未几，翠玉至，合家团圆，幼子花烛，其喜可知。月馀，玉谓生曰：“妾原不欲复履红尘，乃夫妻子母之情，妾不能恝，故承妹召，勉为一临，实不能奉事终身。”言已而杳，不复至。

    Explanation

    313

    The husband doubted whether it was a dream, and after a long time, he was startled but then delighted, and began to ask for the son's name. The son replied, "A son is named by his father. Since I have not seen my father, who dares to name me?" The husband said, "You were born in Fujian, so you can be named Fu Sheng. Why didn't you come with your sister?" The son said, "My sister has come twice, but you didn't know, did you?" The husband was confused upon hearing this. He said, "The gold kept by me, which was intended for the funeral expenses of my deceased wife, was all delivered personally by your sister." The husband deeply felt grateful. From then on, he became wealthy enough to dominate the village. The husband had a wealthy relative who had been out of contact for a long time. Upon hearing that the husband suddenly became wealthy, the relative prepared a gift to pay respects. Seeing that Fu was clear and refined, the relative formally arranged for him to be a son-in-law. The husband sighed and said, "Now I understand that good fortune should not be taken lightly." On the day of Fu's wedding, he wept and thought of his mother. The husband told his sister-in-law, and she wrote a talisman, instructing Fu to perform three bows and kowtows before burning it. Soon after, Cui Yu arrived, and the whole family reunited. The young son's wedding was held, and the joy was evident. After a month, Cui Yu told the husband, "Originally, I did not want to return to the world of mortals, but the feelings of husband, wife, children, and mother are so strong that I cannot be indifferent. Therefore, I responded to my sister's call and kindly came once, but in reality, I cannot serve for life." She spoke these words and then disappeared, never to return again.

    314

    虚白道人曰：观某生之遭遇，不惟身亡，兼绝后嗣，乃以施药一节，得绝处逢生，嗣子裕后。谁谓捐资乐施为无益举也？但可借之以积善，不可因之以丧德，当以生妻之言为金石也。

    　　著手成春，俯视即是，使留仙为之，不过尔尔。马竹吾

    　　不信狐而得狐之济，转令人有望于狐矣。 黄琴轩

    　　情生文，文生情。情者见之不觉动情，文者见之谓之能文。 盖防如

    　　有议论，有步骤，有斡补，有结构。 何子英

    　　文笔如无缝天衣，又若在山阴道上，令人应接不暇。技亦神矣哉！ 尹亦山

    　　读是传能不羡某生之奇遇，而常念生妻与狐女之正言，斯为善读书者。 杨子厚

    　　唾骂甘受一段，是黄石公教留侯故智。文善于操纵，极烟离雨合之奇。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　刘翁

    　　刘翁存德，世籍泰安县东之山口庄，家殷饶。庄中历有一、六集场，因于闹市设酒肆，生意隆盛，遂至巨富。

    Explanation

    314

    The Immortal of Empty White said: Observing the experiences of a certain Mr., not only did he lose his life, but also his descendants were extinguished. Yet, through the act of giving medicine, he found life in a desperate situation, and his descendants prospered. Who would say that giving money and being joyful in doing so is a futile act? One can use it to accumulate good deeds, but one must not let it lead to the loss of virtue. One should take the words of the living wife as if they were stone.

    With a touch, he brought about a miraculous recovery, as if it were right below one's feet. If Mr. Lianxian were to do it, it would be nothing more than this. — Ma Zhuwu

    Not believing in foxes but getting help from them, it changes one's expectations of foxes. — Huang Qinxuan

    Emotion gives rise to writing, and writing gives rise to emotion. Those who see it are moved by emotion without realizing it, and those who see it consider it to be good writing. — Gai Fangru

    There is discussion, there are steps, there is supplementation, and there is structure. — He Ziyi

    The writing is like a seamless celestial robe, and it is like being on the mountain road in Shanyin, where one cannot keep up with the sights. The skill is also divine indeed! — Yin Yishan

    To read this biography and not envy the strange fate of Mr., and to always remember the wise words of the living wife and the fox girl, this is a good reader. — Yang Zihou

    A section where one willingly accepts insults, it is the ancient wisdom of Master Huangshi Gong teaching Mr. Liuhou. The writing is skillful in manipulation, reaching the extreme of the strange mingling of smoke and rain. — Li Yu of Shangyuan, respectfully annotated

    Mr. Liu

    Mr. Liu Cundeh, whose family register was in Shan Kou Village, east of Taian County, was a man of great wealth. The village had markets on the first and sixth days of each month. He set up a wine shop in the bustling market, and his business flourished, leading to great prosperity.

    315

    族侄某，中年无子，买一幼妇作妾，向翁称贷。翁曰：“需钱几何？”答以十二千。翁应诺。忽闻肆外哭声甚哀，急出视，某亦从之，见少年男女相向哭。翁问之，其男曰：“吾杨忠，此吾妻韩氏。自丰邑逃荒到此，资斧绝，实难存活，不得已鬻妻自度。生离难堪，是以伤耳。”翁曰：“鬻给谁氏？”忠指某曰：“即是人。”翁遂向某曰：“渠既不欲生离，汝可另行物色，若需钱文，即数十千亦不少取尔息。”某喜而去。忠曰：“使吾二人团聚，固属美意。食用无着，仍不免饿殍死。”翁曰：“吾如数给汝身价。”韩氏曰：“吾异乡人，承惠钱文，赁屋将去其半，馀钱无多，用度尽，难保他不另卖吾。”言已，泣涕不止。翁曰：“勿尔。吾庄外有闲房几间，借汝居住，门外有田数亩，租汝耕种，或可赖以固存。”忠夫妇闻之，伏地叩谢。翁遂如言安置之。

    Explanation

    315

    A certain cousin, middle-aged and without children, bought a young woman to be his concubine and borrowed money from the old man. The old man asked, "How much money is needed?" The cousin replied, "Twelve thousand." The old man agreed. Suddenly, they heard a very sad cry outside the shop. They hurriedly went out to see, and the cousin followed. They saw young men and women crying facing each other. The old man asked them, and the man said, "I am Yang Zhong, and this is my wife, Han Shi. We fled from famine in Fuyi and came here, but with no means of sustenance, we could not survive. We had no choice but to sell her to support ourselves. The pain of being separated is unbearable, which is why we are so distressed." The old man asked, "To whom was she sold?" Yang Zhong pointed to the cousin and said, "To this person." The old man then said to the cousin, "Since they do not want to be separated, you can look for someone else. If you need money, even tens of thousands, I won't take much interest." The cousin left happily. Yang Zhong said, "Allowing us two to be reunited is indeed a good deed. But with no means of sustenance, we still cannot avoid starving to death." The old man said, "I will give you the full price." Han Shi said, "I am a stranger from another place. Receiving your generosity of money, renting a house will take half of it, and the remaining money is not much. All expenses will be used up, and it's hard to guarantee he won't sell me again." She finished speaking and wept uncontrollably. The old man said, "Do not worry. I have a few empty rooms outside my estate, and I will lend you to live there. There are several acres of land outside the gate, and I will rent it to you for cultivation, perhaps it can help you survive." Upon hearing this, Yang Zhong and his wife fell to the ground and thanked him. The old man then arranged things as he said.

    316

    忠经历艰辛，倍笃勤俭，数年渐宽裕，遂将所给身价加利奉偿。公爱其诚实，止受原项而返其息。忠深感翁德，惭无以报。每值翁家需人，忠夫妇同赴，杂家人操作。会翁嫡孙方四岁，炎天生痘，险症变逆，肉肿而疮漫平，至九日而殇。翁痛恨之，赤其身，命忠携出埋之。埋儿处，即忠租地。忠掘穴二尺，纳尸其中，不忍遽拥之以土，先以土掩其下体，而留其首。俄，见唇口微动，手试之，有微息，忠急去土抱出，呼吸渐大。忠欲走告翁家，韩氏曰：“告之而小郎果生，固喜，否则更增老主人忧戚。

    Explanation

    316

    Zhuo, having experienced hardships, became even more diligent and thrifty. After several years, he gradually became more affluent and repaid the principal and interest given to him. The master loved his honesty and only accepted the principal, returning the interest. Zhuo deeply felt the master's kindness and was ashamed to have no way to repay it. Whenever the master's household needed help, Zhuo and his wife would go together and work alongside the other family members. One day, the master's legitimate grandson, who was only four years old, developed smallpox. The condition worsened, causing swelling and spreading sores, and he died on the ninth day. The master was extremely angry and ordered Zhuo to take the body out and bury it. The place where the child was buried was the land that Zhuo rented. Zhuo dug a hole two feet deep, placed the body inside, but could not immediately cover it with soil. He first covered the lower part of the body with soil, leaving the head exposed. Soon, he saw the lips and mouth moving slightly. He touched them and felt a faint breath. Zhuo quickly removed the soil and carried the child out, and the breathing gradually became stronger. Zhuo wanted to go tell the master's family, but Han said, "If you tell them and the little master does indeed survive, that would be good. Otherwise, it would only add to the master's sorrow."

    317

    不如暂抱归吾家，若仍死，则埋之；万一绝处逢生，大愈后喜报未为晚。”忠韪之。前曾路拾穿山甲四五钱，闻是药最能透发痘症隐伏之毒，煎而服之。此药性最雄烈，服后痘暴起成泡，泡破出水不止。忠急取道上热土铺盖之。儿已三日不食，至次日，忽呼母求食。忠急市糯米煮稀粥食之。痘渐结痂如钱厚。廿日后，痂尽落，能自起步室内，但思母情极，不时蹄哭。忠遂令妻诣翁家，乘间禀白。至，则刘妻忆孙，方暗泣室内。韩氏曰：“小郎固未死，现在奴家，思亲孔殷，祈即往视。”刘妻闻之，急同孙母遄往，见之大喜，如获月明珠。因问孙得生之故，忠细述之。由是翁遇忠益厚云。

    Explanation

    317

    If there's still a chance of survival, bring him home to me; if he still dies, bury him. After a full recovery, reporting the good news is not too late." Zhong agreed to this. Previously, he had picked up a few grams of pangolin on the road and heard that this medicine is best at expelling hidden toxins of smallpox. He boiled and drank it. This medicine has the most potent and intense properties. After taking it, the smallpox blisters up violently, forming bubbles that break and continue to ooze. Zhong quickly took hot soil from the road to cover it. The child had not eaten for three days. By the next day, he suddenly called for his mother for food. Zhong hurriedly bought glutinous rice and cooked a thin porridge for him. The smallpox gradually scabbed over, as thick as coins. Twenty days later, all the scabs fell off, and he was able to walk around the room on his own. Missing his mother intensely, he would often cry out. Zhong then instructed his wife to go to the grandfather's house to take the opportunity to inform. When she arrived, Liu's wife, thinking of her grandson, was secretly crying in the room. Han said, "Little master is indeed not dead; he is now with me. Missing his parents very much, please come to see him immediately." Liu's wife, upon hearing this, hastily went with the grandmother. Seeing him, they were very happy, as if they had found a precious pearl. They then asked about the reason the grandson survived. Zhong explained it in detail. From then on, the grandfather treated Zhong even more kindly.

    318

    虚白道人曰：刘翁之裕后，或不止处忠一举，然无此举亦难得嫡孙重生，其中确有造物福善之意。

    　　刘翁使杨忠夫妇团聚，仁也；如数给伊身价，信也；安置其身，礼也；爱其老诚，智也；受原价而返其息，义也。刘翁行此五者，皆美德也。有德者必有后，嫡孙重生，天理则宜然也。 盖防如

    　　翁之厚德，固人所难，而食报之速，亦出意外。阅之，可以感发人之善心。 杨子厚

    Explanation

    318

    Xu Bai the Daoist Master said: "Old Man Liu's actions to ensure the well-being of his descendants were not limited to one act of righteousness. However, without this act, it would have been difficult for his legitimate grandson to be reborn. There is indeed a clear intention of the Creator to bestow blessings on the virtuous."

    Old Man Liu caused Yang Zhong and his wife to reunite, which is benevolence; he gave her the full amount of her value, which is trustworthiness; he settled her, which is propriety; he loved her for her loyalty and sincerity, which is wisdom; he received the original price and returned the interest, which is righteousness. These five actions by Old Man Liu are all virtues. Those with virtue will surely have descendants, and the rebirth of the legitimate grandson is as it should be according to heavenly principles.

    Prevent as if... The old man's great virtue is indeed difficult for others to match, and the rapid receipt of rewards is also unexpected. Reading this can stir up people's good hearts. Yang Zihou.

    319

    林 闺 英

    　　彭将军，四川人，谈者忘其官讳，盖武弁裔而凌夷者也。性直爽而嗜赌。凡事亦知礼节，但一遇樗蒲，诸务悉废。适逢年凶，双亲同日殂谢，棺木无出，痛恨实深，乃祈赙于舅氏。彭有母舅某孝廉，富室也，素常周恤其家，今值甥有大故，至即以钱缗付之。

    　　彭负钱归，时已黄昏。邻有赌局，因墙倾，往常聚赌其家。忽斗声聒耳，窃听之，即素相识之博友，急赴排解，劝各入局复赌。见赌者钱注丰美，技痒欲入局，恐亲死未殓，招人非议。既归室，心怔忡不寐。时妻陈氏已寐，潜起持钱欲往，回顾灵床，旋复置之。既而兴发不能忍，携钱径去，未几全输。潜回再取钱去，又输。思欲罄资以赌，冀得珠还合浦，比回，而妻已醒。彭将携资复往，妻阻之，且力劝曰：“钱文已负多半，不惟无颜见阿舅，使二亲以土蒙面，于汝心安乎？”彭俯首邑邑，追悔无及，而所剩之钱，实不足以市桐棺，遂乘夜藁葬，惭愧不胜，弃妻出亡。

    Explanation

    319

    Lin Guiying

    Peng, a general from Sichuan, is a man whose official title and name are often forgotten by those who speak of him. He is a descendant of a military officer who has fallen into decline. Peng is straightforward and cheerful but loves gambling. He knows etiquette in all things, but whenever he encounters dice games, all other matters are abandoned. Just when a bad year occurred, both his parents died on the same day. There was no coffin available, and his grief was deep. He then asked for a loan from his maternal uncle. Peng had a maternal uncle who was a scholar-official, and he was a wealthy man who usually helped Peng's family. Now that his nephew had a great misfortune, he immediately gave him a bundle of money.

    Peng returned with the money, and it was already dusk. There was a gambling session nearby. Because the wall had collapsed, they usually gathered to gamble at his home. Suddenly, the sounds of betting were loud in his ears. He secretly listened to it and realized it was a gambler he knew well. He hurried to mediate and persuaded everyone to rejoin the game. He saw that the gamblers had a lot of money and wanted to join the game out of desire. He was afraid that his parents had not yet been buried and would be criticized by others. He returned to his room, and his heart was restless, and he could not sleep. His wife Chen had already fallen asleep. He secretly got up and took the money to go, but he looked back at the altar and put the money back. Soon after, he could not resist the urge and took the money and went straight away. In a short time, he lost everything. He secretly returned and took more money to go again, but he lost again. He thought he might bet everything, hoping to get his money back. When he returned, his wife was already awake. Peng was about to take the money and go again, but his wife stopped him and strongly advised him to say, "Most of the money has been lost. Not only will you have no face to see your maternal uncle, making your parents be buried with dirt covering their faces, how can you be at peace with yourself?" Peng lowered his head and sighed, regretting it too late. The remaining money was not enough to buy a coffin made of paulownia wood. He then buried his parents at night with a simple grave, feeling ashamed and could not bear it. He abandoned his wife and fled.

    320

    孝廉某，谨具庶馐楮帛致祭亡姊，至则灵已殡，甥已外出，乃令彭妻引至葬所祭之。祭毕四望，大喜曰：“此墓龙真穴的，禄马得位，旗叠火星，必立勋疆场。但棺木虽薄，亦迟六七年，惟藁葬焉发贵始速。”言已，以金赠彭妻而去。

    　　彭某之出亡也，本无定方，闻汉中年丰，欲谋食于彼。至宁羌，遇一人，亦以事故逃亡者，同病相恤，因共栖止，而所有资斧，悉为其人乘间窃去，遂丐食于道路。会有甘肃行客，恐路有不测，欲佣一人作伴，见彭身躯雄伟，大喜，告以己意。彭亦喜，不索值，客饮食与俱。彭乍得饱食，不啻离幽狱而登天堂。至，客格外厚酬之。彭囊资少裕，欲旋里，而囊资又复遗失，叹曰：“吾必为他乡饿鬼。”复事乞丐。一日途中拾一包裹，内皆细软，心窃喜，以为天赐。遇县群捕，疑其为偷，将彭至一庄首庙中问之，彭答以路拾。

    Explanation

    320

    A certain Xianglian, with simple dishes and paper and silk, performed a memorial service for his deceased sister. Upon arrival, he found that the spirit had already been buried, and his nephew was already out. He then ordered Peng's wife to lead him to the burial site to perform the memorial service. After the memorial service, he looked around and was greatly pleased, saying, "This grave has true dragon and dragon's eye, favorable positions of stars, and a specific configuration, which will certainly bring great deeds on the battlefield. However, even though the coffin is thin, it will also take six to seven years, but only a temporary burial will bring wealth quickly." Having said this, he gave gold to Peng's wife and then left.

    Peng, who had fled, originally had no fixed direction. Hearing that Hanzhong had a good harvest, he wanted to seek sustenance there. He reached Ningqiang and met someone who had also fled due to some incident. They helped each other in their misfortune, so they lived together, but all his resources were stolen by the other person while taking advantage of a momentary opportunity, so he began begging for food on the road. A traveler from Gansu, fearing unexpected dangers on the road, wanted to hire a companion. Seeing Peng's robust physique, he was greatly pleased and told Peng of his intention. Peng was also pleased and did not ask for payment. The traveler provided him with food and companionship. Peng suddenly had a satisfying meal, feeling as if he had left a dark prison and entered paradise. When they arrived, the traveler generously rewarded him. Peng's bag of money was slightly better off, and he wanted to return home, but his resources were lost again. He sighed, "I will surely become a starving ghost in a foreign land," and resumed begging. One day, while on the road, he picked up a package containing valuables. He secretly rejoiced, thinking it was a divine gift. He was questioned by a group of pursuers who suspected him of being a thief and brought him to a village temple to question him. Peng explained that he had found the package on the road.

    321

    更迭盘诘，彭无异词。众捕怒，裸其衣，缚而拷问。彭百口不能辨，挞楚甚苦，唯求速死。彭所拾物，即是庄林员外长女所遗，且悉姑家物，忽然遗失，举家为之惶恐。忽家人报曰：“所遗包裹，乞人拾之，捕役谓其为偷，现在庙中拷问，已滨死。”林公闻之，率家人急赴庙。庙门紧闭，捶楚之声惨不可闻。公令家人逾垣开门。公曰：“诬良为盗，私行拷问，王法不容。”役不服，公曰：“吾先言渠所拾之物，如不对则真为贼。”遂历言之。役伪曰不是。公令家人搜视之，一一不爽，役始惧而散。彭伤重不能步，公令人舁于其家养之。伤愈为公服役。

    Explanation

    321

    After repeated questioning, Peng gave no contradictory statements. The constables, enraged, stripped him of his clothes and bound him for interrogation. Despite his numerous protests, Peng could not defend himself against the severe torture, which was extremely painful, and he only sought a quick death. The items Peng had found belonged to Lin Gong's eldest daughter, who was also from her mother's family. The sudden loss of these items caused great anxiety for the entire family. Suddenly, the family reported, "The lost package was picked up by a beggar, and the constables accused him of theft. He is now being tortured in the temple and is near death." Upon hearing this, Lin Gong, accompanied by his family, rushed to the temple. The temple doors were tightly shut, and the sounds of beating and torture were so horrific that they were unbearable to hear. Lin Gong ordered his family to climb over the wall and open the temple doors. He said, "Accusing an innocent person of theft and conducting an illegal interrogation is unacceptable under the law." The constables refused to accept this, and Lin Gong said, "I will first describe the items Peng found. If they do not match, then he is truly a thief." He then listed the items in detail. The constables pretended to deny it. Lin Gong ordered his family to search and verify the items, and they matched perfectly, causing the constables to fear and disperse. Peng, severely injured and unable to walk, was carried home by Lin Gong's family to recover. After his wounds healed, Peng served the public.

    322

    公第三女极美，及笄未字。一日昼寝，梦楼后有黑虎卧树下。惊醒异之，因启楼门下视，所梦之虎，仍卧原处，大惊，细审多时，乃彭某也。女心知彭福命不可量，欲嫁之以托终身，又恐斯愿难遂，踌躇者久之，乃取素纸一方，上书数字。书毕毁之，复书复毁，凡四五次。后书毕，包以重物，执倚楼门，以俟彭醒。彭方起身坐地，女故咳一声，彭抬首见女笑容可掬，疑睡眼朦胧所致。欻女掷一物于面前，拾视之，纸包同心戒指一付，纸上书“二漏后以花梯上楼”八字，大喜仰视，楼门已闭。及二更，果楼门大开。彭急移花梯上楼，见女手托香腮，若有所思。彭将近女狎抱之，女以纤手推之，厉色微言曰：“那边坐。”彭如命。对坐良久，女不语。

    Explanation

    322

    The Third Daughter of the Man

    The third daughter of the man was extremely beautiful, having reached the age of marriage but not yet betrothed. One day, she took a nap during the day and dreamt of a black tiger lying under a tree behind the tower. She woke up in alarm, finding it strange, and opened the tower door to look down. The tiger from her dream was still lying in the same spot. She was greatly shocked and examined it closely for a long time, only to realize that it was Peng, a man. She knew that Peng's fortune was immeasurable and wished to marry him to secure her future, but she also feared that this wish might not be fulfilled. She hesitated for a long time before taking a piece of plain white paper and writing a few characters on it. After finishing, she tore it up, repeated the process, and did so four or five times. Finally, after writing, she wrapped the paper with a heavy object, held it against the tower door, and waited for Peng to wake up. Peng was just getting up and sitting on the ground when the girl intentionally coughed once. Peng raised his head and saw the girl with a radiant smile, suspecting that it was due to his drowsy eyes. Suddenly, the girl threw an object in front of him. He picked it up and saw that it was a pair of matching rings wrapped in paper, with the words "Climb the tower using the flower ladder after the second watch" written on it. He was overjoyed and looked up, only to find that the tower door was closed. At the second watch, the tower door indeed opened wide. Peng quickly moved the flower ladder and climbed the tower, where he saw the girl resting her chin on her hand, as if deep in thought. Peng approached her and embraced her closely, but she pushed him away with her delicate hands and spoke softly but with a stern expression: "Sit over there." Peng obeyed. They sat facing each other for a long time, and the girl remained silent.

    323

    彭微动，女曰：“起身何为？妾非淫奔者流。妾相君非贪贱中人，故欲委身奉事，藉图封赠，但私合如是，非礼已极，恐君得志后，弃予如遗。”彭曰：“少有进步，必先媒定。”女不语。彭请誓以神明，女从之。彭跪而言曰：“吾彭某若以贵贱异心，所不与林氏……请问芳名？”女答以闺英。彭复曰：“所不与林氏闺英偕老者……”女遽以手掩其口。彭即执女手而起，乘势拥女于怀。女曰：“勿尔。兹虽苟合，亦不可过于了草，可以酒胾权为合卺礼。”彭笑从之。彭不善饮，女媚劝之，彭亦强女同饮，各醉而始寝。女曰：“妾悬揣情形，君必有嫡妻。”曰：“无之。”女曰：“果无之，君亦私交有人。”异日，彭见女着新屦，曰：“卿自作乎？”曰：“非也，此妾大姊屦。

    Explanation

    323

    Peng moved slightly, and the woman said, "Why are you getting up? I am not a woman of loose virtue. I believe you are not a man who would settle for lowly status, so I wish to dedicate myself to you in service, hoping for official recognition. However, such a private union is a grave breach of propriety, and I fear that after you achieve success, you will abandon me like discarded refuse." Peng replied, "If there is any further progress, it must be preceded by a formal matchmaker arrangement." The woman remained silent. Peng then asked her to swear by the gods, and she agreed. Peng knelt and said, "If I, Peng, were to treat you differently based on your social status, I would not be with the Lin family... May I ask your name?" The woman replied with "Guiying." Peng continued, "If I were not to live with the Lin family's Guiying... " The woman quickly covered his mouth with her hand. Peng immediately took her hand and stood up, taking the opportunity to embrace her. She said, "Do not do that. Although this union is hasty, it should not be too casual; we can use wine and meat as a makeshift wedding ceremony." Peng laughed and agreed. Peng was not good at drinking, but the woman flattered him and encouraged him to drink, and Peng also insisted that she drink with him. They both became drunk and then went to sleep. The woman said, "I suspect that you must have a legitimate wife." Peng replied, "No, I do not." The woman said, "If you truly do not, then you must have private affairs with someone else." On another day, Peng saw the woman wearing new shoes and asked, "Did you make them yourself?" She replied, "No, these are my older sister's shoes."

    324

    ”彭闻之而笑，女曰：“何笑为？”彭曰：“昨所拾包裹内，有小靴一双，瘦小可爱。尝自言曰：安得如是之妻以接吾目？今得妻卿，且见令姊之屦，如见令姊之足，夙愿已遂，故笑之。”女曰：“此狂笑。此妾姊之屦，非妾姊之足。视屦如足，妾姊之足，君亦尝得把握耶？”彭一日昼劳于役，黄昏仰卧，不觉睡去。及醒，时已夜半，急上楼，见案有肴酒，曰：“将假此以消良夜耶？”女不答。视女春面生愁，秋波含泣，大惊曰：“得无以吾来太迟耶？”女曰：“非也。此酒非与君消夜，实为君祖道。半年之聚，一夕仳离，不能无悲。”彭愕然问故，女曰：“怀安败名，君宜早为出头计。”曰：“卿言是也。焉往而善？”女曰：“君将门之后，可由行伍为进身之阶。”彭曰：“从事行伍，亦自不易。

    Explanation

    324

    Peng heard this and laughed. The woman asked, "Why are you laughing?" Peng replied, "Yesterday, in the package I picked up, there was a pair of small, lovely boots. He once said to himself: 'How can I get a wife like this to meet my eyes?' Now that I have you as my wife, and I see your sister's shoes, it's like seeing her feet. My long-held wish has been fulfilled, so I laughed." The woman said, "This is a crazy laugh. These are my sister's shoes, not her feet. Seeing shoes as feet, have you ever held my sister's feet?" One day, Peng worked hard during the day, and at dusk, he lay down and fell asleep without realizing it. When he woke up, it was already midnight, and he rushed upstairs to see food and wine on the table. He said, "Are you planning to use this to pass the good night?" The woman did not answer. Seeing the woman's spring-like face filled with sorrow, her autumn-like eyes containing tears, Peng was greatly surprised and asked, "Is it because I came too late?" The woman said, "No. This wine is not for passing the night with you, but for bidding you farewell. Six months of being together, one night of separation, can't help but feel sad." Peng was startled and asked why. The woman said, "Hai'an has ruined his reputation; you should think of a way out soon." Peng said, "You're right. Where should I go to improve my situation?" The woman said, "You are from a military family; you can use military service as a stepping stone to advance yourself." Peng said, "Serving in the military is not easy either."

    325

    ”女曰：“妾父与某游击交莫逆，妾已修父荐书，执以往，必见提拔。明晨即可起程。”言至此，泪如涌泉，呜咽不能语。彭曲劝之，多时始止。女曰：“私蓄碎银十数两，权为资斧。”遂将银书并付彭。忽闻鸡唱，女曰：“君可以行矣。倘有进步，务寄一信来。”彭曰：“书何由达？”女曰：“庄东首梅媪者，妾乳母。寄信于媪，媪自能转寄。”言已，促彭行。三年之间，彭以军功位至参将，即烦某游击作冰，得纳雁林公家。及归妻，林送之，始知新婿为彭。及晚，林媪窃听之，彭曰：“仆升官时，卿闻之喜否？”女曰：“彼时固喜，尤不若今日喜。”彭笑曰：“人之所娶皆处子，仆不然，何不幸若是。”女亦笑曰：“人于娶时，始知为处子，君早知之，君之幸实倍他人。”媪以之白林，林甚耻之。

    Explanation

    325

    The woman said, "My father and a certain military officer were inseparable friends. I have written a recommendation letter from my father, and I will take it to him; he will definitely promote me. I can start my journey tomorrow morning." As she spoke, tears flowed like a spring, and she sobbed, unable to speak. Peng tried to comfort her, and after some time, she stopped. She said, "I have privately saved a few ounces of silver, which I will use as travel expenses." She then handed the silver and the letter to Peng. Suddenly hearing the rooster crow, the woman said, "You can go now. If you make any progress, be sure to send me a letter." Peng asked, "How will the letter reach you?" The woman replied, "There is an old woman named Mei at the east end of the village. She is my wet nurse. Send the letter to her, and she will be able to forward it to me." After saying this, she urged Peng to leave. Within three years, Peng rose to the rank of commander through military merit. He then asked the military officer to act as a matchmaker and was able to marry into the Ye Lin family. When he returned home to his wife, the Lin family sent her to meet him, and they learned that the new son-in-law was Peng. By evening, the old woman from the Lin family secretly listened to them. Peng said, "When I was promoted, did you hear and feel happy?" The woman replied, "I was happy then, but even more so now." Peng laughed and said, "Most people marry virgins, but I am not. How unfortunate is that?" The woman also laughed and said, "Most people only find out they are virgins at the time of marriage, but you knew from the start. So you are actually much luckier." The old woman relayed this to the Lin family, and they were deeply ashamed.

    326

    一日，彭与妻夜饮，林曰：“妾相君非贫贱中人，今果然矣，可谓至诚前知。”彭曰：“卿于我固有先见之明，但卿有切己之事而卿不自知，卿盖嫡而庶者。”林曰：“知之，但知之少迟耳。若早知一时，即君位至合阁，亦无楼上之私合。”因细询颠末。彭将赌输殡资，面受妻责及藁葬双亲，一切向林言之。林曰：“陈姊宽厚，使妾在，当日必将执亲遗杖捶楚无算。”彭闻之为之变色。彭以军务外出，月馀未归。林以使令不足，佣一少妇。林视之，举止幽闲，似非常为人役者。

    Explanation

    326

    One day, Peng and his wife were drinking together at night. Lin said, "I predicted that you would not be a poor and lowly person, and now it has indeed come to pass. You truly have foreknowledge through utmost sincerity." Peng replied, "You indeed have foresight regarding me, but you have a matter concerning yourself that you are unaware of. You are actually the legitimate wife but are treated as a concubine." Lin said, "I know it, but I knew it a bit late. If I had known earlier, even if you reached the position of Grand Secretary, there would be no secret meetings in the upstairs room." She then asked for the details. Peng recounted how he had lost money from gambling and the burial of his parents, and all this to Lin. Lin said, "My sister-in-law is kind and generous. If I were there, she would have beaten me mercilessly with her father's cane." Peng heard this and his face changed color. Peng left for military affairs and did not return for over a month. Lin hired a young woman because the maidservants were insufficient. Lin observed her and found her behavior to be calm and composed, unlike a typical servant.

    327

    问其邦族，答以姓彭，四川人，为寻夫到此。林愕然细诘，知为官人嫡配，曰：“翁姑双亡时，姊果以土盖亲面责不孝人耶？”陈曰：“此吾与拙夫语，夫人何由备悉？”林曰：“吾非他，与姊共仰望终身于一人。”陈未敢深信。林使人取公服衣陈，令人扶持，修嫡庶礼。陈曰：“即如夫人言，亦不必行此大礼。”林曰：“礼不可废，分不可逾。”未几彭归，林迎之曰：“妾奉迓君结发夫人至。”彭曰：“卿有是言，已见盛情。”欻有婢媪拥一夫人出，视之，果陈氏，大喜，谓林曰：“卿可谓贤德人。”嗣衙中操演，林每垂帘观之，赏罚明公，兵悉畏服焉。

    　　虚白道人曰：地理之说，果足信耶？昔有一地，龙真穴的，沙水环抱，其发也必矣。然其家则恶贯将盈，乡人皆恶者也。朱子过而见之曰：“此地不发是无地理，此地若发是无天理。”嗣果不发。由是观之，可知有天理，斯有地理。世之妄求富贵者，不讲天良，徒旁求地师，苛择风水，岂不愚而迂耶！

    Explanation

    327

    Lin asked about her clan and family, and she answered with the surname Peng, saying she was from Sichuan and had come here to find her husband. Lin was startled and asked in detail, learning that she was the official's legitimate wife. He asked, "When both your in-laws had passed away, did your sister really cover the face of your deceased husband with soil and scold you for being ungrateful?" Chen replied, "This was a conversation between me and my humble husband. How could you know all these details?" Lin said, "I am not someone else; I share the same hope of a lifelong union with one person as your sister does." Chen was not fully convinced. Lin had public robes brought for Chen and had people support her, performing the rites for the legitimate and concubine. Chen said, "Even if it's as you say, there's no need to perform such a grand ceremony." Lin replied, "Rituals cannot be abandoned, and social distinctions cannot be crossed." Soon after, Peng returned. Lin welcomed him, saying, "I have come to welcome your first wife." Peng said, "Your words show great kindness." Suddenly, a maid and an elderly woman escorted a lady out. Upon seeing her, it was indeed Chen. Peng was greatly delighted and said to Lin, "You can truly be called a virtuous person." Subsequently, in the government office, drills were conducted. Lin would always watch through a curtain, and rewards and punishments were clearly just. The soldiers all feared and respected him.

    The Immortal Xubai said: Is the theory of geomancy truly credible? There was once a place where the dragon was true and the dragon's lair was precise, surrounded by sand and water. It would surely prosper. However, the family was filled with evil and was despised by everyone in the village. Master Zhu passed by and saw it, saying, "If this place does not prosper, it is because there is no geomancy; if it does prosper, it is because there is no heavenly reason." Indeed, it did not prosper. From this, we can see that there is heavenly reason, and thus there is geomancy. Those who foolishly seek wealth and status without considering their moral conscience, merely seeking geomancers and obsessively choosing auspicious sites, are not foolish and impractical?

    328

    亦天降大任于是人也，而竟若是得之，异矣！ 黄琴轩

    　　龙真穴的，地固吉矣，而亲丧未殡，辄以殓葬之资，公肆博赌，卒至草葬，天理沦没已尽矣。竟妻贤妾美，安享尊荣，岂天网之或有所漏欤？抑别有说欤？秦次山

    　　胡 二 姐

    　　开封宗生，字小坡，仪表可人。读书乡村，门对旷野，每逢烈风急雨，农人多趋避其斋，心虽厌之，无可如何。一日狂风骤至，继以暴雨如注，意野人骚扰在所不免，而竟无一人来，心窃喜。未几，有女子携一包裹，冒雨而来。视之极美，鬓发垂露，眉黛尽湿，葛衣贴体，微露肌肤。入室后，以巾拭面，已而出包中干衣，并不遮避，对生脱去湿衣衣之。生素端方，见女肤如凝脂，情难自制。女衣毕，将湿衣置诸椸上，自言曰：“绣屦止一两，为雨泥所污，心甚恨之。

    Explanation

    328

    Translation:

    Alas, when great responsibilities are entrusted to a person, yet they attain it in such a way, it is indeed strange! — Huang Qinxiang

    A true dragon site, the land is indeed auspicious. However, before the funeral of a close relative, he used the funds for burial to gamble publicly, and in the end, he resorted to a hasty burial, completely abandoning heavenly principles. Yet his wife is virtuous, his concubines are beautiful, and he enjoys honor and respect. Is there a flaw in the heavenly net, or is there another explanation? — Qin Cishan

    Tr. 2nd Sister Hu

    Zongsheng of Kaifeng, courtesy name Xiaopo, was a handsome and well-mannered man. He lived in a rural area, with his door facing an open field. Whenever there were fierce winds and heavy rain, farmers often sought shelter in his study, though he disliked it, he could do nothing about it. One day, a sudden gust of wind was followed by a torrential downpour, and he expected that the people in the wild would inevitably cause some disturbance, but no one came. He secretly rejoiced. Soon after, a woman carrying a bundle came into the house, drenched in the rain. She was extremely beautiful, with her hair and eyebrows soaked by the rain. Her coarse cotton robe clung to her body, slightly revealing her skin. After entering the room, she wiped her face with a handkerchief, then took out dry clothes from her bundle and did not cover herself, instead helping Zongsheng remove his wet clothes and put on the dry ones. Zongsheng, who was usually upright, found her skin as smooth as congealed fat and could not control his emotions. After she had dressed him, she placed the wet clothes on the clothes rack and said to herself, "My embroidered shoes, only one or two pairs, were stained by rain and mud, and I greatly regret it."

    329

    ”乃脱其靴，求生代去泥涂，遂上生榻，坐以俟之。生视之，靴乃五文刺绣，沾濡殆尽，爱而惜之。女来时，已将日暮，移时昏黑，而雨尚未止。生燃灯默坐，女曰：“院中有人否？”曰：“无之。”曰：“君能为坐怀不乱之柳下惠耶？且妾以避雨到此，与君同室宿，即终宵无沾染，亦难自表贞洁。”生闻之大喜，急近女，将为代脱衫裤，而女早赤身以俟。事已，问其里居，女曰：“勿深究，要不远耳。”生虞后会无期，女言明夕自至。女每夕必携嘉肴旨酒与生同食饮。及寝，必强生与合。

    Explanation

    329

    The man then took off his boots and asked the man to remove the mud from them. He then climbed onto the man's bed and sat waiting for him. The man looked at him, and the woman had already stripped herself naked in anticipation. The boots were embroidered with five patterns and were nearly soaked through, and the man admired and cherished them. The woman arrived as dusk approached, and the night grew dark, but the rain still hadn't stopped. The man lit a lamp and sat in silence. The woman asked, "Is there anyone else in the courtyard?" He replied, "No." She then said, "Can you be as virtuous and self-controlled as Zilu (柳下惠)? And I came here to take shelter from the rain, and if we stay together all night without physical contact, it would still be difficult for me to prove my chastity." The man heard this and was greatly pleased. He quickly approached the woman, intending to help her remove her clothes, but she had already stripped herself naked in anticipation. Afterward, he asked where she lived. She said, "Don't probe too deeply, but it's not far." The man worried that they might never meet again, but the woman promised to come again the next evening. Every evening, she would bring fine food and wine to share with the man. When it was time to sleep, she would insist on being intimate with him.

    330

    月馀生归。妻盛氏讶其神气萧索，生实告之。盛大惧，不令生下乡。次夜生梦与女交，醒视则身卧斋中，大惊曰：“吾何以在此？”女曰：“君与盛氏系伉俪，家居数夕不为过，乃欲独擅其美，何可得乎？”生知女非人，固问之。女曰：“妾胡氏，实为狐。”生与胡交已久，明知为狐亦无惧，但畏其太淫耳。嗣生欲归，明以回期语狐，狐亦听之，而生实不敢愆期。盛氏见夫日益惫，恐为狐蛊死，劝夫绝之，而胡不去。生以符禳驱逐，而胡惑之尤甚。自分必死，因书《戒色词》于扇，以触目惊心。词云：“红颜虽好，精气神三宝，都被野狐偷了。眉峰绉，腰肢袅，浓妆淡扫，弄得君枯槁。旷发一枝花，箭射英雄应弦倒。病魔缠绕，空去寻医祷，房术误人不少。这烦恼，自家讨，填精补脑，下手应须早。把凡心打叠，访仙翁，学不老。”

    Explanation

    330

    After more than a month, the husband returned. His wife, Lady Sheng, was surprised by his despondent appearance. He told her the truth. Lady Sheng was greatly alarmed and forbade him from going to the countryside. On the next night, he dreamed of having intercourse with the woman, and upon waking, he found himself lying in the study. He was greatly startled and exclaimed, "Why am I here?" The woman said, "You and Lady Sheng are a married couple; spending several nights at home is not excessive. You cannot monopolize beauty, how could it be possible?" The husband knew the woman was not human and firmly asked her. She replied, "I am Lady Hu, and I am actually a fox spirit." The husband had known her for a long time, and although he knew she was a fox spirit, he had no fear, but he feared her excessive lust. Later, the husband wanted to return home and clearly told the fox spirit his intended return date, and the fox spirit agreed, but the husband was actually afraid to miss the deadline. Lady Sheng saw her husband growing increasingly exhausted and feared that the fox spirit was bewitching him to death. She urged him to sever ties with the fox spirit, but the fox spirit did not leave. The husband used talismans and rituals to drive her away, but the fox spirit became even more bewitched. Believing himself to be doomed, he wrote a cautionary poem on a fan to shock himself upon seeing it. The poem reads:

    "Though beautiful women are indeed beautiful, the three treasures of essence, energy, and spirit have all been stolen by the wild fox. With furrowed brows, slender waist, heavily made up but lightly brushed, you have made me withered and emaciated. A single flower blooms in the wilderness, an arrow shot at a hero causes him to fall. Tormented by illness, in vain seeking medical help and prayers, sexual techniques have misled many. This trouble, self-inflicted, one must replenish essence and brain, and act early. Gather one's mundane thoughts, seek out a Daoist master, and learn immortality."

    331

    一日执扇赴友塾，友见之，曰：“君志在戒色，而身复蹈之，何身心殊异如是？”生实言其苦。友曰：“渠既不可以术逐，未必不可以理论。”生心然之。归与胡曰：“恻隐之心，人物皆有。必令仆死于卿，是不仁也；情同夫妇，则义犹伉俪，而不加体恤，是不义也；仁义人所固有，而卿悉度外置之，是不智也。仆死，卿有此三失。仆死不足惜，恐卿不容于天地间矣。”胡闻之，茫然若失，良久曰：“请从此永别。”生曰：“勿别。

    Explanation

    331

    One day, holding a fan, he went to a friend’s school. The friend saw him and said, “You intend to abstain from sexual desire, yet you fall into it again. How can your mind and body be so different?” The student spoke truthfully about his suffering. The friend said, “If he cannot be chased away with a method, perhaps he can be addressed through reasoning.” The student agreed. Returning home, he said to Hu, “Compassion is a feeling shared by all beings. To demand that I die for you is unkind; if we are as close as husband and wife, then our duty is like that of a married couple, and yet you show no care or consideration—it is unjust. Benevolence and righteousness are inherent in people, yet you place them entirely outside yourself—it is unwise. If I die, you have committed these three faults. My death is not worth lamenting, but I fear you will not be able to exist in the world.” Hu heard this and was茫然若失, after a long while saying, “Please let us say goodbye forever.” The student said, “Do not part.”

    332

    仆病入膏肓，决无生理，请视仆死以为快。”胡恻然曰：“妾非乐君死，盖私情难自制，贵恙不能医耳！”曰：“卿即不能，岂无能者，盍求医于秦以活负情人？”胡默默若有所思，既而曰：“诚有之，但恐不利于妾。”生问其人，答言“妾妹二姐”。生闻之，喜不自胜，揖恳指引。胡若中悔，而驷不及舌，曰：“晚上，妾与君偕往。”及晚，携手同行，路虽黑暗，而觉甚平坦。未几，至一山洞，洞烧高烛，若俟客。既相见，胡曰：“此即小妹二姐。”生视之，颜色之娟，较胡更艳。甫坐，胡曰：“此宗郎。

    Explanation

    332

    My illness has reached the point of no cure, and there is no chance of survival; please see my death for your satisfaction." Hu felt sorry and said, "I am not happy that you will die, it is difficult to control my private feelings, your illness cannot be cured!" The man said, "If you cannot, is there no one who can help? Why not seek a doctor in Qin to save the one who loves you?" Hu was silent as if thinking deeply, and then said, "Truly, there is someone, but I fear it will be harmful to me." The man asked about the person, and she replied, "My younger sister." The man was overjoyed and bowed to ask for guidance. Hu seemed to regret her words, but it was too late to retract them, so she said, "Tonight, I will accompany you." By evening, they walked hand in hand. Although the road was dark, it felt very flat. Soon, they arrived at a mountain cave, where high candles were burning, as if waiting for guests. After they met, Hu said, "This is my younger sister." The man looked at her, and her beauty was even more charming than Hu's. As soon as they sat down, Hu said, "This is Lord Zong."

    333

    姊从事数月，令得虚劳之疾，祈贤妹医治之。”二姐笑曰：“姊何不忍于宗郎？”胡含羞不语。二姐曰：“姊自去，断不令姊大负情郎。”胡坚坐不动，二姐复曰：“姊姊得毋以宗郎强健，不遣归斋与姊欢会耶？”胡羞愧无以自容，始逡巡去。胡去后，生拥女于怀。二姐曰：“君欲何为？病势如此，冥路甚迩。犹欲速死耶？”生曰：“卿美如天人，得亲肌肤，虽死无憾。”女笑曰：“无已，请来日医贵恙。”遂共欢好。二姐曰：“妾姐太淫，业杀三人。兹遇妾，君之福也。但病愈后，须静养月馀。

    Explanation

    333

    The elder sister had been married for several months when she contracted a wasting disease. The younger sister pleaded, "Please treat your illness, elder sister." The younger sister smiled and asked, "Why are you so unkind to your husband, elder sister?" The elder sister blushed and remained silent. The younger sister continued, "I will not leave you to abandon your husband." The elder sister sat firmly and did not move. The younger sister then said, "Are you leaving because you think your husband is strong and healthy, and does not need you?" The elder sister felt ashamed and could not bear it any longer, so she reluctantly left. After the elder sister left, the husband embraced the younger sister. The younger sister asked, "What do you want to do? Your condition is so severe, and the path to the afterlife is near. Do you still wish to die quickly?" The husband replied, "You are as beautiful as a celestial being. To be close to you, even if it means death, I have no regrets." The younger sister laughed and said, "Very well, I will treat your illness tomorrow." They then engaged in a loving relationship. The younger sister added, "My elder sister is too promiscuous and has been involved in the deaths of three people. Now that I have met you, you are fortunate. However, after you recover, you must rest quietly for a month."

    334

    ”生曰：“设令姊复扰奈何？”曰：“妾姊来，设法抵挡，必将从此永别。”生未深信，曰：“仆名已登鬼录，赖卿再生之，欲报高厚，何由屡承妆次？”曰：“明晨妾以五色带赠君，忆妾，束之即至。”次日，女以丹药进生。服药后，令生仰卧，为之遍身按摩，手经处，骨若醉，未几睡去。及醒，病若失，而身仍卧斋中。起视，枕边有五色带，大喜。既而胡来，见生亦喜，曰：“明人不荐医。然非妾指引，病何由愈？”及寝，而生不能人道，胡曰：“婢子负心。”遂去不复来。生归，历言于妻。盛喜极，为之焚香遥遥拜谢。

    Explanation

    334

    The man said, "What if my sister causes trouble again?" She replied, "If my sister comes, we will devise a way to resist, and we will surely part ways forever." The man did not fully believe her, saying, "My name is already recorded in the book of the dead. Thanks to your help, I have been reborn. I want to repay your great kindness, but how can I repeatedly receive your favors?" She said, "Tomorrow morning, I will give you a five-colored belt. If you miss me, tie it and I will come." The next day, the woman gave the man a pill. After taking the medicine, she made the man lie down and massaged his entire body. Wherever her hands touched, his bones felt as if they were drunk, and soon he fell asleep. When he woke up, his illness seemed to have disappeared, and he was still lying in his study. He got up and saw a five-colored belt next to his pillow, and he was very happy. Later, Hu came, and seeing the man, she was also happy, saying, "A wise person does not recommend a doctor. However, without my guidance, how could your illness be cured?" When they went to bed, the man could not perform, and Hu said, "The servant has a fickle heart." She then left and never came back. The man returned home and told his wife everything. His wife was extremely happy and burned incense to thank her from a distance.

    335

    一月后，生欲赴二姐之约。盛意去一狐，复交一狐，恐为所伤，竭力劝止之。生不听，潜束带而去，情意之笃，倍于胡。次日，即促生归，且择定日期，一月止许会面一次。生按期往来。一日为公事过期，欲次日往，竟束带无灵。下月，又为阴雨所阻，以故三月未赴。适盛生子六日殇，生欲诉其苦于二姐，正逢会期，束带而往。见呱呱者在床，曰：“是谁氏之子？”二姐笑曰：“妾生之，不知其为谁氏子？”生曰：“仆多此一问，无怪卿以是言为对。”遂将盛生子殇语之。

    Explanation

    335

    One month later, the man wished to keep an appointment with his second sister. With great intention, he got rid of one fox and then met another, fearing that he would be hurt. He tried his best to stop him, but the man did not listen and secretly put on formal attire and left. The depth of his affection was more than twice that of Hu. The next day, he was immediately urged to return, and a date was selected, allowing only one meeting per month. The man went back and forth according to schedule. One day, he missed the deadline due to official duties and intended to go the next day, but putting on formal attire was ineffective. The following month, he was again blocked by rain, and thus did not visit for three months. Just then, Sheng's son died six days after birth. The man wanted to tell his sorrow to his second sister, and it happened to be the appointed meeting day. He put on formal attire and went. Seeing a crying baby on the bed, he asked, "Who is this child?" The second sister smiled and said, "I gave birth to him, but I don't know who his father is." The man replied, "I asked this question unnecessarily; it's not surprising that you answered like that." He then told her about the death of Sheng's son.

    336

    二姐曰：“妾正虑无方养育此子，君襁负去，交盛姊乳之。即谓姊子未殇，人应信之。”生如女言，携去给盛。盛大喜，保如己出，命名寄生。至六七岁，二姐忽思之，令生携去一视。既去，二姐不令同生归。生曰：“盛氏不见寄生，势将想死。”曰：“下月令归，不食言。”生不得已自回。盛果怼之曰：“与子同往，不与子同来，听渠挽留，君何畏之甚也？且妾养育数年，渠即欲子还，亦宜善言，乃何诳去而强留之？”生曰：“卿勿言，下月子不归，仆也任其咎。”嗣生果偕子归，盛始喜。后寄生同生往来，盛亦莫之疑也。

    Explanation

    336

    The second sister said, "I am just worried about how to raise this child. You should carry him away in swaddling clothes and hand him over to my sister to nurse. Then we can claim that the child is still alive, and people will believe us." The husband followed her advice and took the child to give to Mistress Sheng. Mistress Sheng was very happy and treated the child as if it were her own, naming him Jisheng. When the child was six or seven years old, the second sister suddenly missed him and told the husband to take him back for a visit. After they left, the second sister did not allow the husband to return with the child. The husband said, "If Mistress Sheng does not see Jisheng, she will be extremely worried." The second sister replied, "Next month, I will let him return, and I will not break my word." The husband, having no choice, returned alone. Mistress Sheng angrily rebuked him, saying, "You went with him, but you did not bring him back. You let him stay, why are you so afraid? And I have raised him for several years. If he wants the child back, he should kindly ask for him. Why did you deceive him away and forcibly keep him?" The husband said, "Please do not say more. If the child does not return next month, I will take full responsibility." Later, the husband indeed returned with the child, and Mistress Sheng became happy. Afterward, Jisheng and the husband visited each other, and Mistress Sheng also did not suspect anything.

    337

    一日，生见二姐双眉愁锁，异而问之。曰：“适以术卜君寿数，不意若是之短而促也。”生曰：“尚有阳算几何？”曰：“近在五六年。”生惧，求济于女。女曰：“妾实不能为力。”俯首沉吟，良久曰：“某日午刻，有叶仙师过府署前，破巾絮衣长髯，肩负蒲团者是。君见而尾之，至无人处跪求师之，若得收录，君可飞升，妾亦得附骥尾远劫数矣。”生如女言，果得师事仙师。至生将终之日，生归，无病卒。盛以祸延仓猝，无所措手，深以为忧。俄来一少妇，衣重孝，呼天而泣。举家不识，寄生见之，曰：“吾母也。”因投女怀而泣，以触女恸，皆失声。盛对灵饮泣，二姐劝之曰：“良人弃吾二人长逝，皆妹与姊命薄妨害之，哭之无益，不能活也。”遂出资致办丧具，衣衾棺椁，备极华美。盛甚德之。至殡之前一夜，盛梦夫与二姐谈笑中庭，既而同去。及晓，二姐果杳。大疑，潜启棺视之，盖空棺也。盛知夫仙去，乃瘗空棺以隐其事。

    Explanation

    337

    One day, the man saw the second sister's brows furrowed in worry and asked her about it. She said, "Just now I used divination to predict your lifespan, unaware that it would be so short and urgent." The man replied, "How many years of life remain?" She answered, "Nearly five to six years." The man was afraid and sought help from the woman. She said, "I indeed cannot exert effort." Bowed head and pondered deeply, after a long time she said, "On a certain day at noon, there is a Daoist master named Ye passing by in front of the government office, torn headscarf, patched robe, and long beard, carrying a straw mat on his shoulder. You see and follow him, go to a place where no one is and kneel to ask him. If you get accepted, you can ascend to immortality, and I can also attach to the horse's tail and distance the calamity." The man followed her words, and indeed got to serve as a disciple to the Daoist master. On the day when the man was about to die, he returned, died without illness. The wife because the disaster extended suddenly, no place to do, deeply considered as worry. Suddenly came a young woman, wore heavy mourning clothes, called heaven and cried. All the family did not recognize, the wife saw it and said, "My mother." Then threw herself into her embrace and cried, because touched her grief, all lost their voice. The wife faced the spirit and cried silently, the second sister advised her and said, "Husband abandoned us two and long departed, all the younger sister and older sister's fate thin harm, cry for no benefit, cannot live." So she contributed money to arrange the funeral preparations, clothing, coffin, and casket, all extremely luxurious. The wife greatly appreciated her. On the night before the burial, the wife dreamed of her husband and the second sister chatting in the courtyard, then they left together. By morning, the second sister indeed disappeared. Greatly puzzled, she secretly opened the coffin to look inside, it was an empty coffin. The wife knew her husband had ascended to immortality, so she buried the empty coffin to conceal the matter.

    338

    虚白道人曰：狐可以理服，况人乎？世之以横逆加人者，或其人之仁礼与忠有未至耳，盖与禽兽无择，实为妄人者，今亦罕有也。

    Explanation

    338

    The Daoist recluse said: A fox can be persuaded by reason; how much more so, then, a human being? Those who impose harshness on others may lack benevolence, propriety, and loyalty, behaving no differently from beasts. Those who are truly deluded are now rare.

    339

    戒色词可诵，驱狐术甚奇。 马竹吾

    　　宗生何幸而得胡氏，又何幸而得二姐？ 何子英

    　　遇狐而病，事之常；再遇狐而仙，文之幻。读者戒慎其常而无侥幸至幻也，则几矣。 杨子厚

    　　某 公 子

    　　二邑巨家，结儿女之亲，余仅闻女家世籍齐邑。嗣女双目失明，女父以女不堪嫔富室，敬烦原媒退婚。男父曰：“婚既结而复退，世间岂有是理！然娶媳双失目，亦非细故。”言已，俯首踌躇。媒曰：“此系女家情愿，非君父子二三其德。”公曰：“虽然，事关伦常，不宜轻诺。”言际，有五尺之童在侧，即女之婿也，忽插言曰：“媳妇失目则议退婚，设吾失目，岳家断不敢生退婚之心。”公曰：“汝不嫌乎？”曰：“命也，何嫌之有？”男父笑谓媒曰：“勿议此，童子欲之，天缘定矣。”嗣瞽女及笄，于归时，女父市美女为婢陪送之，而婿惟笃夫妇之义，婢妾则备员而已。后五子登科，悉瞽夫人所生，婢妾无出焉。

    Explanation

    339

    The Words of Abstaining from Lust are worth reciting, and the Method of Driving Away Fox Spirits is indeed extraordinary. Ma Zhugu

    How fortunate is Zongsheng to have married into the Hu family, and how fortunate to have gained two sisters-in-law? He Ziyi

    Encountering a fox spirit and falling ill is a common occurrence; encountering a fox spirit again and becoming a deity is a fantastical event. Readers should be cautious of the common occurrences and not hope for the fantastical outcomes, then they will be close to understanding. Yang Zihou

    A Certain Gentleman

    Two prominent families from neighboring counties arranged a marriage alliance between their children, and I only heard that the girl's family was originally from Qi County. The daughter was born blind, and the father of the blind daughter, considering her condition, asked the original matchmaker to arrange for the cancellation of the marriage. The father of the groom said, "Having arranged a marriage and then canceling it, is there such a thing in the world! However, marrying a blind woman is also a serious matter." He paused, looking down and hesitating. The matchmaker said, "This is the decision made by the girl's family, not due to any change in the intentions of your family." The gentleman said, "Although so, this concerns family ethics, and one should not make a hasty promise." At that moment, a young boy of five feet tall stood by, who was the groom, and suddenly interjected, "If the wife is blind, then we can discuss canceling the marriage, but if I were blind, the in-laws would certainly not consider canceling the marriage." The gentleman said, "Are you not bothered by this?" The boy replied, "It is fate, what is there to be bothered about?" The father of the groom smiled and told the matchmaker, "Do not discuss this further, the boy desires it, it is a matter of fate." Later, the blind daughter came of age, and at her wedding, the father of the blind daughter provided beautiful girls as maidservants to accompany her. The groom was devoted to his wife, while the maidservants merely fulfilled their roles. Afterward, the couple had five sons, all born to the blind wife, and the maidservants did not bear children.

    340

    虚白道人曰：凡人至十馀岁时，已知女之妍媸，慕少艾者比比也。某婿之言，固人所不能言，亦人所不欲言也。人不能者而能之，其能奇；人不欲者而欲之，其欲尤奇。五子登科，固缘祖功宗德，然生五子之夫人，其福命亦不同寻常也。庭前五桂，媲美燕山，谓非不嫌瞽妇之所致哉？

    Explanation

    340

    The Recluse of Pure White said: By the age of ten or more, ordinary people have already known the beauty and ugliness of women, and those who admire young men are everywhere. The husband's statement, though not something that can be said, is also something that is not desired to be said. Being able to say what others cannot is indeed unusual; desiring what others do not desire is even more unusual. The success of five sons passing the imperial examinations is certainly due to the ancestral merit and virtue of the family. However, the woman who bore the five sons also had an extraordinary fortune. The five laurel trees in front of the courtyard rival the beauty of Yanshan, is it not due to the blindness of the woman?

    341

    即不嫌瞽妇一节推之，生平之盛德可想。作善降祥，事虽异，而理则常也。 马竹吾

    　　此亦笃夫妇伦之报也。 黄琴轩

    　　五尺之童有此卓识，伟哉！ 何子英

    　　己所不欲，勿施于人，大贤以下所难。某公子之言，只是恕字勘得透耳？行文亦复简洁。 杨子厚

    　　白猿

    　　闽中多猿，孤吟夜月，长啸云山者，在在有之。有货草扇客，肩担若干，将市于山庄。路经山麓，憩于树下，取扇自挥，以祛溽暑；为诸猿窥见，悉至，各执一扇，四散摇之。肩担贸易，咸系贫人，扇悉为诸猿执去，资本尽丧，不禁大声叫苦。适有耘田农人，植其锄，走向卖扇者曰：“勿以此为苦，欲令诸猿还扇亦自易。”客急求术。农人曰：“客居他乡，不知猿性。君执扇急摇数摇，大言曰：‘此扇无用！’急掷于地，猿必效之。

    341

    If we consider the one aspect of not being bothered by a blind woman, one can imagine the great virtues of his life. Good deeds bring good fortune, the events may be different, but the principle remains constant. — Ma Zhuwu

    This is also the reward for being devoted to the relationship between husband and wife. — Huang Qinxuan

    A child of five feet tall has such remarkable insight, how great! — He Ziying

    Do not do to others what you do not want done to yourself, even the great sages find it difficult. The words of a certain nobleman, it's just that he understands the principle of forgiveness thoroughly. The writing is also concise. — Yang Zihou

    The White Ape

    There are many apes in Fujian, those who sing alone under the moon at night and howl among the clouds and mountains, they are everywhere. There was a merchant carrying some goods, about to sell in a mountain village. Passing through the foot of the mountain, resting under a tree, taking a fan to wave, to dispel the sultry heat; seen by the apes, all came, each holding a fan, waving them around. Carrying goods for trade, all were poor people, the fans were all taken by the apes, losing all capital, could not help but loudly complain. Just then there was a farmer plowing the fields, putting down his hoe, walking towards the fan seller and said, "Do not be troubled by this, if you want the apes to return the fans, it is easy to do." The merchant urgently asked for a method. The farmer said, "Living away from home, not knowing the nature of the apes. You hold the fan and quickly wave it several times, loudly say, 'This fan is useless!' quickly throw it on the ground, the apes will surely imitate you."

    342

    ”客试之，果如农人言。又一客，推雨笠一车，值天乍晴，停车戴笠，以遮赤日。群猿见之，各取一笠，悉于山坡危坐戴之。客视其笠，失去四十馀顶，心虽痛恨，无可如何。后来一人，见之大笑。客怒曰：“吾恚而君喜，何幸灾乐祸如是！”其人曰：“非也。吾观众猿情形，宛如小儿嬉戏，殊属可笑。吾有一术，可令众猿立还君笠。吾与君伪为打降，作怒君戴笠之势，君伪为畏我，急摘笠掷之，猿必学君之举动，而笠可全得。”试之，果然。猿之性如此，无足异也。

    Explanation

    342

    A guest tried it, and indeed as the farmer said. Another guest pushed a cart full of rain hats. When the sky suddenly cleared, he stopped the cart and put on a hat to shade himself from the sun. The monkeys saw this and each took one hat, all sitting upright on the hillside wearing them. The guest lost over forty hats and, though his heart was filled with regret, could do nothing about it. Later, another person saw this and laughed. The guest, angered, said, "I am angry and you are happy, why do you take pleasure in others' misfortune like this?" The person replied, "No, it is not so. I observed the situation of the monkeys, and it was like children playing, it was particularly laughable. I have a method that can make the monkeys return your hats immediately. I and you will pretend to fight, making a gesture of anger at you wearing the hat, and you will pretend to fear me, quickly taking off the hat and throwing it. The monkeys will surely imitate your actions, and you can get all the hats back." When they tried it, indeed so. The nature of the monkeys is like this, there is nothing unusual about it.

    343

    车照者，山外人也。家贫亲老，惟日樵柴市钱，为奉养之资。去山少远，晨往夕归，常带干糇以充午饥，往往为众猿窃食之。一日，樵未毕而已饥，趋赴藏饵所，视之，已为众猿食尽，大怒，自言曰：“尔屡食我之食，不少分我之劳，岂不羞愧？”言已，愤理樵事。猿听之，若喻车意，既而同代采取，聚少成多，不逾时，已足一肩。车摇手止之，猿乃罢。次日，车多带干糇，分食众猿，猿服劳益力，日可得柴两肩，后以为常。一日车至，不见一猿，忽闻猿啼急而哀，疑之。俄而，众猿自山下见车，群拽之行。

    Explanation

    343

    Chariot Zhao was a person from outside the mountains. His family was poor and his parents were old, so he only engaged in daily chopping of wood to sell for money, as a means of support and sustenance. He lived a bit far from the mountains, going there in the morning and returning at dusk, always carrying dried food to satisfy his midday hunger, which was often stolen by the monkeys. One day, before he had finished chopping, he became hungry and rushed to where he had hidden his food. Upon seeing it, he found that it had all been eaten by the monkeys, and he became very angry. He spoke to himself, saying, "You have repeatedly eaten my food without sharing any of the labor, do you not feel ashamed?" After speaking, he angrily continued chopping wood. The monkeys listened to him, as if understanding Chariot Zhao's intention. They then took turns gathering, and gradually accumulated enough wood to fill one shoulder. Chariot Zhao shook his hands to stop them, and the monkeys ceased. The next day, Chariot Zhao brought more dried food and shared it with the monkeys. The monkeys worked harder and more diligently, and each day they could gather enough wood to fill two shoulders. This became their regular routine. One day, when Chariot Zhao arrived, he did not see any monkeys. Suddenly, he heard the monkeys crying urgently and sadly, which made him suspicious. Soon after, the monkeys appeared from the mountain and pulled him along.

    344

    车从之，至一深涧，立崖之半有横石一片，一白猿坠其上，上下不可得，故哀鸣也。众猿指视，若急求济救者。车欲取绳于樵所，猿不听行，手语之，猿始放车回。车取绳至，以绳一首授白猿。白猿不解结绳。车乃以绳系涧边古木，执之而下，系猿腰，上而提之。既救白猿，仍回采樵，群猿助之如故，白猿坐视如监。有顷，白猿去而复还，以巨叶包食物令车食。车视之，如白蜜，未敢遽尝。猿先食以示无毒，车乃食。其味如桃，食尽三分之一，未食时饥甚，食后不惟充饥，更觉精神倍爽，筋骨强健，心知其异，遂留之以进双亲。亲食之，悉弃杖而步，俨同少壮，车大喜。

    Explanation

    344

    A cart followed it to a deep ravine, where halfway up a cliff stood a horizontal stone. A white ape had fallen onto the stone, unable to get up or down, and thus cried out in sorrow. The other apes pointed and looked, as if urgently seeking rescue. The cart wanted to fetch a rope from the place where woodcutters were, but the apes did not allow it to go, gesturing with their hands. Finally, the apes allowed the cart to return. The cart fetched the rope and gave one end to the white ape. The white ape did not know how to tie the rope. The cart then tied the rope to an ancient tree by the ravine, held onto it, and descended, tying it around the ape's waist, and went up to pull it. After saving the white ape, the cart still returned to gather firewood, and the other apes helped as before. The white ape sat watching like a supervisor. After a while, the white ape left and then returned, wrapping food in large leaves and giving it to the cart to eat. The cart looked at it and it looked like white honey, but did not dare to taste it immediately. The ape ate it first to show it was not poisonous, and the cart then ate it. It tasted like peaches, and the cart ate a third of it. Before eating, the cart was very hungry, and after eating, not only did it satisfy hunger, but it also made one feel more spirited and the body stronger. Knowing in his heart that it was a strange thing, the cart kept it to present to his parents. The parents ate it and all threw away their walking sticks and walked, looking as if they were young and strong. The cart was very happy.

    345

    一日，货柴得白金数钱，缠置腰间，忘留于家。心恐遗失，时出检视，为白猿所见，若知银为人世之急需，遂去。未几，衔一银来，置车前。车视之，约有五十两，大喜曰：“尚有之否？”猿额之，复去，终日得五六枚。半月后，猿止衔二枚来，不复去，车曰：“无矣乎？”猿点头。车心愿已足，暂憩片时，将自归。猿意车坐待其取，遂急去，多时不至。车将归而猿适来，其行甚缓，有极不得意之势。口衔一物，去车少远即吐之，若有恐车见厌弃之意。车趋视之，赤金也，喜而指之曰：“此较白者价贵。”猿闻之跃起。车重言之，白猿跳跃去，不多时，衔一条来。车曰：“日之夕矣，可小休。”遂归。

    Explanation

    345

    One day, Merchant Huo obtained several taels of silver, wrapped and placed them around his waist, forgetting to leave them at home. Worried about losing them, he frequently took them out to check, and a white monkey saw him. The monkey seemed to know that silver was urgently needed in the world and then left. Soon after, the monkey carried a piece of silver and placed it in front of the cart. Huo looked at it, and it was about fifty taels. He was greatly delighted and said, "Are there any more?" The monkey nodded, and then left again. Throughout the day, he received five or six pieces. After half a month, the monkey only carried two pieces and did not leave again. Huo said, "Are there no more?" The monkey nodded. Huo's wish was fulfilled, so he rested for a short while and was about to return home. The monkey thought the cart was waiting for it to take something and hurriedly left, but did not return for a long time. Just as Huo was about to return, the monkey arrived, moving very slowly with an extremely dissatisfied appearance. It carried something in its mouth and dropped it a short distance away, as if fearing the cart would see and reject it. Huo hurried to look at it and saw that it was red gold. He was delighted and pointed at it, saying, "This is more valuable than the white one." The monkey heard him and jumped up. Huo repeated the statement, and the white monkey jumped away. In no time, it carried a piece of gold. Huo said, "The day is late; it is time for a short rest." Then he returned home.

    346

    车复得赤金千馀两，财雄一乡，不忘所自，每具食物，大食群猿，岁五六次。车父母悉享期寿。车百廿馀岁，犹强健如五六十岁人。车之入山食群猿也，呼哨一声，众猿立至，人咸戏以猴王称之。

    　　虚白道人曰：观白猿之于车照，可谓以德报德矣。然猿一兽耳，安知报复？是车无图报之心可知也。而猿终有以厚报之，是可知有德于人者终有报之之人，人有报之之时，其厚福正未可量也。若无德者，其何所几期？

    Explanation

    346

    Chē once again obtained several thousand taels of red gold, becoming wealthy in one village, and never forgot his origins. He often prepared food and fed the group of monkeys generously, doing so five or six times a year. Chē’s parents all enjoyed their full lifespan. Chē was over a hundred and twenty years old, still strong and healthy like someone in their fifties or sixties. When Chē entered the mountains to feed the monkeys, he would whistle once, and the monkeys would immediately come. People often jokingly called him the Monkey King.

    The Daoist Master Xūbái said: Observing the white ape’s relationship with Chē, one could say it was a case of virtue repaying virtue. However, the ape is merely an animal; how could it understand the concept of repayment? It is clear that Chē had no intention of seeking repayment. Yet, the ape eventually rewarded him generously, showing that those who have virtue towards others will ultimately be repaid by someone. There will be a time when they are repaid, and the great blessings they receive are truly immeasurable. But for those without virtue, what can they hope for?

    347

    情真景真事真意真，真令人目不暇赏。 何子英

    　　晋宋人《州郡记》多此等体，在录中又是一样笔墨。才人文字，固无所不有。 马竹吾

    　　猿有“尾君子”之称。若此猿者，真可谓君子矣。上元李瑜谨注

    　　某乙

    　　一孀妇生有二子：某甲、某乙。乙不孝不弟，居恒不知恭兄。与甲同居，私蓄货财，甲亦不敢遏止之。母偶为盘诘，辄恶声以相向。其犯上之举，擢发难数。乙私蓄意足，欲与甲分居各爨。甲曰：“各爨可耳。坡地数亩，可留为养亲之资，何必分？”乙疾视曰：“更迭奉养，何需资？”甲唯唯。

    　　既分，兄贫弟富，更兼饥馑相因，甲衣食不足，竭力奉事，尚幸不至冻馁。某乙家食有美疏，而以疏者奉母。每食无馀，兼有不饱。偶不饥食少，乙疑母嫌食疏，辄怒目曰：“似此饭食，某甲一日亦未必两进。”母闻之，怒不敢言，惟于无人时饮泣而已。

    Explanation

    347

    Translation:

    The emotions, scenery, events, and intentions are all genuine, truly making one's eyes unable to take it all in. — He Ziyi

    The State and County Records of the Jin and Song people often contain such styles, and in the record, they are another kind of writing. The writings of talented people are indeed without limits. — Ma Zhuwu

    Monkeys are known as "Tail Gentlemen." If this monkey is truly called a gentleman, it is truly a gentleman. — Annotated by Li Yu of Shangyuan

    Mouyi

    A widow had two sons: Moujia and Mouyi. Mouyi was disrespectful to his parents and not respectful to his elder brother, always without showing respect to his elder brother. Living together with Moujia, he secretly hoarded wealth, and Moujia also dared not stop him. When the mother occasionally questioned him, he would respond with harsh words. His acts of defiance against his superiors were too numerous to count. Mouyi, having enough of his secret hoarding, wanted to separate from Moujia and live separately. Moujia said, "Living separately is fine. There are several acres of sloping land, which can be left as a resource for supporting our parents, why separate?" Mouyi looked at him angrily and said, "Taking turns in providing support, what need is there for resources?" Moujia nodded in agreement.

    After the separation, the elder brother was poor while the younger brother was rich, and they both suffered from famine. Moujia, with insufficient food and clothing, served with utmost effort, still fortunately without freezing or starving. In Mouyi's home, there were fine vegetables, but he offered poor quality food to his mother. Each meal had no leftovers, and sometimes there was not even enough to satisfy hunger. When he ate less than usual, Mouyi suspected that his mother disliked the poor quality food, and he would glare at her and say, "Such food, Moujia might not even eat twice a day." The mother, upon hearing this, was angry but dared not speak, and she could only weep in private when no one was around.

    348

    一月，甲应奉养，而甑冷囊空，一日不能两餐，遂谓母曰：“祈暂就食于弟，吾少有进项，即去奉请。且母在弟家住几日，吾嗣后如数补之，亦无亏于弟。”母从之。次日早去，乙见而问之，母以甲言告。乙曰：“吾不能。无力奉事，甲短于才；从其饥饿，母蹇于命。于吾何与？”曰：“吾住此几日，汝兄按日补之。”乙曰：“吾仍不欲。譬如借给他人钱，毫无利息，俟月馀始楚之，出钱者有何利益？”乙母曰：“惟然，汝炊饭已熟，吾饭后自去。”乙怒曰：“母何痛甲不痛吾？费在吾而省在甲，惟母欲之，吾实不能如命。”曰：“吾非痛汝兄，实饥耳。”乙曰：“母即饥，宜速回食于甲。吾饭即分给贫人，决不令母食。”母怒，欲强食之。乙妻力按锅盖，不听母启。乙复在外大呼兄名，口出不逊。母羞愧难堪，大哭而去。未出庄，忽无云而雷，其声甚厉，回首见乙家烟火飞腾。急回视之，釜破饭扬，乙夫妇不惟身首异处，其骨肉粉碎无算矣。

    Explanation

    348

    In the first month, Jia should have been supporting his mother, but he was in dire poverty, with his cooking pot cold and his money bag empty. He could not have two meals a day. One day, he said to his mother, "Please go and stay with your younger brother temporarily. I will have a little income soon and will come to serve you. And while you stay at your younger brother's place for a few days, I will compensate him fully afterward, without causing any loss to him." The mother agreed and left. The next morning, Yi saw her and asked why she had come. The mother told him what Jia had said. Yi replied, "I cannot. I lack the ability to serve, and Jia lacks talent. Let him starve, and the mother has a hard fate. What does this have to do with me?" The mother said, "I will stay here for a few days, and your brother will compensate you daily." Yi said, "I still do not want to. It is like lending money to others with no interest; after a month or more, the lender gains nothing." Yi's mother said, "That is true. You have already cooked the rice; I will leave after I eat." Yi became angry and said, "Why do you pity my brother but not me? The expense is on me, and the savings are for him. It is only what you want, but I really cannot comply." The mother said, "I am not pitying your brother; I am really hungry." Yi said, "If you are hungry, you should quickly return to eat with Jia. I will give my rice to the poor and will never let you eat." The mother became angry and tried to force him to eat. Yi's wife firmly held the pot lid and would not let the mother open it. Yi then shouted his brother's name loudly outside and spoke disrespectfully. The mother was ashamed and could not bear it, so she cried bitterly and left. Before she left the village, suddenly there was thunder without clouds, and the sound was very loud. Looking back, she saw smoke and fire rising from Yi's house. She quickly turned back to look and saw the pot broken and the rice scattered. Yi and his wife were not only separated head from body; their bones and flesh were shattered beyond count.

    349

    虚白道人曰：某乙之事，闻者莫不痛心切齿，恨造物竟生是人也，世间竟有是人也。其报应在当时，洵痛快人心也！

    Explanation

    349

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: "The matter concerning Mr. X, when heard, causes everyone to be deeply grieved and furious, hating that the Creator has produced such a person, and marveling that the world has such a person. The retribution for his actions occurs in the present, indeed, it is a relief to the heart."

    350

    极力翻腾，深得蓄势之法。 何子英

    　　有天理。 渔樵散人志

    　　阿娇

    　　佛山赏菊，济南八景之一也。菊开时，即石缝径侧亦悉露团香叶，风舞异葩。每逢重阳，城中外妇女，多登山拜佛，且采菊以应时令。风流人士，亦三五成群，山上乎逍遥。遇美人，辄假同途，恣情月旦。
