From this, we can infer that he was without flattery when in a humble position, without greed when faced with wealth, and acted in the world without harming others for personal gain. He did not engage in oppressive taxation or exploitation. All these qualities can be trusted. Therefore, it is not accidental that extreme poverty allows one to marry into a wealthy family and raise a son who attracts wealth and prosperity.

    396

    秦 丽 娟

    　　仪征诸生茅大受，以岁试扬州，与江都生秦德元相识。财丑分齐，遂成莫逆。杯酒之间，因而一诺成姻，盖茅之子、秦之女也。嗣茅室式微，秦欲退婚，其妻不平，日相诟谇。秦终以无婚帖为辞。茅知之，讼秦。听讼者亦以茅为无据，不得直，气愤而卒。

    　　初，茅与秦结亲后，尝携子瑜赴郡，馆于秦室。时两小无猜，乳母或戏之曰：“茅相公要媳妇否？”瑜曰：“要。”曰：“若汝丽姊，好否？”曰：“好。但恐姊之嫌吾。”丽曰：“不嫌。”丽娟渐长，家人犹述是言以为戏。女闻之，羞甚，家人始相戒不言。兹女闻父悔婚，恒饮泣不食。母慰之曰：“勿悲，汝父之昏命，吾终不依从。”瑜年已十六，欲上控无资，不得已，思往岳家诟辱以泄忿。至，则秦适出门未归。母见之，急呼女见茅郎。女来，约入室而母去，并嘱家人勿前。信宿，厚赠而行。秦归，云已以女许于某绅。女知之，夙夜隐忧。幸某身膺重服，迎娶尚迟时日。

    Explanation

    396

    Qin Li Juan

    Mao Dashi, a scholar from Yizheng, became acquainted with Qin Deyuan from Jiangdu during his annual examination in Yangzhou. They had a disagreement over wealth and social status, but they became inseparable friends. In a moment of drinking, they made a promise of marriage, with Mao's son and Qin's daughter as the couple. After the decline of Mao's family, Qin wanted to break off the engagement. Qin's wife was unhappy and often scolded him. Qin finally used the lack of a marriage contract as an excuse. Mao knew about it and sued Qin. The judge also found no evidence against Mao and did not receive justice, dying of anger.

    At the beginning, after Mao and Qin became engaged, Mao once took his son Yu to the prefectural capital and stayed at Qin's house. At that time, the two children were innocent, and the wet nurse sometimes teased them, saying, "Does Master Mao want a wife?" Yu said, "Yes." She said, "How about your beautiful sister?" He said, "Good. But I'm afraid my sister will dislike me." Li said, "Not dislike." Li Juan grew up, and the family still told this story as a joke. When Li heard it, she was very embarrassed, and the family began to warn each other not to say it. This girl heard that her father wanted to break off the engagement and always wept and did not eat. Her mother comforted her, saying, "Do not be sad, your father's decision, I will never follow." Yu was already sixteen and wanted to file a lawsuit but had no money. He had no choice but to think of going to Qin's house to insult and humiliate him to vent his anger. When he arrived, Qin had just left and was not yet back. Qin's mother saw him and quickly called Li to see Mao's son. Li came, and they went into the room while the mother left, and she also told the family not to come forward. After several days, they gave generous gifts and left. When Qin returned, he said that he had already promised his daughter to a certain gentleman. Li knew about it and was worried day and night. Fortunately, the gentleman was wearing heavy mourning clothes, and the wedding would be delayed for some time.

    397

    未几，丽以娠语母，母亦扼腕。比邻有布商毛某，杭州人，每二年一回家。其妻于氏爱丽娟幽闲，认为义女，时相往来。女乃自诣于室，含泣长跪。于大惊，详问之。既而曰：“矢志靡他，女儿家第一美事，固人人乐于成全者。若果生男，请为代育。”比生子，始与夫携之去。此时丽虽难以为情，然较弃诸梦中犹愈也。绅某将服阙，迨吉有日，女与婢春兰宵遁。次日，借宿农家，实即秦之佃户，暗报于秦。秦正觅女，得佃户信，立乘马去。女在佃家，佃妇意极殷勤，夜已阑，不言寝所，女疑之。忽闻外言主人至，纷纷出迓。俄一美人入曰：“卿父来矣，可从吾去暂避之。”女与春兰遇父于门，见父负气入，不知何以不见自己也。

    397

    Not long after, Li informed her mother of her pregnancy, and her mother was also distressed. A neighboring cloth merchant named Mao, from Hangzhou, returned home every two years. His wife, Yu, loved Li's gentle and quiet nature and adopted her as a foster daughter, often visiting each other. Li then went to Yu's room, weeping while kneeling for a long time. Yu was surprised and asked her in detail. Later, she said, "Sworn to remain faithful to one person is the first virtue of a woman, something that everyone is happy to help with. If a boy is born, please take care of him." When the child was born, the father took him away with him. At this time, Li found it hard to bear emotionally, but it was better than being abandoned in a dream. A certain official would soon complete his mourning period, and when the day for a joyous occasion arrived, Li and her maid Chunlan fled secretly at night. The next day, they stayed overnight at a farmer's house, which turned out to be Qin's tenant. The tenant secretly informed Qin, who was looking for the daughter. Receiving the information, Qin immediately rode away on horseback. While staying at the tenant's house, the tenant's wife was very hospitable but did not mention where to sleep, causing Li to suspect something. Suddenly, Li heard that the owner had arrived, and everyone went out to greet. Soon, a beautiful woman entered and said, "Your father has come. You can follow me to hide temporarily." Li and Chunlan met her father at the gate, saw him enter in a bad mood, and did not understand why he did not see her.

    398

    秦入，不见女，与佃妇俱不解其何故，怅怅而返。丽娟从美人出，春兰随之，同入一山洞，心知美人为仙，拜问之。曰：“非仙也，狐也。但妹不从吾来，尊父必舁去强配某绅。妹曾与茅郎同枕衾，是二夫也，且知妹矢志不为。”曰：“字之耳，实未会面。”狐笑曰：“妹尚忆‘郎君且慢’、‘郎君且慢’之言乎？”丽闻之，红飞满面，曰：“当时姊在何处也？”曰：“狐界居阴阳之间，故人不得见。且天下有不淫之女，无不淫之狐，但畏天谴，非有夙缘不敢肆行妄为。然于男女婚媾，每乐视其声音笑貌，以广见闻，以为此真人世间之大兴会也。惜乎！人不如狐。”春兰曰：“仙姑既施慈惠，盍使得见茅郎以成阙志。

    Explanation

    398

    The Qin entered and did not see the girl, and along with the tenant woman, did not understand the reason why, and returned with a sense of disappointment. Lijuan followed the beautiful woman out, Chunlan followed her, and they entered a mountain cave together. Knowing in her heart that the beautiful woman was a fairy, she bowed and asked her. The woman said, "I am not a fairy, but a fox. But if the sister does not come with me, your respected father will carry her away and forcibly match her with a certain gentleman. The sister once shared the same bed with Mola, making her two husbands, and I know the sister has sworn not to do so." Lijuan said, "I only promised him, but we never actually met." The fox laughed and said, "Does the sister still remember the words 'Gentleman, please wait,' 'Gentleman, please wait'?" Lijuan heard this and her face turned red, saying, "Where was my sister at that time?" The fox replied, "The fox realm exists between the realms of yin and yang, so humans cannot see it. And while there are chaste women in the world, there are no chaste foxes. But we fear heavenly retribution and dare not act recklessly without a predestined bond. However, in matters of marriage between men and women, we often observe their voices and appearances to broaden our knowledge, thinking this is a great gathering in the human world. Alas! Humans are not as good as foxes." Chunlan said, "Since the fairy has bestowed kindness, why not allow her to see Mola to fulfill her unfulfilled wish?"

    399

    ”狐曰：“尚未。迟速离合亦有定数，如婢子言，则天下无怨女旷夫矣。俟某绅死，茅郎父子俱贵，此其时也。”女闻是言，知生子事狐姊亦洞晓，遂欲从之学炼真术。狐曰：“自古无不忠孝之神仙。汝虽得逃避于此，茅郎与母今疏食不饱，奈何？吾有积金，汝弗亲奉之以为先容？”乃出绣履一双，令女著之，曰：“此即不患跋涉矣。”不数步，觉行空如云。未几，至一大门，见墙垣倾圮，老屋残灯未息，茅母兀坐，茅郎侍立于旁。急入伏拜，母愕然问：“谁何？”曰：“儿妇秦氏。”曰：“汝何得到此？”对曰：“不遑细述。”遂置银于几，欲行。茅曰：“汝家觅汝甚亟。”曰：“是以匆匆者，正为此。”言已而去，茅亦不知所以也。

    Explanation

    399

    The fox said, "Not yet. The slow and fast separations and unions are also predestined. If what the maid says is true, then there would be no resentful women or unmarried men in the world. Wait until the gentleman dies, and both Mao Lang and his father become noble; that will be the time." The girl, upon hearing these words, realized that the matter of having a son with the fox sister was also thoroughly understood, and thus wanted to follow her to learn the true cultivation techniques. The fox said, "Since ancient times, there are no loyal and filial immortals. You may escape here, but Mao Lang and his mother are now eating sparse food and are not full; what to do? I have accumulated wealth; you should not personally present it as an introduction." Then she took out a pair of embroidered shoes and told the girl to wear them, saying, "This will not worry about traveling." After taking a few steps, she felt as if walking in the air like clouds. Not long after, they arrived at a large gate, where they saw the walls and fences in ruins, an old house with a dimly lit lamp still burning, and Mao Lang's mother sitting alone while Mao Lang stood by her side. The girl hurriedly entered and knelt down, and the mother was startled and asked, "Who are you?" The girl replied, "My daughter-in-law Qin." The mother asked, "How did you get here?" The girl answered, "No time to explain in detail." She then placed the silver on the table and wanted to leave. Mao Lang said, "Your family is looking for you urgently." The girl said, "That is why I am in such a hurry, for this reason." After speaking, she left, and Mao Lang also did not know why.

    400

    后十年，茅入泮，逾科复领乡荐。秦女之子由于氏成立，已捷南宫，榜下，以知县用，分发江苏。将行，于氏嘱云：“汝官本省，便可归家省亲矣！”详告之。比将近里门，而狐与丽娟、春兰已先至。丽因向母备言狐姊之恩，母亦感泣，令瑜并妻之，而纳春兰焉。子名保生，官江都数年。秦家已中落，保生追念母党，又时周恤其外祖父母云。

    Explanation

    400

    Ten years later, Mao entered school and, after another examination cycle, was again recommended as a scholar. The son of the Qin woman was raised by the Yu family and had already passed the imperial examination. After the announcement of the results, he was appointed as a county magistrate and assigned to Jiangsu. Before he left, the Yu family instructed him, saying, "Since you will be serving in this province, you can return home to visit your family." When he was near his hometown gate, the fox, Li Juan, and Chunlan had already arrived. Li Juan told her mother in detail about the kindness of the fox sister, and the mother also wept with gratitude. She ordered Yu to marry her daughter and also took Chunlan as a concubine. His son was named Baosheng and served as a county magistrate in Jiangdu for several years. The Qin family had fallen on hard times, and Baosheng remembered his mother's relatives and often provided them with assistance.

    401

    虚白道人曰：秦如有耻，当不食茅粟。余邑有类秦某者，或戏之曰：“君得养女之济矣！”观其色赧赧然。

    　　詹 如 仙

    　　明诸生詹曰典，云南世家，有女如仙，盖以其生姿如仙而名之也。女十有五时，有道姑宿于其家，见女，大喜曰：“此谪仙，非红尘中人物。”女闻之，遂拜道姑为师，教以修炼术。詹恐引女入邪，力拒道姑使去。适有叶太史欲娶女为其子长青媳，詹使妻商女，女不欲，强字之。女有玉狮子一对，素常把玩，叶家纳币，因配入回礼中。女由是不时啼泣，食饮渐减。詹忧之，谓女曰：“叶既委禽，万不能改。且伊门第实冠一邑，郁郁何为？”女曰：“儿不羡渠富贵，儿有大言，万望容纳。儿大婢伍氏亦宦门后，望父认为己女，代儿适叶室。儿决意修仙，实不能唯命是从。”言已，啼泣不已。詹以女方病，因伪应之曰：“亦可，汝愈，从长计议。

    Explanation

    401

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: If Qin Shi Huang had a sense of shame, he would not have eaten the crops of the people. In my town, there is someone like Qin Shi Huang, and someone joked with him, saying, "You have achieved the success of raising a daughter!" Seeing his face turn red with embarrassment.

    Jian Ruxian

    Jian Yudi, a scholar during the Ming dynasty, was from a noble family in Yunnan. He had a daughter named Ruxian, whom he named because her natural beauty was like that of a fairy. At the age of fifteen, a Daoist nun stayed at his home and saw the daughter. She was greatly delighted and said, "This is a celestial being, not an ordinary person of the mortal world." The daughter heard this and then took the Daoist nun as her teacher, learning Daoist practices for immortality. Jian was afraid that the daughter would be led astray and strongly refused the Daoist nun, sending her away. Coincidentally, there was a historian named Ye who wanted to marry the daughter to his son Changqing. Jian asked his wife to discuss the matter with the daughter, but she did not want to marry Ye, so he forced the marriage. The daughter had a pair of jade lions that she usually played with. The Ye family gave a dowry, and the jade lions were included in the return gifts. Since then, the daughter often cried and gradually reduced her food and drink. Jian was concerned and told her, "Ye has already proposed, and it cannot be changed. Moreover, his family is the most prestigious in the county. Why are you so depressed?" The daughter said, "I do not envy their wealth and status. I have a bold statement, and I hope you will accept it. My servant girl Wu is also from an official family. I hope you will adopt her as your own daughter and let her marry into the Ye family instead. I am determined to pursue Daoist practices and cannot obey orders blindly." After she finished speaking, she continued to cry. Jian thought the daughter was ill and pretended to agree, saying, "It is also possible. If you recover, we can discuss it further."

    402

    ”女愈后，遂与婢同寝食，亲爱之情，同胞不啻也。屡与伍言替代事，伍终以为妄，置若罔闻。及女于归届期前三日，将寝时，女言于伍曰：“叶郎兹已入泮，吾妹得嫁若人，亦不辱。”伍惊曰：“前言果实耶？”女曰：“此何事，何敢相欺？连宵梦吾师相招，别在今夜，奠雁时勿违父命，重予不孝。”伍曰：“姊师为谁耶？”曰：“即八仙中何仙姑也。”伍氏叹曰：“姊登仙籍，勿置妹等于度外！”女曰：“是何言也？妹之情义固时挂心头，吾身虽未属叶郎，而父母之命岂同儿戏，欲视叶郎如路人不得也！”对语夜深，伍盹睡。醒，不见女，急告詹。詹心知其异，并不寻觅。及亲迎，直妆伍氏从新郎去。叶与伍鱼水相得，实不知鸿离鱼网也。

    Explanation

    402

    After her recovery, the girl lived with her maid, sharing meals and sleeping in the same room. Their affection for each other was no less than that of siblings. She often spoke to Wu about the matter of substitution, but Wu always dismissed it as nonsense and paid no heed. Three days before the wedding, as they were about to go to sleep, the girl told Wu, "My brother Ye has now passed the examination and entered the ranks of scholars. My sister marrying such a man would not be a disgrace." Wu was startled and asked, "Is what you said earlier true?" The girl replied, "What is there to be deceitful about? I have had a dream every night that my teacher called me. Parting is tonight. Do not disobey your father's command when you present the pair of geese during the wedding ceremony, for it would be a grave act of filial impiety." Wu asked, "Who is your teacher?" The girl answered, "She is He Xian Gu, one of the Eight Immortals." Wu sighed and said, "If you ascend to the ranks of immortals, do not forget your sister!" The girl responded, "What are you saying? My feelings for you are always on my mind. Although I have not yet become Ye's wife, my parents' command is not a trivial matter. I cannot treat Ye as a stranger!" As they spoke, the night grew late, and Wu fell asleep. Upon waking, he found the girl gone and quickly informed Zhan. Zhan knew something was unusual but did not search for her. When the groom came to fetch the bride, Wu's wife dressed up and left with the new groom. Ye and Wu had a harmonious relationship, unaware that the girl had left her family like a fish leaving its net.

    403

    叶刚直，最恶不平事。一日，乘马访友归，路经松林，闻林内叫苦声甚哀，因系乘入视。见一人席地自饮，一人赤缚于树，身无完肤，情状甚惨。迫而问之，其人曰：“吾张自防，生有息女，宿太史欲充媵妾。吾当时未允，乃使其弟于林深逼令吾纳女，拷掠无已。兹愿奉纳，犹不得免。祈恳垂怜，代吾缓颊。”叶素知宿显宦，好行凶德，弟倚兄势，狂悖尤甚。本不欲与语，怜张无辜，遂曰：“渠既悔祸，理似可宥。”宿弟曰：“干汝甚事！”令从人拥之使去。叶强与理较，从人执杖击之。叶尝从事武技，至是大怒，夺从人器械将宿弟刺死，从人惧而散。叶急释张，乘马驰归。至家，时已昏暮，伍氏迎谓曰：“君宜速逃，迟则祸及！急如深山寻得虎伏峪，自有救星。”曰：“卿何由知？”曰：“适昼寝，梦神指示耳。”叶闻之，急乘马而去。

    Explanation

    403

    Ye was upright and resolute, hating unjust matters. One day, while riding a horse to visit a friend, he passed through a pine forest and heard a very pitiful cry coming from inside. He dismounted and went to investigate. He saw one person sitting on the ground drinking, and another bound to a tree, naked and severely injured, with a very pitiful situation.

    He approached and asked, "What is going on here?" The bound man replied, "I am Zhang Zifang. I have a daughter, and the prominent official Su wanted to take her as a concubine. I refused at first, so he had his younger brother force me to accept the daughter in a deep part of the forest. They beat me mercilessly. Even now, I am willing to give her up, but they still won't let me go. Please help me, and plead for my mercy."

    Ye knew Su was a prominent official who liked to do cruel deeds, and his younger brother relied on his elder brother's power, being even more rebellious and defiant. He did not want to get involved, but feeling sorry for Zhang's innocence, he said, "If he has already repented, it seems reasonable to forgive him." Su's younger brother retorted, "What business is it of yours?" and ordered his followers to drag Ye away. Ye tried to argue, but the followers beat him with sticks. Ye had practiced martial arts and was skilled in combat. He became very angry, took the weapons from his followers, and killed Su's younger brother. The followers were frightened and scattered.

    Ye quickly freed Zhang and rode his horse home. By the time he arrived, it was already dusk. His wife, Wu, greeted him and said, "You should escape quickly, or else you will be in trouble! Hurry to the Tiger Crouching Valley in the deep mountains; there will be a savior." Ye asked, "How do you know this?" She replied, "I had a dream during my daytime sleep, and a god showed me the way." Ye believed her and hurriedly rode away.

    404

    伍独居，有事关心，久不成寐。夜未央忽闻叩门声，伍意官人回复，问之，则女子声口。纳而视之，绝代彼姝也。女伫立以泣，问之不应，良久始曰：“妾张氏，即官人所救张自防之女。妾父自松林归，历言其事。妾出，见二人持凶器入，自言为太史所使，立害妾父命。妾急匿，渠搜妾不得而去。妾无依，欲投官人以充婢媵，否则奉事夫人，为婢终身。”言已复泣。伍曰：“吾亦恐祸及，将归詹门以自庇。现官人远游，汝能追寻乎？”曰：“能。但恐见面时官人不相信。”伍曰：“无虞此。”遂出叶衣，令女男装。装毕，伍视而笑曰：“若是服饰，不知者见之，又将平地生波。”遂赠以资斧，复以玉狮一枚授之，曰：“此官人夫妇之信物。”女愕然曰：“此言何谓也？”伍遂将詹氏修仙、自己代嫁，一切历言于女。且曰：“官人出，实亦觅原妻詹氏。”寺钟报晓，女别伍自去。

    Explanation

    404

    Wu lived alone, preoccupied with his thoughts, and could not sleep for a long time. Deep into the night, he suddenly heard a knock on the door. Thinking it was the man returning, he asked who it was, and a woman's voice replied. He opened the door and saw a beauty beyond compare. The woman stood there weeping. When he asked her, she did not respond. After a long while, she said, "I am Zhang, the daughter of Zhang Zifang, whom the gentleman saved. My father returned from the pine forest and recounted the events. I went out and saw two men enter with weapons, claiming to be sent by the Grand Historian to kill my father. I hurriedly hid, and they searched for me but could not find me and left. I have no one to rely on and wish to seek refuge with the gentleman to serve as a concubine, or else serve the lady, dedicating my life as a servant." After speaking, she wept again. Wu said, "I also fear that misfortune will befall me, and I plan to seek refuge with the Zhang family to protect myself. The gentleman is far away on a journey; can you follow him?" She replied, "I can. But I fear that when we meet, the gentleman may not believe me." Wu said, "Do not worry about that." He then took out a leaf robe and had the woman dress as a man. After she was dressed, Wu looked at her and laughed, saying, "With such attire, those who do not know will see and create trouble out of nothing." He then gave her funds and a jade lion, saying, "This is a token of the couple." The woman was startled and asked, "What does this mean?" Wu then told her about the lady's practice of Daoist cultivation, his own substitution as a bride, and everything else. He also said, "The gentleman went out to search for his original wife, Lady Zhan." The temple bell announced the dawn, and the woman bid farewell to Wu and left on her own.

    405

    叶长青之逃难也，改名詹长青，入深山月馀，不闻有虎伏峪地名，因货马作资，缓缓寻觅。后至一山庄，四面皆山，峭壁插天，如列锦屏，爱之。遂赁室以居，恣意游赏。适村中塾师病故，因受其徒而馆焉。计在避难，亦无望救星照临矣。张女尝从父学子平，因善其术。别伍后，资卖卜以给食，备尝险阻，嗣游至叶某设帐之庄。先是庄有农人景某者，生女极美，及笄未字。一日，道姑借宿其家，相之曰：“是女宜嫁远方人。

    Explanation

    405

    Ye Changqing, while fleeing from disaster, changed his name to Zhan Changqing and entered the deep mountains for more than a month. He did not hear of a place called Hu Fu Valley, so he sold his horse to raise funds and searched slowly. Later, he arrived at a small village surrounded by mountains, with steep cliffs reaching up to the sky, like a screen of brocade, which he admired. He then rented a room to live in and enjoyed the scenery freely. Just then, the private teacher of the village had died, so he took over the teaching position. He was in the midst of seeking refuge and had no hope of divine intervention. Zhang's daughter had learned divination from her father and was skilled in the art. After leaving her army, she made a living by selling divination services and had experienced many hardships. She later traveled to the village where Ye Changqing was teaching. Before this, there was a farmer named Jing in the village who had a very beautiful daughter, who was of marriageable age but still unmarried. One day, a Taoist priestess stayed at the farmer's home and looked at the girl, saying, "This girl should marry someone from afar."

    406

    ”景笑曰：“吾负田力穑，终身不到三十里外，外交无人，何由嫁于远？”道姑曰：“五日后必有远方人来，美如冠玉，身服齐衰，手执报君知，为人算命者，即其人也。”景摇首不语。道姑曰：“五日后若无来人，当以吾言为妄。”景遂信之，屈指以俟。及期，张女适至庄。过景门，景观其容貌服色及手所执卦板，悉如道姑言，大喜。邀至其家，饮以酒，细询之。女伪言姓叶，父母双亡，无他家口。问其年庚，则云十七。景曰：“与小女同庚。”此景之漏言，而女未及细审也。

    Explanation

    406

    Jing laughed and said, "I have been a farmer all my life, tilling the land, and I have never traveled more than thirty miles from home. I have no connections outside my village, so how could I marry someone far away?" The nun replied, "Five days later, there will certainly be a person from afar coming. She will be as beautiful as jade, dressed in formal attire, holding a divination board, and she will be a fortune-teller." Jing shook his head without speaking. The nun said, "If there is no one coming five days later, then my words will be seen as false." Jing believed her and began counting the days in anticipation. When the time came, Zhang arrived at the village. As she passed Jing's door, Jing observed her appearance, clothing, and the divination board she held, and everything was exactly as the nun had said. He was greatly pleased. He invited her to his home, served her wine, and asked her many questions. The woman lied and said her name was Ye, that her parents had both passed away, and that she had no other family. When asked about her age, she claimed to be seventeen. Jing said, "She is the same age as my daughter." This was a slip of the tongue by Jing, and the woman did not investigate further.

    407

    景曰：“君善风鉴否？”曰：“知其大概。”饭后，景引之相宅，故呼女出，后役以家事。女指景女曰：“此为公何人？”答以小女。睨之，佳冶窈窕，既美且艳。心计曰：“美哉！吾见犹爱，况男子乎？”景曰：“敝舍何如？”对曰：“巽门坎室，厨居震方东，四宅之上吉宅也。但厨偏北方，半属于艮，艮土受巽，震木克艮，为少男，主小儿不旺。”景曰：“是也，内人连生四子，仅活其一。

    Explanation

    407

    Jing said, "Are you skilled in fortune-telling?" The other replied, "I have a rough understanding." After the meal, Jing led him to inspect the house and called out his daughter, entrusting her with household affairs afterward. The girl pointed to Jing's daughter and asked, "Who is this for you?" He replied that it was his younger daughter. Glancing at her, she was beautiful, graceful, and elegant, both beautiful and charming. He thought to himself, "How beautiful! Even I find her lovely, how much more so for men?" Jing then asked, "How about my humble abode?" The other responded, "The door faces southeast (Xun) and the main room is the entrance hall (Kan). The kitchen is located in the east (Zhen). It is one of the four auspicious houses. However, the kitchen is slightly northward, partially belonging to the northeast (Gen). The northeast (Gen) receives the southeast (Xun), and the east (Zhen) wood overcomes the northeast (Gen), indicating a younger son, which suggests that the younger children will not thrive." Jing said, "That is true, my wife has given birth to four sons, but only one survived."

    408

    似此何如？”女曰：“厨系三楹，将门移开南间，即为水木相生，绝无妨碍矣。”其时举家瞩女，而景女尤甚。女暗笑曰：“渠必以吾为男子，可惜心思误用。”遂告辞。景固留之。次日，即有庄人向女论亲，言景某欲以女妻之。女辞曰：“羁旅之人，违家千馀里，不日将归，奚可就婚于此？”媒曰：“已议及此，君归则从之耳。”女又曰：“重服在身，必大祥后方可议婚，其能待乎？”媒曰：“此大礼，何不能待之有？”女思心事未遂，资斧将尽，不如暂应之，俟访得心上人再为计议。女遂止于景，暗访叶生，不复云游矣。

    Explanation

    408

    "Like this, what do you think?" the girl said. "The kitchen has three bays; if we move the door to the south bay, it will be in harmony with the principle of water nourishing wood, and there will be no hindrance." At that time, the whole family was watching the girl, and Jing Girl in particular. The girl secretly smiled and thought, "He must think I am a man; it's a pity that my thoughts are misused." She then bid farewell. Jing insisted on keeping her. The next day, there were people from the village who came to discuss marriage, saying that Jing wanted to marry her to a woman. The girl declined, saying, "I am a traveler, away from home by thousands of miles, and I will return soon. How can I marry here?" The matchmaker said, "We have already discussed this. When you return, you will follow through." The girl replied, "I am in mourning attire. Only after the completion of the major mourning period can marriage be discussed. How can I wait?" The matchmaker said, "This is a major ritual. How can you not wait for it?" The girl thought, "My heart's desires have not been fulfilled, and my travel funds are nearly exhausted. It might be better to agree temporarily and wait until finding the right person before making further plans." So she stayed with Jing, secretly seeking out Ye Sheng, and no longer wandered around.

    409

    景某之子亦从叶读。一日，景饮叶酒，张氏为陪。氏闻叶语似为同乡，问其郡邑，相违甚远，疑之。乘景不在，遂试之曰：“君詹氏乎？殆詹氏之婿乎？”叶色动，不语。女决其为叶，遂复曰：“邑有所谓叶长青者，其谁乎？”叶勃然变色。女曰：“弟特为觅兄到此。”叶惊曰：“君为宿某所使耶？”曰：“非也。奉令正詹伍夫人之命来耳。”叶笑曰：“君失言矣！一人奚有二姓？”曰：“二姓之故，君自知之，何为故问？”言已，出玉狮一枚示之曰：“君识此否？”叶见之大骇，自忖曰：“家藏之物，何得落外人手？”方欲接来谛视，女复袖藏之。叶曰：“此物何来？”语次景入，女曰：“暇时为君细述之。”饭后，叶邀女移玉，女答以继至。叶俟至日暮，亲身邀之。女请诘朝相见，叶弗听，握腕促行。女不得已从之去。至，以玉狮授叶，叶视之，果其家藏。

    Explanation

    409

    Jing's son also studied under Ye. One day, Jing invited Ye to drink wine, with Zhang accompanying them. Zhang heard Ye speak in a way that suggested they were from the same hometown. She asked him about his hometown, and upon learning that it was far away, she became suspicious. When Jing was not around, she tested Ye by saying, "Are you from the Zhan family? Or are you a son-in-law of the Zhan family?" Ye's face changed, and he remained silent. The woman concluded that he was Ye, and then said, "In our county, there is someone named Ye Changqing. Do you know who that is?" Ye suddenly turned pale. The woman said, "I specially came here to find my elder brother." Ye was startled and asked, "Are you acting on behalf of Susu?" She replied, "No, I came here at the command of Lady Zhan Wu." Ye smiled and said, "You're mistaken! How can one person have two surnames?" She said, "The reason for the two surnames, you already know, why ask?" After speaking, she took out a jade lion and showed it to him, asking, "Do you recognize this?" Ye was greatly startled and thought to himself, "How could a family treasure end up in the hands of an outsider?" Just as he was about to take it to examine it closely, the woman put it back in her sleeve. Ye asked, "Where did this come from?" While they were talking, Jing returned. The woman said, "I will tell you in detail when we have free time." After the meal, Ye invited the woman to move the jade. She replied that she would come later. Ye waited until dusk and personally invited her. The woman asked to meet the next day, but Ye did not agree and grabbed her wrist, urging her to hurry. The woman had no choice but to follow him. Upon arrival, she gave the jade lion to Ye, who looked at it and confirmed it was indeed a family treasure.

    410

    询其由来，女笑而不言。叶曰：“兄与弟同姓耶？叶姓邑中甚少，仅有数家，悉弟亲族。以意度之，兄之不姓叶犹弟之不姓詹也。”女曰：“弟欲氏叶，故冒姓叶。弟实张姓，即君前在松林所释张自防之子。”叶闻之，上下瞰女。笑曰：“瞰弟何为？”叶曰：“假难成真，真难成假。卿非张氏之子，殆张氏之半子也。芳容如是，勿怪宿某求之不得而不释然。”女曰：“弟与兄初会，何相戏如是之甚？”叶曰：“非吾戏卿，实卿欺吾。”遂立将女所着布靴脱下，曰：“尚欲抵赖耶？”女含羞不语，遂相狎。历述往事，悲喜交集。女请仍事男装，伪为朋友，叶笑应之。一日，值景初度，席设内庭，女乘间告曰：“吾非不欲为婿，实非为婿之人。”因历述逃难访叶巧遇之故，且自脱其履示以下体。景翁媪见之，不胜惊讶，莫知所为。女曰：“叶某系诸生，世家后，年正青春，永无归期，得婚于此为赘婿，两有裨益。

    Explanation

    410

    When asked about the origin, the girl smiled but did not speak. Ye said, "Are you and I of the same surname? The surname Ye is very rare in this town, with only a few families, all of which are my relatives. By inference, your not being surnamed Ye is like my not being surnamed Zhan." The girl said, "I wanted to take the surname Ye, so I adopted it. I am actually surnamed Zhang, the son of Zhang Zifang whom you released in the pine forest before." Upon hearing this, Ye looked up and down at the girl. He laughed and said, "Why are you looking at me like that?" Ye said, "It's hard to turn falsehood into truth, and it's hard to turn truth into falsehood. You are not the son of the Zhang family, but you are almost half of the Zhang family. With such beauty, do not blame me for not being able to obtain what I desire and thus not being able to let go." The girl said, "I first met you, why did you tease me so much?" Ye said, "It was not me teasing you, but you deceiving me." He immediately took off the girl's cloth boots and said, "Do you still want to deny it?" The girl blushed and remained silent, and they became intimate. They recounted past events, filled with a mix of sorrow and joy. The girl asked to continue wearing male clothing and pretend to be friends, and Ye laughed and agreed. One day, during the celebration of Jing's initial birthday, a banquet was set up in the inner courtyard. The girl took the opportunity to say, "I am not unwilling to be a son-in-law, but I am not the type to be a son-in-law." She then recounted the reason for escaping from trouble, visiting Ye, and meeting by chance, and took off her shoes to show her lower body. Jing's grandparents saw this and were greatly surprised, not knowing what to do. The girl said, "Ye is a scholar, a descendant of a prominent family, in the prime of youth, with no fixed return date. If he marries here as a son-in-law, it would be beneficial for both parties."

    411

    如不河汉吾言，愿上令爱而己次之。”翁媪皆喜，遂择吉成礼。合卺后，女曰：“此所谓鹬蚌相持，渔人得利！”复谓新人曰：“男子非汝一己之男子，次夜宜见还，勿贪爱不遣也。”叶代女笑叱之。叶欲留女同寝，女曰：“吾不惯看活春宫。”一日，三人玩月未寝，忽闻村中陡起哭声，叶出问之。盖庄有以樵为生者，被虎伏峪之虎所害，故家人哭之。叶问峪在何山，去此多少路。庄人曰：“东南去此数十里，摩天岭之半岭是也。”叶入告二女，言欲明日往探之。张欲与偕往，叶从之，遂并乘而往。约行五十里，马不能陟，欲舍马而徒。居人曰：“自前去，有凶无吉！”盖再行十数里即众虎出没处也。叶闻，问峪之所在，其人遥指之。曰： “峪有洞否？”曰：“相传松柏密处有洞府，其地深险，无人敢去。二年前尝有人见二女子携手入峪，未闻见其出者。”张氏曰：“二女中必有詹氏！”叶曰：“然。

    Explanation

    411

    If you do not consider my words as high as the river Han, I wish to marry your eldest daughter and then your second daughter. The old man and the old woman were both pleased, so they chose a good day to hold the wedding ceremony. After the wedding, the woman said, "This is what is meant by the鹬蚌相持,渔人得利!" She then told the new husband, "This man is not just your husband; he is also my second husband. You should return to me the next night and do not be greedy and keep him here." The man laughed and scolded her. The man wanted to keep the woman with him, but the woman said, "I am not used to seeing live action pornography." One day, the three of them were playing under the moon and had not yet gone to sleep when they suddenly heard a loud cry from the village. The man went out to ask what was happening. It turned out that a villager who made a living by cutting wood was killed by a tiger in the valley, so his family was crying. The man asked which mountain the valley was in and how far it was from there. The villagers said, "It is southeast of here, about dozens of miles away, in the middle of Mount Mo Tian." The man went back and told the two women about his plan to go and investigate the next day. The woman wanted to go with him, and the man agreed, so they both went there on horseback. After traveling about fifty miles, the horse could not go any further, so they wanted to leave the horse and walk. The villagers said, "From here on, there is only danger, no benefit!" The man heard this and asked where the valley was. The villager pointed it out from a distance. The man asked, "Is there a cave in the valley?" The villager said, "It is said that there is a cave in the dense forest of pine and cypress. The place is deep and dangerous, and no one dares to go there. Two years ago, someone saw two women holding hands enter the valley, but no one has heard of them coming out." The woman said, "One of the two women must be Jizhen!" The man said, "Yes."

    412

    ”盘桓多时，悒悒而归。叶曰：“不知福地，无处逃生，命也；既知其地，坐以待毙，非命也。”张氏曰：“妾试往探之。”叶曰：“岂有明知必死而自寻者乎？卿何轻生若此！”张曰：“不然。妻之与夫，不啻臣子之与君父。古之忠臣孝子济君父之难，当其往也，岂逆料其必能生还而始往乎？能无惧耳！且闻峪多虎，未尝目睹，安知非讹传之不足凭信？即有之，亦或适逢其他往，得侥幸入洞。即遇害，亦为夫死，岂可以轻生律之者哉？”叶曰：“虽然，卿勿往。以吾累卿，心实有不安。”张曰：“可无妾，不可无君，妾志决矣！”明晨，执玉狮而行。叶曰：“执此何用？”曰：“此詹氏之物，非此不足取信。”生送张至马不能陟之处，张曰：“君立视妾往，不见，然后自归，勿系念也。妾不得已出此九死一生之谋，其济，君之福也；不济，亦不怨不尤。”言已，浩然长往。叶视张行二、三里，即为木石遮蔽，叹息而归。

    Explanation

    412

    After lingering for a long time, they returned home with a heavy heart. Leaf said, "Not knowing about the blessed land and having no place to escape to is a matter of fate; knowing about the place and waiting for death is not a matter of fate." Zhang said, "I will try to investigate it." Leaf said, "Is there anyone who would willingly seek death knowing it is certain? Why are you so willing to sacrifice yourself?" Zhang said, "Not so. A wife is to her husband as a subject is to her ruler. Ancient loyal ministers and filial sons helped their rulers and fathers in times of difficulty. When they went, did they expect to return safely? They simply did not fear. Moreover, I have heard that the valley is full of tigers, but I have never seen one. How can I believe such rumors? Even if there are tigers, perhaps I will encounter them at other times and manage to enter the cave. If I meet harm, it will be for my husband's sake. How can you judge me so lightly?" Leaf said, "Although so, you must not go. I am burdening you, and I feel deeply uneasy." Zhang said, "I can do without myself, but not without you. My mind is made up." The next morning, she took the jade lion and set off. Leaf said, "What use is this?" Zhang said, "This is the property of the Zhan family, and it is necessary to prove my identity." Leaf saw Zhang walk two or three miles and then disappear behind trees and rocks, sighing and returning home.

    413

    张氏之只身入峪也，数里而外，遍兽蹄鸟迹，径无人蹊。再入之，虎啸猿吟，毛发猬竖。约行二十里，忽有二虎遽起，直赴张氏。张见之，手无寸铁，不禁举玉狮作遥击之势。虎见之，从身旁过，复回卧张来路，不敢前。张莫解所以，欲回，二虎当路，遂执玉狮复进。嗣连遇诸虎，皆畏避。日未暮，已届洞口，其翠柏苍松，大皆二三围。回首见二虎伏洞口，一左一右。张曰：“吾至此，不畏汝矣！”遂举玉狮昂然而入。内一道姑高坐，瞑目如睡。

    Explanation

    413

    Zhang, entering the valley alone, walked several miles beyond, where the ground was covered with animal tracks and bird prints, and there were no human paths. Upon entering further, he heard the roars of tigers and the cries of monkeys, causing his hair to stand on end like a hedgehog. After about twenty miles, suddenly two tigers leaped up and rushed directly towards Zhang. Seeing this, Zhang, who was unarmed, could not help but raise the jade lion to make a remote attack gesture. The tigers, seeing the jade lion, passed by him and returned to lie down on the path he had come from, not daring to advance. Zhang did not understand why this was happening and wanted to turn back, but the two tigers blocked his way. He then held the jade lion and continued forward. Subsequently, he encountered more tigers, all of which avoided him. Before sunset, he reached the cave entrance, where the green cypress and dark pine trees were mostly two or three times the size of a person. Looking back, he saw two tigers lying at the cave entrance, one on each side. Zhang exclaimed, "I have come to this place and no longer fear you!" and raised the jade lion and entered the cave confidently. Inside, a Taoist nun was sitting upright, with closed eyes as if sleeping.

    414

    见张，惊曰：“汝何得到此？”见张执玉狮，复曰：“汝叶氏耶？”张曰：“吾姓叶，非氏叶也。”曰：“何为来者？”张遂将叶公子为不平伤人逃难，一一细言之。道姑闻之，忽悲忽喜。张心知仙姑必詹氏，遂曰：“仙姑詹氏耶？”詹不语。张复曰：“伍姊代嫁者非子耶？妾等误入局中，欲出不得，不如姊之托身局外得自由也！”言至此，詹氏扶之起，叹曰：“吾身出局外，心在局中，身心两地，苦尤异常。不然，阿妹何能到此安然无恙也？”张茫然不解。詹曰：“吾平素以他宝物降虎，而举玉狮以示之，故虎见皆畏避。盖以玉狮系叶玉人把玩物，必时佩于身，倘以故来此，可借之以免虎伤，即可赖之以入洞府。

    Explanation

    414

    Seeing Zhang, she was startled and said, "How did you come to be here?" Seeing Zhang holding the jade lion, she asked again, "Are you of the Ye family?" Zhang replied, "My surname is Ye, but I am not of the Ye family." She then asked, "Why have you come?" Zhang then detailed the reasons for his arrival, explaining that he had fled due to an incident involving Ye Gong, a nobleman who had caused trouble. The nun heard this and was suddenly filled with alternating emotions of sorrow and joy. Zhang, realizing that the nun must be Zhan, asked, "Are you Nuns Zhan?" Zhan remained silent. Zhang continued, "Is it not you who was substituted in marriage for the sister? We were mistakenly caught in a situation and cannot escape. It would be better to place yourself outside the situation and enjoy freedom!" Upon hearing this, Zhan helped Zhang up and sighed, "My body is free, but my mind remains entangled. Being separated in this way, the suffering is especially severe. Otherwise, how could my sister have come here safely and unharmed?" Zhang was confused and did not understand. Zhan explained, "I usually use other treasures to subdue tigers, but I showed the jade lion to them, and they all fled in fear. The jade lion is a plaything of Ye Yur, a nobleman, and he must always wear it. If he comes here, I can borrow it to avoid tiger attacks and use it to enter the cave temple."

    415

    吾之用心若是，犹为度外置妹等耶？”张恍然，称谢不已。曰：“玉人之事何以处？”詹曰：“妹之玉人，勿向吾同称之也。”张笑曰：“姊适才自言之，他人言之即不应耶？”詹含羞曰：“三五日吾师必来。来时，与妹切求之。”未几，仙姑果至。见张曰：“此含奇冤之张氏耶？”张泣诉之。仙姑曰：“勿尔！吾自有以伸之。”言及叶生，曰：“吾自有以救之。”语及伍氏、景氏，曰：“吾自有以安之。”谓张氏曰：“吾徒之父母字女于叶，违之必有天殃，汝可导叶来此完婚。”张甚喜，而不敢行。仙姑以剑授之曰：“倘遇不测，拔剑掷之，可逢凶化吉。

    Explanation

    415

    My heart is like this, still considering my sisters as outsiders?" Zhang suddenly understood and continuously expressed his gratitude. He asked, "What should I do about the matter of the jade person?" Zhan replied, "Do not call it the same as me when referring to my sister's jade person." Zhang smiled and said, "If you just said it a moment ago, why should others not say it?" Zhan blushed and said, "Within three to five days, my teacher will surely come. When he comes, you can urgently ask him for help." Soon after, the fairy arrived. Seeing Zhang, she asked, "Is this Zhang who bears a strange injustice?" Zhang wept and recounted his story. The fairy said, "Do not do that! I have my own way to deal with it." When mentioning Ye Sheng, she said, "I have my own way to save him." When speaking of Wu and Jing, she said, "I have my own way to comfort them." She told Zhang, "My disciple's parents betrothed a daughter to Ye. If we violate this, there will be divine retribution. You can guide Ye here to complete the marriage." Zhang was very happy but dared not act. The fairy handed him a sword and said, "If you encounter any danger, draw the sword and throw it. It will turn misfortune into good fortune."

    416

    ”张拜受而别，不知何以行急如飞，瞬息即至。与叶、景言之，叶喜与行，景不欲；叶约以月半必归，景始允。将行，忽三人自外入，各执凶器，言奉官差拿要犯叶某，叶仓皇无措。张急拔剑向空掷之，忽三首落地，俄尸首化为青烟，被风吹散。叶大喜，辞景而行。至洞，先谒仙姑，嗣与詹氏合卺。将寝，张谓詹氏曰：“今夜之事，伍氏不能代矣。”次日，仙姑曰：“尔夫妇三人俱有仙缘，虽在洞府，谅不岑寂。但不可以无事事，修真之术，詹氏自能传授。暇可乘云循行下土，福善祸淫，替天行道，功德自不可量。三年后，吾来课尔等功修。”遂授以仙家妙术，既精而始去。

    Explanation

    416

    Zhang bowed and received the instructions before departing, not knowing why he traveled as swiftly as a flying arrow, arriving in an instant. He spoke with Ye and Jing, and Ye was eager to go, while Jing was reluctant. Ye promised to return by the middle of the month, and Jing finally agreed. As they were about to depart, three men suddenly entered from outside, each holding a weapon, saying they were official agents sent to arrest the wanted criminal Ye. Ye was in a state of panic and had no idea what to do. Zhang quickly drew his sword and threw it into the air, and suddenly three heads fell to the ground. In an instant, the bodies turned into blue smoke, which was blown away by the wind. Ye was greatly relieved and bid farewell to Jing before setting out. Upon reaching the cave, he first paid his respects to the immortal lady, and then married Jiang. As he was about to sleep, Zhang told Jiang, "What happened tonight, Wu cannot replace it." The next day, the immortal lady said, "All three of you have a connection to immortality. Even though you are in the cave, you will not be lonely. However, you should not be idle. The methods of spiritual cultivation, Jiang can teach you. In your spare time, you can ride the clouds and travel through the lower realms, bringing blessings to the virtuous and misfortune to the wicked, acting on behalf of heaven. Your merit and virtue will be immeasurable. Three years later, I will come to evaluate your spiritual progress." She then taught them the magical techniques of the immortals, and only after they had mastered them did she depart.

    417

    叶洞居及年馀，一日，谓詹曰：“吾屡梦与伍氏、景氏相会，且觉与渠经理家务，而身实仍在洞府，其故何也？”詹曰：“假者可以为真，真者勿以为假。一身而三处享其福，师所以代君谋者周祥备至，奈何以梦寐视之？”叶始悟。三年后，仙姑果至，见叶等功修已成，喜不自胜。一日，同叶等下山游瞩，有二役押一犯官由山下过，忽涧中出一虎，直赴犯官，张欲往救，仙姑止之。眼看虎噬犯官，饱其肉而去。张曰：“往日师以慈悲为心，今何坐视而不救？”仙姑曰：“犯官非他，即尔仇人宿某。”张闻之愕然。仙姑手指背负笊篱曰：“此尔之功臣也。使化尔真身，冒名京控，宿以之罢官，故获罪充军，路由此过。”张伏地叩谢曰：“久欲复仇，未获钧命，不敢私往。今得目睹仇人丧虎腹，不啻手刃也，何快如之！”

    Explanation

    417

    For more than a year, Ye lived in a cave. One day, he told Zhan, "I often dream of meeting people from the Wu and Jing families, and I feel as though I am managing household affairs with them, yet my body is still in the cave. What could be the reason for this?" Zhan replied, "What is false can become true, and what is true should not be thought of as false. You are enjoying blessings in three places at once, and the master has meticulously planned for you. Why do you view this as a dream?" Ye then understood. Three years later, the goddess indeed arrived. She saw that Ye and others had completed their spiritual cultivation and was overjoyed. One day, she went down the mountain with Ye and others to tour and observe. As they were passing, two officials were escorting a criminal official who was passing by the foot of the mountain. Suddenly, a tiger appeared in the ravine and went directly toward the criminal official. Zhang wanted to go and save him, but the goddess stopped him. Zhang watched as the tiger bit the criminal official, satisfied itself with his flesh, and then left. Zhang said, "In the past, the master had compassion as his heart. Why does he now sit and watch without saving?" The goddess said, "The criminal official is not someone else; he is your enemy, Su." Zhang was shocked upon hearing this. The goddess pointed to a tool that was carried on the back and said, "This is your meritorious person. It transformed into your true form, used a false name to file a complaint in the capital, caused Su to be dismissed from office, and therefore incurred punishment and was sent to military service. The road passed through this place." Zhang prostrated on the ground and bowed to thank, saying, "I had long wanted to take revenge, but I did not receive the master's command and therefore dared not go privately. Now, I am able to see my enemy die in the tiger's belly, no less satisfying than killing with my own hands. How much more satisfying than that!"

    418

    叶长青有砚友彭琏，宦游四十馀年，罢官归里，泛舟洞庭。忽对面来画船二只，锦缆绣缦，备极豪华。内一少年高坐，旁列二美人陪饮，皆明眸皓齿，翠黛云容，世所罕有。彭审视少年，识是长青，不禁大呼曰：“叶兄别来无恙乎？”叶停舟，立邀彭某过船，重整酒馔，宾主对饮。彭曰：“自别芝颜，四十馀载，屈指寿数，足下应年届古稀，何仍丰昌若是？”叶曰：“得仙人驻颜术，故不知老之将至尔。”彭曰：“适才同饮者为谁？”曰：“山荆耳。

    Explanation

    418

    Ye Changqing had a close friend named Peng Lian, who had spent over forty years in official service. After retiring, Peng returned to his hometown and sailed on Lake Dongting. Suddenly, two ornate boats appeared in front of him, decorated with luxurious and intricate designs. Inside one of the boats, a young man was seated prominently, with two beautiful women beside him. They had bright eyes, white teeth, and a cloud-like appearance, which was rare in the world.

    Peng recognized the young man as Ye Changqing and called out, "Ye, are you well?" Ye stopped his boat and invited Peng to join him. They reorganized the wine and food, and the two friends drank together. Peng said, "It has been over forty years since we last saw each other. Counting your age, you should be approaching seventy. How is it that you still look so youthful?" Ye replied, "I have learned the secret of preserving youth from immortals, so I do not feel the approach of old age."

    Peng asked, "Who was the woman who drank with you just now?" Ye answered, "She is my wife."

    419

    ”彭讶曰：“闻君山居，尚不得屡还旧府，何以复婚于两湖耶？”叶曰：“其话甚长，未遑细述。仆未回山庄，兹已数载，有一家信敢烦迂道寄之。”彭应诺。叶助之资斧，赠以珠玉，其值不下千馀金。彭喜出望外，受书而行。至，见叶门如市，宅之壮丽，生平未睹。既相见，叶盖颁白人也，大疑。彭曰：“昨于洞庭得晤尊颜，君何旋归之速也？”叶曰：“无之。仆居虎伏峪月馀而归，嗣未远游，仆岂有分身术得赴洞庭耶？”彭曰：“君之家报，弟已带来。”欲取怀而与，探之，已无有矣。彭颇疑讶。谈次，问曰：“君嫡配伍夫人居诸何如？”叶曰：“吾不知也。想伍氏亦已改嫁他人，子孙满前矣。

    Explanation

    419

    " Pengya said, "I heard that you live in the mountains and still cannot often return to your old residence, how can you remarry in the two lakes?" Ye replied, "The story is very long, I did not have time to explain in detail. I have not returned to my mountain retreat for several years, and I have a family letter that I dare to trouble you to deliver on your way." Pengya agreed. Ye helped him with travel expenses and gave him pearls and jade, worth several thousand coins. Pengya was extremely pleased, received the letter, and set out. Upon arrival, he saw Ye's gate bustling like a market and his house magnificent, something he had never seen before. After meeting, Pengya realized that Ye was an elderly person with white hair, which surprised him. Pengya asked, "I met you at Dongting Lake yesterday, why did you return so quickly?" Ye said, "Not so. I stayed in Tiger Rest Valley for more than a month and did not go far away, how could I have the ability to be in two places at once and go to Dongting Lake?" Pengya said, "Your family letter, I have already brought it." He wanted to take it out and give it to Ye, but upon searching, it was already gone. Pengya was quite doubtful and surprised. During the conversation, he asked, "How is your legal wife, Wu Lady?" Ye said, "I do not know. Perhaps Wu Lady has remarried someone else and has many descendants."

    420

    ”言已，呼二子三孙出与彭见，曰：“长幼之节不可废也。”彭留恋数日而去。景氏之子因知有嫡母伍氏。彭家与叶邻村，至家，与老母言遇叶某之奇。母曰：“尔何言之诬耶？前叶某伤人，逃难三月而归。其妻伍氏以面目殊异，闭门不纳。转眼复有真容，言仙人授伪面以远祸，伍始信之。未几，闻官获叶某矣；未几，闻叶某死于囹圄矣。伍领尸殡葬，乃一藜杖。嗣闻张氏京控，又闻宿某获罪充军，死于路，叶始敢以真容与人周旋。现在其次子为郡守，今科二孙同领乡荐，贺客往来，荣耀方盛。

    Explanation

    420

    After speaking, he called out the two sons and three grandsons to meet with Peng, saying, "The etiquette of seniority cannot be neglected." Peng lingered for several days before departing. The son of Jing's family thus came to know about his legitimate mother, Wu. Peng's family lived in a neighboring village to Ye. Upon returning home, he told his old mother about the strange encounter with Ye. She said, "How can you speak falsely? Before, Ye injured someone and fled in difficulty for three months before returning. His wife Wu, because of her unusual appearance, shut the door and did not admit him. In a moment, he regained his true appearance, saying that a celestial being gave him a false face to avoid disaster, and Wu began to believe him. Not long after, she heard that the authorities had captured Ye; not long after, she heard that Ye died in prison. Wu led the body for burial, which turned out to be a walking stick. Later, she heard that Zhang sued in the capital; she also heard that Su was punished and sent to military service, dying on the way. Ye then dared to show his true appearance to people. Now his second son is the magistrate of a county, and this year, his two grandsons were recommended together for the provincial examination. Guests come and go, and the glory is at its peak.

    421

    ”彭闻之而惑滋甚，急备仪物，自往叶家贺之。见叶之容止与山庄所见了无殊异，遂与历述所遇。叶曰：“惟仆于山庄纳妻景氏属实，其馀悉属虚无。”伍所生之子孙悉知山庄有庶母，长子怀德心疑其事，诣彭细询，彭为详言之。德曰：“若然，吾父必仙人。”因托故他出，暗邀彭造山庄视之，景子怀义、怀玉接待孔殷。德欲朝父，义曰：“数日前，父言彭客与大兄某日必至，吾不可再留人世，以骇物听。今早省父，已不知所往。”德留数日，欲回。义欲同回省父，兼朝嫡母，德从之。至家，人言太公于数日前出游未归，德、义等俱意父或有来时，数年无耗，亦无望矣。

    Explanation

    421

    Peng, upon hearing this, became even more perplexed. He hurriedly prepared ceremonial objects and went himself to the Ye family to congratulate them. Upon seeing Ye's demeanor and appearance, which were exactly the same as what he had seen in the mountain retreat, Peng then recounted all that he had encountered. Ye said, "Only I, in the mountain retreat, married Jing as my wife is true; the rest is all fictional." All the descendants of Wu knew that there was a concubine mother in the mountain retreat. The eldest son, Huai De, harbored doubts about the matter and visited Peng to ask in detail. Peng explained it in detail. De said, "If so, my father must be a divine being." He excused himself to go elsewhere and secretly invited Peng to visit the mountain retreat. Jing's sons, Huai Yi and Huai Yu, received him with great enthusiasm. De wanted to pay respects to his father, but Yi said, "A few days ago, father said that Peng's guest and the eldest brother would definitely arrive on a certain day. I cannot stay in the human world anymore to shock the ears of the world. This morning, when I visited father, he was already gone." De stayed for several days and wanted to return. Yi wanted to return together to visit father and also pay respects to the principal mother. De agreed. When they returned home, people said that the grandfather had gone out a few days ago and had not returned. De, Yi, and others all thought that their father might come again, but with no news for several years, they also had no hope anymore.

    422

    一日，伍与子孙共话，忽见少年男妇三人盛服入。审视之，乃夫与詹氏、张氏，大喜。详语家人，令各按次朝拜。张归家葬亲，叶同詹氏归省父母。张为亲继子，葬亲毕，叶等始面伍告辞，转瞬已杳。

    　　虚白道人曰：叶生一身享仙凡富贵之福，三地一时，其德安在哉？曰：秉性刚直耳。由是观之，可知有小德者享小福，有大德者享大福；若无德者，必不免于六极，其何福之有？

    　　借彭琏母子补出三处安置及前事未暇详叙者，文法错综入妙。 马竹吾

    　　此篇头绪甚多，而其间起伏照应处，俱见匠心。读竟，想见先生当日不知如何经营而始有此佳构。人谓有《聊斋》而是书不能并传于后，余谓有是书而《聊斋》亦不得擅美于前。后之阅者，或不以余言为谬也！ 尹亦山

    Explanation

    422

    One day, Wu was conversing with his descendants when suddenly three young men and women entered in fine attire. Upon closer inspection, they were identified as his husband and the ladies Zhan and Zhang. He was greatly pleased. He explained to his family, instructing each to pay their respects in turn. Zhang returned home to bury his parents, while Ye and Zhan went to visit their parents. Zhang, being the adopted son, completed the burial of his parents, and then Ye and the others came to bid farewell to Wu, who disappeared in the blink of an eye.

    The recluse Master Xubai remarked: "Ye Sheng enjoys blessings of both the divine and earthly realms, yet he possesses such virtue? It is because of his straightforward and upright nature." From this, we can understand that those with small virtues enjoy small blessings, and those with great virtues enjoy great blessings; if one has no virtue, they are inevitably subject to the six calamities, and what blessings could they have?

    The story of Peng Lian and his mother is used to fill in the details of three locations and events that were not fully explained earlier, and the writing style is intricate and masterful. — Ma Zhuwu

    This piece has many threads, and the interplay and connections between them all reveal the author's ingenuity. After reading it, one can imagine the effort and planning that must have gone into creating such a fine composition. Some say that even with Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio, this book cannot be passed down to future generations, but I say that with this book, Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio cannot excel before it. Readers of the future may not consider my words mistaken! — Yin Yishan

    423

    通体以道姑作线，头绪虽多，丝毫不乱，备极经营惨淡之致。 程伯孚

    　　崔洁

    　　崔洁，字圣与，大名人也。轻财好友，以此家日微；畴昔座上客亦寥寥如晓星残月，始知世态炎凉，滥交无益。一日郊游，见一人卧地上，知为病旅。崔谓客曰：“寒舍不远，屈客信宿，病愈后行，可乎？”曰：“若然，恩同再造。”遂扶客至家。客自言宗周白氏，世籍东昌。崔亦以姓字语客，急为设榻安憩，意明晨必力疾而去。乃病增重，卧床不起。崔为延医调理，崔妻亦质簪环助之，凡十二日而病始渐愈。先是，庄有富室某女得鬼病，已殆，屡言有能医之者，酬金若干。崔与白对饮，语及之。白喜曰：“果尔，无患居诸无赖矣。吾善符咒，但病后元气未复，不宜为此，授君代行之可也。”崔往，病果愈。五日后，富翁果奉金登门展谢。白于是尽传其术于崔。欲行，而崔妻适卒；殡后，洒泪而别。问后会期。谓崔曰：“吾云游无方，后会有期，不能豫定。”

    Explanation

    423

    The entire narrative is structured around a Daoist nun, with many threads intertwining but none becoming disordered, demonstrating exhaustive and meticulous planning and execution. Cheng BoFu

    Feng Qing

    Feng Qing, courtesy name ShengYu, was from Daming. He was generous with his wealth and valued friendship, but as a result, his family's fortunes gradually declined. In the past, his guests were as sparse as morning stars and waning crescent moons, and he began to realize the coldness of the world and the futility of superficial friendships. One day, while out in the countryside, he saw a person lying on the ground, knowing that the person was a sick traveler. Feng said to the guest, "My humble home is not far away. Would it be an inconvenience for you to stay for two nights? After you recover, you can continue your journey. Would that be acceptable?" The guest replied, "If so, I would be grateful as if reborn." Feng then helped the guest to his home. The guest introduced himself as Bai from the Zhou dynasty, originally from Dongchang. Feng also introduced himself to the guest and quickly arranged a bed for him to rest. He thought the guest would certainly leave the next morning despite his illness. However, the guest's condition worsened, and he could not get out of bed. Feng sought a doctor to treat him, and his wife even pawned her jewelry to help. After twelve days, the guest's illness began to improve. Previously, there was a wealthy family in the village whose daughter had contracted a ghostly illness and was near death. She had repeatedly mentioned that someone could cure her and offered a certain amount of gold as a reward. Feng and Bai were drinking together when the topic came up. Bai happily said, "If that is true, there is no need to worry about the unscrupulous. I am skilled in talismans and spells, but after the illness, my vital energy has not yet recovered, so it is not advisable for me to do this. I will teach you to perform it instead." Feng went to the girl's house, and indeed, her illness was cured. Five days later, the wealthy man indeed brought gold to the door to express his gratitude. Bai then fully taught Feng his skills. When Bai was about to leave, Feng's wife had just passed away; after the funeral, they parted ways with tears. They asked about their future meeting. Bai said to Feng, "I travel without a fixed place, and we will meet again in the future, but I cannot set a date in advance."

    424

    崔自白去后，术大行，委贽者踵接于门，崔以之小康。忽有索姓叟执贽来谒，云子祟于鬼，祈为驱逐。崔从之去，略施符水而归。晚，见女子搴帘入，视之，美而不识，惊问之。曰：“妾即祟索子之鬼也。索举家皆狐，妾爱索子仪表可人，乐与欢好。鬼祟狐也，非祟人也，于君何与？”崔以新鳏，又爱其美，不禁拥女于怀。女曰：“勿尔。鬼祟索氏之狐子，君能驱之，祟驱祟狐子之人，君亦能驱之耶？君诚不为丛驱爵，而善为爵驱丛者也。

    Explanation

    424

    After Cui Zibai left, his magical abilities became widely known and sought after. Those who presented gifts or credentials came one after another to his door, and Cui Zibai's financial situation improved as a result of the demand for his services. Suddenly, an elderly man of the So family presented himself to Cui Zibai, claiming to be troubled by a ghost and requesting his assistance. Cui Zibai followed him and performed a ritual, using magical symbols on water, before returning home.

    That evening, a beautiful woman pulled back the curtain and entered. Cui Zibai was surprised to see her and asked who she was. She replied, "I am the ghost that was troubling the So family. The entire family of the So family are fox spirits, and I love the So family member for her pleasant demeanor and enjoy her companionship. The ghost harms fox spirits, not humans, so what does this have to do with you?" Cui Zibai, being newly widowed and attracted to her beauty, could not help but embrace her. She said, "Don't do that. The ghost that harms the So family's fox spirit, you can drive away, but can you drive away the ghost that harms the fox spirit? You are truly not one who drives birds into a thicket, but one who is skilled at driving birds away from the thicket."

    425

    然妾鬼也，尚不利于狐，况人乎？设人也，愿终身奉之。”崔喜极，遂相狎。鬼曰：“事可一不可再。索女婉儿较妾尤艳，渠正在用君之际，若以女字君，则为之医，否则托故不往。彼爱子之心切于爱女，欲不女于君不得也。”崔善之。索家经崔之符水，鬼祟大绝，索父大喜。未五日，鬼仍至，祟亦甚。鬼且言于病者曰：“崔不来矣，渠欲娶汝妹为继室。若不与，汝勿望再生！”病者语其父。索不信，急遣人执重贽往请，崔果不至。索不得已，如其言许之。崔于是止于索室，日日伪设坛焚符。

    Explanation

    425

    Although I am a ghost, I am still not favorable to foxes, how much less so humans? If I were a human, I would willingly serve them for life." Cui was extremely happy and they then became intimate. The ghost said, "This matter can happen once but not again. The woman Wan'er from the Suo family is even more beautiful than I am, and he is currently using you. If he marries her to you, then he will treat you, otherwise, he will make an excuse not to come. His love for his son is stronger than his love for his daughter, and he cannot help but marry her to you." Cui agreed to this. The Suo family, after Cui's talismanic water, saw the ghostly disturbances cease, and the father was greatly pleased. However, after five days, the ghost returned, and the disturbances were even more severe. The ghost then said to the sick person, "Cui is not coming anymore, he wants to marry your sister as his second wife. If you do not agree, you will not be able to live again!" The sick person told his father. The Suo family did not believe him, and quickly sent someone with heavy gifts to invite Cui, but Cui indeed did not come. The Suo family, having no choice, agreed to his words and consented. Cui then stayed in the Suo household, every day pretending to set up altars and burn talismans.

    426

    及半月，鬼忽曰：“索家人俱出，惟婉儿独居。君入其室，素愿可遂也。”崔乃信步直诣女卧内，女惊曰：“君何以来？”崔曰：“尊亲以卿字吾，卿不知耶？”女曰：“知之。但宜于于归时会面，今非其时也。”崔曰：“今实天从人愿，万望垂怜。”女曰：“妾身虽贱，颇知礼仪。虽有父母之命，而先期乱之，与钻穴隙相类，我实不敢从命。”崔强求之。女欲撑拒而四肢如缚，盖女鬼暗为执之也，遂听崔所为。女曰：“君以术要妾，事或中变，妾纵不能死，断不二夫，君其俟之。

    Explanation

    426

    After half a month, the ghost suddenly said, "All the family has gone out, leaving only Wan'er alone. Sir, enter her room, and your long-held wish can now be fulfilled." Cui then walked directly into the girl's bedroom. Wan'er was startled and asked, "Why have you come?" Cui replied, "My respected parents have betrothed you to me; do you not know?" Wan'er said, "I know. But it is proper to meet during the wedding; now is not the time." Cui said, "Now truly the heavens have granted our wishes; I earnestly hope you will show mercy." Wan'er said, "I am a lowly woman, but I know some etiquette. Even though I have my parents' permission, to act prematurely and disrupt it, similar to peeping through holes, I truly dare not comply." Cui persisted in his request. Wan'er tried to resist but found her limbs bound, because the ghost of the girl secretly bound her, and she then allowed Cui to do as he wished. Wan'er said, "You used your magic to compel me; if circumstances change, even if I cannot die, I will never be unfaithful to two husbands; you should wait for me."

    427

    请君速行，若为家人遇之，羞愧难堪。”崔遂出。鬼曰：“我去，君事必谐，可与订送女之期而归。”崔从之。及期无耗，鬼为往探，返曰：“举家不知去向矣！”崔虽心恨之，无可如何。年馀，忽婉儿抱子至。崔愕然曰：“所抱伊谁？”女曰：“君之孽种，诞生已四月。家人恶其不类，咸欲相害。妾恐提防少疏，思不如乘间送来。”委子欲去，崔执祛挽留。女曰：“可团聚三日，妾父有故他出，四日当即归。”崔曰：“女子有夫即从夫，卿何畏父之甚也？”曰：“妾父得连理萱花带，善制狐，远近老狐皆畏之，何论妾？”三日后辞去。

    Explanation

    427

    "Please hurry," the ghost said. "If you meet them, you will feel ashamed and embarrassed." Cui then left. The ghost continued, "I will leave, and your matter will certainly succeed. You can agree on a date to send her and return." Cui followed the ghost's advice. By the agreed date, there was no news, so the ghost went to investigate and returned saying, "The whole family has disappeared!" Despite being angry, Cui could do nothing about it. After more than a year, suddenly Wan'er came with a child. Cui was startled and asked, "Who is the child she is holding?" The woman replied, "It is your illegitimate child, born four months ago. The family disliked him because he did not resemble them and wanted to harm him. I feared that our precautions were not tight enough, so I thought it better to take the opportunity to bring him." She left the child and wanted to go, but Cui held her arm and tried to persuade her to stay. She said, "We can spend three days together. My father has an urgent matter and has gone elsewhere. He will return in four days." Cui asked, "A woman should follow her husband. Why are you so afraid of your father?" She replied, "My father obtained the paired lotus and lily bracelet, which is skilled at controlling foxes. Both near and far, old foxes are afraid of him. How much more so for me?" Three days later, she left.

    428

    一日，崔与女鬼灯下弄子为乐，而婉儿仓皇至，衣服褴缕，面色黄瘦。大惊，方欲问之。曰：“君杀妾矣！妾父不见君之子，知送归于君，因大怒，逼令改适。妾不从，纳于冷室，疏食不饱。祈速救援，迟将瘐死！”须臾，含涕而去。崔念可与商此事者惟白君，而别后未一至。时方踌躇，闻扣门声，启视，则白也来矣。大喜过望，因语以婉儿之事。曰：“弟亦能伏狐，但未精，恐为所辱。可寻吾师求工其术。”乃急去。未几，婉儿至，泣曰：“君友白君连书三符，将妾父拘去矣！”崔喜曰：“白兄之为，实吾欲为而不能者，真吾良友也！”女怒曰：“妾之初心，真不料及此！方妾之求救于君也，为乞救妾，非乞制父也。

    Explanation

    428

    One day, Cui and the female ghost were having fun with a child by the lamp, when Wan'er suddenly arrived, her clothes tattered and her face pale and gaunt. Cui was greatly startled and was about to ask her what had happened. She said, "You have killed me! My father did not see your child, knew to send it back to you, and became very angry, forcing me to remarry. I refused, and he locked me in a cold room, feeding me meager meals that were not filling. I beg for quick rescue; if delayed, I will die in prison!" She then tearfully left. Cui thought that the only one he could discuss this with was Mr. Bai, but after they parted, he never came once. Cui was hesitating when he heard a knocking at the door. He opened it to look, and there was Bai. Cui was overjoyed beyond expectation and told him about what happened to Wan'er. Bai said, "My younger brother can also subdue foxes, but he is not very skilled, and I fear he will be humiliated. You can find my teacher to learn the skill." And then he hurried away. Shortly afterward, Wan'er returned, crying and saying, "Your friend Mr. Bai wrote three talismans consecutively, and took my father away!" Cui said, "What my younger brother did is exactly what I wanted to do but couldn't; he is truly my good friend!" Wan'er angrily replied, "My original intention was truly not to go this far! When I asked you for help, I was asking for help for myself, not to control my father."

    429

    且妾虽未得事君，业为君生子，夫妇之伦既彰，则翁婿之分即定，何不仁如是乎？”言已，悻悻欲去。崔留之曰：“仆听白兄自为而不恿怂之，即以德报怨也。”女曰：“何怨之有？君要妾父结婚，强要妾眠得孕，彼时不杀妾以除污辱，德莫厚焉。君不以为德，反以为怨耶？”崔曰：“此时庶可少留，以聊尽夫妻子母之欢。”曰：“父果无恙，妾不复去矣。”崔喜，许以见白。将行，女曰：“妾父之连理萱花带，想亦必在白生手，务讨回。狐得此易于成仙。”崔诺而去。至白所，见一狐系槽侧。白曰：“兄之来，殆尚为此老畜耶？”曰：“然。但是物弟之舅，妻之父，子之外祖也。”白曰：“言及此，不能不为兄恕之。”回首视狐，已不见矣，并取带而回。

    Explanation

    429

    Although I have not yet served you, I have already given you a child. The relationship between husband and wife has been established, and the distinction between father-in-law and son-in-law is now set. How unkind can you be like this?" After saying this, she angrily wanted to leave. Cui tried to persuade her, saying, "I listened to Brother Bai act on his own without encouraging him, which is repaying a grudge with kindness." The woman replied, "What grudge do I have? You forced my father to marry, and forced me to sleep with you to conceive. If you hadn't killed me to remove the disgrace, that would have been a great kindness. You don't consider this kindness, but instead see it as a grudge?" Cui said, "At this time, you might as well stay a little longer to enjoy the joy of being husband, wife, and mother-in-law." She said, "If my father is indeed unharmed, I will not leave you." Cui was pleased and agreed to see Bai. As they were about to leave, the woman said, "My father's twin-leaf萱flower belt, I think it must be in Bai's hands. Please make sure to retrieve it. If the fox gets this, it will easily become a spirit." Cui nodded and left. When he arrived at Bai's place, he saw a fox tied to the side of the trough. Bai said, "Your coming here, is it still because of this old beast?" The woman replied, "Yes. But this thing is my uncle, my wife's father, and my son's great-grandfather." Bai said, "When talking about this, I cannot help but forgive you." Looking back, the fox was no longer there, and he took the belt back.

    430

    女自得带而后，顿异素行，其子托女鬼顾复，自于静室端坐修省，崔不再四招之，不至寝室。嗣谓崔曰：“鬼不可以为配，妾亦不宜居夫人位。邻村司某之女艳甚，今新亡。君倩冰人言能活之，渠痛女情切，事必成。”司果喜，即倩媒邀崔医女。婉儿接女鬼同往，推鬼与尸合，司女立苏。是日，司即以婿礼款崔，定期于归焉。自是婉儿得专心静养，不作雄雉下上吟矣。时犹为子制衣履一二事，每托言入山采药，恒望月一归来。子少长，来时渐稀，后不至。

    Explanation

    430

    After the woman obtained the belt, she suddenly changed her previous conduct. Her son was raised by a female ghost, and she sat alone in a quiet room, meditating and reflecting. Cui did not call her four times, and she did not go to the bedroom. She then told Cui, "A ghost cannot be a suitable partner, and I am also not suitable to be in the position of a lady. The daughter of someone named Si from the neighboring village is very beautiful, and she has just recently died. You should ask the matchmaker to say that she can be brought back to life." Si was delighted and immediately asked the matchmaker to invite Cui to revive the girl. Wan'er accompanied the female ghost to the place, pushed the ghost to merge with the body, and the girl of Si immediately revived. On that day, Si immediately treated Cui with the formalities of a son-in-law and set a date for the wedding. From then on, Wan'er could focus on quiet修养 and no longer sang like a rooster. She still made clothes and shoes for her son occasionally, often claiming to go into the mountains to gather herbs and always returning once a month. As the son grew older, his visits became less frequent, and eventually he stopped coming altogether.

    431

    此篇可与《聊斋》长亭传并读。 尹亦山

    　　写鬼狐多情，恻恻动人。 马竹吾

    　　牛偿债（稿本作“常山某”）

    　　常山某，行素不端，惟利是喻。有傅姓烦某贷银百金，某索券去，昧其贷金而己用焉。傅知，问之。某佯怒，以为已交傅手。借主以借券为据，将控傅。傅惧，按期并本息偿之。偿银后，家有畜牛暴死，傅叹祸不单行，闷气填胸，蒙首而睡。梦某来见，气象愁惨，大异平时。问其来意，曰：“特为服役，折还银债耳。”比醒，牧人告牛死复苏。傅疑趋视，果卧如素。曰：“某真来耶！”牛闻之遽起，仰首张口，有欲言之势。牧人问之，傅以梦告。从此举家鞭打叱骂，以解夙恨。如是者八年，某亦卧病不起，身上时有鞭痕。一日，傅又梦某曰：“吾服劳虽久，折还无期，请归鬻产偿之。”傅醒，知牛必死，已而果然。心甚悯之，亦无望其还也。

    Explanation

    431

    This piece can be read alongside "Changting Zhuan" from Liaozhai Zhiyi. —尹亦山

    It writes about ghosts and fox spirits with deep emotion, touching the heart. —马竹吾

    Ox Repaying a Debt (the manuscript reads "Changshan Someone")

    A person from Changshan, who had always been morally corrupt and only understood profit, was asked by a man surnamed Fu to borrow a hundred gold coins. The person took the promissory note and used the loaned money for himself. When Fu found out, he asked him. The person pretended to be angry, thinking he had already handed the money to Fu. The lender used the promissory note as evidence and was going to sue Fu. Fu, fearing legal action, paid back the principal and interest on time. After repaying the money, a domesticated ox in his household died suddenly. Fu sighed that misfortunes never come alone, feeling depressed and covering his head to sleep. He dreamed that the person came to see him, looking sad and different from usual. He asked what the person came for, and the person said, "I came to work off the debt." Upon waking, the herder told him the ox had died and then revived. Fu doubted and went to check, and indeed the ox was lying as usual. He said, "Did you really come?" The ox heard this and suddenly stood up, raising its head and opening its mouth as if to speak. The herder asked, and Fu told him about the dream. From then on, the entire family beat and scolded the ox to resolve their old grudge. This continued for eight years, and the person also fell ill and could not get up, with marks of beatings on his body. One day, Fu dreamed that the person said, "I have worked for a long time, but the repayment has no end. Please go sell your property to repay me." Fu woke up, knowing the ox would die, and indeed it did. He felt very sorry for it and no longer expected repayment.

    432

    嗣闻某病愈，急货良田，折柬招傅。傅往，某执家人礼甚恭，慨然曰：“曩所贷银，实吾吞使。兹如数凑足，万望哂纳。”傅曰：“何必，事已往，可不咎也。”某忽作牛喘曰：“君果不施慈悲，诚忍心害吾。”傅见其神气难堪，曰：“请以八年之劳，止利归本可乎？”某喜，席终，遣人负银送至傅家。后某交易公平，一乡称愿人焉。

    Explanation

    432

    Upon hearing that someone had recovered from illness, he hastily sold his good farmland and sent an invitation to invite a teacher. When the teacher arrived, the host treated him with great courtesy as if he were a family member and said with a sigh, "The silver I borrowed in the past was actually misappropriated by me. I humbly hope you will accept it." The teacher replied, "Why bother? The matter is past, and it can be forgiven." Suddenly, the host started wheezing like a cow and said, "If you truly do not show compassion, you are indeed cruel and harmful to me." Seeing the host's distressed state, the teacher said, "Can we settle the interest and return the principal based on eight years of labor?" The host was pleased and, at the end of the meal, sent someone to deliver the silver to the teacher's home. Later, this person conducted transactions fairly and was praised throughout the village.

    433

    虚白道人曰：债宜急还，理也，况此昧良之事？或吓诈借为嫖赌之资者，其较贸易无本，居诸无赖，迥不侔矣。余邑北鄙有农人之舅氏某，饮博匪类，屡向伊甥强贷。或告以力不能给，某怒以石自破其首，喊谓被甥打伤，非与之钱不已。一日，甥昼寝，忽见舅来，骇曰：“钱已使尽乎！”曰：“然。但今日非来借贷，乃来还债耳。”甥方错愕，舅已不见。醒，知为梦，异之。妻笑入曰：“马生骡。”曰：“骡即某舅！”遂将梦与妻言之。嗣每用骡，家人仍以舅呼之，极调良。偶有货陶器者，骡见之，断缰奔踶，蹄踏粉碎，无瓦全。其人让伊，伊曰：“阿舅毁之，吾愿赔补。”其人讶曰：“何以骡为舅？”伊历言舅氏姓名并投生还债之异。曰：“若然，君无赔。吾欠其钱，今所毁器，适如其数。吾得借此以清债，亦佳。”

    433

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: Debts should be repaid promptly, for it is the right thing to do, especially in such unjust matters. Those who borrow under threat or deceit for gambling and debauchery are far worse than those who trade without capital or idle without shame. In the northern outskirts of my hometown, there was a farmer's uncle named Mr. Someone, who was a drunkard and gambler of disreputable character. He repeatedly demanded loans from his nephew. When told he lacked the means to repay, he angrily struck himself with a stone, breaking his own head, and cried out that he had been beaten by his nephew, insisting he would not stop until he received money. One day, while the nephew was napping, he suddenly saw his uncle appear and asked in shock, "Have you spent all the money?" The uncle replied, "Yes. But today I am not here to borrow; I am here to repay the debt." The nephew was confused and startled, but by the time he realized it, the uncle had already disappeared. Upon waking, he knew it was a dream and found it strange. His wife laughed and said, "A horse gave birth to a mule." He replied, "The mule is my uncle!" Then he told his wife about the dream. Thereafter, whenever he used the mule, the family continued to call it "uncle," and it was extremely well-behaved. Once, when there was a seller of pottery, the mule saw it, broke its reins, ran and kicked, trampling it to pieces, leaving no intact pieces. The seller reproached him, and he said, "Uncle destroyed it; I am willing to compensate." The seller was surprised and asked, "Why is the mule your uncle?" He recounted his uncle's name and the strange story of his reincarnation to repay the debt. The seller said, "If so, you need not compensate. I owe him money, and the destroyed pottery matches the amount. I can use this to settle the debt," and thought it was a good thing.

    434

    更有一事可作笑柄：一大债主积欠无算，死后遇一人曰：“汝欠尚未还。”曰：“吾托生鸡犬以偿之。”又遇一人曰：“汝欠何不还？”曰：“吾托生牛马以偿之。”后遇一人曰：“汝欠金数万，理合还也。”恻然曰：“天下无值数万金之物，虽欲偿之而不能也。无已，请生为若父，多方生财以偿之。”

    Explanation

    434

    There is another thing that can be a laughing stock: a great debtor with accumulated debts beyond calculation. After death, he met someone who said, "You still have debts to repay." He replied, "I will be reborn as a chicken or a dog to repay them." Then he met another person who said, "Why don't you repay your debts?" He answered, "I will be reborn as a cow or a horse to repay them." Later, he met a third person who said, "You owe tens of thousands of gold pieces, and you should repay them." The debtor sighed and said, "There are no items in the world worth tens of thousands of gold pieces. Even if I wanted to repay them, I couldn't. If there is no other way, please be reborn as my father, and I will generate wealth in many ways to repay you."

    435

    冯官屯（稿本作“田思义”）

    　　茌平冯官屯田生，字思义，有孝行。家綦贫，早孤，娶妻李氏甚贤。甫二年，妻暴卒。母旦夕尸饔，不堪其苦，田深以为虞。忽一叟扶杖来，谓田曰：“闻君丧偶，致母劬劳，不急续断弦以慰母心，可谓孝乎？”田问客姓氏。曰：“宿姓，违此少远。”田曰：“君故不知，吾不惟贫无以为礼，且谁肯以爱女适我御穷？”宿曰：“仆有息女，可佐中馈。如君不弃，明晚仆送女至某桥西。君遵齐俗备轿往娶，以夜子初为度，迟速皆不可。”田应诺。宿去后，田向村人言之，咸以为妄。村中固有赁货家，其人曰：“吾借与嫁娶轿子。诸公盍同往，万一其事不爽，岂非美举？”众喜从之。

    Explanation

    435

    Feng Guantun (drafted as "Tian Sheng")

    In Feng Guantun, a village in Ciping, there lived a man named Tian Sheng, whose courtesy name was Siyi. He was known for his filial piety. His family was extremely poor, and he lost his father at a young age. He married a virtuous woman named Li, but just two years later, she died suddenly. His mother, who served meals day and night, could not bear the hardship, and Tian was deeply concerned. Suddenly, an old man came, leaning on a cane, and said to Tian, "I hear you have lost your wife, and this has caused your mother great distress. Is it not filial to quickly remarry and comfort your mother's heart?" Tian asked the old man his surname. The old man replied, "My surname is Su, and I live a little far away from here." Tian said, "You may not know, but I am not only poor and unable to afford a proper wedding, but who would be willing to give their beloved daughter to a man in such poverty?" The old man said, "I have a daughter who can help with household affairs. If you do not reject me, I will send her to a certain bridge westward tomorrow evening. You should follow the customs of Qi and prepare a sedan chair to come and take her. We will meet at the beginning of the night, and neither too early nor too late." Tian agreed. After the old man left, Tian told the villagers about the encounter, and they all thought it was absurd. However, there was a man in the village who rented out goods, and he said, "I can lend you a sedan chair for the wedding. Why don't you all go together? If this matter turns out to be true, it would surely be a good deed!" The villagers were pleased and agreed to accompany him.

    436

    届期，至桥西，毫无动静。众扫兴欲返，忽西来一车两骡，有人遥呼曰：“诸公代田郎迎亲者耶？”佥曰：“然。”曰：“仆即宿某。”于是扶女下车登轿，宿从之至家始去。母见新妇极美，不类贫家妇，恐难处约。乃克尽妇道，其曲意奉母较李氏犹谨。但入门后，家渐盈裕，凡所经营谋画皆如前知。田固疑其非人，恒问之。氏曰：“妾即非人，而所为无异于人，何必研诘？实告君，妾狐也，妾父慕君孝而怜君贫，故遣妾事君。今母寿及期颐，数月后当无病终，棺椁、衣衾已预备；已为君纳婢生子，君实不觉也。”

    Explanation

    436

    On the appointed day, they arrived at the western side of the bridge, but there was no sign of any activity. The group felt disappointed and was about to return when suddenly a carriage pulled by two mules came from the west. Someone called out from afar, "Are you all here to escort Tian's bride?" Everyone replied, "Yes." The person said, "I am Su." They then helped the woman out of the carriage and into the sedan chair, and Su followed them home before leaving. The mother saw that the new wife was extremely beautiful, unlike a woman from a poor family, and feared she would be difficult to manage. However, the new wife fully fulfilled her duties as a wife and served the mother with humility and respect, even more so than Li's wife. After entering the household, the family gradually became prosperous, and all the plans and strategies she made were as if she had known them beforehand. Tian suspected that she was not human and often asked her about it. She said, "I am indeed not human, but my actions are no different from those of a human, so why investigate further? Let me tell you the truth: I am a fox spirit. My father admired your filial piety and pitied your poverty, so he sent me to serve you. Now, your mother will reach the age of a hundred, and she will die peacefully in a few months. The coffin, burial clothes, and other preparations are already made. I have also arranged for you to have a child through a servant, and you will not even realize it."

    437

    先是，女买婢尤氏，劝田纳为小星，田不肯，且厌其丑。女以小术障眼以欺田，使其虽御婢而仍自为御妻也者，以故生子亦以为宿氏所出也，至是始明言之。曰：“妾将复命于父，从兹不来矣。”田留之。曰：“今兹未能，岁馀将自去。”且曰：“妾有一言，冯官屯非久居之所。某年月日，君切记！可速将田产尽货于人，迁徙他处。”田不答。女又曰：“妾语非妄，万勿以妇人言不宜听从。”田始心动。问迁于何地为安。曰：“曲阜。是言不可以语人。村中可与谋者，惟张忠，然彼不问亦不与言也。”

    Explanation

    437

    Previously, the woman bought a maid named You Shi and tried to persuade her husband to take her as a concubine, but he refused and was also repulsed by her appearance. The woman used a trick to deceive her husband, making him believe he was having relations with her while actually having relations with the maid, so the child was thought to be hers. At this point, she openly stated her intention. She said, "I will return to my father and will not come back." Her husband tried to keep her. She replied, "I cannot do it now, but I will leave in a year." She also said, "I have a piece of advice: Fengguantun is not a place to stay long. On a certain date, you must remember! Sell all your land quickly and move to another place." Her husband did not respond. She repeated, "My words are not empty; do not dismiss a woman's words as inappropriate to listen to." Her husband then became concerned and asked where they should move. She said, "Qufu. This matter cannot be told to others. In the village, the only person you can trust is Zhang Zhong, but he will not ask or speak about it."

    438

    一夕同酌，及醉而寝，比醒，已失所在。不二年，咸丰乙卯，冯官屯遇乱，男女无逃出者，惟田、张二家免焉。余闻忠亦孝友人。

    　　益智录卷之七

    　　月仙

    　　江苏徐生，字小峰。访友归，待渡江边，见渔人获双鲜鲤，鲜艳可爱。忽见其鱼转睛盼人，近前审视，复连转数四，异之，因市而放诸江。至家，语妻，妻曰：“鱼转睛，必非凡物。活之，必食其报。”生哂曰：“理或然也。”

    Explanation

    438

    In one night, they drank together and became drunk, falling asleep. When they woke up, the person had already disappeared. Within two years, in the year of Xianfeng Yi Mao (1855), chaos broke out in Fengguantun. No one was able to escape, except for the families of Tian and Zhang, who were spared. I heard that they were loyal, filial, and friendly people.

    A man from Jiangsu named Xu Sheng, with the courtesy name Xiaofeng, returned from visiting friends and waited at the riverbank to cross. He saw a fisherman catch two fresh carp, which were vivid and lovely. Suddenly, he noticed the fish turning its eyes and looking at him. He approached to examine it closely, and the fish turned its eyes several times repeatedly. Finding it strange, he bought the fish and released it into the river.

    When he arrived home, he told his wife about the fish. His wife said, "The fish turning its eyes must be something extraordinary. If we let it live, we will surely receive its reward." The husband smiled and said, "It might be true."

    439

    生家式微。一子名蒲，幼聘同里方学福女为室，欲与毕姻而无资。未几，生病没，蒲竭资营葬，家愈穷。桃夭之咏已及瓜期，萍逐之身如同梗断。闺中少女，不堪虚度青春；露外窭人，靡计能牵红幕。方家叠次催亲，实蓄翻覆意，蒲无以应命，不得已而亡去。

    　　一日渡江，失足坠水，正危急间，水面漂来一木，以手接之。浮沉经夜，木停岸侧，得人扶救而上。时维季秋，天寒水冷，身虽幸生，而困顿成疾，不能步履，匍匐入江村，欲觅投止。见一老人启户出，蒲告以故，哀其垂怜。老人掖蒲入耳舍，脱其湿衣，覆之被，与以食物。蒲病增剧，食已不能下咽，乃自言曰：“吾徐蒲必为他乡之鬼！”言际，有老媪过室门，遂问曰：“子徐姓耶？族居何处？”蒲实告之。媪去复来，曰：“徐小峰子与同宗否？”蒲答曰：“先父也。”媪闻之急去。欻尔，前老人捧衣冠至，曰：“请速着，吾家姑娘即出。”曰：“汝姑娘为谁？”曰：“不必问。

    Explanation

    439

    The Xu Family's Decline

    The Xu family had fallen from prosperity. One son, named Pu, was betrothed at a young age to a girl from the same village, Fang Xuefu's daughter, as his wife. They wished to complete the marriage but lacked the means. Not long after, he fell ill and died. Pu exhausted his resources to bury him, and the family became even poorer. The time for the peach blossom ode had come, but his life was like a wandering duckweed, broken like a floating twig. A girl in the inner chambers, unable to bear the waste of her youth, and a poor man outside, with no means to bring in a red curtain—Fang's family repeatedly urged the marriage, secretly harboring ulterior motives. Pu had no way to respond, and was forced to flee.

    One day, crossing the river, he lost his footing and fell into the water. At the critical moment, a piece of wood floated to the surface, and he grabbed it with his hand. He drifted and sank through the night, the wood stopping at the shore, and he was helped up by someone. It was late autumn, the weather cold and the water chilly. Though he was fortunate to survive, he became ill from exhaustion and could not walk. Crawling into a village by the river, seeking shelter, he saw an old man open the door. Pu told him the reason, pleading for his mercy. The old man helped Pu into a side room, took off his wet clothes, covered him with a quilt, and gave him food. Pu's illness worsened, and he could not swallow the food. He said, "I, Xu Pu, will surely become a ghost in a foreign land!" As he spoke, an old woman passed by the room. She asked, "Are you from the Xu family? Where do you live?" Pu truthfully answered. The old woman went away and returned, saying, "Are you related to Xu Xiaofeng's son?"
