Pu replied, "He is my father." The old woman, upon hearing this, hurried away. Suddenly, the old man appeared with clothing, saying, "Please dress quickly; my daughter will come out." "Who is your daughter?" "There's no need to ask."

    440

    ”衣冠甫毕，有丽人率二婢一媪出，谓蒲曰：“固知恩公之子时运未至，不图困苦如此。”急令婢媪扶蒲行，女后随之。媪曰：“扶客于客舍乎？”女曰：“客舍无人伺候。”媪复曰：“内室乎？”女曰：“可。”未几，入一厅，卧蒲纱帐中。蒲见屋宇华好，陈设芳丽，角枕边有五纹新靴一双。媪急取而掩之，曰：“是物犹置此耶？”蒲知所卧即女榻，心大异。俄顷女入，问蒲胡为到此，缘何得病，蒲历言之。女曰：“勿尤人，缘君命蹇。老母康健否？”曰：“康健。但日用无着，时挂心头。”女曰：“勿虞此。饥乎？”蒲答以不饥。女曰：“岂有连日未食不饥之理！”遂去。少顷，以食物进。

    Explanation

    440

    Just as Par had finished dressing, a beautiful woman, accompanied by two maids and an old woman, emerged. She said to Par, "I knew that the son of your benefactor's time of good fortune had not yet come, but I did not expect him to be in such a state of hardship." She quickly ordered the maids and the old woman to help Par walk, and the lady followed behind. The old woman asked, "Should we take the guest to the guesthouse?" The lady replied, "There is no one to attend to the guest in the guesthouse." The old woman then asked, "Should we take him to the inner room?" The lady said, "Yes, we can."

    Soon, they entered a hall, and Par was laid down in a silken canopy. He saw that the house was splendid and beautifully decorated, with fragrant and elegant furnishings. Near a corner pillow, there was a pair of new boots with five patterns. The old woman quickly took them and covered them, saying, "Is this thing still left here?" Par realized that he was lying on the lady's bed and felt very surprised.

    In a short while, the lady entered and asked Par why he was there and how he had become ill. Par recounted it all. The lady said, "Do not blame others; it is because your fate is unlucky. Is your mother healthy?" Par replied, "She is healthy. But we have no means to support ourselves, and I often worry about it." The lady said, "Do not worry about this. Are you hungry?" Par answered that he was not hungry. The lady said, "How can there be no hunger after several days without food?" She then left. A little while later, she brought food.

    441

    蒲本不思食，恐负女意，强食之，不知何物，但觉其味甚甘，顷刻而尽，意犹未足。女曰：“病体宜节食饮，少迟再食可也。”蒲视女，姿貌如仙，略无伦比。曰：“躬蒙鸿恩，俨同再造，愿闻芳名，俟效结草之报。”女曰：“妾母修道江山，惟妾姊妹居此。妾名月仙，水仙则姊名。”蒲曰：“贱恙增重，奈何？”月仙曰：“勿虑。可无药有喜。”未几，又来一丽人，雅丽不减月仙。见蒲愕然曰：“榻上何人？”月仙曰：“徐小峰之公子。”丽人曰：“徐小峰之公子，即宜卧妹榻耶？宵来阿妹得无欲与同榻乎？羞！羞！”言已，负气去。月仙晕红上颊，脉脉不语。蒲问谁何，女曰：“即姊姊水仙。”蒲不胜叹美。及晚，月仙与蒲寝对面榻。

    Explanation

    441

    Pu originally did not want to eat, fearing he would disappoint the girl's intentions, but forced himself to eat. He did not know what it was, but found its taste very sweet, and finished it in a moment, still feeling unsatisfied. The girl said, "The sick body should moderate food and drink; it can be eaten again after a short delay." Pu looked at the girl, whose appearance was like a fairy, hardly comparable. He said, "I personally received great favor, as if reborn. I wish to hear your beautiful name and await to repay with the grass-knotting gesture." The girl replied, "My mother practices Daoism in the mountains, and only my sister and I live here. I am named Yue Xian, and Shui Xian is my sister's name." Pu said, "My illness has worsened; what shall I do?" Yue Xian said, "Do not worry. There is joy without medicine." Soon after, another beautiful woman arrived, equally as lovely as Yue Xian. Seeing Pu, she exclaimed in surprise, "Who is on the bed?" Yue Xian said, "He is the son of Xu Xiaofeng." The woman said, "The son of Xu Xiaofeng, is he to sleep in my sister's bed? Did my sister perhaps want to sleep with him last night? Shame! Shame!" After saying this, she left in a huff. Yue Xian blushed and remained silent. Pu asked who she was, and the girl said, "That is my sister, Shui Xian." Pu could not praise her enough. By evening, Yue Xian and Pu slept on opposite beds.

    442

    蒲曰：“仆与卿有亲故乎？”月曰：“无亲有故。”蒲问之，月曰：“其故非君所及知。”蒲夜梦月仙促其起，导与俱去。未几至一池塘，池不宽广，其水清澈见底。月曰：“浴之，病当愈。”蒲少迟疑，月推坠之。蒲觉其水温暖，体甚舒畅。半刻间，热不可堪，急呼月仙援之，而月仙已渺。少顷，失足坑坎，过涉灭顶，惊醒，汗出如洗，单衾尽湿。女曰：“汗出勿动，动则汗止。”蒲应诺，忖度曰：“渠何以知吾汗？得无所梦有因乎？”少时汗解，病若失。时已昧爽，乞食于女，女急起奉餐，殷勤臻至。及晚，蒲牵女与同寝。女曰：“当尊恙未愈时，应无如许奢望。”蒲曰：“然。然观寝仆之所即卿寝之处，知卿早有意存焉。”女微笑，遂相欢好。

    Explanation

    442

    Pú said, "Do I have any close relatives or acquaintances with you?" The moon replied, "No close relatives, but acquaintances." Pú asked further, and the moon said, "The reason is beyond your knowledge." That night, Pú dreamed of the moon fairy urging him to wake up and leading him away. Soon they arrived at a pond, not very wide, with water so clear that the bottom could be seen. The moon said, "Bathe in it, and your illness will be cured." Pú hesitated slightly, and the moon pushed him into the water. He felt the water warm and his body very relaxed. After a short while, the heat became unbearable, and he urgently called for the moon fairy to help, but the moon fairy had vanished. Soon after, he stumbled into a pit, was submerged, and woke up in a panic, drenched in sweat, with his single blanket completely soaked. The woman said, "Do not move when you sweat, or it will stop." Pú agreed, and pondered, "How did she know I was sweating? Could there be a reason behind my dream?" After a short time, the sweat subsided, and the illness seemed to disappear. It was already early morning, and he asked the woman for food. She quickly got up to serve him, showing great attentiveness and care. By evening, Pú took the woman to sleep with him. She said, "When you were ill, you should not have had such desires." Pú replied, "Yes. But seeing that the place where I slept is the same as where you sleep, I know you had intentions from the beginning." The woman smiled, and they became intimate.

    443

    次日，水仙来，言曰：“即午洁治豆觞，奉迓致贺，伏冀辱临。”蒲问何所，答以舟中，蒲喜极。水曰：“此地近江，携手同行，可乎？”既而三人谈笑而往。至江边，见有彩船一只，帐幔维新。既登舟，舟自行如矢。蒲异之。逾时至海，二女跳海中，舟亦入水，蒲大惊。见水立如堵，去舟三四尺，转惊为喜。未几，舟落海底，忽见一大门，如官府。月仙、水仙皆宫装立门外以俟。蒲下船，二女导入。舍宇宏丽，宫殿巍然。

    Explanation

    443

    The next day, Water Lily arrived and said, "At noon, we have prepared a modest feast, respectfully inviting you to join us in celebration. I humbly hope you will grace us with your presence." Pu asked where they would be, and she replied that it was on a boat. Pu was overjoyed. Water Lily said, "This place is near the river. Shall we walk together hand in hand?" Soon after, the three of them went on a laughing and talking journey. Upon reaching the riverside, they saw a brightly decorated boat with fresh curtains. Once aboard, the boat moved swiftly like an arrow. Pu was amazed. After some time, they arrived at the sea, where the two women jumped into the water, and the boat also submerged. Pu was greatly startled. He saw the water standing like a wall, several feet away from the boat, and his shock turned to joy. Soon, the boat sank to the bottom of the sea, and suddenly, a large gate appeared, resembling an official building. Moon Lily and Water Lily were both dressed in court attire, standing outside the gate awaiting them. Pu disembarked, and the two women led him inside. The buildings were grand and magnificent, and the palaces stood tall and imposing.

    444

    蒲曰：“此何所？”水仙曰：“此龙君贰室，暂假以贺新郎。”既而肆筵设席，备极丰盛。忽来美女十八人，各执乐器，分列左右，檀板一击，洋洋盈耳。二女曰：“有乐不可无舞，请献小技以侑酒。”遂并起，对舞席前，态度合乐拍，兰麝溢几席。多时始已。蒲揖谢之曰：“下界鲰生，得闻钧天之奏，三生之幸。请彻乐。”女如命。日已晡，水仙请蒲行，曰：“此处不可久留。”二女送蒲至门外，有草束赤须龙一条，令蒲闭目乘之，戒勿视。蒲知其神异，从之。
    觉龙飞升，潜扪以手，鳞甲如生。思欲开目以瞻其异，微睁，见所乘实真龙。四顾，云连海气，风带潮声，惊且喜，以为此人生难睹之奇观。忽觉龙降，乃大惧。复闭目，而龙仍坠。及海，觉有人承接，视之，乃月仙，相携立波面，如履平地。女急拔玉簪投海中，俄有舟自水中出。女携蒲乘之，达海岸，舟忽飞腾，下视名山大川，如垤如线。未几，落庭前；甫下，舟已杳，惟玉簪在地。女拾而簪之。

    444

    Pú asked, "Where is this place?" The Water Deity replied, "This is the second chamber of the Dragon Lord, temporarily borrowed to congratulate the new groom." Soon, a feast was laid out, and it was extremely abundant and lavish. Suddenly, eighteen beautiful women appeared, each holding a musical instrument, arranged on both sides. When the wooden clapper was struck once, the sound filled the ears. The two women said, "If there is music, there must be dance. Please offer a small skill to accompany the wine." Then they all rose together, dancing facing each other in front of the seat. Their movements matched the rhythm of the music, and the fragrance of orchids and musk filled the tables. After a long time, it ended. Pú bowed and thanked them, saying, "A humble being from the lower realms, to hear the music of the heavens is a great fortune in three lifetimes. Please stop the music." The women followed his request. The day had already turned to evening, and the Water Deity asked Pú to leave, saying, "This place cannot be stayed in for long." The two women escorted Pú to the gate, where there was a dragon with a red beard wrapped in grass. They ordered Pú to close his eyes and ride it, warning him not to look. Knowing it was supernatural, Pú followed their instructions.

    He felt the dragon rising and secretly touched it with his hand, the scales and armor feeling real. He thought about opening his eyes to see the wonder, and slightly opened them. He saw that what he was riding was a real dragon. Looking around, he saw clouds connected with the sea's atmosphere, and the wind carried the sound of the tide. He was both surprised and happy, thinking this was a rare and wonderful sight in life. Suddenly, he felt the dragon descend, and he became greatly afraid. He closed his eyes again, but the dragon continued to descend. When they reached the sea, he felt someone catching him. He looked at it, and it was the Moon Deity. They held hands and stood on the surface of the water, as if walking on flat ground. The woman quickly pulled out a jade hairpin and threw it into the sea. Soon, a boat emerged from the water. The woman took Pú and boarded it, and they reached the shore. The boat suddenly flew up, and looking down, he saw famous mountains and rivers, like small mounds and threads. Not long after, it landed in front of the courtyard. As soon as he stepped down, the boat had already disappeared, only the jade hairpin was on the ground. The woman picked it up and put it back in her hair.

    445

    水仙自庭中出，曰：“郎君受惊哉？妾以为乘龙之客无他虑，小妹恐有不测，竟果遭此颠险，夫妇之关切何其笃也。”蒲曰：“卿等系何神人，祈明示，以释疑怀。”月曰：“拟以为神，何敢当。要之，妾等实非人。”一日，月与蒲饮于临江楼。蒲见江水浪叠千层，波翻万里，遂述刘定公之言曰：“微禹，吾其鱼乎？”月曰：“君生不辰，求欲如鱼而不可得。”既而蒲醉，击案而歌曰：“嘉肴毕具兮，食无鱼。”月仙不悦，曰：“君何不能忘情于鱼？妾不便食鱼，若明言不便之故，恐君亦不忍食。”

    Explanation

    445

    A water fairy emerged from the courtyard and said, "My lord, are you startled? I thought a distinguished guest would have no other concerns, but my younger sister feared an unpredictable event, and indeed she suffered this perilous situation. How deep is the concern between husband and wife!" Pu replied, "What deities or spirits are you? Please clearly show yourselves, to dispel my doubts." Yue said, "Considered as deities, how dare we accept. Essentially, we are not human." One day, Yue and Pu drank together at the pavilion by the river. Pu saw the river water with waves piled up in thousands of layers, rolling for ten thousand miles, and then quoted the words of Liu Dinggong: "Without Yu, would we be fish?" Yue said, "You were born at an unlucky time, and your desire to be like a fish is unattainable." Afterward, Pu became drunk and hit the table while singing, "The delicious dishes are fully prepared, but there is no fish to eat." The fairy Yue was not pleased and said, "Why can't you forget your attachment to fish? I am inconvenient to eat fish. If I clearly state the inconvenience, I fear you will also be unwilling to eat."

    446

    月知蒲时运甚恶，不令出游，惟日以酒棋为乐。水仙偶至，惟姊妹谈笑，不与蒲语。蒲心恨之，因以语月，月曰：“俟妾乘间为君调处之。”一夕，月曰：“妾姊善睡，今忘禬户，君速往，有一夜之交，情意自不能薄。”蒲喜极，起身欲去，月止之曰：“姊卧，身旁常设宝剑以自卫，惧受其伤，当先取来。”蒲取剑交月仙，复往。水仙觉而醒，急取剑，蒲笑曰：“仆早取寄他处。”水仙曰：“阿妹害我。”晨兴尤月仙，愠见于色。月仙曰：“姊忘禮户，他见房门虚掩而为之，于妹何咎？”水仙曰：“取剑之谋，亦渠自筹耶？”月仙不能答。自是水仙与蒲款暱若月仙，但日暮即辞去。一夕蒲诣水仙寝室，则室门坚禬，呼之不应。次夕复然。月仙笑曰：“勿往矣。得意之事，可一不可再。”

    Explanation

    446

    Yue knew that Pu's fate was very bad, so she did not allow him to go out, and he only found joy in wine and chess. When Shui Xian occasionally came, she only chatted and laughed with her sister and did not speak to Pu. Pu hated her in his heart and told Yue about it. Yue said, "I will wait for an opportunity to mediate for you." One night, Yue said, "My sister is good at sleeping, and now she has forgotten to close the door. You should go quickly, and there will be a one-night encounter. The feelings will naturally not be thin." Pu was extremely happy and got up to leave, but Yue stopped him and said, "My sister is lying down, and she always sets a precious sword by her side for self-defense. You fear being injured by her, so you should first take it." Pu took the sword and handed it over to Yue, then went again. Shui Xian felt and woke up, hurriedly taking the sword. Pu laughed and said, "I took it early and sent it elsewhere." Shui Xian said, "My sister harms me." In the morning, she especially blamed Yue, and anger appeared on her face. Yue said, "My sister forgot to close the door, and he saw the room door half open and did it. What fault does it have to her sister?" Shui Xian said, "Whether the plan to take the sword is also something he thought of himself?" Yue could not answer. Since then, Shui Xian and Pu were close like Yue, but at dusk, they immediately left. One night, Pu visited Shui Xian's bedroom, but the room door was firmly closed, and calling her, there was no response. The next night, it was the same. Yue laughed and said, "Do not go again. A pleasant thing can happen once but not twice."

    447

    一日，月仙姊妹治具与蒲饮，而膳馐盛他日，蒲讶之。水仙曰：“贵诞在即，君忘诸乎？”蒲闻之，触动心怀，不觉蹙额太息。水仙曰：“宜喜而忧，如有忧而喜乎？”蒲曰：“非也。计别老母兹已数月，奉养无人，是以忧耳。且仆自幼聘方氏，渠年已过笄，尚无力完婚，是亦憾事。”因言方翁有嫌贫绝婚之意。月仙曰：“既疏定省，理合速返。兹有妾姊妹奉事，方家既嫌贫，可任其择富者嫁之。”蒲不悦曰：“君子之道，造端夫妇，既有成说，岂可改乎？”月仙笑曰：“妾反言以相试，君果以妾言为是，妾亦厌君薄德而为之寒心。君归，舟乎？车乎？”答曰：“乘车劳，乘舟逸，愿舟。”二女皆笑。执烛出，于庭前地下画彩船一只，应用之物堆集其中。

    Explanation

    447

    One day, the sisters Yuexian and Shuixian prepared provisions for a meal with Pu, and the dishes were more elaborate than usual, which surprised Pu. Shuixian asked, "Your birthday is approaching soon, have you forgotten?" Upon hearing this, Pu was deeply moved and sighed, furrowing his brow. Shuixian said, "Should one be happy yet worried, or worried yet happy?" Pu replied, "No, I am worried because I have been away from my elderly mother for several months, and there is no one to take care of her. Moreover, since childhood, I have been engaged to a woman from the Fang family, who has now reached the age of marriage, but I lack the means to complete the wedding, which is also a regrettable matter." He mentioned that the woman's father had expressed disdain for poverty and might consider breaking off the engagement. Yuexian said, "Since you have already been away from your parents for a long time, it is proper to return quickly. Here, we can take care of her. If the Fang family looks down on poverty, they can let her choose a wealthier man to marry." Pu was displeased and said, "The path of a virtuous person begins with marriage. Once a promise has been made, how can it be changed?" Yuexian smiled and said, "I spoke in the opposite way to test you. If you truly believe my words, I am also disgusted by your lack of virtue and deeply disappointed. When you return, will you go by boat or by carriage?" Pu replied, "Traveling by carriage is tiring, while traveling by boat is easy. I prefer the boat." Both sisters laughed. They held candles and went out, drawing a colorful boat on the ground in front of the courtyard and piling useful items inside it.

    448

    时蒲已半酣，二女扶之出，蒲曰：“舟安在？”二女曰：“画于地下。”蒲乘之不疑，方欲请会期，而舟已在空中。俄而至家，落室前，舟即不见。审视堂门已閤，闻母念己而泣，蒲曰：“儿来矣。”母启门见蒲，绨袍维新，反悲为喜。蒲惭无物以奉亲，旋视舟中所载有炉食，大喜，敬捧以献。母曰：“吾不饥。汝出门一月后，柴米将尽，深以为忧。侵晨院中忽有钱米若干，如是者已数次，不然，吾早饿死多日。”蒲曰：“此钱米必是儿所遇仙女所馈。”遂为母细述之。母曰：“汝所遇殆水中仙，且忌鱼，必鱼之精。”蒲曰：“母言是。吾家有何深恩能及鳞族？”母曰：“汝父在日，尝于江边市双鲤放之，或即是乎？”蒲与母感激不胜。因议定花烛佳期，遣人告方。

    Explanation

    448

    As Pu was slightly intoxicated, two women helped him out. Pu asked, "Where is the boat?" The two women replied, "It is drawn on the ground." Pu rode it without hesitation, just about to ask for the meeting time, but the boat was already in the air. Soon, it arrived at home, landing in front of the room, and then disappeared. Upon examining the main door, which was already closed, he heard his mother crying, missing him. Pu said, "I have come." His mother opened the door and saw Pu, dressed in a new cotton robe, and her sadness turned to joy. Pu felt ashamed that he had nothing to offer his mother, but soon noticed there was food and money in the boat, and he was greatly pleased. He respectfully carried it to present to his mother. The mother said, "I am not hungry. After you left a month ago, our firewood and rice were almost gone, and I was deeply worried. Early in the morning, suddenly there were some money and rice in the courtyard, and this happened several times. Otherwise, I would have starved to death long ago." Pu said, "This money and rice must be a gift from the fairy I met." He then told his mother in detail. The mother said, "The one you met is probably a water fairy, who avoids fish, and must be a fish spirit." Pu said, "Mother, you are right. What deep kindness could our family have done for the fish?" The mother said, "Your father, when he was alive, used to buy two carp at the riverbank and release them. Could that be the reason?" Pu and his mother were overwhelmed with gratitude. They then decided on the wedding date and sent someone to inform Fang.

    449

    先是蒲出亡之后，里有土豪丁某，欲聘方女为子妇。方畏丁，因向媒曰：“吾女幼字徐蒲，今蒲贫不能娶，必将退婚，如果退，则惟命是从。”丁闻蒲归，呼蒲至其家，意欲逼令退婚。适丁有他故，不暇理此，令人引蒲于别院闲屋，逻守之。守者曰：“君欲与方家绝婚耶？如不欲，可速逃，迟则性命难保。”蒲大惧，由后门遁。时淡月夕斜，微分路径，约行里许，已到江边。回视追者，号呼而来。忽见岸下有渔船，意欲上船，哀渔人拯救。既上船，船自行孔疾。旋视之，非船，乃一大鼋，仰首而行，但闻水声，袜履俱不湿。蒲心知系月仙遣渠迎接，反惧为喜。多时，忽见岸有双灯不动，若俟客。至灯所，鼋登岸，蒲下，鼋蠢蠢入水去。

    Explanation

    449

    Before Pu fled, there was a local powerful person named Ding who wanted to marry Fang's daughter to his son. Fang feared Ding and told the matchmaker, "My daughter was betrothed to Xu Pu when she was young, but now Pu is poor and cannot marry. The betrothal will certainly be annulled. If it is annulled, I will follow your command." Ding heard that Pu had returned and called Pu to his house, intending to force Pu to annul the betrothal. Just then, Ding had another matter and had no time to deal with this, so he had someone lead Pu to a secluded room in a separate courtyard and guard him. The guard said to Pu, "Do you want to break off the betrothal with Fang's family? If you do not want to, you can escape quickly. If you delay, your life will be in danger." Pu was greatly afraid and fled through the back door. At that time, the moon was low in the sky, and the path was barely discernible. After about a mile, he had already arrived at the riverbank. Looking back, he saw the pursuers shouting and coming. Suddenly, he saw a fishing boat by the shore and intended to board it, pleading with the fisherman for rescue. After boarding the boat, the boat moved very fast on its own. Looking around, he realized it was not a boat but a giant turtle, moving with its head raised. He only heard the sound of water, and his socks and shoes were not wet. Pu knew in his heart that it was the Moon Fairy who sent the turtle to welcome him, and he turned fear into joy. After a long time, he suddenly saw two stationary lights on the shore, as if waiting for a guest. When he reached the place of the lights, the turtle climbed onto the shore, and Pu got off. The turtle clumsily went back into the water.

    450

    挑灯人曰：“官人来何迟也？”视之，乃水仙之二婢，大喜，从婢行，月仙姊妹门迎之。至中庭，蒲谢默佑之德，且细述颠末。水仙曰：“妾知之。似此恶棍，勿俾遗种。但渠追君不及，必将泄忿于老母。”蒲惊曰：“且为奈何？”二女曰：“勿虞。”遂饮之酒以释惊。追蒲者，土豪之子率家人为之也。及诸江，则蒲在舟中矣。江边有小舟，因欲驾舟追之。豪子先登，既上，非舟，乃水漂朽板一片。大惊，急欲上岸，而身随木沉，遂溺死。丁某痛子死，欲害蒲母以偿子命，急使人将蒲母延至。丁执杖击之，盛怒之际，手倍重，一击而毙。视之，非蒲母，乃邻媪也。丁某拟抵，然终不知何以伤邻媪也。盖蒲母至丁门时，忽见一幼妇牵之曰：“渠欲害母，勿入。”蒲母见人另扶一媪入。己从妇行，而人莫之见也，大疑。

    Explanation

    450

    The Lamp-Lighter said, "Why are you coming so late, sir?" Upon looking, he saw that it was the two maids of Shuixian, and greatly rejoiced, following them. The sisters Moon and Shuixian welcomed him at the gate. Upon reaching the courtyard, Pu thanked them for their silent protection and benevolence, and also recounted the entire course of events in detail. Shuixian said, "I know about it. Such a villain should not be allowed to leave descendants. However, since he was unable to catch up with you, he will certainly vent his anger on your mother." Pu was startled and asked, "What can we do about it?" The two women replied, "Do not worry." They then gave him wine to drink to calm his fears. The one who was pursuing Pu was the son of a local bully, who led his family members to do it. By the time they reached the river, Pu was already on a boat. There was a small boat by the riverbank, and they intended to use it to pursue him. The son of the bully boarded first, and once he got on, it was not a boat but a rotten plank floating on the water. Greatly alarmed, he hurried to get on shore, but his body followed the wood and sank, drowning him. Dīng mourned the death of his son and intended to harm Pu's mother to avenge his son's death. Hastily, he sent someone to bring Pu's mother to him. Dīng held a stick and struck her. In a moment of intense rage, his hand became twice as heavy, and with one strike, she died. Upon seeing it, it was not Pu's mother but a neighborly old woman. Dīng intended to make amends, but in the end, he did not know how he had injured the neighborly old woman. When Pu's mother arrived at Dīng's door, she suddenly saw a young woman take her by the hand and say, "He intends to harm your mother, do not enter." Pu's mother saw someone else helping another old woman enter. She followed the woman, but no one saw her, and she was greatly suspicious.

    451

    未几，妇携行云中，俄落巨宅内，闻人言曰：“老母来矣。”见少年男、妇出迓，视之，其男即蒲，大喜。既入庭，二女伏拜起立。母问之，蒲曰：“悉儿媳。实即儿所遇之仙女。”母曰：“登何仙籍？”曰：“媳之履历，母尝忆得，不必细询。”二女争奉甘旨，尽定省。母乐之，遂忘娶妇事。二旬后，水仙曰：“花烛届期。”蒲曰：“知之。诸事未备，奈何？”女曰：“妾从郎君去，一切事妾悉任之。”约明晨奉母同往。蒲喜，禀于母，母曰：“道既云远，往亦不易。”蒲曰：“明晨渠自有妙术。”及夕各寝，醒则已旋归，母不胜惊讶。

    Explanation

    451

    Not long after, the woman carried him through the clouds, and suddenly they descended into a grand mansion. They heard someone say, "The old mother has arrived." They saw a young man and woman come out to greet them. Upon seeing them, the young man was identified as Pu, and he was very happy. After entering the courtyard, the two women bowed and stood up. The mother asked them, and Pu replied, "These are all my daughters-in-law. They are indeed the fairy that my son met." The mother asked, "Which celestial register does she belong to?" Pu said, "Her background is known to you, so there is no need to ask further." The two women competed to offer delicious food and fulfill the duties of filial piety. The mother was delighted and forgot about the matter of marrying off her daughter. After two weeks, the fairy said, "The time for the wedding ceremony has arrived." Pu replied, "I know. But everything is not yet prepared, what can we do?" The fairy said, "I will go with you, and I will take care of everything." She agreed to meet the mother the next morning to go together. Pu was very happy and told his mother. The mother said, "The journey is far, it will not be easy." Pu said, "The next morning, she has her own wonderful method." By evening, they went to bed, and when they woke up, they had already returned. The mother was extremely surprised.

    452

    女洒扫闲室作柜屋，曰：“财物悉置一室，取用便甚。”蒲视之，室中毫无所有，而聘礼等若干，悉取足焉。亲迎日，女曰：“钱物悉备，无妾事，请辞。”忽不见。未几，复回曰：“几误大事。有一巨凶，日时难以前定。君昨初来时所乘船只，尚在庭前地下，如见船出，举家速登，勿恋财物。”言已复杳。蒲归妻后，言及水仙所嘱，妻深以为妄。一日黄昏，忽见庭前船现，大惊，急同家人扶老母上船。有武夫十数人，突自外入，口事谩骂。听其言，悉丁某恶党，势将辱蒲以泄丁忿。蒲方欲温语拊循，忽有声如雷自地中出，甚厉，既而船升地陷，蒲宅基倏成无底深谷，恶党十数人同安水葬矣。

    Explanation

    452

    A woman cleaned a spare room and built a cabinet house, saying, "All valuables are stored in one room, making it very convenient to use." Pu looked inside and found the room empty, yet he managed to retrieve the wedding gifts and other items. On the day of the wedding, the woman said, "All the money and goods are prepared; there is no need for me to do anything. Please let me go." Suddenly, she disappeared. Soon after, she returned and said, "I nearly caused a major mistake. There is a great calamity whose timing cannot be predetermined. The boat you rode when you first arrived is still in the courtyard. If you see the boat leaving, quickly board it with the whole family, do not be attached to possessions." She then disappeared again. After Pu married his wife, he mentioned the instructions given by the water deity. His wife deeply believed them to be nonsense. One evening, suddenly the boat appeared in front of the courtyard, causing great alarm. He quickly helped his family and elderly mother onto the boat. A dozen or so armed men suddenly entered from outside, cursing and swearing. Upon hearing their words, it became clear they were part of a gang led by someone named Ding, who intended to humiliate Pu to vent their anger. Just as Pu was about to calm them with gentle words, a loud sound like thunder erupted from the ground, very fierce. Soon after, the boat rose and the ground sank, and Pu's house suddenly became an endless deep valley. The dozen or so wicked people were all drowned together.

    453

    徐蒲举家之乘船飞升也，少顷，至月仙第内。婢媪承迎，不见二女。蒲问之，婢媪曰：“适在此。”蒲意移时必自至，乃终夜无耗，始大疑。晨兴，见闲室若干间，海错盈满，价值无算，大惊。言于母曰：“二女不来矣。”母曰：“何言之？”曰：“观此海物盈室，知其报父德者止矣。”果如蒲言。方母意女亦死于水，不时涕泣。方氏亦虞母挂念，但东西距二百馀里，往返不易，且虑溺人之家缠扰。蒲曰：“设有昨所乘之船，夜去明来，岂不甚便。”言已，船忽出现。蒲大喜，与妻乘船而去。后往来以车马，船不出。

    　　虚白道人曰：父种德而子享，固矣。然亦有能享不能享之分焉。使其不仁不智、无礼无义，报之者纵不忍立视其死，而早心厌之矣。如徐蒲者，得如仙之二女，而不忘媒定之嫡，即此一节观之，洵不以情害义者，虽享格外之福，曰能享。

    Explanation

    453

    Xu Pu and his entire family ascended by boat, and soon they arrived at the residence of the Moon Fairy. Female servants welcomed them, but the two daughters were not there. Xu Pu asked about the daughters, and the servants replied, "They were just here." Xu Pu thought that the daughters would return after some time, but they did not come all night, so he began to doubt. In the morning, he saw several empty rooms filled with an abundance of seafood, of inestimable value, and was greatly surprised. He told his mother, "The two daughters are not coming anymore." His mother asked, "Why say that?" Xu Pu replied, "Seeing this room full of sea creatures, I know that those who repay the father's virtue are all here." Indeed, it was as Xu Pu had said. His mother believed that the daughters had also died in the water and often wept. The Fang family also worried that his mother would miss them, but the distance between east and west was over two hundred miles, making it difficult to travel back and forth, and they also feared that the family of the person who drowned might bother them. Xu Pu said, "If we had the boat we rode yesterday, we could leave at night and return in the morning, which would be very convenient." After he said this, the boat suddenly appeared. Xu Pu was greatly pleased and went away with his wife on the boat. Later, they traveled by carriage and horse, and the boat no longer appeared.

    The Daoist named Xubai said: "The father sows virtue and the son enjoys the benefits, indeed. However, there is also a distinction between those who can enjoy and those who cannot. If one is unrighteous, unwise, uncivilized, and unjust, those who repay them may not bear to see them die immediately, but soon grow tired of them. As for Xu Pu, he received two daughters like immortals and did not forget the betrothed daughter he had arranged for, so in this regard, he is indeed someone who does not let emotions harm righteousness. Even if he enjoys extraordinary blessings, he can truly enjoy them."

    454

    推仁之恩，受仁之报，汉武帝池鱼衔环不足为异也。 马竹吾

    　　市鱼放生，仁也；得妻思嫡，义也。博爱之谓仁，行而宜之之谓义，蒲生兼有焉。通篇以船字穿插，击首而尾应，击尾而首应，击中而首尾俱应，所谓文家三应法也。 盖防如

    　　路案

    　　齐河桥为长清、齐河二邑边界，以桥顶为限。道光乙未岁，桥头死一人；偏西，实在齐河界内。齐邑尹诣验，见尸有刃伤，系属谋财害命，案关重大，遂托反尸验伤，移尸于桥顶之东，曰：“尸在长清界内。”以误报责地保而去。长清役禀于官，兼言移尸之故。长尹遣人请齐尹同赴验视。长尹先到，于尸怀中搜得账单一纸，上载买布若干，使钱若干，极清楚。

    Explanation

    454

    Final Translation:

    The grace of benevolence bestowed and the reward of benevolence received is not extraordinary, even in the case of the fish in the pond of Emperor Wu of Han that held a ring in its mouth. — Ma Zhugu

    Buying fish and releasing them is an act of benevolence; thinking of one's rightful wife upon getting married is an act of righteousness. Broad love is called benevolence, and acting appropriately is called righteousness. Pu Sheng possesses both. The entire text is interspersed with the character for "ship," striking the head and the tail responds, striking the tail and the head responds, striking the middle and both head and tail respond together—this is known as the "three response method" of literary writing. — Gai Fangru

    Case of the Bridge in Qihé

    The Qihé Bridge lies on the border between Changqing and Qihé counties, with the bridge top serving as the boundary. In the year of Dàoguāng Bǐngwèi, a person died at the bridgehead; however, the actual location was slightly west, within the territory of Qihé. The magistrate of Qihé came to investigate and found that the body had knife wounds, indicating a case of murder for gain. Given the seriousness of the case, he claimed that the body had been moved and requested to re-examine the injuries, relocating the body to the east of the bridge top and stating, "The body is within Changqing's territory." He blamed the local protector for the misreporting and left. The officials of Changqing reported to their superiors, also mentioning the reason for the body's relocation. The magistrate of Changqing sent someone to invite the magistrate of Qihé to jointly inspect the body. The magistrate of Changqing arrived first and found a receipt in the deceased's chest, detailing the purchase of cloth and the amount of money spent, all clearly recorded.

    455

    未几，齐尹至。长尹请于邻近庙中商酌。长尹谓齐尹曰：“尸在桥西，何为移于桥东？”齐尹曰：“未也。”长尹曰：“行客被杀，必有血迹。今桥西有血迹，桥东尸下无之，非移尸而何？”齐尹不能答。长尹笑曰：“似此案，恐兄无由审究，弟其代之。”时乡人入庙观者甚众，长尹令役闭门，发怒曰：“尔等来此，将盗听官言，为凶手报信耶？”喝令每人杖二十放行。齐尹不知其意，为乡人缓颊。长尹曰：“若然，罚使每人出布半匹，不拘色，五人互保。定于第三日在此交收，违者责不贷。”先将愿出布者，记清姓名里居，约出三十馀名。后者曰：“身等家贫，实无钱买布。”长尹曰：“三人共出布半匹可乎？”佥曰：“可。”约又放出三十馀人。长尹曰：“吾不强人所难，尔等既无钱出布，务各恿怂出布者，早市布于此交纳，勿干重责。”众唯唯而去。长尹约齐尹，届期同来此收布，齐尹应诺，遂归。

    Explanation

    455

    Not long after, the Chief Magistrate of Qi arrived. The Deputy Magistrate requested to discuss the matter at a nearby temple. The Deputy Magistrate told the Chief Magistrate, "The body is on the west side of the bridge, why was it moved to the east side?" The Chief Magistrate replied, "Not yet." The Deputy Magistrate said, "A traveler was killed, there must be bloodstains. Now there are bloodstains on the west side of the bridge, but none under the body on the east side, if not moving the body, what else could it be?" The Chief Magistrate could not answer. The Deputy Magistrate laughed and said, "In this case, I fear you cannot investigate it, let me handle it." At that time, many villagers entered the temple to watch. The Deputy Magistrate ordered the servants to close the door and became angry, saying, "You all came here to secretly listen to the officials' words, to inform the murderer, right?" He ordered each person to be beaten with twenty strokes and then released. The Chief Magistrate did not understand his intention and interceded on behalf of the villagers. The Deputy Magistrate said, "If so, each person will be penalized by contributing half a bolt of cloth, regardless of color, with five people guaranteeing each other. It will be settled on the third day here, and those who violate will be severely punished." First, those who are willing to contribute cloth, their names and places of residence were recorded, approximately thirty names. The others said, "We are poor, we really don't have money to buy cloth." The Deputy Magistrate said, "Can three of us contribute half a bolt of cloth together?" All said, "Yes." Approximately thirty more were released. The Deputy Magistrate said, "I do not force you to do what is difficult, since you have no money to contribute cloth, each must encourage those who will contribute cloth, bring the cloth here early for submission, and avoid severe punishment." The crowd nodded and left. The Deputy Magistrate agreed with the Chief Magistrate to come here together on the appointed day to collect the cloth, the Chief Magistrate agreed, and they returned.

    456

    至第三日，两尹先后到。出布者各抱布以俟，长尹按次验布。验后，将布仍交其人，问其布或市于某甲，一一注明。验毕，统计其布，市于某甲者过半。尹问某甲何在。盖某甲亦出布之人，众共指之，曰：“即是人。”尹问甲曰：“业布几年矣？”答曰：“初作此生理。”尹曰：“汝所买之布，兰者几何，白者几何，共计几何，汝记之乎？”甲各以数对。尹曰：“非也。殆兰者若干，白者若干，共计若干，恐汝误计。”尹又曰：“汝布都鬻给谁氏？”甲曰：“仅卖于出布者数匹，他未发市。”尹曰：“卖于出布几何，下馀几何，汝知之乎？”甲又各以数对。尹曰：“亦非也。

    Explanation

    456

    By the third day, the two magistrates arrived one after the other. Those who brought cloth held it in their arms awaiting inspection, and the senior magistrate inspected the cloth in order. After the inspection, the cloth was returned to the person, and the magistrate asked whether the cloth was bought from a certain person, noting it down one by one. After the inspection, the cloth was counted, and more than half was bought from a certain person. The magistrate asked where this person was. It turned out that this person was also one who brought cloth, and everyone pointed to him, saying, "This is the person." The magistrate asked the person, "How many years have you been dealing in cloth?" The person replied, "I just started this business." The magistrate said, "How many blue, how many white, and how many in total did you buy, and do you remember?" The person answered with numbers. The magistrate said, "No, it is about this much blue, this much white, and this much in total, I fear you might have miscalculated." The magistrate then said, "To whom did you sell all your cloth?" The person said, "I only sold a few pieces to those who brought cloth, and the rest has not been sold yet." The magistrate said, "How many did you sell to those who brought cloth, and how many are left, do you know?" The person answered with numbers again. The magistrate said, "No, it is not so."

    457

    殆卖给出布者若干匹，下馀若干匹耳。”急差役往甲家取布。移时，役取布回。数其布，果与甲所言不对，而与尹言不差。尹笑谓甲曰：“汝自买之布，反不知其数，吾悉知之，汝知其故乎？”答曰：“不知。”齐尹不胜惊讶，问其所以。尹指某甲曰：“此御人之贼，闵不畏死者也。”遂出一账单示齐尹。齐尹曰：“此单何来？”尹曰：“于死者怀中得之。某甲之布与账单之布数目不异，明系甲贪其布而杀其人无疑矣。”某甲闻言，魂飞魄越，遂自招认。供言：“死者系齐河某村人，贩布为业。心利其货，因与交友，挽留住宿，辇布至我家。夜托伴行接他友，至桥顶而杀之，遂掩有其布。众所市之布匹，实是死者之物。”

    Explanation

    457

    A merchant had probably sold several bolts of cloth, leaving a remainder of several bolts. The local official, Qi Yin, immediately sent a servant to fetch the cloth from A's house. After some time, the servant returned with the cloth. When the cloth was counted, it did not match what A had said, but it matched what Mr. Yin had claimed. Mr. Yin smiled and said to A, "You bought this cloth yourself, yet you don’t know its quantity, while I know everything about it. Do you know why?" A replied, "I don’t." Qi Yin was astonished and asked for the reason. Mr. Yin pointed to A and said, "This is a thief who fears nothing, even death." He then produced an account sheet for Qi Yin to see. Qi Yin asked, "Where did this account sheet come from?" Mr. Yin replied, "I found it in the dead man’s chest. The number of bolts A had and the number on the account sheet are the same, clearly indicating that A coveted the cloth and killed the man without a doubt." Upon hearing this, A was shocked to the core and confessed voluntarily. He stated, "The deceased was a man from a certain village in Qihé, who traded cloth for a living. He was attracted by the value of the goods, befriended the man, and invited him to stay overnight. He brought the cloth to my house. At night, he pretended to go with a companion to meet another friend, but on the bridge, he killed him and took possession of the cloth. The cloth sold by the crowd was actually the deceased’s property."

    458

    尹既得正凶，遂谓出布者曰：“汝等之布，既系价买，各人携去。岂真罚汝以肥官囊耶？盖劫夺之物，其售必贱，借汝等市布以征凶身之所在耳。”言已，听各抱布去。复差役传死者之家人，领尸与布，并追某甲所卖布价与之。两邑士民，悉颂神君。惜偶忘其官讳，容再访之。

    　　虚白道人曰：怪案奇冤，总有端绪，为官者不肯深用其心以求之耳。长尹于尸怀中得一帐单，在他人亦不过以帐单知死者为布客已耳，而尹则以之而获凶首，且以之而使凶首自招，固处处见尹之智，亦处处见尹之仁也。

    　　此篇可采入《无冤录》、《牧津》等书后。 马竹吾

    　　予不羡长尹有折狱之明，而羡其有周公之美。移尸不校是不骄也，代审路案是不吝也，如有周公之才之美。使骄且吝，则布商之冤莫明于地下。噫！居官如齐尹者，恶在其为民父母也。 盖防如

    Explanation

    458

    The magistrate having identified the culprit, he then addressed the cloth sellers, saying, "Your cloth, which was purchased at a price, each person can take it away. Is it truly to punish you to enrich the official's purse? It is because stolen goods are always sold cheaply, and I am using your cloth to find the location of the criminal." After saying this, he allowed each person to take their cloth. He then sent a messenger to notify the family of the deceased, to take the body and the cloth, and to reclaim the price of the cloth sold by someone. The officials and common people of both counties praised the divine official. Unfortunately, I forgot his official title, I will inquire again later.

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: Strange cases and unjust deaths always have some clue; it is only that officials are unwilling to deeply investigate them. The magistrate found an account sheet in the deceased's clothes. For others, it would only be to know that the deceased was a cloth merchant. But the magistrate used it to catch the main criminal and used it to make the criminal confess. This shows the magistrate's wisdom and benevolence everywhere.

    This case can be included in books like The Book of Unjust Deaths and Pastoral Notes. Ma Zhugu

    I do not envy the magistrate's ability to judge cases, but I envy his virtue like that of Duke Zhou. Not correcting the body shows he is not proud, taking over the case shows he is not stingy. If he has the talent and virtue of Duke Zhou, if he were proud and stingy, then the cloth merchant's injustice would remain unknown. Ah! If officials were like the magistrate of Qi, how could they be parents to the people? This is to prevent such things.

    459

    巩生

    　　巩生振先，章邑人。未入泮时，尝训蒙于济郡之西关。一日，薄暮出游街外，见一及笄女郎由面前过，姿致摇曳，芬香袭人，爱而尾之。女屡含笑回顾，心大惑，从至标山下，忽睹爽垲，门阀壮丽，女迳入。生自言曰：“昨赴泺访友，路出乎此，未尝见此巨第。”惊异间，内出一少年，丰仪可人，邀生入。生以未与谋面，辞之。少年固请，乃从之入。家人举烛进茗，各展姓氏，生以知少年姓胡名修。胡曰：“有一言奉礍，勿嫌冒昧。弟有一妹，欲委禽者数家，妹悉不欲。适言门外有客，学问中人也，令弟邀入款待。窥妹意，似属意于君，愿君纳之。”生闻之大喜，起身致谢。胡遽入，须臾即出。既而家人捧酒胾来矣，既而婢媪拥女艳妆出矣。胡令与生交拜，已，坐而饮。胡连饮二杯，笑言曰：“今日之饮，无需陪客。”遂辞去。生问女青春芳名，女一一告之。

    Explanation

    459

    巩生

    　　巩生振先，是章邑人。在他未通过科举考试之前，曾在济郡的西关教授儿童。一天傍晚，他走出街外游玩，看见一位少女从面前走过，姿态优雅，芬芳扑鼻，他爱慕并尾随她。少女多次含笑回头，他心中大惑不解，跟着她来到标山脚下，突然看到一个凉爽干燥的地方，门口宏伟壮丽，少女径直走了进去。巩生自言自语道：“昨天去泺地拜访朋友，路过这里，从未见过这么大的宅子。”他感到惊讶，这时从里面走出一位少年，相貌英俊，邀请他进去。巩生因为没有事先见过面，拒绝了。少年坚持邀请，他才跟着进去。家人点上蜡烛，端来茶水，各自介绍姓名，巩生得知少年姓胡名修。胡说：“有一句话想对你说，不要觉得冒昧。我有一个妹妹，有几家想提亲的，但她都不愿意。刚才她说门外有位客人，是个学识渊博的人，让我弟弟邀请他进来款待。我看出她对你有意，希望你能接受她。”巩生听了非常高兴，起身致谢。胡赶紧进去，不久又出来。接着家人端来了酒菜，随后丫鬟们带着妆扮漂亮的少女走了出来。胡让巩生与少女互相行礼，之后坐下饮酒。胡连喝了两杯，笑着说：“今天的酒不用陪客。”说完就离开了。巩生问少女的名字，少女一一告诉他。

    460

    生曰：“卿赴西关何为？”女曰：“妾自有事，固非伪作芳饵以钓君。”生愕然曰：“卿亦通文墨耶？”女曰：“不敢言通。窃自揣度，不止能君所能而已也。君读书二十馀年，未掇一中，妾为君耻之。假令身为男子，采芹如拾芥耳。”生曰：“八股文字如一茎草化丈六金身，天下事未有难于此者。固不同酒食，是议之易易也。”女曰：“富贵功名，丈夫分内事。人能得，君不能得，愧为丈夫矣。”生默默无言。移时，问女门第，女曰：“正欲语君。盖与其暗弃于后，未若明拒于前。妾有可憎，君如憎之，请即行，勿为妾浼。”生曰：“其憎维何？”曰：“君试猜之。”生曰：“卿其狐乎？”女曰：“然。君于此何有一言之智也？”生曰：“观今之事与卿之貌，早决大半。仆久愿得狐交，相见恨晚，何憎之有？”女喜曰：“明晨君早归，妾自继至。”生忧书室不便，女曰：“无妨。妾虽朝夕伴君，他人不之见。”定夜同寝。昧旦促生行。

    460

    The man said, "Why are you going to the Western Gate?" The woman replied, "I have my own affairs; I certainly did not pretend to be a bait to lure you." The man was surprised and asked, "Are you also knowledgeable in literature and writing?" The woman said, "I dare not claim to be knowledgeable. I privately believe that I can do things that you cannot. You have studied for over twenty years but have not achieved any success in the imperial examinations; I am ashamed for you. If only you were a man, passing the examinations would be as easy as picking up mustard seeds." The man said, "The rigid eight-part essay required in the imperial examinations is like transforming a single blade of grass into a golden body six feet tall; nothing in the world is more difficult than this. It is certainly not like drinking and eating; this is an easy matter to discuss." The woman said, "Wealth, rank, and fame are matters that belong to men. If others can achieve them, but you cannot, you should be ashamed to be a man." The man remained silent. After a while, he asked about her family background. The woman said, "I was about to tell you. It is better to openly reject than to secretly abandon later. If there is something about me that you find repulsive, please leave now, and do not let me trouble you." The man said, "What is it that you find repulsive?" She said, "You try to guess." The man said, "Are you a fox?" The woman said, "Yes. You have no wisdom in this matter." The man said, "From what I see now and your appearance, I have already decided. I have long wished to make friends with a fox; I regret meeting you so late. What reason to hate?" The woman happily said, "Tomorrow morning, you should return early, and I will follow you later." The man was worried about the inconvenience of the study. The woman said, "It's no problem. Even though I will be with you day and night, others won't see me." They decided to sleep together at night. At dawn, she urged him to leave early.

    461

    馆有厨灶，每日女奉食饮与生同馔，诸徒及庖人果无见女者。甲与乙，皆生同门友，文优于生，每会课，师定名次，生辄殿。甲乙谋曰：“下课以首一名为客，后二名为东道主。”生不欲，二人强之。生言于女，女曰：“有妾在，保君常嘉宾式燕。”至课，女代生作文，名果列二人之上。次课复然。二人议生必有夹带，约同试于师前。至期，女与生同往，生仍居前名。二人饮生酒，生醉，自言曰：“嗣后勿为是谋。如屡为之，有厚扰而无酬爵，盖仆之文实有仙助也。”言已而悔。二人固问，遂以狐妻对。时乙得书院正课，旧规连课三等者，降为副。乙已两居三等，恐课降，因烦生转恳女代作文。生恐以漏言致狐责，不敢遽应。乙曰：“无他望，但求免降已耳。”生不得已，应之。归言于女，女曰：“传语于渠，课日未刻文到。

    Explanation

    461

    The inn had a kitchen and stove, and every day the girl served food and drink, sharing meals with the student. The other students and the cooks indeed did not see the girl. Jia and Yi, both fellow students of the student, had writing skills superior to his. At every class meeting, the teacher determined the rankings, and the student always came last. Jia and Yi plotted, saying, "After class, the first-ranked student will be the guest, and the second and third-ranked students will be the hosts." The student did not want to, but the two forced him. The student told the girl, and she said, "With me here, I will ensure that you remain a frequent guest and enjoy the hospitality." When the class met, the girl wrote the essay on behalf of the student, and his name indeed ranked above theirs. The next class was the same. The two suspected that the student must have cheated, and they agreed to test him in front of the teacher. On the appointed day, the girl and the student went together, and the student still ranked first. The two gave the student wine, and he became drunk, speaking to himself, "From now on, do not engage in this scheme. If you continue to do this, you will cause great trouble but receive no reward. It is because my writing is truly assisted by the divine." He regretted his words after speaking them. The two persisted in asking, and he finally admitted that the girl was his fox wife. At the time, Yi had won the main class, and according to the old rule, those who ranked third in consecutive classes would be demoted to a secondary class. Yi had already ranked third twice, and he feared being demoted, so he asked the student to persuade the girl to write the essay for him. The student was afraid that revealing this would anger the fox, so he did not dare to respond immediately. Yi said, "I have no other hope, I only want to avoid being demoted." The student, having no choice, agreed. He returned home and told the girl, who said, "Tell him that by the time of the class, the text will arrive."

    462

    ”乙录女文，案取特等之末。乙诣生斋展谢，甲亦从之去。至，见案有酒胾，乙曰：“速何嘉客，而设此乎？”生曰：“敬为君二人设。”乙摇首不语。忽闻一女子娇声大言曰：“如有他客来，即谓为他客设可。竭诚治具，意指为他，大负主人敬客之心。”乙闻其声，不见其人，心知为狐，起身曰：“昨劳清神，五内感激，特来拜谢。”女曰：“何谢为？此文人土产耳。”生问课取名次，乙答以特等之末。甲向女笑言曰：“卿之文，仅少优于吾二人。若在书院较优绌，得录于是，犹为侥幸。”女曰：“卿言是也。卿等若有特等之诣，无烦转恳女学士矣。”甲曰：“卿何以‘卿’称吾等？”女曰：“卿卿吾，吾亦卿卿，吾一卿卿，人悉卿卿。”各大笑。女曰：“上课之取特等也，非吾仅能为特等文，以乙兄但求免降，故如命为之。

    Explanation

    462

    The Record of the Girl's Essay

    乙 recorded the girl's essay and placed it at the bottom of the top-tier category. 乙 went to 生's study to express his gratitude, and 甲 followed him. Upon arriving, they saw a table set with wine and meat. 乙 said, "Why did you invite distinguished guests and prepare this meal?" 生 replied, "I prepared this for you two." 乙 shook his head and remained silent. Suddenly, a woman's voice, soft and clear, spoke loudly, "If you invite other guests, it is natural to prepare for them. To prepare with utmost sincerity, implying it is for others, greatly辜负s the host's respectful intentions towards the guests." 乙 heard her voice but did not see her, and he knew she was a fox. He stood up and said, "I was greatly moved by your spirit yesterday, and I came to thank you." The woman said, "Why thank me? This is just the work of a scholar." 生 asked about the ranking of the essays, and 乙 replied that it was at the bottom of the top tier. 甲 smiled at the woman and said, "Your writing is only slightly better than ours. If we were to compare in the academy, being selected in this category would still be a stroke of luck." The woman said, "You are right. If you two could achieve the top tier, there would be no need to ask the lady scholar." 甲 said, "Why do you call us '卿'?" The woman said, "You call me '卿', and I call you '卿'. If I call you '卿', everyone will call you '卿'." They all laughed. The woman said, "The selection of the top tier is not that I can only write top-tier essays. Because 乙 only seeks to avoid being demoted, so I did as commanded."

    463

    如肯以奖赏银为酒酌共享之，下课吾能代取超等。”乙喜从之。乙果取超等第一。乙烦生治具，约甲同往。甲曰：“有劳狐嫂矣。”女曰：“既称为嫂，胡加以狐？”甲曰：“忘嫂为狐，口称为狐，非忆嫂为狐，故称为狐。嗣不敢以狐嫂称之矣。”女笑曰：“子意吾嗔称狐，故连言数狐，不知子即去嫂称狐，吾亦度外置之。”既而，未见人入而肴酒满案。甲请女同坐，女曰：“男女不同席，不敢废礼。”甲曰：“既聆謦咳，盍使弟等一睹仙颜？”女曰：“可。吾在门外矣。”视之，果有女子华妆立，其丰姿之娟，真如月中仙子。多时始不见。甲曰：“一睹芳容，我魂欲消。不知肯真个令人消魂否？”女曰：“令正尝令人消魂，故以此律贞节人。”甲闻之，有惭色。少顷复曰：“狐嫂娟好如是，巩兄有福消受。如弟，早死多日。

    Explanation

    463

    If you are willing to share the reward money for drinking together, I can help you get the highest grade after class. He gladly agreed. Indeed, he achieved the highest grade. He asked the student to prepare the food and invited Jia to join. Jia said, "I thank you for your effort, Fox Sister." The woman replied, "Since you call me sister, why do you add the word 'fox'?" Jia explained, "I forgot that she is a fox, so I called her a fox. I did not remember that she is a fox, so I called her a fox. I will not use the term 'Fox Sister' again." The woman laughed and said, "You thought I would be angry about being called a fox, so you mentioned it several times. You didn't know that you had already removed the term 'sister' and called her a fox. I have also put it aside." Soon, no one entered, yet the food and drink were already on the table. Jia asked the woman to sit with him, but she said, "Men and women should not sit together, so I dare not violate the etiquette." Jia said, "Since I have heard your voice, why not let my younger brother see your beautiful face?" The woman said, "Sure. I will be outside the door." When they looked, there was indeed a woman standing elegantly dressed, her graceful appearance truly like a celestial being. After some time, she disappeared. Jia said, "Just seeing your beauty, my soul is about to be consumed. Do you really have the power to enchant others?" The woman said, "My beauty has often made others lose their composure, so I adhere to proper conduct." Jia felt embarrassed after hearing this. A little while later, he said, "Fox Sister is so beautiful, Brother Geng has great fortune. As for me, I would have died long ago."

    464

    ”女笑曰：“死有……”生戒以勿言，女曰：“姑为郎君恕子，不然，吾固不能让人者。”三人饮酒，兼谈诗文。女曰：“此非用功时也。”乙曰：“然。兹有一令，不能者罚。”遂曰：“金字旁，铜与铅，出字分开两座山。一山出铜，一山出铅。”甲曰：“木字旁，柜与橱，林字分开两段木。一木为柜，一木为橱。”生曰：“水字旁，汤与酒，吕字分开两个口。一口饮汤，一口饮酒。”乙曰：“巩嫂既在此，亦宜入令。”女曰：“吾虽在此，未尝入席，岂可入令？”甲曰：“此令量亦狐嫂不能行。”女曰：“能。言之勿怪。人字旁，你与他，爻字分开两把叉，一叉伤你，一叉伤他。”乙曰：“弟未与嫂戏，奈何戏弟若是之甚？”女曰：“吾过矣。请异日敬理杯茗，以赎吾愆。”于是，文人多知生有狐妻，故与往来，与女谈笑，虽善戏谑者不能屈。

    Explanation

    464

    The woman laughed and said, "There's something about death..." The young man warned her not to speak, but she replied, "Please forgive me for the sake of the young master, otherwise, I would not be able to yield to others." The three of them drank and discussed poetry and literature. The woman said, "This is not the time to work hard." The second person said, "Indeed. Here's a game, and those who cannot play will be punished." He then said, "The character '出' (chū) has a gold radical, and it means copper and lead. The character '出' can be broken down into two mountains. One mountain produces copper, and the other produces lead." The first person said, "The character '柜' (guì) and '橱' (chú) have a wood radical, and the character '林' can be broken down into two pieces of wood. One piece of wood makes a cabinet, and the other makes a chest." The young man said, "The character '汤' (tāng) and '酒' (jiǔ) have a water radical, and the character '吕' can be broken down into two mouths. One mouth drinks soup, and the other drinks alcohol." The second person said, "Since Sister Gong is here, she should also join the game." The woman said, "I am here, but I have never sat at the table, so how could I join the game?" The first person said, "This game is too difficult for Sister Fox to play." The woman said, "I can do it. Don't be surprised. The character '你' (nǐ) and '他' (tā) have a person radical, and the character '爻' can be broken down into two forks. One fork hurts you, and the other hurts him." The second person said, "I have never played with Sister, why are you teasing me so much?" The woman said, "I was wrong. Please accept my apology and a cup of tea another day to make up for my mistake." As a result, many literati knew that the young man had a fox wife, so they visited him and chatted with her, even those who were good at joking could not make her submit.

    465

    生岁终解馆，欲与女偕归。女不可，生曰：“卿独处，难免岑寂。”女曰：“君归妾亦归，惟先君来期而俟此。”春初，生赴塾，女果在焉。不几日，闻章邑县试，生欲赴之，女曰：“君命方蹇，有凶无吉，可勿赴。”生不听。县府试毕，偶欲回家省亲，女以数十金馈之，曰：“积之已久，妾无用处，可藏之以备不测。”至家，母适有微恙，意理治即愈，竟旬不痊而终，得用女馈金理葬具。始知女之馈金、劝不试，皆为亲丧也。葬后入塾，见女衣縗絰，书亲主位哭室内。生不禁与之俱哭，曰：“不图卿有如是之孝思。”女曰：“不得赴君家分大事之忧，不得对亲灵尽自致之哀，抚衷自问，实难自安。”生不胜感叹。生母服将阕，复丁父忧。而生连年以女力，得书院膏火奖赏，家少裕。服满，岁试仍不售。次年院试，生祈女代。

    Explanation

    465

    At the end of the year, the scholar left the school, intending to take the girl home with him. The girl refused, and the scholar said, "You will be alone, which will inevitably be lonely." The girl replied, "If you go home, I will go home too, but I will wait for my father's return." Early in the spring, the scholar went to the school, and the girl was indeed there. Not long after, he heard about the county examination in Zhan County and wanted to attend. The girl said, "Your fortune is currently unfavorable; there will be misfortune but no good luck. You should not go." The scholar did not listen. After the county and prefectural examinations, he occasionally wanted to return home to visit his family, and the girl gave him several dozen gold pieces, saying, "I have saved this money for a long time; I have no use for it. You can keep it for unexpected events." When he returned home, his mother had a slight illness, and he thought she would recover with proper care. However, she died after ten days, and the girl's gift of money was used for the funeral expenses. It was then known that the girl's gift of money and advice against taking the exam were both for the sake of the family's bereavement. After the funeral, the scholar returned to the school and saw the girl wearing mourning clothes, writing in the place of her deceased father, and weeping in the inner room. The scholar could not help but join her in tears and said, "I did not expect you to have such filial thoughts." The girl replied, "I could not go to your home to share in the great sorrow, nor could I fully express my grief before my father's spirit. Reflecting on my heart, I truly find it difficult to be at peace." The scholar was overwhelmed with emotion. His mother's mourning period was almost over, and he suffered from the loss of his father. Thanks to the girl's efforts over the years, the scholar received rewards from the academy, and the family became slightly more prosperous. After completing the mourning, the annual examination was still unsuccessful. The next year, during the academy examination, the scholar asked the girl to take his place.

    466

    女应诺，暗随生入场。首艺题“高也明也”至“一卷石之多”，余仅忆其伏下用女娲氏炼石补天事，科取章邑第二名。后女从生入岁试场，以冠军食饩。其首艺题为“盖曰”，塾中多传诵之。至乡试，生坚请女偕入。女曰：“妾实不敢。现在关圣帝君监场，委平将军督理南省，周将军督理北省。帝君时亲身巡查，神威犹昔，无有敢犯。”生不听。女曰：“无贪，实难。君无登贤书命，如妄求之，必致不祥。君如素位而行，可安饱终身，妾亦得与君偕老。不然，缘分恐从此绝矣。”生固求。女曰：“请尝试之。妾匿君卷袋入，但得卷袋无破，则有几希望。”进场日，生应点而入，至龙门，忽闻女曰：“周将军至矣！”破袋而去，壁墙忽塌一段如刀削。生无心为文，苟且完卷而出。冀女犹在书房，而竟乌有。朝占雀噪，夕卜灯花，总无耗，念想綦切，遂致迷乱如疯颠。

    Explanation

    466

    The woman agreed and secretly followed the man into the examination hall. The first essay question ranged from "high and bright" to "a single stone," and I only remembered that it involved the story of Nüwa smelting stones to mend the sky, which earned me second place in the county examination. Later, the woman accompanied the man into the annual examination and won the championship, receiving a stipend. The first essay question was "Cover it with," and it was widely recited in the school. When it came to the provincial examination, the man persistently asked the woman to accompany him. The woman said, "I really dare not. Now Lord Guan is overseeing the examination, and General Ping is in charge of the southern province, while General Zhou is in charge of the northern province. Lord Guan personally inspects from time to time, and his divine power remains as it was, no one dares to violate." The man did not listen. The woman said, "Without greed, it's already difficult. If you don't have the order to enter the list of the worthy, if you recklessly seek it, it will surely bring misfortune. If you act according to your position, you can live a life of comfort and fullness, and I can also grow old with you. Otherwise, our缘分 might be cut off forever." The man insisted. The woman said, "Please try it. I will hide your answer sheet in my bag, but if the bag is not torn, there is some hope." On the day of the examination, the man was called in, and when he reached the gate, he suddenly heard the woman say, "General Zhou is here!" The bag was torn, and a section of the wall suddenly collapsed like a knife cut. The man had no heart to write, and hastily finished the exam and left. Hoping the woman was still in the study room, but she was nowhere to be found. In the morning, the sparrows were noisy, and in the evening, the lamp flower was divined, but there was no news, the longing was intense, and he became so confused that he was like a madman.

    467

    虚白道人曰：贪之累人甚矣。巩生者，得安饱终身，嘉偶偕老，似可以已矣。乃听狐言于前，弗从狐言于后，以至文学之妻，一朝长离；倜傥之士，佯狂自废。孰非贪得无厌之所致也哉！

    Explanation

    467

    The Daoist Master Xubai said: "The burden of greed is very great. Gongsheng, who enjoyed a life of comfort and fullness, a good spouse, and lived together to old age, it seemed he could stop there. But he listened to the fox's words at first, yet did not follow them later, leading to the wife of a literatus who suddenly became distant; a free-spirited man who feigned madness and thus ruined himself. Who is not the result of insatiable greed?"

    468

    狐枪手善于捣鬼，与世之冒名顶替者无少异。机械日深，自迷本相，宜其遇正神而披靡也。 马竹吾

    　　点睛处在一贪字。“贪”字近“贫”，凡贪得者终致贫乏，戒之哉，贪！ 盖防如

    　　较《聊斋?狐谐》，可谓青胜于兰。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　毕成

    　　咸丰五年六月二十日，黄河之束河兰工漫口，黄水下注，菏泽十分成灾。先是，邑西北毕家寨，地势本下，大雨时行，寨中恒水深二三尺，幸一二日即消，虽足为患，寨人久习而安之。

    　　有毕成者，世居于寨。老母每逢阴雨，辄忧虞不食，百般喻解，莫释惧心。成欲迁徙，而故土难移，因鬻沃壤数亩，竭资筑楼，奉母居之。遇大雨，成令家人劝母勿起。母问水，则以雨虽大水未进寨慰之。

    Explanation

    468

    The Fox Gunner is adept at deceit, no different from those in the world who impersonate others. As their tricks become more sophisticated, they lose sight of their true nature, and it is appropriate that they are defeated when faced with a righteous spirit. — Ma Zhugu

    The key point is the word "greed." The character for "greed" is close to "poverty," and those who greedily seek will ultimately become impoverished. Beware of greed! — Gu Fangru

    Compared to Liaozhai Zhiyi: Fox Harmony, this can be said to surpass it in quality. — Carefully annotated by Li Yu of Shangyuan

    Bi Cheng

    On the twentieth day of the sixth month in the fifth year of the Xianfeng era, the breach at the Shuhe River in the Yellow River caused the Yellow River water to flood downstream, resulting in great disaster in Heze. Previously, in the northwest of the county, there was a village called Bi Family, which was naturally low-lying. Heavy rain often fell, and the water in the village was usually two or three feet deep. Fortunately, it usually receded within one or two days, though it was still a nuisance. The villagers had grown accustomed to it.

    There was a man named Bi Cheng who lived in the village all his life. His elderly mother always worried and lost her appetite whenever it rained. No matter how he tried to reassure her, she could not shake her fear. Bi Cheng wanted to move, but it was difficult to leave his hometown. So he sold some fertile land, used all his savings to build a house, and had his mother live there. Whenever it rained heavily, Bi Cheng would ask his family to persuade his mother not to leave the house. If his mother asked about the water, he would reassure her by saying that even though the rain was heavy, the water had not entered the village.

    469

    一日忽来一中年媪，自言善事老人，成佣以伺母。未几，母令成货产治终具，成遵命。因兼有迁居之意，遂多货之。所有产业，约去多半。鬻后，母又不令成治具，盖言虽出于母而实佣媪暗地教之也。不几日，黄水忽到，举家登楼。眼看庄舍倾圮，已成水国，而水将及楼门，成大惧。佣媪曰：“勿惧，彼救星来矣。”媪指处，漂来大木二段，如屋梁。媪曰：“可跨此逃命。”成言与老母跨一木，所有银两载于其上；家人共跨一木。而成实意一木不能载重，将摒弃银两以拯母，所以必言载银两者，恐家人分跨为母累也。方跨木，媪已杳，楼亦淹没，而二木不浮不沉，虽湿下衣，而稳如舟车。经一日夜，始遇船只拯救。既上船，木化为龙，戏水中，移时始不见。

    Explanation

    469

    One day, a middle-aged woman suddenly appeared, claiming to be skilled in caring for the elderly and offering to serve as a maid to take care of the mother. Soon after, the mother ordered the son to sell their property to prepare for the funeral, and he obeyed. Because they also had the intention to move, they ended up selling much of their property, about half of it. After selling the property, the mother no longer ordered the son to prepare for the funeral, as the words were actually suggested by the maid behind the mother's back. A few days later, the yellow floodwaters suddenly arrived, and the whole family climbed to the upper floor. They saw their village and houses collapse, turning into a watery kingdom, and the water was about to reach the door of the upper floor, causing the son great fear. The maid said, "Do not be afraid, the savior has come." She pointed, and two large pieces of wood floated by, like roof beams. The maid said, "You can cross on these to escape." The son said that he and his mother would cross on one piece of wood, with all their silver on it; the rest of the family would cross together on the other piece of wood. But in reality, one piece of wood could not bear the weight, so he intended to abandon the silver to save his mother. That is why he insisted on carrying the silver, fearing that if the family split the load, it would burden his mother. As they were crossing on the wood, the maid disappeared, and the upper floor was also submerged, but the two pieces of wood neither floated nor sank, although they got wet, they were as stable as a boat or a carriage. After a day and night, they were finally rescued by a boat. After they got on the boat, the wood transformed into dragons, playing in the water, and after a while, they disappeared.

    470

    虚白道人曰：厚者薄之，而薄者未有能厚者也。毕成之得举家全活，实缘重拯母命之所致耳。如怜财保妻子之念重，则茫茫大劫，难必独家人之无恙也。

    　　此篇劝孝文字有功伦纪。 马竹吾

    　　媪，菩萨也；漂木，慈航也。所谓菩萨现身说法，慈航普渡一切众生，此文可名为孝感篇。 盖防如

    Explanation

    470

    虚白道人曰：那些慷慨大方的人最终会变得吝啬，而吝啬的人却永远无法变得慷慨。毕成之所以全家得以保全，实在是因为他重视拯救母亲的生命所致。如果一个人过于看重钱财和保护家人，那么在茫茫大劫中，很难保证家人平安无事。

    　　这篇文章通过劝导孝道，对维护伦常纲纪有积极的作用。 马竹吾

    　　那位老妇人是菩萨的化身；那块漂流的木头是慈航的象征。正如菩萨现身说法，慈航普渡一切众生，这篇文章可以称为《孝感篇》。 盖防如

    471

    大劫难，大善可免。成意中唯一老母，不重资财，不重妻子，宜其至诚感神也。 子厚

    　　虚娘

    　　吉廷芳，陕西汉中人。为人懦弱，而秉性直实。贫无衣食，孤绝伦常，以训蒙为生计。偶失馆，投友不遇，枵腹归。因思无食终为饿殍，不如速死之为愈也。路旁有松林，遂解带系树枝而缢。忽觉有人解救，开眸而视，见一红妆笄女立面前，大异之。曰：“子何来？谓预知吾缢而来拯救，此必无之事；谓不知吾缢而来拯救，时明月东升，已将二鼓，子系女身，何为独行到此？”女曰：“吾亦将自缢于此，见君缢，哀死情切，而自缢之心顿止。”吉曰：“睹子服饰，固非饥无食、寒无衣者，胡为出此？”女曰：“贫不犹君，孤甚于君。”吉曰：“吾父兄妻子俱无，孤已极矣。”女曰： “同一孤也，吾系女流，自相较为甚。”吉曰：“然。”女曰：“愿君从妾去，君不贫，妾亦不孤。

    Explanation

    471

    Great calamities can be averted by great virtue. The sole object of one's intentions is a mother who does not value wealth or her family members, and her sincerity is so profound that it moves the divine. ——Zi Hou

     Virtual Lady

 Ji Tingfang, a man from Hanzhong, Shaanxi, was physically weak but morally upright and honest. He was poor, isolated from social norms, and made a living by teaching children. One day, he lost his teaching position, sought help from friends without success, and returned home hungry. Thinking that he would eventually starve to death, he decided it was better to die quickly. He found a pine forest by the roadside and hanged himself by tying his belt to a tree branch. Suddenly, he felt someone rescuing him and opened his eyes to see a young woman dressed in red standing in front of him, which surprised him. He asked, "Why are you here? Do you think you knew I was going to hang myself and came to save me? That's impossible. Do you think you came to save me without knowing about my attempt? It's already past two drums, and the moon is rising in the east. You're a woman, why are you walking alone here?" The woman replied, "I was also planning to hang myself here. When I saw you, I was so moved by your sorrow that I stopped my own suicide." Ji said, "Seeing your clothes, you clearly don't seem to be suffering from hunger or cold. Why are you doing this?" The woman said, "I'm poorer and more isolated than you." Ji said, "I have no father, brothers, or wife, and I am completely alone." The woman said, "We are both alone, but I am a woman. By comparison, my situation is worse." Ji said, "Yes." The woman said, "I wish you would come with me. You won't be poor, and I won't be alone."
    472

    ”吉问女姓氏，答以“虚娘惠氏，违此不远”。吉喜，从之去。约行四五里，忽睹一第，舍宇华好。女导入，婢媪成群，高堂有翁媪对坐，女曰：“妾父母。”令吉拜之。旁立二少年，女曰：“妾二兄。”令吉揖之。已而引吉入别室，婢奉酒胾，与吉对饮。吉曰：“卿孤耶？”女笑曰：“不孤，前言戏之耳。盖妾父梦神人，言妾与君有缘，故使妾亲身救君。父且曰：妾无媒嫁君，恐为物议，明晨令妾同君早归。”吉曰：“仆家固甑冷囊空。”女曰：“父知之。量必有以处此。”五更促吉起，曰：“大车既载矣。”吉出，见财物满车，大喜，与女同乘归。至家，东方始明。载来粮食无盛器，女曰：“盛粮之器亦无耶？”吉曰：“闲室中尽有之。”吉往取之，既而返曰：“器中各有食粮。”女笑曰：“君固不贫而言贫者，亦妄耳。”未几仓箱俱盈。

    Explanation

    472

    Ji asked the woman about her surname, and she replied, "Xuniang Hui, not far from here." Ji was pleased and followed her. They walked about four or five miles, and suddenly they saw a mansion, its buildings splendid and beautiful. The woman led Ji inside, where a crowd of maidservants greeted them. In the main hall, an elderly couple sat facing each other. The woman said, "These are my parents." She instructed Ji to bow to them. Standing beside them were two young men, whom the woman introduced as her brothers, and she told Ji to bow to them as well. Soon, the woman led Ji into a separate room, where maidservants served wine and meat, and they drank together. Ji asked, "Are you an orphan?" The woman laughed and said, "No, I am not an orphan. I was joking earlier. My father dreamed that a divine being told him that I had a connection with you, so he sent me to save you. He also said, 'I cannot marry you to you without a proper match, which might cause people to talk, so I will let you return early tomorrow morning.'" Ji said, "My home is truly empty and poor." The woman replied, "My father knows this. He will certainly find a way to take care of this." At the fifth watch, the woman urged Ji to get up, saying, "The large carriage is already prepared." Ji went out and saw that the carriage was filled with goods, and he was very pleased. He rode back home with the woman. By the time they arrived, the eastern sky was just beginning to lighten. There were no containers for the grain, and the woman said, "Do you not have containers for grain?" Ji said, "There are containers in the empty room." Ji went to fetch them, and when he returned, he said, "Each container has its own food and grain." The woman laughed and said, "You were not poor but said you were, that was also false." After a short while, the granaries and chests were all full.

    473

    吉素多称贷，见吉暴富，俱向讨，女一一出粜偿之。曰：“独无欠君者耶？”吉曰：“有之。某甲欠钱若干，有帐可凭，渠言仆肯立给收据，则如数清还。仆立后，渠以收字为据，言不少欠。”女怒曰：“竟有如是之昧良者耶？非讼之不可。”吉曰：“彼有收据，讼之亦未必得直。”次日，吉见案有字纸，视之，乃立给某甲之收字，曰：“此字何从来？”女曰：“某甲之事，神人共怒，必神为之也。”吉遂讼之。官断如数归楚。吉谓女曰：“仓箱之粟，收据之还，皆卿之力与？”女曰：“妾实不能。

    Explanation

    473

    Ji always borrowed a lot, and when he suddenly became wealthy, everyone came to demand repayment. The woman sold grain one by one to repay them. She asked, "Is there no one who owes you?" Ji replied, "There is. Mr. A owes a certain amount of money, and there is an account to verify it. He said that if I would give him a receipt, he would pay back the full amount. After I gave the receipt, he used it as proof and said he did not owe anything." The woman angrily said, "Is there really such a wicked person? It cannot be avoided through litigation." Ji said, "He has the receipt, so even if we litigate, we might not win." The next day, Ji saw a piece of paper on the desk and looked at it, which was the receipt he had given to Mr. A. He said, "Where did this come from?" The woman said, "The matter of Mr. A, both gods and people are angry, it must be done by a god." Ji then sued him. The official ruled that he should return the money as agreed. Ji said to the woman, "The grain in the granary and the repayment of the receipt, is it all your doing?" The woman said, "I really couldn't."

    474

    如能之，亦美事，何妨明言。”吉终疑之，曰：“兹有一事，尚可行否？仆幼聘石氏女为妻，以贫故，得渠白金十数两，立有退婚书于彼。能挽回否？”女曰：“可。但恐石氏非福人，娶之为妾累。君烦原媒通嫁娶期，媒若曰：闻君有退婚书。君曰：无之。则谐矣。”吉如女言。媒见石翁道吉意，石曰：“退婚已将二载。”媒曰：“有退婚书否？”石曰：“有。”检视之，书被鼠啮，字迹十不存一。石知不可为据，且闻吉不贫，遂复应允。过门后，石氏极平善，事女如姑。年馀生一子，产后多病，不能养。女代养之如己出。

    Explanation

    474

    If it can be done, it is a good thing, why not speak openly?" Ji was still doubtful and said, "There is one matter I would like to ask about. When I was young, I betrothed a woman from the Shi family as my wife. Due to poverty, I received some silver from him and wrote a formal document to break off the engagement. Can this be reversed?" The woman replied, "Yes, but I fear the Shi family may not be a good match, and marrying her would be a burden. Please ask the original matchmaker to set the wedding date. If the matchmaker says, 'I hear you have a document to break off the engagement,' you should say, 'No, I do not.' Then everything will be fine." Ji followed the woman's advice. The matchmaker went to see Shi's father and conveyed Ji's intentions. Shi said, "The engagement has been broken off for nearly two years." The matchmaker asked, "Do you have the document to break off the engagement?" Shi replied, "Yes." The matchmaker inspected it and found that the document had been gnawed by mice, with only a tenth of the text remaining. Shi knew it could not be used as evidence and, hearing that Ji was no longer poor, agreed to the marriage again. After the wedding, the Shi woman was very kind and treated her like an elder sister. A year later, she gave birth to a son, but she fell ill after childbirth and could not care for the child. The woman took care of the child as if it were her own.

    475

    先是，有以猫赠吉者，白质黑花，光洁可爱，吉珍之。女见猫似有畏意，曰：“君家五世不养此，至于子之身何反之？”吉不听，嗣养猫至六七头。女虽不甚畏惧，心颇厌之，复劝吉分赠他人。吉曰：“子非鼠，何如是之畏猫？”仍不听。至夜，女与子俱不见。未几，石氏卒。孑然独处，况味难堪，苦思惠。因忆惠之亡去以养猫一事，遂尽分散之。年馀仍无耗，不得已，复娶左氏之女。左不善居室，六七年间，清贫如故。

    Explanation

    475

    Before this, someone gave a cat to Ji, which had white fur with black spots, was bright and clean, and very lovable. Ji treasured it. The daughter saw the cat and seemed to have a sense of fear, saying, "Your family has not kept this kind of animal for five generations, why did you change it during your generation?" Ji did not listen and subsequently kept cats up to six or seven. The daughter, although not very afraid, was quite tired of it and again advised Ji to distribute them to others. Ji said, "You are not a mouse, why are you so afraid of cats," and still did not listen. By night, the daughter and son both disappeared. Not long after, Shi died. Alone and isolated, the situation was unbearable, suffering greatly thinking of Hui. Because he remembered that Hui had left due to keeping cats, he then distributed them all. After more than a year, there was still no news. Having no choice, he married the daughter of Zuo. Zuo was not good at managing the household, and for six or seven years, they remained as poor as before.

    476

    吉尝学子平术，遂以糊口四方。至城固，店居，每日赴村场为人讲命。一日至梅湖，过巨室门，内出一媪呼之，言给小郎君算命。媪导厅前，就坐推算，妇女垂帘听之。算毕，前媪搴帘出曰：“君能教书否？”曰：“能。”媪曰：“即算命之小郎君，得年七岁，欲拜门墙，量不摈斥。”吉喜从之。媪复导吉入别院，舍亦精洁。未几，媪捧酒食出，视之，皆夙昔所嗜食、惠女所常奉者。睹物思人，不禁太息。媪问之，吉曰：“吾思吾嫡妻惠氏。”

    Explanation

    476

    Ji once learned the art of fortune-telling, thus making a living by traveling around. He arrived in Chenggu, where he stayed in an inn, and every day went to village markets to tell fortunes for people. One day, he came to Meihu, passed by a large house, and an old woman inside called him, saying she wanted him to tell the fortune of her young master. The old woman led him to the hall, where he sat and began his calculations. Women listened through the curtains. After finishing, the old woman pulled back the curtain and said, "Can you teach books?" Ji replied, "Yes." She said, "The young master who had his fortune told is seven years old and wishes to become your disciple. Please do not reject him." Ji happily agreed. The old woman then led Ji into another courtyard, where the room was also clean and tidy. Soon after, the old woman brought out wine and food. Ji looked at it and saw that it was all the food he had liked in the past and what his wife, Huini, used to serve. Seeing the familiar items, he couldn't help but sigh deeply. The old woman asked him why, and Ji said, "I am thinking of my legitimate wife, Huini."

    477

    次日，媪送学生出。吉视之，极清秀，心计曰：“吾子若在，亦若大。得旦夕训诲之，岂非天伦乐事。”吉乍入书斋，闷甚。一日出游庄外，见庄首一败落人家，墙垣倾圮，惟存草舍。一贫妇自内出，蓬头垢面，情形堪悯。视之，惠女也。急至女前，执其手曰：“卿何困穷如是。”女曰：“一言难尽。”吉曰：“仆今设帐于此，所得束金尽足一人用度，无忧吃着无出。”女曰：“君何得到此？”吉将女去后景状，历历向女言之。

    Explanation

    477

    The next day, the old woman sent the student out. Mr. Ji looked at him and thought, "If my son were here, he would be just like him. To receive his daily instruction, isn't that a joyful family affair?" Mr. Ji felt very bored when he first entered the study. One day, he went for a walk outside the village and saw a dilapidated house at the edge of the village. The walls were crumbling, and only a simple thatched hut remained. An old woman came out of the house, with unkempt hair and a dirty face, her situation pitiable. Mr. Ji recognized her as his former student, Huai Nu. He quickly approached her and took her hand, saying, "Why are you so poor?" She replied, "It's hard to explain in one word." Mr. Ji said, "I am currently teaching here, and the payment I receive is sufficient for one person's expenses, with no worries about food and clothing." She asked, "How did you end up here?" Mr. Ji recounted the circumstances after she had left, describing them in detail to her.

    478

    女曰：“君外出，君之家豹乌圆应悉饿坏。”吉曰：“卿去后，即分给他人。为卿故，迄今不养猫，屈指已七年有馀。”女闻之，为之动容。吉曰：“日已向午，朝食已多时乎？”女曰：“不举火者已二日。”吉戚然曰：“书房尚有千馀钱，可暂作柴米需。卿少待，仆即回。”及吉取钱回，不见惠。意惠以他故偶出，立门外俟之。庄人曰：“先生何俟？”吉曰：“内人居此，偶出，将俟其归。”庄人曰：“先生误矣。此屋系绝产，无人居已数年。”吉闻之，怅然自归，而惑滋甚。

    Explanation

    478

    The woman said, "You went out, and everything at home must be in disarray by now." Ji replied, "After you left, I immediately gave it to others. For your sake, I haven't raised a cat since then, counting on my fingers, it's been more than seven years." Upon hearing this, the woman was moved. Ji said, "It's already past noon. Have you eaten yet?" The woman replied, "I haven't cooked for two days." Ji sighed and said, "There are still more than a thousand coins in the study room, which can temporarily be used for firewood and rice. Please wait a moment, I will return immediately." When Ji returned with the money, he didn't see the woman. He thought she had gone out for some other reason, so he stood outside the door waiting for her. A villager asked, "Why are you waiting, sir?" Ji said, "My wife lives here and went out occasionally. I will wait for her to return." The villager said, "You are mistaken, sir. This house is a house without an owner, and no one has lived here for several years." Upon hearing this, Ji returned home sadly, even more confused.

    479

    不几日，见僮仆匆忙洒扫庭内，问之，曰：“将于某日演剧庆主人寿。”吉自笑曰：“吾亦某日生人，亦系暗祝吾寿。”至日，老媪出请。吉入，见惠氏、左氏俱艳装出迓。吉惊喜交集，谓惠曰：“卿之神异，仆已素知。”谓左曰：“卿何得在此？”左曰：“惠姊矫君命接来耳。”惠指吉学生曰：“此即石氏所生。君忽睹若大之子，七年抚育，君宜拜谢。”吉笑曰：“仆固感情卿。非此，亦养老送终无人。”婢媪进衣冠，冠有金顶。吉曰：“朝廷名器，岂可虚假。”惠曰：“为君纳监，戴此不为僭。”吉大喜，衣冠毕，鞠部笙歌，街邻咸集，酒筵款待，俨然世家。夕，剧止客散。

    Explanation

    479

    Not long after, Jí saw the servants hurriedly cleaning the courtyard. When he asked about it, they said, "A play will be performed on a certain day to celebrate the host's birthday." Jí laughed to himself, saying, "I was also born on that day, and I secretly wish for my own birthday." On the day of the celebration, an elderly woman came out to invite him. Jí entered and saw Huì and Zuǒ both dressed in beautiful attire to greet him. Jí was filled with a mix of surprise and joy, and he said to Huì, "Your extraordinary nature, I have known for a long time." Turning to Zuǒ, he asked, "How did you come to be here?" Zuǒ replied, "Sister Huì pretended to be your command to bring me here." Huì pointed to Jí's student and said, "This is from the Shí family. You suddenly see such a large son, who has been nurtured for seven years, you should thank him." Jí smiled and said, "I naturally feel affection for you. Without this, there would be no one to care for me in old age or at the end of my life." The maidservants brought out his ceremonial attire, which had a golden top. Jí said, "Courtly emblems of rank, how could they be false?" Huì said, "For you to enter the imperial examination, wearing this is not overstepping." Jí was very happy, having finished dressing. The troupe of musicians and performers, and the neighbors all gathered. A banquet was held to entertain, and everything was as if from a noble family. In the evening, the play ended and the guests dispersed.

    480

    惠曰：“今日之乐，量君梦想不到。”吉曰：“然。日昨卿蒙不洁以欺仆，其意何居？”曰：“试君耳。妾如见弃于彼，君亦不得到此。原期吾子完婚日，使君受新妇参拜。以君恋恋之情，致君多享福数年。”既而曰：“君知演戏庆祝之深意乎？”曰：“不知也。”惠曰：“吾儿已长大，人以妾家无男老，无与为婚。人今见君父子翩翩，提亲者将接踵而至。”不数日，果有数家愿缔姻好。惠择淑女有福相者，聘为妇。过门后，惠忽欲归省，吉曰：“从未归省，今何顿异素行？”惠曰：“曩之归也，夜去明来，君自不知。今去不即返，将住数日，以休妾心，不得不告。”言已而杳，嗣无耗。

    　　虚白道人曰：鼠之为物，固不足惜。然五世不养猫，亦好行其德之事也。鼠之厚报之也，亦宜。

    Explanation

    480

    Hui said, "The joy of today is beyond your wildest dreams." Ji replied, "Indeed. Last night, you deceived me with your intimacy. What was your intention?" Hui said, "I was testing you. If I had been abandoned by him, you would not have been able to come here. I hope on the day our son gets married, to have you receive the new wife's formal greeting. Due to your affectionate feelings, you will enjoy many years of happiness." Ji then asked, "Do you understand the deeper meaning behind the celebration?" Hui replied, "I do not." Hui continued, "My son has grown up, and people say that our family lacks male heirs, making it difficult to find a suitable marriage. Now, people see your son and your family as charming, and many families are eager to propose. Within a few days, several families expressed their willingness to marry our son." Hui chose a virtuous and auspicious young woman and arranged for her to become his wife. After the wedding, Hui suddenly wanted to return home for a visit. Ji asked, "You have never returned home before. Why are you acting so differently now?" Hui said, "Last time, I left at night and returned in the morning, which you did not notice. This time, I will stay for several days to rest, so I must inform you." She spoke these words and then disappeared, and there was no further news.

    The Vain White Man said: The mouse, as a creature, is indeed not worth much. However, not keeping cats for five generations is also a kind of good deed. The mouse's generous repayment is indeed fitting.

    481

    离奇变幻，笔端令人不测。 马竹吾

    　　吉有不忍人之心，得享妻子之乐，所谓祸福为人自召。 盖防如

    　　篇中于鼠报德意，绝未一语道破，意境大高。 叶芸士

    　　水母三娘

    　　水母三娘，孝妇也，博山颜神镇人，姓颜氏，号文姜，宋时敕封顺德夫人。旧传夫人生前，及笄适郭门，人以三娘称之。夫早逝，郭母遣嫁。三娘曰：“母之令媳嫁也何意？如嫌媳为累，愿各爨以事母。”母曰：“非也。为媳青年耳。”三娘曰：“女不可二夫，否则禽兽不如。”姑闻之，喜甚。三娘勤事纺织以事母。进馔时，母每有愁容；竭奉旨甘，仍无愉色。母谓三娘曰：“甘旨非贫家所宜，吾惟嫌水苦咸耳。”三娘思近村水皆如是，有大河违庄少远，约有五里，遂每日挹注以供母食。春秋虽劳，犹可忍受。夏日汗出如洗，小衣尽湿。冬月行未及家，水冻筲中。每逢天变，势难自往，不惜重费，雇人代取。十年之久，未尝一日缺母食。

    Explanation

    481

    The writing is strange and ever-changing, keeping one unpredictable. — Ma Zhugu

    　　Gong Ji has a compassionate heart, enjoying the happiness of family life, which is what is meant by misfortunes and blessings being self-invoked by one's actions. — Gai Fangru

    　　In the story, the intention of the mouse's gratitude is not revealed in a single word, making the artistic conception very high. — Ye Yunshi

    　　Water Mother San Nian

    　　Water Mother San Nian was a filial woman from Bo Mountain Yan Shen Town, surnamed Yan, nicknamed Wen Jiang, and during the Song Dynasty, she was officially bestowed the title of Shunde Lady. It is said that before her death, at the age of marriage, she married into the Guo family, and people called her San Nian. Her husband died early, and the mother of the Guo family ordered her to remarry. San Nian said, "What does the mother mean by ordering her daughter-in-law to marry? If you think the daughter-in-law is a burden, I wish to live separately to serve my mother." The mother said, "No, it's just because the daughter-in-law is young." San Nian said, "A woman cannot have two husbands; otherwise, she would be less than an animal." Her mother-in-law heard this and was very pleased. San Nian diligently wove and spun to serve her mother. When serving food, the mother often had a worried look; even when serving the best food, the mother still had no joyful expression. The mother told San Nian, "Rich and delicious food is not suitable for a poor family; I only dislike the water being bitter and salty." San Nian thought that the water in nearby villages was all like this, and there was a big river a bit far from the village, about five li away, so she would fetch water daily to provide for her mother. Though laborious in spring and autumn, it is still bearable. In summer, she sweats profusely, and her undergarments get soaked. In winter, before reaching home, the water freezes in the bucket. Every time there is a change in the weather, it is difficult to go herself, so she would not hesitate to spend a lot to hire someone to fetch the water. For ten years, she never missed a day of providing water for her mother.

    482

    一日，取水归至庄首，少休树下，忽一人牵坐骥至。骥见水欲饮，其人麾之，触筲倒地，水尽倾，三娘不语而泣。其人曰：“此非缺水之地，水倾再汲亦易，以此而泣，何咎仆之甚？”三娘曰：“吾非咎君子。此水取之少远，吾力不能再取，而家中急需乎此，事出两难，是以泣。”其人问之，三娘曰：“母非此不食。”曰：“每日取之乎？”三娘曰：“每日取之，已十年有余。”其人愕然曰：“勿泣。吾有一术，能使原水归器。”乃以策就湿地画符一道，俄而筲中水满，而湿地已干。遂以策授三娘曰：“归家注水巨瓮，将策竖立其中。

    Explanation

    482

    One day, while fetching water back to the village entrance, she rested briefly under a tree. Suddenly, a person led a sitting horse to the scene. The horse, seeing the water, wanted to drink, and the person waved it away. In the process, the bucket knocked over and fell to the ground, spilling all the water. The woman named San did not speak but wept. The person said, "This is not a place where water is scarce; refilling the bucket is easy. Why cry so much?" San replied, "I am not blaming you. This water is fetched from a place that is quite far, and my strength cannot allow me to fetch it again. Our home desperately needs this water, and I am in a difficult situation, which is why I am crying." The person asked, "Does your mother not eat without this water?"
