After I married into the family, they began to associate, and their relationship gradually became closer." The official suspected that there was indeed something else going on. Zhao Zhong had been robbed twice, and it was not impossible that Shi Wen was behind it. Moreover, both times Zhao was robbed, it was after he had drunk with Shi Wen, which made the situation highly suspicious. The official then told the woman, "Do you remember the details of the borrowed items?" The woman replied, "There is a handwritten account sheet from Shi Wen, which lists everything clearly." The official said, "Tomorrow, you must secretly deliver this account sheet. It might lead to the capture of the thief and clear your husband's name. Never tell anyone else about this." The woman agreed. The official's subordinates then summoned the woman's mother-in-law and asked her, "Why do you want to sell your daughter-in-law to Shi Wen?" The woman's mother-in-law said, "Shi Wen said that if we marry her to him, he will not only waive the borrowed items but also return the previous land deed without asking for any payment." The official said, "Your daughter-in-law is beautiful; selling her will bring a lot of money. Do not marry her to Shi Wen. If you disobey, you will be severely punished." The woman's mother-in-law agreed and left.

    528

    公将六班总头役杨某呼至，语以李氏被窃等情，授以密计，令照办理。杨某遂觅名妓，语以公意，认为己女。烦同班能言者王某，见史文，言愿以女妻之。史文曰：“吾欲娶赵忠之妻李氏。”王某曰：“必不谐。盖日昨本官差吾将李氏之姑传至，官当堂谕之，不许李氏嫁汝，渠何敢故违！且杨某之女，较李氏尤艳，汝如见之，必魂飞天外。”史心动，曰：“吾可以见之否？”王曰：“可。某街有酒肆，女时由肆外往来探亲。肆饮而俟，必遇之。”史喜，立欲从王去。王曰：“何急也？明日吾候于彼。”史应诺而王去。

    Explanation

    528

    The official summoned the local supervisor Yang, and informed him of the theft of Li's family, giving him a secret plan to follow. Yang then found a famous courtesan, told her the official's intentions, and had her pretend to be his own daughter. He asked a capable speaker from the same rank, Wang, to meet with Shi Wen and propose that he marry the courtesan. Shi Wen said, "I want to marry Li, the wife of Zhao Zhong." Wang replied, "It will certainly not work. Because yesterday the official sent me to bring Li's aunt here, and decreed in court that Li is not to marry you. How dare she deliberately disobey! Moreover, Yang's daughter is even more beautiful than Li. If you see her, your soul will fly away." Shi Wen was moved, and asked, "Can I see her?" Wang said, "Yes. There is a tavern on a certain street, and the woman often goes to visit relatives outside the tavern. Drink there and wait, and you will surely meet her." Shi Wen was pleased and immediately wanted to go with Wang. Wang said, "Why so eager? I will wait for you there tomorrow." Shi Wen agreed and Wang left.

    529

    王以与史应对之言语杨某。次日史至，立出钱市肴与王饮。未几，王曰：“杨女来矣。”史见笄女与媪并行至，审视之，玉肌花貌，果愈李氏。媪谓女曰：“肆中客众，发财气象。”女笑应，斜睨而过。史立肆前目送之。王曰：“渠不久必回，可再饱视之。”遂移饮肆前以俟。未几，女与媪果偕归。至肆前，女腿带适开。媪令束之，曰：“金莲瘦小若是，何能佐婿家中馈事？”女笑曰：“勿代他人致忧。”女束带，谈笑而去。王曰：“容颜如何？”史曰：“处处可人。渠要聘金几何？”王曰：“明日复命。”次日，王见史曰：“杨某不索聘金，唯要金银首饰等数件。

    Explanation

    529

    Wang conversed with the historian about Yang. The next day, the historian arrived, and Wang immediately spent money to buy food and drank with Wang. Shortly after, Wang said, "The daughter of Yang has come." The historian saw a young woman and an old woman walking together. He examined her carefully and found that she was indeed more beautiful than Li. The old woman told the girl, "There are many guests in the shop; it looks prosperous." The girl smiled and replied, glancing sideways as she passed by. Wang said, "He/she will surely return soon, you can look at him/her again." They then moved to drink in front of the tavern to wait. Soon, the girl and the old woman returned. As they passed by the tavern, the girl's leg band just opened. The old woman ordered her to tie it up, saying, "Such slender and small feet, how could she manage household affairs for her husband?" The girl smiled and said, "Do not worry about others." She tied up her leg band and left, talking and laughing. Wang asked, "How is she?" The historian said, "She is charming in every way. How much dowry does she want?" Wang said, "I will report back tomorrow." The next day, Wang told the historian, "Yang did not ask for a dowry, only wanted a few pieces of gold and silver jewelry."

    530

    然首饰等物，新人过门，全行带回，与汝无损也。”史问之，王历言其物，史悉应之。于是择吉纳聘。杨将所得史文之物，悉献于公。公视之，与史文开载赵忠所借，样式分两悉同。大怒，立差役拘史文到，问之曰：“汝借给赵忠之物，汝家尚有一样者否？”史曰：“无之。”公以首饰等物示之，曰：“此汝聘杨某之女之物，非即借给赵忠之物乎？”史曰：“不是。”公以帐单示之，曰：“若果不是，何以与汝开载样式分两悉同？”史曰：“烦匠人照样打造。”公曰：“何人打造？”史不语。公曰：“明是汝借出而复窃回，尚强辨耶？”令役刑之。

    Explanation

    530

    The ornaments and other items will be returned to the new wife upon her marriage, causing no loss to you," said the official. Shi Wen asked about them, and Wang listed the items, to which Shi Wen responded in full. Thus, they chose an auspicious day for the engagement ceremony. Yang presented all the items he had received from Shi Wen to the official. The official examined them and found that their descriptions and specifications matched exactly with those of the items S Zhao had borrowed, as recorded in Shi Wen's account. He was greatly angered and immediately dispatched officials to summon Shi Wen. He asked him, "Do you still have any items like those you lent to Zhao Zhong?" Shi Wen replied, "No, I do not." The official showed him the ornaments and other items, saying, "These are the items you used to betroth Yang's daughter. Are they not the same as those you lent to Zhao Zhong?" Shi Wen said, "They are not." The official then showed him the account, asking, "If they are not the same, why do their descriptions and specifications match exactly?" Shi Wen said, "I asked the craftsman to make them according to the same specifications." The official asked, "Who made them?" Shi Wen remained silent. The official said, "It is clear that you borrowed them and then stole them back. Why do you still try to deny it?" He ordered the officials to punish Shi Wen.

    531

    刑已，公曰：“杨某无女，吾令认妓为女，伪为嫁汝，以赚汝首饰等物。今证据不爽，汝何得不认？”史关口夺气，遂认之曰：“实身窃回。”公曰：“赵忠当地于汝，其地价亦汝醉之以酒而窃取之。”史欲言，公止之，曰：“汝且勿言，吾视汝如见肺肝。汝之窃赵财物，非为财，实为色。汝爱李氏之美，故欲赵速贫而图其妻。”史以心意被公猜破，不得不招。公遂将地判归李氏。问史曰：“汝发财几年矣？”史曰：“五六年。”公曰：“金银等物非寻常百姓家所易有，其物何来？赵忠因汝窃自缢，汝已无生理，可实言。”史自知罪无可宥，遂吐实。盖漏网之巨盗，更名史文。

    Explanation

    531

    After the punishment was carried out, the public official said, "Yang has no daughter, and I ordered someone to recognize a prostitute as his daughter, falsely claiming that she was married to you in order to deceive you into giving away your jewelry and other valuables. The evidence is conclusive; how can you deny it?" The man, overwhelmed with fear, admitted, "I did indeed steal them back." The official continued, "Zhao Zhong sold land to you, and the price of that land was also obtained by getting you drunk and stealing it." The man tried to speak, but the official stopped him, saying, "Hold your tongue. I see your true nature as clearly as seeing your innermost thoughts. You stole Zhao's property not for money, but for the sake of beauty. You love the beauty of Li, and that is why you wanted Zhao to become poor quickly so you could marry his wife." Realizing that his thoughts had been guessed by the official, the man could no longer deny it. The official then ruled that the land be returned to Li. Turning to the man, he asked, "How long have you been wealthy?" The man replied, "Five or six years." The official said, "Gold, silver, and other valuables are not easily obtained by ordinary people. Where did you get them? Zhao Zhong hanged himself because of your theft, and you have no chance of escape. Tell the truth." Knowing that his crime was unforgivable, the man confessed. He was a major criminal who had escaped justice, and he had changed his name to Shi Wen.

    532

    司徒政自幼结邻村赵义之女三官为妻。政年已逾冠，因父服未阙，故未亲迎。政从塾师读。一日，同砚友周木连出游，信步至赵义庄外丘陵上，下有桃园，周与赵同村居，遂指曰：“彼即令岳之桃园。”园有笄女，周复曰：“彼笄女即令正。今桃实有蕡，君盍假摘桃以瞻芳姿。”政从之。见女，故为多言以餂之，曰：“吾特来摘桃食，勿谓不相识。吾居某村，姓司徒，名政，得年二十一岁，某月某日某时生。”女微笑曰：“吾识君。

    Explanation

    532

    From a young age, Simu Zheng married San Guan, the daughter of his neighbor Zhao Yi. Zheng was already over twenty years old, but due to the mourning period for his father not having ended, he had not yet gone through the formal wedding ceremony. Zheng studied under a private tutor. One day, he went out for a leisurely stroll with his classmate Zhou Mulian. They wandered to the outskirts of Zhao Yi's village, where there was a hillside with a peach orchard below. Zhou, who lived in the same village as Zhao, pointed and said, "That is the peach orchard of your father-in-law." There was a young woman in the orchard, and Zhou remarked, "That young woman is your wife. The peaches are ripe now; why don't you go ahead and pick some to admire her beauty?" Zheng followed his suggestion. When he saw the girl, he chattered to her in an attempt to flatter her, saying, "I especially came here to pick peaches. Don't say we don't know each other. I live in a certain village, my surname is Simu, my given name is Zheng, I am twenty-one years old, born on a certain month, day, and hour." The girl smiled and said, "I know you."

    533

    吾非算命先生，何必言君生辰八字？”急摘数桃与政曰：“君速去，勿为人遇以作话柄。”政受桃时掺执女手，将欲有言。女伪曰：“邻妇来矣。”政释女，女急退避之，政亦去。当政之来也，三官比邻钱氏女适至，见政，隐身树后以视之。政既去，钱女从树后转出，曰：“摘桃人何去之速也？得若个好婿，接谈片时佳甚，子速之去，性与人殊矣。”三官曰：“无之。”钱曰：“渠来时自言生辰，去时执子之手。吾悉闻见，尚云无之耶？”三官曰：“诚有之。祈妹缄口，以免嘲笑。”钱笑应之。

    Explanation

    533

    I am not a fortune-teller, so why should I mention your birth details?” Quickly picking some peaches, she said to Zheng, “Go quickly, don't let others see you and make it into gossip.” Zheng took the peaches and held the woman's hand, about to say something. The woman pretended and said, “The neighbor's wife has arrived.” Zheng released the woman, and she quickly retreated. Zheng also left. When Zheng arrived, San Guan's neighbor Qian Nü just happened to come, saw Zheng, and hid behind a tree to watch. After Zheng left, Qian Nü stepped out from behind the tree and said, “Why did the peach picker leave so quickly? He got such a good match, talking for a short while was quite pleasant, you made him leave quickly, his nature is different from others.” San Guan said, “No, there wasn't.” Qian said, “He mentioned his birth details when he came, and held your hand when he left. I saw and heard everything, how can you still say there wasn't?” San Guan said, “Indeed, there was. Please keep quiet, to avoid being laughed at.” Qian laughed and responded.

    534

    异日，周木连自塾归，过赵义门，适三官独立门口，睨之，婉如清扬，不觉神驰。诣家，欲火孔炽，实难自禁。闻女自居一室，室靠闲园有牖，因思逾垣隔窗与语，少慰渴想。既至，垣有倾圯，遂直入，微扣窗棂。盖三官有邻女钱氏常与伴宿，三官因连夜服事母病，惟钱女在室。闻扣声，知非贼，问之，木连伪托司徒政曰：“日昨摘桃人。”钱知为三官婿，遂伪为三官之言曰：“君深夜来此何为？”曰：“昨睹玉色，时凝寤寐，敬来一会。”钱曰：“此非会时也。”木连曰：“何时得会？”钱曰：“嫁娶。”木连曰：“情极不能待。卿不悯怜，请死于此。”钱思冒三官名，暗与情郎一会，亦佳，启窗纳之，遂相狎。木连曰：“仆爱卿双翘，愿赐一履以寓情怀。”钱曰：“妾所着，旧而秽。妾有新履，可以赠君。”遂将三官之刺绣五纹新小靴给木连。木连与钱连会三夜，始赴塾。

    Explanation

    534

    One day, Zhou Mulian returned from the private school and passed by Zhao's gate. As he happened to see Miss San Guan standing alone at the door, he glanced at her, and her graceful and elegant appearance captivated him. Upon arriving home, his desire burned intensely, and he found it difficult to restrain himself. Hearing that the woman lived alone in a room adjacent to a quiet garden with a window, he thought of climbing over the wall and speaking through the window to ease his longing. When he arrived, he found the wall partially collapsed, so he went straight in and gently tapped the window frame. It turned out that Miss San Guan had a neighbor's daughter named Qian, who often stayed with her. Miss San Guan had been tending to her mother's illness all night, leaving only Qian in the room. When Qian heard the knocking, she knew it was not a thief and asked who it was. Zhou Mulian pretended to be Sima Zheng and said, "I am the person who picked peaches yesterday." Qian recognized him as Miss San Guan's husband and pretended to speak for Miss San Guan, asking, "What brings you here at this late hour?" Zhou Mulian replied, "I saw your beauty yesterday and have been thinking of you day and night. I respectfully come to pay a visit." Qian said, "This is not the right time for a meeting." Zhou Mulian asked, "When can we meet?" Qian replied, "After marriage." Zhou Mulian said, "My emotions are so intense that I cannot wait. If you do not pity me, I will ask to die here." Qian thought that pretending to be Miss San Guan and secretly meeting her lover would be good, so she opened the window and let him in. They then became intimate. Zhou Mulian said, "I love your beautiful feet. I wish to receive a pair of shoes to express my feelings." Qian said, "The shoes I am wearing are old and dirty. I have a new pair of shoes that I can give to you." She then gave Zhou Mulian a new, embroidered, and decorated pair of small boots belonging to Miss San Guan. Zhou Mulian and Qian met for three consecutive nights before finally going back to the school.

    535

    月馀，木连托故归，盖以不能忘情其所私也。夜静欲赴，甫出门，遇对门车三饮酒归。立谈之际，不觉坠三官之靴于地。车拾之，木连恳求掷还。车曰：“子无妻室，此物何来？实言，吾即与之。”木连初不肯，车壮盛，力不能强求，不得已，直告之。车曰：“若然，子必与吾同去一次，吾始与。”木连决言不可。车曰：“吾既知其处，不难自往。”言已欲去。木连掣之，车推木连跌仆迳去。木连急起追之，将及赵园垣倾处，木连从后以石击之，车头破脑出而死。

    Explanation

    535

    More than a month later, Muguan pretended to return home, because he could not forget his feelings for his private matter. At night, he wanted to go, just as he was about to leave, he met his neighbor, Car San, who was returning home after drinking. During their brief conversation, without realizing, he dropped San Guan's shoe on the ground. Car picked it up and Muguan earnestly requested that it be thrown back. Car said, "You don't have a wife or family, how did you get this? Tell the truth, I will give it to you." Muguan initially refused, but Car was strong and powerful, and Muguan could not force him. Reluctantly, he told the truth. Car said, "If that's the case, you must go with me once, and I will give it to you." Muguan firmly refused. Car said, "I already know where it is, it's not difficult for me to go there alone." After saying this, he wanted to leave. Muguan grabbed him, and Car pushed Muguan, causing him to fall and run away. Muguan quickly got up and chased after him. Just as he was about to reach the collapsed wall of Zhao Garden, Muguan struck him from behind with a stone, and Car's head was crushed and he died.

    536

    车父喊禀于公。检验时，车父以女履呈于公，言于死尸怀中得之。时公闻旁观之妇人曰：“是履乃……”相违少远，仅闻此三字。公令役将私议之妇唤至，曰：“适才汝‘是履乃’之言吾已悉闻，可再言之。如有一言不实，重责不贷。”妇惧，曰：“适才吾言是履乃赵三官之履，遗失多日，寻觅不得，奈何在死尸怀中？”公曰：“汝言是实，与吾所闻不爽。三官之父为谁？”妇曰：“渠父名赵义，已卒，唯有老母黄氏在。”公曰：“渠居何处？”妇指闲园曰：“即是第。”公视之，见靠园有室系活窗，曰：“乡村不宜如此，盖不利于贼盗也。

    Explanation

    536

    The cart driver reported the matter to the official. During the inspection, the cart driver presented a woman's shoe to the official, stating that it was found in the dead person's embrace. At that moment, the official heard a bystander woman say, "This shoe belongs to..." but the sound was muffled, and only the first few words were audible. The official ordered the attendant to bring the woman who had spoken privately to him. He said, "Just now, your words 'this shoe belongs to' I have fully heard. Please repeat them. If even one word is false, you will face severe punishment without mercy." The woman, frightened, said, "Just now, I said this shoe belongs to Sān Guān, who lost it many days ago and could not find it. How could it be in the dead person's embrace?" The official replied, "What you said is true, and it matches what I heard. Who is the father of Sān Guān?" The woman pointed to the garden and said, "This is his house." The official looked and saw a room near the garden with a living window. He said, "In the countryside, it should not be like this, as it is not conducive to thieves."

    537

    ”令役传黄氏至，曰：“汝女尝失履乎？”黄恐累于命案，曰：“未也。”公执女履曰：“此非汝女之履乎？”黄曰：“亦非也。”妇曰：“吾已禀明。”黄始承之。公令取对履。黄取至，果大小花样无少异。令女役带黄氏母女进州。公意三官必以履赠所私，而托言遗失。及见女，貌虽美都，毫无淫意，复疑之。托言谓黄氏曰：“汝母女他居不便，居衙中可也。”令婢媪醉三官，乘其醉睡，窥其私，俨然处子。公益疑，问三官曰：“汝履何以在尸怀中？”女曰：“不知。吾履失已月馀。”公曰：“遗失之处汝知之否？”女曰：“知之，即靠闲园活窗之室。月前有邻女钱氏伴吾宿于室，吾因母病傍母眠，惟钱女自宿于室四五日，吾履忽亡其一。”公令役传钱女。

    Explanation

    537

    The officer summoned Huang to come and said, "Did your daughter ever lose her shoe?" Huang, fearing it might implicate her in a murder case, replied, "No." The magistrate took the girl's shoe and said, "Is this not your daughter's shoe?" Huang answered, "No, it is not." The woman said, "I have already reported it." Huang then admitted it. The magistrate ordered to fetch the matching shoe. Huang brought it, and indeed, they were exactly the same in size and pattern. The magistrate ordered the female officer to bring Huang's mother and daughter to the state. The magistrate thought that the third official must have given the shoe to his lover and claimed it was lost. When he saw the girl, although she was beautiful, she showed no signs of promiscuity, leading him to doubt her again. Pretending to Huang, he said, "It is inconvenient for your mother and daughter to live elsewhere; you can stay in the government office." The magistrate ordered the maidservants to get the third official drunk, while he was drunk and asleep, they spied on his private affairs and found out that the girl was still a virgin. The magistrate doubted even more and questioned the third official, "Why is your shoe in the corpse's embrace?" The girl replied, "I don't know. My shoe has been missing for over a month." The magistrate asked, "Do you know where it was lost?" The girl answered, "Yes, it was in the room near the idle garden with the living window. A month ago, a neighbor girl named Qian stayed with me in the room. I slept beside my mother because she was sick, while Qian stayed in the room for four or five days, and my shoe suddenly went missing." The magistrate ordered the officer to summon Qian.

    538

    不日，女母偕女到堂。公见女容饰不雅，谓女母曰：“汝女字人否？”曰：“未也。”公曰：“若大之女而不字人，大失为母之心。”问钱女曰：“三官供言，月前汝伴宿于其家，有诸乎？”女母代答曰：“有之。”公复问曰：“三官因母病伴母，汝自宿其室四五日，有诸乎？”女母不知，不能代答，谓其女曰：“可实言之。”女曰：“亦有之。”公曰：“汝既自居其室，三官之履即于彼时不见，汝必知履之所在。”女答言不知，公笑曰：“必汝自宿之时，以履赠交好之人矣。”女不服，且出言不逊。公怒，令女役强验其私。

    Explanation

    538

    In a few days, the woman's mother accompanied her to the court. The official observed that the daughter's appearance was not elegant and said to the mother, "Has your daughter been married?" The mother replied, "No." The official said, "If your daughter is of marriageable age and has not been married, you have greatly failed to fulfill the heart of a mother." The official then asked the woman, "According to the testimony of the Three Officials, a month ago, you stayed overnight at his house. Is this true?" The mother answered on behalf of her daughter, "Yes, it is true." The official asked again, "The Three Officials stayed with the mother because she was ill. Did you stay in his room for four or five days on your own?" The mother did not know and could not answer, so she told her daughter, "Tell the truth." The daughter said, "Yes, I did." The official said, "If you stayed in his room, the shoes of the Three Officials must have disappeared at that time. You must know where the shoes are." The daughter answered that she did not know. The official smiled and said, "You must have given the shoes to a close friend when you stayed there." The daughter denied the accusation and spoke rudely. The official became angry and ordered the female servant to forcibly examine her private parts.

    539

    女役复曰：“实非处子。”公笑曰：“汝身破于何人？”女不答。公怒，呵役刑之。女惧曰：“实与三官之婿司徒政有交。渠索赠，当即以三官之履赠之。”公曰：“汝与司徒政素相识乎？”女曰：“不识。”公曰：“既不相识，何以知为司徒政？”女将三官看桃，其婿摘桃，并月前某日夜扣窗棂，自称摘桃人，一一言其情节。复曰：“彼时渠以吾为其妻三官，吾即冒三官之名启窗纳之，因连会三夜，属实。”公呼三官上堂，以摘桃之事问之。三官细言之，与钱所言大同小异。公因令三官母女暂归候传，出票立传司徒政。以有紧急公事赴凤翔，委官审究。

    Explanation

    539

    The female servant replied, "In fact, she is not a virgin." The official laughed and said, "Whom did you lose your virginity to?" The servant did not answer. The official became angry and ordered the servant to be punished. The servant, out of fear, said, "I did have a relationship with Si Tu Zheng, the husband of San Guan. He asked for a gift, and I gave him San Guan's shoes." The official asked, "Do you know Si Tu Zheng personally?" The servant replied, "I do not." The official said, "If you do not know him personally, how do you know it was Si Tu Zheng?" The servant explained that on a certain night before, she was looking at peaches, and her husband, Si Tu Zheng, picked a peach and knocked on the window, introducing himself as the peach-picker. She recounted the details of the incident. She added, "At that time, he thought I was his wife San Guan, so I pretended to be San Guan and opened the window to let him in. We met for three consecutive nights, and this is true." The official then called San Guan to the hall and asked her about the peach-picking incident. San Guan explained in detail, and her account was generally the same but with minor differences. The official then ordered San Guan and her mother to return home and await further notice, issued a warrant for Si Tu Zheng, and delegated an official to investigate because of urgent official business in Fengxiang.

    540

    及归，案已定，候公出详。盖承审者以严刑逼政，政已畏刑而招杀车三之事矣。公披阅案卷，见供判不符，情实未真，遂提钱女问之曰：“汝与奸夫连会三夜，或每夜更换，不是一人？”钱曰：“是。”公曰：“事在黑夜，何以知是一人？”钱曰：“其人背后左肩下，有疮疤如钱，每夜手触之。”公令提政上堂，赤政上身验之，无疤。令钱视之，钱大骇。公曰：“必汝与所私者情厚，不肯言其姓名，而移祸于政。”女不应。公令刑之，女稽首曰：“即刑死吾，吾亦不敢承认。”时从人请公退食。公令人犯暂下，即席闷坐饮，深思遐想，案无情由，及日暮举烛不言食。

    Explanation

    540

    Upon returning, the case was already decided, and the magistrate was waiting to review it. The examining official had used severe punishment to pressure Zheng, who, already fearing the punishment, confessed to the murder of three people. The magistrate reviewed the case files and found that the confession did not match the judgment, and the truth was not clear. He then summoned the girl and asked her, "Did you meet with your lover three nights in a row, or did you change partners every night, not the same person each time?" The girl replied, "Yes." The magistrate said, "It was in the middle of the night; how could you know it was the same person?" The girl replied, "There is a scar on the left shoulder of the back of the person, like a coin, which I touched every night." The magistrate ordered Zheng to be brought to the hall, stripped of his upper body for inspection, and found no scar. He ordered the girl to look, and she was greatly frightened. The magistrate said, "You must have a close relationship with your lover, unwilling to reveal his name, and have shifted the blame onto Zheng." The girl did not respond. The magistrate ordered her to be punished, and the girl knelt and said, "Even if I die from the punishment, I will not dare to admit it." At this time, someone asked the magistrate to step aside for a meal. The magistrate ordered the accused to be temporarily released, sat alone at the table, deeply pondering, finding no evidence, and by dusk, still holding the candle without speaking of food.

    541

    忽得端绪，不遑食，急升堂，问司徒政曰：“汝之摘桃，本心欲往耶，抑被人恿怂而去？所摘之桃，自食之耶，抑与人共食？”政将与窗友周木连同游及摘桃之事，历历细言之。公曰：“汝得生路矣。”遂令去其刑具。令役立传周木连到案，问之曰：“令政桃园戏妻者，非汝耶？”木连曰：“是。”公曰：“冒摘桃人而淫其妻者，亦汝耶？”木连不认。公曰：“汝冒政而欲淫其妻，钱女冒其妻而与汝交。钱女言汝背后左肩下有疮疤如钱，若无之，则真非汝。”令役袒其背视之，果有疮疤如女言。公曰：“钱女之言不妄，汝与钱女有私亦不妄。”木连不言。公令提钱女面质，木连不得不认。公曰：“若然，车三亦汝伤害矣。”木连不服，公曰：“钱女所赠之履，何以在车三尸怀中？”木连自维无理可辨，遂直言害车三之故，案始定。

    Explanation

    541

    Suddenly gaining the lead, he had no time to eat, hurriedly entering the hall, he questioned the official Simu Zheng, saying, "Was it your original intention to pluck peaches, or were you coerced or persuaded to go? Did you eat the peaches yourself, or did you share them with others?" Zheng recounted in detail the incident of his journey with his friend Zhou Muliang and the act of plucking peaches. The official said, "You have found a way to live." He then ordered the removal of Zheng's restraints. He immediately summoned Zhou Muliang to appear in court, asking him, "Was it not you who impersonated the peach plucker and had relations with his wife?" Zhou Muliang replied, "Yes." The official said, "Was it not you who impersonated the peach plucker and had relations with his wife?" Zhou Muliang denied it. The official said, "You impersonated Zheng to have relations with his wife, and Qian Girl impersonated his wife to have relations with you. Qian Girl said that you have a scar on your left shoulder like a coin. If it is not there, then it is not truly you." The official ordered a servant to uncover his back, and indeed, there was a scar like the one described. The official said, "Qian Girl's words are not false, and it is not false that you have a secret affair with her." Zhou Muliang remained silent. The official ordered Qian Girl to be brought in for a face-to-face confrontation, and Zhou Muliang could not help but admit it. The official said, "If so, Chu San was also harmed by you." Zhou Muliang refused to admit it, but the official said, "How did Qian Girl's shoes end up in Chu San's corpse?" Zhou Muliang found no reason to argue and finally stated the reason for harming Chu San, and the case was finally settled.

    542

    虚白道人曰：天与水违行而讼起，险与健相攻而讼成。刑固不可废，讼亦不能无，而听之实不易易也。魏戍望轻，不断梗阳之讼；子反辨短，莫决皇戍之辞。以知有忠信之实，慈惠之心，而始优为之。故听讼者无先入之成心，则闲孚皆有当；无违理之偏听，则不肖无所容。惟有以尽其相，穷其神，而摘伏惩奸，始破小人之胆；惟有以关其口，夺其气，而诘奸锄暴，始安良善之心。不然，而欲悉其聪明，致其忠爱，亦綦难矣。陇州三案：一巧分伪诈，具是非之明；一深用智谋，成细微之案。至于周木连之案，深思端绪，罪坐真情，俾无辜无戴盆之冤，淫凶无漏网之幸。使云生李太守知之，亦将录于《刑案汇览》。

    Explanation

    542

    The Master of Pure White said: When heaven and water move in opposite directions, disputes arise; when danger and strength attack each other, disputes are formed. Punishment is inherently unavoidable, and disputes cannot be entirely avoided, yet to hear and judge them is truly not easy. Wei Shu was too lenient, and the disputes in Gengyang were unresolved; Zi Fan was too short-sighted, and the disputes in Huang Shu could not be resolved. Thus, it is known that with genuine loyalty and trustworthiness, and a compassionate heart, one can begin to excel in doing it. Therefore, those who hear disputes without preconceived notions will find all arguments appropriate and convincing; without listening to one-sided, unreasonable arguments, the unworthy have no place to hide. Only by fully understanding their nature and exhausting their subtleties, uncovering hidden plots and punishing the wicked, can one break the courage of the wicked; only by shutting their mouths and taking away their spirit, interrogating the wicked and eliminating the violent, can one reassure the hearts of the good and righteous. Otherwise, and to fully utilize their wisdom and express their loyalty and love, is truly very difficult. The three cases of Longzhou: one skillfully distinguishes between falsehood and deceit, with clear understanding of right and wrong; one deeply uses wisdom and strategy, resolving subtle cases. As for the case of Zhou Mulian, by deeply thinking about the origins and holding accountable based on true facts, the innocent have no unjust burden, and the wicked have no chance to escape. If known to Li Taishou, it would also be recorded in The Comprehensive Review of Criminal Cases.

    543

    事妙文亦妙，两堪不朽。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　王富段成

    　　岁次己末夏季，十九日未初，武库焚，声如怒雷，闻三十馀里。余斋违城六、七里，其声更厉，窗纸为之破，斋中尘落如雾。初以为无云而雷，及南望，浓烟飞腾，上冲晴空，意谓武库被火。移时，问南来之人，果然。次日以他事赴城，因往观之。武库地基，火药坐坑四处，阔皆二、三步，深悉四、五尺，砖石木料悉为火药冲去；邻近房舍全无，人之被火药所伤，塌屋所压而死者，不知其数。其时，有无故远之而脱其难者，有无故近之而遇其害者，诚生死有命矣。

    Explanation

    543

    A Wonderful Event and a Wonderful Account, Both Immortal

    Annotated by Li Yu of Shangyuan

    In the year of the Earth Horse, during the summer season, on the nineteenth day of the Wei hour, the armory burned, and the sound was like a furious thunder, heard for more than thirty miles. My study was six or seven miles from the city, and the sound was even louder, breaking the window paper and causing dust to fall like mist in the study. At first, it was thought to be thunder without clouds. When looking south, thick smoke rose, reaching the clear sky, leading to the thought that the armory was on fire. After some time, asking people who came from the south, it was indeed confirmed. The next day, going to the city for other matters, I went to see it. The foundation of the armory had firecracker pits in four places, each wide about two or three steps and deep about four or five feet. Bricks, stones, and wood were all blown away by the firecrackers. All nearby houses were destroyed, and the number of people who were injured by the firecrackers, crushed by falling buildings, and died is unknown. At that time, there were those who escaped danger by chance, and those who encountered harm by chance, truly fate determines life and death.

    544

    有木工王富者，西关人。一弟名贵，嗜赌，往往赌输典衣，富屡为回赎之。是日，富在武库修理木器，贵质衣而赌，输，赴武库寻兄索钱赎之。富见贵赤身，问之，贵实告。富怒，揪发捶楚，贵忍受不返手。库人拉问之，知渠为胞兄弟，曰：“令弟既已质衣，理合出钱使弟赎回。”富曰：“回赎已非一次。”库人曰：“即非一次，胞弟无衣，亦不宜坐视。”富曰：“即出钱与之，难必其不复赌，其衣未必能赎。”库人谓贵曰：“赎衣后，务必来此，使令兄见之。”贵应诺。贵执钱去，未几衣衣回，立富面前不语。富见之，不禁暗喜，谓贵曰：“日将午，汝必自晨未食。”贵答以不饥。富曰：“勿欺。吾傢伙篮中尚有钱文数十，可取去买饭用。

    Explanation

    544

    There was a carpenter named Wang Fu, a native of Xiguan. He had a younger brother named Gui, who was addicted to gambling and often pawned his clothes when he lost. Wang Fu repeatedly redeemed the clothes for him. On this day, while Wang Fu was repairing wooden items in the armory, Gui pawned his clothes and gambled, losing and then went to the armory to ask his elder brother for money to redeem them. Wang Fu saw Gui naked and asked him about it. Gui truthfully confessed. Wang Fu became angry, grabbed him by the hair and beat him, but Gui endured the beating without retaliating. The warehouse workers pulled and asked him, and upon learning that they were brothers, they said, "Since your younger brother has already pawned his clothes, it is reasonable for you to pay money to redeem them." Wang Fu replied, "Redeeming has not been the first time." The workers said, "Even if it is not the first time, your younger brother without clothes, it is not right to watch without intervening." Wang Fu said, "If I give him money now, it's hard to guarantee he won't gamble again, and his clothes might not be redeemable." The workers told Gui, "After redeeming the clothes, you must come here so that your elder brother can see you." Gui agreed. Gui took the money and left, and soon returned with his clothes, standing before Wang Fu without speaking. Wang Fu saw him and could not help but feel secretly pleased, saying to Gui, "It's almost noon, you must not have eaten since morning." Gui answered that he was not hungry. Wang Fu said, "Don't lie. There are still several coins in the basket of tools, you can take them to buy food."

    545

    ”贵取钱东行，愧悔交深。不深饥，因登北门楼，卧石台。既而有二人来，视之，素相识之博友。一人曰：“汝兄有难，可速救之。惟东行数百步，可免祸。”一人曰：“适才渠受其兄之紾，怀恨必深，即语之，亦未必听从。”贵曰：“是何言也！兄之紾吾，是吾自取，于兄何怨。”言已而醒，知为梦。忽忆其二人早死，大惊，急起而赴。恐直言兄不相信，遂伪言曰：“邻街张某欲作室数间，请兄敦匠事。今张某在汇波阁立俟见兄，定兴工之期。”富亦闻张某欲修造，遂信之，立同贵行。至阁，问贵曰：“张某安在？”贵未及答，而武库焚。及富见在库者悉遇害，曰：“张某，吾之救星也。”贵曰：“张某无修造事，实弟伪言之。”始以梦鬼之事语兄。

    Explanation

    545

    Guì took money and went east, feeling deep guilt and regret. He was not very hungry, so he climbed the northern gate tower and lay on a stone platform. After a while, two people came, and upon seeing them, he recognized them as long-time acquaintances and good friends. One of them said, "Your brother is in trouble; you can quickly save him. Only go east for hundreds of steps, and you can avoid disaster." The other said, "Just now, he suffered from his brother's mistreatment and must harbor deep resentment. Even if you tell him, he may not necessarily listen." Guì said, "What kind of words are these! My brother's mistreatment of me is something I brought upon myself; what do I have to resent him for?" After speaking, he woke up, knowing it was a dream. Suddenly remembering that the two people had died early, he was greatly surprised and quickly got up to go. Fearing that his brother would not believe him if he spoke directly, he fabricated a lie and said, "Mr. Zhang near the street wants to build several rooms and asks you to manage the construction work. Now, Mr. Zhang is at Hui Bo Pavilion, waiting to see you to set the date for starting work." Fù also heard that Mr. Zhang wanted to build and believed it, immediately going with Guì. Upon arriving at the pavilion, Fù asked Guì, "Where is Mr. Zhang?" Before Guì could answer, the armory was set on fire. When Fù saw that all those in the armory had been killed, he said, "Mr. Zhang is my savior." Guì said, "Mr. Zhang has no building plans; it was actually a lie I fabricated." Then, he told his brother about the dream about the ghost.

    546

    余庄人某，贸易归，路经齐河桥，在桥下饭肆买用饮食，见一人与肆人言武库事，欲听之以证传闻，而其言已终，仅闻其人言曰：“吾以五百钱免遭横死，幸莫大焉。”言已匆匆去。某问诸肆人，肆人细述之，曰：“适去之人，食兵马粮，姓段，有二名，本名志成，在官名成。武库事前数日，在此少休，吾问其姓氏，段兼言为火药事，奉官差进省。时桥头一大车输载不行，盖以桥头坏一石，因伤车轴故也。段见而问之，吾历言其故。段趋视而回曰：‘坑坎不阔，修补亦易，岂可坐视为行客累？’吾曰：‘无施主。’段问所费，我对曰：‘五百文足矣。’段曰：‘所费有限，吾欲捐输。惜吾公事在身，不暇为此。’吾曰：‘客果有此善心，吾愿代劳，以襄盛事。’段曰：‘可。’吾曰：‘客几日可毕公事？’段曰：‘大约六、七日即回。’吾曰：‘客来时验功，断不肯迟延，以负善意。

    Explanation

    546

    A man from my village, returning from trade, passed by Qihé Bridge. While buying food and drink at a restaurant under the bridge, he saw a man conversing with the restaurant owner about matters related to the armory. He wanted to listen in to verify a rumor he had heard, but the conversation had already ended. He only heard the man say, "I managed to avoid a fatal accident with 500 coins, which is a great fortune." After saying this, the man hurried away.

    The man then asked the restaurant owner for details. The owner explained, "The person who just left was eating military rations. His name is Duan, and he has two names. His personal name is Zhicheng, but his official name is Cheng. A few days before the armory matter, he stopped here for a short rest. I asked him about his name, and he mentioned that he was on official duty related to gunpowder and was heading to the provincial capital. At the time, a large cart was stuck at the bridgehead because a stone had broken, damaging the cart's axle. Duan saw this and asked about the situation. I explained the cause. Duan quickly went to inspect and returned, saying, 'The pit is not too wide, and the repair is easy. How can we sit idly by and burden the travelers?' I replied, 'There is no one to sponsor the repair.' Duan asked about the cost, and I told him, '500 coins would be enough.' Duan said, 'The cost is limited, and I would like to contribute. Unfortunately, I am occupied with official duties and cannot do it myself.' I said, 'If you truly have this good heart, I am willing to take on the task to assist in this good deed.' Duan agreed, 'That’s fine.' I asked, 'How many days will it take you to complete your official duties?' Duan replied, 'I will return in about six or seven days.' I said, 'When you come back, you can inspect the work. I will definitely not delay, so as not to辜负your good intentions.'"

    547

    ’段喜，出钱而去。段去后，吾即觅石工修理。段回见之，大喜曰：‘如此坚固，无累行人，悉君之力也与！’吾曰：‘乐施在君，吾何力之与有？’既而，段自言曰：‘吾之得归，诚万分之幸。’吾问之，段曰：‘武库焚时，吾在武库盹睡，忽闻唱名声，一唱一应，如对册。唱及吾本名志成，应之者曰：勾除矣。其人问故，应名者曰：以其有重修齐河桥之阴功。吾梦寐间闻之大疑，转念吾本名无人知，或人与吾同姓名。即有人摇吾醒曰：官府立传。吾朦胧从之东行。其人在前，止违十数步。过北门，其人忽不见，心方惊疑，而武库焚。’吾曰：‘若然，君之得免横祸，即以修补桥顶石坑之事乎？盖虽所费无多，究属修补，既为修补，即可谓重修，其事虽微，其用心实巨。不然，何以得感神明，除君名于鬼册也？’段始惊悟曰：‘君言是也。’”庄人某归，知余方事著述，因特造斋细述之。

    Explanation

    547

    A Man Named Duan Gives Money to Repair a Bridge

    When Duan Xǐ donated money to repair the bridge, he left afterward. After Duan departed, I immediately found stone workers to repair the bridge. When Duan returned and saw the repaired bridge, he was greatly pleased and said, "It is so sturdy now, without burdening travelers. Is it all your effort?" I replied, "The joy of giving lies with you. What role did I play in it?" Later, Duan said, "My return home was truly a matter of great fortune." I asked him why, and he explained, "When the military warehouse burned, I was asleep in the warehouse. Suddenly, I heard a voice calling out names, and each call was answered, as if from a register. When my name, Zhìchéng, was called, the respondent said, 'Erased.' The person asked why, and the respondent replied, 'Because of the merit of repairing the bridge.' I was greatly puzzled in my dream and thought, perhaps someone else has the same name as me. Then someone shook me awake and said, 'The government is establishing a record.' I half-awake followed him eastward. The person was ahead of me, only a few steps behind. We passed the northern gate, and the person suddenly disappeared. I was just beginning to feel surprised when the military warehouse burned."

    I said, "If so, your escape from disaster was because of the act of repairing the top of the bridge's stone pit? Although it cost very little, it was still a repair. Since it was a repair, it can be called a major repair. Although the matter was small, the effort put into it was great. Otherwise, why would it move the gods and remove your name from the list of the dead?" Duan began to realize and said, "Your words are right." A man from Zhuang returned home, knowing that I was engaged in writing, and therefore specially came to my house to recount it in detail.

    548

    虚白道人曰：以木工之兄而笃于友弟，斯亦奇矣；以嗜赌之弟，适受大辱于兄，一闻兄有大难，遂释怒忘怨，急急赴救，为尤奇。以食粮之人，而诚于轻财，斯亦美矣；在行路之际，且有公事在身，偶见车陷于桥，辄解囊出钱，切切修理，尤为美。夫事至于奇而尤奇，美而尤美，则出乎寻常矣。事出寻常，则时在人耳目间，神不有以保护之，无以见造物福善之举。王富段成之得免横祸，不亦宜乎？

    Explanation

    548

    The recluse Xubai said: The elder brother, a woodworker, was devoted to his younger brother, which is already unusual. The younger brother, addicted to gambling, was greatly humiliated by his elder brother. Yet, upon hearing that his elder brother was in great danger, he immediately forgave his anger and forgot his resentment, hastening to rescue him, which is even more extraordinary. A person who is sincere in being generous with money is already admirable; while traveling, with official duties in hand, he happened to see a cart stuck in a bridge and immediately took out money, carefully repairing it, which is even more commendable. When events reach the level of being extraordinary and even more so, beautiful and even more so, they are beyond the ordinary. Such events, being extraordinary, are not common and are within the reach of people's ears and eyes. If God did not protect them, there would be no way to see the creator's blessing for good deeds. Wang Fu and Duan Cheng were spared from sudden disaster, is it not appropriate?

    549

    勿谓善小而不为，其斯之谓欤！ 平陵段以梅

    《益智录》（下） [清] 解 鉴 著
    作者:l4z5 发表时间: 2004/12/20 12:51 点击:256次 修改 精华 删除 置顶 来源 转移 收藏
    　　益智录卷之八

    　　顾 清 高

    　　（此缺一页半，约五百馀字）穴中，掘穴半尺即得之，然可勿与卜，盖卜则坏张六名。”顾曰：“止言物坠鼠穴，不露张六名。”翠可之。顾以翠言告车三。车三果于鼠穴得之。张复佣之，车不可。

    Explanation

    549

    Do not consider good deeds too small to be done; is this what it means! Pingling Duan Yi Mei

    The Yizhilu (Lower) [Qing Dynasty] by Xie Jian
    Author: l4z5 Posted on: December 20, 2004, 12:51 PM Clicks: 256 times Edit, Highlight, Delete, Pin, Source, Transfer, Save

    The Yizhilu Volume Eight

    Gu Qinggao

    (This is missing half a page, about 500+ characters) In a hole, digging half a foot deep one finds it. But it can be done without divination, because divination would ruin Zhang Liu's name. Gu said, "Only say that the object fell into the mouse hole, do not reveal Zhang Liu's name." Cui agreed. Gu told Che San what Cui said. Che San indeed found the object in the mouse hole. Zhang hired him again, but Che did not agree.

    550

    一老媪问子病，曰：“吾子勾成，年十七矣，忽得异症，今病笃，敢问尚有一线生路否？”顾曰：“来日听信。”顾语于翠。翠曰：“已登鬼册，不可为也。”顾告媪，媪恸哭，哀求细为推究。顾百辞劝解不去，不得已，曰：“吾妻知之，或兼能拯汝子，可亲身苦求之。”媪喜。见翠言所求，长跪不起。翠怨顾曰：“君多言，妾未掌生死簿，安能知人寿夭。”媪曰：“老身止此子，子死，吾亦不欲独生。祈大施法术，拯老身母子命。”言已，饮泣不止。顾从旁恿怂之。翠谓媪曰：“请起。仅有一术：子归，扬言子已死，门打提幡，使令郎卧灵床，覆纸被，严关门户，家人悉举哀。巳、午、未时过，子可寿至古稀。”硃书符三道，令媪糊大门、寝门及纸被上。

    Explanation

    550

    An old woman asked about her son's illness, saying, "My son, Guo Cheng, is seventeen years old. He suddenly contracted a strange disease, and now it is severe. May I ask if there is still a chance of survival?" The narrator replied, "I will inform you tomorrow." The narrator then told this to Cui. Cui said, "His name is already on the book of the dead; it cannot be done." The narrator informed the old woman, who wept bitterly and pleaded for more detailed information. The narrator tried to persuade her but she would not leave. Reluctantly, the narrator said, "My wife knows about this, and she might be able to save your son. You can personally seek her help." The old woman was pleased. She told Cui what she wanted and knelt down, refusing to get up. Cui blamed the narrator, saying, "You talk too much. I do not hold the book of life and death, how can I know whether someone will live or die?" The old woman said, "I have only this son. If he dies, I do not wish to live alone. Please use great magic to save my son and my own life." After speaking, she wept uncontrollably. The narrator encouraged her from the side. Cui told the old woman, "Please get up. I have only one method: you go home and spread the word that your son is dead. Hang a mourning banner at the door, let your son lie on the bier, cover him with a paper quilt, shut the doors and windows tightly, and have all the family members mourn. After the hours of巳,午, and未 pass, your son can live to be seventy years old." The narrator wrote three red talismans and instructed the old woman to paste them on the main door, bedroom door, and the paper quilt.

    551

    媪受符欲去，翠复曰：“令郎若愈，万勿来此致谢。”媪如翠言设施，成果愈。又一姣好少年平树德，问功名。顾卜之不吉，疑之，请次日为之细推。翠闻之，曰：“吉。妾命题令渠作文，君为笔削之，两月后岁试必入泮。受业门生之报，将高标门墙外矣。”顾言于平曰：“非见子文不能断。”平请命题。顾即以翠所出之题告之。平作文呈顾，顾细为删改。平见之大喜，遂师事顾，按课作文，顾亦每课尽心修饰。及院场，平录课文，取案元。由是卜名大著，自高声价，而握粟者仍接踵而来，且扣平入泮之故，愿从顾学者有徒。翠为苛择，仅得有友五人焉。及科试，五徒应试，皆取案元。盖县府院试及古场也，其一拨入府学，亦居第一名。于是朋来自远，从学日众。

    Explanation

    551

    The old woman received the talisman and was about to leave, but Cui said, "If your son recovers, do not come here to thank me." The old woman followed Cui's advice and indeed, the boy recovered. Another handsome young man named Ping Shude came to inquire about his prospects in officialdom. Gu divined it as inauspicious and doubted it, so he asked for a detailed analysis the next day. Cui heard this and said, "It is auspicious. I will propose a topic for him to write an essay, and you will revise it. Two months later, he will definitely pass the annual examination and enter the school. As a reward for teaching a student, your reputation will be highly esteemed outside your door." Gu told Ping, "Without seeing your essay, I cannot make a judgment." Ping requested a topic. Gu immediately told him the topic that Cui had proposed. Ping wrote the essay and presented it to Gu, who carefully revised it. Ping was very pleased with the result and began to follow Gu as his teacher, writing essays according to the schedule. Gu also carefully revised each essay. When the time for the provincial examination came, Ping wrote the essay and got the top rank. Thus, Gu's reputation grew, and he raised his standing. People who wanted to offer money still came one after another, and they also inquired about the reason for Ping's success, wishing to learn from Gu. Cui selected them strictly and only got five friends. When the time for the imperial examination came, the five students took the exam and all got the top rank. This included county, prefectural, and provincial examinations, as well as ancient tests. One of them was selected to attend the prefectural school and also ranked first. Therefore, friends came from afar, and the number of students increased daily.

    552

    顾遂止卜设帐，同人称美局焉。忽来一人，容饰彬雅。自言：“姓殷，居邻邑。有一女，年十七，三月前得迷症，卧不起。兹饮食渐减，病甚笃。闻先生善卜，故不远百里而来，敢烦占断。”顾曰：“仆不业卜已二载，且是谋非吾所能及也。”殷固求之。顾曰：“三日报命。”殷去。及晚，顾语于翠。次日系顾初度，翠饮过量，醉。顾自斋回，灯明于室，见一白狐卧榻上。忽出，立寝门外大言曰：“锡灯熔化矣！奈何不俟予先寝？”翠答于室曰：“妾醉，故合衣假寐，非寝也。”顾乃入，曰：“殷某之事如何？”翠曰：“殷女之魂，被王姓巫镇压秋千院苦水井中，欲殷求其医治而图其赀。殷弗求之，故无愈期。

    552

    Guo then set up a tent for divination, and others praised the situation. Suddenly, a person arrived, dressed politely and elegantly. He introduced himself, saying, "My surname is Yin, and I live in a neighboring town. I have a daughter, aged seventeen, who fell ill with a mysterious illness three months ago and cannot get up. Now her food intake is decreasing, and the illness is very serious. I heard that you are good at divination, so I came from far away, daring to trouble you to make a judgment." Guo replied, "I have not been practicing divination for two years, and this matter is beyond my ability." Yin insisted on it. Guo said, "I will report back in three days." Yin left. By evening, Guo told Cui about it. The next day was Guo's birthday, and Cui drank too much and got drunk. Guo returned from his study, the lamp was lit in the room, and he saw a white fox lying on the bed. Suddenly, the fox got up and stood outside the bedroom door, loudly saying, "The tin lamp has melted! Why didn't you wait for me to sleep first?" Cui answered from inside the room, "I was drunk, so I was just pretending to sleep, not actually sleeping." Guo then entered and asked, "How is the matter of Yin?" Cui replied, "Yin's daughter's soul is trapped in the well of the swing yard by a witch surnamed Wang. The witch wants Yin to seek her medical treatment to gain her money. Since Yin does not seek her treatment, there is no hope of recovery."

    553

    可用顷筐盛女衣入井，呼女名穿衣，连呼数声，提筐至女卧室，将衣覆女身，即愈。君止言遗魂于井，勿言被人镇压可也。”顾曰：“卿未卜先知，得勿仙乎？”翠曰：“妾之行藏，君已目睹，何故问？”顾曰：“未也。”翠曰：“门外大言之心，妾实感激不尽。”顾以翠言语殷，殷匆匆去。一日顾与翠把酒闲话，忽见殷率一妇与及笄女入。顾惊问之，殷指妇、女曰：“此吾妻与吾女也。小女得君术，病果愈。未愈之先，吾尝言有能医之者，嫁之。欲践前言，故送女到此，以充媵妾。”顾曰：“仆已有二妻，断不能如命。”心恐翠嗔，故词严厉色以辞之。翠笑曰：“意愿难瘦。设再有二三美人，量君亦乐于容纳。且是女丽而贤，君之家室嗣必赖其成就。”于是为理合卺事。及晚，翠谓殷曰：“良人之所好，妹知之乎？”殷笑而不言。翠曰：“移时妹即知之。”

    Explanation

    553

    A small basket can be used to carry the girl's clothes into the well, calling her name and dressing her, calling out several times, carrying the basket to the girl's bedroom, covering her body with the clothes, and she will be cured immediately. You only say that her soul is in the well, do not say that she was suppressed by someone. Gu said, "You can predict the future without divination, are you not a fairy?" Cui said, "My actions and whereabouts, you have already seen, why ask?" Gu said, "Not yet." Cui said, "The heart of making a big statement outside, I truly feel deeply grateful." Gu was afraid that Cui would be angry, so he used strict words and a stern expression to refuse. Cui smiled and said, "It is difficult to refuse one's wishes. If there were two or three more beautiful women, I believe you would also be happy to accommodate them. And this girl is beautiful and virtuous, your family and descendants will surely rely on her to succeed." So they arranged the marriage ceremony. By evening, Cui said to Yin, "What my husband likes, do you know?" Yin smiled but did not speak. Cui said, "After some time, you will know."

    554

    顾陡思故乡，欲归，而虞资斧不足，商于殷。殷曰：“尽足用矣。盖连年君所得银钱，另置一处，未少动。”顾愕然曰：“日用何来？”翠曰： “非君之所得，即妾之所出。他人尚能比亻次耶？”于是，将所蓄悉交于殷，曰：“除路费外，可密藏之。”殷愕然问故，翠曰：“其故不可预言。”顾治任归。至中途，遇山水阻去路，店居以俟水消。偶出游，见山下一兰若，墙垣倾圮而殿宇巍然。入视之，见神案上有石子大如拳，异之。殿隅有石数块，因举石投其中。壁间有以香煤画飞禽者，翅足悉备而无首，因用神前香煤代画之。

    Explanation

    554

    But he suddenly longed for his hometown and wanted to return, yet he worried that his funds were insufficient. He consulted Yin about this. Yin said, "There is more than enough to use. It is because year after year the silver and money you received were placed in another place, not moved at all." Con startled and said, "Where did the daily expenses come from?" Cui replied, "Not what you received, but what I provided. Can others still match this?" Therefore, he handed over all his savings to Yin and said, "Except for travel expenses, you can secretly store the rest." Yin was startled and asked the reason. Cui said, "The reason cannot be predicted." Con prepared his luggage and returned. When he reached halfway, he encountered mountains and rivers blocking the way forward. He stayed in an inn to wait for the water to recede. Occasionally, he went out for a walk and saw a small temple at the foot of the mountain. The walls were crumbling, but the halls and buildings were majestic. He entered and looked, seeing stones on the altar as large as fists, which he found strange. There were several stones in the corner of the hall. He therefore lifted a stone and threw it into it. On the wall, there were drawings of flying birds made with perfumed charcoal, all the wings and feet were complete, but they had no heads. He therefore used perfumed charcoal in front of the deity to draw them.

    555

    忽自外来一大汉，连顾左右，立顾身后视之。顾绘毕，大汉笑曰：“神案之石子，其君投于殿隅耶？”顾曰：“然。”其人大喜，曰：“有一事相商，祈君移玉从我去。”顾视其人，面目凶横，辞之。其人握顾腕强之，顾不得已从之。山径崎岖，行甚不易，约五、六里，至一幽谷石室中，有八、九人席地聚饮。大汉大言曰：“吾请得大王来矣。”众悉起，逊顾上坐。他一人曰：“吾等十人，作无本生意，悉粗率无谋，欲请文学之士，推作首领。庙中神案之石子，壁间之飞禽，皆假之为卜缘也，君悉应之，可知天缘有分矣。”顾知为伙贼，力辞之。

    Explanation

    555

    Suddenly, a large man appeared from outside, looking around repeatedly and standing to check behind him. After Con finished painting, the large man laughed and said, "Is it true that the king threw the stone from the altar into the corner of the hall?" Con replied, "Yes." The man was greatly pleased and said, "There is something I wish to discuss with you. Please come with me." Con looked at the man, who had a fierce and cruel appearance, and refused him. The man grasped Con's wrist and forced him to go. The mountain path was rugged, making the journey very difficult, and they traveled about five or six miles before arriving at a secluded valley with a stone chamber. Inside, eight or nine people were sitting on the ground, drinking together. The large man boasted, "I have brought the great king here!" Everyone stood up and invited Con to sit in the most honored place. Another person said, "We ten of us are engaged in risky business, all rough and lacking in strategy. We wish to appoint a literary man as our leader. The stones on the altar and the birds on the walls are all used for divination. You have responded to everything, showing that fate has a plan." Con realized they were bandits and strongly refused them.

    556

    一人怒曰：“来时由君，去时恐不由君。”言已，举刀欲杀顾。顾大惧。忽来一人曰：“且慢，顾先生吾契友也。先生系副贡，不解吾等营生，约之入伙，为累实多。”谓顾曰：“吾送先生归。”至中途，顾问：“何处与汝有旧？”其人曰：“吾名张六，昔车三翡翠玉玩实吾藏置鼠穴。先生知之而不言，迄今感念不置。”顾曰：“汝知务施报，亦豪杰也，奈何为此不法，自寻苦恼？”张六曰：“吾亦悔祸。君善卜，请即为吾卜之。”顾令张自言一时。张曰：“即此时。”顾曰：“时为戌初。”遂袖占之，惊曰：“大祸在即，可急赴旅店避之。

    Explanation

    556

    One person angrily said, "When you came, it was at your will; when you leave, it might not be at your will." After speaking, he raised his knife to kill Gu. Gu was greatly afraid. Suddenly, a person came and said, "Wait a moment, Mr. Gu is my sworn friend. You are a junior scholar-official, and you do not understand our livelihood. Inviting you to join us would be a real burden." He told Gu, "I will escort you back." On the way, Gu asked, "Where did you have old acquaintance with me?" The person replied, "My name is Zhang Liu. The three jade carvings of emerald green were actually hidden by me in a rat's hole. You knew about it but did not speak up, and I still feel grateful for it." Gu said, "You know the principle of repaying kindness, you are also a hero. Why do you engage in such illegal activities and cause yourself trouble?" Zhang Liu said, "I also regret the consequences. You are good at divination, please divine for me immediately." Gu asked Zhang to say the time. Zhang said, "It is this moment." Gu said, "The time is戌初 (戌初 is a specific time in the Chinese time system)." He then divined by holding something in his sleeve and said, "A great disaster is imminent. You should hurry to an inn to avoid it."

    557

    ”甫至店，时明月莹澈，见一武官率百馀兵丁衔枚驰过。顾曰：“祸事即在此。”张欲尾之，顾阻之曰：“渠既由此而往，必由此而去。待之可也。”移时，官获数盗而来。自门隙视之，悉张六党类。张曰：“设今吾不拯君，吾亦不能脱此大难。其中确有报应也。”言已而去。顾向二妻述遇难脱难之故，尤氏曰：“妾知之。若无张六，妾亦设法拯救。但怒贼举刀时，深恐张六来少迟耳。自富民起程时，妾即欲别君，所以迟延至今者，亦为君有此大难。难星已过，请从此永别。”言已而杳。殷氏惊曰：“尤姊何往？殆非人乎？”顾历言之，殷始知尤氏为狐。

    Explanation

    557

    As soon as Zhang arrived at the inn, the bright moon shone clear and bright. He saw a military officer leading more than a hundred soldiers galloping past with their mouths closed. Turning around, the officer said, "The disaster is here." Zhang wanted to follow him, but Zhang turned around and stopped him, saying, "Since he went from here, he must leave from here. Waiting for him is possible." After some time, the official came back with several captured thieves. Looking at them through the crack of the door, Zhang saw all of Zhang Liu's party members. Zhang said, "If I did not rescue you, I also could not escape this great difficulty. There is certainly retribution in it." He finished speaking and left. Zhang turned to his two wives and described the reason for encountering and escaping the disaster. You Yu said, "I know it. If not for Zhang Liu, I would also find a way to rescue you. But when the angry thieves raised their knives, I was deeply afraid that Zhang Liu would come a little late. Since the wealthy man set out, I immediately wanted to part from you. The reason why I delayed until now is also because you have this great difficulty. The star of difficulty has passed, please farewell forever from now on." He finished speaking and disappeared. Yin Shi was surprised and said, "Where did Sister You go? Is it probably not a human?" Zhang turned to and detailed it, and Yin Shi finally knew that You Yu was a fox.

    558

    顾至家，殷见顾嫡妻山氏。山不礼，殷厌其骄傲，因各居；山亦听之。未几，顾没。山无资，不得已商于殷。殷出资理殡事。山德之，欲与同居，殷不可。山继没，殷复出资殡之。山子名命，虽已成丁，未有室。山治家严谨，山没，命无忌惮，渐肆饮赌，产业罄尽，就食于殷，殷厚恤之。命窃鬻殷物作饮赌费，殷叱之，命不服。将鞭之，命曰：“鞭母生之子可，吾非母所宜鞭也。”殷大怒，摈命门外不令入。命外游数日，食饮无门，不得已，诣殷请罪。殷不受，命惭而去，乞丐为生。冬着秋衣，怜寒无人，苦不堪言。

    Explanation

    558

    Upon arriving at the home, Yin saw Gu's principal wife Shan. Shan was not respectful, and Yin was dissatisfied with her pride, so they lived separately; Shan also agreed to this. Not long after, Gu died. Shan had no resources and was forced to negotiate with Yin. Yin provided funds to handle the funeral arrangements. Shan was grateful to Yin and wanted to live with him, but Yin refused. Subsequently, Shan passed away, and Yin again provided funds for her burial. Shan's son was named Ming. Although he was an adult, he still did not have a wife. Shan managed the household strictly. After Shan's death, Ming became reckless, indulged in drinking and gambling, and squandered all their property. He came to rely on Yin for food, and Yin generously supported him. Ming secretly sold Yin's belongings to fund his drinking and gambling. Yin scolded him, but Ming did not comply. Yin was about to whip him, but Ming said, "You can whip my mother's son, but I am not the one you should whip." Yin was greatly angered and expelled Ming from the house, not allowing him to enter. Ming wandered outside for several days, unable to find food or drink, and was forced to go to Yin to seek forgiveness. Yin did not accept his apology, and Ming, feeling ashamed, left and began to beg for a living. In winter, he wore autumn clothes, and despite his suffering, no one cared.

    559

    一日乞钱货肆，其财主郝翁谓命曰：“汝苦寒如是，盍回家祈怜于令堂？”命曰：“母心狠，祈之无益，且母不如媪仆。盖吾每至饥饿难堪时回家，仆媪俟母寝，另为吾炊。食后，将食馀令吾筐携之，且凑给钱文二、三百。”郝翁哂之，曰：“今吾有友设施棉衣，汝明日来，代为求之。”命喜甚。次日早至，郝为求裳衣二件。命大喜，将执之而去。郝曰：“且慢，施主恐汝仍衣身衣，货此饮赌，须将身衣脱下。”命易衣而去。至春季天暖，命货棉衣夹，夏鬻表衣里；至冬仍号寒行市墟，郝仍为之易棉衣。命之德郝不啻再造矣。命之从事乞丐也，每遇大雪淫雨，不能干人，辄有賙以钱文或干糇者。命以为时运之通，而不知其中之有故也。

    Explanation

    559

    One day, the protagonist was begging for money in a shop. The shop owner, Mr. Hao, said to him, "You are so cold, why don't you go home and beg for pity from your mother?" The protagonist replied, "My mother's heart is cruel; begging from her is of no use, and she is not as kind as the old maidservant. Whenever I am hungry and go home, the servants wait until my mother sleeps and cook something special for me. After I eat, they give me the leftover food to take home and sometimes give me some extra money." Mr. Hao laughed at him and said, "Now I have a friend who is distributing cotton clothes. You come tomorrow, and I will ask for two for you." The protagonist was very happy. The next morning, he arrived early, and Mr. Hao asked for two pieces of clothing for him. The protagonist was delighted and was about to leave with the clothes. Mr. Hao said, "Wait a moment. The benefactor is afraid that you will still wear your own clothes and sell them for drinking and gambling, so you must take off your own clothes." The protagonist changed into the new clothes and left. The protagonist's gratitude to Mr. Hao was no less than being reborn. As a beggar, the protagonist often could not beg from people during heavy snow and rain, but there were often people who gave him money or dried food. The protagonist thought it was due to his good fortune, but he did not know the real reason behind it.

    560

    一日，佣送行客赴他邑，归，于路忽腹痛难支，急走。忽见一第，一少妇立门外，意欲急前哀其烦人医治，尚违数武，倒地不能言，而心甚明了。少妇急遣人扶命入客舍，卧榻上。少妇自言曰：“此等暴病，立能毙人，再迟片刻，即不治。”急以药丸纳命口，以清水送下。少时，痛顿止，口亦能言，但四肢无力不能起。少妇曰：“汝顾命耶？”命曰：“然。”曰：“穷困如是，酒赌宜戒也。如能戒之，吾语汝以生全之门。”命曰：“矢从此戒之。”少妇曰：“汝归，至邑东门内某店，问有客名勾成者，自道姓名，渠必厚恤也。”言已，执灯掩扉去。

    Explanation

    560

    One day, a hired worker was escorting a departing guest to another county. On his return, he suddenly fell severely ill with abdominal pain on the road and could hardly bear it, so he ran hastily. Suddenly, he saw a mansion, and a young woman stood at the gate. He intended to go forward quickly and beg her to trouble herself to treat him, but he was still several steps away when he fell to the ground unable to speak, though his mind was very clear. The young woman quickly sent someone to carry him into the guesthouse and lay him on the bed. She said to him, "Such sudden illnesses can kill a person instantly. If delayed even a moment, it would be incurable." She quickly gave him a pill to put in his mouth and had him swallow it with water. After a short while, the pain suddenly stopped, and he could speak again, but his limbs were weak and he could not stand up. The young woman asked, "Do you want to live?" He replied, "Yes." She said, "In such poverty, gambling and drinking should be avoided. If you can abstain, I will tell you the way to preserve your life." He said, "I vow to abstain from now on." The young woman said, "When you return, go to a certain shop inside the eastern gate of the county, ask for a guest named Gou Cheng, introduce yourself by name, and he will certainly show great kindness." After speaking, she took the lamp and closed the door and left.

    561

    命心计曰：“宜询妇姓氏，容日报效。”转念：“渠既知吾姓名，必与有亲谊，明晨细询未晚也”。未几睡去。及醒，闻松涛盈耳，开眸审视，身卧松林中，大惊。时东方已晞，见衾裯维新，上覆新衣，并袜履悉备，而所着敝衣俱失所在。因着衣而起，枕边有钱二千。病后步履维艰，遂觅代步归。如少妇言，诣店访问，果有勾成。既相见，勾问顾命先君名字，命语之。勾曰：“是吾恩人也。”问命际遇，命直言无隐。勾曰：“弟贸贩他省货物，颇有利息。但吾一人太孤，兄愿伴弟为之否？”命曰：“愿。”勾甚喜。二人同心协力，生意兴隆。甫二年，勾以母年迈，不欲远游，遂以馀剩之物赠命，携本资而去。

    Explanation

    561

    The heart of the matter thought and said, "It is appropriate to inquire about the woman's surname; I will report back tomorrow." Then he reconsidered, "Since he knows my name, he must have some familial relationship with me; it is not too late to ask in detail tomorrow morning." Soon after, he fell asleep. When he woke up, he heard the sound of pine trees rustling in the wind, and he opened his eyes and looked around carefully. He found himself lying in a pine forest, greatly surprised. The east was already brightening, and he saw that the bedding was new, with new clothes on top, and shoes and socks were all prepared, but the old clothes he was wearing had disappeared. He put on the new clothes and got up, finding two thousand coins beside his pillow. After being ill, walking was difficult, so he sought a means of transportation to return home. As the young woman had said, he went to the store to inquire, and indeed, there was a person named Guo Cheng. After meeting, Guo asked about the name of the person who had been entrusted with the responsibility, and Ming told him. Guo said, "He is my benefactor." Guo then asked about Ming's circumstances, and Ming spoke openly without hiding anything. Guo said, "I trade goods from other provinces and make quite a profit. But I am alone and feel isolated; would you be willing to accompany me?" Ming said, "Willing." Guo was very pleased. The two worked together harmoniously, and the business flourished. After two years, Guo, whose mother was old, did not want to travel far, so he gave Ming the remaining goods and took his capital with him.

    562

    命欲继勾生意而无资，因向郝翁道其意。郝曰：“是不难，吾出本资，获利与吾平分。”命曰：“此不待言。”郝曰：“若然，五日后再议。”嗣郝果出资给命，听其贸易。命福至运转，获利较勾加倍。岁终，携利面郝清算。郝曰：“与令堂算之可也。盖本资实令堂所出。”命闻之愕然。郝曰：“不但此，子连年所衣之棉衣，悉令堂亲身送到，且屡问子之棉衣尚衣之否。吾曰‘衣之’则喜，吾言‘不知’则忧，必急使人探访。令堂为子操心洵苦矣！”命惭愧交集，身似无容，急至家见母，伏地请责。母拽起之曰：“汝无罪，惟所好不可耳。今改矣，可既往不咎。”命见母面带笑容，而目中有泪，问之。母曰：“吾见汝喜出望外，但忆汝历受百苦，为之伤心耳。”命曰：“儿虽乞丐为生，未受饥饿。”母曰：“吾知之。

    Explanation

    562

    Wang wanted to continue his business but lacked funds, so he went to Mr. Hao to express his intention. Mr. Hao said, "It's not difficult; I will provide the capital, and we will split the profits equally." Wang replied, "This is a given." Mr. Hao said, "If so, let's discuss it again in five days." Subsequently, Mr. Hao indeed provided the capital to Wang, allowing him to engage in trade. Wang's luck turned around, and his profit was double what Gou made. At the end of the year, Wang brought the profits to Mr. Hao for settlement. Mr. Hao said, "You can settle the account with your mother. Actually, the capital was provided by your mother." Wang was astonished upon hearing this. Mr. Hao said, "Not only that, but the cotton clothes you have worn for years were all personally delivered by your mother. She also frequently asked if you were still wearing those cotton clothes. When I said 'you are still wearing them,' she was happy; when I said 'I don't know,' she was worried and would immediately send someone to check. Your mother has indeed been very troubled for you!" Wang felt a mix of shame and embarrassment, feeling as if he had no place to stand. He hurried home to see his mother and fell to the ground, asking for punishment. His mother pulled him up and said, "You are not at fault; it's just that your preferences were not right. Now you have changed, so I will not hold past mistakes against you." Wang saw his mother with a smile on her face but tears in her eyes, and he asked her about it. His mother said, "I am delighted to see you, but I recall all the hardships you have endured, which makes me sad." Wang said, "Although I have lived as a beggar, I have not suffered from hunger." His mother said, "I know that."

    563

    然仆媪之为汝再炊，实属吾意；汝意外所得之钱文、干糇，多吾烦人给之也。”命闻之，情触心怀，泪珠沾巾。母与之俱哭，曰：“汝勿哭。吾为汝认郝翁为义父，自作寒衣，烦渠转给。汝嗜赌好饮，恐当典不能赎，故当日必令易衣而去。门后墙壁所挂污衣，即汝行乞时之衣。所以必置污衣于面前者，见衣如见汝，少慰吾心也。”命视之曰：“母用心如此，儿一毫不知，负母苦心矣。”家人劝之曰：“喜事临门，不宜过伤。”母收涕谓命曰：“汝今春得资贸易时，知汝改过，当即为汝定亲，择于后甲嫁娶。汝今不来，明晨必使人呼汝矣。”既过门，命赴岳家宴。母意其必醉，乃清醒而归，喜曰：“汝自何日戒酒，竟如是之清也？”命细述送客他邑，归病，少妇救拯之事。母曰：“少妇即汝狐母尤氏也。”亦为细述尤之始终。

    Explanation

    563

    Indeed, it was my intention for the servant and the elderly woman to cook for you again; the money and dry food you unexpectedly received, I asked someone to give it to you." Upon hearing this, Ming's emotions touched his heart, and tears wet his handkerchief. The mother cried with him, saying, "Do not cry. I will recognize Old Man Hao as your adoptive father, and I will make warm clothes for you, asking him to deliver them. You enjoy gambling and drinking, and I fear that if you pawn things, you might not be able to redeem them. Therefore, I insisted that you change clothes before leaving. The dirty clothes hanging on the wall behind the door are the clothes you wore when you were begging. I placed the dirty clothes in front of me so that seeing the clothes is like seeing you, which slightly comforts my heart." Ming looked at the clothes and said, "Mother, your care is so thorough, yet I was completely unaware, and I have辜负了您的苦心." The family members advised him, "There is a joyful event approaching, and it is not appropriate to be overly sad." The mother wiped her tears and told Ming, "When you obtained capital for trade this spring and I knew you had reformed, I immediately arranged for your marriage, choosing a date in the following year. Since you are not here now, I will send someone to call you tomorrow morning." After the wedding, Ming went to the banquet at his father-in-law's house. The mother thought he would surely be drunk, but he returned clear-headed. He happily said, "From what day did you stop drinking? You are so clear-headed!" Ming then detailed how he sent guests to another town, fell ill on the way back, and was saved by a young woman. The mother said, "The young woman is your fox mother, You." She also detailed the entire story of You.

    564

    虚白道人曰：隐恶，美德也。人能慎之，获福自不可量。顾某之遇张六、脱群贼之大难，得殷氏、保冢子之不肖，皆以此致之。以是知言人之不善之必不免于后患矣。

    　　郭璞善卜而不免王敦之祸，逊顾生远矣。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　江 在 新

    　　汉章戴公，湖北人。以举人大挑一等，分发四川。公货产携眷赴川候补。年馀，始得授资州仁寿知县事，甫一月而卒。未卒之先，自知病不能愈，深为家人虞。欲令归，而家无产业，且无资斧；欲令家仁寿，而衣食无着。遂自叹曰：“我死不瞑目矣！”忽忆所闻绵州在新江孝廉广交友，轻财好施，深恨与渠无素。不得已，暗修书缄，封固授妻子，戒勿启，曰：“吾与江在新有莫逆交，执缄往投，必不冻馁汝母子也。”公卒，无葬地，因停柩古寺。

    Explanation

    564

    虚白道人曰：隐恶，美德也。人能慎之，获福自不可量。顾某之遇张六、脱群贼之大难，得殷氏、保冢子之不肖，皆以此致之。以是知言人之不善之必不免于后患矣。

    　　郭璞善卜而不免王敦之祸，逊顾生远矣。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　江 在 新

    　　汉章戴公，湖北人。以举人大挑一等，分发四川。公货产携眷赴川候补。年馀，始得授资州仁寿知县事，甫一月而卒。未卒之先，自知病不能愈，深为家人虞。欲令归，而家无产业，且无资斧；欲令家仁寿，而衣食无着。遂自叹曰：“我死不瞑目矣！”忽忆所闻绵州在新江孝廉广交友，轻财好施，深恨与渠无素。不得已，暗修书缄，封固授妻子，戒勿启，曰：“吾与江在新有莫逆交，执缄往投，必不冻馁汝母子也。”公卒，无葬地，因停柩古寺。

    The Virtuous Man of Empty White said: Concealing evil is a virtue. If one can be cautious in this regard, the blessings one receives will be immeasurable. Consider my own encounters: meeting Zhang Liu and escaping the great peril of the bandits, and securing the unworthy heir of the tomb through the kindness of Yin's family—both were achieved through this principle. From this, I know that speaking ill of others will inevitably lead to future troubles.

    Guo Pu, though skilled in divination, could not escape the calamity brought by Wang Dun; Xu Gu was far wiser than he. — Annotated by Li Yu of Shangyuan.

    Jiang Zai Xin

    Dai Gong of the Han Dynasty, a native of Hubei, was appointed through the imperial examination system as a first-class scholar and assigned to serve in Sichuan. He sold his property and took his family to Sichuan to await an official position. After more than a year, he was finally appointed as the magistrate of Renzhou in Zizhou, but died just one month after taking office. Before his death, he knew his illness could not be cured and deeply worried about his family. He wanted to send them back, but his family had no property and no funds. He also considered leaving them in Renzhou, but they had no means of supporting themselves. Then he sighed to himself, saying, "I will not rest in peace after my death!" Suddenly, he remembered hearing that Jiang Zai Xin of Mianzhou was a scholar of high moral character, generous with his wealth, and widely respected. He deeply regretted not having known him before. Unable to do otherwise, he secretly wrote a letter, sealed it, and gave it to his wife and children, instructing them not to open it. He said, "I have a close friendship with Jiang Zai Xin; if you take this letter to him, he will surely not let you starve or freeze." After his death, he had no place to bury him, so his coffin was left in an old temple.

    565

    公子名濋，年十五岁，与母沈孺人并姊治任往投江公。至，濋向阍人自道历履，细述父言，并将父手缄交之，令渠代投。阍人禀江公。公自维与戴汉章素不识面，见书缄外面书某字：“江仁兄玉披”，大疑。急拆其缄视之，内仅书“戴汉章顿首百拜”，其他无一字，不胜惊讶。会意曰：“戴公与吾本无素，无言可书，故止书拜名，令吾猜哑谜也。”急令妻迎沈氏于内庭款待，请戴濋入客舍。公见濋姿质丰昌，举止淑慎，暗喜曰：“不愧为宦门之后。”伪曰：“仆与令先君在京师结拜时，量贤侄不过五六岁，今已成人矣。

    Explanation

    565

    The young master named Chu, aged fifteen, along with his mother, Lady Shen, and his sister, prepared the journey to visit Mr. Jiang. Upon arrival, Chu introduced himself to the gatekeeper, detailing his life experiences, and then narrated his father's words, handing over his father's letter to be delivered by the gatekeeper. The gatekeeper reported to Mr. Jiang. Mr. Jiang thought to himself that he had never met Dai Hanzhang, and upon seeing the letter with the words 'Dear Brother Jiang' written on it, he was greatly puzzled. He hurriedly opened the envelope and looked inside, finding only the words 'Dai Hanzhang bows deeply and repeatedly,' with no other words, which greatly surprised him. Understanding the meaning, he said, "Dai Gong and I have never been acquainted; there was nothing to write, so he only wrote his name in a bow, making me guess a riddle." He quickly ordered his wife to welcome Lady Shen to the inner courtyard for hospitality and invited Dai Chu to the guesthouse. Mr. Jiang saw that Chu had a full and healthy appearance, graceful and cautious manners, and secretly rejoiced, saying, "He truly does not disgrace his noble lineage." He said, "When I and your late father formed a sworn brotherhood in the capital, I estimated your esteemed nephew to be no more than five or six years old; now he has grown up."

    566

    穆卜之期早逝乎？”濋起对曰：“未也。因无吉地，暂停枢寺院内。”公遂请善风鉴者为之择地。地既得，命家人同濋如仁寿，移柩葬绵州。为之耗银钱，其事小；为之毕大事，其德巨也。戴公之候补省会也，称贷钱行银若干两。债主闻濋移柩葬绵，从之来，坐索不去。濋百辞恳求宽限，债主不应。其项江公亦一时不能出，遂向债主曰：“分三次，吾代还之，以数月为限，可否？”债主曰：“君果欲代还，一年亦可。须将借券改立君名下。”公亲笔立券，债主始去。公按期如数归还，馀项为之无存。濋母子心实有不安焉。

    Explanation

    566

    When the time for the divination came too soon, 濱 responded, "Not yet. Because there is no suitable burial ground, the coffin is temporarily resting in the temple hall." The official then asked a skilled geomancer to choose a place. Once the site was selected, he ordered his family to accompany 濱 to Renshou and move the coffin for burial in Mianzhou. The expenditure of silver and money for this matter was minor; but in completing this significant task, the virtue was great. When 戴公 was waiting to take up his post in the provincial capital, he borrowed several taels of silver from a moneylender. The creditor heard that 濱 had moved the coffin for burial in Mianzhou and came along, sitting and demanding payment without leaving. 濱 pleaded with many excuses for an extension, but the creditor did not agree. The matter involving Jiang Gong could not be resolved at once, so he said to the creditor, "In three installments, I will repay it, with a limit of several months, is that acceptable?" The creditor replied, "If you truly wish to repay, one year is also acceptable. The loan document must be changed to your name." The official wrote the document himself, and the creditor finally left. He repaid the debt on schedule and in full, and the remaining amount was spent. 濱 and his mother were truly uneasy.

    567

    濋姊及笄。适有武信骑尉谷某，少年丧妻。谷年仅长濋姊三、四岁。谷遣媒求亲，公恿怂濋母应之。过门止在数月内，而公女亦与之同月嫁娶。公所赠濋姊之嫁妆，与己女无少异。濋之读也，公亲教之。十八入泮，有乡绅某爱其才，愿与结姻，烦公为媒。公商濋母而代委禽焉，继为之完婚。濋母念葬夫及子娶女嫁悉江公一人之力，濋母子之德公，不啻海岱矣。濋忽生懈志，公百辞劝勉，置若罔闻，惟言及“戴公如在，必不任子优游而不加训诲”，濋闻之蹙然动容，伏案勤读。数日后，怠荒如故。

    Explanation

    567

    As the main character's sister reached the age of marriageable age, a young man named Gu, a minor military official, who had recently lost his wife, proposed marriage. Gu was only three or four years older than the main character's sister. He sent a matchmaker to ask for her hand, and the father encouraged and persuaded the mother to agree. The wedding took place within a few months, and the father's daughter also married in the same month. The gifts given to the main character's sister were not much different from those given to the father's daughter. The father personally taught the main character. At the age of 18, he passed the county-level examination. A local gentry person admired his talent and wished to marry him to his daughter, asking the father to act as a matchmaker. The father consulted the main character's mother and arranged the marriage. The main character's mother remembered that everything, including burying her husband and arranging the marriages of her children, was done by Jiang Gong alone. The mother and son were immensely grateful to Jiang Gong. Suddenly, the main character developed a lazy attitude. The father tried to persuade him with many words, but the main character ignored them as if he had not heard them. He only remembered his father's words: "If your father were still alive, he would not allow you to be idle and untrained." The main character became serious and moved by the words, diligently studying at his desk. After a few days, he returned to his old habit of laziness.

    568

    屡试皆然。公以知濋有孝思，遂托言谓濋曰：“连日梦令先君到此，视子功课。可将令尊神主请出，供书案上。”濋如命。公每日拈香曰：“公子不成名，皆弟不善教之罪也。”暗窥濋，功用倍往日，月馀不懈，且有对神主而泣之时。公暗喜。后濋请代公上香，公从之。濋自是日迈月征，逊志时敏，不待教而勤苦自矢。三年领乡荐。公率濋赴京会试，路受辛苦，必令早眠。野多风霜，不嫌起迟。送场必俟濋入而始返，接场则濋未出而先到。公视濋犹子，濋亦视公犹父也。濋得赐进士，公之心以慰，公之责亦以尽矣。

    Explanation

    568

    Every time it was tested, the result was the same. Knowing that Chu had filial thoughts, the public pretended to say to Chu, "For several days, I have been dreaming that your late father came here to see how you are doing with your studies. You can bring out your father's spirit tablet and place it on the writing desk." Chu followed the instructions. The public offered incense daily and said, "If the young master does not achieve fame, it is all my fault for not teaching well." Secretly observing Chu, the public noticed that his efforts doubled compared to before, for more than a month without slackening, and there were times when he cried in front of the spirit tablet. The public was secretly delighted. Later, Chu asked to light incense for the public, and the public agreed. From then on, Chu made daily progress and monthly achievements, being humble in heart and quick in action, and without being taught, he voluntarily worked hard. He passed the local examination after three years. The public led Chu to the capital for the metropolitan examination, suffering hardships on the road, making sure to go to bed early, and not minding waking up late due to the many winds and frosts in the countryside. When sending him to the examination hall, the public would wait until Chu entered before returning, and when picking him up, the public would arrive before Chu left. The public treated Chu like a son, and Chu also regarded the public like a father. Chu was granted the title of Jinshi, and the public's heart was comforted; the public's responsibility was also fulfilled.

    569

    时濋姊丈谷某官武翼都尉，闻濋归，享仪致贺。宴饮间，盛称江公曰：“处友之道如公者，可谓情之极，义之尽矣。不知晚生岳父，谁昔之奉书，如何恳切也。”公笑曰：“令内弟业已成名，事可明言。”遂出一书示谷曰：“此令岳丈所修之缄也。”濋与谷并肩而视，见缄内止书拜名，他无一字，大疑。公向濋曰：“令尊与仆实无旧，不便托妻寄子，故以空函寄仆，令仆会意为之也。”濋闻之，急整衣冠，伏地叩谢。起曰：“向者仅知公之义，今始知公恩义兼尽也。”谷曰：“此恩不可以一谢而遂已也。”濋然之，遂面拜公为义父。后出仕，屡奉银物为公寿。公终，濋服饰哭泣如子焉。

    Explanation

    569

    At that time, Cú's elder brother-in-law, Mr. Gu, who held the position of Wuyi Duiwei, heard of Cú's return and offered a feast to congratulate him. During the banquet, Mr. Gu highly praised Jiang Gong, saying, "The way of dealing with friends like you, can be called the utmost in emotion and righteousness. I do not know, my late father-in-law, how earnestly he wrote the letter and what it said." Jiang Gong smiled and said, "Your younger brother has already gained fame, so the matter can be openly stated." He then took out a letter to show Mr. Gu and said, "This is the letter written by your father-in-law." Cú and Mr. Gu looked at it together and saw that the letter contained only a respectful name, with no other words, which greatly puzzled them. Jiang Gong turned to Cú and said, "Your father and I had no prior acquaintance, so it was inconvenient to entrust my daughter and son to you. Therefore, I sent an empty letter to you, asking you to understand the meaning and act accordingly." Upon hearing this, Cú hurriedly dressed and bowed to the ground to express his thanks. He then said, "Before, I only knew of your righteousness; now I realize your kindness and righteousness are fully realized." Mr. Gu said, "This kindness cannot be repaid with just one expression of thanks." Cú agreed and formally adopted Jiang Gong as his father. After Cú entered official service, he frequently sent silver and gifts to celebrate Jiang Gong's birthday. When Jiang Gong passed away, Cú dressed in mourning and cried like a son.

    570

    虚白道人曰：今之托妻子于友，而受冻馁者多矣。即不尽然，而贫友来投，避而不面者有之；视朋友之妻子如路人者有之；面受友人之托，及友死而反之者有之。类此者，其人不足责，而友之之人真为无目矣。江公者，竟以无素之人，伪曰契友，而即以契友视其妻子。公盖以为：人既谓吾为友，必吾可以为友。吾不尽友之道，非欺友也，实自欺也。如是之友，不惟百中无一，千中无一，直万中无一矣！

    　　此与《古今奇观》中刘仁普事仿佛。 上元李瑜谨注

    　　美 人 图

    　　秋子丰，楚人，善画。一日画一美人，方毕，幼子成目注之。丰戏之曰：“汝长大，即令作汝妇。”丰裱之，挂诸寝室，每食，谓成曰：“饿坏汝媳妇矣。”成即盛食供之。及长，知父戏己，而珍之异他物。嗣悬之床头，不时瞻玩，即从塾师读，亦必携之。

    Explanation

    570

    虚白道人曰：今之托妻子于友，而受冻馁者多矣。即不尽然，而贫友来投，避而不面者有之；视朋友之妻子如路人者有之；面受友人之托，及友死而反之者有之。类此者，其人不足责，而友之之人真为无目矣。江公者，竟以无素之人，伪曰契友，而即以契友视其妻子。公盖以为：人既谓吾为友，必吾可以为友。吾不尽友之道，非欺友也，实自欺也。如是之友，不惟百中无一，千中无一，直万中无一矣！

    Translation: The Master of Void White said: "Today, many entrust their wives and children to friends and suffer from cold and hunger as a result. Even if not entirely so, there are those who avoid meeting poor friends when they come seeking help; there are those who treat their friends' wives and children as strangers; and there are those who accept a friend's trust face-to-face but return it upon the friend's death. Such people are not worth blaming, but the friend is truly blind. Mr. Jiang, treating someone with no prior connection as a sworn friend, and regarding their wives and children as such, believes that since people call him a friend, he must be able to be a friend. He does not fulfill the duties of friendship, not because he deceives his friend, but because he deceives himself. A friend like this is not only one in a hundred, one in a thousand, but one in ten thousand!"

    此与《古今奇观》中刘仁普事仿佛。 上元李瑜谨注

    Translation: This is similar to the story of Liu Renpu in The Strange Tales of Ancient and Modern Times. Dated Shangyuan, Li Yu respectfully annotated.

    美 人 图

    Translation: The Portrait of a Beauty

    秋子丰，楚人，善画。一日画一美人，方毕，幼子成目注之。丰戏之曰：“汝长大，即令作汝妇。”丰裱之，挂诸寝室，每食，谓成曰：“饿坏汝媳妇矣。”成即盛食供之。及长，知父戏己，而珍之异他物。嗣悬之床头，不时瞻玩，即从塾师读，亦必携之。

    Translation: Qiu Zifeng, a man from Chu, was skilled in painting. One day, he painted a beautiful woman, and when he finished, his young son Cheng gazed at it intently. Zifeng teased him, saying, "When you grow up, I will make you marry this woman." He then framed the painting and hung it in his bedroom. Every time he ate, he would say to Cheng, "Don't let your future wife starve." Cheng would then bring out plenty of food for her. When Cheng grew older, he realized his father was joking, but he cherished the painting more than any other object. Later, he hung it by his bedside, often admiring it, and even carried it with him when he studied under a private tutor.

    571

    一年，师塾违家少远，日惟朝、午家食，晚不归。因午携干糇，以备晚飧。一夕取食，则无矣。次夕复然，大疑。以为独寝一室，门时外锁，窃食无人，因穴窗屡窥之。忽见一美人执食物而食，审谛之，画图中人也。急启户入视，美人已杳，而美人图仍挂壁间，犹疑梦想眼花。嗣连日食物不少动。越数日，所食又失其所在，遂虚掩室门以袭之。日暮，师与砚友俱归，潜至居室，自窗窃窥。见女方离画图而下，甫及地，成推门骤入，掺女祛曰：“窃食之人，今始得之。”女惊曰：“君吓死妾矣！请释妾。妾虽有罪，断不畏罪而逃。”成释之。回视画图如故，曰：“适见卿从画图下，何以画图美人仍在耶？”女曰：“妾乃画之精灵。

    Explanation

    571

    After one year, the protagonist lived away from home for a short distance, eating only breakfast and lunch at home but not returning for dinner. He would bring dry food for dinner. One evening, when he took the food, it was gone. The next evening, the same thing happened, and he became greatly puzzled. Believing that no one else was in the room, as the door was locked from the outside, and thinking no one could steal the food, he began to peer through the window repeatedly. Suddenly, he saw a beautiful woman holding food and eating. Upon closer inspection, he realized it was the person in the painting. He quickly opened the door to look inside, but the beautiful woman had disappeared, and the painting remained hanging on the wall. He still suspected it was a dream or an illusion.

    For several days, the food remained untouched. After a few more days, the food was missing again. He decided to leave the room door slightly open to catch her. As evening approached, his teacher and study companions returned, and he crept to the room, peering in through the window secretly. He saw the woman just descending from the painting, and as she reached the ground, he pushed the door open suddenly, grabbing her arm and saying, "The thief of food, I have finally caught you." The woman was startled and said, "You almost scared me to death! Please let me go. I may be guilty, but I will never flee out of fear of punishment." He released her.
