The case of Master He, who received the help of a fox, transformed his fierce wife into a virtuous partner, was surely not a matter of chance.

    　　Volume Ten of the Enlightening Records

    　　Geng Rumei

    　　Geng Rumei, a military scholar, lived in the area south of Mount Song. His younger brother, Ruguai, was born to their stepmother. When Ruguai was one year old, their stepmother passed away, and a hired woman took over nursing him. At night, fearing that the wet nurse might not care for the infant properly, the stepmother carried him to sleep with her. Since her wife Ding also had a son, they could not raise both children together. As Ruguai grew older, they hired a teacher to educate him, and he followed the lessons. However, by the time he was fifteen or sixteen, he suddenly stopped studying and began to play with ignorant children. Rumei tried to encourage and guide him, but Ruguai seemed to pay no heed. Later, he gradually started gambling with disreputable people and would often be away for several days without returning home or eating at home. Only when Rumei called for him would he come back. When Rumei wanted to arrange a marriage for Ruguai, Ruguai scolded him harshly, defying his elder brother's wishes.

    658

    一日患头风，医药不效。梅忧之，至废寝食。闻有人善治此疾，相违少远，早起遄臻，遍访无其人。日已夕，见三人在冈上籍地饮。将问之，三人俱起。一人曰：“好哥哥来矣。”梅不知所谓，其人曰：“以德化顽弟，又为跋涉寻医，非笃于友者不能。然近闻令弟手足已凉，固非凡手所能治。”梅闻之泣下，哀求方术。一人向二人曰：“胡兄，此症非章兄之妹不为功。”章不豫，谓：“袁兄是何言？”梅因跪章前不起。袁又谓：“二兄常言曾蒙口惠于耿兄。夫惠出于口，究亦惠也。耿兄有急难之忧，似不宜袖手。

    Explanation

    658

    One day, Mei suffered from a headache, and medicines proved ineffective. Mei was so worried about it that he neglected his meals and sleep. He heard that someone was good at treating this illness, and though they were slightly far apart, he got up early and hurried to reach them, visiting everywhere but finding no one. It was already evening when he saw three people lying on the ground drinking on a hill. He was about to ask them, and the three of them all stood up. One person said, "Good elder brother has come." Mei did not understand what was meant, and the person said, "To transform the stubborn younger brother with virtue, and to travel far to seek a doctor, one who is not deeply committed to friendship cannot do it. However, I recently heard that your younger brother's hands and feet have already grown cold, and it is certainly beyond the ability of ordinary hands to treat." Mei heard this and wept, sorrowfully requesting the method. One person turned to the other two and said, "Brother Hu, this condition cannot be cured without the help of Brother Zhang's sister." Zhang was displeased and said, "What are you saying, Brother Yuan?" Mei then knelt before Zhang and did not stand up. Yuan said, "The two of you often said that you were once verbally favored by Brother Geng. A favor given in words is still a favor. Brother Geng is in urgent trouble, and it seems inappropriate to stand idly by."

    659

    ”胡谓章曰：“令妹道术已成，盍烦医治？”章曰：“小妹独居炼修，恒不见人，岂肯觌医少年书生？”袁曰：“兄命之，或不敢违。”胡且力劝，章愠曰：“君亦有妹，能陪吾妹同往，则惟命是从。”胡曰：“能。”耿急起谢。共谓梅曰：“明日二妹自去。”梅归，语妻，丁氏以为妄。翌日将午，果有二女降于庭，皆国色。丁请二女入室，闻胡女谓章女曰：“姊道貌若是，何遽为他人作嫁？”章女曰：“长兄跪邀，不得不来。姊胡为乎？”胡女曰：“妹兄言，妹不来，姊亦不来。妹以为，妹来实无谓。妹兄两揖妹，妹不忍故违也。”丁叹美不已，请章医病。章曰：“以红绫蒙病者首。”丁以章言语桂，桂疑之。章请胡陪医。桂觉医以中指按其额角，大气吹天庭，奇香透脑，痛立止。

    Explanation

    659

    "Tru" said to Zhang, "Your younger sister has completed her Daoist practices. Why not trouble her to treat the patient?" Zhang replied, "My younger sister lives alone and practices Daoism, always unseen by others. How could she meet a young scholar-doctor?" Yuan said, "If your elder brother commands it, she might not dare to disobey." Tru urged strongly, and Zhang became angry, saying, "You also have a sister. If she can accompany my sister, then she will follow your command." Tru said, "I can." Geng quickly rose and thanked him. Together they told Mei, "The two sisters will go on their own tomorrow." Mei returned home and told her wife, Ding, who thought it was absurd. The next day, just before noon, indeed two women descended into the courtyard, both exceptionally beautiful. Ding invited the two women into the room and heard Tru's sister say to Zhang's sister, "Sister, your Daoist appearance is like this. Why are you suddenly preparing a wedding for someone else?" Zhang's sister replied, "My elder brother invited me while kneeling. I cannot help but come. What are you doing, sister?" Tru's sister said, "My elder brother said that if I do not come, you will not come either. I think my coming is actually pointless. My elder brother bowed twice to me, and I could not bear to deliberately disobey." Ding admired them continuously and asked Zhang to treat the patient. Zhang said, "Cover the patient's head with red silk." Ding conveyed Zhang's words to Gui, who doubted it. Zhang asked Tru to accompany the doctor. Gui felt the doctor pressing the middle finger on his forehead, a strong breath blowing on his forehead, a strange fragrance penetrating his brain, and the pain stopped immediately.

    660

    知医系女子，突将红绫扯去，乃天人，不违颜咫尺。二美俱羞甚，瞬息已杳。未几，桂饮食渐减，阅月黑瘦如鬼。梅问之，曰：“身无痛痒，何病之有？”但心如有事，每日戚戚。丁氏曰：“日昨之病也，幸有章女医之立愈。”言未终，桂即曰：“昔日之病，固赖渠而愈；今日之病，实因渠而得。”丁惊问：“叔之病，以渠得耶？若然何不聪，渠殆仙人也。”桂不语，梅以为深忧。次日，袁至，梅谢之，且告以忧，并问前日口惠之说，何以毫不记忆？袁曰：“君与同年某入山射猎，某善射，君曾戏喊：‘山中之物，务各避之！’某果终日不获，志诸乎？”梅始恍然，始知胡为狐、章为獐。

    Explanation

    660

    A woman who knew medicine, suddenly tore off the red silk, revealing herself to be a heavenly being, no more than an arm's length away. Both beauties were extremely embarrassed, and in an instant, she was gone. Soon after, Guì's food and drink consumption gradually decreased, and after a month, he became as black and thin as a ghost. Méi asked him, saying, "I don't feel any pain or itching, what illness do I have?" But his heart felt as if something was happening, and he was anxious every day. Dīng said, "It was the illness of yesterday, fortunately, the female doctor named Zhāng cured it immediately." Before he finished speaking, Guì said, "The illness of the past indeed relied on her to recover; today's illness was actually obtained because of her." Dīng asked in surprise, "Uncle's illness, did you get it because of her? If so, why are you not intelligent, she is probably a fairy." Guì did not speak, and Méi thought deeply worried. The next day, Yuān arrived, Méi thanked him and told him about his worry, also asking about the story of the verbal promise from the previous day, why he did not remember it at all? Yuān said, "You and a certain classmate went hunting in the mountains, that person was good at shooting, you once playfully shouted: 'Everything in the mountains, each must avoid it!' That person indeed did not catch anything all day, did you remember it?" Méi finally realized, and finally knew that Hú was a fox and Zhāng was a deer.

    661

    曰：“然则君与为友，得无猿乎？”袁笑曰：“君真智慧过人。但令弟虽因病得病，仆纵竭力玉成，欲分君忧，章女方怀恨，媒之必不谐。胡妹其可？”梅又恐胡氏亦不从，祈善为说辞。曰：“断不负托。”去而复返曰：“明日胡即送妹至。”曰：“何其速也？”袁曰：“令弟病不宜迟。”桂得胡女，心愿已足，不日已瘥。梅择日为弟成礼。届期，袁又自至，曰：“世事不测，信然矣。章兄言其妹医令弟时，受饱看之辱，归尤章，日夜啜泣，以为江汉难濯其耻。章慰之：妹得嫁之，此辱不足言矣。其妹始不泣。今烦仆作伐。”梅虑弟幼，不宜有二妻。袁曰：“然。然医弟之德不可没也。”梅难之，因语妻。妻往商于胡女，女曰：“有妹在，自无妨。”梅许之。二女同日合婚。

    Explanation

    661

    The speaker said, "In that case, would our friendship not be like that of a monkey?" Yuan smiled and said, "You truly have wisdom beyond others. However, your younger brother, though he gained a disease, even if I exert all my efforts to help him recover and wish to alleviate your worries, Zhang's sister, who was humiliated by being stared at, will certainly not agree to the match. What about Tru's sister?" Mei was afraid that Tru's family might not agree, and asked Yuan to speak on his behalf. Yuan said, "I will certainly not fail in my trust." He left and returned shortly, saying, "Tru will send his sister tomorrow." Mei asked, "How quickly is that?" Yuan said, "Your younger brother's illness should not be delayed." Gui was satisfied with Tru's sister, and soon recovered. Mei chose a day to perform the wedding ceremony for his younger brother. On the appointed day, Yuan came again and said, "Unpredictable events in life are indeed true. Zhang's brother said that his sister was humiliated by being stared at when she treated your younger brother, and upon returning, she blamed Zhang, weeping day and night, believing that the shame of the Yangtze and Han rivers could not wash it away. Zhang comforted her, saying that if she could marry her, this humiliation would not be worth mentioning. His sister then stopped crying. Now I am here to propose the match." Mei was concerned that his younger brother was too young to have two wives. Yuan said, "Indeed, but the virtue of treating your younger brother cannot be overlooked." Mei hesitated, so he discussed it with his wife. His wife went to negotiate with Tru's sister, who said, "With my sister in the family, there is no problem." Mei agreed, and the two sisters were married on the same day.

    662

    桂由是改行，折节读书，唯试辄不售。梅望之綦切。胡、章谓桂曰：“君不成名，负伯伯苦心矣，吾二人者亦难辞其责。今与君约：若获一衿，可奉事两月；中式，愿团聚二年；惟会殿后，白首无他说。”桂以为儿戏，院试仍不录。及归，二妻渺然，惭甚。次年入郡庠，窥其室，胡氏在焉，问：“章何不来？”曰：“阄拈下月矣。”嗣胡去章来，比六旬，桂谓章：“明朝卿果去耶？”曰：“然。胡姊之命不敢违。”曰：“卿等何忍也？”女曰：“非妾等忍，实恐负伯伯之情。君务苦读，明年登贤书，契阔无多日。不然，三年仳离，情何以堪？”次年，桂落孙山，读益力。梅欲为另娶，桂不可。下科中经魁，二妻偕归。桂戏之曰：“卿等若在家，生子将若大矣。”二女笑曰：“幸未误事，妾等早生子矣。”各呼子至，桂更喜。捷联南宫。二女不见老，后为子娶妇，不知者每以姑媳为妯娌焉。

    Explanation

    662

    桂因此改变行为，降低身份去读书，但每次考试都不中。梅对他的期望非常迫切。胡、章对桂说：“你不成名，辜负了伯伯的苦心，我们二人也难辞其咎。现在与你约定：如果你获得一衿，可以侍奉我们两个月；如果中式，愿意团聚两年；只有殿试后，白头偕老，没有其他话说。”桂认为是儿戏，院试仍然不录取。等到回家，两位妻子都不见了，非常惭愧。第二年进入郡学，窥视他的房间，胡氏在那里，问：“章为什么不来？”答：“抽签下个月。”随后胡离开，章来，过了六十天，桂对章说：“明天你果然要走吗？”答：“是的。不敢违背胡姊的命令。”桂说：“你们怎么忍心？”女子说：“不是我们忍心，实在是怕辜负伯伯的期望。你务必苦读，明年登贤书，分离的日子不多。否则，三年分离，情何以堪？”次年，桂落榜，读书更加努力。梅想给他另娶，桂不同意。下科考中经魁，两位妻子一起回来。桂开玩笑说：“你们如果在家，生的孩子将多大了。”两位女子笑着说：“幸好没有耽误，我们早生孩子了。”各自叫孩子来，桂更加高兴。捷报联名南宫。两位女子不见老，后来给儿子娶媳妇，不知道的人常把她们当作妯娌。

    663

    虚白道人曰：惠而在口，非实惠也，异类犹言念不置。今之蒙人实惠者，势盛则利其馀润，势衰则掉臂去，甚至为操戈，为下石，其羞此狐、獐也实甚。

    Explanation

    663

    The Virtuous White Hermit said: Benevolence on the lips is not real benevolence; even other species still talk about it without stopping. Those who now receive real benefits from people, when they are powerful, take advantage of the surplus; when they decline, they wave their arms and leave, even going so far as to wield weapons and throw stones. Their shame is indeed great compared to foxes and hares.

    664

    林芳

    　　林芳，名士也，善画梅。冬至前，忽有艳女过墙来。问其母姓，答以花氏。乐与好合。晨起，花倩林画梅一株，上点缀八十一蕊，粘壁间。花日以硃笔涂一蕊，九尽涂遍，俨然红杏矣。是年逢闰，花曰：“旧闻一联，迄无确对，今对之矣。”林问何联。曰：“丁香花百头千头万头，可对益节藕一脚半脚两脚。”林嘉许之。花去后，林联捷，人以为杏宴之兆。

    　　李 司 训

    　　蒲台李公向荣，以附生报捐训导。同班三人，而公为殿。平原出缺，渠二人俱系济南，因部选归公。固官运之通也，然其中则有别故。先是，公父亦秀才，昆仲二人，友于甚笃。其弟杀人，公父以仲尚无后，愿代仲自首；弟争之，终不可。下囹圄多年，后遇恩赦，军而得归。公之读书成名及捐纳训导，皆其叔之力，亦借以报兄德耳。齐东宋雨田先生，与李公通家，偶为言及，乃知天似有意于其间。

    Explanation

    664

    Translation

    Lin Fang

    Lin Fang was a distinguished scholar, skilled in painting plum blossoms. Before the Winter Solstice, a beautiful woman suddenly appeared, passing through the wall. When asked about her family name, she replied that it was Hua. They enjoyed each other's company and desired union. In the morning, Hua asked Lin to paint a plum tree with 81 buds, which he then stuck on the wall. Each day, Hua colored one bud with a red brush, and after coloring all 81, the plum tree transformed into a red apricot tree. That year, it was a leap year, and Hua said, "I have heard of a riddle that has never been solved, but I have found the answer now." Lin asked what the riddle was. She replied, "Lily-of-the-valley has a hundred heads, a thousand heads, ten thousand heads, which can be matched with a lotus root having one foot, half a foot, two feet." Lin praised her for her cleverness. After Hua left, Lin achieved success in his exams, and people believed this was a sign of good fortune.

    Li Si Xun

    Li Xiangrong from Putai became a supervisor through a donation. There were three people in his class, and he was the last. When a position in Pingyuan became vacant, the other two were from Jinan, and thus the selection was given to Li. This was indeed a smooth path in his official career, but there was another reason behind it. Earlier, Li's father was also a scholar, and he had two brothers who were very close. His younger brother committed murder, and Li's father, seeing that his younger brother had no descendants, volunteered to take the blame. The younger brother protested, but Li's father insisted. He spent many years in prison and was eventually released due to a general amnesty. Li's success in his studies, his fame, and his appointment as a supervisor were all thanks to his uncle, and he also used this opportunity to repay his brother's kindness. Mr. Song Yutian from Qidong, who was acquainted with Li, once mentioned this, and it became clear that heaven seemed to have a special intention in this matter.

    665

    先生又言：一绅士李公，原其致贵之由，盖因待塾师甚厚，其他可置弗论。公有五子，从北直张孝廉读。一日，张公之子俊至。孝廉大怒，曰：“历年所寄束贽，除家中日用，应有赢馀。汝母来信，言家中苦寒异常。汝不浮荡，何至如是？”明晨促之归，曰：“睹汝情形，必有病，可速去，勿死于此。”李公并不挽留，伪送之，而实引俊别墅居。视其身，风流疮将结，急为延医治之，月馀始瘥。李曲言于张，使从在塾读。张不可，李劝之，张感李意而始允。李令与先生同馔。及课期，张以“戒之在色”命题，俊文有云：“人本愚也，一笃于色，则无人而不智；人本智也，一迷于色，则无人而不愚。”张公见之，又大怒曰：“下流人则作下流语。”将夏楚之。李闻知，谓张公曰：“文情活泼，意到笔随，翰苑才也。颖悟若是，福命正未可量。”张始为之色解。后公五子俱显达。俊亦得两榜，榜下以知县用。

    Explanation

    665

    The Master also recounted: There was a scholar-official named Li Gong. The reason for his high status and wealth was due to his generous treatment of his private tutor, while other factors could be set aside without discussion. He had five sons who studied under Zhang Xianli, a scholar-official from the northern region. One day, Zhang's son Jun came to visit. Zhang Xianli became very angry and said, "Over the years, the fees you have sent as gifts to the teacher should have covered household expenses and left some surplus. Your mother wrote that your home is suffering greatly. If you are not indulging in frivolous behavior, how could things be so bad?" The next morning, he urged Jun to return home, saying, "Seeing your condition, you must be ill. Go back quickly and do not die here." Li Gong did not try to detain him, but pretended to send him away while actually bringing Jun to live in a villa. Upon examination, Jun had a venereal disease that was about to heal. Li Gong immediately sought a doctor to treat him, and after a month, Jun recovered. Li Gong then explained the situation to Zhang Xianli, asking him to allow Jun to continue studying at the school. Zhang Xianli refused, but Li Gong persuaded him, and Zhang Xianli, moved by Li Gong's intentions, finally agreed. Li Gong then arranged for Jun to eat with the other students. During the examination period, Zhang Xianli set the topic "Be cautious of lust" for the essay. Jun's essay included the lines: "Man is originally foolish; if he becomes overly attached to lust, no one remains wise. Man is originally wise; if he becomes overly entangled in lust, no one remains foolish." Zhang Xianli saw this and became furious again, saying, "A person of low morals writes such words." He was about to punish Jun. Li Gong heard about this and told Zhang Xianli, "The writing is lively and spontaneous, with thoughts flowing naturally to the pen. It shows the talent of a literary scholar. With such keen insight, his fortune and destiny are truly beyond measure." Zhang Xianli then calmed down. Later, all five of Li Gong's sons achieved prominence. Jun also passed the imperial examinations and was appointed as a county magistrate.

    666

    鸦 片 烟

    　　鸦片烟，嗜食者其害无穷，诚古今未有之奇劫也。有某夫妇对灯而眠，吸至夜半，天良发现，太息曰：“吾二人形消骨立，荡产破家，覆宗绝祀，皆累于烟。断烟则上瘾，忌烟则丧生，为之奈何？”忽有声喘喘然，仰视，一人倚床立，形容古怪，面黧瘦萎黄，肩耸没顶，头垂注胸，吓而问之。曰：“烟鬼也。吾于此道阅历深矣，有言相劝：无瘾不必吸，有瘾不必忌。若云节食，则唯圣贤能之，而圣贤又必不瘾于兹。”吹灯而去。

    Explanation

    666

    Opium Smoke

    Opium smoke, for those who indulge in it, brings endless harm, truly a unique catastrophe without precedent in ancient or modern times. Once there was a couple who slept by the lamp, smoking until midnight. Suddenly, their conscience awoke, and they sighed, saying, "We two are skin and bones, our property and family fortune ruined, our ancestral lineage destroyed—all due to opium. To quit smoking, one becomes addicted; to abstain from smoking, one dies. What can we do about it?" Suddenly, they heard a wheezing sound. Looking up, they saw a person leaning against the bed, with a strange appearance, a sallow and emaciated face, shoulders rising above the head, and a head hanging down to the chest. Startled, they asked him. He said, "I am an opium addict. I have deep experience in this path and have words to offer as advice: if not addicted, there is no need to smoke; if addicted, there is no need to abstain. If one says to moderate, only the sage and the wise can do it, and the sage and the wise would not be addicted to this." He blew out the lamp and left.

    667

    益智录卷之十一

    　　蒋廉

    　　前明福建蒋孝廉之子廉，初聘舅氏鱼渊女为妻。家渐凌夷，鱼悔之。廉为养母行丐于市，鱼以为辱，谋诸妇。妇曰：“盍市一棺，勿令女知，盖棺坚钉，伪言女死。妹一临哭，心绝望矣。可秘之，再为女择嫁。”鱼听妇言，置棺于庭，令媪伴女，不令下楼。一日，女自窗外窥，见姑母至，既而闻庭中有哭声，又见姑泣而去；问媪，对以不知。嗣渐知父谋，恨之。后改字于韩忠。嫁有日矣，黄昏时见一乞人自楼下过，闻其声，蒋廉也。女急下，从后门私约而上。

    Explanation

    667

    Record of Wisdom, Volume XI

    Jiang Lian

    During the late Ming dynasty, Jiang Lian, the son of Jiang Xiaolian of Fujian, was initially engaged to the daughter of his maternal uncle, Fish Yuan. As the family gradually declined, Fish Yuan regretted the engagement. Jiang Lian, in order to support his mother, began begging in the market. Fish Yuan considered this a disgrace and consulted his wife. The wife suggested, "Why not buy a coffin, keep the daughter unaware, seal it tightly, and falsely claim that she has died. Once the younger sister cries, her heart will be utterly despaired. You can keep it secret and then choose another husband for the daughter." Fish Yuan followed his wife's advice, placed a coffin in the courtyard, had an old woman accompany the daughter, and did not let her go downstairs. One day, the daughter looked out the window and saw her aunt arrive. Soon after, she heard crying in the courtyard and saw her aunt cry and leave. She asked the old woman, who replied that she did not know. Later, the daughter gradually learned of her father's plot and hated it. She later changed her name to Han Zhong. On the eve of her wedding, at dusk, she saw a beggar pass by the楼下 (which could mean "under the eaves" or "in the courtyard"). Upon hearing his voice, she recognized him as her father, Jiang Lian. The daughter quickly went downstairs and secretly met him through the back door.

    668

    廉曰：“卿未死也？”女曰：“死生小事，君应饥，可先食。”具馔以饱廉。楼有二新柜，即陪嫁物也。女将衣物并于一，曰：“媪出即回，请君暂藏于此。”廉入柜而媪与母俱来，始言嫁女事。女以柜中有人，伪喜应之，且言：“今晚将薄污我私，可无须媪伴。”母亦喜，与媪俱去。开柜出廉，而欢寝焉。黎明，仍匿廉于柜。旁午，有邻女托故强邀女。鱼遂急将妆奁遣人送于韩。比女回，而新柜已无。女虽暗自悔恨，实亦无可如何，计不如早奔夫家。往见姑曰：“媳实未死。然儿夫在韩家柜中矣。”历言之，姑曰：“奈何？”曰：“听之而已。”

    Explanation

    668

    Liang asked, “Are you not dead?” The woman replied, “Death and life are trivial matters. You should be hungry, so please eat first.” She prepared food to satisfy Liang. There were two new cabinets in the room, which were part of the dowry. The woman gathered her clothes and placed them together, saying, “When the old woman leaves, please return, and ask you to temporarily hide in here.” Liang entered the cabinet, and the old woman and the mother came in, beginning to discuss the matter of marrying off the daughter. The woman, knowing there was someone in the cabinet, pretended to be happy and said, “Tonight, I will allow you to touch my private parts lightly, and there is no need for the old woman to accompany us.” The mother also seemed pleased and left with the old woman. They opened the cabinet and brought Liang out, and then they slept together. At dawn, the woman again hid Liang in the cabinet. In the afternoon, a neighbor’s daughter, using an excuse, strongly invited the woman. The woman hurriedly sent the dowry to Han through someone. When the woman returned, the new cabinet was gone. Though she secretly regretted it, she could do nothing about it, and decided it would be better to go to her husband’s home early. She went to see her mother-in-law and said, “I am indeed not dead. However, my husband is in the cabinet at Han’s house.” She explained the situation in detail, and the mother-in-law asked, “What should we do?” The woman replied, “Just listen to it.”

    669

    适韩忠家唯有一妹，忠有他事，夜不能归。收鱼奁后，即嘱妹守房，交钥而去。及晚，婢媪客眷尽散，女禬扉欲开柜看新人履，执烛启柜，见内坐一人，大骇，烛亦坠地灭。廉以女为鱼氏，就柜上狎抱之。女曰：“君知妾为谁？”曰：“卿非鱼氏耶？”曰：“妾韩氏。”廉曰：“吾故疑柜之何以不胫而走也。”女曰：“君新嫂之旧夫乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“妾固料此事当有变。初不意鱼网而鸿离也，是真天作之合。”事毕，女曰：“一日之恩，妾已沦肌浃髓。

    Explanation

    669

    At Han Zhong's home, there was only his sister. Han Zhong had other matters and could not return home at night. After taking the fish chest, he instructed his sister to guard the room and handed over the key before leaving. By evening, all the maids, servants, and guests had dispersed. The woman opened the door and wanted to open the cabinet to look at the new shoes. Holding the candle and opening the cabinet, she saw someone sitting inside and was greatly frightened, causing the candle to fall and extinguish. Lian thought the woman was Fish Lady and hugged her on the cabinet. The woman asked, "Do you know who I am?" Lian replied, "Are you not Fish Lady?" The woman said, "I am Han Lady." Lian said, "I had always suspected why the cabinet had moved without legs." The woman said, "Are you the ex-husband of my new sister-in-law?" Lian replied, "Yes." The woman said, "I had always expected that this matter would change. I did not expect the fish net and the wild goose to part, but this is truly a match made in heaven." After the matter was done, the woman said, "One day's favor, I have been deeply moved."

    670

    兄归，吾二人皆无颜，不如同归君家。新人若来，即为妾也嫂，君亦可以得一妻；若不来，则为妾也嫡，君更可以得二妻。”廉此时毫无主张。女收拾金帛，与廉偕归。及至家，鱼氏正侍母而言：“柜中人何迄今无消息耶？”回首见廉与韩氏入，怪而问之曰：“何尚有不嫌吾丐夫者？”因相得益欢。唯韩忠失妹又失妇。及知俱归于蒋，痛忿鱼渊不置，将兴讼。鱼夫妇惧，许为买二婢以偿。廉得韩结金帛，菽水亦可常奉；加以二女同志，事姑婉顺，甚慰母心。廉乃得仍理父业，后中进士。

    Explanation

    670

    When Lian returned, both of us were ashamed, and it would be better to return to your home together. If the new wife comes, she will be my elder sister-in-law, and you can also get a wife; if she does not come, she will be the legitimate wife, and you can even get two wives. At that time, Lian was at a loss for what to do. The woman gathered gold and silk and returned with Lian. When they arrived home, Yu's wife was attending to her mother and speaking, "What news has the person in the cabinet had until now?" Turning around, she saw Lian and Han entering and was surprised and asked, "Why is there still someone who does not嫌弃 my beggar husband?" They became even happier together. Only Han Zhong lost his sister and his wife. When he learned they had both returned to Jiang, he was extremely angry at Yu Yuan and was about to sue. Yu's wife and husband were afraid and agreed to buy two maidservants as compensation. Lian received Han's wealth and could regularly provide for his mother. Moreover, the two women were of like mind, served their mother-in-law gently and respectfully, greatly pleasing her heart. Lian was able to continue his father's business and later passed the imperial examination.

    671

    余母

    　　邑侯余孟侨先生，以举人需次山东。性情古朴，既居官，仍手不释卷，诵读如书生。以故在省候补多年，宦场鲜有识者。中丞丹初阎公，秋闱后，偶见其所拟墨，悦之，由是得摄篆历城。政事一以慈惠为心，有杜母之风，民因呼余老娘云。后升任胶西，官至刺史。

    　　合 欢 头

    　　庄岳之间有妇人焉，性情啬吝，凡事皆然；虽男女大欲，亦迥殊于人。夫每欲交之，必曲诱十馀夕，始得褫其裤。纵竭力周旋，其颦蹙抽缩，终使男子不得驰骤，狎之亦不欢，以故胎屡毈。其一偶至十月未坏，将大产。妇患之，自念门户窄小，安容人出，身势必决裂。无何，觉痛，遂大困。不得已，亦效临盆。乃儿甫露头之半，忽用力一比合焉，娩婆惊曰：“娘子且缓，儿头夹断矣！”方囫囵一声，但新婴自眼以下，鼻上，如蜂腰。及长，犹似合欢葫芦样。偶入市，见者无不窃笑。

    Explanation

    671

    余母

    The County Magistrate Yu Mengqiao, a juren (provincial examination pass) awaiting an appointment in Shandong, was a man of simple and ancient disposition. Even after taking office, he continued to read incessantly, reciting texts as if he were still a scholar. Because of this, he remained on the waiting list for many years, and few in the officialdom knew him. After the autumn metropolitan examination, Vice-Governor Yan Danchu happened to see Yu's draft essays and was pleased with them, and thus Yu was appointed to oversee the prefecture of Licheng. In his governance, he was guided by kindness and benevolence, embodying the virtue of the legendary Lady Du, and the people affectionately called him "Old Mother Yu." Later, he was promoted to the prefecture of Jiaoxi, eventually reaching the rank of governor.

    合欢头

    In the neighborhoods of Zang and Yue in Luoyang, there lived a woman who was miserly in nature, in all things; even in matters of sexual desire, she was markedly different from others. Her husband, whenever he wished to be intimate with her, had to coax her for over a dozen nights before she would undress. Even when he made great efforts to please her, her frowning and withdrawing expression ultimately prevented him from being able to fully engage, and she could not even find joy in such encounters, leading to repeated miscarriages. One time, however, she carried a child to term at ten months, and was about to give birth. The woman was distressed, thinking that her narrow doorway could not accommodate the baby, and that her body would surely tear apart. Soon after, she felt pain and became greatly distressed. Unable to do otherwise, she also went into labor. The baby's head was just half-exposed when suddenly she exerted force to fit it together. The midwife exclaimed, "Madam, please wait! The baby's head is being crushed!" In a single, whole sound, the newborn's face from the eyes downward and above the nose was narrow, like a wasp's waist. As the child grew, his face still resembled a happy gourd. Once, he went into town, and all who saw him could not help but whisper and laugh.

    672

    严 三 凤

    　　严三凤，字秋泉，邑之好秀才。为人玩世不恭，善戏谑，开口辄惹人笑，而己独不笑，故以是得名。未进时，惯作代倩文字，以此应童科最久。每院试，搜检役颇讨厌，甚至挝衣捋带，上下其手。有携酒者，必倾诸瓮，备牛饮也，严恨之。时值夏日，解溺瓶中，以新荷渍酒塞其口而入。搜役见之，闻香以为佳酿，去塞急覆诸瓮而沫浮焉。怪之，严曰：“何怪？童便耳！饮之安心神，除恶秽，但病者用作引，少许即可。若辈固瞀乱，或多多益善耶？”役大恚。因此，巡场者迭守之，使不暇他顾。乃得泮游，而攘酒之弊端遂除。

    Explanation

    672

    严三凤

    严三凤，字秋泉，是县里的一位著名秀才。他为人玩世不恭，擅长开玩笑，开口总是让人发笑，而他自己却不笑，因此因此而闻名。在他尚未进入仕途之前，习惯代人写文章，因此参加童子试的时间最长。每次院试，搜检的官员都非常讨厌他，甚至会拍打他的衣服和拉扯他的腰带，暗中做手脚。如果有带酒的人，他一定会将酒倒入瓮中，准备痛饮，这让严三凤非常憎恨。正值夏天，他将尿液解入瓶中，用新鲜的荷叶浸泡酒后塞住瓶口，然后带入考场。搜检的官员看到后，闻到香味以为是好酒，急忙打开瓶塞，将酒倒入瓮中，结果泡沫浮起。官员们感到奇怪，严三凤说：“有什么好奇怪的？这只是童子尿！喝了可以安心神，去除恶秽，但病人用来引药，只需少许即可。你们这些人本来就糊涂，是不是越多越好？”官员们非常生气。因此，巡场的官员轮流守着他，使他无暇他顾。最终，他得以在泮池游玩，而偷酒的弊端也因此被消除。

    673

    董 二 晕

    　　董二晕者，名非自命，人盖以其性无定、行无恒，行二，故以“二晕”呼之也。籍广西临桂。家綦贫，佣身庄农家。一晚，一贼匿其居室卷席中。董见之，爰沽酒市肴，禬扉。将酒烘热，谓贼曰：“酒热矣。来，吾与汝饮。”贼心计室中止董一人，知董所呼在己而不敢少动。既而，董复曰：“吾谓席中朋友也。”贼不得不出。董识贼，姓王。对饮时许，纵之去。一夜，闻有动静，潜起，启扉暗出，见一贼穴主人屋墙透，仰卧而入。董执其足返接其手，拽之入己室。火之，仍前王姓贼，释其手，曰：“子何复窃于此？”王曰：“吾之所窃有分别。今复窃尔主人者，为其刻薄成家也。”董义之，复纵之去。

    Explanation

    673

    Dong Er Yun

    Dong Er Yun was not given a name by choice; people called him "Er Yun" because of his indecisive nature and inconsistent behavior. He hailed from Lingui, Guangxi, and lived in extreme poverty, working as a farmhand for a family. One evening, a thief hid in a rolled-up mat in his room. Dong saw him and then bought wine and food, closing the door. He warmed the wine and told the thief, "The wine is hot now. Come, let us drink together." The thief, calculating that there was only Dong in the room, knew that Dong was calling for him and dared not move. After a while, Dong said again, "I thought it was a friend in the mat." The thief had no choice but to come out. Dong recognized the thief, who was named Wang. They drank together for some time before Dong let him go. One night, he heard some noise, secretly got up, opened the door and slipped out, seeing another thief digging through the wall of the owner's house and entering it. Dong grabbed the thief's feet and pulled him into his own room. He lit a fire, and it was still the Wang-named thief. Dong released his hand and said, "Why did you come back to steal here?" Wang replied, "There is a difference in what I steal. Now stealing from your master is because he is stingy in his household." Dong respected this and let him go again.

    674

    秋后，董每天早起拾遗，天渐寒，冷亦难堪。妻祝氏贤甚，纺织为董做棉衣，亲身送至，董甚喜。一日，起过早，北风忽作，因至赌窖暂避，见赌者钱丰，质衣而赌，输。违家不远，至家呼妻起。妻见无棉衣，问之，以实告。妻曰：“不贪人之钱，不输己之衣。”出钱令夫赎衣，曰：“勿再赌。”董诺而回，见赌者未散，欲珠还合浦，复赌，又输。惭，因出亡。妻知之，烦人赎其衣以俟，无耗。二月，生一子。忽窗外有人呼董二兄，祝氏闻之，曰：“吾夫外出已二月馀。”其人曰：“吾知之。吾姓王，贼也，与二兄有一面之交。今窃得白金若干，以半奉二嫂为日用。银在窗外，吾去也。”多时无动静。祝出视之，果有白金二锭，约百馀两。嗣王屡以物馈祝，皆以夜。祝本勤俭善居室，得王助，六、七年以成殷实。恐久为王累，于王送物时，隔窗语之曰：“得君助，衣食已足，请已之。”王应诺。盖自是王无馈也。

    Explanation

    674

    After the autumn, Dong would wake up early every day to collect stray items. As the weather gradually became colder, the cold became unbearable. His wife, Zhu, was very virtuous and would weave and make cotton clothes for Dong, personally delivering them to him, which made Dong very happy. One day, he got up too early, and a north wind suddenly arose. He therefore went to a gambling den to take a temporary refuge. He saw that the gamblers had plenty of money and were betting with their clothes as collateral, and they lost. Not far from home, he went home and called his wife to get up. His wife saw that there was no cotton coat and asked him about it. He told her the truth. His wife said, "Do not covet other people's money, do not lose your own clothes." She took out money to let her husband redeem his clothes and said, "Do not gamble again." Dong agreed and returned. He saw that the gamblers had not dispersed and wanted to return like the pearls to Hépǔ, so he gambled again and lost again. He felt ashamed and therefore fled. His wife knew about it and had someone redeem his clothes in anticipation, but there was no news. Two months later, she gave birth to a son. Suddenly, someone outside the window called out, "Brother Dong." Zhu heard and said, "My husband has been away for more than two months." The person said, "I know. My name is Wang, and I am a thief. I have met you once before. Today I stole some white gold and am giving half to your wife for daily use. The silver is outside the window, I am leaving now." There was no movement for a long time. Zhu went out to look and indeed found two ingots of silver, about a hundred and several taels. Afterward, Wang frequently gave things to Zhu, all at night. Zhu is naturally thrifty and good at managing the household. With Wang's help, she became prosperous in six or seven years. She was afraid that she would become a burden to Wang for too long. When Wang came to deliver things, she spoke through the window to him, "With your help, my clothing and food are sufficient, please stop." Wang agreed. From then on, Wang did not give anything more.

    675

    董二晕之出亡也，年少壮，沿路送行客、助劳人，所得钱文每有馀剩，爰制冠带，市緼袍，虽不美盛，不敝污。违家日远，不日至贵州。黎明，见一人以小车推木料二块。其人姓苗，载稍重，有微堤，不能上。董上之，曰：“吾与子同路，请助子。”遂牵其车而行。至其家，并妥其事而后行。苗留之，曰：“日已夕，明天早行可也。”董从之。苗食董。苗适有紧急匠事，饭后即为之。董尝幼习木工，略知其事，因效苗为之。苗喜，佣之。及半年，苗言与妻曰：“吾本他邑人，仅有一女。

    Explanation

    675

    Dong Erhun fled his home. Young and strong, he helped travelers along the way and assisted the weary, often having leftover money. He then made headwear and sash, and bought a coarse robe. Though not luxurious or splendid, the robe was not worn out or dirty. As he departed from home day by day, he eventually reached Guizhou. At dawn, he saw a man pushing two pieces of wood with a small cart. The man was named Miao, and the load was a bit heavy, causing a slight slope that he couldn't climb. Dong helped him, saying, "I am on the same road as you, please let me help you." He then pulled the cart and went with him. When they arrived at Miao's home, Dong arranged matters and continued on his journey. Miao kept him, saying, "The sun has already set, it is better to start early tomorrow." Miao fed Dong. Miao happened to have an urgent carpentry task, which he did after eating. Dong had once learned carpentry as a child and had a rough idea of the work, so he imitated Miao and did it. Miao was pleased and hired him. After half a year, Miao said to his wife, "I originally came from another town, and I have only one daughter."

    676

    董某诚实，吾欲赘之，以为终身之靠。”妻亦欲。因赘董，使从苗姓。苗出积蓄制恒产，亦称小康。一日，董赴集买物，过赌场，见赌者似不精熟，因同赌，竟输。思欲得本资而止，竟全输，空手而归。妻问之，以实告，妻未语。董惭悔交深，愤理匠事，失手将右足小指伤去。他日以他事反目，董曰：“日昨吾赌负之事，汝心终不忘，故有今日之事。吾始知仰食裙带失丈夫气，悔当日不宜赘于汝家也。”妻曰：“吾不为往事。君既有悔，从心所欲可也。”董曰：“可。”董早出不归。妻疑之，嗣果无耗。

    Explanation

    676

    董某诚实，吾欲赘之，以为终身之靠。妻亦欲。因赘董，使从苗姓。苗出积蓄制恒产，亦称小康。一日，董赴集买物，过赌场，见赌者似不精熟，因同赌，竟输。思欲得本资而止，竟全输，空手而归。妻问之，以实告，妻未语。董惭悔交深，愤理匠事，失手将右足小指伤去。他日以他事反目，董曰：“日昨吾赌负之事，汝心终不忘，故有今日之事。吾始知仰食裙带失丈夫气，悔当日不宜赘于汝家也。”妻曰：“吾不为往事。君既有悔，从心所欲可也。”董曰：“可。”董早出不归。妻疑之，嗣果无耗。

    Translation:
    Dong was honest, and I wanted to marry him into our family, considering him a lifelong support. My wife also agreed. Because of this, Dong married into the Miao family and took their surname. The Miao family established a permanent property, and they were considered moderately prosperous. One day, Dong went to the market to buy things and passed by a gambling house. He saw that the gamblers seemed inexperienced, so he joined them and ended up losing. He thought he would stop once he got back his principal, but he lost everything and returned empty-handed. His wife asked him about it, and he told her the truth. She did not say anything. Dong felt deeply ashamed and regretful, and in his anger, he lost control while working as a craftsman and injured his right little toe. One day, they fell out over another matter, and Dong said, "Yesterday's gambling loss is something you never forget, so this happened today. I now realize that relying on others for food has made me lose my manhood, and I regret that I should not have married into your family." His wife said, "I do not dwell on past events. Since you have already regretted, do as you please." Dong replied, "Okay." He left early and did not return. His wife suspected something and later found out there was no news of him.

    677

    董之负气而出也，二日后颇自悔，而耻于自返，遂遵大路而行。不日，届四川秀山，卖工夫以糊口。一日，佣身菜翁家，菜喜其壮盛，佣之月馀，昼出理田，晚归食宿于工人草屋中，自言苗姓。菜某有义女及笄，一日，语父曰：“吾家苗姓短工似非常为短工者。”菜闻女言，即烦人媒说，以女嫁之。盖菜以女非亲生，恐择嫁不如女意，惹其埋怨。闻女赞董，以为女属意于董，而女以为养父之命不宜违，诚天缘有分也。董娶女后仍理匠事。女母系继娶，子女皆非其出，故钟爱女，不时暗助。未几，生一子。至子八、九岁时，家业有成矣。董居诸有成算，唯子苗云祥读，省费不计。云祥天资明敏，入泮后娶妇。董自谓一生际遇如此已为极美，不知后有进于此者。忽兴念嫡妻，不知艰难何似，假出游，乘马归。

    Explanation

    677

    Dong, in a fit of anger, left his home. Two days later, he began to regret his actions but was ashamed to return. He then followed the main road and continued on his way. Soon, he arrived in Xiu Mountain, Sichuan, where he sold his labor to make ends meet. One day, he was hired by a vegetable farmer's family. The farmer was pleased with his strength and kept him for over a month. Dong worked in the fields during the day and returned to the workers' thatched hut in the evening, where he introduced himself as Miao. The farmer had an adopted daughter who had reached the age of marriageability. One day, she told her father, "The short worker from the Miao family seems to be more than just an ordinary laborer." Upon hearing this, the farmer immediately arranged for a matchmaker to propose marriage to Dong. The farmer feared that his adopted daughter might not be happy with her choice of husband, which could lead to resentment. He had heard that his daughter praised Dong, and believing that she had feelings for him, he thought it was fate. After marrying the daughter, Dong continued his work as a carpenter. The daughter's mother, who was a second wife, had no biological children of her own and therefore cherished her adopted daughter, often secretly helping her. Soon after, they had a son. By the time the son was eight or nine years old, the family had become prosperous. Dong, satisfied with his life, focused solely on his son's education, spending generously without counting the costs. His son, Miao Yunxiang, was bright and quick-witted, passed the county-level examination, and married a wife. Dong believed that his life had been extremely fortunate, unaware of future developments. Suddenly, he remembered his legitimate wife, wondering what her life had been like. He pretended to go on a trip and returned home by riding a horse.

    678

    抵家，见门阀宏深，类素封。心谓妻已嫁，宅归异姓。既而，一少年华服出，董欲与言，其人已过。忽一老人谓少年曰：“汝何往？”指董曰：“汝父来矣！”适董妻出，见之曰：“果尔父。”董外出二十五年，一朝团聚，乐何如之。妻历言家事，知子吉祥已入泮，喜甚。亦自叙外出之事。妻子观其行色，固知不贫，其馀未敢深信。适邻庄有中乡试者，姓梁。董同庄人赴贺，见壁粘四川题名录一纸，上列苗云祥之名，大喜。梁问之，董曰：“此小儿。”梁暗哂之，以为姓且不同，安得为子？董言于妻子，妻子亦妄听之。董之归也，会妻菜氏所生之子苗云祥赴乡试。中式归，不见父，问母，母曰：“半月前出游未归，无处迎接，俟之而已。”年终无耗。

    Explanation

    678

    Upon arriving home, he saw a grand and imposing mansion, similar to that of a wealthy family. He thought his wife had remarried and the house had been taken over by another family. Shortly after, a young man in fine clothes emerged from the house. The narrator tried to speak to the young man, but he had already passed by. Suddenly, an elderly man addressed the young man, asking, "Where are you going?" Pointing at the narrator, he said, "Your father has come!" Just then, the narrator's wife came out and recognized him, saying, "Indeed, your father." The narrator had been away for twenty-five years, and now they were reunited, how joyful it was! The wife recounted family matters, and the narrator learned that his son Jixiang had entered the school, which made him very happy. He also recounted his own story of being away. His wife and son observed his demeanor and knew he was not poor, but they were hesitant to believe the rest. Just then, a man from a neighboring village who had passed the provincial examination, surnamed Liang, was being congratulated. The narrator and his fellow villagers went to congratulate him and saw a list of successful candidates from Sichuan posted on the wall, with Miao Yunxiang's name listed, which greatly pleased them. Liang asked the narrator, who replied, "This child." Liang secretly smiled, thinking that since the surname was different, how could he be the son? The narrator told his wife and son, and they also believed it. When the narrator returned home, coincidentally, the son born to his wife Cai, named Miao Yunxiang, was taking the provincial examination. He passed the examination and returned, but did not see his father. He asked his mother, who said, "He left on a trip two months ago and has not returned. There was no place to meet him, so we just wait." By the end of the year, there was no news.

    679

    比春正，菜氏谓子曰：“汝父必回籍矣，可赴临桂访之。”云祥如临，访之数日，并无苗姓人家。一日，过梁孝廉第，知为新贵，遂进谒之，同年之谊，倍笃常情。苗自道为寻父到此，且以之询梁。梁曰：“敝处无与君同姓者。”忽睹壁间题名录，忆董言，曰：“有一人或知之，君可亲问之。”遣家人请董，曰：“汝谓有秀山客在此，请渠光陪，渠必至。”梁家人语于董，董谓妻子曰：“吾子来矣。意其既中举，必来寻吾。”妻子尚有疑心。既而，董与云祥至。令其衣冠朝嫡母、拜兄长，举家始大喜，肆筵作贺。数日后，云祥请父同回秀山，吉祥母子不欲。迟延月馀，云祥与父谋，乘夜暗归。吉祥知之，遣人追回。爰是不欲父出游，即出游，必使人伴之，并将云祥车马行装掩藏他处，云祥欲自归不得也。

    Explanation

    679

    By the time spring arrived, Cai Shi told her son, "Your father must have returned to his hometown. You should go to Lingui to visit him." Yunxiang went to Lingui and searched for several days but found no one with the surname Miao. One day, he passed by Liang Xielian's residence and learned that Liang was a newly appointed official. He then paid a visit, and their bond as fellow examinees deepened, becoming much stronger than usual feelings. Yunxiang explained that he had come to Lingui to search for his father and asked Liang for help. Liang replied, "There is no one with your surname in my area." Suddenly, Yunxiang noticed a list of names on the wall and remembered what Dong had said. He said, "There might be someone who knows about it. You can ask him personally." Liang sent his family to invite Dong and said, "You mentioned there is a guest from Xiushan here. Please have him come along, and he will definitely arrive." Liang's family informed Dong, who told his wife, "My son has come. I think he must have come to find me after passing the exam." His wife still had some doubts. Soon after, Dong and Yunxiang arrived. Yunxiang was instructed to dress formally and pay respects to his mother and elder brother. The whole family was overjoyed and held a feast to celebrate. A few days later, Yunxiang asked his father to return to Xiushan together, but Jixiang and her son did not want to. After delaying for more than a month, Yunxiang and his father planned to return secretly at night. Jixiang found out and sent someone to chase them back. From then on, she did not want her father to travel. If he did go out, someone had to accompany him. She also hid Yunxiang's carriages, horses, and belongings elsewhere, so Yunxiang could not return on his own.

    680

    不得已，具禀临桂邑宰苗公。公问之曰：“董吉祥，汝之子耶？”董曰：“嫡妻所生。”公复曰：“苗云祥，亦汝之子耶？”董曰：“外出另娶之妻所生。”公曰：“汝姓董，何复姓苗？”董曰：“其中有故，不便细禀。”公曰：“汝欲就养何子？”董曰：“皆欲也。事不自由，听之而已。若蒙恩断来往由己，则感德无极矣。”问云祥，云祥曰：“举人愿奉亲归秀山，定省数月即送回。”问吉祥，吉祥曰：“数月后，吾弟不送回如何？”公曰：“吾亦难保其必送回。然非汝一己之父，不止汝一人欲奉养，究竟如何？”吉祥曰：“吾弟奉养吾父二十五年矣，吾亦欲奉养二十五年，而后送回。”公笑曰：“汝父现年约五十有馀，人生七十古来稀，汝再自奉二十五年，将就木焉，岂情理也哉？汝等且归，吾即烦绅士为汝调处。”公退，见母而笑。母问之，公曰：“今日有一案奇甚。二子争养其父，其父亦不能自主，儿亦难以听断。”遂历述之。

    Explanation

    680

    Translation

    Unavoidably, the petition was presented to the magistrate of Línguì County, Miáo Gong. The magistrate asked, "Dǒng Jíxiáng, are you his son?" Dǒng replied, "Born of my legitimate wife." The magistrate then asked, "Miáo Yúnxiáng, is he also your son?" Dǒng answered, "Born of a wife who remarried after leaving home." The magistrate said, "You bear the surname Dǒng, why do you also bear the surname Miáo?" Dǒng replied, "There is a reason, which I cannot explain in detail." The magistrate asked, "Which son do you wish to support?" Dǒng said, "I wish to support both. The matter is not within my control; I will leave it to fate. If you grant us the freedom to decide for ourselves, I will be deeply grateful." The magistrate then asked Miáo Yúnxiáng, who said, "As a scholar, I am willing to take our father to Xiùshān for a few months of visits and then return him." The magistrate then asked Dǒng Jíxiáng, who said, "After a few months, what if my brother does not return him?" The magistrate replied, "I cannot guarantee that he will return him. However, this is not just one person's father; both of you wish to support him. What do you think?" Dǒng Jíxiáng said, "My brother has supported our father for twenty-five years, and I also wish to support him for twenty-five years before returning him." The magistrate laughed and said, "Your father is now over fifty years old. In ancient times, reaching seventy was rare. If you support him for another twenty-five years, he will be on his deathbed, which is not reasonable. You may go back for now, and I will entrust the local gentry to mediate the matter." After the magistrate returned home, he saw his mother and smiled. His mother asked him about it, and the magistrate said, "Today, I had a very strange case. Two sons were disputing over caring for their father, and the father could not make his own decision. It was difficult for me to pass judgment." He then recounted the entire case.

    681

    母曰：“汝父临桂人，吾忘其籍邑。今姓董者年纪几何？面目奚似乎？”公曰：“然。”母曰：“汝谋视其右足无小指，即汝父。”公即传案，示谋于役。董将上堂，役故以臭水污其两靴而自认以误，董不暇更易。既上堂，公迎问靴污之故。董言之。公令人取新靴易之。既脱靴，见董右足果无小指，急退禀母。母曰：“吾已穴窗窥明，是汝父。”公急遣人请父兄于内书房，向父稽首曰：“儿高坐，父北面而跪，曩即不知，亦死有馀辜矣。

    Explanation

    681

    The mother said, "Your father is from Lingui, but I have forgotten the county where he was registered. How old is the person who now has the surname Dong? How does his appearance resemble yours?" The son replied, "Yes." The mother said, "You should look at his right foot to see if it lacks a little toe; that is your father." The son then had the case brought forward and showed the plan to the servant. Dong was about to ascend the hall, but the servant deliberately stained his two boots with dirty water to falsely claim the mistake. Dong did not have time to change his boots. When he ascended the hall, the magistrate welcomed him and asked why his boots were dirty. Dong explained it. The magistrate then had new boots brought to replace the old ones. He hurried back to report to the mother. The mother said, "I have already peeped through a hole in the window to see clearly; that is your father." The magistrate quickly sent someone to invite the father and elder brother to the inner study. He bowed to the father and said, "The son sits high, and the father kneels facing north. If I had not known before, I would have been guilty of a grave sin."

    682

    ”董不胜惊骇，方欲细询，苗夫人出，笑谓董曰：“君弃妾而逃，妾以君为死矣。今犹在人世耶？”指县尹曰：“君去三月生此子。连捷即用，初蒞此任也。”董大喜，遂为云祥述赘苗之事，曰：“吾之改姓苗，即此故也。”令二子复姓董。尹名呈祥，命名之同，亦神奇。时王某犯案被押，闻县尹为董公之子，曰：“吾出头有日矣。”人问之，王不语。不几日，果得释归。此吾徒刘元吉闻而言之。谈此事者即临桂人，与董公同乡焉。

    　　虚白道人曰：董公名晕，晕而不晕也。执草窃而释之，依然以梁上人为君子之意也；以仰食妻室，每乾纲不振，因而他适，是未失丈夫之气也；后复娶妻生子，家成业就，若可终身，乃念及结发，弃之而归，是能笃夫妇之伦也。如是之人，而名之为晕，则今之自命不晕者，其有惭此晕人者固多矣。世之藐视人者，己多可藐之事；藐人益甚，则己之可藐益著。犹日事徼讦以为知直，恶能免名贤之所恶也！

    Explanation

    682

    Dong was greatly surprised and frightened, and was about to inquire in detail when Miao's wife appeared and smiled at Dong, saying, "You abandoned me and fled. I thought you were dead. Are you still alive in this world?" She pointed to the magistrate and said, "You left three months ago and this child was born. He achieved success in consecutive examinations and was immediately employed, initially taking up this position." Dong was overjoyed and then recounted to Yunxiang the story of how he had married into the Miao family, saying, "I changed my surname to Miao for this very reason." He ordered his two sons to resume their original surname, Dong. The magistrate's name was Chengxiang, and the coincidence of the names was also remarkable. At the time, Wang had been arrested for a crime and, upon hearing that the magistrate was Dong's son, said, "I will have a chance to clear my name." When people asked him about it, Wang remained silent. Not long after, he was indeed released and returned home. This was heard and told by my disciple Liu Yüjī. The person who talked about this matter is from Línguì, and is a fellow villager of Dong Gong.

    The Vǎibái Daoist said: Dong Gong's name was Yun, but he was not really confused. He released the bandit, still treating him as a gentleman; he supported his wife, and whenever she lacked authority, he would leave, showing that he had not lost his masculine spirit; later, he remarried and had children, establishing a family and a career, which could have lasted a lifetime, but he remembered his first wife and left her to return, demonstrating his commitment to the relationship between husband and wife. Such a person, who is called "confused," would make many of those who today claim to be "not confused" feel ashamed. The world often looks down on others, but such people often have much to be looked down upon themselves. The more they look down on others, the more their own shortcomings become apparent. Those who daily engage in slandering and accusing others, thinking they are wise and upright, cannot escape the disapproval of the famous and virtuous.

    683

    龙真穴的

    　　某翰林，现官南省督抚，先世亦农商之家。人咸谓其祖父母葬地龙真穴的。先是公大母终，族人不令葬先茔，戚友悉为不平，势将致讼。其大父曰：“吉人自有吉地，何必先茔即福田耶？且以此兴讼，破己之产，亦破人之产，大伤族情，实不忍为。”遂迁葬，于是而发。可知龙真穴的，仍须在心地上寻尔。

    　　绛云

    　　安庆孝廉宋公，谈者忘其名字。一夕出游，时梨花盛开，莺栖清艳，蝶醉浓香，徘徊赏玩，直至溶溶月上始归。至斋题一绝云：“画廊人静月初斜，窗外清阴透碧纱。一缕闲情吹不散，结成幽梦上梨花。”录毕，置青毡下。次日，同年生过访，言：“有人善召箕仙，盍往观之？”公素不信此，未便固违，遂同往。请乩人悉在，公问：“诸君请何仙人？”众云不一。公伪言：“去岁旧居停，与其友请绛云仙女，极有灵应。

    Explanation

    683

    Translation:

    Auspicious Site

    A certain scholar-official, currently serving as a governor-general in a southern province, came from a family of farmers and merchants. People all said that the site where his grandparents were buried was truly auspicious. Before his grandmother passed away, the family did not allow her to be buried in the ancestral tomb, and relatives and friends were all upset, threatening to bring a lawsuit. His grandfather said, "A good person naturally finds a good place; why must the ancestral tomb be a field of merit? Moreover, to bring a lawsuit over this would not only ruin our own property but also others', greatly harming family sentiment, which is truly something I cannot bear to do." They then moved the burial site, and it turned out to be auspicious. Thus, it is known that even if a site is truly auspicious, one must still seek the truth in one's heart.

    Jiang Yun

    The scholar-official Song from Anqing, whose name is forgotten by those who talk about him, went out for a stroll one night when the pear blossoms were in full bloom, the orioles perched on the delicate beauty of the flowers, and the butterflies intoxicated by the rich fragrance. He wandered and enjoyed the scenery until the moon rose high before returning. Upon reaching his study, he wrote a short poem: "The gallery is quiet, the moon slants at the beginning of the month; the clear shade outside the window penetrates the green gauze. A thread of idle sentiment cannot be blown away, forming a dreamy vision on the pear blossoms." After writing it, he placed it under the green mat. The next day, a fellow examinee visited and said, "There is someone who is skilled at summoning the spirit of the soot in the divination bowl. Why don't we go and watch?" The official had never believed in such things and did not want to offend him, so they went together. The diviners were all present, and the official asked, "What deities are you inviting?" They replied that there were many. The official pretended to say, "Last year at my old residence, I and my friend invited the fairy Jiang Yun, who was extremely responsive."

    684

    诸君盍请之？”众曰：“公识其符否？”曰：“识之。”因仿请女仙符，少为更移，绘以授众。爰拈香焚符，未几箕动，降坛诗，即宋公所作梨花诗也。公深骇异，不好言诗系己作，亦从众赞美而已。继书四字曰：“宋公多言，致余多此一往。兹有事奉托。”众问何事，箕书：“难言也。然当言不得不言。吾与白若玉有宿缘，祈诸公媒订之。”盖白即在坛，青年丧偶未续也。众向白言，白亦喜应，戏问几时纳采，何处亲迎；再问，箕已不动。诸人同谈至更定始散。

    Explanation

    684

    Why not ask for her?” The group said, “Do you recognize the symbol?” He replied, “I do.” So he imitated the request for a female fairy's talisman, made slight changes, drew it, and gave it to the group. They began by burning incense and the talisman. After a short while, the writing brush moved, and a poem descended from the altar, which was the pear blossom poem by Song Gong. He was deeply amazed and did not dare to say that the poem was his own work, but he also joined in praising it. Then, four characters were written: “Song Gong spoke too much, causing me to have this one visit. I have something to entrust.” The group asked what it was, and the writing brush wrote: “It's hard to say. But when it must be said, it must be said. I have a predestined relationship with Bai Ruoyu, and I ask all of you to arrange a marriage for us.” It turned out that Bai was present at the altar, a young woman who had recently lost her husband and was not yet remarried. The group spoke to her, and she was also happy to agree. They teasingly asked when the betrothal gift would be presented and where the bride would be escorted; when they asked again, the writing brush had stopped moving. They all talked until late at night before dispersing.

    685

    白生家无亲眷，唯一媪一仆。至家，仆俟于门，谓：“适有一媪扶一女郎来。”白知为绛云，急入室，见女红妆坐帐中。白入，媪扶之下榻，与白并坐，真有“国色比玉香犹胜，仙姿如花语更真”之概。媪治具，令白与女交杯饮。白曰：“闻卿与仆有缘，敢问何说也？”女曰：“言之骇人，且坏古人名节，君不必深究也。”他日，乩友谓白：“焚符而绛仙不至，如何？”白曰：“费心，费心，杜兰香早下嫁矣。”后值重阳，诸坛友相约登高，有六七农人请召乩问雨期。众议请吕仙，或曰：“今日白生因事他出，何不仍召绛云？”于是连焚三符，竟至，乩书：“前系散仙云游，由己；今为人妇，宜夫命是从。

    Explanation

    685

    White's family had no relatives, only an elderly woman and a servant. Upon arriving home, the servant awaited him at the door and said, "Just now, an elderly woman brought a young lady here." White recognized her as Jiao Yun and hurried into the room, where he saw the young lady dressed in red sitting in the canopy. The elderly woman helped her down from the bed and sat beside White, and she truly embodied the phrase "Her beauty surpasses jade, and her fragrance is even more exquisite; her divine grace is like a flower, and her words are even more truthful." The elderly woman prepared a feast and made White and the young lady drink from the same cup. White said, "I heard that you and I have a connection. Dare I ask what it is?" The young lady replied, "To speak of it would be shocking and would tarnish the reputation of ancient people. You need not delve deeply into it." Another day, a spirit medium friend told White, "We burned the talisman, but Jiao Xian did not appear. What should we do?" White said, "It's a bother, it's a bother. Duran Xiang has already been married off." Later, on the Double Ninth Festival, the friends from the altar gathered to climb high, and six or seven farmers requested a spirit medium to inquire about the rainy season. The group suggested summoning Lü Xian, but someone said, "Today, White is elsewhere on business. Why not still summon Jiao Yun?" They then burned three talismans consecutively, and she finally arrived. The spirit medium wrote, "Previously, I was a wandering immortal, roaming at will; now I am a wife and should follow my husband's commands."

    686

    夫谓吾当遵阴教，总不宜与诸公接谈。今命召三次，勉应之，祈速言其事。”众告：“秋来雨少，农人切望，问何日乃雨？”女曰：“此天意，吾不敢泄漏。请以九九算数作谜，诸公猜之：二九一十八，二九不是一十八；三八二十四，三八不是二十四；四七不是二十八；五六不是三十。内寓四字书一句。”众莫解。忽有人曰：“吾知之矣。一二一九共合十一，二九不是一十八；一三一八亦共合十一，三八不是二十四；四与七，五与六，皆共合十一。寓句其实皆十一也。今九月九日，后日必有雨，盖后日即十一日也。”众以为然，遂以告农人。十一日果大雨。白闻之曰：“卿何好事好盛又为此也？”女曰：“诺。从此决不应召。”未二年，女辞去，白始鸾续。

    Explanation

    686

    If they say I should follow the teachings of the hidden path, I should not generally interact with all of you. Now that I have been summoned three times, I am reluctantly responding. Please quickly tell me the matter." The assembled group said, "This autumn has seen little rain, which farmers are eagerly awaiting. When will it rain?" The woman replied, "This is the will of heaven, and I dare not reveal it. Please create a puzzle using the nine-nine multiplication table, and let all of you guess it: Two nines make eighteen, but two nines do not make eighteen; three eights make twenty-four, but three eights do not make twenty-four; four sevens do not make twenty-eight; five sixes do not make thirty. There is a four-character phrase hidden in one sentence." None of them could understand. Suddenly, someone said, "I know it now. One two and one nine together make eleven, two nines do not make eighteen; one three and one eight also make eleven, three eights do not make twenty-four; four and seven, five and six, all make eleven. The hidden phrase actually refers to eleven. Today is the ninth day of the ninth month, and the day after tomorrow will definitely rain, because the day after tomorrow is the eleventh day." All agreed, and so they informed the farmers. On the eleventh day, it indeed rained heavily. Bai heard this and said, "Why are you doing this good and grand thing?" The woman replied, "Yes. From now on, I will never respond to such calls again." After two years, the woman left, and Bai began his life anew.

    687

    开癞

    　　南方不知何省，深山中女子有生癞之说，盖为山瘴所染也。一得斯疾，土人识之，无有以之为妻者，如妻之，男子必死。惟于破瓜时，令女自处，靓之，任其出游，诱他乡男子与之交，名曰开癞，其毒自消，始有问名者。得与男交，多则一月，少则二十日，辞男令行。行时，饮饯馈赆，意甚殷切，劝其急归，盖恐其死于路也。男去后，父母扬言其事，以为择配。

    Explanation

    687

    Leprosy Opening

    In the southern provinces, there is a belief that women can contract a leprosy-like condition due to exposure to mountainous瘴气 (miasma or toxic air). When a woman contracts this condition, local people recognize it, and no one will marry her. If a man marries such a woman, he is believed to die. However, at the time of her first sexual encounter, she is made to look attractive and is allowed to go out freely. She is encouraged to have sexual relations with men from other places, a practice known as "opening the leprosy," which is believed to cure the condition. After having sexual relations, she is allowed to stay with the man for a month or a minimum of 20 days before sending him away to continue his journey. When she sends him away, she gives him a farewell drink and gifts, showing her earnestness and sincerity, and urges him to return quickly, fearing that he might die on the journey. This practice is socially accepted as a way of choosing a suitable match.

    688

    地有万氏女得斯疾。万令从俗而行，女不欲，曰：“请死，不损人利己。”迟至二年，无奈父母之命不能屡违，不得已，诱一少年与同寝处。少年自言周璋，寒士，游学到此。实本姓武，伪为周也。男女甚相得。比一月，万令女遣之，女不忍，又半月，病势难堪。缘与女同处益久，则其发益猛，再迟则不能去矣。女窃父藏，谋与偕行。周疑其言不由衷。女曰：“君不能久于世，妾不可二夫，此两全之术，君何疑焉？”爰乘间偕亡，未出山而止，僦舍以居。俄而毒发，周求女延医理治，女曰：“君病不能治也。”遂语以必死之故。言讫而泣，日夜不停声。忽窗外有人曰：“哭无益也。”女知其异，曰：“哭无益，不哭有益乎？”其人曰：“有。此去东南三十里有摩天岭，岭半有洞，为麻姑仙养静处，可往求之。但彼处多长蛇，恐子不敢去耳。”女曰：“敢。惟不识路径。”其人曰：“吾可导汝行。”曰：“子何人也？”曰：“游鬼也。哀汝情切，故以告。

    Explanation

    688

    There was a woman from the Wan family who contracted this illness. The Wan family instructed her to follow the custom, but she did not wish to comply. She said, "I request death, so as not to harm others for my own benefit." After delaying for two years, she could not repeatedly disobey her parents' commands, so she had no choice but to lure a young man to share her bed. The young man introduced himself as Zhou Zhang, a poor scholar who had come to study here. In reality, he was surnamed Wu and was pretending to be surnamed Zhou. The man and woman were very compatible. After a month, the Wan family ordered her to send him away, but she could not bear to part with him. After another half month, the illness became unbearable. Because of sharing the same space for a longer time, its onset became more severe, and if delayed further, it would be impossible to leave. The woman stole from her father's hidden stash and planned to run away together. Zhou doubted that her words were not sincere. She said, "You cannot live long in this world, and I cannot have two husbands. This is a way to achieve both. Why do you doubt?" They took the opportunity to escape together, but they stopped before leaving the mountain and rented a place to live. Soon after, the illness manifested, and Zhou asked the woman to seek medical treatment. She said, "Your illness cannot be cured." She then explained the reason for her inevitable death. After speaking, she wept, and the tears did not stop day or night. Suddenly, someone outside the window said, "Crying is of no use." The woman knew that the person was unusual and asked, "If crying is of no use, is not crying of use?" The person replied, "Yes. Thirty li southeast of here is Mothai Mountain. Halfway up the mountain is a cave, where the Immortal Machu cultivates tranquility. You can go there to seek help. However, there are many long snakes there, and I fear you may not dare to go." The woman said, "I dare. I just do not know the path." The person said, "I can guide you." She asked, "Who are you?" The person replied, "I am a wandering ghost. I grieve deeply for your situation, so I inform you."

    689

    天明见有小旋风，即吾也。”周闻之，虑为蛇害。女曰：“果为蛇害，君无夫死妇醮之虞；若幸免于蛇，得仙术以愈君疾，则妾为夫不畏死之心得以自明，君之福，妾之幸也。”乃行。果有羊角风在前转旋，从之。入深山，忽见一蛇如车轮，向女而来。女思无可逃，闭目以俟，竟未遭其吞噬。复前行，遍地皆蛇，大小无数。但蛇近女身，俱掉头不吸，而女入蛇乡，觉身更清爽。因得至洞府拜见麻姑，言夫病状。麻姑告以泄毒于妓或可愈。女归以告周。

    Explanation

    689

    During the dawn, she saw a small whirlwind and said, "That is me." Zhou heard this and feared being harmed by snakes. The woman replied, "If indeed harmed by snakes, you will have no worry of a widow remarrying after her husband's death; if fortunately spared by the snakes, I will obtain the elixir of immortality to cure your illness, thus proving to you that I am not afraid of death for your sake. This will be your fortune and my happiness." They then proceeded. Indeed, there was a spiral wind in front of them, and they followed it. They entered a deep mountain and suddenly saw a snake as big as a carriage wheel coming towards the woman. Thinking there was no way to escape, the woman closed her eyes and waited, but the snake did not devour her. Continuing forward, they found the ground covered with countless snakes of all sizes. However, when the snakes approached the woman, they all turned their heads and did not bite. As the woman entered the snake-infested area, she felt even more refreshed. She then arrived at the cave temple to meet Magu and described her husband's illness. Magu advised that releasing the poison through a prostitute might cure it. The woman returned and informed Zhou.

    690

    周曰：“卿尚不欲损人利己，吾为此乎？”女曰：“所损止一妓耳，何妨？”周曰：“妓非人乎？损之而有益于吾，吾亦不为，况未必能愈耶？”女劝之再四，而周仍不听。次早又赴洞，见乱云迷径，峭壁插天，洒涕而返。不意夫竟为一大蛇盘绕，涎垂满面矣。入舍，见蛇口有衔草，遗之去。周此时已不省人事，呼之苏。女遂以蛇衔草煎之，服一剂而疾若失。乃知麻姑之所以救之者即在此也。后周捐贡入北闱，联步南宫，得翰林庶吉士。凡泥金捷报皆是武璋。

    Explanation

    690

    Zhou said, "You still do not wish to harm others for your own benefit, would I do such a thing?" The woman replied, "The harm is only to one prostitute, what's the harm?" Zhou responded, "Is a prostitute not a person? If harming her would benefit me, I would not do it either,何况未必能愈耶？" The woman urged him repeatedly, but Zhou still refused to listen. The next morning, he went to the cave again and saw a path obscured by swirling clouds, with sheer cliffs reaching up to the sky. He shed tears and returned. Unexpectedly, his husband had turned into a large snake coiled around him, with drool hanging from its mouth. When he entered the house, he saw the snake's mouth holding a herb, which it left behind. At that moment, Zhou was unconscious, and when called, he regained consciousness. The woman then boiled the herb the snake had held and gave it to Zhou to drink. After taking one dose, his illness disappeared. It became clear that Magu's method of saving him was indeed through this herb. Later, Zhou donated his quota to enter the Northern Examination, and they walked together into the Southern Palace, becoming a candidate for the Hanlin Academy. All the official announcements of success were from Wu Zhang.

    691

    带产出继

    　　东村某，兄弟各爨，弟富而兄贫。兄卒无子，妻刘氏又复失明，势必饿死。邻里共怜之，遂约村中数人往见某，代刘祈賙恤，竟不允许。众曰：“乡党有急，尚宜賙之，况嫂乎？”曰：“分管时，兄未怜吾幼，多与毫厘。賙急犹可，賙嫂实不欲。”众恚而出曰：“某二子而伊兄无子，托言伊嫂欲继伊子为嗣，某不欲其子出继，或賙其嫂。”众复入向某言之，某笑曰：“继吾子以自养，吾嫂之计亦巧矣。且是令吾子弃饱暖而受饥寒，吾岂欲乎？”众正言之，曰：“律无绝长之理，法制如是。”某仍不听，言愈弗逊。众益恚，见刘氏，令渠当官过继。醵资遣佣人导刘去，具呈控某，准。某惧，哀戚里求息讼，将家产阄分，因使其长子带产出继。

    Explanation

    691

    A Woman with Two Sons and the Inheritance

    In the eastern village, there were two brothers who lived separately. The younger brother was wealthy, while the elder brother was poor. After the elder brother's death, he left no children, and his wife, Liu, had become blind, leaving them in a precarious situation that would inevitably lead to starvation. The neighbors, feeling pity for their plight, agreed among themselves to visit the younger brother and ask for assistance for Liu. However, the younger brother refused to help.

    The neighbors said, "When fellow villagers are in need, we should still help them. How much more so for the elder brother's wife?" The younger brother replied, "When we divided the property, the elder brother did not show much kindness to me, giving me only a little. Helping in times of emergency is still acceptable, but helping the elder brother's wife is truly undesirable."

    The neighbors were angry and left, saying, "The younger brother has two sons, while the elder brother has no children. It is suggested that the elder brother's wife wants to adopt one of the younger brother's sons as her heir. The younger brother does not want his son to be adopted and prefers to help his elder brother's wife instead."

    The neighbors returned to speak to the younger brother again. He laughed and said, "Adopting my son to support myself, the elder brother's wife's plan is also clever. But this would mean making my son go from having food and warmth to hunger and cold, which I do not want."

    The neighbors spoke earnestly to him, saying, "According to the law, there is no provision for cutting off the inheritance of the eldest, and the law is set up this way." The younger brother still refused to listen and became even more disrespectful. The neighbors were even more angry and decided to take Liu to court to force an adoption. They collected money and sent a servant to take Liu to court. They filed a complaint against the younger brother, and the court agreed.

    The younger brother was afraid and begged in the village to settle the lawsuit. He divided the family property equally and made his eldest son adopt the elder brother's wife, thus becoming her heir.

    692

    虚白道人曰：嫂贫而养之，无嗣而继之，分也，何待人言？至言之不听，其人已不足齿数矣。究之不能不养，不敢不继，为不养不继者之榜样，是养之继之，而仍为天下之罪人也。

    　　鬼狐遗方

    　　凡吞盐卤者，血凝即死，以未点卤之豆腐汁灌之即愈，此鬼遗方。盖卤毒悉归豆汁，其理易明也。如比近无卖豆腐家，急取豆研汁，亦可济事。吞信石者，急用防风一两，研末，清水调灌，亦愈，此狐遗方也。防风只去风湿之药，而能化砒毒，令人不解。按《本草》，防风能杀附子毒。夫附子极热，信石亦极热，防风能解信石热毒，或即以其有杀附子热毒之能乎？二方前辈著述多载之，余恐传闻不广，故复录于此。

    Explanation

    692

    Xubai the Daoist Master said: "Nurturing one's poor sister-in-law and continuing the family line when there is no heir is one's duty; what need is there for others to say it? If one refuses to heed sound advice, such a person is already unworthy of mention. In the end, one cannot avoid nurturing and cannot dare not to continue the family line, setting an example for those who do not nurture or continue the family line, yet remains a sinner to the world."

    Formulas left behind by ghosts and foxes:

    Those who swallow salt brine will have their blood congeal and die. If they are given undiluted soy milk, they will recover. The reasoning is easy to understand. If there is no nearby tofu shop, quickly grind soybeans into juice, and it can still help.

    **Those who swallow arsenic should quickly use one liang of Fangfeng, grind it into powder, mix with water, and administer it, and they will recover. This is a formula left behind by a fox. Fangfeng is only a wind-dampness remedy, yet it can neutralize arsenic poison, which is puzzling. According to the Bencao (Herbal), Fangfeng can neutralize the toxicity of Fuzi (aconite). Fuzi is extremely hot, and arsenic is also extremely hot. Could it be that Fangfeng's ability to neutralize the heat toxicity of arsenic is due to its ability to neutralize the heat toxicity of Fuzi? Many predecessors' writings have recorded these two formulas. I am concerned that these remedies are not widely known, so I am recording them again here."

    693

    查 修 文

    　　查修文，闽人。贸易归，违家尚有三十馀里，雨忽至。数廿步外有小庙，可容五、六人，急为趋避。先有少妇在其中，饰虽荆布，神情绝秀。因将行囊置庙门内，令妇闭户，曰：“倘避雨者众，甚不便。”庙幸背风，有微厦，雨不沾衣。夜半始霁。查终夜未与妇再交一语。未辨色，呼妇启扉，取包裹去。妇日出始行，见去人遗布褡，内有银五两许，钱三百，携归。妇单氏，夫穆瑞图。一日，妇与小姑闲语，曰：“人言世少好人，亦实有好人，盖往往外貌麟鸾，中韬鬼蜮。如昨愚嫂所遇，可谓真君子。

    Explanation

    693

    Chu Xiwen

    Chu Xiwen was a merchant from Fujian. On his return from a trading journey, he was still thirty or more miles from home when it suddenly started raining. About twenty steps ahead, there was a small temple that could accommodate five or six people. He quickly sought shelter there. There was already a young woman inside, dressed in simple but elegant attire, with a remarkably beautiful demeanor. He placed his belongings inside the temple and instructed the woman to close the door, saying, "If many people seek shelter, it would be very inconvenient." Fortunately, the temple was sheltered from the wind and had a small eave that kept the rain off, so they stayed dry. The rain stopped at midnight. Throughout the night, Chu did not speak to the woman. At dawn, he called out to her to open the door and retrieve his belongings. The woman began her journey at sunrise and found a left-behind cloth bag, which contained about five taels of silver and three hundred coins. She took the items back home. The woman was Shan Shi, the wife of Mu Ruitu. One day, she was talking with her sister-in-law, saying, "People often say there are few good people in the world, but there are indeed good people. They often appear virtuous on the outside but may have hidden malicious intentions. As for my foolish sister-in-law’s encounter yesterday, it can truly be called a gentleman’s act."

    694

    ”妹问之，妇遂细述避雨之事。妹哂曰：“其人即君子，遇嫂亦未必君子，殆嫂嫂自谓之君子乎？”妇自知言出莫追，然问心无亏，遂并银与钱示之，曰：“此渠所遗。”妹方检视，其兄适至，问银物何来，妹为述之。穆曰：“此事果真，真不愧为君子。”既而谓妻曰：“兹闻汝母暴病，盍与吾同赴彼处一问？”母固无疾，女疑之。比至，谓妇翁曰：“汝女昨归遇雨，时运甚好，拾得银钱如许。”委诸几上而去。父问女，女以实对。父曰：“尔情虽真，而婿意不了矣。”又恐过激生变，只好留女于家。

    Explanation

    694

    The sister asked about it, and the wife then carefully recounted the story of how she had taken shelter from the rain. The sister laughed and said, "That person is a gentleman, but even when he meets his wife, he might not be a gentleman, perhaps the sister-in-law considers herself a gentleman?" The wife knew that once words were spoken, they could not be recalled, but her conscience was clear, so she showed both the silver and the money to her, saying, "These were left by him." Just as the sister was examining them, the husband arrived and asked where the silver and money came from. The sister recounted the story. Mu said, "If this is true, then he truly does not disgrace the title of gentleman." Then he told his wife, "I have heard that your mother suddenly fell ill, why don't we go together to ask about it?" The mother actually had no illness, and the daughter suspected it. When they arrived, he told the father-in-law, "Your daughter returned home yesterday and encountered rain, and her luck was very good, so she found this much silver and money." He left it on the table and went away. The father asked the daughter, and she answered truthfully. The father said, "Though your feelings are true, your husband's mind is not at ease." Fearing that an excessive reaction might cause a change, he had to keep the daughter at home.

    695

    穆归见褡，问妹。曰：“亦嫂所拾。”穆忖度曰：以银钱赠人，未有并褡予之者。转念妇既与男子同庙宿，事终可疑。穆固牵车贩鬻，异日旅次，遇同行诸友约饮酒肆。查亦在坐，与穆同乡，又久相识，忽见穆布褡曰：“是我之所遗，褡中尚有他物，君见之否？”穆曰：“见之。君遗于何处？”查曰：“某日避雨宿某庙中，绝早而行，遗之。”穆伪曰：“是吾即拾彼庙中。”查曰：“奇哉，少妇至贞洁，何并此物亦不顾也？”穆问所由，查遂自夸其与少妇同居，终夜不生邪念。穆曰：“君谁欺？素好眠花藉柳，何忽有坐怀不乱之操？”查正色曰：“为娼妓者损弃廉耻，借声色以养生；好狎游者耽玩温柔，倾资财以买笑。

    Explanation

    695

    Mu returned and saw the cloth, asking his sister. She replied, "It was also picked up by my sister-in-law." Mu pondered and said, "Giving silver and money to someone, there has never been a case where the cloth was also given." Upon reflection, since the woman stayed in the same temple with a man, the matter was still suspicious. Mu usually pulled a cart and sold goods. One day, on a journey, he met fellow travelers who invited him to drink at an inn. Zha was also present, from the same hometown as Mu, and they had known each other for a long time. Suddenly, Zha saw Mu's cloth and said, "It is what I left behind, there are other things in the cloth, have you seen them?" Mu said, "I saw them. Where did you leave them?" Zha replied, "On a certain day, I took shelter from the rain in a temple and left early in the morning, leaving it behind." Mu pretended and said, "It was I who picked it up in that temple." Zha said, "Strange, a young woman is so chaste, why would she not care about this object?" Mu asked the reason, and Zha boasted about staying with the young woman all night without any improper thoughts. Mu said, "Who are you trying to fool? You usually sleep with women, how come you suddenly have the virtue of not being tempted?" Zha replied seriously, "Prostitutes abandon their integrity and use sex to sustain themselves; those who like to play with women indulge in tenderness and spend money to buy smiles."

    696

    譬诸贸易，有卖有买，庸何伤？若乘妇女独处而遂污之，纵事不显露无损名誉，而致妇女怀惭终身，甚至含羞自尽，丧德孰甚焉！且易地而论，污人妇女者之妇女若被人污，当必痛心疾首，欲得甘心。岂可以己所不欲之事而加诸人也！”穆摇首不语。查复曰：“吾当日若有淫行，必遭雷击。”穆乃冁然自喜，揖查曰：“实告君，所遇少妇，即仆贱内。今闻君言，知伊被屈，遣人往接，未必能来。君倘与岳家有素，敢烦善为调停。”查曰：“可。”因归言于单翁，送女于穆，破镜重圆。穆乃语妻曰：“今而知查兄真君子，卿言信不诬矣。”益共德查，爰折简招查至。席间，穆令妹靓妆出，执杯劝查饮，曰：“闻兄中馈无人，仆愿以舍妹续琴弦，以报兄不御内子之恩，何如？”查喜，立起展敬。后穆妹连生二子，次子官至二品，有贤声。

    Explanation

    696

    Consider it like trade, where there is buying and selling, what harm is there? If one takes advantage of a woman who is alone and defiles her, even if the matter does not become public and does not damage one's reputation, it causes the woman to feel ashamed for life, and even leads her to take her own life out of shame. What greater loss of virtue could there be! Moreover, if we consider the situation from the other side, the woman who has been defiled would surely feel deep pain and desire to seek justice. How can one do to others what they would not want done to themselves?” Mu shook his head without speaking. Zha said, “If I had engaged in immoral behavior, I would have been struck by lightning.” Mu then smiled in satisfaction and bowed to Zha, saying, “I must tell you, the young woman I encountered is my wife. Now that I have heard your words, I know she was wronged. I sent someone to fetch her, but she may not come. If you have a long-standing relationship with her father-in-law, I humbly ask you to mediate on my behalf.” Zha said, “I will.” He then went home and told Dan翁, who sent his daughter to Mu, and their broken mirror was mended. Mu then told his wife, “Now I know that Brother Zha is a true gentleman. Your words were not false.” They both admired Zha, and so they wrote a letter inviting Zha over. At the banquet, Mu had his sister-in-law dress up elegantly and come out to offer Zha a cup of wine, saying, “I have heard that my brother has no one to manage his household. I wish to offer my younger sister to continue the family line, to repay my brother for not taking advantage of my wife. What do you think?” Zha was pleased and immediately stood up to drink a toast. Later, Mu's sister-in-law gave birth to two sons, the second of whom became a high-ranking official known for his virtue.

    697

    附录一：序、跋、题辞

    　　跋

    　　庚申之春，余罢官后侨寓稷下，杜门养疴，惟以书籍自娱。客有言《益智录》者，亟购其书读之，亦搜神志怪之流；而笔意矫矫绝俗，迥非近今操觚家可比。其著名为历城解子镜，名鉴。爰访其人，而历邑鲜有知其姓字者。嗣闻其设帐于黄台山，在城北八里许，因宛转招致之。无何，扣扉见访，则皤然一白叟也。询其生平，自云：少应童子试，至老不遇，卒未获衿；家贫，恃训蒙为业，今行年已六十矣。其人清癯鹤立，意致温雅，语言讷讷，如不能出诸口。而于诸子百氏之书，多所涉猎，工文善诗，究心于古。此编则诵读之馀，戏仿淄川蒲氏《聊斋志异》而成者。以此窥解子，犹泰岱之一拳，沧溟之一勺耳。谈者见其规仿《聊斋》神肖，谓可与《聊斋》争席，余谓不然。

    Explanation

    697

    In the spring of the year corresponding to the stem-branch combination of "Geng Shen," after I was dismissed from office, I temporarily resided in Jixia, where I closed the door and rested while sick, entertaining myself with books. Someone mentioned "Yizhi Lu," and I urgently bought and read the book. It is a collection of strange and supernatural stories, and the style is so outstanding that it surpasses the ordinary, far surpassing the recent writers.

    The author is identified as Jieling Xiaozijing, also known as Jie Jian. I sought him out, but few people in Licheng knew his name. Later, I learned that he taught at Huangtai Mountain, about eight miles north of the city, so I managed to invite him over. Soon after, he knocked on the door to visit me, and he appeared as an old white-haired man. When I inquired about his life, he said: "I took the preliminary examination for the imperial examinations when I was young, but I never succeeded and did not obtain a degree. My family is poor, and I rely on teaching children for a living. I am now sixty years old." He was slender and stood like a crane, with a warm and elegant demeanor, and his speech was slow and hesitant, as if he could not easily express himself. However, he had read widely from the works of various philosophers and scholars, was skilled in writing and poetry, and had a deep interest in ancient culture and history. This compilation was created in his spare time, as a playful imitation of Pu Songling's "Liaozhai Zhiyi."

    From this, one can see that Jie Jian is but a small part of the grandeur of Mount Tai and a small part of the vastness of the ocean. Those who discuss his work note that it closely imitates "Liaozhai Zhiyi" and suggest that it could compete with "Liaozhai Zhiyi" for the same seat. I, however, do not agree with this view.

    698

    《聊斋》天才横逸，学问奥博，后人讵易相踵？然《聊斋》以怀才不遇，特借此以抒其抑郁，故其书呵神詈鬼，嬉笑怒骂，无所不有，殆亦发愤之所为作耳。解子少负隽才，一无遇合，至垂白之年，犹坐穷山中，训童子以糊口，其穷厄视《聊斋》为何如？而所为书，无一肮脏语，无一轻薄语，劝善惩淫，一轨于正。虽与《聊斋》同一游戏之笔，而是书独能有裨于世道，是其读书养气之功，视《聊斋》差有一长也。然吾因之有感矣。

    Explanation

    698

    Liaozhai is a work of extraordinary talent, its author's genius freely expressed, and his learning profound and extensive. How easy is it for later generations to follow in his footsteps? However, Liaozhai was created by someone who felt unrecognized for his talent, using it as an outlet for his frustration. Thus, the book curses gods and demons, and encompasses laughter, jest, scolding, and cursing, with nothing left out. It is indeed a work born of frustration. The author of this text, Jiezi, was young and endowed with exceptional talent, yet he found no opportunities for success. By the time he reached old age, he still lived in a remote, impoverished area, teaching children to make a living. How does his poverty compare to that of Liaozhai? Yet, his writing contains not a single impure or vulgar word, not a single frivolous or disrespectful word. It encourages good and punishes evil, adhering strictly to the right path. Although it shares the same playful writing style as Liaozhai, this book uniquely benefits society. Its value lies in the author's intellectual and moral development, which surpasses Liaozhai in one aspect. Nevertheless, I am moved by this.

    699

    人情好奇而厌常，震虚声而寡真赏。《聊斋》以沉博绝丽之才，搜奇猎异，出幽入明，自足以耀士林之耳目。而其时又有名公卿负海内龙门之望，片言品题，声价百倍，故虽穷困潦倒，而犹能声华藉藉，倾动一时。解子才非不逮，徒以恂恂乡党，不慕浮华，不矜声气，坐使名字不出于里閈，士大夫几无有知其谁何者，斯非一不平之事耶？顾余宰历城时，解子犹应县试，余以风尘栗六，竟未物色及之。今余解组将归，解子已笃老，乃始相与扼腕而叹也。呜呼，晚矣！

    Explanation

    699

    People's nature is inclined toward the strange and weary of the ordinary, impressed by empty fame but seldom appreciating true merit. Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio, with its profound, extensive, and uniquely beautiful talent, seeks out the strange and unusual, emerging from obscurity into prominence, and is sufficient to dazzle the scholars. During the same period, there were high-ranking officials with great influence across the land, whose single word could boost someone's reputation a hundredfold. Thus, even though he was poor and destitute, he could still spread his reputation widely and loudly, captivating the moment. Jiezi's talent is not inferior; he was merely a modest and humble person in his hometown, not seeking superficial glory, not boasting of his reputation. This led to his name not spreading beyond the village gate, and few scholars knew who he was. Is this not an unjust situation? When I served as an official in Licheng, Jiezi was still taking his county examination, but I was preoccupied with worldly affairs and did not recognize him. Now that I am resigning from office and returning home, Jiezi is already an elderly man, and only now do we clench our fists and sigh together. Alas, it is too late!

    700

    咸丰十年八月，沧州芸士叶圭书跋。

    　　序

    　　余滥竽齐垣，已十有三年矣。历下之名儒硕彦、学士文人，凡耳所闻而目所见者，似已野无遗贤矣，乃未闻竟有解公子镜其人者。戊辰夏六月，其兄持子镜先生所著《益智录》十卷见示。细心浣诵，斑豹全窥，始知子镜先生乃济南之名下士也。何竟未之见而并未之闻也？余滋愧矣，何见闻之不广也！至此益恨相识之晚。观其全部中，或探奇猎异，或谈鬼搜神，大半以游戏之文而寓劝惩之意。至用意之离奇变幻，用笔之悱恻缠绵，虽从《聊斋志异》中得来，而劝善规过之深心，福善祸淫之意旨，凡有关于纲常伦纪、世道人心者，殆有过之无不及也。

    Explanation

    700

    Preface

    For thirteen years, I have been serving in Qi, though I am merely a pretender. In Lixia, I have heard of and seen renowned scholars, distinguished intellectuals, scholars, and literati, and it seems that there are no hidden talents in the countryside. Yet, I have never heard of a person named Gongzi Jing. In the summer of the fifth month of the year of the Horse (戊辰), his brother presented me with Mr. Gongzi Jing's Yizhi Lu in ten volumes. After carefully reading and pondering it, I gained a thorough understanding and realized that Mr. Gongzi Jing is indeed a distinguished scholar from Jinan. How could I not have seen or heard of him? I am truly ashamed of my limited knowledge and experience. I now regret having met him so late. In his entire work, he either explores the strange and unusual or discusses ghosts and searches for spirits, mostly using playful writing to convey the intention to encourage good behavior and discourage bad behavior. His use of strange and ever-changing ideas and poignant and lingering writing, though derived from Liaozhai Zhiyi, carries a deep heart for encouraging goodness and correcting mistakes, and the principle of rewarding virtue and punishing vice, which is relevant to the norms of propriety, the order of the world, and the hearts of people, is perhaps even more so than in Liaozhai Zhiyi.

    701

    以先生之才之学，即掇高科，登显仕，其政绩所著，可以激励世俗、维持风化者，必绰绰乎其有馀矣。乃竟青衿未博，黄卷终身，岂天之报施才人，固当如是耶？岂以一行作吏，鞅掌簿书，日从事于案牍纷纭之会，不足以展其才耶？日驰逐于冠裳文物之场，不足以竟其学耶？故使投闲置散，厄其遇以老其才；铸史熔经，专其心以精其学。欲先生之才之学，不特表暴于当时，直欲流传于奕祀；不特宣扬于一世，直欲永著于千秋耶？果如是，则天置之位置贤豪，其用心亦良苦矣！始焉特为先生惜，继焉不为先生惜，而竟为先生幸焉。先生固可以无憾矣！

    Explanation

    701
