    With the talent and learning of the Master, one could have secured a high rank in the imperial examinations, attained a prominent official position, and the achievements in governance that are recorded would certainly be more than sufficient to inspire and maintain moral and social norms. Yet, the Master did not pass the highest level of the imperial examinations and spent his life studying. Is it that the way heaven rewards talented people is indeed as it should be? Is it that once serving as an official, being burdened with official paperwork, and daily engaged in the chaotic world of official documents, the Master's talents were not fully displayed? Or is it that the Master's scholarly pursuits were not fully realized due to the distractions of official life? Thus, the Master was placed in a position of inactivity, hindered in his opportunities and thus aging in obscurity; he dedicated himself to refining history and the classics, focusing his mind and refining his learning. The Master's talents and learning were not only prominently displayed in his own time but also had a lasting impact, not only widely proclaimed in his time but also permanently recorded in history. If so, then heaven places them in positions for the wise and noble, and their intentions are indeed very earnest! At first, one feels special regret for the Master, but later, one no longer feels regret, but instead feels fortunate for the Master. The Master can indeed have no regrets.

    702

    同治七年六月下浣，张葆諴虞阶氏序。

    　　序

    　　说部书，唐宋人尚已；近今则蒲留仙《聊斋志异》，怡心悦目，殆移我情，不厌百回读也。其叙事委曲详尽而不嫌琐屑，其选词典赡风华而不病文胜，其用笔轻倩波俏而不失纤巧。其奇想天开，凭空结撰，陆离光怪，出人意表，而不得谓事所必无，以乌有子虚目之。向以为绝调独弹，殆寡和矣。辛酉夏，余于役历下，得解君子镜所著《益智录》八册，细读一过，而惊留仙有嗣响也。

    Explanation

    702

    In the sixth month of the seventh year of the Tongzhi era, Zhang Baoxian, also known as Yu Jie, wrote this preface.

    Preface

    Narrative fiction was already established during the Tang and Song dynasties; in recent times, Pu Songling's Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio pleases the heart and eyes, almost changing one's feelings, and can be read a hundred times without tiring. Its narrative is detailed and intricate without being tedious, its choice of words rich and elegant without being overly ornate, and its writing style graceful and playful without being overly delicate. Its imaginative and creative aspects, its fantastical and unexpected elements, and its ability to make the impossible seem possible, without dismissing it as mere fiction, were once considered a unique and unparalleled style, few of which could match it. In the summer of 1871, while serving in Jinan, I obtained eight volumes of Yizhi Lu (益智录) written by Junzi Jing, read it carefully once through, and was surprised to find that Yizhi Lu is a worthy successor to Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio.

    703

    同年友叶芸士廉访谓其为书“无一肮脏语，无一轻薄语，劝善惩淫，一轨于正”，大异乎《聊斋》之呵神詈鬼，以抒其抑郁牢骚之气者，斯言当矣。顾余尤喜其逼肖留仙，而无刻意规摹之迹，是真善学前贤而遗貌取神者。亟宜付梓，以公同好，抑以知操觚为文，师古非袭古也。解君具如此才华，博一青衿不可得，训蒙乡曲，今已垂老，而托心豪素，绝无几微不平之鸣犯其笔端，其学与养为何如矣！学士读书稽古，怀才不遇，即游戏文章，亦足立言不朽，如芸士谓为若劝若惩，有功世道云云者。吾知君雅不欲以斯录自见，而斯录未尝不可以见君；斯录不足以传君，而君固将以斯录传也。质之芸士，当不河汉斯言。

    Explanation

    703

    A fellow scholar and friend, Ye Yunshi, the imperial inspector, remarked about the book: "It contains not a single vulgar or disrespectful word, not a single frivolous or disrespectful word, and encourages good deeds while punishing immorality, all in line with propriety." This is greatly different from Liaozhai’s scolding of gods and ghosts to vent its frustration and discontent. This statement is correct. However, I particularly appreciate how closely it resembles Liaozhai without the traces of deliberate imitation. It is truly a good learner who follows the spirit of the ancients rather than their form. It should be quickly published to share with fellow enthusiasts, and also to understand that writing with a brush is to learn from the ancients but not to copy them.

    Xie, possessing such talent, cannot obtain even a minor official rank, and now teaches in the countryside, on the verge of old age. Yet, he entrusts his heart to the pen, with not a hint of dissatisfaction or complaint in his writing. How profound his learning and cultivation must be! Scholars read and study ancient texts, yet often have talent but are not recognized. Even light-hearted writings can be lasting, as Yunshi said, serving to encourage and admonish, benefiting the world. I know that you prefer not to be known through this collection, yet this collection can still reveal you. This collection is not sufficient to transmit your legacy, but you will indeed be transmitted through this collection. To verify with Yunshi, these words will not be found exaggerated.

    704

    咸丰辛酉夏至后十日，滨州杜乔羽筠巢甫识。

    　　序

    　　自经史以逮诸子百家，其立言不同，而大旨要归劝善惩恶而已。顾正言之或不入，不如喻言之之易入也；庄言之或不听，不如诡言之之动听也。此稗官野史有时亦与经传相发明也。辛酉秋，解君子镜访余于济南讲舍，出所著《益智录》见示。适值逆氛不静，匆匆旋里，未遑卒读。壬戌春，仍返历下，始细读之，叹其寄意之深且远也。士君子乘时得位，往往于文翰无所表见，当时则荣，没则已焉。

    Explanation

    704

    On the tenth day after the summer solstice in the Xin You year of the Xianfeng era, in Binzhou, Du Qiaoyu, known as Junchao, wrote this.

    Preface

    From the annals and histories to the various schools of thought and the myriad works of the Hundred Schools, though their statements differ, their overarching purpose is to encourage virtue and punish wickedness. However, direct and straightforward words may not always be effective; it is easier for people to accept advice conveyed through analogy. Similarly, solemn and serious words may not always be listened to, whereas words that seem deceptive or unconventional often have a greater impact. This is why unofficial histories and folk tales sometimes shed light on the truths found in the classics. In the autumn of Xin You, Gentleman Junjing from Jie visited me at my lecture hall in Jinan, presenting his work The Record of Wisdom for my perusal. At the time, the atmosphere was unsettled, and I had to return home in haste, leaving me unable to read it thoroughly. In the spring of Ren Xu, I returned to Lixia, where I finally read it in detail, admiring the depth and breadth of its underlying meaning. Scholars and gentlemen who seize the opportunity to attain positions of power often find little expression of their ideas in their writings; they may be honored during their lifetimes, but their legacies fade after their deaths.

    705

    即或有志著述，而摭拾诸儒之语录，猎取考据之陈言，令人读不终篇，辄思掩卷。又其甚者，搜隐怪而有悖于经常，骋妍词而不止乎礼义。冀其感人心而维风化也难矣！斯录也，远绍《搜神》、《述异》、《齐谐》志怪之编，近仿《聊斋志异》之作，笔墨虽近游戏，而一以劝惩为主，殆主文谲谏之流欤！所谓与经传相发明者，其在斯与？论诗者谓穷而后工，解君怀才不遇，藉此以抒其怀抱，固宜其文之工也。是录一出，将见洛阳纸贵。其终湮没不彰耶，较取科名登月无仕者，所获固已多矣，何憾哉？

    Explanation

    705

    Even if one has the will to write, gathering and compiling the sayings of scholars, seeking out outdated scholarly opinions, can make one read no more than a few pages and immediately want to close the book. Those who go even further, searching for strange and unusual things that go against the norm, displaying beautiful words without stopping at propriety and righteousness, hope to touch hearts and maintain social norms, but this is difficult! This collection, far tracing the tradition of strange tales from Search for the Supernatural, Record of the Strange, and Qi H

    706

    同治元年秋七月，利津李佐贤序。

    　　序

    　　自经传而外，有《史记》，有诸子书，有百家言，又有五才子及杂著纪说，纷纷不一。其间之博洽者，足以益人之心思；怪异者，足以骇人之耳目。大抵不外乎情与义而已。其工于言情者，凡喜怒哀乐之所发，或合情之正，或溺情之偏，莫不各肖其事。迨夫因情而适于善，徇情而罹于恶，是皆情之所至，而有不可变移者。故言之娓娓，不究其终不止也，至性之本初与后勿论也。其善于言义者，凡纲常伦理之所在，内而系于家庭，外而关乎廊庙，莫不各著其宜。迨夫行义而由于智，守义而蹈于愚，亦皆义之所迫，而有不能推诿者。故道之谆谆，不征其实不已也。至理之当与否，勿论也。

    Explanation

    706

    In the first month of autumn in the first year of the Tongzhi era, Li Zaoxian of Lixian wrote the preface.

    Preface

    Beyond the classics and historical records, there are the Shiji (Records of the Grand Historian), works by various philosophers, writings by many schools of thought, and the Five Masters along with miscellaneous writings and narratives, all diverse and numerous. Among these, those that are broad and profound are sufficient to enrich one's mind; those that are strange and unusual are sufficient to startle one's senses. Generally speaking, they do not go beyond emotion and righteousness. Those skilled in expressing emotions, all expressions of joy, anger, sorrow, and happiness, either conforming to the proper expression of emotion or being overly indulgent in emotion, all vividly depict their respective events. When following emotion leads to good, and when indulging in emotion leads to evil, all are the result of emotion reaching its limit, and are unchangeable. Therefore, they speak eloquently, and one cannot stop listening until the end; the inherent nature and subsequent changes are not discussed. Those skilled in expressing righteousness, all that pertains to the norms of conduct and ethical principles, internally connected to the family, externally related to the state, all clearly demonstrate their appropriateness. When acting righteously due to wisdom, and when adhering to righteousness despite foolishness, all are compelled by righteousness, and cannot be shirked. Therefore, they speak earnestly, and one cannot stop until the truth is fully understood. The correctness of the truth is not discussed.

    707

    吾邑同砚友解子镜者，赋性深淳，为人朴实，博学能文，工诗善赋，最嗜古文，不摩时艺，因托幽情以舒啸，欲寄遐思于离奇，乃作《益智录》。书成，携以示余。余反覆披阅，见其情意缠绵，词理清晰。而言之典雅者，摭拾群经；事之荒唐者，胚胎诸子。言情则如胶似漆，言义则截铁斩钉。有者无之，无者有之，随其意之所及，以抒其口之欲言。虽妖魔狐蛊，牛鬼蛇神，莫不齐赴腕下。鎔经铸史，摛藻扬芳，有莫可名言者矣！至于叙事，其来也突如，其去也悠然，笔法之妙，犹其末焉者耳。解子僻处乡隅，人或以孤陋寡闻目之；困阻黉序，人或以才钝识浅议之。为是书，固借以抒其郁闷，要亦准之情与义焉。斯录虽亚于《聊斋》，实与《聊斋》笔墨无异，固足益人神智，而尤篇篇寓劝惩之意，凡无关世道人心者，概不诠录。余是以乐为之序。

    Explanation

    707

    A Fellow Scholar from My Hometown Named Xie Zijing

    A fellow scholar from my hometown, Xie Zijing, has a deep and sincere nature and is honest and simple. He is well-versed in literature, skilled in poetry and prose, and has a strong preference for classical literature over contemporary trends. He uses classical literature to vent deep feelings and寄托远思于奇异之事。 He collects from various classics and draws inspiration from different schools of thought. His emotional expressions are very close, and his logical expressions are clear and decisive. What exists may not exist, and what does not exist may exist, as he expresses his thoughts and desires as they come to mind. All kinds of supernatural beings and creatures, whether they exist or not, are used in his writing. He melts and casts the classics and history, and spreads beauty and fragrance, with some things that cannot be described in words. As for narrative techniques, events come suddenly and leave gracefully, and the skill of writing is just a minor aspect. Xie Zijing lives in a remote area, where people might consider him ignorant and poorly informed. He is hindered in his studies, where people might consider him slow-witted and lacking in knowledge. He writes this book to vent his frustrations and adhere to principles and morals. This record, although inferior to Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio, is actually similar in style. It is sufficient to enhance people's wisdom and particularly contains moral teachings in every chapter. Anything unrelated to the world and human nature is not included. I am happy to write the preface for it.

    708

    咸丰岁次甲寅小阳春书于拙逸轩，愚弟黄南宾琴轩甫拜撰。

    　　序

    　　夫人之传奇著说，每隐匿其名以泄其忿，或暗藏其事以抒其怀，使后人阅者，艳其词之秀丽，赏其笔之英豪，而于世道人心毫无关系，此最足为文人之大戒也。子镜解子，余同村故交也。少时苦志诗书，未获拾芥；晚岁留心风化，常欲传薪。每于教读之馀，著有《益智录》数卷。凡所见所闻，无不随手抄录，而于忠孝节义之事，更一一详细叙明，使阅者触目警心，天良自动。是于诗教之劝善惩恶之旨，大有体会，其变化世道人心之微意，岂浅鲜哉！如谓叙事之详明，用笔之奇绝，非所以识解子也。是为序。

    Explanation

    708

    Written in the Simple Leisure Pavilion during the small spring of the year Jia Yin in the Xianfeng era, respectfully penned by your humble brother Huang Nanbin, styled Qin Xuan.

    Preface

    When it comes to legendary and fictional narratives, authors often conceal their names to vent their anger or subtly include events to express their feelings. This allows later readers to admire the beauty of the words and the grandeur of the writing, while having no relation to the moral and ethical state of society. This is the greatest warning for writers. Subi is an old acquaintance from my village. In his youth, he strove hard in poetry and books, but did not achieve much; in his later years, he paid attention to moral and social norms, often wishing to continue the tradition. Whenever he had spare time from teaching, he wrote several volumes of The Record of Wisdom. Everything he saw and heard, he recorded promptly, and he detailed the stories of loyalty, filial piety, righteousness, and integrity, so that readers would be shocked into conscience and their innate goodness would be aroused. This deeply embodies the essence of the moral teachings in poetry, promoting good and punishing evil, and subtly conveys the intention to change the moral and ethical state of society, which is not trivial! If one were to focus on the clarity of the narrative and the uniqueness of the writing style, it would not be to understand Subi. This is the preface.

    709

    咸丰五年秋八月，同邑春卿弟吴炳荣谨识。

    　　序

    　　历下解子镜，高士也。博学工诗，雅好古文，不作时艺。生平慕蒲留仙之为人，因仿其笔墨，作《益智录》一书。其文光怪陆离，其词清新俊逸。其写情则缠绵悱恻，其演义则慷慨激昂。其论忠孝也，则易感发人心；其谈节烈也，则可维持世道；其搜神谈鬼也，则能新人闻见；其谈玄纪异也，则足豁人心思。以鸾翔凤翥之笔，写神出鬼没之文，正不徒篇篇锦绣，字字珠玑，止以富丽为工也。奈具此才华而不乐仕进，高卧林泉，以著述自娱，其识度学力，有非时儒所能及者。乙卯仲夏，以所作《益智录》示予，予反复披阅，不禁击节叹赏，而谨为跋语焉。并赠《鹧鸪天》词一首：“胸有才华故欲鸣，书成能使鬼神惊。全凭笔墨传奇异，半借文章写不平。谈节烈，纪忠贞，维持风化写深情。留仙已逝倩谁续，续补《聊斋》《益智》成。”

    Explanation

    709

    In the fifth year of the Xianfeng era, the eighth month of autumn, my fellow townsman and younger brother Chunqing, Wu Bingrong, respectfully records.

    Preface

    Jie Zijing of Lixia is a high-minded scholar. Well-versed and skilled in poetry, he has a refined taste for classical prose and does not compose contemporary literary works. Throughout his life, he admired the character of Pu Lixuan and therefore imitated his writing style, writing a book called Yizhi Lu. His writing is colorful and bizarre, and his words are fresh and elegant. When he writes about emotions, it is tender and poignant; when he narrates stories, it is full of passion and enthusiasm. When he discusses loyalty and filial piety, it easily moves people's hearts; when he talks about chastity and loyalty, it can uphold the world's morals; when he searches for gods and talks about ghosts, it can introduce new knowledge to people; when he discusses the mysterious and records strange things, it can broaden people's minds. With the brush of a phoenix soaring, he writes texts that are mysterious and elusive. It is not just that every page is like brocade and every word is like a pearl, but rather it is not solely for the sake of richness and beauty. Unfortunately, with such talent, he does not enjoy pursuing officialdom and lives in seclusion in the mountains and forests, entertaining himself with writing. His insight, breadth of knowledge, and scholarly ability are beyond the reach of contemporary scholars. In the middle of summer in the year of Yimao, he showed me the Yizhi Lu he had written. I read it repeatedly and could not help but applaud and admire it, and respectfully wrote a preface. He also gave me a poem titled Zhengugutian: "With talent in his chest, he wishes to express himself. The book can make ghosts and gods amazed. Completely relies on the brush to tell extraordinary tales, half relies on writing to express grievances. Talks about chastity and loyalty, records steadfastness, upholds social norms and writes with deep emotion. Pu Lixuan has passed away, who will continue? Continue and complete Liaozhai and Yizhi."

    710

    侯功震百里拜读。

    　　序

    　　壬子春，余设帐于郡城之北鄙，获交防如盖君。落落空斋，村居无聊，每于功课之馀，剪烛烹茶，邀防如作竟夜之谈。一日，以解君子镜所著《益智录》示余。余披阅之下，始知先生为历下名流，一时宿儒，而命薄时蹇，试辄不售。于是绝意功名，授童蒙于黄台；殚心著作，富搜罗于青箱。虽街歌巷议，传之即为美谈；而目见耳闻，著手皆成佳话。以满腹绣虎之才，拘来社鬼；拈一管生花之笔，写彼城狐。乃牵萝补屋，惟知安夫清贫；而哀雁悲蛩，藉此抒其怀抱。嗟嗟！先生之才若此其富，先生之遇若彼之啬。先生之境益苦，而先生之书不自觉其益工矣！故其谋篇也，长而不冗，短而不促；其造句也，整而且练；其运典也，俗不伤雅。而其劝善惩恶之意，其有功于世道人心，岂浅鲜哉！余以为，以是书为消遣释闷之具也可，即以是书为牖民觉世之文亦无不可。如徒以游戏笔墨视此书，其亦失先生作书之旨也已。

    Explanation

    710

    Lord of Power Resonating a Hundred Li, I respectfully read.

    Preface

    In the spring of Rénzǐ, I set up my teaching in the northern outskirts of the prefectural city and befriended Master Fangru. My solitary study in the village was dull, and during the spare time after lessons, I would cut the candle and cook tea, inviting Master Fangru for a long night of conversation. One day, he showed me the Record of Wisdom authored by Gentleman Jingzi. Upon reading it, I learned that Master was a renowned figure from Lixia, a respected scholar of his time, yet his fate was thin and his times were difficult, and he failed in his examinations. Thus, he gave up on official success and taught young students at Huangtai; he devoted himself fully to writing, gathering a rich collection. Though street songs and alley talks passed it on as a beautiful story, what he saw and heard turned into beautiful tales. With a full heart of talented abilities, he brought a local spirit; with a pen that blooms like flowers, he wrote about a cunning person. He mended his house with vines, only knowing contentment with poverty; and with the mournful sounds of geese and insects, he used this to express his aspirations. Alas! The master's talent is so rich, and the master's fate is so stingy. The master's situation is even more bitter, yet the master's book unconsciously becomes even more skillful! Therefore, in his composition, it is long but not redundant, short but not abrupt; in his sentence construction, it is well-organized and polished; in his use of allusions, it is common but not harming elegance. And in his intention to encourage good and punish evil, his benefit to the world's morals and people's hearts is not shallow! I believe, this book can be a tool for entertainment and relieving boredom, and it can also be literature that enlightens the people and awakens the world. If one views this book only as playful writing, one already loses the master's purpose in writing.

    711

    咸丰丙辰十一月朔，平陵亦山尹述甫书于棠华礎馆。

    　　序

    　　《诗》亡然后《春秋》作。《诗》主美刺，《春秋》主褒贬，其义一也。自是以降，惟紫阳《通鉴纲目》懔懔然远承笔削之旨，上昭天道，下翼人伦，所关于人心世运者，良非浅鲜。尝考著书之家，如道家、释家、法家、名家、农家、兵家、医家、纵横家，莫不各抒所见，自成一家之言。而于劝惩之义，则概未有闻。说部中如《搜神记》、《述异记》、《续齐谐记》、《神异经》、《十洲记》、《高士传》、《神仙传》、《洞冥记》、《英雄记抄》、《穆天子传》、《武帝内传》、《飞燕外传》、《杂事秘辛》、《辍耕录》、《云仙散录》、《湘山野录》，皆足广见闻，纪风土，补史乘，资谭笑矣。然而述奇怪则满纸螺亭鼠国，谈神仙则一篇玉液丹砂，夸智谋则使我心惊舌咋，写娟丽则令人目駴情摇。

    Explanation

    711

    On the first day of the eleventh month in the year of Bingchen during the Xianfeng era, the author Yishan Yishufu wrote this at Tanghua Jiuguan in Pingling.

    Preface

    After the decline of the Shijing (Book of Songs), the Chunqiu (Spring and Autumn Annals) was written. The Shijing focuses on praise and criticism, while the Chunqiu focuses on commendation and censure; their meanings are one and the same. Since then, only the Zizhi Tongjian Gangmu (Comprehensive Mirror for Aid in Government: Outline and Commentary) by Ziyang (referring to Zhu Xi) has solemnly inherited the spirit of the Chunqiu, clarifying heavenly principles and supporting human relationships. Its impact on human hearts and the course of the world is indeed profound. I have studied the works of various schools of thought, such as Daoism, Buddhism, Legalism, Mohism, Agrarianism, Military Strategy, Medicine, and Diplomacy. Each of these schools has expressed its own views and formed its own body of knowledge. However, none of them have extensively discussed the principles of encouragement and punishment. Works like Soushen Ji (Record of Searching for the Gods), Shuyi Ji (Record of Strange Events), Xu Qihai Ji (Continuation of the Harmonious Records), Shenyijing (Classic of Strange Supernatural Phenomena), Shizhou Ji (Record of the Ten Continents), Gaoshi Zhuan (Biographies of High-minded Men), Shenxian Zhuan (Biographies of Immortals), Dongming Ji (Record of the Dark and Mysterious), Yingxiong Jichao (Collection of Biographies of Heroes), Mutianzi Zhuan (Biography of the Duke of Mu), Wudi Neizhuan (Inner Biography of Emperor Wu), Feiyan Waizhuan (Outer Biography of Feiyan), Zashi Mixin (Secret Affairs of Miscellaneous Events), Chuogeng Lu (Record of Pausing to Plow), Yunxian Sanlu (Record of Clouds and Immortals), and Xiangshan Yelu (Record of Wild Tales from Xiangshan) are all sufficient to broaden one's knowledge, record local customs, supplement historical records, and provide entertainment. However, they often describe strange phenomena with tales of spiral towers and mouse kingdoms, discuss immortals with descriptions of jade elixirs and cinnabar, boast about strategies that make one's heart race and jaw drop, and depict beauty that startles the eyes and stirs the emotions.

    712

    至于令见之者生慕，闻之者怀惭，刻薄者识偏私之无用，诡谲者悔机械之徒劳，则亦概未有闻。于礏！著书非难，著书而有裨于人心世运则难矣。解君子镜，嗜学士也，名场征逐，垂四十年而未博一衿，晚乃屏弃帖括，以游戏笔墨为娱。庚申春，以所著《益智录》见示，其间忠臣孝子、名士美人，以及夫妇之愚，禽虫之细，纲常之重大，日用之琐微，奇缘怪遇，鬼邪狐妖，靡不毕具。俾读之者有时而慕，有时而惭；有时而惧涉于偏私，有时而恐邻于机械。乃知解君非以笔墨为游戏，直以笔墨为补救也。于礏！著书无可贵，著书而有裨于人心世运如解君者，则诚可贵矣。或曰是仿《聊斋志异》而为之者；或曰是仿《聊斋志异》而为之，并不亚于《聊斋志异》者；或曰是仿《聊斋志异》而为之，尚不及乎《聊斋志异》者。要之，是录自有足以不朽者，无事鳃鳃焉与《聊斋志异》絜短较长也。

    　　愚弟郑锡麟拜题。

    Explanation

    712

    As for those who see it feeling admiration, those who hear it feeling shame, those who are harsh recognizing the uselessness of partiality, and those who are cunning regretting the futility of scheming, such things are generally unheard of. Indeed! Writing a book is not difficult, but writing a book that benefits human hearts and the course of the world is difficult. Jiejun Jing, a lover of learning, spent forty years competing in the arena of fame and success without obtaining even a single degree. Later, he abandoned the study of the classics and took pleasure in writing as a pastime. In the spring of the Gengshen year, he presented his work, "Yizhi Lu," which includes loyal ministers, filial sons, famous scholars, and beautiful women, as well as the foolishness of husband and wife, the triviality of birds and insects, the importance of the fundamental principles of society, the minutiae of daily life, strange and wonderful encounters, and ghosts, evil spirits, fox demons, and other supernatural beings. It is all there. Those who read it sometimes feel admiration, sometimes feel shame; sometimes fear being involved in partiality, sometimes fear being close to scheming. Thus, it is known that Jiejun is not just writing for the sake of writing as a pastime, but to use writing as a remedy. Indeed! Writing a book has no inherent value, but a book that benefits human hearts and the course of the world, like Jiejun's, is truly valuable. Some say it imitates "Liaozhai Zhiyi" in style; others say it is not inferior to "Liaozhai Zhiyi"; still others say it is not as good as "Liaozhai Zhiyi." In summary, this record has its own merit for immortality, and there is no need to compare it with "Liaozhai Zhiyi" in terms of length or quality.

    The humble younger brother, Zhang Xilin, respectfully dedicates this translation.

    713

    序

    　　岁在庚申，余奉讳归籍。适与于团练事，因识解子子镜，盖宿学而未遇者也。日者，出所著《益智录》八卷示余，余受而读之。其笔墨全仿《聊斋志异》，而大旨则归于劝善惩恶。其中言孝弟节义处，剀切悱恻，可歌可泣，可以裨人心，可以补世道，诚渡世之宝筏，非寻常说部家可比也。吾因之奇解子矣！士君子不得志于时，往往以其抑塞磊落之气，形诸笔墨间。故其发为文也，愤时嫉俗，呵神詈鬼，藉题抒写，以自鸣其不平。虽咄咄逼人，亦自雄视一时，而劝惩之义无裨焉。今解子之文，卓卓可传如此，乃终身不得青其衿，可不谓数奇欤！及其立言，则心和气平，有箴规之意，无愤惫之私，款款曲曲，沁人心脾，其读书养气之功，殆有加人一等者乎？抑余又有说者。使解子而置身通显，吾恐一行作吏，此事遂废，又安能优游翰墨，得此奇文共赏耶？故有留仙之不遇，而《聊斋》以显；有解子之不遇，而《益智录》以显。解子亦可以无憾已！

    Explanation

    713

    序

    In the year of Gengshen, I returned to my hometown due to a family bereavement. Coincidentally, I became involved in local militia affairs, and through this, I met Xie Zi, a scholar of long standing who had not yet found his fortune. One day, he presented me with his eight-volume work, Yizhi Lu. I received and read it. His writing style is entirely imitative of Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio, but the main purpose is to encourage good deeds and punish evil. In the sections that discuss filial piety, fraternal love, loyalty, and righteousness, the writing is profoundly moving and emotionally touching, evoking both song and tears. It can benefit the heart and improve the world, truly a precious raft to cross the sea of life, not comparable to ordinary works of fiction. I am thus amazed by Xie Zi! Scholars and gentlemen who are not successful in their time often express their frustration and integrity through their writing. Therefore, when they write, they resent the times and are disgusted by the world, scolding gods and cursing ghosts, using the subject matter to vent their grievances and assert their own sense of injustice. Though their writing may be forceful and impressive, it is also self-aggrandizing, but it lacks the moral purpose of encouraging and punishing. Now, Xie Zi's writing is so outstanding and worthy of being passed down, yet he never achieved official recognition. Can this not be considered a matter of fate? When he writes, however, his heart is calm and his tone is even, with a sense of admonition and guidance, free from the bitterness of frustration. His writing is deeply and intricately crafted, penetrating the heart and lungs. His dedication to reading and self-cultivation is truly exceptional. Yet, I have another point to make. If Xie Zi had achieved official success, I fear that once he became a government official, such writing might have ceased, and how could he have leisurely engaged in literature and enjoyed this extraordinary work? Thus, just as Pu Songling's lack of success led to the fame of Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio, Xie Zi's lack of success led to the fame of Yizhi Lu. Xie Zi can have no regrets.

    714

    咸丰十年十二月，同邑紫峰弟孙官云拜读。

    　　序

    　　士君子不得志于时者，往往发愤著书，以抒其生平所蓄积。其考经证史，博综古今，成一家之言，足广学识而备采择，尚已。次则搜罗异事，编辑琐言，于愤世嫉俗之中，寓善善恶恶之意，虽事多子虚，词非典要，然无损于己，有益于人，犹胜于俪白妃红，雕云镂月，浪费笔墨而已也。小说家言，起于汉晋，而盛于唐宋，自《冥洞》、《搜神》而下，其名更仆难数。近今则《聊斋志异》一书脍炙人口，嗣是作者如林，虽各有所长，要皆出于蓝而不必胜于蓝也。余幼嗜异闻，凡诸异之书，无不窃取观之，家所无者，多方假借。大抵互有短长，未易轩轾。后得河间纪晓岚先生《阅微草堂》五种，见其寓庄于谐，约奇于正，叙事则简而明，言情则隽而雅。

    Explanation

    714

    December of the tenth year of the Xianfeng era, Sun Guanyun, the younger brother of Zifeng from the same county, respectfully reads.

    Preface

    Scholars and gentlemen who are not successful in their time often write with great effort and determination to express their accumulated thoughts and feelings throughout their lives. Some study and verify the classics and histories, broadly synthesize ancient and modern knowledge, and form one's own unique perspective, thereby broadening knowledge and preparing for selection, which is commendable. Others collect strange stories and compile trivial remarks, expressing the intention of praising good and condemning evil within their criticism of the world and disgust with society. Although many of these stories may be fictional and the language not strictly classical, they are not harmful to oneself but beneficial to others, and are still preferable to ornate and flowery writing, intricate and decorative prose, and the mere waste of ink and paper. Fictional writing originated in the Han and Jin dynasties and flourished in the Tang and Song dynasties. From "Mingtong" and "Soushen" onward, the number of such works is too numerous to count. In recent times, the book "Liaozhai Zhiyi" has been widely praised and enjoyed, and afterward, there were many authors. Although each has its strengths, all are derived from the same source but not necessarily superior to it. I loved strange tales from a young age and sought out all such books, borrowing from many sources when they were not available at home. Generally, each work has its strengths and weaknesses, making them difficult to judge or compare. Later, I obtained the five works of Ji Xiaolan, titled "Yuewei Caotang," and found that they express seriousness through humor, present the unusual in a conventional manner, narrate simply and clearly, and express emotions in a refined and elegant manner.

    715

    在先生则为游戏之作，在读者已获药石之益。始知稗官小说，以大手笔为之，其异人固如是也。解君子镜，余应童子试时所识也。通籍后，不见者廿馀年。咸丰己未，余引疾家居，忽来访，出其所著《益智录》示余，且索序。余尝窃论之：《聊斋》善摹情景，抽密呈妍，穷形尽相，才子之文也。其间嬉笑怒骂，无所不有，可为劝惩者十之三。《阅微草堂》则善谈理致，牛毛茧细，推敲辨晰，期于理得心安而后已，著作家之文也。不矜雕饰，不事穿凿，可为劝惩者十之七。暇日得竟览解君之书，见其尽态极妍也，则有似于《聊斋》，而虚白道人评语，则不悖于《阅微草堂》之旨也。岂徒矜奇吊诡，取悦俗目云尔哉？亦可以传矣！至解君之怀才不遇，诸序已详言之，兹不赘。

    Explanation

    715

    In the author's view, this is a playful work, but for the reader, it provides great benefit. Only now do I realize that popular historical narratives, when written by a masterful hand, can be truly extraordinary. Mr. Jie Junjing, whom I knew during my youth when I took the preliminary examinations, has not been seen for over twenty years since he entered officialdom. In the year 1859 of the Xianfeng era, I took leave on grounds of illness and stayed at home, when he unexpectedly visited and presented his work, Yizhi Lu, asking for a preface. I have privately commented on it: Liaozhai Zhiyi excels in depicting scenes, revealing hidden beauty, and portraying every detail, making it the writing of a talented scholar. Within it, there are laughter, jokes, scolding, and criticism, with about one-third serving as moral instruction. Yuewei Caotang Biji, on the other hand, skillfully discusses principles, with extreme detail and careful analysis, striving to achieve logical satisfaction and peace of mind, making it the work of a scholarly author. It does not boast of elaborate decoration or forced innovation, yet about seven-tenths of it serves as moral instruction. During my leisure time, I finally read through Mr. Jie's book and found it fully displays all its beauty, much like Liaozhai Zhiyi. The comments by the pseudonym "Xubai Dao Ren" do not contradict the principles of Yuewei Caotang Biji. Is it merely to boast of strange and mysterious tales to please the common eye? No, it can also be passed down! As for Mr. Jie's talent and unrecognized status, other prefaces have already explained it in detail, so I will not repeat it here.

    716

    愚弟杨福祺拜读。

    　　序

    　　士君子不得志于时，往往愤世嫉俗，激昂慷慨，作不平之鸣。盖有所蕴于中，而不得施之于世，每假文章以泄抑塞磊落之奇。或牛鬼蛇神，创为谬悠之论；或妃青俪白，好为媟亵之词。浅学者虽惊心骇目，而识者叹其才之大，而惜其理之不醇。故《搜神》、《述异》而外，说部无虑数十百种，求其中正和平，合于劝善惩恶之旨者，卒不多得。余友解君子镜，于书无所不窥，下笔辄数千言不懈而及于古。顾性不慕荣利，早岁亦尝业帖括，逐名场，不数年即舍去，惟以著述自娱。设帐黄台山寺，空斋萧然，百氏坐拥，泊如也。

    Explanation

    716

    Translation:

    Respectfully, your younger brother Yang Fugui presents this.

    Preface

    Scholars and gentlemen who are unsuccessful in their careers often resent the world and are disgusted with society, expressing their passions and enthusiasm through cries of injustice. They have something deeply felt within them that they cannot apply to the world, and thus often use writing to vent their pent-up and noble talents. Some create strange and bizarre theories, others mix different elements and write lewd and indecent words. While superficial scholars may be shocked and startled, those with discernment admire their great talent but regret that their reasoning is not pure. Therefore, outside of works like The Search for Gods and Records of the Strange, there are dozens or even hundreds of such works. However, it is rare to find writings that are moderate and harmonious, in line with the purpose of encouraging good and punishing evil. My friend Xie Junjing has read everything, and when he writes, he produces thousands of words at a time, tirelessly reaching back to ancient times. However, he does not seek fame and profit, and in his early years, he also studied the imperial examination texts, pursuing success in the scholarly arena. Within a few years, he gave it up, only enjoying writing for his own amusement. He teaches at the Huangtai Mountain Temple, living in a simple and empty study, surrounded by many books, calm and detached.

    717

    岁丙辰，有《益智录》之作，奇闻异说，随笔条记，方数卷耳。癸亥冬，余奉讳旋里，闻所著已裒然成集。携以相示，快睹全豹。中载孝子悌弟，义士节妇，以及鬼狐妖邪，旁搜博采，详哉其言之。其微显阐幽也，使人可感可兴；其缠绵悱恻也，使人欲歌欲泣；其穷形尽相也，如铸鼎象物，罔两毕现；其传神写照也，如优孟衣冠，声情逼肖。奇而不诡于正，变而不失其常，而总不外乎劝惩者，近是。至若谬悠之论，媟亵之词，与夫肆口低讥，藉抒愤懑之语，概乎其未有，诚有如自序所云者。于此见其学之醇，而其品之优也。此书一出，俾世之读者，隐戢其邪，匿变诈之心，而共返于正直平康之路，则其为功于世道人心也，岂有极哉！

    Explanation

    717

    In the year Bingchen, there was the creation of Yizhi Lu, a collection of strange tales and unusual sayings, recorded casually, amounting to just a few volumes. In the winter of Guihai, I returned home out of filial piety, and upon hearing that the work had been compiled into a substantial collection, I carried it to show me, and I was delighted to see the whole leopard. The book contains accounts of filial sons and respectful brothers, righteous men and chaste women, as well as ghosts, fox spirits, and demons, all gathered and recorded in great detail. It elucidates the subtle and reveals the hidden, moving and inspiring the reader; it is tender and poignant, evoking a desire to sing and weep; it depicts the form and features completely, like casting a bronze vessel to capture all its details; it captures the spirit and likeness, like the attire of Youmeng, with sound and emotion vividly portrayed. Strange but not deviating from the norm, changing but not losing its regularity, and always centered on encouragement and punishment, this is indeed the case. As for vague and unfounded theories, vulgar and indecent words, and the free and critical speech that vents anger and frustration, none of these are present, truly as described in the preface. From this we see the purity of his learning, and the excellence of his character. This book, once published, will make the readers of the world hide and restrain their evil, conceal deceitful intentions, and return together to the path of righteousness and peace. How can its contribution to the world and the hearts of people have an end?

    718

    同治三年岁次甲子秋八月，愚弟汪仲洵拜题。

    　　序

    　　盖闻川岳之灵，蓄之久者其气秀；而斯人之德，积之厚者其光华。此势所必至，理有固然也。予近村解子镜先生，业儒有年，学称富有。虽终身未拾一芥，而考其生平，想见其为人，德高闾里，品重乡党，殊非可以功名重轻者。洎乎晚年作有《益智录》一书，岂投闲置散，不得志于世，藉以吐其胸中之奇耶？及阅其书，而知其设心立意，无非讽劝斯人，曲为点化，皆于世道人心有关，非以闲情弄笔墨者比，譬诸清夜晨钟，发人深省。凡奇怪不经之事，悉举而归诸行著习察之常，为因为果，俾知戒惧，即慎独之义也；共识平情，即絜矩之道也。其言近，其旨远，牖民孔易，而其所以扶世立教者，不即于是乎在哉！至若布局之密，造句之工，运笔之妙，应浓以浓，应淡以淡，犹其外焉者也。吾愿阅是卷者，勿泥乎其事而取其文，勿僅取乎其文而原其心也。是为序。

    Explanation

    718

    In the autumn of August, in the year of the stem-branch "Jiazi" of the Tongzhi era (1864), I, your humble younger brother Wang Zhongxun, respectfully present this preface.

    Preface

    It is said that the spirits of mountains and rivers, when cultivated over a long period, become refined; and the virtues of a person, when deeply accumulated, shine brightly. This is a trend that must inevitably occur, and it is a natural principle. My esteemed elder Mr. Xie Zijing from the nearby village has been a scholar for many years and is renowned for his extensive learning. Although he never attained any official position, upon examining his life, one can infer his character. His virtue is highly esteemed in the village, and his moral standing is respected among his fellow townspeople. He is certainly not one to be judged by worldly success or failure. By the time he reached old age, he wrote a book titled "Yizhi Lu" (Record of Wisdom). Was it because he was placed in a position of idleness, unfulfilled in the world, that he used this book to express his unique insights? Upon reading the book, one realizes that his intentions were to advise and guide people, subtly enlightening them, all of which are relevant to the ways of the world and the hearts of people. It is not merely a pastime of idle writing. It is like the sound of a bell at dawn, awakening deep reflection. All strange and extraordinary events are explained through the lens of everyday observation, cause and effect, so that one may know to be cautious, which is the principle of being cautious when alone; it also promotes fairness and justice, which is the way of the square measure. Its words are close, but its meaning is profound, making it very easy to enlighten the people, and the reason it supports the world and establishes education, is not it right here? As for the intricate layout, the skillful sentence construction, the masterful use of the brush, the appropriate use of richness and simplicity, these are secondary considerations. I hope those who read this book will not be bound by the events and take away the text, nor merely take the text and trace its heart. This concludes the preface.

    719

    同治三年岁次甲子秋八月，世晚王廷槐植三拜读。

    　　序

    　　昔昌黎氏曰：“物不得其平则鸣。”至哉言乎！自六经四子外，凡有著作，有能外于此者乎？《左氏》、《国语》、《吕氏春秋》，迁之《记》、固之《书》，老、庄之玄奥，屈、宋之哀怨，他如曹、刘、潘、陆、颜、谢、李、杜之诗，莫不挹精华于典籍，抒愤懑于胸臆。礑扌离词力托和平，而揆其所由，实激昂感慨，有不能已于中者。至于稗官，上溯《齐谐》，下迨《聊斋》，皆一脉也。吾堂伯母解氏侄子镜者，有高世之才，无偶世之遇，年逾知命，而竟一芹莫采。彼仓之位置，将欲假之以鸣乎？假之以鸣，故亟使之不得其平。然遇虽不平，而发之于文，必使理得其平，以视夫徒矜笔妙、无关正义者，有迥殊焉。此其力扶名教，足以资人考镜、启人聪明者多矣。书名《益智》，其智固洞悉百家，而羽翼经传，不徒自炫才藻已也。天下智者见之，谓之智，殆即是录之定评欤？是为序。

    Explanation

    719

    Translation:

    In the third year of Tongzhi, the year of Jiazi, in the eighth month of autumn, Wang Tinghuai, a late student, pays his respects and reads.

    Preface

    The late Han Yu of Changli once said, "When things are not in harmony, they make a sound." How profound these words are! Beyond the Six Classics and the Four Books, is there any writing that can escape this principle? Zuo Zhuan, Guoyu, Lüshi Chunqiu, Sima Qian's Shiji, Ban Gu's Hanshu, the profound teachings of Laozi and Zhuangzi, the sorrowful and resentful expressions of Qu Yuan and Song Yu, as well as the poetry of Cao Cao, Liu Zhen, Pan Yue, Lu Ji, Yan Yan, Xie Lingyun, Li Bai, and Du Fu—all draw their essence from classical texts and express their pent-up emotions from the heart. Though the language is refined and conveys a sense of tranquility, when one considers the underlying reasons, it is filled with strong feelings of indignation and reflection, something that cannot be suppressed within. As for unofficial historians, tracing back to Qi Hua and extending down to Liaozhai Zhiyi, they all share a common thread. My cousin, Miss Xiejing, daughter of my aunt, possesses exceptional talent but has had no fortunate encounter with the world. Having passed the age of wisdom, she has remained unappreciated. Is her position meant to allow her to express herself? If so, it is made very difficult for her to achieve harmony. Yet, though her encounter is not harmonious, when she expresses herself through writing, she ensures that the reasoning is harmonious. Compared to those who merely boast about literary skill and are unrelated to righteousness, there is a distinct difference. Her efforts in supporting the norms of society are sufficient to provide a basis for examination and enlighten people. The book is titled Yizhi (Enlightening Wisdom). Its wisdom is thoroughly informed by all schools of thought, supporting and complementing the classics, not just showing off literary talent. Wise people from around the world will see it and call it wise, likely the definitive evaluation of this record. This is the preface.

    720

    咸丰六年重阳日，愚表弟笠民谭金诏谨识于都门济南馆舍。

    　　序

    　　昔人云“见驼峰谓马臃肿”，盖讥俗士眼光如豆也。不知六合之外，何奇不臻；百子之书，众美毕罗。尼山氏有所不语，岂必绝无者始不语哉？然而《搜神》志怪，《洞冥》述异，非不惊世骇俗也；刀山剑树，牛鬼蛇神，非不礒目怵心也。类皆矜奇吊诡，无裨事实，故罕觏其书。自《聊斋志异》出，而一切稗官野史咸为搁笔，后之说部莫有能登其堂哜其胾者。解子子镜，绩学士也，年逾知命，未尝领一衿。其磅礴郁积，无所发抒之意，每寓之于文，久之，裒然成帙，名曰《益智录》。乙卯秋，携以相示。

    Explanation

    720

    On the Chongyang Festival of the sixth year of the Xianfeng era, my humble cousin Lamin Tan Jinzhao respectfully records this in the guesthouse in Jinan, the capital city.

    Preface

    Ancient people said, "Seeing a camel's hump and thinking it is a fat horse," which is a criticism of the narrow-mindedness of common people. It is not known that beyond the six directions, what wonders are not reached; the books of a hundred scholars, all beauty is gathered. Confucius did not speak about certain things, but did he only refrain from speaking about things that did not exist? However, Soushen records strange things, Dongming narrates extraordinary events, which are not shocking to the world and the common people; the metaphorical expressions of terrifying and bizarre things, which are not startling the eyes and terrifying the heart. These are all boasting of strange and bizarre things, of no benefit to facts or reality, hence such books are rarely seen. Since the emergence of Liaozhai Zhiyi, all unofficial histories have been set aside, and later works cannot match its quality. Xie Zijing is a scholar of great learning, over fifty years old, never having obtained a degree. His overflowing thoughts and feelings, having nowhere to express them, often expressing them through writing. After a long time, they were gathered into a collection, named Yizhi Lu. In the autumn of the year of the Rabbit (1865), he brought it to show me.

    721

    余适养疴空斋，倚枕翻阅，半皆近时事，藉以驱睡魔，意得甚。惜余固陋，不足传解子，心窃憾焉。今夫怀才瑰异，致身通显者比比矣。否则，有所著述，藏诸名山，待其人焉可也；不然则驾名于王公巨卿，附骥尾以彰矣。余何人斯，乌能序解子书而传之哉？然尝读《志异》，见其驱遣庄、列，出入骚、雅，睥睨一世之豪杰，开拓万古之心胸，而传之久远。其书满家者，则于世道人心为兢兢焉。今是书，吾不知其文视留仙何若，而渡迷津，觉宝筏，犹是先生之遗意也。即以是质诸解子也可，敢序解子之书哉！

    　　同学愚弟梦蕉氏王履中盥题。

    　　序

    Explanation

    721

    I was recuperating in an empty study, leaning on my pillow and reading, mostly contemporary events, which I used to drive away the sleep demon, and I was very pleased. Alas, I am ignorant and limited, not worthy to transmit to Jiezi, and I secretly feel regret. Nowadays, those who possess extraordinary talent and attain high and prominent positions are numerous. Otherwise, if one has written works, one can hide them among famous mountains, waiting for the right person, which is acceptable; or one can ride on the reputation of high-ranking officials, following in their wake to be known. What kind of person am I, how could I write a preface for Jiezi's book and transmit it? However, I have read Strange Tales, and I see that it drives away and uses the works of Zhuang and Lie, enters and exits the works of Chu Ci and Shijing, looks down on the heroes of the world, opens up the hearts and minds of the ages, and has been transmitted for a long time. Those whose books fill their homes are cautious in the ways of the world and the hearts of people. This book now, I do not know how this text compares to Liusu, but to cross the misty river, to realize the treasure raft, still in the spirit of the master. Even to use this to question Jiezi, dare I write a preface for Jiezi's book?

    　　A fellow student and younger brother, Mengjiao Wang Lüzhong, respectfully inscribed.

    722

    余平时披览《聊斋》一书，见其心神所注，实关风会，未尝不拍案惊奇，赏其寓之善藏也。不意留仙先生于此书尚未著毕，而竟归诸梦寐，其所行于后世者，仅过半耳。惜无人焉起而续之，以成先生志也。时咸丰五年，余自历下归，路经黄台山，闻有书声出自庙堂，知其中有塾师在焉，遂进谒之。晋接之下，始知为历下子镜先生也。询有所作否，曰：“有。”遂出《益智录》草稿数卷以示余。余览之，心神恍惚，似归《聊斋》幻境。先生殆留仙转世乎？不然，何形神之想象若是耶？先生自名之曰《益智录》，非先生之智益，实有以益乎人之智也。名之曰“益智”，谁曰不然？凡我同人，借往观者，不可以风月主人、烟花总管而为先生律也。先生之为人，特达无求，名利不慕。积半生之困厄艰辛，发而为千百万言，以舒生平之志气，真先生之一片婆心耳！奈茫茫六道，多成藩溷之花；的的三山，幸存半江之露。知先生者，其在青山明月间乎？是为序。

    Explanation

    722

    In my leisure time, I peruse the book Liaozhai Zhiyi, and I am always struck by the depth of thought and emotion it conveys, which truly reflects the prevailing customs and trends of the era. I cannot help but bang the table in astonishment, admiring the skill with which its meanings are subtly embedded. Unexpectedly, Mr. Liuxian had not yet completed this book when he passed away, leaving only half of it for future generations. It is regrettable that no one has risen to continue it, to fulfill his intentions. It was in the fifth year of the Xianfeng era that I returned from Lixia, passing through Huangtai Mountain, where I heard the sound of reading coming from a temple. Knowing that there was a private tutor within, I paid him a visit. During our meeting, I learned that he was Mr. Zijing of Lixia. I asked if he had any writings, and he replied, "Yes." He then presented me with several volumes of the draft of Yizhilu. As I read it, my mind and spirit were in a state of daze, as if I had returned to the fantastical world of Liaozhai. Is Mr. Liuxian reborn? Otherwise, how could the imagination of form and spirit be so vivid? The master himself titled it Yizhilu, not to say that his own intelligence was enhanced, but rather that it truly serves to enhance the intelligence of others. To call it "beneficial to the mind," who would disagree? All of us who borrow it for reading should not judge it by the standards of a "moonlight master" or "flower master." The master's character is unique and free from desires, and he does not covet fame and wealth. He has expressed in countless words the hardships and difficulties of half a lifetime, to express his lifelong aspirations, truly with a heart full of compassion! Alas, in the vast six realms, there are many vulgar flowers; in the distant Three Mountains, only a half-river's dew remains. Those who understand the master, do they not reside in the green mountains and bright moonlight? This is the preface.

    723

    咸丰五年六月初三日书于山阴书屋。长山县李恩寿伯敷氏草。

    　　序

    　　《聊斋》一书，其事多子虚乌有，其文半牛鬼蛇神，说者谓《聊斋》之志荒矣。顾其事虽子虚乌有，而其理则白日青天；其文虽牛鬼蛇神，而其志则忠臣孝子。惟《聊斋》之志荒，斯《聊斋》之心苦。书所为继盲《传》腐《史》，称绝笔也。同邑友解子，绩学士也，工文善诗，尤癖好古文。脱使英年奋迹，俾出其所学以润色圣世太平，一时著作当必有与《聊斋》同工而异曲者。乃文章憎命，竟未得显其功名，迨晚岁，遂绝意进取，惟日以吟风弄月，笔墨游戏为娱。于是当课读之暇，爰仿《聊斋》体例而成《益智录》一书。夫是录之作也，本放达不羁之才，托荒唐不经之事，以发其郁勃不平之气。是录之所以作，即《聊斋》之所以作。是书也，殆真有与《聊斋》异曲同工者矣。然则后之读是书者，以是书为《聊斋》之续貂也可，即以是书为《聊斋》之同调也亦可。

    Explanation

    723

    June 3rd, 1855, written in the Study of Shanyin. By Li Enshou, courtesy name Bo Fu, from Changshan County.

    Preface

    The book "Liaozhai Zhiyi" contains many events that are fabricated and nonexistent, and its writing is partly about demons and ghosts. Commentators say that the spirit of "Liaozhai" is barren. However, although the events are fabricated, the principles are clear and straightforward; although the writing is about demons and ghosts, the spirit is that of loyal ministers and filial sons. Only because the spirit of "Liaozhai" is barren does "Liaozhai" express deep sorrow. The book is intended to follow the blind "Zhuans" and the corrupt "Shis," and is called a masterpiece. My fellow townsman Xie is a scholar of great learning, skilled in writing and poetry, and especially fond of ancient prose. If he had shown his talents in his youth, he would have contributed to the prosperity of the enlightened era, and his works would certainly have been comparable to "Liaozhai" in skill but different in style. But fate was against him, and he did not achieve fame. By his later years, he gave up on seeking advancement and only found pleasure in composing poetry and playing with his pen. During his leisure time from teaching and reading, he imitated the style of "Liaozhai" to create "Yizhi Lu" (Record of Wisdom). This record draws on his free-spirited and unrestrained talent, using absurd and unorthodox stories to vent his pent-up frustration. The reason for this record's creation is the same as that of "Liaozhai." This book truly has the same skill as "Liaozhai" but a different style. Thus, readers of this book may consider it a continuation of "Liaozhai" or a companion piece to it.

    724

    咸丰九年冬十一月，次山愚弟侯维垣拜识。

    　　题辞十章

    　　《益智》新编绝妙辞，争先快睹已多时。

    Explanation

    724

    In the winter of the eleventh month of the ninth year of the Xianfeng era, the foolish younger brother of Mr. Cishan, Hou Wei Yuan, respectfully acknowledges.

    The newly compiled book "Enriching Wisdom" contains exquisite verses, and I have been eager to see it and have already done so for a long time.

    725

    琳琅今得窥全豹，始见庐山真面奇。

    　　解翁胸次何超超，一样葫芦异样描。
    　　内寓劝惩外示诡，文如庄列与骚萧。

    　　绝代旷怀轶世才，山中著作隐黄台。
    　　等闲一管书空笔，曾濯泺源济水来。

    　　目见耳闻尽毕罗，外孙幼妇拟曹娥。
    　　陆离光怪入良史，留得忠贞孝义多。

    　　搜神谭鬼百篇中，言若涌泉气若虹。
    　　把酒东窗一展卷，云阴风淡雨濛濛。

    　　姑妄言之姑妄听，一堂虚白一灯青。
    　　蛇神牛鬼惊人句，浑似东方《神异经》。

    　　词人题咏尽成章，莫道笔荒墨又唐。
    　　我欲追随访古迹，玉环金礔入奚囊。

    　　镂影雕空笔引虹，前身应是聊斋翁。
    　　山精野兔闲吟哦，社鬼城狐点染工。

    　　休论真假多狐疑，著手成春字字宜。
    　　若使瑶编付绣梓，《鸡林》莫与争传奇。

    　　直与史争光，高怀不可量。
    　　诙谐言外见，覆诫语中藏。

    Explanation

    725

    The Precious Gems Now Reveal the Whole Picture, and the True Essence of Mount Lu Is Revealed.

    The gentleman's mind is truly extraordinary; the same gourd is depicted in various ways.
    Within it lies admonition and punishment, while outwardly it shows strangeness; the writing is like that of Zhuangzi, Liezi, and the Li Sao.

    With extraordinary and far-reaching thoughts, he possesses talent that surpasses the world; his works in the mountains are hidden on the Yellow Platform.
    Casually wielding a brush in the air, he has been purified by the clear waters of the Luo and Ji rivers.

    Everything seen and heard is fully recorded; the stories of filial piety are compared to those of Cao E.
    Strange and colorful phenomena enter good history, preserving many examples of loyalty, fidelity, and filial piety.

    Searching for gods and talking about ghosts in a hundred writings, the words flow like a gushing spring, and the spirit is like a rainbow.
    Holding wine by the eastern window and unrolling the scroll, the clouds are dim, the wind is gentle, and the rain is misty.

    Let me say it casually, let me listen casually; in a simple room with a single green lamp.
    The startling sentences of snake gods and ox ghosts are like the Shen Yi Jing from the East.

    Poets and writers compose verses, all becoming complete works; do not say that the brush is barren and the ink is from the Tang dynasty.
    I wish to follow and visit ancient sites, collecting jade rings and golden bells in my small bag.

    Intricate and detailed writing leads like a rainbow; his predecessor should be the author of Liaozhai Zhiyi.
    The mythical beings of the mountains and wild rabbits idly recite, while the village ghosts and city foxes are skillfully embellished.

    Do not concern yourself with whether it is true or false, full of suspicion; with a touch, spring is created, and every word is appropriate.
    If the precious book is printed, no one can compete with it in terms of legends.

    Directly competing with history for brilliance, his lofty aspirations are immeasurable.
    Witty words are seen beyond the surface, and warnings are hidden within the language.

    726

    世岂思狐媚，歌将著鬼狂。
    　　奇书欣展读，评骘待渔洋。

    　　癸丑冬，友人案头有《益智录》数篇，披读之下，不禁欣赏。友更言他篇之佳，予甚有全豹未窥之憾。乙卯春，先生以全集相寄，并命予为序。予不才，敬作题辞十章，即希诲政。

    　　世晚盖意城防如甫敬题。

    　　题辞六章

    　　鼠神狐圣富礕罗，想见灵均向壁呵。
    　　万物疏观穷色相，重言较比寓言多。

    　　广增《周说》续《虞初》，已补东方骂鬼书。
    　　娓娓清谈霏锯屑，翻新端不类抄胥。

    　　仙缘佛果合为家，著手成春论粲花。
    　　会得个中惩劝意，心田种子茁灵芽。

    　　岂真搴秀石门端，意蕊词条作是观。
    　　绮语无嫌游戏出，炼心直胜太飞丸。

    　　我亦闲居订古疑，击撞金石自娱戏。
    　　杂篇偶撷英华库，文字因缘信有之。

    　　久耳芳徽未识荆，得从卷里悟平生。
    　　他年稿付梅花刻，许与名山并寿名。

    Explanation

    726

    The World Might Ponder the Cunning Charm of Foxes, and Songs Might Sing of the Wildness of Ghosts.
    A Rare Book Delights Me as I Read, and I Await Wang Shizhen's Judgment.

    In the Winter of 1893, a friend had several chapters of Yizhi Lu on his desk. As I read them, I could not help but admire them. My friend also spoke of the excellence of other sections, and I felt a great regret for not having seen the whole. In the Spring of 1895, the author sent me the complete work and asked me to write a preface. Though I am not talented, I respectfully composed ten verses as a dedication, hoping for your guidance.

    This dedication is intended to honor the author in a manner befitting a respected figure.

    Six Verses of Dedication

    Mouse gods and fox saints, rich in lore, I imagine Qu Yuan facing the wall and exclaiming.
    Viewing all things with a broad perspective, exploring their myriad forms, the use of repetition and comparison in fables is abundant.

    Expanding on The Zhou Talks and continuing Yu Chao, it has supplemented the Eastern Tales of the Supernatural.
    With eloquent and clear discourse, it refreshes the mind with its unique style, truly unlike the work of a mere copyist.

    The connection between fairy tales and Buddhist fruits, transforming the world with a touch, is like a brilliant flower.
    Understanding the moral lessons within, the seeds of wisdom in the heart grow and flourish.

    Is it truly the beauty of the stone gate, or is it the essence of the flower that one should observe?
    Elegant language, without fear of being playful, hones the heart, surpassing even the elixir of immortality.

    I too, in idle seclusion, resolve ancient doubts, striking and colliding with the metal and stone for my own amusement.
    Occasionally gathering the essence from a collection of miscellaneous writings, there is indeed a bond between words and fate.

    For a long time, I have heard of your fame but have not met you in person. Now, I can understand your life through the pages.
    In the future, when I submit my manuscript to be carved on the plum blossom, I hope to share its immortality with the famous mountains.

    727

    竹吾弟马国翰拜草。

    　　题辞

    　　造凤原非小技，雕虫定无奇文。读此卷神情古异，藻采纷披，纬史经经，雅俗得体。蚁珠九曲，曲曲皆巧；人身百窍，窍窍悉通。锦绣肠中，无端奥妙，可谓大才。 仲霖愚弟侯雨人拜读。

    　　此书名为《益智录》甚当，即愿以此赠之。盖以前人有《闻见录》，不可袭取其名故也。 仲霖又识。

    　　录中诸作，叙事见性情之正，树义明理道之大。气深灏瀚，文极澄清。阐幽显微，尤得《春秋》善善欲长之意；规过劝善，内寓诗人温柔敦厚之风，真有功于世道人心之作也。可名为“劝惩录”。

    　　题词

    　　〔总牌双调新水令〕一杯浊酒下《离骚》，莽天涯把香魂重吊。涂山翻秘录，湘水洗情苗。牢落吟瓢，都付与鲍家稿。

    　　〔驻马听〕木客花妖，魑魅多情红豆少；江妃海若，精灵无恙碧天遥。三生亭畔牡丹娇，五云城上芙蓉笑。才多恨转饶，醉昆仑踏遍了邯郸道。

    Explanation

    727

    A humble offering from my younger brother Ma Guohan.

    Preface

    Creating something as grand as a phoenix is not a minor skill, and carving insects will not produce extraordinary writing. Reading this scroll, one finds an ancient and unique spirit, with rich and varied ornamentation, comprehensive and thorough, suitable for both refined and popular audiences. Like a maze of ants carrying pearls, each turn is clever; like a human body with many openings, all are connected. It contains hidden and intricate beauty, indeed a great talent.

    A humble read and appreciation by my younger brother Hou Yuren.

    This book is appropriately titled "The Record of Wisdom," and I wish to give it this name. Formerly, there was a "Record of Observations," and I do not want to imitate its name.

    Further recognized by Hou Yuren.

    The various works in the record, in narrating, reveal the correctness of character; in establishing principles, they elucidate great truths. The spirit is deep and vast, the writing extremely clear. It expounds the subtle and the hidden, particularly capturing the meaning of the "Spring and Autumn Annals" in favoring good deeds and encouraging their continuation. It corrects faults and encourages virtue, embodying the gentle and sincere style of poets. Truly, it is a work that benefits the world and the hearts of people. It can be titled "The Record of Encouragement and Reprimand."

    Epigraph

    [General Title, Double Tune, New Water Melody] With a cup of turbid wine, I descend into "Li Sao"; I wander the vast earth, reinvoking the fragrant soul. The secret records of Mount Tu are overturned, and the waters of Xiang wash the seeds of emotion. The solitary poet's gourd, all given to the drafts of the Bao family.

    [Stay and Listen] The tree spirits and flower demons, the spirits and fairies of the forest and sea, are full of emotion, but few are as passionate as the red beans; the water goddess and sea deity, the spirits are unharmed, their blue sky far away. The peonies are beautiful by the Three Lives Pavilion, the lotuses smile on the Five Clouds City. With abundant talent, sorrow only increases; drunk, I traverse the Kunlun Mountains, treading the path of Handan.

    728

    〔沉醉东风〕吊秋月女郎坟小，锁春阴燕子楼高。访天台有落花，泛海外多仙草。尽巫山雨窟云巢，梦里如烟卷地消，也要把烟痕细描。

    　　〔雁儿落〕说不尽逐杨花命薄飘飘，有几个玉堂人金闺料。算多少红楼花月身，都睡了黄土风流觉。

    　　〔得胜令〕问谁个天渚整星桥，月殿响云萧；玉枕寻江浦，瑶环觅汉皋。萧条，步仙踪环珮渺；寂寥，惜芳尘寤寐劳。

    　　〔甜水令〕只有些仿仿佛佛，隐隐现现，诗魔萦绕。不是彩云抛，是笔底精魂做花片儿，一字字都化做艳李夭桃。

    　　〔碧玉箫〕听风弄林梢，似有个人娟俏；看月漾花条，似有个人幽窈。情难了，拨秋灯尽力瞧。月儿又摇，风儿又袅，风娟月媚谁同调。

    　　〔拙鲁速〕从今后策神鳌要问碧霄，驾长蛟要破海潮。锦字也休烧，铁网也休捞。声萧萧是万树秋号，势滔滔是三江暮涛。华岳是岧峣，峨嵋是逋峭。恰便似跨湘，赋《大招》，只少个木兰舠，碧玉桡。

    Explanation

    728

    Final Translation

    〔沉醉东风〕
    Mourning the small tomb of the young lady under the autumn moon, the high swallows' tower is locked in the shadowy spring. Visiting Tiantai, where fallen flowers are found, and sailing across the sea to find many magical herbs. The rain caves and cloud nests of Wushan, dreams dissipate like smoke spreading over the earth, yet one must carefully trace the traces of smoke.

    〔雁儿落〕
    Endless is the fate of following willow catkins, floating aimlessly, how few are the people of high social status with refined tastes. Count how many beautiful and luxurious lives, all end up sleeping in the ground, their fleeting beauty forgotten.

    〔得胜令〕
    Who has the ability to build the bridge of stars in the celestial river, the sound of clouds in the moon palace; searching for a jade pillow by the riverbank, seeking a jade ring in the Han River. Desolate, following the footsteps of immortals, the sound of jade pendants fading away; lonely, cherishing the traces of beauty in dreams.

    〔甜水令〕
    Only some vague and fleeting images, the influence of poetic inspiration. Not the scattering of colorful clouds, but the soul of the writer turning into beautiful flowers, each word transforming into a vivid and enchanting scene.

    〔碧玉箫〕
    Listening to the wind playing among the tree branches, it seems as if there is a beautiful and charming person present; watching the moonlight reflecting on the flower branches, it seems as if there is a mysterious and secluded person present. Emotions are difficult to resolve, so one must try hard to look at the autumn lamp. The moon sways again, the wind whispers again, and who can match the grace of the wind and the charm of the moon.

    〔拙鲁速〕
    From now on, one must ride the divine turtle to ask the blue sky, and drive the long dragon to break the sea waves. Do not burn the brocade letter, and do not use the iron net to fish. The sound of the wind is like the autumn wind through a thousand trees, and the momentum is like the evening tide of the three rivers. Mount Hua is towering, and Mount Emei is steep and rugged. Just like crossing the Xiang River, composing the poem "Da Zhao," only lacking a wooden boat and a jade oar.

    729

    〔尾声〕玉鱼金碗关情抱，是长吉囊中诗料。蘼芜日日生，豆蔻年年老。只愁他普天下作鬼的，相思何日了。

    　　读竟佳著，无任钦佩，泣鬼搜神，尽皆入妙。文则大海回澜，事则夏云奇峰，殆与《聊斋志异》、《池北偶谈》、《虞初新志》诸书争席，洵足名世寿世，有益风教者不少，诚董狐之妙笔，风雅之大观也。沄弱少诗书之训，长无笔墨之缘，忽睹奇文，为之拍案者累日。谨填数词以志幸。时在咸丰丁巳花朝后日，识于桐荫花馆。

    　　同邑大梦居士愚弟余澐云川氏拜读。

    　　题词二章

    　　开编千万字，一字一珍珠。
    　　艺苑推班马，泉台得董狐。
    　　搜神留秘笈，谈鬼慰穷途。
    　　等此雄奇略，高才绝世无。

    　　我亦伤心侣，观君倍黯然。
    　　青衫迟旧梦，黄卷著新编。
    　　魑魅留真相，诗书结素缘。
    　　瓣香诚不愧，奇想继留仙。 浙江绍兴府萧山县瀛仙蔡庆元初稿

    Explanation

    729

    [Tailpiece]
    The jade fish and golden bowls carry emotional weight, these are the poetic materials of Li He. The millefolium grows daily, and the clove-bud ages year by year. Only worry that all spirits will long for it, when will the longing end?

    Upon reading this excellent work, I cannot help but admire it deeply. It moves ghosts and searches for spirits, all of it is exquisite. The text is like the vast sea with its waves returning, and the events are like the towering peaks of summer clouds. It is almost on par with works such as Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio, Random Notes from the North Pond, and New Records from the First Era of Yu. It is indeed a masterpiece that will be remembered through the ages and beneficial to the moral and social order. It is truly the exquisite brush of Dong Fu, a grand spectacle of elegance.

    After reading this, I filled out several poems to express my joy. It was on the day after the flower festival in the year Ding Si of the Xianfeng era, written in the Pine Shade Flower Pavilion.

    A Dream Dweller, the younger brother of Yu Yinyuan, from Xingxiang County, Zhejiang Province, Jiaxing Prefecture, Yixian, Cai Qingyuan, initially drafted.

    Two Poems on the Subject

    Opening the book, a thousand words, each word is a pearl.
    In the garden of literature, it stands alongside Ban and Ma,
    In the netherworld, it is the brush of Dong Fu.
    Searching for spirits leaves behind a secret text,
    Talking about ghosts consoles the weary path.
    Such heroic and strange sketches,
    There is no greater talent in the world.

    I am also a companion who laments,
    Seeing you makes me feel even more sorrowful.
    The green robe lingers in the old dream,
    The yellow scrolls are the new compilation.
    Chimeras leave behind their true forms,
    Poetry and books form a pure connection.
    With sincere incense, there is no shame,
    Continuing the legacy of the immortal.

    730

    题词二章

    　　皎月秋霜老气横，是非名利不相争。
    　　娱闲笔墨成游戏，寄托遥深见性情。
    　　百琲珍珠穿穴密，一方古镜照神清。
    　　如来妙演莲华法，普作慈航渡众生。

    　　编成《益智》适优游，说部应推第一流。
    　　眼际烟云观宇宙，笔端衮莼拟《春秋》。
    　　雕镌造化楼修凤，刻画纤微棘作猴。
    　　曼倩无人柯古去，此书常在世间留。 受业梁健谨题

    　　改烟雨楼志异元序

    　　忆余志学之年，尝从先大人赴外家，道出黄台山庄，遇一癯叟，在门立谈。过之，谓识此人否？是乃续《聊斋志异》者。当时余记其状貌、里居、姓字甚悉，未见其著书也。童稚识浅，臆《聊斋》何能续？先生殆徒贻狗尾识耳！以故旷世逸才，数十年作者失之交臂。壬辰腊，儿子按远携所著《益智录》来，披读再四，毋爽然曰：“此璞玉也，何可久湮！”春日多佳，遂忘谫陋，点窜涂乙，校正其讹。

    Explanation

    730

    Two Poems on the Subject

    The moon shines bright and autumn frost is stern, an imposing presence, not competing with right and wrong, fame, and profit.
    Writing becomes a game of leisure, revealing deep emotions and character.
    Like pearls strung together, the writing is intricate and valuable, like an ancient mirror reflecting a clear and pure spirit.
    The Buddha's profound teachings, like the lotus flower, offer universal compassion and salvation.

    The book Yi Zhi is compiled to provide leisure and enjoyment, and should be considered among the best in its genre.
    Viewing the universe as if through a misty lens, the writing is as detailed and refined as the Spring and Autumn Annals.
    Intricate and skillful, the writing is like carving a phoenix, and even the smallest details are meticulously crafted, like carving a monkey.
    Sima Xiangru is no more, and the book will remain in the world, a lasting testament to its value.
    Respectfully inscribed by Liang Jian, a disciple.

    Revised Preface to the Original Yan Yulu Zhiyi

    Recalling my years of learning, I once traveled with my father to my mother's family home, passing through Huangtai Mountain Village, where I met an emaciated old man standing at the gate. As I passed, he asked if I knew him, saying he was the one who continued Liaozhai Zhiyi. At the time, I remembered his appearance, residence, and name in great detail, but did not see his book. As a child, my understanding was limited, and I doubted how Liaozhai could be continued. The old man might have been merely adding a dog's tail to the work! Thus, a talented writer was missed for decades. In the late winter of the Renchen year, my son brought Yi Zhi Lu to me, and I read it repeatedly, exclaiming, "This is a piece of uncut jade; how can it remain buried for long!" The pleasantness of spring led me to forget my own inadequacies, and I began to edit and revise the text, correcting its errors.

    731

    删十一卷为八卷，仍从先生志，改名《烟雨楼续聊斋志异》。盖以世事愈出愈奇，《聊斋》后不能无异，即不可无所志。惟是谈鬼喜妄，情同苏子之烂漫，几于神道设教，编辑刍荛矣！纵品评月旦，笔削阳秋，讵禭拟大家班氏，而要非画蛇添足，岂续《西厢》、续《水浒》者所可同年语哉？或谓：世之传书贵神似，不贵形似。《春秋》继《诗》，《左氏》、《史记》本《春秋》，即《聊斋》手笔，亦学盲左刑迁而独辟蹊径者也。余曰：“固然。然神肖既可，形神毕肖有何不可？”群疑为之一笑而释。

    　　光绪十九年癸巳暮春廿日一轺宋翘撰。

    Explanation

    731

    The original eleven volumes were reduced to eight, still following the master's will, and renamed as Yanyulou Continuation of Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio. This was done because the world's affairs become increasingly strange, and there cannot be no differences after Liao Zhai; it is also impossible to not record them. However, the work tends to talk about ghosts and be prone to exaggeration, with the same unrestrained nature as Su Shi, almost like setting up divine teachings, edited by the common people! Even if they criticize and judge, cut and edit with the precision of the Spring and Autumn Annals, how can it be compared to the great family of Ban, but it is not adding a foot to a snake. How can it be compared to those who continued The Western Chamber and Water Margin? Someone might say: in the world, transmitted books value spirit likeness, not form likeness. The Spring and Autumn Annals followed the Book of Poetry, and Zuo's Commentary and Records of the Grand Historian are based on the Spring and Autumn Annals. Even if Liao Zhai is the work of the author, it also learns from Zuo Qiuming and Sima Qian and opens a new path. I said: "Indeed. But if spirit likeness is possible, why not both spirit and form likeness?" All doubts were dispelled with a laugh.

    Written by Song Qiao on the 20th day of the last month of spring in the year of Gui Si during the Guangxu era of the Qing dynasty.

    732

    刻《烟雨楼续聊斋志异》改本例言

    　　一、是编原仿蒲氏《志异》而作，其中字句有与《聊斋》如出一手，或少变换而愈新者，悉仍其旧。其词意近袭，有类演义小说家者，概为删去。

    　　一、是书初名《益智录》，闻原稿旧有三部，后失其二。仆所见者，卷端有叶芸士先生手书行草一序。抄选讫，仍还故主。改为《烟雨楼续聊斋志异》，附家藏《留仙四六文》一卷于后。

    　　一、篇中实有踳驳处、冗长处、未能免俗处。仆非好为去取，深恐言之无文，行而不远，且有蒲氏在前，极盛难继，易致阅者之厌。原书俱在，识者鉴之。

    　　一、文章本宇宙间公共之物，自鸣天籁，不择好音，故诸卷中窜易之处，亦未便一一注明。作者既非抛砖引玉，改者岂必点铁成金？

    　　一、删定之后细阅，尚不免有粗鄙语。覆思此闲编也，惟思雅俗共赏，况蒲志中如“蒸饼”、“抱腰”、“高粱叶”等字亦往往有之，似不宜过刻。

    Explanation

    732

    Preface to the Revised Edition of Yanyulou Continued Strange Stories of Liaozhai

    1. This compilation was originally modeled after Pu's Strange Stories. Where the words and meanings are identical or slightly altered to become even newer, all were retained as they were. Those with words and meanings that are too similar, resembling storytelling novelists, were generally deleted.

    2. Originally named Yizhi Lu, I heard that the original manuscript had three copies, but later two were lost. What I saw had a handwritten cursive preface by Mr. Yè Yúnsì at the front of the volume. After copying and selecting, it was returned to the original owner. It was changed to Yanyulou Continued Strange Stories of Liaozhai, and one volume of my family's collection of Liuxian's Four-Six Prose was appended at the end.

    3. There are indeed flaws, redundancies, and parts that cannot avoid being conventional. I do not like to arbitrarily remove or keep. I deeply fear that if the words lack elegance, they will not be widely accepted, and with Pu's work preceding, it is hard to surpass, making it easy to make readers厌烦.

    4. Literature is a public good in the universe, naturally resonating like the sound of nature, without choosing good sounds. Therefore, places where changes were made are not convenient to note them one by one. The author did not intend to throw out a brick to attract jade, and those who revise do not necessarily turn iron into gold.

    5. Even after careful review, there are still some coarse and vulgar expressions. Reconsidering, this is just a casual compilation, only thinking it should be enjoyed by both refined and common people. And in Pu's work, words like "steamed bun," "embrace waist," and "sorghum leaf" are also often found, so it seems不宜过刻.

    733

    一、是书出迄今垂三十馀年，吾乡并无传抄，遑问国与天下。诚有如叶令所云：名字不出里閈，士大夫鲜知其谁何。斯非不平之事哉！兹者他山之石，谋呼将伯，勉付手民。先生有知，庶可无抱璞之泣也。历城宋翘一轺甫识。

    　　序

    　　粤若鸟翼寒冰，周诗入颂；雉雊彤日，商史垂书。《礼》则丘首正狐，《易》则车心载鬼。以至右文启化，左氏修词，鬼且辨乎旧新，蛇亦分乎内外。豕或传其人立，后鷁更记其退飞。伯有来乎，市则昼散；杜回抗矣，野与鬼谋。莫不探二气万类之奇，于以著大中至正之准矣。他如干宝搜神，齐谐志怪；徐福上求仙之策，阮瞻著无鬼之文。是皆泥于一偏，曾何当于大雅！又如北苑之名画为妖，东坡之闲情说鬼；谈道则经取西方，参禅则佛求南海。义既无关乎劝惩，事亦奚贵乎编摩。况乎丛书积海，岂少搜奇选胜之辞；稗史堆山，自多尽态极妍之笔。然未归诸典，则究难许以文章。

    Explanation

    733

    Translation

    This book has been in existence for nearly thirty years now, yet there has been no copying of it in my hometown, let alone in the country or the world. Indeed, as Ye Ling has said: its name does not go beyond the village gate, and few scholars know who it is. Is this not an unjust situation? Now, like borrowing a stone from another mountain, I am seeking help from others and entrusting it to the printer. If the master is aware, perhaps he will no longer shed tears of frustration over his unrecognized talent. Licheng Song Qiao, a simple scholar, has just come to know it.

    Preface

    As the wings of birds and the frozen ice are praised in the Shijing (Book of Songs), and the rooster's crow at dawn is recorded in the Shangshu (Book of Documents), so too in the Liji (Book of Rites), the fox is said to be upright at the top of the hill, and in the Yijing (Book of Changes), the carriage carries ghosts. Thus, as literature awakens civilization and the Zuo Zhuan (Commentary of Zuo) refines language, ghosts are distinguished between old and new, and snakes are divided between inside and outside. Pigs may be said to stand on their hind legs, and later the Yi records its backward flight. When Bo You appears, the market disperses during the day; when Du Hui resists, the wilds conspire with ghosts. None of these phenomena fail to explore the wonders of the two forces and myriad things, thereby establishing the standard of great virtue and ultimate correctness. Others, like Gan Bao who sought spirits and Qi Xie who recorded strange events, or Xu Fu who proposed a strategy for seeking immortality and Ruan Zhan who wrote about the absence of ghosts, are all confined to one-sided views and cannot be considered in harmony with the highest elegance. Moreover, like the famous paintings of the Northern Garden that are said to be bewitched, or Su Shi's idle musings about ghosts, when discussing the Dao one turns to the West for scriptures, and when practicing Zen one seeks the South for the Buddha. Such matters have no relevance to admonition or retribution, and why should they be valued in compilation and editing? Furthermore, with so many books collected in the sea, are there not enough words to search for the extraordinary and select the excellent? With so many trivial histories piled like mountains, there are naturally many writings that portray all states and extreme beauty. Yet if they are not included in the classics, it is ultimately difficult to consider them as true articles of literature.

    734

    山左解子，历下宿儒，录存《益智》为名，文慕留仙而作。写仙家之鸾凤，不赋洛阳；谱水国之鸳鸯，非夸汉女。吹灯而戴女萝，骚追风雅；待月而攀弱柳，记异会真。影绘张三，说风流亦堪警世；骂同刘四，评月旦绝不恼人。细观其竹素万言，洵出彼蒲编一等哉！殆见齐风淫靡，泽贴管子之书；鲁俗轻浮，化泯孔家之政。虚堂镜暗，为表《离娄》；孽海珠沉，代求象罔。慨江河之日下，有礼失求野之思；仰山岳之风高，得曲终奏雅之意。良工亦心苦矣，哲匠其情怡乎？更愿赁千房而观宝籍，福志琅嬛；铸九鼎以象神奸，祥开委宛。书著等身，发天上不传之秘；余时刮目，读人间未有之奇。是为序。

    Explanation

    734

    Shan Zuo Jie Zi, a scholar from the Li Xia area, compiled and preserved Yi Zhi under the name "Recording Wisdom," writing in admiration of the immortal Liu Xian. He depicted the phoenixes of the immortal realm, refraining from describing Luoyang; he recorded the mandarin ducks of the water country, without boasting about Han women. He painted shadows and wore nu luo while blowing out the lamp, pursuing elegance and refinement; he waited for the moon and climbed a weak willow, recording strange encounters with the true. He portrayed Zhang San, describing elegance and grace that can also serve as a warning to the world; he cursed along with Liu Si, commenting on the moon and dawn without offending anyone. Carefully examining its bamboo slips with ten thousand words, truly, it is of the same class as the Pu Bian. Likely seeing the decadence of Qi, moistening the book of Guan Zi; the customs of Lu are light and frivolous, transforming the politics of the Confucian family. The empty hall with a dim mirror, to represent Li Lou; the pearl sinking in the sea of sin, seeking Xiang Wang on behalf of others. Sighing at the daily decline of the rivers and seas, having the thought of losing rites and seeking the wilderness; looking up to the high winds of the mountains and hills, gaining the meaning of concluding with elegance. The skilled worker also suffers in their heart, the wise craftsman is surely content. Wishing to rent a thousand rooms to view the precious books, blessing the aspirations of Lang Huan; casting nine tripods to symbolize the gods and demons, auspiciousness opening in Wei Wan. Writing as tall as one's body, revealing the secrets of heaven that are not transmitted; at times, one must look at things with new eyes, reading the wonders that have never been seen in the world. This is the preface.

    735

    同治五年夏六月，三韩松亭氏何毓福拜撰。

    　　附录二：稿本所存原《益智录》目录

    　　第一卷

    　　何福 鬼联 冤缢 小宝 柳逢春 陈若愚 安燕贻 薛维东 狐夫人

    　　第二卷

    　　梅仙 巨蝎 上官勇 蜈蚣 申术士 袁岫云 某伟 于媪李义 应富有 宋蕙娘 顾道全 张清 金瑞

    　　第三卷

    　　苏玉真 义狼 姚五官 瑞红 贺举人 聂文焕 福德会馆杨彩云 赵阁老 琼华岛附海咸河淡论 杜仲 隗士杰

    　　第四卷

    Explanation

    735

    Appendix II: Original Directory of Yi Zhi Lu Preserved in the Manuscript

    First Volume

    He Fu, Ghost Union, Wrongful Hanging, Xiao Bao, Liu Fengchun, Chen Ruoyu, An Yanyi, Xue Weidong, Fox Lady

    Second Volume

    Mei Xian, Giant Scorpion, Shangguan Yong, Centipede, Shen Taoist, Yuan Xiuyun, Certain Great One, Yu Lady, Li Yi, Ying Fuyou, Song Huinian, Gu Daoquan, Zhang Qing, Jin Rui

    Third Volume

    Su Yuzhen, Righteous Wolf, Yao Wuguan, Rui Hong, He Juren, Nie Wenhuan, Fude Temple, Yang Caoyun, Zhao Chancellor, Penglai Island, Discussion on Sea Salty and River Fresh, Du Zhong, Wei Shijie

    Fourth Volume

    [Note: The fourth volume is not provided in the source text, so it cannot be translated.]

    736

    散花仙女 义犬 节烈女 酒友 李智 许怀芳 谷一穗华月娘 方安仁 伍丽娘 谢恭 江氏 金英 弓杰 牛鬼

    　　第五卷

    　　翠玉 刘翁 林闺英 胡二姐 某公子 白猿 某乙 阿娇义鬼 碧玉 请乩 来生债 旱脚鱼 潘泽沅

    　　第六卷

    　　秦丽娟 常山某 詹如仙 崔洁 黄艳仙 白玉容 普护星田思义 周仙

    　　第七卷

    　　月仙 路案 巩生 毕成 虚娘 水母三娘 琼仙 张春娇章邑生 王威 陇州三案 王富段成

    　　第八卷

    　　顾清高 江在新 美人图 某邑案 瑞雪 赵诚 恶梦许翠娥 齐氏 矫娘 牛子良

    　　第九卷

    　　荷花三娘 苏成 陈恺 类桃源 张李氏 颜星丽 吉人封云 康履泽 樊稼修 贺梦龄 义仆 人面疮

    　　第十卷

    　　耿如梅 五子登科 某公 林芳 夙冤 龚静山 轻财拒色节孝妇 某甲 施生 李司训 鸦片烟 奚霖

    　　第十一卷（无)

    Explanation

    736

    Translation:

    Scattering Flowers Fairy, Loyal Dog, Chaste and Loyal Woman, Drinking Companion, Li Zhi, Xu Huifang, Gu Yihui Huayue Lady, Fang Anren, Wu Linyang, Xie Gong, Jiang Shi, Jin Ying, Gong Jie, Niu Gui

    Fifth Volume

    Jade Green, Liu Old Man, Lin Guiying, Hu Erjie, Some Nobleman, White Ape, Some Second, Ajiao Spirit, Jade Green, Divination Board, Debts of Future Lives, Drought-Resilient Fish, Pan Zeyuan

    Sixth Volume

    Qin Lijuan, Some Person from Changshan, Zhan Ruxian, Cui Jie, Huang Yanxian, Bai Yurong, Protector Star Tian Sidi, Zhou Xian

    Seventh Volume

    Moon Fairy, Road Case, Gong Sheng, Bi Cheng, Ghost Woman, Water Mother Three Ladies, Qiong Xian, Zhang Chunqiao Zhang Yisheng, Wang Wei, Longzhou Three Cases, Wang Fuduan Cheng

    Eighth Volume

    Gu Qinggao, Jiang Zai Xin, Beauty Painting, Some County Case, Auspicious Snow, Zhao Cheng, Bad Dream Xu Cui'e, Qi Shi, Jiao Niang, Niu Ziliang

    Ninth Volume

    Lotus Three Ladies, Su Cheng, Chen Kai, Peach Blossom Spring, Zhang Lishi, Yan Xingli, Good Person Feng Yun, Kang Lüze, Fan Jiaxiu, He Mengling, Loyal Servant, Face Ulcer

    Tenth Volume

    Geng Rumei, Five Sons Pass the Examinations, Some Official, Lin Fang, Old Grudge, Gong Jingshan, Frugal and Reclusive Chaste and Filial Woman, Some A, Shi Sheng, Li Sishun, Opium, Xi Lin

    Eleventh Volume (None)
